Heat ghee and oil in a handi, add paste and cook for 7-8 minutes until oil separates from masala. 3. Add onions, capsicum, tomatoes and butter, cook for 4-5 minutes. 4. Add water to adjust consistency, add fried paneer and cook until done. 5. Finish with fresh coriander leaves and cheese. 6. For fried paneer: heat oil, fry paneer slabs until light golden and crispy. 7. Serve hot with ladi pav and roti.

name: Thecha Aloo
instructions: 1. In a cooker, heat oil and add potatoes, salt, sugar, asafoetida, turmeric powder and green chillies. 2. Add water and cook for 2-3 whistles until potatoes are tender. 3. Make thecha: grind green chillies, coriander stems and coriander leaves into coarse paste. 4. In a handi, heat ghee, add cumin seeds and prepared thecha, saute for 2-3 minutes. 5. Add boiled potatoes and ghee, cook for 4-5 minutes. 6. Add water to adjust consistency and give a quick boil. 7. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with coriander and lemon wedge. 8. Serve hot with methi puri.

name: Palak Anda Curry
instructions: 1. Heat ghee and oil, add bay leaf, cumin seeds, dry red chillies and saute for a minute. 2. Add onions and salt, cook until light golden. 3. Add coriander powder, turmeric powder, degi red chilli powder and cook for a minute. 4. Add tomatoes, ginger garlic paste, yogurt, coriander stems and cook for 4-5 minutes until oil separates. 5. Add spinach, salt, sugar and cook for 4-5 minutes. 6. Add water, cover and simmer until cooked. 7. Use hand blender to blend gravy into coarse paste. 8. Add water to adjust consistency and dry masala. 9. Add hard boiled eggs, sugar, salt and simmer for 7-8 minutes. 10. Finish with crushed fenugreek leaves and ghee, give a quick boil. 11. Serve hot with roti and steamed rice.

name: Crispy Chilli Gobi
instructions: 1. Make batter: mix refined flour, cornstarch, salt, sugar, vinegar, water and oil. 2. Coat cauliflower florets in batter. 3. Deep fry in hot oil until light golden and crispy, drain on absorbent sheet. 4. Make sauce: mix tomato ketchup, red chilli sauce, green chilli sauce, vinegar and soy sauce. 5. Heat oil in a wok until smoky hot, add dry red chillies, coriander stem and ginger. 6. Saute for a minute, then add onion and mixed bell peppers. 7. Toss for 2-3 minutes on high heat. 8. Add prepared sauce and fried cauliflower, toss for 2-3 minutes. 9. Serve hot with spring onion and coriander garnish.

name: Mumbai Masala Egg Curry
instructions: 1. Make paste: grind garlic, ginger, coriander leaves, green chillies, salt and oil into smooth paste. 2. Heat oil in a handi, add peppercorns, cloves, cumin seeds and let them splutter. 3. Add onions and cook for 2-3 minutes until translucent. 4. Add prepared paste, yogurt, coriander powder, degi red chilli powder, turmeric powder and cook for 7-8 minutes until oil separates. 5. Add water and pav bhaji masala, give a quick boil. 6. Add boiled eggs, water and sugar, simmer for 4-5 minutes on medium heat. 7. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with salad and coriander. 8. Serve hot with lal mirch ka pulao (red chilli rice).

name: Punjabi Tari Wala Paneer
ingredients: 1 heaped tsp degi red chilli powder
instructions: 1. Make ginger garlic green chilli paste: grind green chillies, ginger, garlic and salt into coarse paste. 2. Heat ghee in a handi, add fenugreek seeds, cumin seeds and prepared paste, saute for a minute. 3. Add black & green cardamom, coriander stems, onion, ghee and oil, cook for 4-5 minutes until onion gets light golden. 4. Add coriander powder, turmeric powder, degi red chilli powder and cook for 2 minutes. 5. Add little water and tomato puree, cook for 4-5 minutes until oil separates. 6. Add hot water and soaked fenugreek leaves, cover and simmer. 7. Fry paneer cubes in hot oil until light golden, then soak in warm salted water to soften. 8. Add soaked paneer to curry, add salt and cook for 5-6 minutes. 9. Serve hot with roti or naan.

name: Dhaba Chicken Curry
ingredients: 1 heaped tbsp degi red chilli powder
instructions: Marinate chicken with salt, ginger-garlic paste, red chilli powder and mustard oil. In a handi, heat mustard oil until smoky and add cardamom, peppercorns, cloves. Cook onions for 5-6 minutes until light golden, add tomatoes and puree, cook briefly. Add ginger-garlic paste and ghee, cook 4-5 minutes. Add red chilli powder, coriander powder, turmeric and salt, cook 1 minute. Add marinated chicken and hot water, cover and simmer 12-15 minutes until chicken is tender. Finish with green chillies, ginger, coriander leaves, black pepper and garam masala. Boil once more and serve hot with roti and rice.

name: Jodhpuri Kabuli Pulao
instructions: Soak rice for 30 minutes. Cook rice in ghee and water for 4-5 minutes until half cooked, add peas and cook 5-6 minutes until done. Transfer to a tray. Prepare curd mixture by beating curd with ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, red chilli and coriander powder. In a handi, heat ghee, add cumin and kashmiri chillies, cook 1 minute. Add curd mixture, coriander powder and cook 7-8 minutes until oil separates. Add cream and fried vegetables, set aside. Fry vegetables, nuts and bread separately until crisp and golden. Layer in handi: rice, fried nuts, fried bread, repeat layers, top with saffron milk and coriander. Heat tawa, place handi on it, cook 2-3 minutes on high, then 15-20 minutes on reduced flame. Rest 10-12 minutes before serving.

name: Chicken Seekh Kebab Roll
ingredients: 1 tbsp tender coriander stems, finely chopped
1 heaped tbsp degi red chilli powder
instructions: Prepare dough by kneading flour with salt, ghee, oil, carom seeds and water until smooth. Rest dough with oil coating. Prepare seekh kebab by marinating chicken with fenugreek leaves, coriander stems, green chillies, ginger, ghee, red chilli powder, garam masala and salt. Grind cardamom and peppercorns for dry masala and add to marination. Cook seekh kebabs in oil for 10-12 minutes. Prepare sauce by heating oil and ghee, adding onions, vinegar and salt, then tomato puree, spices, ketchup, ghee and sugar, cook until thick, finish with coriander. Prepare salad with onion, green chillies and lemon juice. Roll out paratha from dough and cook on pan with ghee. Assemble roll by spreading sauce on paratha, adding cooked kebab, salad and green chutney, roll and butter top. Garnish with onion rings, coriander and lemon wedge.

name: Murgh Malai Kofta
ingredients: 1 tbsp tender coriander stems, finely chopped
instructions: Prepare kofta marination by mixing chicken, coriander stems, green chillies, ginger-garlic paste, salt and breadcrumbs, then add butter and form into lemon-sized balls. Shallow fry koftas in oil for 5-6 minutes until half cooked. Prepare gravy by heating ghee, cooking onions until translucent, adding cashews, curd and ginger-garlic paste, cook until oil separates. Add hot water, cover and cook 10-12 minutes on low flame. Cool and blend into smooth paste. Roast black peppercorns, cloves, green cardamom, mace and salt for 2-3 minutes, grind into fine powder. In a handi, add gravy, salt, green chillies and boil, add fried koftas and sugar, cook 4-5 minutes. Finish with ghee and fresh cream. Transfer to serving dish. Prepare tadka by heating ghee, adding kashmiri chillies, red chilli powder, cook briefly. Garnish with dry masala, tadka, silver vark, fenugreek leaves and coriander. Serve hot with roti or naan.

name: Iffa Chicken Curry
ingredients: 2 heaped tbsp ginger garlic green chilli paste
2 heaped tbsp degi red chilli powder
1 tbsp tender coriander stems, finely chopped
instructions: First marination: coat chicken with ginger-garlic-green chilli paste, mayonnaise and salt, rest 10-15 minutes. Second marination: prepare mixture of curd, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, cornstarch, mayonnaise and salt, coat marinated chicken, rest 10-15 minutes. Prepare dressing by blending garlic, cheese, boiled egg whites and fresh cream into smooth paste. Prepare salad by combining green chillies, ginger, mint leaves and coriander stems. Heat oil in pan, place marinated chicken and cook 10-15 minutes, flipping at intervals. Spread prepared dressing evenly on chicken, cover and cook 4-5 minutes until fully cooked and tender. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with prepared salad, crushed fenugreek leaves, black pepper powder, boiled egg slices and coriander sprig. Serve hot.

name: Jammu Style Rajma
instructions: In a cooker, add soaked kidney beans with soaking water, green chillies, ginger, black cardamom, black peppercorns, sugar, salt, coriander stems. Add chilli powder, turmeric, asafoetida, ghee and cook for 5-6 whistles until 80-90% done. Prepare tadka separately: heat butter, ghee, oil and sauté ginger, chillies, tomatoes until mushy (4-5 min). Add coriander leaves and butter. Add prepared tadka to beans with masala and cook until fully done. Finish with lemon juice. Serve hot with roti or rice.

name: Paneer in Peri Peri Sauce
instructions: Place grill rack on high flame. Roast tomatoes, red pepper, onion, green chillies directly on flame until charred on all sides. Remove green chillies first, continue with remaining vegetables. Cool in bowl, steam 5 min, then peel skins off tomatoes and pepper. Chop vegetables roughly. Grind roasted vegetables with soaked chillies, garlic, cashews into smooth purée. Cut paneer into 2 large slabs, pan-fry in oil until golden brown on both sides. Cool and cut into cubes. Toss capsicum and onion pieces briefly until lightly charred. Heat olive oil in kadhai, sauté ginger-garlic paste 1-2 min. Add peri peri purée, salt, pepper. Cover and cook on low 3-4 min. Adjust consistency with hot water. Add honey and lemon juice. Mix in paneer cubes, tossed vegetables, and coriander. For rice: heat butter, add coriander and cooked rice, toss gently until evenly coated. Serve together hot.

name: Chilli Garlic Paneer Sandwich
instructions: For chilli garlic sauce: blend paneer, curd, kashmiri chillies, garlic, honey, lemon zest, lemon juice, salt, pepper, oregano into smooth sauce. For sandwich chutney: blend coriander, green chillies, garlic, roasted chana dal, chat masala, salt, ice cubes with water into thick chutney. Prep vegetables: shred cabbage, julienne capsicum and carrot, slice onion. Mix all vegetables together. Dice paneer, season with lemon juice, salt, pepper, chilli powder. Gradually add chilli garlic sauce to vegetable mixture and mix well. For assembly: spread soft butter on one side of bread slices. Apply green chutney on inner sides of both slices. On one slice, spread vegetable-paneer mixture, cover with second slice. Heat tawa on medium flame. Butter the tawa, place sandwich on it. Put plate on top and press gently. Check once in between. When bottom is golden and crisp, butter the top, flip, cover with plate, and toast second side until crisp and golden. Remove and serve.

name: Foxtail Millet Upma
instructions: Wash foxtail millet thoroughly under running water, rubbing gently to remove dust. Discard water, add fresh water, soak 15-20 min. Prep vegetables: finely chop onion, ginger, green chilli. Coarsely chop carrot, capsicum, french beans. Heat ghee in kadhai over medium flame. Fry halved cashews and peanuts until lightly golden, remove and keep aside. In same kadhai, sauté chana dal and urad dal until light golden. Add mustard seeds, let them crackle, add hing and curry leaves. Stir briefly until aromatic. Add chopped onion-ginger-chilli mixture, sauté until onions turn translucent. Add chopped vegetables and sauté 1-2 min until lightly cooked but still retain bite. Pour measured water, stir well, bring to simmer. Taste and adjust salt. Once water simmers, strain soaked millet and add to kadhai. Stir gently to distribute evenly. Tie lid with kitchen cloth, cover, cook on lowest flame 15 min until millet absorbs water. Remove lid and check - no water should remain at bottom. Cover again, switch off flame, rest 5-7 min. Add lemon juice, coconut, coriander, fried cashews and peanuts. Gently fluff with spatula. For coconut chutney: blend coconut, roasted chana dal, tamarind, green chillies, pepper, cumin seeds, salt with water into fine chutney. Temper with oil, chana dal, urad dal, mustard seeds, curry leaves, red chillies. Serve upma hot with coconut chutney.

name: Chicken Russian Cutlet
instructions: Chop onion, ginger, garlic, carrot, peas, corn finely. Heat butter and oil in pan, sauté vegetables until soft. Add tomatoes and cook until mushy. Season with salt and pepper. Cut chicken into pieces. In a pot of water with ginger, garlic, salt and black pepper, poach chicken pieces until cooked through (about 15-20 min). Remove chicken and let cool. Shred the cooked chicken finely. Mix shredded chicken with cooked vegetables and mayonnaise to form a binding mixture. Peel and grate boiled potatoes, mix into chicken-vegetable mixture. Season with salt and pepper. Shape mixture into oval cutlets. Beat eggs in a bowl. Coat each cutlet with egg wash, then roll in breadcrumbs. Heat oil in deep pan. Fry cutlets until golden brown and crispy on all sides. For green chutney: blend coriander, green chillies, garlic, roasted chana dal, chat masala, salt, ice cubes with water into smooth chutney. Serve hot cutlets with green chutney.

name: Chaunsa Dal
instructions: 1. Wash urad dal thoroughly and dry it completely with a kitchen napkin. 2. Dry roast dal on medium heat in a cast-iron tawa until fully toasted and fragrant. Cool completely. 3. Grind cooled dal coarsely (not fine powder). 4. Pound green garlic, ginger, green chilli, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, and red chillies into coarse paste in mortar and pestle. 5. Heat mustard oil in kadhai until it just smokes. Add wild mustard seeds and hing, then add paste. Cook until raw aroma disappears. 6. Lower flame, add wheat flour, cook stirring continuously until raw smell goes. 7. Add urad dal powder and mix well, cook for 1 minute. 8. Add salt and powdered spices. 9. Gradually add hot water while whisking to avoid lumps. 10. Simmer on low flame for 45 minutes stirring occasionally. 11. Adjust consistency with hot water if needed. 12. Finish with fresh coriander.

name: Spinach Rice with Paneer
instructions: 1. Wash rice and soak. Blanch spinach for 15-20 seconds in salted hot water. Transfer to ice water immediately. Squeeze and blend with mint, coriander, green chilli, ginger, garlic and ice cube into smooth puree. Refrigerate. 2. Dice all vegetables. 3. Heat oil, crackle cumin seeds, add sliced onion until golden. Add carrots, cook 2-3 minutes, add capsicum and sweet corn. Season with red chilli flakes, salt, sugar and cook 1 minute. 4. Add spinach puree and cook 2 minutes. 5. Add hot water and drained rice. Cover tightly with cloth under lid and cook on lowest flame for 12 minutes. 6. Check doneness with spoon. Rest for 15 minutes after cooking. 7. For salsa: roast tomatoes on open flame until charred, cover and steam 3-4 minutes, rinse in ice water, trim eyes, quarter them. Chop onion, add with remaining ingredients to electric chopper and pulse coarsely. 8. Pan-grill paneer slab seasoned with salt and pepper in olive oil until golden on both sides. 9. Serve spinach rice hot with roasted tomato salsa and paneer.

name: Nolen Gurer Rosogolla
instructions: 1. Heat nolen gur with 2 cups water on low flame, stirring occasionally until melted. Transfer to bowl and cool. 2. Bring milk to gentle simmer in heavy-bottomed vessel. Mix vinegar and water in a bowl. Set strainer with double muslin over bowl and keep ice-cold water ready. 3. Turn off flame after simmering, stir for 2-3 minutes to cool slightly. Gradually add vinegar-water mixture while stirring until milk curdles completely. 4. Strain chenna immediately through muslin cloth and rinse thoroughly with ice-cold water to remove vinegar taste. 5. Squeeze muslin cloth to remove excess moisture. Chenna should be moist but not dripping. 6. Using palm base, rub chenna against plate repeatedly until it becomes smooth, creamy, and does not stick. 7. Test by rolling small ball without cracks. Gather all into dough and cover with damp cloth. 8. Divide into 20g portions and roll each gently between palms into smooth crack-free balls. Cover with damp muslin. 9. Dissolve sugar in 7 cups water in wide kadhai on high flame. Cover and bring to rolling boil. 10. Gently add rosogollas one by one, let boil uncovered for 2-3 minutes. Cover and continue boiling on high flame for 12-15 minutes maintaining rolling boil. 11. Uncover and check that rosogollas are well puffed and spongy. 12. Remove with ladle and transfer to cold water. Cool completely until they sink. 13. Gently squeeze each rosogolla once to remove excess water without compressing. Transfer to nolen gur syrup and soak for 5-6 hours or overnight in fridge.

name: Prawn Dum Biryani
instructions: 1. Soak rice in water for 30 minutes. 2. Slice onions thinly and cut tomatoes into quarters. 3. Make ginger-garlic paste and mix with yogurt, spices. 4. Marinate prawns in yogurt-spice mixture for 30 minutes. 5. Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot, add bay leaves, cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, cumin seeds and let them crackle. 6. Add fried onions to the ghee and maintain a layer. 7. Boil water with salt and spices, add soaked rice and cook until 70% done. Drain rice. 8. Arrange marinated prawns and tomatoes on the base, layer rice on top. 9. Sprinkle fried onions, mint, coriander over rice layer. 10. Cover pot tightly with foil and lid. Cook on high flame for 2-3 minutes until steam forms, then reduce to low and cook for 12-15 minutes. 11. Turn off flame and let rest for 5-10 minutes without opening. 12. Open carefully and mix biryani gently with fork. Serve hot.

name: Doodh Sev Bhaji
instructions: 1. Soak cashews in hot water and cover until soft. 2. Finely chop onion using string chopper. 3. Finely chop tomato using string chopper. 4. Finely chop ginger, garlic, and green chillies together. 5. Heat oil in kadhai on high flame. 6. Add cumin seeds and mustard seeds, let them crackle. 7. Add ginger-garlic-chilli mixture and sauté until raw aroma disappears. 8. Add hing immediately and add chopped onions. Cook until onions soften and just start to change colour. 9. Add chopped tomatoes, salt, turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, and garam masala. Mix well. 10. Add splash of hot water and cook masala properly until oil separates. 11. Drain soaked cashews and grind into fine paste with splash of water. 12. Add cashew paste to kadhai and mix well. Cook on high flame for few minutes until masala leaves the sides of pan. 13. Add splash of hot water and lower flame. 14. Add milk and mix properly. Increase flame and bring to gentle boil until gravy becomes creamy and slightly thick. 15. Taste and adjust salt if required. 16. Add thick sev or gathiya and mix gently. Simmer briefly so sev absorbs flavour but doesn't become soft. 17. Finish with crushed roasted kasuri methi, pinch of garam masala, and fresh coriander. Mix gently. 18. While serving, optionally drizzle fresh cream and add butter knob and fresh coriander. 19. Serve with tandoori roti or bread.

name: Thandai
instructions: Soak almonds, pistachio, melon seeds, poppy seeds, fennel seeds, black peppercorns, green cardamom, rose petals and saffron in water for 5-6 hours or overnight in refrigerator. Blend soaked mixture with water, rock sugar, ginger powder and 250 ml chilled milk into fine paste. Strain through muslin cloth over stock pot. Repeat extraction with 250-300 ml more chilled milk and strain again. Sieve rabdi and fresh curd together into thandai base. Add remaining chilled milk and mix well. Chill in refrigerator before serving. Optionally garnish with saffron strands and slivered nuts.

name: Chicken Tikka Roll
instructions: Dough: Mix refined flour, wheat flour, salt and sugar. Gradually add warm milk while mixing to form soft dough. Knead smooth and elastic with push-stretch motion. Cover and rest 1 hour. Rumali Roti: Divide rested dough into 30-35g pedas, oil lightly, rest 10-15 minutes. Heat tawa, flatten peda and roll very thin. Oil tawa lightly, wipe excess, place roti on high flame. Flip when bubbles appear, cook both sides briefly. Stack 3 pedas together and roll gently into single thin roti. Cook on hot tawa partially until layers show. Remove and separate layers, cook each fully on both sides. Chicken: Cut breast into thin flat strips, marinate with ginger-garlic paste, lemon, turmeric. In second bowl, whisk mustard oil, chilli powder, garam masala, salt, black salt, ginger-garlic paste and curd. Add to chicken and mix. Smoke marinade with red-hot charcoal and ghee for 3-4 minutes covered. Fold strips and skewer on water-soaked satay sticks. Cook on hot tawa with ghee, turning continuously until charred and cooked. Green Chutney: Blend coriander, mint, green chilli, garlic, ginger, tamarind, salt with ice cube. Red Chutney: Blend kashmiri chilli, garlic, tamarind, green chilli, jaggery, salt with ice cube. Assemble: Place cooked tikka strips and chutneys on rumali roti and roll.

name: Strawberry Shrikhand
instructions: Line strainer with double-layered muslin cloth and add dahi. Gather edges, twist gently and lightly squeeze to remove free whey. Place tied curd back in strainer, cover with plate and add weight on top. Refrigerate for 12-15 hours overnight. Remove weight and plate. Transfer hung curd to fine sieve and press through to remove graininess. Whisk vigorously for 3-4 minutes until smooth, light, glossy and airy. Wash strawberries, trim tops and transfer to mixer jar. Add sugar and blend into smooth purée. Transfer to saucepan on medium flame, bring to boil and continue cooking while stirring until thickened to jam-like consistency coating back of spoon (10-12 minutes). Cool completely without mixing. Add cooled compote to shrikhand base in small batches, mixing gently after each addition. Mix fully for uniform pink color or fold lightly for marbled streaked effect. Chill before serving. Garnish with biscuit crumble, chopped strawberries and mint leaves.

name: Mawa Jalebi
instructions: Sugar syrup: In saucepan add sugar and water, dissolve on high flame and boil for 1 minute keeping syrup thin. Add lemon juice and stir well to prevent crystallization. Cool to slightly warm state and set aside. Batter: Heat mawa in kadhai on low flame and mash gently until soft and melts slightly. Gradually add milk in batches while stirring on low flame, achieving semi-thick consistency. Switch off flame and cool. Add corn flour in 3 batches, mixing gently after each addition to avoid lumps. Once cooled completely, strain through sieve for extra smooth consistency achieving thick dropping texture. Transfer to piping bag. Frying: Heat oil to exactly 155°C. Cut piping bag opening to 1-1.2 cm. Pipe batter into hot oil in spiral shapes making 1.5 circles per jalebi. Keep flame low and fry patiently. If jalebi doesn't float, use spatula to release from surface. Fry until deep dark brown color similar to kala jamun, do not let blacken. Flip and fry second side faster until dark. Remove carefully and immediately transfer into warm sugar syrup. Soak in syrup for at least 10 minutes. Remove and place on sieve to drain excess syrup. Serve hot or cold.

name: SK Style Mutton Curry
instructions: Marination: In bowl add mutton with ginger-garlic-green chilli paste and 0.5 tsp turmeric, mix well coating every piece. Optional: place red-hot charcoal in steel bowl inside mutton, pour ghee over it, cover immediately and let smoke infuse for 3-4 minutes. Remove charcoal and set aside. Vaatan paste: Place papad jali directly on high flame. Put onions, dry coconut and green chillies on it, roast until well charred. Transfer to board, cool slightly and peel off burnt onion skin. Add roasted items to mixer jar along with ginger, garlic and large handful fresh coriander stems, add little water and grind to smooth fine paste. This aromatic paste is flavor base. Curry: Heat ghee and oil in heavy-bottomed kadhai on medium-high flame. Add bay leaves, cinnamon, javitri, green cardamom, black cardamom and cumin seeds, let crackle and release aroma. Add 300g chopped onions, cook stirring regularly until deep golden brown. Lower flame, add agri masala and 0.5 tsp turmeric with splash hot water. Stir well and cook until rawness goes away and oil starts separating. Add prepared vaatan and whisked curd, stir continuously and cook until thick, crumbly and glossy with oil separating. Add marinated mutton, mix well coating with masala, cook on medium-high flame 5-7 minutes allowing meat to sear and release moisture. Cover and cook on low flame until mutton becomes soft and tender, stirring occasionally and adding hot water as needed for gravy consistency. Once fully cooked, adjust gravy consistency if needed. Finish with garam masala, crushed kasuri methi, fresh coriander and lemon juice squeeze. Serve hot with rice, rice bhakri and chilled solkadhi.

name: Amritsari Fish Fry
instructions: Cut fish into chunky pieces along natural centre line. Grind garlic cloves into paste and divide - reserve 1 tbsp for marinade, thin remainder to garlic water. Marinate fish with salt, hing and turmeric for 10 minutes. Prepare besan batter by adding besan, corn flour, turmeric, kashmiri chilli, salt and gradually mix with garlic water to make thick yellow batter. Add crushed ajwain and rest briefly. Heat mustard oil until smoking, cool slightly. Coat fish lightly with batter and fry at 170°C for 3-4 minutes until cooked, drain and rest 10-15 minutes. Increase oil temperature to 185°C and flash-fry for 30 seconds until crispy. Prepare chaat masala by dry roasting cumin, coriander, peppercorns, carom seeds, cinnamon and cloves, cool completely and grind with salt, black salt, ginger powder, hing, amchur and yellow chilli powder. For chutney, grind mint, coriander, onion, garlic, ginger, green chilli, tamarind, black salt and salt into coarse paste with ice cube. Serve fish hot with generous sprinkle of chaat masala and chilled green chutney.

name: Aloo Gobi
instructions: Cut cauliflower into bite-sized florets and remove core, peel tender inner stem and cut into pieces. Soak cauliflower in water with salt and keep submerged. Peel potatoes, cut into similar-sized pieces and submerge in water. Using mortar and pestle, pound ginger and green chilli into coarse paste. Heat kadhai over high flame and add mustard oil until it lightly smokes. Lower flame, add cumin seeds, ajwain and hing to crackle. Add drained potato pieces and stir well on medium-high flame for 1 minute, then cover and cook on lowest flame for 3-4 minutes. Uncover, stir potatoes and add ginger-chilli paste with drained cauliflower. Mix well, cover and cook for 2-3 minutes to allow vegetables to steam. Remove lid, add salt and powdered spices (turmeric, coriander, garam masala, cumin powder). Stir gently to coat vegetables, cover again and cook on low flame for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally. Once potatoes and cauliflower are fully cooked and soft, taste and adjust salt. Finish with pinch of dry mango powder and fresh coriander.

name: Mushroom Do Pyaza
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 2 tbsp
instructions: Cut 3 onions into quarters, separate into petals and reserve centre portions. Thinly slice remaining 4 onions plus the reserved centre portions for gravy. Slice tomatoes, finely chop coriander stems. Clean mushrooms gently, pat completely dry, then trim stems and cut large ones into halves. Heat mustard oil in kadhai over high flame until lightly smoking, add whole spices and crackle. Add sliced onions and cook over high flame stirring continuously, adding splashes of hot water while cooking to brown evenly and faster without burning. Once well browned, add ginger-garlic-chilli paste, stir well and cook until raw aroma disappears. Lower flame, add all powdered spices with splash of water, mix well and cook until oil separates. Add sliced tomatoes, whisked curd and salt, stir well and cook over high flame for a couple minutes. Cover and cook over medium-low flame until tomatoes turn completely mushy and oil separates. Slit green chillies and coarsely crush coriander seeds. Add prepared mushrooms to gravy, mix well and cook over high flame for 2 minutes. Cover and cook on low flame until mushrooms are cooked through and release moisture. Check gravy consistency - should be semi-thick. In separate pan set over high flame, add little oil. Add onion petals, slit green chillies, crushed coriander seeds, coriander stems and dried red chilli. Toss over high flame for 2 minutes until onion petals are lightly charred yet crunchy. Add this tempering to mushroom gravy with ginger julienne, garam masala, roasted kasuri methi and fresh coriander. Mix well, taste and adjust salt.

name: Maharashtrian Masale Bhat
instructions: Wash rice 2-3 times until water runs clear, then soak in fresh water. Prepare vegetables - break cauliflower into small florets, wash ivy gourd and cut into quarters, dice potato, rinse peas. Grind cinnamon, black peppercorns and cloves into coarse powder. Crush ginger and green chilli into coarse paste. Heat oil in wide vessel on medium-high flame and let heat up. Add cumin seeds, mustard seeds, hing and bay leaves to crackle. Add all prepared vegetables along with green peas, add ginger-green chilli paste and coarse spice powder. Mix everything well and cook for 1 minute. Add curry leaves, cashews, turmeric, goda masala and salt. Mix well and cook on medium-high to high flame for 2-3 minutes. Cover vessel and cook on low flame for 2-3 minutes so vegetables steam slightly. After 2 minutes, open lid and give quick stir. Add hot water and jaggery, stir well until jaggery dissolves completely. Taste water and adjust salt if required. Increase flame to high and bring everything to proper boil. Tie clean cloth around lid. Drain soaked rice and add to boiling water. Stir gently once and bring back to boil. Cover and cook on low flame for 15 minutes. After 15 minutes, open lid and check bottom using back of spoon. If water remains, cover again and cook a couple more minutes. Once done, switch off flame and let masale bhat rest for 15 minutes before serving. While serving, fluff gently with spatula and add fresh coriander, grated fresh coconut and more ghee if desired.

name: Methi Na Gota
instructions: For pakoda: Sieve besan with baking soda. Add fine rava and gradually whisk in water while whisking continuously to form thick smooth besan batter. Whisk well to incorporate air for light pakode. Cover batter and rest for 15-20 minutes. While batter rests, finely chop methi leaves. Set oil in deep kadhai to heat for frying. After resting, add chopped methi to batter along with fresh coriander, green chilli, ginger, coriander seeds, ajwain, hing, turmeric, pepper, salt, sugar and lemon juice. Mix gently using spatula. Add few tablespoons of hot oil from frying kadhai into batter and mix well - this step helps pakode become crisp outside while staying moist inside. Final batter should be semi-thick, not runny, and scoop easily with fingers. When oil is hot, scoop small portions using fingers and gently push into oil using thumb. Fry on medium flame turning occasionally until evenly golden, crisp and cooked through. Remove with spider and transfer to sieve to drain excess oil. Fry remaining batches similarly. In same hot oil place spider and add slit pakoda chillies - slitting prevents spluttering. Once fried, transfer to bowl and sprinkle with salt. For besan kadhi: Whisk besan with water to form smooth lump-free mixture. Add turmeric, salt and sugar, whisk again until well combined. Heat groundnut oil in saucepan on high flame. Once hot add mustard seeds to crackle. Add hing, curry leaves and green chillies, stir briefly then immediately pour in besan mixture. Stir continuously and bring to gentle simmer. Cook over medium flame for 3-5 minutes stirring often - kadhi will thicken further as it cools so avoid overcooking. Taste and adjust seasoning. Cool slightly then add lemon juice and mix well. Let kadhi cool down completely. Serve hot crispy Methi na Gota immediately with salted fried chillies, besan kadhi and hot chai.

name: Ragi Idli
instructions: Wash and soak ragi, rice, and urad gota for 6-8 hours. Soak poha separately. Grind ragi and rice with ice-cold water to a fine but slightly coarse texture. Grind urad dal with poha to a fine, fluffy batter. Combine batters and whisk vigorously for 2-3 minutes. Ferment overnight (8-15 hours depending on temperature) until batter rises. Gently mix to release air, add salt. Grease idli plates and pour batter 3/4 full. Steam for 12-15 minutes until a toothpick inserted comes out clean. Cool slightly and unmould. Serve hot with coconut chutney and sambar.

name: Spaghetti Aglio e Olio
instructions: Boil water with salt and add spaghetti without breaking. Cook until al dente. Meanwhile, slice garlic thinly and chop parsley. Heat olive oil on high flame, reduce to low, add sliced garlic and cook gently until softened and beginning to change color. Add red chilli flakes and stir. Add 2 ladles of pasta water and stir vigorously to emulsify oil and water into a light sauce. Transfer cooked pasta directly into the sauce, add chopped parsley and salt to taste. Stir vigorously until pasta is nicely coated and sauce is glossy. Add more pasta water if needed to adjust consistency. Serve immediately.

name: Kathiyawadi Kadhi
instructions: Sieve sour curd and besan together to remove lumps. Add turmeric, coriander, red chilli, and cumin powder with salt. Whisk well and add water to form smooth mixture. Crush garlic and green chillies coarsely in mortar and pestle. Heat oil in kadhai on medium flame. Add mustard seeds and cumin seeds, let them crackle. Add hing, fenugreek seeds, bay leaves, cinnamon stick, and curry leaves. Sauté briefly. Stir kadhi mixture and slowly add to kadhai while stirring to prevent curdling. Bring to gentle boil, reduce flame to medium. Add curry leaf sprigs and simmer for 5-7 minutes. Cover and cook for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until rich color and aroma develop. Discard curry leaf sprigs and bay leaves. Adjust thickness and taste. For final tadka: heat ghee, add dried red chillies and Kashmiri red chilli powder, immediately pour over kadhi. Finish with fresh coriander. Serve hot with bajra rotla, rice, or pulao.

name: Chicken Bharta
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 1 tbsp
instructions: Soak cashews in hot water until soft. Slice chicken thin, marinate with ginger-garlic-green chilli paste, salt, turmeric, kashmiri red chilli powder, lemon juice, and mustard oil. Heat tawa on high flame with oil, cook chicken pieces 2 minutes each side until cooked. Transfer to bowl and rest. Chop onions and cook in pan with oil and cumin seeds until golden brown. Add coarsely chopped garlic, ginger, and green chillies, cook 2 minutes. Add turmeric, kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala with water, cook until masala releases oil. Cover and cook on low flame. Drain soaked cashews and grind into smooth paste, add to pan. Shred cooked chicken with hands keeping slightly chunky. Add shredded chicken to pan with fresh cream, butter, coriander stems, lemon juice, kasuri methi powder, and garam masala. Mix well and adjust consistency with water if dry. Adjust salt and optionally add honey for balance. Finish with fresh coriander and sliced green chillies. Serve with tandoori roti or rice.

name: Rassawala Khaman
instructions: Make khaman batter: Mix chickpea flour, semolina, salt, turmeric, hing, yogurt, oil, ginger, and green chilli. Add water gradually to form smooth batter consistency. Let ferment for 3-4 hours until batter rises and becomes airy. Add baking soda just before steaming. Pour into greased molds filling 2/3 full. Steam for 12-15 minutes until set. Meanwhile, for rassa: dry roast coriander, cumin, and chilli powder. Soak tamarind and extract pulp. Heat oil, add mustard seeds and cumin seeds until they crackle. Add curry leaves and asafoetida. Add extracted tamarind pulp, cooked spice powder, chilli powder, salt, and water. Bring to boil and simmer for 5 minutes until flavors blend. Add jaggery and adjust taste. Unmold cooled khaman, soak in hot rassa just before serving. Garnish with fresh coriander and sliced green chillies. Serve hot.

name: Eggless Tres Leches Cake
instructions: Mix all-purpose flour, sugar, baking powder in a bowl. In another bowl, whisk together oil, yogurt, and vanilla extract until smooth. Combine wet and dry ingredients until just blended (do not overmix). Pour batter into microwave-safe cake pan. Microwave on high for 8-10 minutes until a toothpick inserted comes out clean. Cool completely. Meanwhile, combine condensed milk, evaporated milk, and coconut milk to make tres leches mixture. Pierce cooled cake all over with a fork and slowly pour the tres leches mixture over the cake, allowing it to absorb. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours. Whip fresh cream with powdered sugar and vanilla extract until stiff peaks form. Spread whipped cream over chilled cake. Garnish generously with fresh strawberries and mint leaves. Slice and serve chilled.

name: Vegetable Bajra Khichdi
instructions: Soak bajra 6-8 hours or overnight, then discard water. Wash moong dal in sieve. Add soaked bajra to pressure cooker with moong dal, water, salt, turmeric, and 1/2 tbsp ghee. Pressure cook on high for 1 whistle, then medium for 6-7 whistles. Meanwhile prep vegetables: slice onions, chop tomato, crush ginger-garlic-green chilli, dice beans-carrot-potato, keep peas ready. After whistles complete, release pressure naturally. Heat kadhai with 2 tbsp ghee, crackle cumin seeds, red chilli, hing. Add onions until light colour forms. Add curry leaves, potatoes, cover and cook 5 min. Add peas, carrot, beans, tomato, salt, turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, hot water. Cover and cook 4-5 min until vegetables done. Add cooked khichdi mixture, adjust consistency with hot water. Finish with garam masala, kasuri methi, lemon juice, fresh coriander. Serve hot with dahi, pickle and papad.

name: Mutton Misal Pav
instructions: Marinate good quality mutton well. Smoke it briefly with charring to add smoky flavour. Prepare vatan base: brown onions till light colour, add lots of garlic, ginger, green chilli, coriander stems, tomato, charred coconut. Heat ghee in heavy pot with curry leaves and whole spices. Add the vatan paste and agri masala spice mix. Cook marinated mutton in this masala on medium-high flame until meat is soft, juicy and tender. Simmer until fat rises to surface (called turi). Finish with homemade garam masala, squeeze of lime juice, chopped fresh coriander. Serve hot with pav bread.

name: Black Chickpea Soup
ingredients: black chickpeas kale chane dried 1 cup
instructions: Soak black chickpeas overnight or 8-10 hours. Boil soaked chickpeas in water until soft and cooked through, about 30-40 minutes. In a separate pot, heat oil and crackle cumin seeds. Add chopped onion and cook until translucent. Add ginger-garlic paste, green chilli, turmeric powder. Cook for 1-2 minutes. Add cooked chickpeas and their cooking liquid. Bring to boil and simmer for 10 minutes. Taste and adjust salt. Serve hot garnished with fresh coriander.

name: Chana Dal Chikki
instructions: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan on medium flame. Add jaggery and stir constantly until it melts completely, about 5-7 minutes. Add roasted chana dal and mix immediately and continuously to coat all pieces evenly with melted jaggery. Once combined, quickly pour on a greased or parchment-lined surface before it sets. Let cool for 2-3 minutes, then break into pieces with hands or a spoon. Store in airtight container.

name: Aloo Patta Gobhi Matar
instructions: Peel and cut potatoes into medium dice. Chop cabbage into bite-sized pieces. Keep green peas ready. Heat oil in a wok or large pan. Add finely chopped onion and cook until translucent. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilli, cook 1 minute. Add diced potatoes and stir well. Cover and cook on low-medium heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add chopped cabbage, green peas, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, and salt. Mix well and cover. Cook on low flame for 5-8 minutes until all vegetables are tender. Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve hot garnished with fresh coriander.

name: Shirley
instructions: Boil and mash potatoes. Blanch fenugreek and spinach leaves briefly, squeeze excess water, chop fine. Mix mashed potatoes with chopped greens, gram flour, and all dry spices. Finely chop green chilli and mix in with ginger paste. Add salt to taste and combine well. Divide into equal portions and flatten into round tikkis. For chutney: blend green coriander, radish leaves, green chilli, ginger, lemon juice, salt, black salt, roasted cumin powder into smooth paste. Heat oil in deep pan. Deep fry tikkis until golden brown on both sides, about 2-3 minutes. Drain on paper towel. Serve hot with chutney.

name: Matar Pulao
instructions: Rinse basmati rice 2-3 times until water runs clear. Heat ghee in heavy-bottomed pot and add cumin seeds, let crackle. Add sliced onions and cook until golden brown. Add all whole spices and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant. Add rinsed rice and sauté for 2-3 minutes, stirring gently to coat with ghee. Add fresh green peas and salt to taste. Pour water and bring to boil. Cover pot with foil then place lid, reduce heat to low and cook undisturbed for 18-20 minutes. Turn off heat and let rest for 5 minutes without opening lid. Fluff gently with fork and serve hot.

name: Kesariya Rice Kheer
instructions: Soak saffron strands in warm milk. Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot and roast the rinsed rice for 1-2 minutes. Add the saffron-infused milk and remaining milk to the rice. Add bay leaves and cardamom. Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally, for 20-25 minutes until rice is completely soft and creamy. The kheer should have a thick, pudding-like consistency. Add roasted nuts at the end. Sweeten with sugar and serve warm.

name: Matar Paratha
ingredients: 1 cup fresh or frozen green peas
instructions: Make a soft dough with wheat flour, water, and salt. Boil and mash the peas. Mix mashed peas with roasted cumin seeds, chili powder, asafoetida, and salt for the filling. Divide dough into balls, roll out thin, spread ghee, sprinkle filling, roll into a spiral, and flatten. Pan-fry on medium-high heat with ghee until both sides are golden and crispy. Serve hot with yogurt and pickles.

name: Tilkut Ladoo
instructions: Dry roast sesame seeds in a pan on medium heat for 3-4 minutes until fragrant. In a separate pan, heat jaggery with ghee over low heat until it melts and becomes liquid. Once the jaggery is melted, add the roasted sesame seeds and mix well. Add crushed cardamom, black pepper, and chopped dry fruits. Mix thoroughly. Cool slightly until mixture can be handled. Grease hands with ghee and shape into smooth, firm ladoos. Store in an airtight container.

name: Til Ladoo
instructions: Roast the sesame seeds on medium heat for 3-4 minutes until golden and fragrant, stirring constantly. In another pan, heat ghee and add grated jaggery. Stir until jaggery is completely melted and combined with ghee. Add the roasted sesame seeds to the jaggery mixture. Mix in crushed cardamom and black pepper powder. Cool for 2-3 minutes until it can be handled. Grease hands with ghee and shape the mixture into round ladoos. Let them cool completely and store in an airtight container.

name: Moong Dal Ladoo
ingredients: 1/2 cup mixed dry fruits (almonds, cashews, raisins)
instructions: Dry roast moong dal in a pan on medium heat for 8-10 minutes until it turns golden and fragrant. Remove and cool. Separately, roast chopped dry fruits in ghee until golden. Powder the roasted moong dal in a mixer. In the same pan with remaining ghee, add jaggery and heat until melted. Add the moong dal powder and mix well. Add roasted dry fruits, cardamom, nutmeg, and black pepper. Mix thoroughly and cool slightly. Grease hands and shape into smooth ladoos. These can be stored in an airtight container for several months.

name: Matar Karanji
ingredients: 1.5 cups fresh or frozen green peas
2 tablespoons ghee or oil (for tempering only)
instructions: Make a soft dough with flour, salt, and water. Boil green peas and mash lightly. Heat a small amount of ghee, add cumin seeds, then add mashed peas. Mix in chopped green chili, chili powder, asafoetida, and salt. Let filling cool. Divide dough into balls, roll out thin ovals, place filling on one side, fold and seal edges. Steam in a steamer basket with water for 12-15 minutes until cooked through. For added flavor, temper briefly in hot oil after steaming if desired.

name: Veg Spring Rolls
ingredients: 2 cups mixed vegetables (cabbage, carrots, bell peppers)
1 tablespoon cornstarch mixed with 2 tablespoons water for sealing
instructions: Make a thin dough with flour, water, and salt. Let it rest. Prepare wrapper sheets by ladling thin layer of dough onto a greased pan and cooking gently without browning. For filling, stir-fry mixed vegetables with soy sauce, ginger-garlic paste, sesame oil, and green onions until cooked but still crisp. Cool filling completely. Place filling on wrapper sheet, fold edges inward, roll tightly, and seal with cornstarch paste. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy on all sides. Drain on paper towels and serve hot with sweet and sour sauce.

name: Neer Dosa
instructions: Rinse sama rice and grind with water to a smooth, thin batter (no fermentation needed). Add salt to taste. Heat a non-stick pan or cast-iron skillet and lightly grease with ghee. Pour thin ladle of batter and spread into a thin circular crepe. Cook on high heat until set and lightly cooked on the bottom (no browning). For the chutney, grind fresh coconut with green chilies, cumin seeds, and rock salt. Serve warm neer dosa with the coconut chutney on the side.

name: Til Gud Ladoo
instructions: Dry roast sesame seeds on medium heat until golden. In a pan, add jaggery and ghee, heat until melted. Add roasted sesame seeds to the melted jaggery mixture. Stir well to combine. Pour onto a greased plate to cool slightly. Roll into balls while still warm. Store in an airtight container.

name: Matar Ki Vadi
instructions: Cook fresh green peas until tender. In a pan, heat oil and add onion, ginger, green chili. Stir fry until onion turns translucent. Add cooked peas, salt, turmeric and chaat masala. Mix well to combine. Spread the mixture on a cloth or tray to sun-dry for 2-3 days until completely dry and crispy. Store in an airtight container for year-round use in curries or to make instant aloo matar sabzi.

name: Moong Dal Veggie Idli
instructions: Soak moong dal for 30 minutes and drain well. Grind with minimal water to make a coarse batter. Heat oil in a pan, add ginger, green chili and onion. Fry for 2 minutes. Add carrot and peas, cook for 3 minutes. Mix this vegetable mixture with moong dal batter. Add salt, turmeric and baking soda just before cooking. Pour into greased idli molds. Steam in an idli maker for 8-10 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean. Remove and serve hot with sambar and chutney.

name: Baingan Tawa Masala
ingredients: brinjal (eggplant) 3 medium, cut into round slices
instructions: Heat oil in a tawa/griddle on low-to-medium heat. Add onion and fry until soft. Add ginger, garlic and green chili, cook for 1 minute. Add tomato and all spice powders. Cook until tomato softens. Add brinjal slices and salt. Mix well. Cover and cook on low heat for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until brinjal is fully cooked and masala coats it evenly. The dish should be dry and spiced. Serve hot or at room temperature.

name: Rajma Chawal
instructions: Soak kidney beans for 4 hours. Boil until tender. In a pot, heat oil and add cumin seeds. Add minced ginger and garlic, sauté until fragrant. Add chopped onions and cook until golden. Add diced tomatoes and cook until soft. Add cooked kidney beans with turmeric, coriander, and chili powder. Simmer for 5-10 minutes. Cook rice separately and serve fluffy rice with the rajma curry.

name: Jackfruit Seed Sabzi
instructions: Boil jackfruit seeds until tender and remove outer layer. Heat coconut oil and temper with mustard seeds and curry leaves. Add diced onions, minced ginger and garlic, sauté until golden. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft. Stir in boiled jackfruit seeds along with turmeric, chili and coriander powders. Add freshly grated coconut and simmer for 8-10 minutes. Season with salt to taste.

name: Laksa
instructions: Blend onions, garlic, ginger, red chillies, turmeric and lemongrass into a paste. Heat oil and fry the paste until fragrant. Add shrimp paste and cook briefly. Pour in coconut milk and fish stock, add tamarind paste and palm sugar. Simmer for 15-20 minutes to develop flavors. Cook rice noodles separately. Place noodles in a bowl, pour hot laksa broth over them. Top with fried tofu, bean sprouts, Thai basil leaves, and serve with fresh lime.

name: Veg Dum Biryani
ingredients: whole spices for rice: bay leaf, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves
instructions: Soak basmati rice in water for 30 minutes. Cube potatoes and carrots. Slice onions into thin rings. Mince garlic and ginger, chop green chillies. In a heavy-bottomed pot, heat oil and ghee. Add cumin seeds, bay leaves, cardamom, cinnamon, and cloves. Add sliced onions and fry until golden brown. Add ginger-garlic mixture and green chillies, cook 2 minutes. Add cubed potatoes and carrot, fry for 2-3 minutes. Add green peas and mix well. Add yogurt and all powdered spices: kashmiri red chilli, turmeric, coriander, garam masala, and salt. Cook on medium flame for 10 minutes until vegetables are partially cooked and gravy thickens. In separate pot, boil water with salt and whole spices. Add soaked rice to boiling water and cook until 70% done (rice should have bite). Drain rice. Layer rice over vegetable gravy. Sprinkle fresh coriander and mint on top. Cover pot with aluminium foil, then place heavy lid on top. Cook on high flame for 1-2 minutes until steam forms, then reduce heat to low and cook for 40-50 minutes for dum cooking (steam-cooking). Turn off flame and let rest 5 minutes without opening lid. Gently mix with fork, transfer to serving dish and serve hot.

name: Shahi Matar Paneer
instructions: Heat oil in a kadhai, add all whole spices and let them crackle. Add sliced onions and sauté until translucent. Add tomatoes, garlic, ginger, green chillies, coriander stems, salt, red chillies, and cashews. Mix well and cook on high flame for 3-4 minutes. Add hot water, cover and cook until tomatoes are completely mushy. Cool and blend into smooth puree. Cut paneer into slabs and shallow fry until lightly golden, then cut into cubes and soak in warm salted water. Heat oil in kadhai, make a spice paste with turmeric, red chilli powder and coriander powder in water, cook briefly. Add prepared gravy, season with salt and sugar, cook for 4-5 minutes until oil separates. Add green peas, cover and cook on low flame until soft. Add soaked paneer cubes gently, then add butter, fresh cream, garam masala, kasuri methi powder and fresh coriander. Mix well and simmer briefly.

name: Veg Burrito Bowl
instructions: Make paneer: heat butter and oil, cook paneer pieces with chilli flakes and oregano until golden. Make rajma: in fresh oil, cook garlic, onion, tomato together, add cooked rajma with schezwan chutney and simmer. Make rice: cook rice with butter, oil, coriander leaves and lemon juice. Make salad: combine chopped onion, capsicum, corn, mixed herbs, chilli flakes. Make dressing: blend greek yogurt, garlic, black pepper, coriander leaves and salt. Assemble bowl: layer cooked rice as base, add rajma curry, paneer pieces, fresh salad. Drizzle yogurt dressing on top. Garnish with sliced avocado and cherry tomato.

name: Bombay Sandwich
instructions: Make green chutney: blend coriander leaves, green chili, ginger, garlic, lemon juice with chana dal, cumin seeds and turmeric until smooth. Prepare potato filling: boil and peel potatoes, mash and mix with ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, salt, black pepper, chat masala and lemon juice. Assemble sandwich: spread green chutney on bread slice, add potato filling layer, top with sliced onion, tomato, cucumber, capsicum and beetroot. Place second bread slice on top, apply butter on outside. Toast on griddle over medium heat until golden brown. Top with nylon sev and serve immediately with extra green chutney.

name: Bathua Dal
instructions: Wash and soak mixed dal. Heat ghee and oil in a pot. Temper with cumin seeds, bay leaves, cinnamon stick and black cardamom. Add finely chopped onion and cook until golden brown. Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute. Add chopped tomato and cook until completely soft. Pour in the soaked dal and clean bathua saag (chopped). Add enough water and bring to boil. Simmer until dal is completely cooked and soft (about 20-25 minutes). Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, black pepper powder and garam masala. Mix well and let simmer for another 2-3 minutes. Adjust salt and consistency. Serve hot with roti or rice.

name: Healthy Vegetable Soup
instructions: Chop all vegetables into small uniform pieces. Heat butter in a pot over medium heat. Add finely minced garlic and ginger, sauté for 30 seconds. Add finely chopped onion and cook for 1-2 minutes. Add baby corn, mushroom, broccoli, carrot, cabbage, french beans and both peppers. Stir well and cook for 2-3 minutes. Pour enough water (approximately 3-4 cups) and bring to a boil. Let vegetables cook for 5-7 minutes until tender. Make a slurry with wheat flour and water (without cornflour as per the recipe), add this to the soup while stirring to thicken lightly. Simmer for 2-3 minutes. Season with salt to taste. Serve hot as a light appetizer or starter course.

name: Aloo Matar Sabzi
instructions: Heat cooking oil in a heavy-bottomed pan. Add cumin seeds and bay leaves, let them crackle and release aroma. Add finely chopped onion and cook until golden brown. Add minced ginger, garlic and slit green chillies, cook for 1 minute. Add chopped tomato and cook until completely soft and oil starts to separate. Add diced potato (cut into uniform medium pieces) and cook for 3-4 minutes. Add fresh green peas and mix well. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, black pepper, hing and garam masala. Mix thoroughly. Add 1/2 cup water, cover and cook on medium heat until potatoes are completely tender (about 10-12 minutes). Stir occasionally. Continue cooking until oil separates on top and potatoes are cooked through. Adjust salt and spices. Serve hot with roti, rice or bread.

name: Paneer Biryani
instructions: Soak rice in water for 15-20 minutes and drain. Cut paneer into medium cubes. Heat oil and ghee together in a pressure cooker base. Add bay leaf, cinnamon, black cardamom, cardamom, clove and cumin seed. Let the whole spices crackle and release their aroma. Add thinly sliced onion in batches and cook until golden brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Add chopped tomato and cook until soft. Add paneer pieces and peas, stir well. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder and biryani masala. Mix thoroughly so all ingredients are well coated with spices. Add the soaked rice and stir to combine evenly. Pour 1.75 glass of water and add salt to taste. Close the pressure cooker lid and cook on high heat for 2 whistles (about 4-5 minutes from when steam starts). Remove from heat and allow pressure to release naturally (do not open immediately). Once pressure releases, open lid gently. Fluff rice with a fork without breaking grains. Garnish with chopped coriander leaves and mint leaves. Serve hot.

name: Ragda Aloo Tikki Chaat
instructions: For ragda: boil green peas with garlic, green chilli, cumin seeds, and turmeric until soft, mash slightly. For aloo tikki: boil potatoes, mash, mix with coriander powder, cumin powder, black pepper, chilli powder, and salt, shape into patties, shallow fry until golden. To assemble: place aloo tikki on plate, top with ragda, garnish with chopped onion, tomato, green chilli, and coriander. Add green chutney, tamarind chutney, sprinkle chaat masala, roasted cumin powder, and sev. Serve hot.

name: Matar Poori
instructions: For poori: blend fresh peas, spinach, coriander leaves, green chilli, garlic, and ginger into paste. Mix wheat flour and semolina, add pea mixture, black pepper, salt, and knead into soft dough. Roll into small circles and deep fry in hot oil until puffed and golden. For sabzi: heat oil, add chopped ginger, tomato, green chilli, cook until soft, add boiled potatoes, turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, black pepper, salt and cook until combined. Serve poori hot with sabzi.

name: Gobhi Paratha
instructions: Finely chop cauliflower and dry it well to remove excess moisture. Mix with minced ginger, garlic, green chilli, coriander leaves, black pepper powder, red chilli powder, amchur powder, and salt to make filling. Knead wheat flour with salt, oil, and water to make soft dough. Divide into balls, roll thin, fill with cauliflower mixture, seal edges, and roll again gently to avoid tearing. Cook on hot tawa with ghee on both sides until golden brown. The key is dry filling to prevent water from tearing the paratha.

name: Tandoori Vegetable Platter
instructions: For paneer: mix 3 tbsp curd with ginger garlic paste, tandoori masala, turmeric, kashmiri red chilli powder, black pepper, mustard oil, besan, and salt. Marinate paneer cubes for 30 minutes. For vegetables: boil potatoes till half done, cut vegetables into chunks. Mix 1/2 cup curd with besan, tandoori masala, red chilli powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, ginger garlic paste, kasuri methi, mustard oil, and salt. Marinate vegetables and baby potatoes for 30 minutes. Thread marinated paneer and vegetables onto skewers or arrange on baking sheet. Grill or bake at high heat until charred and cooked through. Serve hot.

name: Amla Murabba
instructions: Select fresh amlas. Traditional method involves pricking amlas and soaking them to remove bitterness. Combine amlas with sugar and water in proportions that allow the fruit to absorb sugar gradually over time. Cook on low heat, stirring occasionally, until amlas become soft and juicy like gulab jamun texture. The key is slow cooking and proper sugar balance. Once cooled, store in sterilized jars. This murabba lasts for months without spoiling and can be consumed with bread or as a condiment.

name: Methi Peanut Sabzi
instructions: Wash and chop methi leaves finely. Roughly chop peanuts. Heat 4 tbsp oil in a pan, add minced garlic and cook until fragrant. Add chopped onion and green chilli, saute until soft. Add methi leaves and cook on high heat to evaporate excess moisture. Add chopped peanuts, turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well and cook on medium heat for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until methi is cooked and peanuts are soft. The sabzi should be dry yet flavorful. Serve hot with roti or bread.

name: Matar Muthiya
instructions: Mix fresh matar, coriander leaves, garlic, green chilli, and ginger. Add jowar flour, bajra flour, besan, turmeric, red chilli powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, and black pepper. Mix with curd and salt to form a dough. Steam in a pressure cooker or muthiya press. Temper with cooking oil, mustard seeds, cumin seeds, asafoetida, and curry leaves. Finish with fresh coriander and lemon juice.

name: Spicy Matar Sandwich
instructions: Prepare green chutney by grinding coriander leaves, garlic, green chilli, roasted chana dal, lemon juice, and salt. Make filling with mashed green peas, green chilli, and garlic cloves. Spread green chutney on bread, layer with pea stuffing, add cheese slice, and butter. Grill on a pan or sandwich maker until golden and cheese melts.

name: Egg Pulao
instructions: 1. Boil and peel eggs before starting recipe. 2. Wash and soak basmati rice until water runs clear. Soak while prepping other ingredients. 3. Using string chopper, finely chop onion, garlic, ginger and green chillies together. Transfer to bowl. 4. In same chopper, roughly chop mint and coriander. Keep aside. 5. Cut tomato into large dices for faster cooking. 6. Heat heavy-bottomed flat vessel or kadhai. Once hot, add oil and let it get hot. 7. Add cumin seeds and let them crackle. 8. Add chopped onion mixture and sauté over medium flame until golden brown. 9. Add tomatoes, whisked yogurt, turmeric, chilli powder, biryani masala and salt. Mix well and cook until oil separates. Add splash of hot water if required to continue cooking. 10. Add chopped coriander and mint, stir and cook for another minute. 11. Score boiled eggs with knife so they absorb flavor. Add eggs to masala and stir gently to coat well. 12. Using same cup measure used for rice, pour 2 cups hot water (for rice soaked 30 minutes). Drain soaked rice and add to masala. 13. Stir gently, taste and adjust salt if needed. 14. Once pulao comes to gentle simmer, sprinkle more coriander and mint on top. 15. Cover vessel with lid wrapped in clean kitchen towel to trap steam. 16. Cook on lowest flame 12-15 minutes until rice is cooked and no water remains. 17. If slightly undercooked, add splash of hot water, cover again and cook a few more minutes. 18. Switch off flame and let pulao rest 10-15 minutes - this step is very important. 19. Gently fluff rice with spatula before serving. Serve hot with raita and onion salad.

name: Mixed Vegetable Cutlet
instructions: Mash boiled potatoes and mix with grated paneer. Finely chop onion, capsicum, carrot, green chilli, and coriander leaves. Add to potato mixture with lemon juice, black pepper, chilli flakes, mixed herbs, and salt. Mix well and form into oval cutlet shapes. Coat each cutlet with corn flour and maida paste, then dip in bread crumbs. Heat oil in a pan and deep fry cutlets until golden brown on all sides. Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

name: Hara Chana Masala
instructions: Heat cooking oil in pressure cooker. Add cumin seeds, cardamom, bay leaf, and cinnamon stick. Add ginger and garlic paste (or minced). Sauté until fragrant. Add chopped onion and cook until translucent. Add tomato pieces and cook until soft. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala, and black pepper. Mix well. Add fresh green chana and salt. Add 1 cup water. Close lid and cook on high pressure for 2-3 whistles or until chana is tender. Release pressure and open lid. Adjust seasoning and garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Broccoli Cheese Paratha
instructions: Prepare paratha dough by mixing wheat flour, salt, and water. Knead until smooth and soft. Let rest 15 minutes. Finely chop broccoli and cook lightly with onion, green chilli, coriander leaves, chilli flakes, and mixed herbs. Mix cooked broccoli with grated cheese and salt. Divide dough into 4 portions. Roll each portion, fill with broccoli-cheese mixture, seal edges, and gently roll into a paratha. Heat a griddle or tawa, place paratha, and cook on both sides until golden brown, brushing with ghee or oil. Serve hot with yogurt or pickle.

name: No Oil Punjabi Rajma
instructions: Soak rajma overnight. Grind onion, ginger, garlic, and green chilli into paste. Roast bay leaf, black cardamom, and cumin seeds. Roast onion-ginger-garlic paste until raw flavor is gone. Add turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, and coriander powder. Puree tomatoes and add, roast well. Add drained rajma and roast for 5-7 minutes on high flame. Add water and bring to boil. Pressure cook with 1 whistle on high, then 2-3 whistles on low. Toward the end, add kasuri methi. Cook for 5 more minutes to thicken gravy. Add garam masala and fresh coriander. Serve hot.

name: Aloo Methi Paratha
instructions: Knead wheat flour with salt, chopped methi leaves, and water to make soft dough. Let rest for 15 minutes. Boil potatoes and mash them. Roast coriander seeds and cumin, then crush. Mix mashed potatoes with roasted spices, green chilli, ginger, chilli powder, chaat masala, coriander powder, salt, and fresh coriander. Divide dough into 4 equal portions. Roll out each portion, place potato filling in center, fold and seal edges. Gently roll into a paratha (flatbread). Heat griddle with oil and cook each paratha on both sides until golden brown and crispy.

name: Mexican Paneer Burrito
instructions: Make Mexican seasoning by dry roasting and grinding cumin, coriander seeds, cinnamon, cloves, and oregano. Cube paneer and marinate with olive oil, mexican seasoning, garlic, lemon juice, and salt. Cook rice with butter and onion, then add coriander and lemon juice. Prepare salsa by mixing tomato, onion, coriander, corn, green chilli, lemon juice, and salt. Make burrito sauce by mixing garlic, hung curd, chilli sauce, salt, lemon juice, and mexican seasoning. Cook refried beans by sauteing onion, garlic, green chilli in oil, then adding boiled rajma and mashing. Fry marinated paneer until golden. Assemble burrito: lay tortilla, add rice in center, top with paneer, refried beans, lettuce, avocado, cheese, salsa. Drizzle burrito sauce, wrap tightly, pan-fry until crispy. Serve with additional salsa and sauce.

name: Masoor Dal Tikki
instructions: Soak masoor dal for 1.5 hours, then wash well and drain. Grind dal with cumin, green chilli, garlic, onion, and ginger keeping the mixture coarse. Add chilli powder, coriander powder, chaat masala, turmeric, kasuri methi powder, fresh coriander, salt, and grated carrot. Mix well without adding water. Shape into round patties. For dip: heat oil, fry dry red chillies until they darken and puff, set aside. In remaining oil, add chopped garlic and cook until golden brown. Pour oil and garlic into hung curd. Crush fried chillies and add to curd with salt, black salt, roasted cumin powder, green chilli, and coriander. Mix well. Shallow fry or air fry tikkis on medium heat until golden and cooked through. Serve with chilli garlic dip.

name: Rava Vada
instructions: For vada: Heat 3 tbsp oil, add asafoetida, mustard seeds, cumin, black pepper, and curry leaves. Stir until mustard seeds crackle. Add ginger, green chilli, chilli flakes, and stir. Add water and salt, bring to boil. Add semolina and fresh coriander, stir constantly. Cook until thick (semolina absorbs water). Cool completely. Shape into vadas by rolling into balls and pressing a hole in the center with thumb. For chutney: Heat 3 tbsp oil, add asafoetida and raw peanuts, roast on low flame. Add onion, ginger, garlic, and curry leaves, roast for 1 minute. Add tomato pieces, salt, and kashmiri chilli powder. Roast until tomato becomes soft and yellowish. Cool completely and grind to smooth paste. Temper with 1 tbsp oil, mustard seeds, and urad dal. Deep fry vadas in hot oil until golden brown on all sides. Serve hot with tomato peanut chutney.

name: Masoor Dal Pulao
instructions: Soak masoor dal for 1-1.5 hours (overnight is best). Soak basmati rice for 45 minutes. Roughly cut onion, tomato, garlic, and ginger. Heat oil in pressure cooker, add bay leaves, peppercorns, cloves, and cumin seeds. Saute until fragrant. Add cut onion, garlic, ginger, and saute. Add green chillies and saute for 2 minutes. Add tomato pieces and saute. Add turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder, and kitchen king masala. Stir and add drained dal. Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring gently with light hands to keep dal whole. Add drained rice and mix gently. Add water (approximately double the volume of rice). Bring to boil on high flame while stirring once with light hands. Close lid and cook on low flame until water is absorbed and rice is fluffy. Turn off heat and let rest for 10 minutes without opening lid. Fluff with fork and serve.

name: Coconut Mawa Modak
instructions: Grate fresh coconut finely. Grind grated coconut to a fine paste. Roast the ground coconut in a dry pan on low flame until it becomes slightly dry and fragrant. Cool completely. In a mixing bowl, combine roasted coconut with mawa (khoya), sugar, and cardamom powder. Mix well until evenly combined. Fold in chopped pistachios and chopped almonds. Divide the mixture into 12 equal portions (approximately 35g each). Take each portion and mould into a modak shape (traditional teardrop shape with a fluted top) using hands or a modak mould. If using hands, shape into a ball first, then create the characteristic ridges. Arrange on a serving plate. Serve as a sweet offering or dessert.

name: Lauki Ki Vadi
instructions: Grate bottle gourd finely. Mix with salt and let sit for 10 minutes to draw out water. Squeeze out excess water from the gourd. Dry roast cumin seeds and coriander seeds separately, then pound coarsely. In a mixing bowl, combine grated gourd with asafoetida, pounded cumin, pounded coriander, chopped ginger, green chilli, turmeric, chilli powder, garam masala, roasted sesame, fresh coriander, and kasuri methi. Add lemon juice. In another bowl, mix rice flour with gram flour (besan). Add this flour mixture to the gourd mixture and combine well. Add water only if needed to bring mixture together (it should be moist but not wet). Lightly oil a 7x7x2 inch square mould. Transfer mixture to mould and press firmly. Steam in a steamer for 20-25 minutes until set. Demould and cut into square pieces. Heat oil in a pan and shallow fry each piece until golden brown on both sides. Serve hot with mint chutney or tamarind chutney.

name: Dal Sultani
instructions: 1. Boil toor dal with water, turmeric, chilli powder and salt until soft (approximately 30-40 minutes). 2. In a separate vessel, toast black cardamom, cardamom, and cloves for the dal masala. 3. Mix cooked dal with curd and cream. 4. Heat ghee and temper with cumin and sliced garlic. 5. Add chopped onions and green chillies, sauté until golden. 6. Add the dal mixture to the tempering along with milk. 7. Garnish with rogan and coriander. 8. Serve hot.

name: Soya Keema Paratha
ingredients: saffola gold oil - 3 tbsp plus as required
instructions: 1. Boil and drain soya chunks, then mash/blend them. 2. Heat oil and temper with cumin and ginger. 3. Add chopped onions and green chillies, cook until soft. 4. Add soya chunks with spices (turmeric, red chilli, coriander, chaat masala, pao bhaji masala). 5. Mix in kasoori methi and coriander, set filling aside. 6. Prepare dough with wheat flour, salt, ghee, and water. 7. Roll dough, place soya filling in center, seal and roll into paratha. 8. Cook on hot tava with oil on both sides until golden and crispy.

name: Punjabi Tari Wale Chole
ingredients: kasoori methi whole - a generous pinch
instructions: 1. Make a puree of onions, ginger, garlic, green chillies, and tomatoes. 2. Heat oil and add black cardamom and cumin seeds. 3. Add the puree and cook until oil separates (about 5-7 minutes). 4. Add turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, coriander powder, chole masala, and amchur. 5. Add boiled chickpeas and stir well. 6. Add salt, black salt, kasoori methi, and optional baking soda. 7. Simmer for 10-15 minutes until flavors blend. 8. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve.

name: Moong Dal Shahi Tadka
instructions: 1. Soak moong dal and boil with water, turmeric, salt, ginger, and green chillies until very soft. 2. Heat ghee and add asafoetida, then add whole spices (bay leaf, black cardamom, cloves, peppercorn) and red chillies. 3. Add sliced/chopped onions and cook until golden. 4. Stir in kashmiri chilli powder. 5. Pour boiled moong dal into the tadka, mix well. 6. Add curd and stir gently. 7. Simmer for 5-10 minutes. 8. Finish with butter, cream, kasoori methi, and fresh coriander garnish.

name: Vrat ke Dahi Vade
ingredients: oil - 2.5 tbsp for frying plus oil for tempering
instructions: 1. Wash samak rice thoroughly. 2. Heat oil, add peppercorn and cumin, then add green chilli and ginger. 3. Pour water and bring to boil, add rock salt and samak rice. 4. Cook for 5 minutes until rice is soft but not mushy. 5. Spread rice on plate to cool completely. 6. Grate boiled potatoes and mix with cooled rice and coriander. 7. Shape mixture into small vada balls, ensuring no cracks. 8. Heat oil and deep-fry vadas on medium-high heat until golden. 9. Cool vadas completely. 10. Prepare sweet curd by mixing curd with sugar and rock salt. 11. For chutney: combine water, pomegranate powder, amchur, ginger, roasted cumin, black pepper, rock salt, and jaggery; boil until thickened. 12. Soak fried vadas briefly in dilute curd/rock salt mixture to soften. 13. Assemble: place vadas on plate, top with sweet curd, chutney, and garnish with pomegranate seeds and ginger.

name: Pumpkin Basil Soup
instructions: Cut pumpkin into chunks and carrots into rough pieces. Crush garlic. Heat butter with oil, add bay leaves, peppercorns, and fennel seeds. Add crushed garlic and diced onion, cook on high heat 2-3 minutes. Add potatoes, then pumpkin and carrots. Season with turmeric, nutmeg, salt, and add basil leaves. Sauté 3-4 minutes. Add water and close pressure cooker. Cook on high heat until first whistle, then low heat for 2 more whistles until vegetables are soft. Cool, remove bay leaves, and blend until smooth. Sieve if desired for extra smoothness. Heat soup, add grated cheese if using. Serve hot with bread croutons as garnish.

name: Heeng Dal Tadka
instructions: Boil soaked chana dal with salt and turmeric in pressure cooker (2-4 whistles). Heat oil in a pan, add asafoetida, dry red chilli, mustard seeds, then cumin seeds. Add chopped green chilli and ginger, fry lightly. Add curry leaves and roast. Mix turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, and coriander powder with water into a paste, add to the tempering. Roast the spice paste on medium flame until oil releases. Add boiled dal and water as needed, simmer until thick. Finish with lemon juice and fresh coriander. Adjust salt to taste.

name: Minestrone Soup
instructions: Blanch tomatoes in boiling water for 10-12 seconds, ice-shock, peel and deseed. Dice tomatoes. Heat olive oil, add bay leaves. Add diced onion, celery, and carrots (mirepoix base), sauté without browning. Add chopped garlic, fresh thyme, and oregano. Cook 2-3 minutes on high heat. Add tomato puree and salt, roast for 2-3 minutes until raw taste disappears. Add diced potatoes and beans, sauté 2-3 minutes. Add blanched tomatoes, zucchini, pumpkin, chilli flakes, and pepper. Mix and add water. Pressure cook on high for one whistle. Turn off heat and let rest 2 minutes. Open and add macaroni pasta, boiled beans, peas, spinach, and basil. Simmer on low until pasta is cooked. Make garlic butter (chopped garlic mixed with softened butter). Toast french bread slices in a pan with garlic butter on low heat until crispy. Serve hot soup with garlic bread and grated parmesan cheese.

name: Lemon Coriander Noodles
instructions: Boil water in a pot. Add instant noodles and cook according to packet instructions. Drain noodles in a colander. Heat oil in a wok or pan, add chopped garlic and green chilli, stir-fry for 30 seconds. Add the cooked noodles and tastemaker masala packets, toss well. Sprinkle salt and turmeric powder. Mix continuously for 1-2 minutes on high heat. Add fresh coriander and mix. Finish with lemon juice (1.5 lemons) and toss. Garnish with chopped spring onion. Serve hot.

name: High-Protein Veg Dip
ingredients: olive oil for roasting and tempering
instructions: Soak chickpeas and rajma overnight. Boil in pressure cooker with salt, baking soda, and water until very soft (2-3 whistles). Roast vegetables (onion, tomato, garlic, cauliflower, carrot, eggplant) in olive oil until caramelized. Combine boiled chickpeas and rajma with roasted vegetables and select water (reserve liquid). Grind with lemon juice, tahini, salt, pepper, garlic cloves, fresh coriander, roasted cumin, and green chilli until smooth, creamy consistency. Spread on a plate. For tempering, heat olive oil with cumin seeds and chopped garlic until garlic is light golden, add chilli powder after turning off heat. Pour tempering over dip and garnish with fresh coriander and roasted cumin powder.

name: Gajar Ki Barfi
instructions: Peel and grate 1 kg carrots. Heat 2 tbsp ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan, add grated carrots and cook on high heat, stirring frequently. As carrots release moisture and ghee is absorbed, continue cooking. Add 1 cup milk gradually and keep stirring. Add sugar and mix well. Continue cooking until the mixture becomes thick and leaves the sides of the pan (15-20 minutes total). Dry roast nuts separately (cashew, almond, pistachio, walnut). Add cardamom powder and roasted nuts to the carrot mixture, stir well. Transfer to parchment-lined mould while still warm. Press down firmly. Cool to room temperature. Do not refrigerate. Cut into square pieces and serve.

name: Moong Dal Idli
instructions: Soak moong dal overnight and drain. Grind dal with curd, ginger, green chilli, curry leaves, and salt into a fine thick paste, adding water gradually (should be coarse-textured). Heat oil for tempering, add asafoetida and mustard seeds, let crackle, add turmeric. Mix tempering with batter. Add grated carrots and coriander. Just before steaming, add baking soda and stir gently. Grease idli moulds with oil, fill 3/4 way full. Steam for 12-15 minutes. For chutney: Remove tomato stem, halve tomatoes. Heat oil on high, place tomato halves cut-side down for 2-3 minutes without stirring. In same oil, add asafoetida, mustard, cumin, garlic, curry leaves, green chilli. Stir constantly. Flip tomatoes and cook other side until colored. Turn off heat, add kashmiri chilli powder, let cool. Grind cooled tomatoes with all tempering spices, salt, and black salt. Mix in chopped onion, coriander, and lemon juice. Serve soft idli hot with chutney.

name: Onam Special Kerala Sambhar
instructions: Soak and boil toor dal with green chillies and turmeric. Roast chana dal, urad dal, coriander seeds, cumin, black pepper, methi seeds, and dry red chilli to make sambhar masala; grind to powder. Add asafoetida to masala. Chop all vegetables into small pieces. Add vegetables and sambhar masala to boiling dal with tamarind water. Cook until vegetables are tender. Add tomatoes and okra towards the end. Temper with coconut oil, mustard seeds, methi seeds, dry red chilli, and curry leaves. Mix in fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Coconut Chocolate Ice Cream
instructions: Extract and blend fresh coconut meat with coconut water and water. Boil the coconut milk, add sugar and cornstarch, mix well. Add vanilla essence. Pour mixture into molds and freeze until solid (4+ hours). Melt dark chocolate with coconut oil. Remove frozen ice cream from molds. Dip into melted chocolate coating. Freeze again until coating sets. Serve chilled.

name: Soupy Veg Noodles
instructions: Chop vegetables finely. Melt butter and sauté garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Add onion and hard vegetables (carrot, mushrooms) and stir fry on high heat. Add tastemaker masala, turmeric, and chilli sauce. Add noodle powder (crushed from noodle cakes) to thicken the soup. Pour water and bring to boil. Add remaining vegetables (broccoli, tomato) when soup reaches boil. Add soya sauce and adjust salt. Boil instant noodles separately in water and drain. Top noodles with soup and garnish with spring onions.

name: Corn Pakoras
instructions: Grind corn coarsely and keep some whole corn aside. Heat ghee and temper with asafoetida, mustard seeds, cumin seeds, and curry leaves. Add ginger and green chilli, sauté briefly.
