Add corn, turmeric, and chilli powder. Cook well and add milk to make creamy khees. Cool completely. Prepare batter: mix besan, salt, chilli powder, turmeric, kasoori methi, coriander, and water to make thick dough-like batter. Mix cooled corn mixture with chopped coriander. Dip corn mixture in batter and deep fry in hot oil until golden. Serve hot with cheese dip.

name: Gur Makhana Laddu
instructions: Dry roast makhana on low heat until powdery, cool and grind to powder. Roast cashews, almonds, fennel seeds, green cardamom, black cardamom seeds, and sesame seeds lightly, cool and grind coarsely. Mix makhana powder, dry fruit powder, desiccated coconut, and raisins. Melt jaggery with water to make thin syrup. Heat ghee and add makhana-dry fruit mixture with dry ginger powder. Mix gently. Remove jaggery syrup from heat, skim foam, and add to mixture. Cook for 30 seconds only. Cool completely on a plate. Shape into laddus with clean hands when cool. Store in airtight container.

name: Tamarind Chutney
instructions: Boil water with tamarind, sugar, salt, black salt, kashmiri chilli powder, and roasted cumin powder until tamarind breaks down. Strain well, mashing pulp to extract thickness. For tempering: heat oil and add dry red chilli, coriander seeds, cumin seeds, cinnamon, black cardamom, fennel seeds, peppercorn, and cloves. Roast lightly until spices change color and release aroma. Pour tempering into strained chutney and mix well. Cool and store in airtight container. The spice tempering enhances flavor significantly.

name: Dry Fruits Barfi
instructions: Blanch almonds and cashews in boiling water, peel skins, wash and dry. Reduce 750ml milk by half through slow cooking. Grind dry fruits fine with 250ml milk to make smooth puree. Add sugar to reduced milk and stir until dissolved. Add dry fruit puree slowly, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Cook on medium heat, stirring continuously until mixture thickens and leaves the pan. Add roasted sesame seeds and desiccated coconut. Continue cooking until mixture gathers like dough and rolls back from the pan. Pour into butter paper lined mold while hot. Spread evenly with light hands. Cool completely (about 1 hour). Garnish with black cardamom seeds, pistachios, and rose petals. Refrigerate 30 minutes before cutting into pieces.

name: Millet Cutlet
instructions: Heat oil and temper with asafoetida, cumin seeds, black pepper, and curry leaves for 10-15 seconds. Add green chilli and ginger, sauté briefly. Add water and salt (taste to ensure properly salted). Bring to boil and add roasted peanuts, cashews, and all vegetables (carrots, beans, corn, peas). Add turmeric and chilli flakes. Pour in samak rice, stirring to prevent lumps. Cook until rice is tender and water is absorbed, becoming thick like khichdi. Turn off heat. Cool slightly, then mix in fresh coriander leaves. Check salt and adjust. Divide into 13 equal portions and shape into round cutlets with clean hands. Heat ghee on griddle and shallow fry cutlets on medium-low heat until golden brown on both sides. Serve hot.

name: Arbi Ki Sabzi
instructions: Peel and prep arbi. Prepare tadka with oil, cumin seeds, ginger-garlic paste. Add chopped onions and cook until golden. Add spices (turmeric, red chilli powder). Combine with prepped arbi and cook until tender. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot with roti.

name: Rajasthani Chana Dal Tadka
instructions: Soak chana dal for 3-4 hours, then boil with water, salt, and turmeric until cooked. Prepare ginger-garlic paste from green chillies, garlic, and ginger. Heat oil, add cumin seeds and sesame seeds, then ginger-garlic paste. Add onions and cook until translucent. Add turmeric and red chilli powder, then dal water and curd. Mix well and cook until oil separates. Add boiled dal, carom seeds, and adjust consistency with dal water. Prepare tadka: heat ghee, add cardamom, kashmiri chillies, chilli powder, and peppercorns. Finish dal with tadka and garnish with ghee, coriander, and chilli oil. Serve hot with roti.

name: Chicken Chatkara
instructions: Marinate chicken drumsticks with ginger-garlic paste, coriander leaves, chilli flakes, crushed peppercorns, salt, lemon juice, and oil for 1 hour. Prepare chatkara gravy: heat oil with chilli flakes and peppercorns, add soy sauce, ketchup, ginger-garlic paste, vinegar, and honey. Add marinated chicken and water, cook until done. Prepare dry masala by mixing red chilli powder, black pepper, coriander powder, mango powder, asafoetida, and cumin. Beat eggs for egg mixture. Create coating: dip cooked chicken in flour, then egg mixture, then crushed cornflakes. Deep fry until crispy and golden. For finishing gravy, heat oil with green chilli, add prepared chatkara gravy and water, then fried chicken. Garnish with lemon wedges, masala, and coriander. Serve hot.

name: Lucknowi Aloo Dum
instructions: Prepare curd mixture by mixing curd with red chilli powder, turmeric, coriander powder, cumin powder, and ginger-garlic paste. Heat oil, add onions and cook until golden (5-6 minutes). Add green and black cardamom, then curd mixture and cook until oil separates. Add water, salt, and dried apricots, let simmer. Prepare rice flour slurry with ghee and water. Fry potatoes in hot oil until crispy. Add rice flour slurry to gravy, then add coriander stems, dried apricots, and fried potatoes. Cook for 7-8 minutes on medium flame. Add sugar, salt, crushed fenugreek leaves, black peppercorns, and fresh coriander. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with coriander sprig. Serve hot with roti and naan.

name: Chicken Jalfrezi
instructions: 1. Marinate chicken with degi red chilli, turmeric, coriander, salt and curd. Keep aside. 2. In a handi, heat ghee and oil, add bay leaf, fennel and cumin seeds, cook chopped onions until translucent. 3. Add crushed black peppercorns, tomato puree and ginger garlic paste, cook for 4-5 minutes until oil separates. 4. Add degi red chilli, coriander and turmeric powder, cook for 1 minute. Add water, salt and boil. 5. Add marinated chicken with water and ghee, cover and cook until done. 6. Add onion petals, tender coriander stems and fresh coriander leaves. 7. Add capsicum and tomato strips, toss well. Finish with fresh cream and ghee. 8. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with fresh cream and coriander. Serve hot with roti or naan.

name: Balti Paneer Peshawari
ingredients: 1 kg paneer cut into large slabs
1 tbsp tender coriander stems with roots
instructions: 1. Prepare masala by dry roasting coriander seeds, peppercorns, cloves, cardamom and cashews for 2-3 minutes, then grind to fine powder. 2. In pressure cooker, heat ghee, add bay leaf, ginger, dry Kashmiri chillies and saute for 1 minute. 3. Add tomatoes, salt and cook 4-5 minutes. Remove tomato skins and add turmeric, degi red chilli and coriander powder, saute 2 more minutes. 4. Add water, tender coriander stems and ghee, boil, cover and pressure cook for 3-4 whistles until cooked. 5. Grind cooled gravy into smooth paste. 6. Fry paneer slabs in hot oil until light brown, drain on absorbent sheet. 7. Marinate fried paneer with prepared masala. 8. In handi, heat ghee until smoky, add prepared gravy and cook on high for 4-5 minutes. Add masala and water, simmer. 9. Add marinated paneer, green chillies, fresh coriander and sugar, cook 2-3 minutes on high flame. 10. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with coriander and green chillies. Serve hot with roti or naan.

name: Mutton Do Pyaza
ingredients: 1 heaped tbsp degi red chilli powder
2 tbsp tender coriander stems with roots
instructions: 1. In pressure cooker, heat mustard oil until smoky, add cinnamon stick, bay leaf, black cardamom, peppercorns and cloves, cook 2-3 minutes. 2. Add roughly sliced onions and cook until translucent. Add mutton with bones and ginger garlic paste, cook for 1 minute. 3. Add coriander powder, turmeric and degi red chilli powder, cook for 1 minute. 4. Add tomatoes, curd and tender coriander stems, cook for 5-6 minutes. 5. Cover and pressure cook for 8-10 whistles until mutton is tender. Depressurize and set aside. 6. In a separate pan, heat ghee and add dry Kashmiri chillies, onion quarters, salt and green chillies. Cook on high flame for 2-3 minutes. Transfer to tray. 7. In handi, add cooked mutton, ghee and hot water, bring to quick boil. 8. Add sauteed vegetables and cook for 2-3 minutes. 9. Finish with fresh coriander leaves and crushed fenugreek leaves. 10. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with coriander. Serve hot with roti.

name: Palak Matar Soup
instructions: 1. Cook green peas and spinach: in pressure cooker, heat ghee, add garlic and cook for 1 minute. Add peppercorns, cumin seeds, green peas, spinach, ghee, ginger and cook for 2 minutes. 2. Add water, salt, pinch of sugar and pressure cook for 2-3 whistles until cooked. 3. Cool slightly and grind mixture into smooth paste using hand blender. Set aside. 4. Prepare muthiya: mix fresh fenugreek leaves, jowar flour, gram flour, turmeric, degi red chilli, cumin, black pepper, asafoetida, salt, ghee and water to form semisoft dough. 5. Divide into small lemon-sized balls and form muthiya shapes. Fry in hot oil until cooked. Drain on absorbent sheet. 6. Prepare tadka: in handi, heat ghee and oil, add fennel seeds, asafoetida, cumin seeds and let splutter. Add fresh green peas and cook for 1 minute. 7. Add degi red chilli, turmeric, coriander and cumin powder. Add ground peas-spinach mixture and simmer for 10-12 minutes on medium flame. 8. Finish with ghee and mix well. 9. Transfer to soup bowls and garnish with fresh cream, chilli oil, prepared muthiya and coriander. Serve hot with prepared muthiya.

name: Methi Matar Kadhi
ingredients: 1 cup fresh fenugreek leaves for batter
instructions: 1. Prepare curd mixture: mix curd, water, ginger green chilli paste, salt, sugar and gram flour. Set aside. 2. Prepare pakoda batter: mix fresh fenugreek leaves, ginger green chilli paste, carom seeds, salt, gram flour and ghee to make semi-thick batter. 3. Prepare makhana: in pan, heat ghee, add degi red chilli powder and foxnuts, toss on high flame for 1 minute. Transfer to tray and set aside. 4. Prepare kadhi: in handi, heat ghee, add ginger, dry Kashmiri chillies, fresh fenugreek leaves and cook for 5-6 minutes. 5. Add fresh green peas, asafoetida, turmeric, degi red chilli and coriander powder. Cook for a while. 6. Add prepared curd mixture, salt, water and adjust consistency. Let simmer for 10-12 minutes. 7. Once boiling, make small balls from pakoda batter and drop gently into boiling kadhi. Cook on medium flame until dapkas float to top. 8. Add ghee, tender coriander stems and boil for a while. 9. Finish with jaggery and green chillies, quick boil. 10. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with fried makhana and coriander. Serve hot with roti or steam rice.

name: Old Delhi Chole
instructions: Soak chickpeas overnight. Create bouquet garni with tea leaves, cardamom, peppercorns, cloves. Boil chickpeas with bouquet garni, bay leaves, and kashmiri chillies for 25-30 minutes until soft. Cook spinach separately and make paste. In kadai, heat oil and add bay leaf, cinnamon, kashmiri chillies, and cumin seeds. Saute onions until golden. Add all powdered spices, ginger-garlic paste, and tomatoes. Cook until oil separates. Add spinach paste, boiled chickpeas, and coriander stems. Cook for 10 minutes. Finish with ghee and paneer cubes. Garnish with ginger, fried chilli, and coriander.

name: Malai Pyaaz Ki Sabji
instructions: Char onions over open flame for 2-3 minutes until blackened. Mix beaten curd with ginger-green chilli paste and powdered spices. Make ginger-green chilli paste by grinding green chillies and ginger. Heat oil in kadai, add cumin, fennel, cardamom, bay leaves, and button chillies. Add curd mixture and cook 5-6 minutes until oil separates. Add charred onions, fresh onions, and water. Cover and cook until soft. Finish with fresh cream and fried cashews. Garnish with cashews, ratlami sev, and coriander. Serve hot with roti.

name: Kadhai Chicken Keema
instructions: Marinate minced chicken with coriander powder, turmeric, degi chilli powder, and salt. Grind dry masala ingredients (mace, peppercorns, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf) to coarse powder. Heat oil in handi, saute onions until golden. Add prepared masala and chilli flakes, cook for 1 minute. Add ginger, green chillies, tomato puree and cook 5-6 minutes until oil separates. Add green garlic, coriander stems, spinach, and curd. Cook briefly. Add marinated chicken keema, mix well, cover and cook 10-12 minutes on medium heat. Finish with green chilli pickle and fresh coriander. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with green chilli, coriander, and lemon wedge. Serve hot with pav or roti.

name: Egg Ghee Roast
instructions: For stock: Heat ghee, saute sliced onions, add coriander stems, peppercorns, cardamom, water, cashews, salt, and tamarind. Simmer and blend to smooth puree. Strain and keep aside. For paste: Grind soaked bedgi red chillies with garlic cloves to smooth paste. For curry: Heat ghee in handi, add cinnamon stick and prepared paste. Saute 4-5 minutes. Gradually add stock, salt, and curry leaves. Cook on medium heat until oil separates. Add ghee and boiled eggs. Cook well. Add hot water if needed. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with coriander. Serve hot with paratha or dosa.

name: Rajma Ke Kebab
instructions: Soak rajma overnight. Discard water and pressure cook with halved potatoes, salt, and water for 3 whistles on medium heat. Cool and mash cooked rajma and potatoes until smooth. Shape small portions into roundels and press gently into tikkis. If sticky, grind dry poha and add gradually for binding. Mix in ginger, green chilli, mint, coriander, and all powdered spices. Shape into 15 equal tikkis, each 50 grams. Heat pan and brush lightly with ghee. Place tikkis and cook over medium-low heat until golden and crisp on both sides. Serve hot with green chutney and rajma soup.

name: Paneer Kali Mirch
instructions: In kadhai, add roughly cut onions, cashews, melon seeds, ginger, garlic, green chillies, tej patta, cardamom, and cinnamon. Pour hot water to cover, cover and cook 10-15 minutes until soft. Cool completely. Transfer to mixer and blend into smooth puree. Discard whole spices if preferred. Heat butter and oil in kadhai, add slit green chilli, ginger julienne, and coriander stems. Saute briefly until fragrant. Add base gravy and mix well. Season with salt, sugar, and crushed black pepper. Add hot water to adjust consistency and let simmer. In separate pan, shallow fry paneer pieces until golden on both sides. Add fried paneer to simmering gravy. Top with kasuri methi powder and pinch of garam masala. Stir well to coat paneer. Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve hot with naan or roti, garnished with rogan and black pepper.

name: Haryali Tawa Pulao
instructions: Wash basmati rice 3-4 times until water is clear, soak for 30-45 minutes. Bring salted water with turmeric to rolling boil. Drain rice and add to pot, stir gently, cook until almost done (grains cooked but with slight bite). Drain rice on a sieve or spider strainer. Fluff with fork to release steam and cool completely. Blanch spinach in hot water for 15-20 seconds, transfer to ice water. Blend blanched spinach with coriander, mint, ginger, garlic, and green chilli into fine paste. Heat tawa or flat-bottomed pan over high flame. Add butter and oil, add cumin seeds and sliced onions, stir well, cook until lightly golden. Add all remaining vegetables, stir, cook 1-2 minutes. Add spinach paste with all powdered spices, salt, sugar, and splash of hot water, cook 3-4 minutes. Add cooled rice, kasuri methi, lemon juice, garam masala, and fresh coriander, stir well. Serve hot with raita.

name: Soya Badi & Veggies in Tandoori Sauce
instructions: Boil soya badi in water until soft and cooked through. Strain and set aside. Prepare tandoori sauce base by blending or cooking tomato, ginger, garlic, and spices with yogurt. In a pan, cook the sauce over medium heat, add prepared vegetables and cooked soya badi, simmer until vegetables are tender and sauce coats well. Prepare lemon coriander rice separately by cooking rice with lemon juice and fresh coriander. Plate soya badi curry with rice on the side. Serve hot.

name: Kaju Makhana Curry
instructions: In kadhai, combine tomatoes, 20-25 cashews, coriander stems, green chilli, ginger, Kashmiri red chillies, salt, green cardamom, bay leaf, and cinnamon stick. Pour in hot water to cover ingredients, cover with lid, and boil until tomatoes are completely mushy. In separate kadhai, heat ghee and oil together. Fry 30-35 cashews until golden and set aside. In same pan, fry makhana until crisp. Transfer both to bowl. Cool tomato mixture completely, discard whole spices, blend into smooth puree using water as needed. Heat butter and oil in kadhai. Add cumin seeds and let crackle. Stir in turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, and garam masala. Pour in tomato cashew puree and cook well until color deepens and oil begins to separate. Season with sugar and adjust salt if needed. Add fried cashews and makhana to gravy. Stir in ginger julienne, sliced green chilli, roasted kasuri methi powder, a pinch of garam masala, and fresh coriander. Add hot water if needed to adjust consistency. Simmer briefly. Serve hot with roti, naan, or paratha.

name: Ada Pradhaman
instructions: Boil ada in water until soft but still holding its shape. Strain and rinse lightly with water to stop cooking. Melt jaggery with a little water and strain to remove impurities. Heat ghee in a pan and fry grated coconut until light golden brown. In same ghee, fry cashews until golden and set aside. Add raisins to ghee and let puff up. Strain coconut, cashews, and raisins and set aside. In the same ghee, add the cooked ada and cook briefly, then pour in the jaggery syrup. Simmer for 10-12 minutes until jaggery and ada are well combined. Add thin coconut milk and cook until mixture thickens. Lower the flame and add thick coconut milk along with cardamom powder. Stir gently without cooking further to keep the kheer creamy and rich. Finally, fold in the fried coconut, cashews, and raisins at the very end. Serve warm or chilled.

name: Palak Paneer Bhurji
instructions: Heat milk over medium-low flame. Heat butter and oil, add cumin seeds until they crackle. Roast besan until golden. Add chopped onions and tomatoes with salt and powdered spices. Add water and cook until tomatoes turn mushy. Add roughly chopped spinach with water and cook until moisture evaporates. Once milk simmers, curdle it with vinegar-water mixture. Strain paneer and add to cooked spinach mixture. Mix well, add milk to adjust consistency to semi-thick. Add ginger julienne, garam masala, kasuri methi powder, and fresh coriander. Sprinkle black pepper and chaat masala. Serve hot with laccha paratha or bread.

name: Sev Tamatar Ki Sabzi
instructions: Heat groundnut oil in kadhai. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, hing, curry leaves, garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Sauté until fragrant. Add turmeric powder and kashmiri red chilli powder, stir quickly. Add diced tomatoes and cook on high flame for 3-4 minutes. Cover and cook on low flame for 4-5 minutes until tomatoes turn soft. Remove lid and mash tomatoes slightly. Add coriander powder, remaining chilli powder, and cumin powder. Cook until oil begins to separate. Add reserved tomatoes and jaggery. Pour in hot water, bring to boil and simmer for 3-4 minutes. Add more water as sev absorbs liquid. Adjust salt. Add sev, fresh coriander, and garam masala. Stir gently and cook briefly. Serve hot with roti or bread.

name: Fruit Rabdi
instructions: Roast makhana in ghee over medium heat until light and crisp. Cool completely and grind into fine powder using electric chopper. In heavy-bottomed kadhai, bring milk to gentle boil then reduce flame and simmer for 10-12 minutes. Add makhana powder while whisking continuously to avoid lumps. Add saffron, vanilla essence, and nuts. Cook for 5 minutes on low flame. Add condensed milk and stir well. Continue cooking for 2-3 minutes until rabdi reaches semi-thick consistency. Turn off flame and let rabdi cool completely. Once chilled, fold in all diced fruits (add only before serving to retain freshness). Garnish with slivered pistachios and pomegranate. Serve chilled.

name: Dhaba Style Paneer Curry
instructions: Heat oil in kadhai, add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Add sliced onions and cook until golden brown. Add tomatoes, coriander stems, garlic, ginger, green chillies, and salt. Sauté for a few minutes. Splash hot water, cover and cook until tomatoes turn soft and mushy. Cool slightly and blend into smooth puree with water. Heat ghee in kadhai, add bay leaves, green cardamom, cumin seeds, black cardamom, and cinnamon. Sauté until fragrant. Add prepared puree and stir well. Add Kashmiri red chilli powder, turmeric, coriander powder, and spicy chilli powder. Cook on medium flame until gravy darkens and oil starts separating. Adjust consistency with hot water, season with salt and sugar. Cover and let simmer gently. Meanwhile, shallow fry paneer pieces until golden on both sides. Add fried paneer to simmering gravy. Add roasted kasuri methi powder, garam masala, and sliced green chillies. Add fresh coriander and stir gently. Serve hot with tandoori roti, naan, paratha, or steamed rice.

name: Grilled Paneer Vegetable Burger
instructions: Prepare paneer-vegetable patty by grating paneer with grated vegetables (carrot, capsicum, french beans), add salt, pepper and binding agent. Form into patties and chill. Char grill onions with salt and pepper on high flame until lightly burnt. Prepare burger sauce by mixing ketchup, mayo, green chilli sauce, and mustard. Heat grill over high flame and oil it well. Grill the chilled patties until dark char marks appear on both sides, about 4-5 minutes per side. Toast burger buns lightly on the grill. Assemble burger: place sauce on bottom bun, add grilled patty, add grilled onions, fresh cucumber slices, tomato slices, lettuce, and pickles. Top with bun and serve immediately while hot.

name: Veg Cheese Chilli Corn Sandwich
instructions: Make chutney: blend fresh coriander, curry leaves, green chilli, garlic, sandwich masala, salt, bread, ice cube, and water into thick chutney. Finely chop onion, capsicum, red bell pepper, and sweet corn. Mix chopped vegetables with sandwich chutney, oregano, red chilli flakes, sandwich masala, and grated cheese for filling. Butter bread slices on all sides, spread chutney on each. On 2 slices spread filling generously, add optional paneer and sprinkle masala. Stack with 3rd slice and press gently. Heat grill pan with butter, place sandwich and press lightly with plate. Toast until bottom is crisp and golden, butter top slice, flip and cook other side until crisp and golden. Cut into halves or quarters and serve hot with chutney and ketchup.

name: Mushroom Butter Masala
instructions: Blend tomatoes, garlic, ginger, coriander stems, green chilli, and cashew into fine puree. Heat butter and oil in kadhai, add cumin seeds, cardamom, cinnamon, bay leaf, and chopped onions. Cook until onions turn golden brown. Add powdered spices with splash of hot water and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add tomato puree, salt, sugar, and green chillies. Cover and cook 5-7 minutes until gravy thickens and oil separates. Trim and cut mushrooms into halves if large. Add mushrooms to gravy, stir and cook on high flame 2-3 minutes. Add hot water, cover and cook 4-5 minutes until mushrooms release moisture and become tender. Add fresh cream, butter, kasuri methi, garam masala, and fresh coriander. Stir well and serve hot with roti.

name: Basil Chilli Chicken
instructions: Prep vegetables: slice capsicum and onions into thin strips, chop spring onion whites and greens separately, chop ginger, garlic, green chillies together. Cut chicken breast into very thin slices. Velvet chicken: add baking soda and 1-2 tbsp water, stir vigorously in circular motion, repeat 2-3 times until chicken looks slimy. Add salt, white pepper, soy sauce, oil, and corn flour, mix with thin coating. Heat oil to 165°C and fry chicken in batches until light golden (3-4 minutes). Drain in sieve. Make cornflour slurry with water. Heat wok with oil, add ginger-garlic-chilli mix, spring onion whites, onion, and capsicum. Stir-fry briefly on high heat. Add dark soy sauce, light soy sauce, hot water, white pepper, vinegar, salt, and sugar. Simmer sauce. Add fried chicken and cornflour slurry gradually, stirring until sauce thickens and becomes glossy. Add torn basil leaves and spring onion greens. Toss well and serve.

name: Pyaaz Laccha Paratha
instructions: Make dough: add wheat flour and salt to bowl, gradually add water and combine until dry flour comes together. Knead into smooth semi-soft dough, add 1 tsp oil, knead in, shape into ball, lightly oil surface, cover with damp cloth and rest. Make filling: thinly slice onions from stem to root, place in bowl with large pinch salt and rest 2 minutes. Squeeze excess moisture, transfer to clean bowl. Add all powdered spices, coriander, green chilli, vinegar, mustard oil and mix well. After dough rests, take portion and shape into roundel. Roll into thin chapati. Add generous filling in centre, make single cut from centre to edge, roll into cone, press down to form laccha pedha. Dust with flour and roll into semi-thick laccha paratha. Heat tawa on medium flame, place paratha and dry-cook both sides until light brown spots appear. Apply ghee on both sides and cook until golden, crisp, and flaky. Serve hot with dahi and achaar.

name: Drumstick Soup
instructions: Lightly peel drumsticks and cut into pieces. Add drumsticks to kadhai with onion, ginger, garlic, green chillies, coriander stems, bay leaf, cloves, cinnamon, pinch of salt, and enough hot water to cover. Bring to boil, cover and cook 5-6 minutes until drumsticks are tender. Discard whole spices and strain mixture, keeping stock aside. Let solids cool slightly, blend them with fresh coriander and mint using some reserved stock until smooth. Strain blended mixture back into kadhai with remaining stock, discarding woody drumstick fibres. Add hot water to adjust consistency as needed. Bring to gentle simmer, season with salt, black salt, roasted cumin powder, black pepper, and lemon juice. Serve hot.

name: Veg Chowmein with Soya Badi
instructions: Boil soya badi in water for 5-7 minutes until soften and tender, drain well. Boil noodles in water until cooked, drain and set aside. Prep vegetables: slice onion, capsicum, julienne carrot, shred cabbage, chop spring onion, mince garlic, ginger, green chilli. Heat oil in wok or kadhai on high flame. Add ginger, garlic, green chilli and stir-fry briefly. Add onion and capsicum, stir-fry on high heat. Add carrot and cabbage, continue stir-frying. Add boiled soya badi and toss well. Pour in light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, white pepper, and salt. Add splash of hot water. Add cooked noodles and spring onion whites, toss everything together until well mixed. If needed, mix corn flour with water and add to thicken sauce slightly. Cook for 1-2 minutes until flavours combine. Garnish with spring onion greens and serve hot.

name: Dahi Puri with Kokum Chutney
instructions: Make kokum chutney by cooking kokum extract with sugar and spices, cool completely. Blend coriander, mint, ginger, green chillies, cumin, asafoetida, black salt, chana dal, salt and ice into a spicy chutney. Toast a mix of spices, add black salt and chilli powder, blend into magic chaat masala. Whisk curd with sugar and black salt and pass through sieve to make smooth curd topping. To assemble: crack a puri, add boiled potato, spicy chutney, kokum chutney, bundi, sprouts, and chilled curd topping. Add more spicy chutney, magic chaat masala powder, pomegranate seeds and fresh coriander. Serve immediately.

name: Tameta Puri (Tomato Bhajiya)
instructions: Sieve gram flour into a bowl, add salt, carom seeds and baking soda, mix dry ingredients. Add water gradually while whisking to avoid lumps, adjust to flowing consistency and let rest. Make green chutney by grinding coriander, mint, ginger, green chillies, cumin, chaat masala, sugar, lemon juice and ice into thick coarse paste. Cut firm tomatoes into 1.5-2 cm thick slices. Spread spoonful of chutney onto each slice and layer. Whisk batter, dip chutney-coated tomato slices to coat completely. Fry in 160°C oil over medium-high flame until light golden brown in batches. Cool completely on a sieve. Double fry for 1-2 minutes in hot oil to make super crisp. Serve hot but carefully as extremely hot inside.

name: chilli garlic tawa chicken
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 1-1.5 tbsp
instructions: Cut chicken breast into thin slices. Add sliced chicken to a bowl with salt, vinegar, ginger garlic green chilli paste, and kashmiri red chilli powder; marinate for 1 hour if desired. Make red chilli paste by blending soaked kashmiri red chillies with water until smooth. Heat tawa over high flame and add oil. Add chopped garlic and red chilli paste, stir well. Add whisked curd followed by garam masala, spicy red chilli powder, salt, and a splash of hot water. Cook until oil separates. Add a pinch of sugar to balance flavors. Adjust consistency with hot water and place chicken slices evenly on tawa. Cook on medium-high flame for 2-3 minutes per side. Once coated with masala, add fresh coriander stems, fresh coriander, and butter. Serve hot as a starter or with pav.

name: akkha masoor
instructions: Wash and soak sabut kaali masur for 1-2 hours. Add soaked dal to pressure cooker with 1 inch water above dal and a pinch of salt. If soaked, pressure cook for 1 whistle on medium flame; if unsoaked use 2-3 whistles. Allow natural depressurization and set aside. Heat oil in kadhai, add cumin seeds until they splutter. Add chopped onions and cook until golden brown. Lower flame, add turmeric powder, kashmiri red chilli powder, and kanda lahsun masala with a splash of hot water. Add boiled dal and mix well. Let simmer and mash slightly with ladle to release starch. Crush garlic and green chilli into paste with mortar and pestle. Heat oil in small pan, sauté garlic-chilli paste until golden. Add cumin seeds, asafetida, and kashmiri red chilli powder, then immediately pour this tadka over simmering dal. Stir well, adjust salt, and garnish with fresh coriander. Serve hot with bhakri or steamed rice.

name: aloo chole pulao
instructions: Wash basmati rice thoroughly until water runs clear; soak in fresh water. Soak chickpeas for 6-8 hours or overnight. Discard water and pressure cook chickpeas with fresh water, whole spices, and baking soda for 1 whistle high flame + 2 whistles medium-low flame. Heat ghee and oil in heavy-bottom vessel over high flame. Add cumin seeds, then sliced onions; sauté until golden brown. Add ginger garlic paste and green chillies, cook until raw aroma disappears. Add sliced tomatoes, whisked curd, salt, and powdered spices; cook until oil separates. Add potato pieces and cover for 4-5 minutes until potatoes almost cooked. Drain chickpeas and discard whole spices. Add chickpeas to potatoes. Pour measured hot water, adjust salt. Drain soaked rice completely and add with coriander and mint. Stir gently and simmer on medium flame. Cover lid with kitchen towel and cook on lowest flame for 12-15 minutes. Check doneness by lifting lid carefully; if water remains, cook 2-3 minutes more. Switch off flame and rest for 15-20 minutes. Gently fluff with spatula. Serve hot with raita.

name: tawa bhindi masala
ingredients: oil 3-4 tbsp for frying, 1-2 tbsp for gravy
kashmiri red chilli powder 1/2 tsp frying, 2 tsp gravy
instructions: Select medium-sized fresh lady fingers; bend tip to check for clean snap. Wash thoroughly and pat dry completely with cloth; air-dry 2-3 minutes to remove surface moisture. Trim tops and make single slit through center of each bhindi. Heat oil in fry pan and add slit bhindi; fry on medium heat until almost cooked and lightly golden. Transfer fried bhindi to bowl. Mix all powdered spices in separate bowl and sprinkle over fried bhindi with lemon juice; coat evenly. Reserve 1-2 teaspoons spice mix for gravy. Heat oil in fry pan, add cumin seeds and crackle. Add finely chopped onions and sauté until golden brown. Add ginger garlic paste and green chillies; cook until raw smell disappears. Lower flame, add red chilli powder, turmeric, and coriander powder with splash of hot water to prevent burning. Cook masala until oil separates. Blend tomatoes with soaked cashews to form smooth puree. Add puree to pan along with whisked curd and salt; cook until oil separates again. Add hot water to adjust gravy to semi-thick consistency. Add spice-coated fried bhindi, ginger juliennes, slit green chillies, coriander stems, and fresh coriander. Fold gently without breaking bhindi. Serve hot with tandoori roti or paratha.

name: oil-free pumpkin soup
instructions: Cut pumpkin into quarters and scoop out all seeds. Peel using preferred method: slice quarters and remove skin with knife, peel like watermelon slice, or use quality peeler. Cut peeled pumpkin into rough dices. Peel red carrots and roughly chop. Roughly cut onion. Add chopped pumpkin, carrots, onion to pressure cooker along with roughly sliced ginger, garlic, green chilli, fresh coriander stems, celery stalk, cashews, whole spices, salt, and enough water. Pressure-cook for 3 whistles. Allow cooker to depressurize naturally, then open lid. Remove and discard whole spices. Let mixture cool completely. Transfer cooled mixture into mixer jar and blend into smooth, fine puree. Transfer puree to cooker or pot and bring to gentle simmer. Add hot water to adjust consistency as needed. Season with additional salt and pepper. Finish with squeeze of lemon juice. Balance flavor with honey or sugar if pumpkin isn't naturally sweet. Serve warm.

name: Vegetable Vermicelli Upma
instructions: 1. Roast vermicelli in a pan without oil until light golden. Keep aside. 2. Heat oil in a pan, add cumin and mustard seeds, let them crackle. 3. Add chopped onions and ginger, sauté until onions become translucent. 4. Add carrot, peas and green chilli. Stir-fry for 1-2 minutes. 5. Add turmeric powder and salt. 6. Pour in water and bring to a boil. 7. Add roasted vermicelli and mix well. 8. Cover and cook on medium-low heat until all water is absorbed and vermicelli is cooked through (5-7 minutes). 9. Stir occasionally to ensure even cooking. 10. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Aloo Koliwada
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 2 tbsp
instructions: 1. Make chutney: Grind coriander, mint, garlic, ginger, green chilli, onion, tomato, tamarind, jaggery, black salt and chat masala into fine paste with minimal water. Keep slightly thick. 2. Peel potatoes and cut into quarters. Par-boil in salted water for exactly 5 minutes - toothpick should enter center with slight resistance. 3. Strain and let cool completely on a sieve so coating sticks well. 4. In large bowl, prepare koliwada coating: mix oil, all powdered spices, ginger-garlic paste, whisked yogurt, lemon juice, baking soda, gram flour and corn flour into smooth thick paste. 5. Heat oil in kadhai to 170°C. 6. Add cooled par-boiled potatoes to coating and coat lightly with hands. 7. Drop coated potatoes into hot oil in batches. Let fry undisturbed for 1 minute to set coating, then stir and fry on medium-low heat until crisp and golden. 8. Transfer to sieve to drain excess oil. Repeat with remaining potatoes. 9. Serve hot with squeeze of lemon, sprinkle of chat masala and green chutney.

name: Moringa Thepla
instructions: 1. Remove moringa leaves from stems, wash thoroughly, pat dry and chop finely. 2. In large mixing bowl, add wheat flour, jowar flour, gram flour, chopped moringa leaves, ajwain, asafoetida, sesame seeds, ginger-green chilli paste, salt, turmeric, red chilli powder, garam masala, yogurt and oil. Mix well. 3. Gradually add water and knead into soft, smooth dough. 4. Apply oil to surface, cover with damp cloth and let rest 15-20 minutes. 5. Heat tawa over medium-high flame. 6. Take small portion of dough, roll into ball, dust with dry wheat flour and roll into thin round thepla with rolling pin. 7. Add 2-3 drops oil to hot tawa, wipe clean, sprinkle wheat flour and wipe again to prevent sticking. 8. Place rolled thepla on tawa. Dry roast both sides until light spots appear, then add very little ghee and cook quickly over high heat. Avoid overcooking to keep thepla soft. 9. Serve hot with butter, yogurt and pickle.

name: Instant Rava Uttapam
instructions: MILAGAI PODI: 1. Toast chana and urad dal in pan over low flame until fragrant. Add sesame seeds and toast until slightly golden. Cool completely. 2. Toast red chillies in same pan over low flame until crispy. Cool in separate bowl. 3. Break chilli stalks, grind chillies in mixer into fine powder. 4. Add toasted dals, sesame seeds, asafoetida and salt. Grind coarsely - do not make fine powder. Store in airtight container in fridge. UTTAPAM BATTER: 5. In large bowl, add upma rava, sour yogurt, water and salt. Whisk well into lump-free batter. Cover and rest 15 minutes - this is crucial for rava to soak moisture. 6. Heat dosa tawa over medium flame. 7. Add eno to rested batter and mix well - this helps uttapam rise. GHEE PODI UTTAPAM: 8. Sprinkle water on hot tawa - should sizzle immediately and evaporate. 9. Pour ladleful of batter and let spread naturally into thick round. Allow to set slightly, then add ghee around edges and on top. 10. Cover and cook on lowest flame 3-4 minutes until bottom turns golden and crisp. 11. Uncover, add more ghee, sprinkle milagai podi generously. Serve hot. CHEESE PODI UTTAPAM: 12. Pour batter on tawa, top with grated cheese, cover and cook on low flame until cheese melts and base becomes crisp. 13. Drizzle ghee around edges, sprinkle podi and garnish with spring onion greens. ONION TOMATO UTTAPAM: 14. Pour batter on tawa, quickly top with onions, tomatoes, green chillies and salt. Add ghee. Cover and cook on low flame until base turns golden and crisp. 15. Flip carefully, let vegetables caramelize. Flip again, add ghee, sprinkle podi and fresh coriander.

name: Instant Rasam
instructions: INSTANT RASAM POWDER PREMIX: 1. In a pan over low flame, roast arhar dal until fragrant. 2. Add coriander seeds, cumin seeds and roast together until fragrant. Add fenugreek seeds and roast. 3. Toast black pepper and red chillies in same pan over low flame until crispy. 4. Cool all roasted ingredients completely. 5. Break chilli stalks, grind chillies in mixer into fine powder. 6. Add roasted dals, seeds, curry leaves, turmeric, asafoetida and salt. Grind into fine powder. 7. Store in airtight container - can be used for several months. PREPARING RASAM: 8. Chop and grind tomato and ginger into fine paste. 9. Heat oil in pan and sauté tomato-ginger paste over medium-high heat. 10. Add 2-3 tbsp rasam powder to the paste. 11. Pour in 2-3 cups water. 12. Add salt and red chilli powder to taste. 13. Bring to a boil. 14. Add tamarind pulp for tang. 15. Add fresh coriander leaves and butter. 16. Simmer for 1-2 minutes. 17. Serve fresh hot rasam with rice or as a soup. Optional tempering: Add oil to small pan, crackle mustard seeds, add curry leaves and pour over rasam.

name: Bajra Laddu
instructions: Roast pearl millet in ghee until fragrant. Roast sesame seeds separately until golden. Toast almond flakes, melon seeds, and soak raisins. Combine jaggery powder with roasted millet and sesame. Add ghee, ginger powder, nutmeg, and cardamom. Mix well and add almond flakes, melon seeds, and raisins. Let cool slightly, then roll into balls by hand.

name: Sattu Masala Puri
ingredients: oil or ghee for deep frying
instructions: Grind roasted chickpeas into powder (sattu). Mix wheat flour with salt, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, carom seeds, cumin seeds, and fenugreek leaves. Add sattu powder and mix well. Add water gradually to form stiff dough. Knead well and let rest 15 minutes. Divide into balls and flatten into discs. Heat oil in deep pan and fry puris until they puff up and turn golden brown on both sides. Serve hot with vegetables, pickle, or yogurt.

name: Multigrain Khichdi
instructions: Soak chickpeas, moth lentil, and moong overnight. Rinse and drain. Heat ghee in heavy-bottomed pot and add cumin seeds. When they crackle, add asafoetida and ginger-chilli paste. Add chopped tomatoes and cook till soft. Add all soaked lentils, peas, millet, and rice. Stir in turmeric powder and red chilli. Add salt and 6 cups water. Bring to boil, then simmer covered on low heat for 20-25 minutes until all grains and lentils are soft and cooked through. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Stuffed Jowar Baati
instructions: Mix jowar flour with salt, carom seeds, fenugreek leaves, sesame seeds, 2 tbsp ghee, and baking soda. Add yogurt and knead into soft dough. Let rest 15 minutes. For filling: mash boiled potatoes and mix with salt, red chilli powder, coriander powder, turmeric, garam masala, dry mango powder, ginger-chilli paste, and fresh coriander leaves. Divide dough into balls, flatten, add spiced potato filling in center, seal edges, and flatten gently into thick round shape. Heat oil and ghee mixture for deep frying. Fry baatis until golden brown and crispy on all sides, about 3-4 minutes per baati. Serve hot with chutney, yogurt, or tea.

name: Malai Burfi
instructions: Crumble paneer finely and combine with sugar, milk, and cream in a heavy-bottomed pan. Cook over medium heat, stirring continuously until mixture becomes thick and starts to leave the sides of the pan. Mix in milk powder gradually while stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Continue cooking and stirring for 5-8 minutes until the mixture is well combined and creamy. Pour the prepared mixture onto a greased and parchment-lined tray, flatten to even thickness (about 1/2 inch). Allow to cool for 2-3 hours until set. Cut into square or diamond-shaped pieces. Garnish with pistachio flakes. Store in an airtight container.

name: Elephant Yam Cutlets
instructions: Boil and peel elephant yam. Roast coriander seeds, cumin seeds, black pepper, and clove together, then grind to powder. Mix boiled yam with roasted spice powder, peanut powder, chana powder, turmeric, red chilli, ginger, dry mango powder, salt, black salt, asafoetida, and green coriander. Bind with refined flour. Divide into portions and shape into cutlet rounds. Coat with bread crumbs. Deep fry or shallow fry until golden and crispy on both sides.

name: Almond Puran Poli
instructions: Dry roast semolina in ghee until fragrant. Soak almonds in milk and grind to a paste. Combine roasted semolina with almond paste, jaggery, cardamom powder, and nutmeg powder. Knead dough with flour, refined flour, salt, oil, and remaining milk. Roll out dough thin, fill with prepared almond-jaggery mixture, seal and roll again. Cook on griddle with ghee until golden brown on both sides, flattening gently during cooking.

name: Lauki Halwa
instructions: Peel and grate bottle gourd. Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan and add grated gourd. Fry over medium-high heat until liquid evaporates and halwa turns light golden (about 20-25 minutes). Add sugar and continue cooking until well-combined. Stir in cardamom powder. Reduce heat to low, add cream and mix well. Cook for another 2-3 minutes until halwa comes together. Remove from heat and garnish with roasted cashews, almond flakes, and raisins.

name: Til Gur Burfi
instructions: Dry roast sesame seeds on medium heat for 2-3 minutes until fragrant, then remove. Heat ghee in a heavy pan and add jaggery, stirring constantly until melted completely. Keep the heat on medium to prevent burning. Once jaggery is fully melted, add the roasted sesame seeds and mix thoroughly, breaking up any lumps. Spread the mixture onto a greased plate or parchment paper while still warm. Let it cool and harden. Once set, cut into desired pieces and store in an airtight container.

name: Moong Dal Vrindavan Cheela
instructions: Soak moong dal and rice separately for 30 minutes. Grind them together with green chilli, ginger, asafoetida, and salt to make a smooth batter. For filling, heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds, ginger-chilli paste, and asafoetida. Add chopped boiled potatoes and tomato with salt, and cook until well-combined. Heat oil on a non-stick pan or skillet, pour a ladle of batter and spread thin, similar to dosa. Cook on medium heat until the bottom is light brown. Add filling in the center, fold in half, and press gently. Flip and cook the other side until golden. Serve hot.

name: Navratri Vrat Dry Fruits Namkeen
instructions: Peel and chop fresh coconut into small pieces. Roast peanuts, almonds, and melon seeds separately in a dry pan over medium heat for 2-3 minutes each until fragrant. Roast coconut pieces and makhana (fox nuts) briefly until slightly colored. Heat ghee in a large pan and add all roasted ingredients together. Add crushed black pepper and rock salt, mixing well to coat everything evenly. Continue to roast on low heat for 2-3 minutes while stirring constantly. Spread on parchment paper to cool completely before storing in an airtight container. This namkeen stays fresh for the entire 9 days of fasting.

name: Crispy Bitter Gourd
instructions: Slice bitter gourd thinly and sprinkle salt to remove bitterness, let sit 30 minutes. Dry the slices. Mix with gram flour, turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, sesame seeds, and asafoetida. Shallow fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Drain excess oil on paper towels. Serve hot as a side dish with roti or rice.

name: Lauki Jowar Thepla
instructions: Grate bottle gourd and squeeze excess water. Mince ginger and green chilli. Mix all flours with grated gourd, spices (turmeric, coriander, red chilli, cumin, carom seeds), kasuri methi, curd, salt, and oil. Knead into soft dough. Divide into portions and roll flat. Cook on hot griddle until golden spots appear on both sides, using oil as needed. Serve warm with pickle or curd.

name: Baby Corn Curry
instructions: Soak cashews and grind into paste. Clean and chop baby corn. Toast cardamom, clove, and black pepper and grind coarsely. Heat oil, crackle cumin seeds and bay leaf. Add asafoetida, turmeric, red chilli, coriander powders, and kasuri methi. Add tomatoes and cook until soft. Add cashew paste and simmer. Add baby corn and salt, cook 5-7 minutes. Stir in cream. Garnish with coriander and serve with roti or rice.

name: Beetroot Paneer Cutlets
instructions: Boil beetroot and potato separately until tender. Peel and grate finely. Crumble paneer. Mix beetroot, potato, paneer, ginger-chilli paste, all spices (turmeric, red chilli, coriander, amchur, black pepper, cumin), salt, and fresh coriander. Divide into portions and shape into cutlets. Roll in bread crumbs. Shallow fry in oil on medium heat until golden on both sides, 3-4 minutes per side. Drain on paper towels. Serve hot with chutney.

name: Spicy Green Chilli Fry
instructions: Roast peanuts, sesame, coconut, and fennel in dry pan until fragrant. Cool and grind coarsely. Heat mustard oil and crackle mustard and cumin seeds. Add asafoetida. Toast turmeric, coriander, red chilli, and amchur powders for 30 seconds. Add ground spice-nut mixture and stir well. Cut green chillies into 1-inch pieces. Pan-fry chillies in the spiced oil for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add salt to taste. Serve as side dish with rice or roti.

name: Sweet Layered Mathri
instructions: Mix refined flour and semolina. Knead stiff dough using ghee and water. Rest 15 minutes. Prepare ghee-flour mixture (sata) by warming ghee with flour until pourable. Roll dough very thin into circular sheet. Spread sata layer on dough. Roll up like pinwheel and flatten to seal. Roll again and cut into diamond shapes. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Prepare sugar syrup by dissolving sugar in water and heating to 230°F. Dip hot mathri briefly in syrup. Spread on cloth to cool and set. Store in airtight container.

name: Spiced Eggplant in Curd
instructions: Slice eggplant into 1/4-inch rounds and soak in salt water 15 minutes. Drain and pat dry. Shallow fry eggplant slices in oil until golden, 2-3 minutes per side. Drain on paper towels. Whisk curd and add turmeric, red chilli, and coriander powders. Spread spiced curd at bottom of serving dish. Layer fried eggplant slices and spread curd mixture on top. Repeat layers ending with curd. Prepare tempering by heating oil, crackling mustard and cumin seeds, adding dried chilli and asafoetida. Pour hot tempering over layered dish. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve with roti.

name: Mawa Gujiya
instructions: Mix refined flour with ghee and knead a soft dough. Prepare the filling by combining mawa, boora, coconut, chironji, almonds, cashews, raisins and crushed cardamom. Divide dough into portions, roll thin and fill with the mixture. Shape into semi-circles, seal edges, and deep fry until golden and crisp.

name: Aloo Mix Veg Sabzi
instructions: Cut and prepare all vegetables. Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add ginger-garlic, then onions and cook until soft. Add tomatoes and cook until they break down. Add the cubed potatoes and other vegetables. Season with turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, and salt. Stir well. Add garam masala and cook covered until potatoes are tender, stirring occasionally. Serve hot.

name: Cheesy Corn Samosa
ingredients: oil 1 tbsp plus 1/4 cup for frying
instructions: Prepare dough by mixing flour, salt, and oil with water until smooth. Let rest. Combine filling ingredients: cottage cheese, cheese, capsicum, corn, chilli flakes, pepper, oregano, and coriander. Divide dough into small balls and roll out very thin. Cook lightly on tawa to make patti sheets. Cut into strips and fill with the paneer-corn mixture. Fold into triangle shapes and seal edges with flour paste. Deep fry until golden and crispy.

name: Instant Upma Mix
instructions: Dry roast semolina lightly in a pan until fragrant. In another pan, heat ghee and add mustard seeds until they crackle. Add chana dal and urad dal, roast until golden. Add green chillies and curry leaves, cook briefly. Add cashews and peanuts, roast until crispy. Mix everything together with the roasted semolina, add salt and coriander. Store in an airtight container. To prepare upma, heat water, add this mix, stir well, and cook until fluffy.

name: Drumstick and Peas Curry
instructions: Cut drumstick pods into 2-inch pieces and extract the tender pulp from inside. Heat oil and add cumin seeds until they crackle. Add ginger and sauté briefly. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until they soften. Add the drumstick pulp and green peas. Season with turmeric, coriander powder, Kashmiri red chilli, salt, and fenugreek leaves. Mix gram flour with a little water and add to the curry. Stir well and cook covered for 5-7 minutes until peas are tender. Finish with garam masala and fresh coriander. Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Sweet Corn Veg Tikki
instructions: Mix grated carrot, chopped capsicum, grated potato, and green peas in a bowl. Add sweet corn kernels and mix well. Add ginger-chilli paste, coriander powder, red chilli powder, garam masala, amchur powder, and salt. In a separate bowl, mix gram flour and rice flour. Add this flour mixture to the vegetables and mix thoroughly to combine. Divide into equal portions and shape into thin patties. Heat oil in a pan on medium heat and shallow fry the tikkis until golden brown on both sides, about 2-3 minutes per side. Serve hot with chutney.

name: Poha Kachori
ingredients: oil 6 tbsp plus oil for frying
instructions: Soak poha in water for 5 minutes. Dry roast cumin seeds, coriander seeds, black pepper, clove, fennel, and cardamom until fragrant, then grind to coarse powder. Heat oil and add the spice powder, cook briefly. Add soaked poha, asafoetida, and salt, mix well. Add amchur powder, garam masala, and fenugreek leaves. Cool the filling. For the dough: mix refined flour, salt, carom seeds, and oil with water to form a stiff dough, let rest. Divide dough into balls and flatten slightly. Place filling in the center, gather edges and seal, then reshape into a ball. Deep fry in hot oil until golden brown. The kachoris will remain crispy for several days if stored in an airtight container.

name: Moong Dal Semolina Vada
instructions: Soak moong dal and grind to fine paste. Mix moong dal paste with curd, semolina, salt, asafoetida, ginger chilli paste, and coriander. Add oil and eno/baking soda to make a soft batter. Shape batter into vada forms. Steam the vadas in a steamer until fully cooked through (about 8-10 minutes). Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds and cumin seeds, let them crackle. Add curry leaves and toast them. Add sesame seeds and toast briefly. Add turmeric powder and red chilli powder. Pour in tomato sauce and ginger chilli paste, mix well. Toss the steamed vadas in the masala coating until well coated. Transfer to serving plate and garnish with fresh coriander. Serve hot.

name: New York Cheesecake
ingredients: sugar free green stevia sweetener 100g equivalent
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C). Mix graham cracker crumbs with melted butter and press firmly into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan. Beat cream cheese in a large bowl until smooth and creamy, about 2-3 minutes. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Add sugar-free stevia sweetener and mix until well combined. Stir in vanilla extract and salt. Pour filling mixture over the prepared crust. Place the springform pan in a larger roasting pan. Pour hot water into the roasting pan to create a water bath, reaching halfway up the sides of the springform pan. Bake for 55-60 minutes until the cheesecake is set but the center still jiggles slightly when gently shaken. Turn off oven, crack the oven door open slightly, and let cheesecake cool in the oven for 1 hour. Remove from oven and let cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight before serving.

name: Pastel de Nata
instructions: Prepare puff pastry sheets and line into tart molds. Heat milk with cinnamon sticks until steaming. Whisk egg yolks with cornstarch and sugar-free sweetener until pale. Slowly add hot milk to egg mixture while whisking constantly to prevent curdling. Cook mixture on medium heat stirring continuously until thickened to custard consistency. Add vanilla extract. Pour custard into pastry shells. Bake at 200°C for 15-18 minutes until pastry is golden brown and custard tops have slight caramelization.

name: High Protein Bowl
ingredients: 1 cup cooked quinoa or brown rice
2 cups mixed vegetables (cucumber, tomato, bell pepper, carrots)
instructions: Cook quinoa or brown rice as per package instructions. Cube paneer and lightly pan-fry until edges are golden. Prepare mixed vegetables - dice cucumber, tomato, bell pepper, and grate carrots. Chop onion and garlic finely. In a bowl, layer cooked grain at the base. Top with pan-fried paneer, boiled chickpeas, and mixed vegetables. Sprinkle chopped onion, garlic, and fresh coriander. Dress with olive oil and lime juice. Season with black salt and chaat masala to taste. Mix well before serving.

name: Tawa Anda Thecha Pav
instructions: Prepare spicy thecha (chilli and garlic paste). Heat oil in a tawa (griddle) and fry garlic until crispy. Pour beaten eggs and cook until set, stirring occasionally. Add thecha and mix well. Serve hot with pav bread.

name: Solkadhi
instructions: Soak kokum fruit in warm water for 30 minutes. Extract the juice and strain. Mix with coconut milk and curd. Add ground green chilli, turmeric, and salt. Chill and serve as a refreshing drink.

name: Mutton Yakhni Pulao
instructions: Wash and soak basmati rice for 30 minutes. For yakhni: heat ghee, crackle whole spices, add onion-garlic-ginger and mutton, cook 3-4 minutes. Add hot water 1 inch above mutton and pressure cook for 3 whistles on medium flame. Cool and strain broth. For pulao: heat ghee, add cumin, sauté onions until light golden, add cooked mutton and green chillies, stir in whisked curd and garam masala, cook 2-3 minutes. Add yakhni and soaked rice, bring to boil, cover with cloth-wrapped lid, cook on low flame for 15 minutes until rice is done. Rest for 20-30 minutes. Fluff with spatula. Garnish with fried onions, coriander, and mint. Serve with raita.

name: Paneer Chilli Milli
instructions: Blend tomato, cashew, garlic, ginger, and green chilli into fine puree. Shallow fry paneer pieces until light golden and set aside. Heat oil in kadhai, crackle cumin and whole spices, add chopped onions until golden brown. Add garlic and cook 2-3 minutes. Add tomato puree and salt, cook on high flame until oil separates. Add hot water to adjust consistency to semi-thick, add sugar and adjust salt. In separate pan, heat butter and oil, crackle cumin, add french beans and cook 2-3 minutes. Add remaining vegetables and cook on high flame 2-3 minutes. Combine fried paneer and vegetable mixture with gravy, add fresh cream and garam masala. Heat through and serve with butter naan or Indian bread.

name: Vegetable Poha
instructions: Rinse poha gently in sieve with water and let drain, spread on plate to rest 10-15 minutes. Dice all vegetables, keep onion and potato separate. Heat oil in kadhai, add cumin, mustard seeds, green chilli, curry leaves, and peanuts, cook briefly on low flame. Add diced potato and cook on high flame until half done. Add onion and cook until light golden brown. Add remaining vegetables with splash of water, cover and cook on low flame 3-4 minutes. Lower flame, add salt and turmeric, mix in soaked poha with lemon juice, sugar, and coriander. Add splash of hot water, cover and turn off flame. Rest 3-4 minutes. Fluff with fork, garnish with fresh coriander. Optional: serve with sev and pomegranate.

name: Tomato Saar
instructions: Heat oil in a pot, add cumin and mustard seeds, let them crackle. Add crushed green chilli and garlic, cook briefly. Add chopped tomatoes and turmeric powder, cook until tomatoes break down. Add grated coconut and stir well. Add salt and simmer for 10-15 minutes on low flame. The saar should be light, tangy, and can be enjoyed as a soup. Serve warm.

name: Mungodi ki Kadhi
ingredients: kashmiri red chilli 1/4 tsp for tadka
instructions: Whisk gram flour with curd until smooth, avoiding lumps. Heat oil in a pot, add cumin seeds and let crackle. Add gram flour-curd mixture and stir constantly to avoid lumping. Add mangodi, ginger-chilli paste, turmeric, red chilli powder, asafoetida, and salt. Cook on medium flame for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the consistency thickens and oil starts separating from the sides. Prepare tadka by heating oil separately, adding cumin seeds and kashmiri red chilli. Pour tadka over kadhi. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Kesar Pista Kulfi
instructions: In a large bowl, mix milk and half tin of Nestlé Milkmaid until well combined. Add fresh cream, crushed cardamom powder, chopped pistachios, and saffron soaked in warm milk. Stir well until everything is mixed smoothly. Pour the mixture into kulfi moulds or traditional matkas. Freeze for 6-8 hours or overnight until completely set. Demould and serve chilled. Optional: dip briefly in warm water to ease demoulding.

name: Masala Baingan
instructions: Cut brinjal into pieces. Fry in hot oil until golden brown on all sides. Prepare a mixture of yogurt with ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, red chili powder, and salt. Add the fried brinjal to the yogurt mixture. Simmer on medium heat until brinjal is fully cooked and gravy is well incorporated. Garnish with fresh coriander. Serve hot with roti or rice.

name: Rajasthani Dal Dhokli
instructions: Soak arhar dal. Prepare dough with wheat flour, gram flour, salt, turmeric, carom seeds, ginger-chili paste, coriander, and ghee. Roll thin and cut into strips. Cook dal with turmeric and tomato in a pot. When dal is soft, add the dhokli strips and cook until done. In a separate pan, heat ghee and add cumin seeds, then add all spices, ginger, and green chili. Pour this tadka over the cooked dal dhokli. Stir well and serve hot.

name: Baingan Bonda
instructions: Cut boiled potatoes into small pieces and mix with spices (red chili, garam masala, chat masala, green chili, ginger) and coriander. Cut brinjal into thick rings and stuff each ring with the potato mixture. Prepare batter with gram flour, salt, spices, and eno. Dip stuffed brinjal rings in batter. Deep fry until golden and crispy. Prepare chutney by blending coriander, mint, green chili, ginger, black salt, and lemon juice. Serve hot bonda with chutney.

name: Chana Dal Dosa
instructions: Grind rice, chana dal, green chili, ginger, and fenugreek seeds with curd and salt to a thick batter. Add baking soda. Prepare potato filling by tempering mustard and cumin seeds, then adding tomato, spices, potatoes, and coriander. Heat griddle and spread batter thin. Add potato filling or paneer-corn filling when batter starts to set. Fold dosa in half when golden. Prepare chutney with roasted chickpeas, coconut, chili, ginger, and lemon juice. Serve hot dosa with chutney.

name: Rajasthani Atta Malpua
instructions: Crush cardamom pods and soak saffron in warm milk. Mix wheat flour with milk powder. Gradually add milk to form a thick batter, similar to pancake batter. Add a pinch of salt. Prepare sugar syrup by dissolving sugar in water with cardamom and saffron. Heat ghee or oil in a shallow pan. Pour batter into small circles and fry until golden brown on both sides. Immediately dip the hot malpuas into the warm sugar syrup, ensuring they are fully soaked. Serve warm garnished with chopped nuts if desired.

name: Rajasthani Mango Pickle
instructions: Wash and dry raw mangoes. Cut into pieces, removing the pit. Grind or lightly crush fennel seeds, fenugreek seeds, black pepper, cardamom, cloves, and red chilies. Mix mango pieces with salt and let rest overnight. Next day, add turmeric powder, black salt, roasted mustard powder, kashmiri chili powder, kalonji, carom seeds, asafoetida, and the ground spice mixture. Toss well to coat all mango pieces. Heat mustard oil until it stops smoking, then cool slightly. Mix oil and vinegar into the mango mixture. Store in sterilized glass jars in sunlight for 2-3 days, stirring occasionally. Pickle is ready to eat after 15-20 days.

name: kulthi dal
instructions: Soak kulthi dal with baking soda for 30 minutes. Boil with water, bay leaf, cinnamon, cardamom, clove, black pepper until soft. In separate pan, heat ghee and temper cumin seeds. Add ginger, green chilli, asafoetida. Mix in cooked dal. Add turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli powder. Add tomato pieces and cook until dal is fully tender. Adjust salt. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.

name: puran poli
instructions: Soak and cook chana dal with turmeric powder until completely soft, about 20 minutes. Drain and mash dal. Mix mashed dal with jaggery, cardamom powder, and nutmeg powder to form filling. Make dough with wheat flour and salt using water. Knead well. Divide into portions. Roll each portion thin, place filling in center, seal edges carefully. Cook on griddle or tawa over medium heat until golden brown spots appear on both sides.

name: capsicum fry
instructions: Dry roast peanuts until golden, set aside. Dry roast all spices (kashmiri chilli, coriander seeds, fennel, cumin, black pepper, clove, sesame seeds, coconut) until fragrant. Grind roasted spices and peanuts to coarse powder. Cut capsicum into thin strips. Heat oil, temper with mustard seeds and curry leaves. Add ginger, green chilli, asafoetida. Add capsicum strips and salt. Stir-fry until capsicum is cooked but still crisp. Add turmeric powder and dry mango powder. Mix in ground spice powder. Toss well. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.

name: methi gathia sev
instructions: Mix gram flour, salt, and baking soda together. Add black pepper powder, ground clove, carom seeds, kasuri methi, and asafoetida. Mix well. Add water gradually to form a stiff dough, similar to making sev. Load dough into sev maker. Heat oil in deep pan until medium-hot. Press sev maker to extrude long strands directly into hot oil. Fry until golden and crispy, about 2-3 minutes. Remove with slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Cool completely before storing in airtight container.

name: besan atta laddu
instructions: Heat ghee in heavy-bottomed pan. Add wheat flour slowly, stirring constantly. Toast wheat flour until golden and fragrant, about 5-7 minutes. Remove and cool slightly. In same pan, add remaining ghee and gram flour. Toast gram flour similarly until golden, about 5-7 minutes. Cool completely. Mix roasted wheat flour and gram flour with milk powder. Add raisins and melon seeds. Mix in boora thoroughly while mixture is still warm. Form into laddus while warm, using your palms. Cool completely and store in airtight container in cool, dry place.

name: rice modak
instructions: Soak basmati rice for 30 minutes, drain. Heat milk in heavy pan, add rice, cook on medium heat until rice absorbs milk and becomes soft, about 20 minutes. Add saffron strands soaked in warm milk. Stir in ghee, roasted almonds, cashews, and dessicated coconut. Mix in crushed jaggery and cardamom powder. Cook for 2-3 more minutes until jaggery melts and mixture is well combined. Cool slightly until handling temperature. Shape into modak using hands or modak mold by placing filling inside and sealing gently. Garnish with almond and pistachio flakes. Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: lemon chutney pickle
instructions: Wash and cut lemons into thick pieces, discard seeds. Dry roast all whole spices (black pepper, clove, cumin, fenugreek seeds, cardamom) until fragrant. Grind to fine powder. In a clean glass jar, combine lemon pieces with sugar. Add ground spice powder, black salt, turmeric powder, ginger powder, asafoetida, and red chilli powder. Mix thoroughly. Cover jar and let ferment in sun for 15-20 days, stirring occasionally. Once spices dissolve and flavors blend well, it is ready to use. Store in glass container away from direct sunlight. Can keep for more than a year.

name: matar khus khus curry
instructions: Soak poppy seeds for 15 minutes. Grind poppy seeds with tomato, green chilli, and ginger to smooth paste. Heat oil in pan, add cumin seeds, coriander sticks, bay leaf, cinnamon, cardamom, clove. Add the ground paste and cook until oil separates, about 3-4 minutes. Add turmeric powder and coriander powder. Add peas and salt. Pour water as needed to make desired gravy consistency. Simmer for 10-12 minutes until peas are tender. Add red chilli powder and amchur powder. Stir in cream and kasuri methi. Cook for 1-2 more minutes. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.

name: Makhana Paag
instructions: Roast makhana in ghee until golden. Toast dry fruits separately. Add to a base of thickened milk with sugar and cardamom. Simmer until flavors combine.

name: Siddu
instructions: Prepare dough with wheat flour, salt, sugar and yeast. Let rise. Make filling with roasted walnuts, ginger, green chilli and spices. Roll dough, fill with walnut mixture, fold and steam in a covered vessel for 15-20 minutes.

name: Gobi Manchurian
instructions: Cut cauliflower into florets and paneer into cubes. Make batter with refined flour, corn starch, ginger, chilli and salt. Deep fry cauliflower and paneer until golden. In a separate pan, heat oil and stir fry ginger, then add vegetables. Add sauces, vinegar and corn starch slurry. Toss fried vegetables in sauce.

name: Cheese Filled Capsicum Pakora
instructions: Hollow capsicum rings carefully. Insert cheese into each ring. Make batter with besan, chana dal flour and spices. Heat oil for deep frying. Dip cheese-filled capsicum in batter and deep fry until golden and crispy.

name: Sooji Fara
instructions: 1. Make stuffing by boiling soaked chana dal and grinding with green chillies, ginger, coriander and seasonings. 2. Prepare semolina dough with water, salt and minimal oil. 3. Roast onion, tomatoes and green chillies, then blend into chutney with ginger and salt. 4. Make tari sauce by tempering oil with cumin and asafoetida, add torn red chillies, tomato puree and spice powders. Simmer and finish with butter. 5. Shallow fry chana dal stuffed fara patties until cooked. 6. Assemble: place fried fara in the tari sauce, add butter and tadka of oil with cumin, mustard, fennel seeds, red chillies, green chillies and curry leaves. 7. Serve hot with coriander and lemon.

name: Mooli Paratha
instructions: Peel and grate radish, squeeze out water. Knead wheat flour with carom seeds, salt and oil into soft dough. Mix grated radish with masala spices and fresh coriander for filling. Roll dough thin, add radish filling, roll and cook on griddle with ghee until both sides are golden and fluffy.

name: Milk Cheese Pudding
instructions: Boil milk and reduce slightly. Mix corn flour with little milk to make a smooth paste. Add paste to boiling milk and stir continuously. Add sugar and dissolved cheese slices. Cook until thick and creamy. Add crushed cardamom and ghee. Pour into dish, allow to cool and set.

name: Beetroot Kanji
instructions: Cut beetroot and carrot into chunks. Layer in a jar with mustard seeds, fenugreek, red chilli, salt and turmeric. Add water to cover. Cover jar with cloth and ferment for 4-5 days at room temperature until tangy. Strain and use as needed.

name: Rice Dhokla
instructions: Mix rice flour, curd, chopped green chilli, ginger, salt, and sugar powder. Add Eno just before steaming. Heat oil with mustard seeds and curry leaves until seeds pop. Pour the batter into greased idli molds, steam for 10-12 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean. Unmold and serve hot with the seasoned oil drizzled on top.

name: Soya Nuggets Potato Pakora
ingredients: coriander chutney - coriander 1 cup, mint 1/2 cup, green chilli 2, ginger 1/2 inch, lemon juice 1/2, cumin 1/2 tsp, black salt 1/2 tsp, salt 1/4 tsp
instructions: Soak soya chunks in hot water for 15 minutes, squeeze dry, and chop finely. Boil and mash potato. Mix gram flour with soaked soya, mashed potato, salt, spices, ginger-chilli, herbs. Add water as needed to form a thick batter. Heat oil to 180°C. Drop spoonfuls of batter and deep fry until golden brown on all sides, about 5-6 minutes per batch. For chutney: blend coriander, mint, green chilli, ginger, lemon juice with cumin and black salt until smooth. Serve pakora hot with chutney.

name: Vermicelli Kheer
instructions: Roast cashews, almonds, and pistachios in 1 tbsp ghee until golden, set aside. Roast vermicelli in remaining 1 tbsp ghee until lightly golden, about 3-4 minutes. Heat milk in a heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat. Add roasted vermicelli to milk and cook, stirring occasionally, for 8-10 minutes until vermicelli softens and milk reduces slightly. Add sugar and stir until dissolved. Soak saffron in warm milk and add to kheer. Remove from heat and stir in cream and cardamom powder. Top with roasted nuts and raisins. Serve warm or chilled.

name: Peda
instructions: Grind roasted chana into a fine powder. Mix chana powder, boora, and milk powder in a bowl. Add crushed cardamom and mix well. Add cream and 2-3 tbsp ghee, mixing until the mixture comes together into a dough-like consistency. Grease palms with remaining ghee and shape small portions (about 1 tbsp each) into oval or round peda shapes. If the mixture is too dry, add more cream; if too wet, add more milk powder. No cooking needed. Store in an airtight container for up to 2 weeks.

name: Raw Mango Moong Dal Paratha
instructions: For dough: Mix wheat flour, salt, 1 tsp oil with water to form a soft dough. Let rest 20 minutes. For filling: Soak moong dal for 2 hours, cook until just tender but not mushy, drain. Finely chop raw mango, mint, green chilli, ginger. Heat 2 tbsp oil, add cumin seeds until they pop, add asafoetida, cooked moong dal, chopped mango-mint-chilli mixture, turmeric, red chilli, coriander and cumin powder, salt. Cook on high heat for 2-3 minutes stirring constantly until dry. Cool. Divide dough into 8 balls, roll each into a thin circle, place 2 tbsp filling in center, seal edges, gently flatten with hands. Cook on hot griddle with ghee until golden spots appear on both sides, about 3 minutes per side. Serve hot.

name: Jackfruit Pulao
instructions: Cut jackfruit into small bite-sized pieces, soak in turmeric and 1 tsp salt for 15 minutes. Heat 3 tbsp oil, add cumin seeds, let them splutter. Add bay leaf, cinnamon, black pepper, clove, cardamom. Add jackfruit pieces and stir-fry on high heat for 4-5 minutes. Add chopped tomato, green chilli, ginger paste, turmeric, remaining salt, coriander powder, red chilli powder, pav bhaji masala, kasuri methi, garam masala, amchur. Cook on medium heat for 3-4 minutes until jackfruit is semi-cooked. Wash basmati rice, add 2 tsp oil, cook rice separately in a cooker or pot with measured water (1:2 ratio). Add cooked rice to jackfruit mixture, add 1 tbsp ghee, mix gently with a fork. Garnish with ginger julienne and fresh coriander. Serve hot.

name: Bhakarwadi
instructions: Make dough: Mix refined flour, salt, carom seeds, add oil and water gradually to form a soft dough. Knead well and cover for 20 minutes. Prepare filling: Dry roast gram flour in a pan for 2-3 minutes until fragrant. Separately crush cumin seeds, fennel, coriander seeds, sesame, clove, black pepper, black cardamom coarsely. Mix roasted gram flour, crushed spices, grated coconut, turmeric, dry ginger powder, salt, red chilli powder, asafoetida, and sugar in a bowl. Add 1 tbsp oil to filling and mix well. Divide dough into 4 balls. Roll each ball very thin into a square, spread 2 tbsp filling evenly, tightly roll into a log, coil the log into a spiral, flatten gently, pierce with fork. Deep fry on medium heat until golden brown and crispy, about 6-7 minutes. Drain on paper towel. Cool and store in an airtight container.

name: Jowar Muthia
instructions: Squeeze excess moisture from grated gourd using a clean cloth. Mix sorghum flour, gram flour, salt, red chilli powder, turmeric, coriander-cumin powder, ginger-chilli paste, carom seeds, asafoetida, squeezed gourd, and green coriander in a bowl. Add curd and mix well. Add baking soda just before steaming and mix quickly. Grease a steamable mold or heatproof bowl with 1 tsp oil. Fill 3/4 full with muthia mixture. Steam in a pressure cooker or steamer for 8-10 minutes until a toothpick inserted comes out clean. Unmold onto a plate. For tempering: Heat 2 tsp oil, add mustard seeds until they pop, add sesame seeds and curry leaves, pour over steamed muthia. Serve hot with green chutney or yogurt.

name: Badam Pak
instructions: Roast almonds and grind to a coarse powder. Crush cardamom and long pepper. Melt jaggery with ghee on low heat. Add ground almonds, cardamom, long pepper, and dry ginger powder. Stir continuously until the mixture becomes thick and dry. Spread on a greased surface, let cool, and cut into pieces. Garnish with almond flakes.

name: Chhena Kofta Curry
instructions: Make chhena by boiling milk with vinegar and straining. Mix chhena with boiled potato, salt, pepper, green chilli, ginger, and coriander. Form small koftas and roll in refined flour. For gravy, sauté cashews in oil and ghee with whole spices. Add ginger paste and green chilli. Cook with cream and curd. Season with salt and sugar. Gently add chhena koftas to the gravy and simmer. Finish with butter.

name: Gajar Ki Kheer
instructions: Cook rice in water until soft. Peel and grate carrots. Heat ghee and cook grated carrots until soft. Add milk and bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes until thick and reduced. Fry cashews, almonds, and raisins in ghee separately. Add sugar and crushed cardamom to kheer. Mix in fried dry fruits. Cook for another 2-3 minutes and serve warm.

name: Mushroom Soup
instructions: Slice mushrooms and capsicum finely. Heat butter in a pot and sauté mushrooms and capsicum. Add ginger paste and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add 4 cups of water, salt, and black pepper. Bring to boil. Mix corn flour with a little water and add to the soup to thicken. Simmer for 10 minutes. Finish with fresh lemon juice and serve hot.

name: Matar Bedmi Poori
instructions: Grind green peas with ginger and green chilli into coarse paste. Mix with semolina, wheat flour, salt, carom seeds, cumin seeds, red chilli, turmeric, and fenugreek leaves. Add oil and knead into stiff dough. Let rest for 15 minutes. Roll into thick rounds and deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy on both sides.

name: Instant Poha
instructions: Heat oil and temper with mustard seeds, green chilli, curry leaves. Mix in peanuts and poha. Add turmeric powder, salt and sugar. Pour hot water and mix well. Serve immediately garnished with fresh coriander.

name: Kapanya Pare
instructions: Dissolve jaggery in water and let cool. Mix wheat flour, gram flour, nutmeg, ginger powder and salt. Add dissolved jaggery and oil to form stiff dough. Knead well. Roll thin and cut into small pieces. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Spread on cloth to cool. Store in airtight container for up to 3 weeks.

name: Gajar Halwa
instructions: Peel and grate carrots finely. Heat ghee in heavy-bottomed pan, add grated carrots and roast for 2 minutes. Pour milk gradually and cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally. When carrots are tender and milk reduces to 1/4 cup, add chopped dates, raisins, almonds, cashews, grated coconut and cardamom powder. Add jaggery and mix well. Cook for another 2-3 minutes until thick. Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Raw Mango Papad
instructions: Wash and chop raw mangoes into pieces. Blend mangoes with mint leaves into smooth paste. Mix in sugar, salt and black salt. Spread mixture thin on cloth or mat, about 1/4 inch thick. Place in sunny location to dry completely for 2-3 weeks, turning occasionally. Once fully dried and papery, break into pieces and store in airtight container. Keeps for 6+ months.

name: Kesariya Meethe Chawal
instructions: Wash basmati rice. Heat 1 tsp ghee, add dry coconut, cashews, almonds, raisins and roast lightly. Remove and keep aside. In same pan, add remaining ghee and turmeric powder, add washed rice and roast for 1 minute. Add water and cook until rice is tender. Powder cardamom and add with sugar to cooked rice. Mix well and garnish with roasted nuts and coconut. Serve hot.

name: Kacche Gosht Ki Biryani
ingredients: whole spices (bay leaf, cinnamon, cloves, cardamom)
instructions: 1. Marinate mutton in yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chilli powder, turmeric, and salt for 1-2 hours. 2. Soak basmati rice and boil with whole spices until 70% cooked. 3. Layer marinated mutton and par-cooked rice in a heavy-bottomed pot, alternating layers. 4. Scatter fried onions, mint, and coriander on top. 5. Cover with foil and lid, seal edges with dough. 6. Cook on high heat for 2 minutes until steam forms, then reduce to low heat and cook for 45 minutes (dum cooking).

name: Basic Cookies
instructions: Cream butter and sugar together. Add eggs and vanilla, mix well. In separate bowl, combine flour, baking powder and salt. Mix dry ingredients into wet mixture. Scoop dough onto baking sheet in desired shapes. Bake at 375°F for 10-12 minutes until golden.

name: Mango Sticky Rice
