instructions: Cook sticky rice until tender and allow to cool slightly. Warm coconut milk with sugar-free sweetener and salt. Arrange cooked sticky rice on serving plate. Slice fresh ripe mangoes and arrange around rice. Pour warm sweetened coconut sauce over rice and mango. Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Til Gur Laddoo
instructions: Dry roast white sesame seeds on medium heat for 3-4 minutes until fragrant, stirring constantly. Transfer to a plate. Crush black cardamom finely. Roast dry nuts (almonds, peanuts, pistachios, cashews, walnuts) separately for 2-3 minutes until crispy. Coarsely chop roasted nuts. In a pan or kadhai, add roasted sesame seeds. Add crushed jaggery and ghee. Mix well on low heat until jaggery melts and everything combines. Remove from heat when still slightly warm. Add cardamom powder and chopped nuts. Mix thoroughly. Wet hands with water and form small laddoos (balls). Press gently and place on a tray. Cool completely before storing.

name: Methi Paratha
instructions: Blanch and finely chop methi leaves. Mix with wheat flour, minced garlic, green chilli, onion, cumin seeds, and spices. Knead into soft dough with ghee/oil. Rest 15 minutes. Roll into thin circles. Cook on tawa with ghee until golden brown on both sides. Serve hot.

name: Moong Dal Paratha
instructions: Grind moong dal with skin coarsely so small pieces remain visible (not smooth paste). In large bowl, mix whole wheat flour with ground moong dal. Add cumin, kasoori methi, salt, turmeric, chilli powder, asafoetida, ginger, green chilli, onion, mango pickle masala, and mustard oil. Add fresh coriander. Mix all dry ingredients. Gradually add water and knead to make soft, pliable dough. Rest for 15 minutes covered. Divide into 8 equal portions. On floured surface, roll each into 7-8 inch circle, about 1/4 inch thick. Heat oil in tawa or skillet on medium-high heat. Place rolled paratha on hot oil. Press gently and cook until light brown spots appear, about 2 minutes. Flip and cook second side similarly. While cooking, press gently with spatula to encourage even cooking and slight puffing. Apply oil on edges, flip, and cook again for 30 seconds on each side. Press gently with spatula to flatten slightly. Remove to plate when fully cooked with golden spots.

name: Lahsun Adrak Mirch Achar
instructions: Wash and chop green chilies, garlic, and ginger finely. Heat mustard oil in a clean jar. Add all seeds and hing, let them splutter. Add chopped ingredients with turmeric, aamchur, and salt. Mix well and let it sit for few minutes until it cools. Pickle is ready to serve immediately and can be stored in airtight container.

name: Chicken Ghee Roast Masala
instructions: Cut chicken into medium pieces. Grind or crush coriander, cumin, and fennel seeds. Heat oil and ghee, add curry leaves and chopped onion until brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook. Add chicken pieces and cook on high heat for 5 minutes. Add turmeric, red chilli powder, and ground spices. Mix well. Cover and cook on low-medium heat for 45-50 minutes until chicken is tender and oil separates on top. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.

name: Moong Dal Kachori
instructions: Prepare dough with wheat flour, ghee, salt, and water. Let rest 15 minutes. For stuffing: Heat oil, add cumin and fennel seeds, then add moong dal with all spices and hing. Cook 3-4 minutes until aromatic. Cool completely. Divide dough into balls, stuff with moong filling, seal edges. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and puffed. Drain on paper towels and serve hot with chutney.

name: Gajar Mirch Achar
instructions: Cut carrots and green chili into thin matchsticks or small pieces. Heat oil in a pan, add mustard seeds and let them splutter. Add chopped carrots and green chili, cook for 2-3 minutes on high heat. Add turmeric powder, coriander powder, hing, and salt. Mix well and remove from heat. Add lemon juice and mix. Cool completely and transfer to airtight container. Ready to serve immediately.

name: Amla Achar
instructions: Wash and prick amlas with a fork multiple times. Heat mustard oil in a jar, add all spices and let them season the oil for 1 minute. Add pricked amlas and salt, mix gently but thoroughly. Cover and let it sit for 1-2 minutes until cooled. The pickle is ready to serve immediately and improves in taste over time. Store in airtight container away from direct sunlight.

name: Cheese Stuffed Schezwan Mushroom
instructions: Clean mushrooms and remove stems. Finely chop onion, capsicum, and garlic. In a bowl, mix cheese, mixed herbs, oregano, chilli flakes, garlic, and schezwan sauce. Stuff this mixture into mushroom caps. Heat butter in a pan or oven-safe tray. Place stuffed mushrooms, stuffing side up, and cook on medium-high heat for 5-7 minutes until golden and cheese melts. Alternatively, bake in preheated 180°C oven for 12-15 minutes. Serve hot.

name: Punjabi Sarson da Saag
instructions: Blanch sarso ka saag, palak, and bathua for 2 minutes and drain. Grind garlic, ginger, green chili into a paste. Heat ghee in a heavy pot, add chopped onion and cook until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Add blanched greens and cook on high heat for 5 minutes stirring often. Add turmeric, red chilli powder, salt, and maize flour slurry. Reduce heat to medium and cook for 25-30 minutes stirring occasionally until greens soften completely and oil separates on top. Serve hot with makki di roti or bhature.

name: Kala Chana Uthappam
instructions: Soak black chana overnight or for 8 hours. Grind soaked chana with garlic, ginger, green chili, cumin seeds, salt, and minimal water into coarse paste (should be thicker than regular batter). Just before cooking, add baking soda and mix gently. Heat oil on griddle or non-stick tawa on medium-high heat. Pour batter and spread into round shape (like dosa/pancake). Cook until bottom is golden (2-3 minutes), flip carefully, cook other side. Add topped with chopped onion, tomato, green chili, and coriander. Cook another 1-2 minutes. Serve hot with chutney or sambar.

name: Tomato Soup
instructions: Roughly chop tomatoes and carrot. Sauté onion and garlic in butter. Add tomatoes, carrot, bay leaf, and cinnamon stick. Simmer until soft (15 minutes). Strain or blend for creamy texture. Season with salt and black pepper. Serve hot.

name: Veg Thai Curry
instructions: Grind coriander seeds, cumin seeds, lemongrass, bird eye chilli, thai ginger, kaffir lime leaf, garlic, and coriander stems into curry paste. Heat oil in large pot and fry onions until golden. Add curry paste and cook 2-3 minutes. Add cut vegetables (capsicum, zucchini, mushroom, broccoli, carrot). Pour in coconut milk and water. Simmer until vegetables are tender (10-15 mins). Season with salt and black pepper. Garnish with thai basil and serve.

name: Rajma
instructions: Soak rajma overnight and pressure cook until soft (15-20 mins). Heat mustard oil and add cumin seeds. Add onions and cook until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook 1 minute. Add tomatoes and cook until soft. Add all dry spices (turmeric, red chilli, coriander, cumin, garam masala, black pepper). Cook 2 minutes. Add cooked rajma with water. Add bay leaves and black cardamom. Simmer 5-10 minutes. For tadka: heat ghee, add chopped garlic and green chilli, pour over rajma. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Moringa Paratha
instructions: Blanch moringa leaves briefly and chop finely. Mix wheat flour with blanched moringa, minced garlic, chopped green chilli, turmeric, red chilli powder, sesame seeds, and salt. Knead with ghee to form soft dough. Rest 15 minutes. Roll into thin circles. Cook on tawa with ghee until golden brown on both sides. Serve hot with yogurt or pickle.

name: Lauki Rawa Dhokla
instructions: Grate lauki and onion, chop capsicum and green chilli finely. Mix rawa with curd and water. Add all vegetables, coriander leaves, and salt. Mix well and let sit 10 minutes. Grease steaming pan with oil and pour batter evenly. Sprinkle sesame seeds. Steam 15-20 minutes until cooked through. For tadka: heat oil, add mustard seeds and let crackle, add sesame seeds and curry leaves. Pour over cooked dhokla. Cut into pieces and serve.

name: Methi Besan Sabzi
instructions: Wash and chop methi leaves finely. Mix besan with minced garlic, chopped methi, tomato, and all dry spices. Heat oil in large pan and add besan-methi mixture. Cook on high heat 10-15 minutes, stirring frequently to prevent burning and ensure even cooking. Mix should look crumbly and dark brown when ready. For tadka: heat ghee, add chopped garlic and red chilli powder, infuse for 30 seconds and pour over cooked sabzi. Serve hot with roti or rice.

name: Dark Chocolate Orange Cake
instructions: Cream together eggs and maple syrup. Add vanilla essence. Mix flour with baking powder and salt, fold into egg mixture alternately with melted dark chocolate and orange juice. Add orange zest and berries. Pour into prepared pan and bake until golden.

name: Matar Ka Fara
instructions: Prepare dough with flour, salt and water. Prepare filling by mixing boiled peas with chopped green chilli, garlic, salt and black pepper. Divide dough into portions, flatten, fill with pea mixture and seal. Flatten into bread shape. Heat oil, add mustard seeds, cumin and hing. Fry the stuffed breads until golden. Sprinkle prepared tadka on top.

name: Weight Loss Upma
instructions: Heat oil, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds and urad dal. When they crackle, add green chilli and curry leaves. Add all vegetables and sauté. Add sooji and roast for 1-2 minutes. Pour water slowly while stirring to avoid lumps. Cover and cook until sooji is cooked and water is absorbed. Add lemon juice, garnish with coriander and cashews.

name: Punjabi Pyaz Paratha
instructions: Prepare soft dough with atta, salt, oil and water. Slice onions thinly and spread to dry slightly. Mix onions with kashmiri chilli powder, cumin, coriander seeds, green chilli, ginger, coriander powder, chaat masala, fresh coriander and black salt. Divide dough into 6 balls. Roll each thin, brush with oil, add onion mixture, add more oil and flour. Fold and roll to create layers. Cook on hot griddle with oil until golden and crispy, pressing gently to form layers.

name: Punjabi Til Bhugga
instructions: Roast sesame seeds lightly on medium heat without browning completely. Grind half the sesame seeds coarsely and keep other half whole. Grind sugar to powder. Heat khoya gently to soften. Mix roasted sesame (ground and whole), powdered sugar, and softened khoya together. Add crushed black cardamom. Let cool slightly, then shape into round balls or tikkis by applying ghee on hands. Garnish with almonds and sesame seeds. Serve at room temperature.

name: Soya Keema Pav
instructions: 1. Soak soya keema and chunks in water, then boil until tender. 2. In a pan, heat oil and butter together. 3. Add cumin seeds and let them crackle. 4. Add chopped ginger and green chillies, cook for 30 seconds. 5. Add chopped garlic and onions, sauté until golden. 6. Add turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, and coriander powder. 7. Add fresh tomato puree and simmer. 8. Mash boiled potato and add to the masala. 9. Add capsicum and the boiled soya mixture. 10. Season with salt, chaat masala, and pav bhaji masala. 11. Add water and simmer for 5-10 minutes until thick. 12. Finish with kasoori methi and fresh coriander. 13. Toast pav bread with butter. 14. Serve hot with sliced onions, tomatoes, lemon wedges, and butter on the side.

name: Lauki Paneer Chilla
instructions: 1. Boil diced lauki with water and ginger until soft. 2. Mash the boiled lauki. 3. In a bowl, mix besan with suji, salt, chilli powder, turmeric, cumin powder, chaat masala, kasoori methi, and ajwain. 4. Add water to make a smooth batter. 5. Add chopped green chilli and ginger to the batter. 6. In a separate pan, heat oil and add cumin seeds. 7. Add chopped green chilli and ginger to oil. 8. Add boiled green peas and grated carrots. 9. Season with salt and pepper powder, chaat masala. 10. Add grated paneer and fresh coriander to make the filling. 11. On a non-stick pan or tawa, spread a thin layer of chilla batter. 12. Add the paneer-vegetable filling in the center. 13. Fold and press gently to make a chilla. 14. Drizzle oil around and cook until both sides are crispy and golden. 15. Serve hot with chutney or yogurt.

name: Burnt Basque Cheesecake
instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 240°C. 2. Line a 6-inch round pan with butter paper. 3. In a mixing bowl, combine cream cheese and fine grain sugar. 4. Add vanilla flavouring. 5. Beat in eggs one at a time (for egg version). 6. In a separate bowl, mix all-purpose flour and cornstarch. 7. Gently fold the flour mixture into the cream cheese mixture. 8. Pour in unsweetened cream and mix well. 9. Pour the batter into the prepared pan. 10. Bake at 240°C for 30 minutes. 11. The top should be slightly burnt and caramelized. 12. Remove from oven and let cool. 13. Refrigerate for at least 5 hours before serving. 14. Serve chilled, optionally with berries or toppings.

name: Saag Paneer
instructions: 1. Sort and clean mustard leaves, spinach, bathua, methi, and radish leaves. 2. Cut leaves roughly and turnip into pieces. 3. Boil leaves with water, ginger, green chillies, and chana dal until very soft (approximately 20-25 minutes). 4. Drain and blend the boiled greens into a smooth paste. 5. In a heavy-bottomed pan, heat ghee and add cumin seeds. 6. Let cumin crackle. 7. Add chopped ginger and green chilli, sauté briefly. 8. Add chopped garlic and onions, cook until golden. 9. Add chopped paneer pieces and fry until lightly golden on all sides. 10. Add turmeric, chilli powder, and coriander powder. 11. Pour in the green paste and mix well. 12. Season with salt to taste. 13. Simmer for 5-10 minutes until the curry thickens slightly. 14. Finish with butter and cream. 15. Garnish with fresh coriander if desired. 16. Serve hot with roti or rice.

name: Watermelon Panna
instructions: 1. Cut watermelon into chunks. 2. Blend watermelon chunks with their seeds in a mixer grinder. 3. Strain the blended watermelon to extract juice. 4. Pour the watermelon juice into a heavy-bottomed pan. 5. Add sugar and kashmiri chilli powder to the juice. 6. Bring to a boil and cook over medium heat. 7. Scrape the sides of the pan while cooking. 8. Reduce the juice by 50% (from 6 cups to approximately 3 cups). 9. In a separate small pan, dry roast cumin seeds and black peppercorns without oil. 10. Lightly crush the roasted spices on a flat surface. 11. When the watermelon juice is reduced and cooling, add fresh mint leaves with stalks. 12. Remove from heat and let cool completely. 13. Remove mint leaves from the stalks using a simple pulling motion. 14. Add salt, black salt, and fresh lemon juice to taste. 15. Add the roasted and crushed cumin-pepper mixture and stir well. 16. Pour into a glass bottle for storage. 17. Keep refrigerated and use within 7-10 days. 18. To serve: dilute concentrate with water, add ice cubes, mint leaves, and lemon wedges.

name: Dahi Wala Paneer
instructions: 1. Cut paneer into 2-inch cubes. 2. Marinate paneer with salt, turmeric, and chilli powder. 3. Heat mustard oil in a pan until it smokes well. 4. Deep fry the marinated paneer cubes until golden on all sides. 5. Remove and set aside on a paper towel. 6. In a mixer grinder, add curd, cashewnuts, ginger, onion, garlic cloves, green chillies, and black peppercorns. 7. Blend into a smooth paste. 8. In a heavy-bottomed pan, heat oil and add black cardamom. 9. Let it splutter for a few seconds. 10. Add kalonji seeds and whole cloves. 11. Add a slit green chilli and let it infuse. 12. Add turmeric and kashmiri chilli powder. 13. Pour in the cashew-curd paste and stir well to combine. 14. Add water and bring to a boil. 15. Reduce heat and simmer, stirring occasionally. 16. Add coriander powder and mix well. 17. Season with salt to taste. 18. Add the fried paneer cubes to the curry. 19. Simmer for 5-10 minutes. 20. Finish with a pinch of garam masala, mint powder, and kasoori methi powder. 21. Add fresh coriander and a dash of chilli oil. 22. Serve hot with roti, naan, or rice.

name: Mango Falooda
instructions: 1. To make Rabri: Pour 1 liter of full-fat milk into a heavy-bottomed pan. 2. Bring milk to a boil over medium heat, stirring occasionally. 3. Reduce heat and let it simmer, stirring every few minutes to prevent sticking. 4. Continue cooking until milk reduces to about 1/4 of its original volume (approximately 250 grams). 5. Add sugar and cardamom powder while the milk is still warm. 6. Add rose water and mix well. 7. Let the rabri cool completely. 8. To make Falooda: In a separate pan, mix mango puree, vanilla custard powder, and powdered sugar. 9. Add water and whisk to remove lumps. 10. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until thick and creamy. 11. Pour the hot falooda mixture into an ice bath (ice cubes with chilled water). 12. Let it set briefly, then pass through a falooda machine or piping bag to create the characteristic strands. 13. Collect the falooda strands in chilled water. 14. Bloom sabja seeds by soaking in water for 5-10 minutes. 15. To assemble: In tall glasses, add a base layer of rose syrup. 16. Add a layer of mango falooda strands. 17. Add bloomed sabja seeds. 18. Add diced mango pieces. 19. Pour chilled rabri on top. 20. Add a scoop of vanilla ice cream. 21. Garnish with tutti frutti and chopped nuts (almonds, cashews, pistachios). 22. Serve immediately while cold.

name: Mango Puran Poli
instructions: Prepare filling by cooking dal with jaggery and spices. Make dough with maida and ghee. Roll dough, fill with mango-dal mixture, seal edges. Flatten into circular poli. Cook on tawa until light brown spots appear, applying ghee on both sides until cooked through.

name: Cabbage Kofta Curry
instructions: Mix cabbage with grated vegetables, spices, and besan to form dough. Deep fry spoonfuls into koftas until golden. Separately, temper oil with hing and spices. Sauté onions with ginger garlic paste. Add tomato puree and curd. Simmer to create gravy base. Add fried koftas and cook on low heat for 5 minutes. Garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Methi Matar Pulao
instructions: Heat oil and add cumin seeds, cloves, and black pepper until they crackle. Add chopped onions and cook on high heat until transparent. Add chopped ginger, slit green chillies, and minced garlic. Stir well. Add roasted kasuri methi leaves. Pour water (2 cups per 1 cup rice) and bring to boil. Add salt and rice, mix gently once. Keep flame high until water level reaches rice. Turn flame to low, add peas, cover with lid. Simmer until water is absorbed completely. Let steam for 10 minutes before opening lid.

name: Aam Panna Popsicle
instructions: Peel and cut raw mangoes into thick pieces. Add to pressure cooker with 500ml water. Add sugar and close lid. Pressure cook for 2 whistles. Cool completely. Transfer to blender with soaking water. Add roasted cumin, chilli powder, black salt, peppercorns, fresh mint leaves, and optional food colour. Grind until smooth. Pour into popsicle molds, leaving 1cm space for expansion. Freeze until solid (overnight or 6-8 hours). Demold and serve.

name: Turai Ki Sabji
instructions: Peel ridge gourds and cut into thick chunks. Heat mustard oil and fry amritsari wadi until brown, remove and set aside. Add mustard seeds, cumin, and fennel seeds to the same oil. Add roughly chopped onions and cook on high heat for 3-4 minutes. Add chopped garlic and green chillies. Stir in turmeric, red chilli powder, and coriander powder. Roast spices well. Add tomato puree and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add water (1/4 cup), then add ridge gourd pieces. Add salt and broken wadi pieces. Cover and simmer on low heat until turai is soft. Finish with garam masala, kasoori methi, and fresh coriander.

name: Kesar Badam Phirni
instructions: Boil 1 liter full fat milk. Soak and peel 25 almonds, grind with 5 cardamom pods into smooth paste with a dash of milk. Add almond paste to boiling milk and stir continuously. Soak 4 tbsp rice for 30 minutes, then grind coarsely with a pulse mode. Add ground rice to the boiling milk while stirring constantly to prevent lumps. Add saffron strands. Cook for 10-15 minutes until mixture thickens to creamy consistency. Add sugar after rice is fully cooked. Stir well and pour into clay pots or bowls. Chill in refrigerator for at least 2 hours before serving.

name: Watermelon Coconut Barfi
instructions: Cut watermelon, remove skin and seeds. Blend flesh with seeds and filter to get juice. Reduce watermelon juice by half over medium heat. Grate ginger finely with a dash of water. Add sugar to reducing juice. Once volume reduces by half and color becomes concentrated, add ginger juice and whole mint sprigs. Simmer for 5 minutes, then remove mint. Mix cornstarch with 5 tbsp water to form slurry. Add to watermelon mixture while stirring continuously to avoid lumps. Cook until thick and transparent. Add rose water, vanilla essence, and food colour. Pour into lined 7x7 inch tray. Cool completely, then chill in refrigerator. Cut into squares and optionally dip edges in desiccated coconut.

name: Chocolate Oats and Honey Bar
instructions: Mix 3 cups oats with 3/4 cup chopped assorted nuts in a bowl. In separate bowl, mix 60g melted butter with 1/4 cup peanut butter and 1/4 cup honey. Add cinnamon powder to oat mixture. Combine wet and dry ingredients thoroughly. Press tightly into a 10x8 inch rectangular mold lined with parchment paper. Chill in freezer for 30-45 minutes. Remove from mold, trim edges, and cut into bars using a warm knife. Melt 300g dark chocolate. Dip bars into melted chocolate, coating 3/4 of each bar. Place on parchment paper and let set.

name: Karela Ki Sabzi
ingredients: salt (1 tsp for marination, to taste for cooking)
instructions: Wash bitter gourds and cut into thin slices, removing seeds if tough. Marinate with salt and lemon juice, let rest in sun or room temperature for 30 min. Squeeze out bitter water and wash under running water to remove extra bitterness. Heat mustard oil in pan, add mustard seeds, cumin and fennel seeds, let them crackle. Add green chillies, then bitter gourd slices. Cook on high heat for 10 min until tender and water evaporates. Add turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder, salt, black salt and chaat masala. Stir well and lower heat. Add chopped onions for moisture and sweetness. Cover and simmer for 5-7 min on low heat. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and finish with a spoonful of ghee for aroma.

name: Dal Wale Chawal
instructions: For pulao: Heat ghee, add asafoetida, black cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, peppercorns, and cumin seeds. Crackle mustard seeds and add raw onions (don't brown). Add slit green chillies. Sauté chana dal for 2-3 min. Add salt and boil water. Add soaked basmati rice to boiling water with a light stir. When water level reaches rice, lower heat and cover. Cook on lowest flame until water dries, then rest 10-12 min without opening lid. For chutney: Heat oil with cumin and fennel seeds, add garlic, ginger, peanuts. Roast peanuts until they crack. Add both types of green chillies and roast with exhaust fan on. Cool and grind with coriander leaves, lemon juice, salt, black salt, chaat masala, and cold water until smooth.

name: Pani Puri
instructions: For puris: Mix semolina with oil and lukewarm water to form soft dough, rest 2-3 min and knead well. Divide into 5-6 gram pieces, roll thin with oil, cut into circles. Deep fry on medium-high heat, press gently so they puff up like balloons. Drain and store in airtight container. For aam panna pani: Boil raw mangoes with water until soft (2 whistles in cooker). Cool and grind with mint, coriander, green chilli, peppercorns, chilli powder, cumin powder, salt, black salt and sugar. Strain through cloth. Add ice and water, then boondi. For watermelon pani: Extract and strain watermelon juice. Cook with kashmiri chilli powder and sugar until reduced by 50% (from 6 cups to 3 cups). Turn off heat, add mint leaves and cool. Add salt, black salt, roasted cumin, pepper powder. Cool completely. Add ice and lemon juice before serving. To serve: Fill puri with potato and chickpeas, dip in either spicy (mango) or sweet (watermelon) water and eat immediately.

name: Moong Dal Uttapam
instructions: Blend skinless moong dal with ginger, green chillies, curry leaves, cumin seeds, peppercorns and little water until smooth. Transfer to bowl and add gram flour, semolina, curd, salt, sugar and baking soda. Add water to get thick pancake batter consistency. Let rest briefly. For chutney: Grind coconut, tamarind, green chilli, ginger, peppercorn, cumin and curry leaves with water into smooth paste. Heat oil for tempering and add asafoetida, dry red chilli, cumin, mustard seeds, urad dal, chana dal and curry leaves until they splutter. Add to chutney and mix well. Pour chutney into serving bowl. To make uttapam: Heat griddle with oil, pour batter to form thick pancake. Immediately add chopped capsicum, tomato, onion and coriander as toppings. Cook until bottom is golden and crisp, then flip and cook other side. Serve hot with coconut chutney.

name: Thandai Rasmalai
instructions: For thandai masala: Heat pan on high heat, add almonds, cashews, pistachios, cardamom, cinnamon, peppercorns, fennel seeds, watermelon seeds, poppy seeds, nutmeg and dried rose petals. Heat until moisture dries but do not brown. Add saffron, cool completely, grind in pulse mode to coarse powder (avoid overgrinding to prevent nuts releasing oils). Store in fridge up to 1 month. For chena: Boil cow milk, turn off heat and let cool 2 min while stirring. Add diluted vinegar slowly while stirring lightly until milk separates. Add cold water and squeeze out water properly. Rub chena with light hands to make it smooth and creamy (don't over-rub). For rasmalai: Mash chena well, shape into small round discs (22 gms each). For syrup: Heat water and sugar together, skim off impurities. Check one-thread consistency without forming two-thread (thick but no threads). Add rasmalai to boiling syrup and dissolve flour in water, sprinkle into syrup to create bubbles that cook rasmalai evenly. Cook 15 min on high heat, add water if syrup thickens, until rasmalai puffs up. For flavored milk: Boil 500 ml milk with sugar, add rose water, saffron and thandai masala. Cook 2-3 min. Cool completely. Place half sugar syrup in pan, add rasmalai, add equal amount of cold water to thin syrup, let soak min 1 hour. Add rasmalai to cold flavored milk, garnish with chopped nuts and thandai masala. Refrigerate min 3 hours before serving.

name: Suji Ka Sandwich
instructions: For batter: Mix semolina with curd, salt, water and oil. Add asafoetida, mustard seeds, ginger, curry leaves and coriander. Add baking soda and rest briefly before use. For stuffing: Heat oil and add dry red chilli, mustard seeds, urad dal, chana dal and cumin until they splutter. Add chopped green chilli and ginger, sauté. Add chopped onion and turmeric, cook until onion softens. Mix in mashed potato with salt and chopped coriander. Set aside and cool. To make sandwiches: Heat griddle with oil. Pour thin batter layer on hot griddle like a crepe. When bottom is cooked, add 2-3 tbsp of potato mixture in center, fold sides inward like a sandwich or roll. Cook until golden on both sides. Serve hot with chutney.

name: Matar Ke Kabab
instructions: Blanch fresh green peas in boiling salted water for 2-3 min until tender. Add spinach leaves briefly. Cool under running water immediately. Grind peas and spinach with minimum water until coarse (some grains should remain). Heat oil, add asafoetida and kalonji seeds. Add ginger paste and chopped green chillies, sauté. Add green peas paste and mix well. Add salt, black salt, cumin powder, coriander powder, chaat masala and kasuri methi. Sauté until mixture becomes thick and holds together (10-15 min of cooking). Spread on plate to cool quickly. Mix cooled peas mixture with mashed potato, salt, chaat masala, chopped green chilli and mint leaves. Form golf ball sized portions. With oiled hands, flatten each portion, place small piece of mozzarella cheese in center, and seal by bringing edges together. Shape into round, smooth kebab. Heat ghee or oil in pan on medium-high heat. Carefully place kebabs and cook gently until golden on all sides (do not use high heat as they are delicate). Serve hot with mint chutney.

name: Mooli Ki Sabzi
instructions: Wash, peel and dice the radishes into small cubes. Chop radish leaves finely (discard thick stems). Heat mustard oil in pan. Add asafoetida and ajwain seeds, let them splutter. Add slit green chillies. Add diced radishes and season with salt, turmeric and red chilli powder. Sauté for 1 minute. Add chopped radish leaves and stir well to combine. Cover pan and cook on low to medium heat for 10-12 minutes until radishes become tender. Sprinkle chaat masala. Remove lid and stir-fry for another 2-3 minutes to let excess moisture evaporate. Serve hot with roti or paratha.

name: Achaari Paratha
instructions: Knead a tight dough with flour, salt, and water. Rest for 10-15 minutes. Chop pickles into coarse paste. Flatten dough, spread pickle paste evenly, cut into 2-inch strips, roll tightly with light dusting of dry flour to create layers. Rest for 10-15 minutes. Heat oil in pan, flatten rolled dough gently to 1/8-inch thickness, cook on medium heat until layers separate. Flip and cook second side. Apply oil/ghee to both sides while pressing lightly. Cook until golden and crispy with visible layers.

name: Egg Roast Masala
instructions: Hard boil eggs for 12 minutes, peel, and keep aside. Heat oil in pan on medium heat. Add mustard seeds and let them splutter. Add dry red chillies, fennel seeds, black peppercorns, and curry leaves. Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant. Add sliced onions and sauté until translucent. Add chopped green chillies, ginger, and garlic. Sauté until raw aroma disappears. Add turmeric, Kashmiri chilli powder, and coriander powder. Mix well. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft and mushy. Season with salt and add garam masala. Mix well. Drop boiled eggs into gravy and cook for a few minutes to absorb flavors. Garnish with curry leaves and fresh coriander.

name: Methi Malai Matar
instructions: Make curry base: Heat oil and add cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, black pepper, and cumin. Add sliced ginger and garlic, sauté until golden. Add chopped onions and cook until golden brown. Add tomatoes and cook until soft. Add turmeric, chilli powder, and coriander powder. Cook until oil separates. Add 2 cups water and simmer for 15 minutes. Blend or pass through sieve. Heat in pan with butter and 1.5 cups water. Prepare methi: Heat butter in separate pan with garlic, ginger, green chilli. Add fresh methi leaves and sauté. Add green peas and cook for 3-4 minutes. Season with salt, chilli powder, coriander powder, and garam masala. Mix curry base into sautéed methi and peas. Simmer for 5 minutes. Stir in cream just before serving.

name: Thai Chicken Satay
instructions: Make curry paste: Blend pearl onions, red chillies, galangal, garlic, lemongrass, turmeric, coriander powder, cumin powder, salt, 2 tbsp jaggery, and 100ml water to smooth paste. Marinate chicken: Cube or cut chicken into pieces, mix with salt, soya sauce, and 3 tbsp curry paste. Marinate for 30 minutes. Thread chicken onto wooden skewers. Heat 4 tbsp oil and cook satay skewers on grill or pan until cooked through and lightly charred. Make peanut sauce: Heat 1/3 cup oil, add 4 tbsp curry paste and fry for 2 minutes. Add 2 tsp chilli powder (optional), 4 tbsp tamarind water, 2 tbsp jaggery, and 2 cups water. Simmer for 5 minutes. Grind roasted peanuts coarsely, add to sauce, season with salt, and simmer for 3 minutes. Serve satay with peanut sauce on the side, accompanied by cucumber, onion, and lemon wedges.

name: Churros with Strawberry Chocolate Sauce
instructions: Make churro dough: Boil water with 1 tbsp sugar and 5 tbsp butter. Remove from heat, add 1 cup flour and 1/2 tsp cinnamon. Mix until dough forms. Cool slightly. In separate bowl, beat 3 eggs. Gradually add eggs to dough with 1 tsp vanilla extract. Transfer to piping bag with large star nozzle. Heat oil for frying. Pipe 4-5 inch long dough directly into hot oil, fry until golden on both sides. Mix 4 tbsp sugar with 1 tbsp cinnamon powder. Toss fried churros in cinnamon sugar while warm. Make sauce: Puree 5 strawberries with 1.5 tbsp sugar. Heat 1 cup milk with vanilla flavoring and cornstarch slurry. Add pureed strawberries, white chocolate, and pink food colour. Stir until chocolate melts. Serve churros warm with strawberry chocolate sauce and chocolate sprinkles.

name: Tinde Ka Bharta
instructions: Brush tindas lightly with oil. Roast over open flame or on pan on medium-high heat, turning frequently until skin is charred and soft, about 8-10 minutes. This imparts smoky flavor. Cover roasted tindas in bowl for 5 minutes to create steam, making peeling easier. Peel off charred skin under running water using hands. Roughly chop peeled tindas. Heat mustard oil in kadhai on high heat. Add asafoetida, dry red chilli, and punjabi vadi (if using). Let vadi cook slowly. Add cumin seeds. Add garlic and let it brown slightly. Add chopped onions, ginger, and green chilli. Sauté on high heat for 3 minutes until onions become translucent. Stir in turmeric, chilli powder, and coriander powder. Add chopped tomatoes and salt. Sauté for 5 minutes until tomatoes soften and oil separates. Add chopped tinda pieces and mix well. Add 1/2 cup water, lower flame, cover, and cook for 5-7 minutes until tinda is cooked and flavors merge. Add kasoori methi and fresh coriander. Mix and serve hot with roti.

name: cabbage ka nashta
instructions: Mix chopped cabbage, onion, capsicum, carrot with all dry spices, besan, semolina, and water to form a batter. Heat tawa or griddle, pour batter to form pancakes, shallow fry until golden on both sides. For chutney: blend tamarind water, mint, fresh coriander, ginger, garlic, green chilli, salt, black salt, chaat masala, and roasted cumin into smooth paste. Serve hot pancakes with tamarind mint chutney.

name: paneer lababdaar
instructions: Make tomato puree by blending tomatoes, green chilli, and cashew nuts. In a handi, heat oil and butter, add bay leaf and sliced onions, fry until golden. Add chopped ginger-garlic, cook until oil separates. Add chopped tomatoes and kashmiri chilli powder, cook until soft. Pour in tomato puree and simmer until thick. In another pan, heat butter, add ginger and slit green chillies, add paneer cubes and salt. Add the prepared masala to paneer, pour water, and bring to boil. Add kasoori methi, garam masala, and fresh coriander. Simmer on low heat for 3-4 minutes. Turn off heat and add cream. Serve hot.

name: chicken shawarma bao
ingredients: oil, 1 tbsp plus 1 1/2 tbsp for cooking
instructions: For dough: dissolve yeast and sugar in milk and water, add flour and knead into semi-soft dough, let ferment. Divide into balls for roti and bao. For roti: roll thin and cook on tawa. For bao: make thick dough rounds, fold and steam for 10-12 minutes. For chicken: heat oil, add tomato puree and ghee, cook 2-3 minutes. Add green chilli, coriander stems, garlic; cook until oil separates. Add chicken, salt, red chilli powder, coriander powder, curd; cook 7-8 minutes medium heat. Finish with oregano and chilli flakes. For mayo: grind garlic and mayonnaise together. For salad: mix tomatoes, onion, green chilli, coriander, lemon juice, salt. Assemble shawarma: spread mayo on roti, add salad, chicken, roll and pan-fry in butter. Assemble bao: fill cooked bao with mayo, salad, chicken; garnish with coriander, chilli oil, ketchup. Serve hot.

name: laxmi gatte ki sabji
ingredients: oil, 2 tbsp plus 3-5 tbsp for cooking
ghee, 1 tbsp plus 1 tbsp for frying
degi red chilli powder, 1 tsp plus 2 tbsp
instructions: For dough: mix besan, salt, curd, turmeric, and oil into semi-soft dough; apply oil and rest. Roll thin roti, spread stuffing evenly, roll like swiss roll, place on greased plate, steam 10-12 minutes; cut into desired shapes and fry in ghee-oil until golden. For stuffing: heat ghee, roast cashew nuts, sesame seeds, carom seeds, peanuts. Add red chilli powder, turmeric, asafoetida, mango powder, coriander powder; cook 2-3 minutes on low flame. Add jaggery and tamarind pulp, cook 4-5 minutes, add salt and crushed black pepper. For curry: heat oil in kadai, add bay leaf and temper with fenugreek, fennel, cumin, mustard seeds, asafoetida, dry red chillies; let splutter. Add chopped ginger and coriander stems, sauté 1 minute. Add curd-spice mixture (beaten curd mixed with spice powders), cook 4-5 minutes on medium heat. Add water to adjust consistency, bring to boil, add tomato and fried gatte, simmer. Finish with remaining ghee and oil, season with salt. Garnish with coriander and serve hot with roti or rice.

name: masoor dal snacks
ingredients: whole masoor dal (soaked overnight), 3 cups plus 1/4 cup
oil, 1 tbsp plus 1.5 tbsp for tadka
instructions: For paste: soak whole masoor dal overnight, blend with garlic, green chilli, ginger, semolina, salt, curd, and water into smooth paste. For tikki: mix boiled potatoes, soaked dal, coriander leaves, red chilli powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, curry leaves, salt, green chilli, onion, and oil; form into tikki shapes and shallow fry until golden; serve with chutney. For tadka: heat oil, splutter mustard seeds and cumin seeds, add curry leaves and dry red chillies, cook 1 minute. For dhokla: mix prepared tadka, batter, lemon juice, baking soda, oil; pour into greased tray, top with coriander leaves, steam 15-20 minutes, demold and cut into squares. For dosa: heat nonstick tawa, season with oil, pour ladleful of batter, spread evenly, apply butter, cook 3-4 minutes medium heat until cooked. For appe: take appe chetti, add butter to cavity, pour spoonful of batter, cook until golden. Serve all variations hot with green chutney or tomato ketchup.

name: thalapakatti dindigul chicken biryani
instructions: For paste: grind garlic, ginger, green chilli, shallots, fresh coriander into smooth paste. For masala: toast coriander seeds, fennel, cumin, peppercorns, star anise, green cardamom, black cardamom, cloves, mace, cinnamon, cashew nuts; grind with red chilli powder and coriander powder into fine powder. For biryani: heat oil and ghee in handi, add mint leaves and sauté 1 minute. Add sliced onions and fry until translucent. Add prepared paste and cook until oil separates. Add chicken, salt, curd and cook 4-5 minutes on medium heat. Add prepared masala powder and ghee, cook 1 minute. Add water, green chillies, coriander leaves, mint leaves and bring to boil. Add soaked rice, salt, and ghee; mix well. Cover with lid and cook on low heat for 15-20 minutes until rice is cooked. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with mint and coriander sprigs. Serve hot with prepared salad (tomato, onion, green chilli, coriander, salt, curd mixed together).

name: Walnut and Dates Halwa
instructions: 1. Boil water and add walnuts, cook for 4-5 minutes until half-cooked. 2. Strain and keep walnuts aside. 3. In a kadai with ghee, add soaked dates and cook for 10-12 minutes until mushy. 4. Add boiled walnuts, ghee, orange juice and salt, cook until ghee separates from the halwa. 5. Transfer to serving bowl and garnish with sliced dates, pistachios, cardamom powder, walnut pieces and silver vark. 6. Serve hot or warm.

name: Saag Chicken
instructions: 1. Make paste by grinding coriander leaves, spinach, green chillies and tomato. 2. Marinate chicken with red chilli powder, mango powder, salt, ginger-garlic paste and mustard oil. 3. Dry roast peppercorns, cloves, black cardamom, coriander seeds, fenugreek seeds, cumin seeds, mace and green cardamom; grind to make garam masala. 4. Heat mustard oil until smoking, add cloves and black cardamom. 5. Add chopped onions and sauté until golden. 6. Add prepared spinach paste and cook 2-3 minutes. 7. Add beaten yogurt and cook 5-6 minutes. 8. Add marinated chicken and cook until tender. 9. Add garam masala, smoke with coal and ghee (dum pukht), then simmer until oil separates. 10. Finish with fenugreek leaves and ghee. 11. Serve with roti or rice.

name: Paneer Tikka Samosa
instructions: 1. Marinate paneer slabs with salt, red chilli powder and lemon juice for 15 minutes. 2. Make second marination with mayonnaise, yogurt, gram flour, spices and ginger-garlic paste; coat paneer and refrigerate. 3. Prepare dough with refined flour, carom seeds, oil and salt; knead and rest. 4. Dry roast cumin, fennel seeds, peppercorns and black cardamom with salt; grind to make dry masala. 5. Fry marinated paneer pieces in oil until cooked. 6. Mix cooked paneer with peas, green chillies, coriander, nylon sev and dry masala to make filling. 7. Prepare samosa by wrapping filling in dough rounds with slurry seal. 8. Fry samosas in oil until golden and crispy. 9. Serve hot with chutneys.

name: Khasta Kachori Aloo Chaat
ingredients: 1 heaped tsp degi red chilli powder
instructions: 1. Make dough with refined flour, salt, carom seeds, oil and water; knead and let rest. 2. Prepare dal stuffing by cooking urad dal paste in oil with cumin seeds, add asafoetida, turmeric, red chilli powder, fenugreek leaves and mango powder; cool. 3. Prepare aloo chaat by tempering oil with cumin and fennel seeds, add boiled potatoes with coriander, turmeric, chilli and pepper powders, asafoetida, sugar and mango powder. 4. Divide dough into thin rotis, spread dal stuffing evenly between two layers, cut desired shapes and fry in oil until golden and crispy. 5. Assemble chaat: layer sweetened yogurt, aloo chaat, green chutney, tamarind chutney and top with fried kachori. 6. Garnish with pomegranate pearls, red chilli powder and coriander. 7. Serve hot.

name: pahadi chaounsa dal
instructions: Wash whole urad dal thoroughly and dry roast in a kadai for 15-20 minutes on low flames. Add ghee and roast for another 4-5 minutes. Cool and grind coarsely. Cook the roasted dal powder with water in a covered handi for 30-35 minutes on medium flames until cooked well. Prepare a paste by grinding ginger, garlic, green chillies, and coriander leaves. Dry roast cumin seeds and sesame seeds together until golden, then grind to fine powder. Mix paste with chopped radish, roasted seeds powder, and lemon juice to make chutney. In another handi, heat oil and add cumin seeds, garlic, dry red chillies, chopped ginger, coriander stems, and asafoetida. Cook for a minute, add water and the cooked dal. Simmer for 10-12 minutes on low flames. Finish with dry mango powder and ghee. Serve hot with steamed rice.

name: mughlai chicken
ingredients: ginger 1.5 inch peeled and roughly chopped
degi red chili powder 1 heaped tbsp
instructions: Marinate chicken with salt, dry fenugreek leaves, degi red chili powder, ginger-garlic paste, and oil. Mix beaten curd with turmeric powder, degi red chili powder, cumin powder, and coriander powder. Heat oil and ghee in a handi, add green cardamom, bay leaf, cloves, cumin seeds, ginger, garlic, and green chillies. Saute for a minute then add diced onions and cook for 4-5 minutes until golden. Add diced tomato, salt, soaked cashew nuts, and curd mixture. Cook for 7-8 minutes until well blended. Add butter and water, then grind everything into a smooth paste. Sear marinated chicken in butter on both sides for 4-5 minutes. Add seared chicken to the gravy with water and cook until done. Prepare tadka: heat ghee, add diced onion, green chillies, coriander stems, fenugreek leaves with water and boil. Add tadka to gravy along with kewra water, rose water, and fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot with roti or naan.

name: chicken ghotala
ingredients: chicken breast 900g cut into large cubes
degi red chili powder 1 heaped tbsp
green chillies 5-6 less spicy and chopped
instructions: Marinate chicken breast with salt, dry fenugreek leaves, degi red chili powder, ginger-garlic paste, and oil. Heat oil in a handi, add cumin seeds, then sliced onion and cook for 2-3 minutes until translucent. Add beaten curd, degi red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until oil separates from the masala. Add fenugreek leaves, marinated chicken, and salt. Cook for 10-12 minutes on medium flames until chicken is cooked. Mash the cooked chicken with a masher and set aside. In another handi, heat oil with cumin seeds. Add chopped ginger, finely chopped onions, and green chillies. Cook until onion is translucent. Add ginger-garlic paste and mix well. Add coriander powder, degi red chili powder, turmeric powder and cook for a minute. Add water, salt to taste, and finely chopped tomatoes. Let cook until tomatoes are mushy. Add coriander leaves, water, and mashed cooked chicken. Boil briefly. Finish with ghee, fresh cream, and crushed fenugreek leaves. Serve hot with ladi pav.

name: fish cutlet
ingredients: green chillies 3 less spicy and finely chopped
ginger 1 inch peeled and finely chopped
boiled potatoes 3 medium peeled and mashed
fresh coriander leaves 2-3 tbsp finely chopped
instructions: Marinate fish pieces with lemon juice, ginger-garlic paste, salt, degi red chili powder, and turmeric powder for 4-5 minutes. Heat oil in a pan and sear marinated fish for 7-8 minutes until cooked well. Transfer to a tray. In a pan with remaining fried oil and ghee, add green chillies and chopped ginger, saute for a minute. Add boiled and mashed potatoes, fresh coriander leaves, and salt. Cook for 2-3 minutes on medium flames. Transfer to a bowl and add shredded fish (deboned, deskinned), egg yolk, black pepper powder, and garam masala. Mix well. Divide into lemon-size balls, shape as desired, and coat with breadcrumbs. Fry cutlets in oil until golden in color. For salad: mix sliced onion, julienned carrot, sliced cucumber, black pepper powder, mango pickle, and fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot cutlets with salad, kasundi, and tomato ketchup.

name: Mumbai Street Style Veg Frankie
instructions: Make dough with flour and water, rest for 30 minutes. Blanch and mash potatoes with salt and spices. Mix shredded vegetables with salt and spices. Roll dough thin, fill with potato and vegetable mixture, add coriander and lemon. Fold and roll as frankie. Cook on tawa with oil until golden and crispy on both sides. Serve hot.

name: Healthy Kaala Chana Soup
instructions: Soak chickpeas overnight or use pre-soaked chickpeas. Grind ginger, garlic, and green chili into fine paste. In pressure cooker, add soaked chickpeas, roughly cut tomato, salt, all spice powders, and water. Pressure cook for 4-5 whistles on high heat, then place on lowest flame for cooking. Let depressurize naturally. Open and stir well. Bring to simmer on high flame. Add fresh coriander and mint leaves. Adjust seasoning. Serve hot.

name: Kolkata Phuchka
instructions: Make maida dough with water and salt, rest 30 minutes. Divide into small balls, flatten and deep fry until golden and crispy puris. Soak tamarind in hot water for 5-6 minutes, cool and blend with salt and sugar to make sweet tamarind water. Boil and mash potatoes with salt and spices. Assemble: cut small opening in puri, fill with spiced potato filling, pour tamarind water, sprinkle bhaja masala and herbs. Serve immediately while hot.

name: Bhajji Pav
instructions: Make besan batter with gram flour, salt, ajwain, and water to thin-but-not-runny consistency, rest 30 minutes. Dry roast garlic, green chili, and coriander stems on high flame until charred, add cumin seeds and oil, roast briefly. Cool and grind into coarse thecha paste with fresh coriander and salt. Make instant tamarind chutney by boiling tamarind in water 5-6 minutes, cool and blend with sugar and salt. Fry besan batter separately to make crispy chura, grind with fried garlic and red chili powder for dry chutney. Slice potatoes thin on mandoline, coat in batter, deep fry until golden crisp. Assemble bhajji pav: spread thecha on pav, add tamarind chutney, layer hot bhajjis, top with dry garlic chutney and fried green chilli. Serve hot.

name: Punjabi Kheer with Jaggery Puda
instructions: Soak rice for 15-20 minutes, discard water. Add soaked rice to pressure cooker with milk, cook on high for exactly 5 minutes until pressure builds, then place on lowest flame for 30 minutes. Let depressurize naturally. Stir well and bring to boil on high flame. Add condensed milk and cook until desired consistency (if serving chilled, keep slightly thin as it will thicken). Add slivered almonds. For puda: dissolve jaggery in hot water with saffron, cool to room temperature. Mix wheat flour with fennel seeds, cardamom, black peppercorns, and salt. Gradually add jaggery water to flour mixture, whisk to smooth lump-free batter, rest 30 minutes. Heat tawa, add ghee, pour small ladle of batter to form circular puda. Cook on medium-high flame until crisp on one side, flip and cook other side until crispy. Serve hot pudas with chilled kheer.

name: Tomato Macaroni Soup
instructions: In a pan, add oil and all base ingredients (onion, tomato, chili, garlic, coriander stems, salt). Heat on high flame until tomatoes soften, add hot water, cover and cook on medium-low flame until tomatoes become completely mushy, stirring occasionally. Cool completely and transfer to mixer jar with water. Blend into fine puree and strain. Add puree to kadhai or saucepan with hot water to adjust consistency (keep slightly thin), add black pepper, oregano, black salt, ketchup, and sugar. Stir well, bring to boil and simmer for a couple of minutes. Add macaroni pasta along with all diced vegetables (carrot, beans, capsicum, corn). Stir well and cook for 8-10 minutes until pasta is cooked and vegetables are tender. Taste and adjust seasoning and consistency as needed. Add lemon juice and fresh coriander. Serve hot.

name: Punjabi Chicken Curry
instructions: Cut onions and tomatoes finely. Heat ghee in a large pan and sauté onions until golden brown. Add garlic and ginger, cook for 1 minute. Add tomatoes and salt, cook until soft. Toast whole spices (jeera, bay leaves, cardamom, cinnamon) in ghee separately until fragrant. Add spices to the onion-tomato base. Add turmeric, red chili powder, and garam masala. Cook masala for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add marinated chicken (with salt, turmeric, red chili, lemon juice). Cook in pressure cooker for 5-7 minutes. Open cooker, add water to achieve curry consistency. Simmer on medium flame for 10-15 minutes. Add fresh coriander and kasuri methi. Serve hot.

name: Vegan Ramen
instructions: Simmer onion, carrot, celery, ginger, garlic, button mushrooms, shiitake mushrooms, and apple in water for 1 hour. Strain broth carefully and season with salt. Cook noodles separately until springy. Marinate tofu in salt and prepare chili oil by blending dried chili, shallots, garlic with oil. Grill marinated tofu in cast iron until marked. Roast corn on direct flame. Blanch pak choy briefly. Make tare sauce by mixing soy sauce, powdered sugar, and chili oil. Assemble: pour hot broth into bowl, add noodles, top with grilled tofu, pak choy, corn, and spring onions. Drizzle with chili oil and serve hot.

name: Pav Bhaji Toast Sandwich
instructions: Boil potatoes, peas, and carrots until soft. Mash together with some ghee. Heat butter in pan, sauté onions and tomatoes until soft. Add garlic, ginger, and cook briefly. Add mashed vegetables to the mixture. Add pav bhaji masala, red chili powder, turmeric, and salt. Cook for 5-10 minutes until well combined. Prepare sandwich chutney and red garlic chutney separately. Toast bread slices until golden and crispy. Spread chutney on toast, top with pav bhaji mixture, cover with another toast slice, and press gently. Serve hot with extra chutney on the side.

name: Vegetable Sweet Corn Soup
instructions: Boil sweet corn for 4-5 minutes until kernels float to surface. Drain and divide into two equal portions. Blend one portion with water into fine puree. Heat kadhai on high heat. Add sweet corn puree with hot water and bring to boil. Simmer on medium-high heat for 7-8 minutes, removing scum that floats to surface. Add salt, sugar, white pepper powder, vinegar, and light soy sauce. Add diced carrot, chopped french beans, and diced capsicum. Cook for 4-5 minutes. Mix corn flour with water and add to soup to thicken (optional). Add diced paneer cubes. Garnish with chopped spring onion greens. Serve hot.

name: Sprouted Moong Appe
instructions: Chop ginger, green chili, curry leaves, carrot, french beans, capsicum, and onion using a chopper. Heat ghee in pan, add chana dal and urad dal, cook for a few seconds until color changes. Add asafoetida, mustard seeds, and cumin seeds, cook briefly. Add chopped vegetable mixture with salt and cook on high flame for 2-3 minutes. Lower flame, add turmeric powder, kashmiri red chili powder, and garam masala, cook briefly. Transfer to bowl and let cool completely. Blend green moong sprouts with fresh coriander, mint, ginger, green chili, black pepper powder, cumin seeds, salt, and water into thick batter. Mix cooled vegetable mixture with batter. Adjust consistency to resemble idli batter. Heat appe pan on high heat and grease lightly with ghee. Pour batter into molds, cover, and cook on medium-low flame for 5-6 minutes. Flip appe using spoon or toothpick, add ghee if desired, cover and cook for 2-3 minutes more until golden brown. Remove and serve with chutney.

name: Vegetable Upma
instructions: Roast upma rawa in 1 tsp ghee over medium-low flame for 3-4 minutes while stirring continuously. Transfer to bowl and let cool. Cut all vegetables into small dice. Fry cashews and peanuts in ghee until light golden brown, set aside. In same kadhai, add mustard seeds and let them crackle. Add urad dal and chana dal, cook on low flame until golden. Add ginger, green chili, and onion, cook on high flame until onion turns translucent. Add curry leaves and stir well. Add all vegetables and cook on high flame for 2-3 minutes. Measure and add 3 cups water with salt, sugar, and black pepper. Bring to rolling boil. Add roasted rawa while stirring continuously to avoid lumps. Cook on medium-high flame while stirring until no separate water is visible. Cover and cook on low flame for 4-5 minutes. Remove lid and stir after 2 minutes. Open lid and stir, then cover again and switch off flame. Let rest for 5 minutes. Add fried nuts, lemon juice, fresh coriander, and remaining ghee. Stir well and serve hot.

name: Chicken Tikka Rice
instructions: Soak basmati rice for 20-30 minutes. Mix chicken with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, green chili, turmeric, kashmiri red chili powder, garam masala, salt, and lemon juice. Marinate for 15 minutes minimum. Soak saffron in hot water. Dice carrot, capsicum, and french beans finely. Heat ghee in large pot, add whole spices (bay leaves, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, cumin, peppercorns) and let them crackle. Add diced onion and cook until golden. Add marinated chicken and cook for 5-7 minutes until partially cooked. Add drained rice and stir gently to coat with ghee. Add vegetables and salt. Pour 3 cups water, bring to boil, cover tightly and cook on medium heat for 15-18 minutes until rice is cooked and chicken is tender. Add saffron soaked in water and mix gently. Cook covered for another 2 minutes. Remove from heat, let rest for 5 minutes. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot with garlic sauce on the side.

name: Chicken 65 Dum Biryani
instructions: Soak basmati rice for 30 minutes. Cut chicken into medium pieces. Marinate chicken in yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, green chili, kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, cumin powder, coriander powder, salt, and lemon juice for minimum 1 hour. Heat oil in deep fryer to medium-high temperature. Deep fry marinated chicken pieces until golden brown and cooked through (chicken 65). Set aside and drain on paper towel. Slice onions thinly. Heat ghee in wide pan and deep fry sliced onions until crispy brown. Reserve fried onions. In same pan, add whole spices (cumin, bay leaves, black cardamom, green cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, peppercorns, mace, nutmeg) to ghee and let crackle. Boil water in large pot with salt and spices, add soaked rice and cook until 70% done. Drain rice. Soak saffron in hot milk. In heavy-bottomed biryani pot or large kadhai, create layers: fried onions, chicken 65, mint, coriander, ginger, coconut, then parboiled rice. Repeat layers, finishing with rice on top. Sprinkle saffron soaked in milk over top. Cover pot with aluminum foil and place lid on top (dum cooking). Cook on high heat for 2 minutes until steam forms, then reduce to low heat and cook for 45-50 minutes. Turn off heat and let rest for 5 minutes without opening lid. Gently open lid and mix biryani with fork, being careful not to break rice grains. Serve hot garnished with extra fried onions and fresh mint.

name: Tomato Basil Rice with Grilled Paneer
instructions: Soak rice for 13 minutes. Prepare basil marinade and coat paneer cubes. Cook marinated paneer separately. Prepare tomato-basil rice by tempering and cooking rice with tomato reduction and basil. Grill the marinated paneer cubes until golden. Fluff the rice and plate together with grilled paneer.

name: Aloo Pyaaz Paneer ki Sabzi
instructions: Make coarse paste of onion, tomato, garlic, chilli, and turmeric. Temper ghee with whole spices and add the paste with coriander and chilli powder, cook 2 minutes covered. Shallow fry paneer cubes until golden, then potatoes until light golden, then onions. Add whisked curd to gravy over low flame. Combine fried vegetables with gravy, adjust consistency with hot water, and cook 3-4 minutes. Garnish with garam masala, kasuri methi, and coriander.

name: Rasmalai Tres Leches Cake
instructions: Prepare a sponge cake batter without eggs using baking soda and curd. Cook the batter in a greased pan over medium heat until set. Pierce the cooked cake and drench with a mixture of condensed milk and evaporated milk. Top with chilled rasmalai payasam and crushed cardamom before serving chilled.

name: Corn Capsicum Masala
instructions: Chop onions, tomato, ginger, garlic, and green chilli. Temper ghee with whole spices, add chopped onions and cook golden. Add ginger-garlic-chilli paste and continue cooking until onions are deep golden. Add powdered spices with splash of water, then add tomatoes, curd, and cashew paste; cook until ghee separates. Adjust gravy consistency with hot water, add corn and cook 4-5 minutes. Add capsicum and cook 2-3 minutes more. Finish with kasuri methi, garam masala, and fresh coriander.

name: Stuffed Mushroom Tikka
instructions: Mix hung curd with marinade spices and add charcoal smoke. Prepare stuffing by mixing grated paneer and cheese with ginger, chilli, mint, coriander, and spices. Scoop mushroom insides to form cup and stuff firmly. Soak bamboo skewers in water, then skewer with onion, capsicum, and stuffed mushroom. Cook using one of three methods: pan-frying with ghee until charred, direct flame with butter basting, or air-fryer at 200°C for 10-12 minutes with butter at 5-6 minute mark. Serve with green chutney, lemon juice, and chaat masala.

name: Sweet Corn Dhokla
instructions: Mix rawa with sour curd, water, sugar, and salt; rest 10-15 minutes. Grind sweet corn with ginger and green chilli into paste. Add corn paste, turmeric, chilli flakes, oregano, and oil to rested batter. Grease dhokla trays. Mix eno with water and add to batter, then pour into trays and tap gently. Steam for 20 minutes until knife comes out clean. Cool slightly then cut into pieces. Temper oil with mustard seeds, green chilli, asafoetida, and curry leaves; pour over dhokla. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve with green chutney.

name: Chilli Chicken
instructions: Cut chicken breast into thin strips against the fibres. Velvet chicken with baking soda and water in circular motions 2-3 times until slimy, then coat with salt, pepper, soy, oil, and corn flour. Heat oil to 165°C and fry chicken in batches until light golden (3-4 minutes); drain in sieve. Boil water and add stock cube for quick stock. Mix corn flour with water for slurry. Heat oil in wok, stir fry ginger, garlic, and coriander stems 2-3 minutes without browning. Add onion and capsicum slices, cook 1 minute. Add stock (250-300ml for dry, 700-800ml for gravy), soy sauces, vinegar, sugar, and pepper. Add fried chicken and fresh coriander, stir well. Gradually add corn flour slurry while stirring until desired consistency. Taste and adjust seasoning; garnish with spring onion greens.

name: Crispy Chicken & Egg Fried Rice
instructions: Marinate chicken with salt and turmeric. Deep fry or pan fry chicken pieces until crispy and golden on all sides. In a separate pan, scramble eggs and keep aside. Fry cooked rice in oil with garlic, ginger, and onions. Add scrambled eggs, soy sauce, vinegar, and green chillies. Toss well and season with salt. Serve fried rice hot with crispy chicken on top and garnish with spring onion.

name: Punjabi Sheera (Besan Ka Sheera)
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan over medium-high heat. Add besan slowly while stirring continuously to avoid lumps. Roast besan in ghee for 10-15 minutes until golden and fragrant. Add water slowly to the roasted besan mixture. Stir well and cook for 5-7 minutes. Add sugar and mix thoroughly. Cook until the mixture becomes thick and pulls away from the sides. Add crushed cardamom, roasted cashews, and raisins. Mix well and serve warm.

name: Chicken Ghee Roast
instructions: Soak byadgi and guntur chillies with cashews in hot water for 1-2 hours. Dry roast coriander seeds, cumin seeds, black peppercorns until fragrant, add poppy seeds and fenugreek seeds briefly. Cool and grind roasted spices with soaked chillies, cashews, garlic, tamarind, and jaggery into a thick paste. Marinate chicken with salt, turmeric, and curd in fridge. Heat ghee in a kadhai, fry marinated chicken over high heat until 90% cooked. Heat ghee on a tawa, add the prepared paste and cook over high heat for 15 minutes, stirring continuously until crumbly and jam-like consistency is achieved. Add ghee in batches while cooking the paste. Once paste is ready with separated ghee, add cooked chicken and cook for 4-5 minutes. Add curry leaves and adjust salt. Serve hot with neer dosa or roti.

name: Grilled Chicken Sizzler
instructions: Preheat sizzler plate. Cook rice with butter. Prepare french fries. Marinate chicken with salt and pepper. Sauté onions until caramelized. Arrange buttered rice and french fries on one side of the sizzler. Grill chicken strips until cooked through. Sauté bell peppers, broccoli, and corn. Arrange vegetables on sizzler. Prepare garlic pepper sauce with minced garlic, pepper, butter and water. Pour hot garlic pepper sauce over everything. Serve immediately on the hot sizzler plate.

name: Veg Manchurian Fried Rice
instructions: Prepare manchurian balls: Mix grated vegetables with cornstarch, ginger-garlic paste and spices. Form balls and deep fry until golden brown. For fried rice: Heat oil in a wok, add garlic and ginger. Add all chopped vegetables and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes. Add cooked rice and break any lumps. Add soy sauce, vinegar, chilli sauce, and tomato sauce. Toss well for 2-3 minutes. Add fried manchurian balls and mix gently. Season with salt and garnish with spring onion and sesame seeds. Serve hot.

name: Veg Korean Corn Dog
ingredients: mozzarella cheese block 200g cut into sticks
instructions: Prepare yeast mixture by mixing lukewarm water, sugar, and yeast. Let sit 5-10 minutes until foamy. Mix yeast water with refined flour to form sticky batter. Cover and let rise until doubled in volume. Peel and dice potatoes, blanch in boiling water for 3 minutes, drain and cool. Cut mozzarella into rectangular sticks and thread on bamboo skewers. After batter rises, set up coating station with diced potatoes, panko breadcrumbs, batter and cheese skewers. Heat oil to 175°C. Dip cheese skewer in batter to coat fully. Immediately roll in diced potatoes pressing lightly. Coat completely in panko breadcrumbs. Carefully drop into hot oil and fry until golden brown. Drain on wire rack. Sprinkle salt and caster sugar. Drizzle with mayonnaise, mustard and ketchup. Serve hot.

name: Paneer Changezi
instructions: Blanch tomatoes in boiling water for 10 minutes, cool and peel. Grind peeled tomatoes into coarse puree. Heat oil in kadhai, add chopped onions and cook until golden brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and continue cooking until onions turn dark golden. Add all powdered spices with hot water and cook until oil separates. Add tomato puree and salt, cover and cook base gravy for 15-20 minutes. Marinate paneer pieces with curd, ginger-garlic paste, green chillies, spices and lemon juice for 30 minutes. Grill marinated paneer pieces until light golden brown on both sides. Heat oil on a tawa, add base gravy with whisked curd and fresh cream. Cook for 10-12 minutes until gravy becomes crumbly. Add hot water to adjust consistency and cook 5-6 minutes more. Add grilled paneer, green chillies, chaat masala and kasuri methi. Cook 2-3 minutes. Garnish with fresh coriander and ginger julienne. Serve hot with tandoori roti.

name: Tomato Bharta
instructions: Set papad jali over medium-high flame. Place tomatoes on jali, brush mustard oil and roast directly over flame until fully charred and soft. Transfer to bowl and cover for a few minutes. Dry roast green chillies, garlic and coriander stems in a pan for 4-5 minutes until lightly charred. Cool completely and grind into thick coarse paste with minimal water. Peel charred tomatoes and chop roughly without mashing completely. Heat mustard oil in a kadhai until it starts smoking, then cool slightly. Add cumin seeds, mustard seeds, curry leaves, prepared paste and onions. Cook over high flame until onions turn soft. Lower flame and add turmeric powder and kashmiri red chilli powder, stir immediately. Add chopped tomatoes with salt, black salt, and sugar. Cook for 2-3 minutes. Taste and adjust salt if needed, add garam masala and fresh coriander. Serve hot with bhakri or roti.

name: Bael Ka Sharbat
instructions: Cut open the bael fruit and extract the pulp. Blend pulp with water until smooth. Strain through a fine sieve to remove fibers. Add sugar, lemon juice and salt to taste. Add crushed cardamom for flavor. Mix well. Chill thoroughly and serve over crushed ice. Can add more water based on desired consistency.

name: Green Moong Sprouts Dosa
instructions: Blend all dosa batter ingredients (sprouts, ginger, green chilli, coriander, mint, cumin, salt, pepper, water and ice) into a thick dosa-consistency batter using a mixer grinder. Heat a dosa tawa/griddle until hot and sprinkle water to test readiness. Pour a ladle of batter and spread into a thin dosa. Set flame to medium and let surface set. Add ghee as needed and cook on medium to low heat until crisp on bottom. Fold and remove. Alternatively, make paneer podi dosa by spreading thick uthappa-style on tawa, topping with grated paneer and podi powder before cooking. For vegetable dosa, chop vegetables finely, mix with grated paneer, spread batter, add vegetable mixture on top, garnish with coriander, salt and pepper, add ghee, cook until golden and flip to cook other side. Serve hot with coconut chutney.

name: Jigarthanda
instructions: Soak almond gum in water for 20-30 minutes until it swells and becomes translucent. In a separate pan, reduce milk with sugar over medium heat until it becomes caramel-flavored and thickens slightly (about 15-20 minutes). Allow the reduced milk to cool completely. In a glass or bowl, add crushed ice at the bottom. Pour nannari syrup over the ice. Add the cooled reduced milk. Top with the soaked almond gum and stir well. Serve immediately with a spoon (to scoop the gum) and a straw.

name: Samosa Sandwich
instructions: Prepare sandwich chutney by blending mint, coriander, green chilli, lemon juice and salt. Prepare sandwich masala with dry spices. Slice bread into desired thickness. Butter each bread slice lightly. Arrange sliced vegetables (onion, tomato, cucumber, green chilli) on one slice of bread. Place whole or halved samosa in the center. Apply chutney generously. Sprinkle sandwich masala and salt. Top with another buttered bread slice. For toast samosa sandwich, heat a tawa and cook the assembled sandwich on both sides until golden and crispy. For sada samosa sandwich, serve without toasting. Cut diagonally and serve hot or at room temperature.

name: Moong Dal Halwa
ingredients: split moong dal (yellow moong dal) 1 cup
instructions: Dry roast moong dal in ghee over medium heat for 2-3 minutes until it releases a nutty aroma and turns slightly darker. Gradually add warm milk to the roasted dal, stirring continuously to prevent lumps. Keep adding milk in portions, stirring constantly to achieve a smooth, lump-free consistency. Once all milk is incorporated, add condensed milk and continue cooking over medium heat, stirring occasionally. Add sugar and crushed cardamom pods. Keep cooking and stirring until the halwa thickens and becomes creamy (approximately 15-20 minutes total). When the mixture starts leaving the sides of the pan, it's ready. Transfer to a serving bowl. Toast cashews and almonds in a separate pan with a bit of ghee, add to the halwa as garnish along with fried raisins. Serve warm, optionally with vanilla ice cream.

name: Broccoli Malai Tikka
instructions: Cut broccoli into large florets. Blanch in boiling salted water for 4-5 minutes until fork-tender but still firm. Transfer immediately to ice-cold water to stop cooking, then drain and pat dry completely. Boil cashews and melon seeds for a few minutes, cool and blend with cheese, green chilli, garlic, ginger and fresh cream into a smooth paste. Mix hung curd with the paste, add corn flour and all powdered spices (black pepper, cardamom, kasuri methi, black salt, chaat masala) and soft butter. Whisk into a smooth lump-free marinade. Add smoky flavor by placing live charcoal in a small bowl inside the marinade, adding ghee and covering for 4-5 minutes. Coat blanched broccoli with marinade and skewer 3-4 florets per skewer. For grill pan method: Heat pan with ghee, cook skewers on medium-high heat until golden brown on both sides, apply melted butter and finish over direct flame for charring. For direct flame method: Hold skewer over flame, rotate regularly until marinade cooks and browns with char spots, apply butter as needed. For air-fryer: Preheat to 200°C, place skewers on tray, cook for 10-12 minutes, apply butter after 5-6 minutes. Serve hot.

name: Aloo Gobhi
instructions: Cut potatoes and cauliflower into small pieces. Dry roast coriander seeds, cumin seeds, fennel seeds, dry red chillies and cinnamon stick. Crush the roasted spices coarsely and add black pepper. Heat oil in a pan and add the crushed spice mix. Add turmeric and red chilli powder. Add potatoes and cauliflower, stirring well to coat with spices. Cook on medium heat until potatoes and cauliflower are tender, stirring occasionally. Season with salt. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Moong Dal Dosa
instructions: Grind moong dal, ginger, garlic, green chili with water to make a fine batter. In a separate pan, heat oil and add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, urad dal, chana dal. Toast until golden. Add ginger, garlic, onion, tomato, and dry red chilies. Cook until soft and grind into red chutney. Heat oil on a dosa tawa. Pour batter and spread thin. Add onion, coriander leaves, peri peri masala on top. Drizzle oil around edges. Cook until golden and crispy. Serve with red chutney.

name: Badam Barfi
instructions: Dry roast almonds on low heat for 5 minutes. Grind roasted almonds into fine powder. Heat ghee in a pan on low heat. Add almond powder and roast until fragrant, stirring continuously for 3-4 minutes. Add jaggery powder and cardamom powder. Mix well. Add cashew and pistachio pieces. Pour mixture on parchment paper and cool. Once set, cut into squares.

name: Mooli Ka Achar
instructions: Cut mooli (radish) into thin strips or small pieces. Mix with salt and let sit for 5 minutes. Heat mustard oil in a pan. Add green chilies and carom seeds. Let oil cool slightly. Combine oil mixture with mooli. Add turmeric powder, mustard powder, and lemon juice. Mix thoroughly. Transfer to a jar and refrigerate. Pickle is ready to consume after a few hours.

name: Aloo Ka Crispy Nasta
instructions: Boil potatoes until soft. Peel and cut into small pieces. Mix with green chili, coriander leaves, cumin seeds, and rock salt. For chutney: grind coriander leaves, roasted peanuts, green chilies, and ginger into a paste. Add lemon juice, curd, cumin seeds, and rock salt. Mix well. Heat oil and fry potato mixture until golden and crispy. Serve hot with vrat ki chutney.

name: Vrat Wala Paratha
instructions: Boil and mash potato. Mix with buckwheat flour, rock salt, and ghee. Knead into soft dough with water. Roll into paratha. Heat ghee on a tawa. Cook paratha on both sides until golden and slightly crispy. For chutney: grind coriander leaves, green chili, and curd together. Serve hot paratha with curt chutney and buttermilk.

name: Ribbon Namak Para
instructions: Mix wheat flour, turmeric powder, chili flakes, black pepper, mixed herbs, oregano, and salt in a bowl. Add cooking oil and ghee. Mix well until breadcrumb texture forms. Add water gradually to form stiff dough. Knead well. Roll very thin. Cut into ribbons or thin strips. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy.

name: Besan Laddoo
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan on low-medium heat. Add besan and roast continuously, stirring frequently for 10-15 minutes until golden and fragrant. Let cool for 5 minutes. Add powdered sugar and cardamom powder. Mix well. Let mixture cool slightly until it's comfortable to handle. Roll into laddoos of desired size.

name: Rasgulla
instructions: Curdle the milk using vinegar to form paneer (chhena). Gently shape the chhena into small round balls. Prepare a sugar syrup by dissolving 2.25 cups sugar in 6 cups water. Bring syrup to boil. Carefully place chhena balls into the boiling syrup. Simmer on medium heat for 30-45 minutes until the balls absorb the syrup, become spongy, and float. The rasgullas should be soft and glossy when ready. Cool and serve chilled.

name: Bengali Labra
instructions: Peel and cut potatoes, pumpkin, cabbage, beans into small bite-sized pieces. Heat oil in a large pan. Add turmeric powder and let it infuse. Add potatoes first (hardest vegetable) and cook for 3-4 minutes. Add pumpkin and cook for 2 minutes. Add cabbage, peas, and beans. Add chilli powder and salt. Stir well and cook on medium heat covered for 10-12 minutes until all vegetables are tender and well-cooked. The dish should have a light, semi-moist consistency. Serve hot.

name: Bhoger Khichuri
instructions: Roast and wash moong dal thoroughly. Prepare and roast the bhaja masala spices separately. Wash gobindobhog rice. In a heavy-bottomed pot, heat mustard oil and ghee. Add panchphoron seeds and let them crackle. Add ginger paste, green chillies, bay leaves, cinnamon stick, cardamom, and dry red chillies. Fry for 1-2 minutes. Add turmeric, chilli powder, coriander, and cumin powders. Add roasted dal and rice. Stir well for 2-3 minutes. Add water in proportion 1.5:1 (rice and dal). Bring to boil on high heat, then cover and reduce to low heat. Cook until rice and dal are tender, approximately 20-25 minutes. Prepare tadka by tempering ghee with cashews and raisins. Pour hot tadka over cooked khichuri. Serve hot with pickles or chutney.

name: Mooli Mirchi Achar
instructions: Peel and cut radish into thin long pieces. Slit green chillies lengthwise, keeping them whole. In a bowl, combine radish and green chillies. Add salt and mix well. Let sit for 5 minutes. Add mustard oil, turmeric, and asafoetida. Mix thoroughly until all pieces are well coated. Serve immediately as a side condiment. Can be stored in a sealed jar for up to 2-3 days at room temperature.

name: Bengali Tomato Chutney
instructions: Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pan. Add panchphoron seeds and let them crackle for 10 seconds. Add dry red chillies and fry for 15 seconds. Add finely chopped tomatoes and cook on medium heat for 5-7 minutes until tomatoes break down completely. Add tamarind pulp, aampapad powder, and sugar. Mix well. Add raisins, cashews, and dates. Reduce heat to low and cook uncovered for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chutney thickens and develops a rich dark color. The consistency should be spreadable, not watery. Season with salt to taste. Cool completely before storing. Serve as a condiment with papad, main courses, or appetizers.

name: Thekua
instructions: Dissolve jaggery in 1 cup water over low-medium heat, stirring occasionally, until fully dissolved. Do not allow to caramelize. Let the jaggery solution cool slightly to lukewarm. In a large mixing bowl, add wheat flour. Slowly pour the jaggery solution into flour while mixing continuously to form a rough dough. Add ghee gradually and knead the dough until it becomes soft and pliable. Crush the 12 green cardamom pods and extract seeds. Powder half the seeds and add to dough along with whole fennel seeds and dried coconut. Mix thoroughly. Divide the dough into 12 equal portions. Roll each portion on a clean surface and press into diamond, round, or desired shape, about 0.5 inches thick. Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan over medium-high heat. Carefully slide 2-3 thekuas at a time into hot ghee. Deep fry for 2-3 minutes on each side until golden brown and crispy, turning gently with tongs. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Badam Katli
instructions: Heat a pan on low flame and add 1 tbsp ghee. Add almond flour and roast gently for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until fragrant and light golden. Transfer roasted almond flour to a plate and let cool to room temperature. In another pan, add grated jaggery with minimal water (2-3 tbsp). Heat on low flame, stirring continuously, until jaggery completely dissolves. Cook for an additional 30 seconds on low heat until the syrup reaches one-thread consistency. Remove from heat. Slowly pour the hot jaggery syrup over cooled almond flour in small batches, mixing thoroughly after each addition. Add remaining 3 tbsp ghee and cardamom powder. Mix continuously until the mixture reaches a fudgy, dough-like consistency. Place mixture on butter paper. Using a rolling pin, roll out to approximately 0.25 inch thickness. Decorate with silver vark while still warm. Let rest for 30 minutes at room temperature. Cut into diamond or square shapes using a sharp knife. Serve immediately or store in an airtight container for up to 2-3 days.

name: Mix Veg
instructions: Deep fry all vegetables and shallow fry paneer. Heat oil, add bay leaf, cumin seeds, ginger, garlic, onion and tomato. Fry for 30 seconds and add water, cook for 3-4 minutes. Remove whole spices and blend remaining mixture into smooth paste. Heat oil, add turmeric and red chilli powder on low flame. Add blended paste with spice powders and cook 8-10 minutes. Add water and fried vegetables with paneer, mix well. Add fresh cream. Add Maggi Masala and kasuri methi. Serve with tandoori roti or naan.

name: Chole Bhature
instructions: For bhatura: Mix flour, semolina, curd, salt, sugar and baking soda. Knead into smooth dough and rest 30 minutes.
