Knead again and roll into bhatura shape, then deep fry in hot oil until golden. For chole: Boil soaked chickpeas with whole spices and tea leaves for 8 whistles. Heat oil, add cumin seeds, bay leaves and cardamom. Add onion paste and cook 5 minutes on low flame. Add ginger garlic paste and tomato paste, cook 5-7 minutes. Add dry spices and salt, mix well. Add boiled chole with water, cook until chole is tender. Add coriander, aamchur powder and ghee. Prepare tadka with ghee, ginger, garlic and Kashmiri chilli, add to chole. Serve hot with onion.

name: Gulab Jamun
instructions: Crumble khoya and paneer together. Mix in maida and baking powder to form a soft dough. Knead gently and shape into small round balls. Heat sugar with water to prepare syrup and dissolve saffron in it. Heat oil or ghee in a pan and deep fry gulab jamuns on medium heat until light golden brown. Immediately immerse the fried gulab jamuns in warm sugar syrup. Soak for at least 30 minutes to allow complete absorption of syrup. Dust with cardamom powder and saffron before serving.

name: Khandvi Bites
instructions: Mix besan, dahi, water, turmeric powder and salt to form a smooth paste. Spread the mixture on a wide surface and roll into a cylindrical shape when set. Cut into bite-sized pieces. Heat oil and add mustard seeds, curry leaves and green chilli for tadka. Pour tadka over khandvi bites. Garnish with fresh grated coconut, coriander leaves and sesame seeds. Serve cold or at room temperature.

name: Suran Ki Sabzi
instructions: Peel suran carefully under running water (wear gloves to prevent itching). Cut into even-sized pieces and boil in salted water for 10-15 minutes until partially cooked. Drain well. Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and bay leaves. Add ginger garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Add tomato paste and cook until oil separates. Add turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder and garam masala. Add boiled suran pieces and stir well to coat with spice paste. Cook on medium heat for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking. Add water if needed to form a thick gravy. Season with salt and garnish with fresh coriander. Serve hot with roti, poori or rice.

name: Chana Dal Lauki Ki Sabzi
instructions: Wash and peel bottle gourd, cut into small pieces. Soak chana dal for 15 minutes and drain. Heat oil in a pan and add cumin seeds. Let them crackle and add asafoetida. Add bottle gourd pieces and stir well. Add chana dal and mix thoroughly. Add turmeric powder and salt. Pour water and bring to boil. Cook on medium heat for 15-20 minutes until dal and lauki are tender and water has reduced to a thick consistency. Add chopped green chilli and cook for 1 more minute. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot as a light meal or as an offering (bhog) during Chhath Puja.

name: Spicy Thecha Maggi
instructions: Prepare thecha by grinding green chilli, peanuts, garlic, coriander, and cumin into a paste. Boil water and cook Maggi noodles. Stir-fry vegetables in a pan. Combine cooked noodles with vegetables and thecha masala. Mix well and serve hot.

name: Zero Oil Healthy Veg Dum Biryani
instructions: Slice onions thinly and fry until golden. Add marinated vegetables with spices and curd. Cook until tender. Separately, boil basmati rice with whole spices in 3 liters water. Layer rice over vegetables in a heavy-bottomed pot. Add rice water and sealing dough. Cook on high heat initially, then simmer on low for dum until rice is cooked and fluffy.

name: Muradabadi Chicken Biryani
instructions: Marinate chicken in curd, lemon juice, spice powders, and pastes for 30 minutes. Fry sliced onions until golden and crispy. Add marinated chicken to the pot and cook until partially done. Separately boil basmati rice with spices until 70% cooked. Layer the rice over chicken in the same pot. Seal the pot with dough and cook on high heat for 2 minutes to create steam, then reduce to low and cook for 10-15 minutes.

name: Eggless Banana Bread Pudding
instructions: Cut bread into cubes and microwave briefly. Blend banana with custard powder, sugar, and milk to create custard mixture. Mix melted butter with bread pieces. Arrange in baking tins and pour custard mixture over bread. Add chocolate chips, raisins, tutti frutti, and blueberries. Bake in preheated OTG at 180°C for 8-10 minutes until set. Garnish with icing sugar and mint.

name: Eggless Caramel Pudding
instructions: Heat sugar in a heavy pan until it turns into brown caramel and quickly coat the glass bowl or baking tin. Cut bread into cubes. Mix custard powder with milk and sugar, then cook until thickened. Pour bread pieces into the tin, then pour custard over them. Steam the pudding in hot water bath for 20-30 minutes. Cool and invert onto a plate to serve with caramel sauce.

name: Moong Dal Khasta Kachori
instructions: Make khasta dough with flour, ghee, and carom seeds. Soak and roast moong dal with spices. Temper the roasted dal with oil, asafoetida, ginger, and spice powders. Cool the filling. Roll dough into thin circles, place filling in center, seal edges. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Serve with sweet chutney and mint chutney.

name: Ragi Dosa with Tomato Chutney
instructions: Mix ragi flour, semolina, rice flour with salt, pepper, onion, ginger, green chilli, and spices. Add curd and water to make a thin batter. Heat a tava or dosa pan and spread batter thinly. Cook on medium-high heat until crispy, flip carefully if needed. For chutney: temper oil with mustard, chilli, dals, and fenugreek; add garlic and tomato; cook until soft and blend to smooth chutney. Serve dosa hot with chutney.

name: Chana Dal Vada
instructions: Soak chana dal for 1-2 hours. Grind soaked dal coarsely with cumin, peppercorns, fennel, and curry leaves. Mix in chopped ginger, green chilli, coriander, and onion. Form into balls or discs. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. For chutney: roast capsicum lightly, blend with coconut, coriander, mint, green chilli, tamarind, and ginger. Temper with oil, mustard, urad dal, and dry chilli. Serve vadas hot with coconut chutney.

name: Masala Mathri
instructions: Prepare dough with maida, semolina, salt and ghee, knead with water until smooth. Dry roast cumin, saunf, red chillies, peppercorns and coriander roots, grind into masala powder. Make second masala by toasting ajwain, coriander and fennel seeds with turmeric, chilli powder and other spices. Layer dough with ghee and cornstarch, then sprinkle masala between layers. Roll thin and cut into pieces. Deep fry until golden and crispy. Serve hot with chai.

name: Ragi Semiya Upma
instructions: Soak ragi vermicelli lightly in water and drain. Heat ghee, add mustard seeds, red chilli, dals, peanuts and allow to splutter. Add ginger, green chilli, curry leaves and chopped onion, sauté until onion softens. Add beans, carrots, capsicum and green peas, cook for 2 minutes. Add the drained vermicelli and stir well. Add water (about 1 cup), salt to taste and cook for 5-7 minutes until vermicelli is tender and water is absorbed. Finish with lemon juice and garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Besan Sooji Idlis with Onion Chutney
instructions: For batter: Mix semolina, gram flour, curd, salt, turmeric and pao bhaji masala. Add water gradually to achieve thick batter consistency, then stir in baking soda just before steaming. For chutney: Temper oil with chana dal, urad dal, fenugreek seeds and coriander seeds. Fry garlic cloves, then add sliced onions and cook until deep golden. Add kashmiri chilli powder and tamarind water, simmer for 5 minutes until flavors meld. Grind lightly keeping some texture, add jaggery and salt to taste. To serve: Steam batter in idli molds for 8-10 minutes. Heat oil with heeng, mustard seeds, kalonji and curry leaves. Toss cooked idlis with this tadka, add sesame seeds, green chilli, carrot, beans and coriander.

name: Aloo Gobhi Kebab
instructions: Boil and grate cauliflower, cook on high heat to remove moisture. Mix with mashed potato, green chilli, ginger, cumin, chaat masala, turmeric, chilli powder and coriander. Fold in grated cheese and gram flour. Shape into round kebabs. For dip: Heat oil, lightly fry peanuts then add green chillies and garlic cloves, fry until golden. Cool and grind with mint leaves, chaat masala, lemon juice, hung curd and salt. To serve: Shallow or deep fry kebabs until golden. Serve with chilli garlic dip and lemon wedges.

name: Lauki Ka Nashta
instructions: Grind rice and boiled potato to a coarse paste. Squeeze grated bottle gourd to remove excess water, mix with paste, onion, green chilli, ginger, peppercorns and salt. Add curd and baking soda just before cooking. Heat oil on tawa, spread batter to form pancakes. Cook on medium heat until golden on both sides. For tadka dahi chutney: Heat oil with heeng, dry chilli and urad dal, add curry leaves and green chilli. Pour over curd with sambhar masala and salt to taste. Garnish with sesame seeds and coriander. Serve lauki chila hot with tadka yogurt chutney.

name: Paneer Tikka Roll
instructions: Apply first marinade to paneer cubes with salt, chilli powder and chaat masala for 15 minutes. Prepare second marinade with mustard oil, green chilli, turmeric, hung curd, garam masala, coriander, ginger garlic paste and lemon juice. Marinate paneer for another 30 minutes. Marinate diced onion and bell pepper together. Grill paneer and vegetable mix on cast iron or tawa over flame until charred and cooked through. Warm malabar parotta on tawa, brush with ghee. Spread mint chutney on warmed parotta, add grilled paneer and vegetables, top with masala onion and fresh coriander. Roll tightly and serve hot.

name: Aloo Katliyan
instructions: Boil potatoes until just cooked, cool and cut into thin slices. Heat ghee, splutter dried chilli and heeng. Add sliced onions and cook until golden brown and crispy. Add turmeric, cumin powder and coriander powder, mix well. Add potato slices carefully, tossing gently to coat with spices. Continue cooking until potatoes develop golden edges and become crispy. Season with salt, black salt and chaat masala to taste. Sprinkle kasoori methi and chilli flakes. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Veg Thai Red Curry
instructions: For paste: Dry roast cumin, coriander seeds, peppercorn and coriander roots. Grind with galangal, lemon grass, kaffir lime leaves and roots, chillies, shallots and garlic with salt and water to smooth paste. For curry: Heat thick coconut milk, add curry paste and cook for 2 minutes. Add water, bring to boil. Add baby corn, carrot, mushroom, broccoli, bamboo shoot and baby brinjal. Simmer for 5-7 minutes. Add kaffir lime leaves, bird's eye chilli, galangal slices and jaggery. Simmer for 3-5 minutes more. Finish with red and yellow peppers and thai basil. Season with salt to taste. Serve hot over steamed rice.

name: Mix Veg Makhanwala
instructions: 1. Prepare tomato curry base by blending tomatoes with onion, garlic, ginger and whole spices. 2. In a pan, heat butter and saute the blended curry mixture until well roasted and aromatic. 3. In another pan, heat oil and butter, saute chopped ginger and green chillies. 4. Add diced potatoes, carrots, beans, cauliflower and mushrooms. Cook until 70% done. 5. Add corn and peas. 6. Stir in the roasted tomato curry base with cooked vegetables. 7. Add chopped spinach, paneer cubes, capsicum and spice powder. 8. Add fresh cream and kasoori methi. Finish with salt and sugar to taste. 9. Plate hot and garnish with cashews and coriander.

name: Aloo Matar Pulao
instructions: 1. Heat ghee and add asafoetida, black cardamom, cloves, bay leaf, cinnamon and cardamom seeds. 2. Add sliced onions and fry until deep golden brown. 3. Add chopped garlic, ginger and green chillies. Cook briefly. 4. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until softened. 5. Add turmeric, chilli powder and coriander powder. Mix well. 6. Add diced potatoes, boiled peas and salt. 7. Add 3 cups water and bring to boil. 8. Rinse basmati rice and add to the pot. 9. Cover and cook until rice absorbs water and potatoes are tender. 10. Add lemon juice, mint, coriander and pao bhaji masala. Serve with raita.

name: Amritsari Paneer Bhurji
instructions: 1. Heat oil and roast gram flour until golden and aromatic. Set aside. 2. In a pan, heat butter and add chopped onions. Fry until translucent. 3. Add ginger and green chillies. Cook briefly. 4. Add turmeric, chilli powder and coriander powder. 5. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft. 6. Add roasted gram flour and mix thoroughly. 7. Crumble paneer into mixture and combine. 8. Add 1 cup water and simmer. 9. Cook until thick textured curry forms. 10. Season with salt, kasoori methi and garam masala. 11. Garnish with coriander and serve with bread and chutneys.

name: Patta Gobhi Ki Sabji
instructions: 1. Cut cabbage into 1-inch pieces. 2. Heat mustard oil, add asafoetida, dry chillies, mustard seeds and cumin. 3. Add sliced onion and fry until light golden. 4. Add ginger and green chillies. 5. Add cut cabbage and turmeric. Stir well. 6. Cover and cook for 3-4 minutes. 7. Add diced potatoes and tomatoes. 8. Cook uncovered, stirring occasionally, until potatoes partially done. 9. Add boiled peas, salt, chilli powder and coriander powder. 10. Cook until potatoes are tender. 11. Add chaat masala and black salt. 12. Garnish with coriander and serve hot.

name: Shahi Malai Kofta
instructions: 1. For white gravy: Boil water with cardamom, cashews, melon seeds and chillies. Cook until soft and blend smoothly. 2. For koftas: Crumble paneer and mix with salt, ginger, chillies, sugar, kasoori methi, garam masala, cardamom powder and flour. 3. Make 10 portions and create shallow indents. 4. For stuffing: Mix khoya, dry fruits, ginger, chilli and coriander. Place in indents and close. 5. Deep fry koftas until golden. 6. For gravy: Heat ghee and oil, add cardamom, bay leaves, chillies and ginger-garlic paste. 7. Add blended cashew-onion gravy and stir. 8. Add curd and cook for 5 minutes. 9. Season with salt and cook until smooth. 10. Finish with butter, ginger, chillies, white pepper, kasoori methi and garam masala. 11. Gently add fried koftas. 12. Add cream, mix and serve.

name: Bread Pakora Chaat
instructions: 1. For filling: Heat oil, add cumin and coriander seeds and let crackle. 2. Add ginger and chillies. Cook briefly. 3. Add mashed potato and mix. 4. Season with salt, chilli powder, turmeric and chaat masala. Mix in coriander. 5. For batter: Mix gram flour, salt, chilli powder, turmeric, ajwain and coriander. 6. Add water to make thick pourable batter. 7. On bread slice, spread potato filling, place paneer slice and sprinkle chaat masala. 8. Fold bread to create pocket. 9. Dip in batter, coating well. 10. Deep fry until golden or pan fry on tawa with minimal oil. 11. To serve: Place on plate, top with curd, tamarind and mint chutneys. 12. Garnish with sev, pomegranate seeds and coriander. 13. Serve warm.

name: Murg Zafrani Korma
instructions: 1. Marinate chicken with salt, ginger-garlic paste and garam masala for 15 minutes. 2. Heat mustard oil, add garlic and let brown. 3. Add sliced onions and fry until golden. 4. Add khus khus, coconut, cardamom, cloves and cinnamon. Fry 2-3 minutes. 5. Add onion paste and cook until golden and oil separates. 6. Add turmeric, chilli and coriander powder. Cook 1 minute. 7. Add curd and mix. Cook 2-3 minutes. 8. Add soaked cashews (blend for creamier gravy). 9. Add marinated chicken and mix well. 10. Add chillies and 1/4 cup water. Cover and cook 20-25 minutes until chicken is done. 11. Stir occasionally. Adjust gravy consistency as needed. 12. Sprinkle garam masala. 13. Garnish with fried onions, coriander and chillies. 14. Serve hot with rice or bread.

name: Paneer Kulcha
instructions: Make dough with flour, salt, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, yogurt, milk, water and oil. Let rest. Prepare stuffing by mixing grated paneer with onion, green chilli, ginger, spices. Prepare imli chutney with tamarind water, onion, ginger, chilli, spices, boiled potato. Roll dough into circles, place paneer stuffing in center, fold and seal edges. Cook on griddle/tawa until golden, brushing with oil. Serve hot with imli ki chutney.

name: Millet Pancakes
instructions: Mix millet atta and urad dal with salt, curry leaves, ginger, cumin and water to form smooth batter. Add chopped vegetables (onion, carrot, tomato, capsicum) and coriander. Mix yogurt with baking soda into batter. Heat oil in pan, pour batter to form pancakes, cook until golden on both sides. For veggie dip: mix raw cabbage, carrot, capsicum, onion with yogurt, lemon juice, pepper and salt. Prepare tempering with oil, mustard seeds, curry leaves, dals, ginger and green chilli, pour into dip.

name: Chocolate Modak
instructions: Reduce milk to half volume in a heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat, stirring occasionally. Powder cardamom. Chop and grind dry fruits (pista, almonds, cashew). Mix reduced milk with sugar, ground dry fruits, desiccated coconut and cardamom powder. Cook for 2-3 minutes, add rose water. Cool completely. Chop milk chocolate and melt it. Fill modak moulds with the coconut-milk mixture, place a piece of chocolate inside, top with more mixture. Chill until set. Drizzle melted chocolate over cooled modaks.

name: Palak Paneer Paratha
instructions: Blanch spinach leaves briefly, squeeze dry and grind with mint, coriander, garlic and green chilli into smooth paste. Make dough with whole wheat flour, all purpose flour, salt and ghee, incorporate spinach paste and water to form soft dough. Rest 30 minutes. For stuffing: mix crushed paneer with onion, cumin, ginger, green chilli, chilli powder, coriander powder, fresh coriander and salt. Divide dough into 8 portions. Roll each into circle, place paneer stuffing in center, fold edges and seal, flatten gently. Cook on griddle until golden, pressing edges to cook evenly. Flip and cook other side. Serve hot with butter, yogurt and pickle.

name: Soya Kizhi Parotta
instructions: Boil water with salt, peppercorn, green chilli, ginger, garlic and turmeric. Add soya chunks and boil until softened, drain well. In a separate pan heat coconut oil, add peppercorn, fennel seeds, garlic and ginger. Add curry leaves, then sliced onions, cook until golden. Add green chilli, turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder and garam masala. Add chopped tomato and cook until soft. Add cooked soya chunks, stir well. Pour in coconut milk and water, simmer for 5-7 minutes until gravy thickens slightly. Take a lachha paratha, place it on banana leaf, spoon soya curry in center, fold edges and wrap tightly. Place wrapped parotta on griddle or pan, cook gently until heated through. Serve hot with ghee.

name: Chocolate Lava Cake
instructions: Chop dark chocolate and butter, melt together over double boiler or in microwave. Crush cookies into coarse powder, mix with milk to form a smooth batter. Pour batter into ramekins or muffin cups, pour melted chocolate-butter mixture in center. Bake in preheated oven at 200 degrees Celsius for 12-14 minutes until edges are set but center remains soft. Let cool for 2-3 minutes, sprinkle with icing sugar and chopped almonds, garnish with tooti fruity. Serve warm.

name: Aloo Baingan Sabzi
instructions: Mix all dry spices (turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder, black pepper, cumin powder, garam masala, salt, black salt, asafoetida, kasoori methi, amchur) together in a bowl. Peel and cut potatoes into medium pieces. Slit baby brinjals lengthwise without cutting completely. Slice onions. Heat mustard oil in a heavy-bottomed pan, add potato pieces and cook on high heat until surface is sealed and slightly golden. Add brinjals and cook for 2-3 minutes. Reduce heat to medium, add sliced onions and stir well. Sprinkle the spice mixture gradually while stirring. Continue cooking for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally until potatoes and brinjals are cooked through. The oil should separate from the sides. Garnish with fresh chopped coriander. Serve hot.

name: Pumpkin Spice Caramel Custard
instructions: Prepare caramel by heating 60 gms sugar in a pan until it turns amber color, immediately pour into ramekins coating the bottom and sides. Toast cinnamon sticks, cloves, nutmeg, allspice and dried ginger lightly in a dry pan for aroma. Grind or infuse these spices in warm milk and strain. In a bowl, whisk 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks together with 35 gms sugar and vanilla extract until pale and frothy. Slowly add the spiced milk to the egg mixture while whisking constantly to prevent curdling. Pour custard mixture over caramel in ramekins. Place ramekins in a water bath (bain marie) with hot water reaching halfway up the sides. Bake in preheated oven at 160-170 degrees Celsius for 20-25 minutes until custard sets but center remains slightly jiggly. Cool completely, then refrigerate for at least 4 hours. Run a knife around edges and invert onto serving plates to release caramel.

name: moonglet
instructions: Soak moong dal for 3-4 hours. Drain and blend with ginger, turmeric, and water to smooth batter. Transfer to bowl, add onions, tomatoes, chillies, cumin, carrots, capsicum, coriander, salt, and curry leaves. Add Eno and mix gently. Heat pan with oil, pour batter, spread thin, drizzle oil around edges, cover and cook on medium-low until golden. Flip and cook other side until crispy.

name: korean fried potato
instructions: Boil potatoes with salt. Make gochujang paste by cooking garlic, vinegar, sugar, rice flour, and kashmiri chilli powder in water until thickened. Fry potato wedges until golden. Make sauce with oil, ginger, garlic, ketchup, gochujang, vinegar, soya sauce, and honey. Toss fried potatoes in sauce. Garnish with spring onions and sesame seeds.

name: kala chana dosa
instructions: Soak chana overnight and blend with ginger, green chilli, garlic, cumin, salt, coriander leaves, semolina, curd, curry leaves, and water. Rest batter for 20 minutes. Heat tawa over medium heat. Pour ladleful of batter and spread in circular motion to form thin dosa. Drizzle oil around edges and on top. Cook until bottom is golden and crispy. Remove from tawa and serve hot with dahi chutney.

name: matar paneer sandwich
instructions: Heat butter, add cumin. Add peas and cook. Add paneer, salt, onion, ginger, chilli, coriander seeds, chilli powder, kasoori methi, chaat masala, anardana, garam masala, and coriander. Cook until well combined. Spread mint chutney on bread slices. Apply matar paneer filling. Toast in pan with butter until golden.

name: afghani paneer tikka
instructions: Marinate paneer cubes with salt, pepper, lemon, and ginger-garlic paste. Blend coriander, onion, chillies, mint, cashews, and cheese into a paste. Mix hung curd, cream, pepper, kasuri methi, chaat masala, garam masala, cumin, and black salt. Combine with green paste and lemon juice. Coat paneer in marinade. Thread onto skewers with capsicum and onion. Bake at 200°C for 40 minutes. Finish with butter and chaat masala.

name: pizza pull apart bread
instructions: Blend garlic with butter and italian seasoning. Prepare stuffing: mix corn, capsicums, onion, tomato, basil, salt, italian seasoning, chilli flakes, and mozzarella. Slice bread into chunks without cutting through base. Spread garlic butter between slices. Layer ketchup and mozzarella. Wrap in foil. Bake at 200°C for 15 minutes. Unwrap, garnish with basil and olives.

name: aloo bhindi masala
instructions: Heat mustard oil. Add dry red chilli and heeng. Add cumin, green chilli, and ginger. Cut potatoes into small pieces, add salt, and fry until golden. Wash and chop bhindi (okra), add to potatoes with more salt. Cook on high heat. Add amchur, turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, and black salt. Cook until bhindi is tender. In separate tadka, caramelize onions in ghee, add kasoori methi, and mix into sabzi. Garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Anda Parantha Roll
instructions: Beat eggs with salt, black pepper, and chopped green chilli. Heat ghee and scramble eggs until cooked through. Warm paratha on griddle with oil and ghee. Mix mayo, ketchup, and chilli sauce together. Spread sauce mixture on paratha. Layer scrambled eggs, chopped onion, tomato, capsicum, lettuce, and grated mozzarella. Roll tightly and serve immediately.

name: Veg Shami Kebab
instructions: Dry roast all masala spices (bay leaf through cardamom) and grind to fine powder. Heat ghee and saute onion until golden. Add ginger, garlic, and green chilli. Add all mixed vegetables (carrot, cauliflower, beans, peas) and chana dal. Add water and spices (salt, turmeric, chilli powder, shami masala). Cook covered until vegetables are completely soft and water evaporates. Mash everything into a coarse paste. Cool slightly and mix in fresh coriander, mint, green chilli, and gram flour. For stuffing: mix hung curd with mint, coriander, chopped onion, and salt. Divide kebab mixture into 6 portions, flatten, fill with curd mixture, seal well, and deep fry in ghee until golden brown. Serve hot with mint chutney and lemon.

name: Multigrain Dosa with Dal Ki Chutney
instructions: Soak bajra, jowar, dals, methi, and rice for 4-6 hours. Grind with water to fine smooth fermented batter. For dal chutney: heat oil and add hing. Add toor dal and cook until golden. Add coriander seeds, cumin, garlic, baby onion, dry chilli, curry leaves, tamarind. Cook until very soft. Grind to smooth paste. For tempering: heat oil, add mustard seeds and urad dal, cook until crackling. Add curry leaves. Pour into chutney and mix. Heat griddle and wipe with ghee. Pour batter and spread thin. Cook until edges turn golden and crispy. Flip and cook other side. Serve hot with dal chutney.

name: Aloo Beans
instructions: Cut potatoes into small cubes. Heat mustard oil over medium-high heat. Add dry red chilli, hing, green chilli, mustard seeds, and cumin. Let seeds crackle. Add ginger and fry for 30 seconds. Add potato cubes and stir-fry for 5-7 minutes. Add french beans, turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder, amchur, kasoori methi, and sambar masala. Mix well and add salt. Cover and cook over medium heat until potatoes and beans are tender, stirring occasionally. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Paratha Quesadilla
instructions: Chop all vegetables finely. Heat ghee on a tawa or griddle. Place one paratha and add mixed vegetables on one half. Season with salt and chilli flakes. Sprinkle basil and oregano. Spread tomato ketchup over vegetables. Add generous amount of grated mozzarella cheese. Fold paratha in half. Cook until cheese melts and paratha becomes crispy. Flip and cook the other side until golden brown. Cut into triangles and serve hot.

name: Lauki Paratha with Sannata Raita
instructions: Make dough with wheat flour, gram flour, salt, kasoori methi, ghee, ajwain, lauki water, and additional water as needed. Let rest 15 minutes. For stuffing: grate lauki and sprinkle salt to draw water. Squeeze out excess water. Mix with cumin, green chilli, onion, coriander, chilli powder, chaat masala, ginger, hing, and wheat flour. Divide dough into 6 balls. Flatten each, place lauki mixture in center, seal edges, and roll out carefully. Cook on hot griddle with ghee until golden on both sides. For sannata raita: mix curd with salt, chilli powder, and water. Heat mustard oil, temper with cumin and hing. Pour into curd mixture. Top with fresh coriander and boondi. Serve hot parathas with raita.

name: Eggless Chocolate Walnut Brownie
instructions: Preheat oven to 350F. Melt dark chocolate chunks and butter together in double boiler or microwave. Remove from heat and mix in sugar and curd until well combined. Stir in vanilla extract. In a separate bowl, sift together flour, cocoa powder, and baking powder. Gently fold dry ingredients into wet mixture until just combined. Fold in chopped walnuts. Pour batter into butter paper-lined cake tin. Bake at 350F for 25-30 minutes until a toothpick inserted comes out clean. Cool in pan for 10 minutes, then turn out onto cooling rack. Cut into pieces and serve.

name: Kele ke Phool ke Pakode
instructions: In pressure cooker, add water, banana flower, water chestnuts and rock salt; cook for 2-3 whistles. Make chutney by grinding coriander, green chillies, ginger, peanuts and cumin. Roast peanuts and cumin seeds in oil. Mix cooked banana flower with chopped vegetables, coriander, chillies, cumin, curd, salt and buckwheat flour. Heat oil in kadai, drop small spoonfuls of mixture and fry until golden. Serve hot with chutney.

name: Hyderabadi Khatte Aloo
instructions: Heat oil in handi, add cardamom, red chillies, cloves and cumin; let splutter. Add onions and salt, sauté until translucent. Add potatoes, salt, turmeric and chilli powder; cook 1 minute. Add water, ginger garlic paste, cover and cook 7-8 minutes. Add coriander powder, tamarind water and peanut paste. Cook until potatoes are tender. Finish with coriander stems, jaggery, sugar and ghee. Roast peanuts and sesame seeds separately, grind into paste and add to gravy.

name: Cham Cham
instructions: Make sugar syrup by simmering water, sugar and cardamom for 10-12 minutes. Prepare rasmalai mixture by boiling milk with cardamom and sugar, add bread and cook 2-3 minutes. Make cham cham dough by crumbling paneer, adding flour and baking soda, then coating with semolina. Cook cham cham balls in sugar syrup for 10-12 minutes. Prepare rasmalai bread mixture and grind with hand blender. Assemble by layering cham cham with rasmalai mixture and soaked bread. Garnish with tutti frutti, almonds, pistachios, cherries and rose petals.

name: Aloo Butter Masala
instructions: Marinate diced potatoes with curd, chilli powder, ginger garlic paste, salt and fenugreek for 5-10 minutes. In handi, heat ghee and oil, add cardamom, cloves, bay leaf and onions. Sauté for 1 minute, add tomatoes and spices. Cook until tomatoes are mushy, add water, cashewnuts and curd. Cover and cook 10-12 minutes, then blend into smooth paste. Roast marinated potatoes in ghee and mustard oil until golden, then smoke with coal and ghee. Prepare finishing gravy with ghee and butter, add paste and simmer 5-6 minutes. Add roasted potatoes, butter and cream. Finish with fenugreek and garnish.

name: Soya Wadi Nutri Kulcha
instructions: In pressure cooker, cook soya chunks and granules with turmeric, chilli powder, salt and oil for 2-3 whistles. Drain and set aside. In handi, heat oil and ghee, add cumin seeds, sauté onions until golden. Add ginger garlic paste, tomato puree and curd; cook until oil separates. Add fried onions and spices, add water and cooked soya, bhavnagri mirchi and ginger. Cook well. For double tadka, heat ghee, sauté sliced onion, raw mango, carrot and chilli 2-3 minutes. Add cooked soya mixture and water, cook 4-5 minutes. Finish with garam masala, black pepper and lemon juice. Serve with kulcha.

name: Sindhi Mutton Curry
instructions: In pressure cooker, heat oil and add cloves, peppercorns, black cardamom, green cardamom and bay leaf; let splutter. Add chopped onions and salt, sauté until golden 3-4 minutes. Add mutton with bones and coriander leaves, sauté 1 minute. Add turmeric, chilli and coriander powder, cook 1 minute. Add chopped tomatoes, salt and tomato puree; cook until oil separates. Add water to adjust consistency, add green chillies. Cover and cook on high for 7-8 whistles until mutton is tender. Add tender coriander stems and fenugreek leaves, mix well.

name: Tandoori Soya Chaap
instructions: Boil soya chaap pieces. Prepare tandoori marinade with hung curd, red chilli powder, turmeric, ginger-garlic paste, and mustard oil. Marinate boiled soya chaap pieces in this mixture. Dice capsicum, tomato, and onion. Heat oil in a pan, add marinated soya chaap with diced vegetables. Cook until vegetables are tender. Finish with lemon juice, garnish with fresh coriander leaves, and drizzle ghee. Serve hot.

name: Parsi Salli Murgh
ingredients: 1 kg chicken drumstick and thigh with bones
1 large frying potato, peeled and julienned
instructions: Heat oil in a handi and fry onions until golden. Add turmeric, red chilli, coriander, and cumin powders, cook for a minute. Add tomato puree and chopped tomato, cook until well done. Add coriander stems, chicken pieces, salt, and ginger-garlic paste. Cook for 10-12 minutes until chicken is half-cooked. Add dried apricots and kolah vinegar, add water, cover, and cook until chicken is done. Meanwhile, slice potato thinly and cut into juliennes. Deep fry until golden and crispy. Transfer curry to serving dish, top with crispy potato salli, garnish with chilli oil and coriander. Serve hot with roti.

name: Veg Thukpa with Laphing
instructions: Knead refined flour with water and yeast into smooth dough, rest 15-20 minutes. Wash dough with water to extract starch, separating gluten and starch. Steamed both for 10-12 minutes separately (starch becomes laphing). For chilli oil: heat oil with bay leaf, cinnamon, star anise, chilli flakes on low flame 2-3 minutes. Add vinegar and soy sauce, cook 2-3 minutes more. Add sesame seeds and spring onion. For thukpa: heat oil, add crushed garlic, mushrooms, carrot, cabbage, cook 2-3 minutes. Add water and simmer. Add spring onion, ginger, green chilli, coriander stems. In serving bowl, add laphing cut into strips, seitan, chilli oil, and hot thukpa. Garnish with coriander and spring onion. Serve hot.

name: Kaju Chicken
instructions: In a handi, heat ghee and add bay leaves, green cardamom, and sliced onions with salt. Cook until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cashew nuts, mix well. Add water, cinnamon stick, and coriander stems. Cook until softened. Grind into smooth paste. Marinate chicken legs with ginger-garlic paste, salt, oil, and green chilli. In a pan, heat ghee and sear marinated chicken on both sides for 4-5 minutes each. Add water and cook until done. In a handi, heat ghee, add green chilli and curd, cook for 5-6 minutes. Add prepared cashew gravy and boil. Add seared chicken with water, salt, and green chilli. Cook until tender. Finish with sugar and salt. Fry cashew halves in ghee with fenugreek leaves, red chilli powder, and salt separately. Garnish with crushed black peppercorns, fried cashews, fresh cream, chilli oil, green chilli, and coriander. Serve hot with paratha or roti.

name: Bharwaan Mirchi
ingredients: degi red chili powder 1 heaped tbsp
instructions: Make a semi-soft dough with gram flour, rice flour, salt, asafoetida, turmeric and water. Roast gram flour in hot ghee, add sesame and carom seeds, cook 1 minute. Add turmeric, asafoetida, coriander, chili powders and spices, cook 3-4 minutes. Prepare green chilis by slitting lengthwise and making a cavity. Stuff chilis with roasted masala mixture. Heat oil and shallow fry stuffed chilis for 7-8 minutes until cooked. Serve hot with steamed rice and dal.

name: Instant Kalakand
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan on medium heat. Add khoya and cook stirring frequently for 3-4 minutes. Pour in condensed milk, add sugar and cardamom powder. Mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes until mixture thickens and reaches fudgy consistency. Transfer to a greased tray, spread evenly and garnish with pistachios and almonds. Cool completely and cut into pieces.

name: Jaljeera
instructions: Toast cumin seeds lightly in a pan. Crush roasted cumin seeds coarsely. In a glass add crushed cumin powder, chaat masala, lemon juice, salt and mint leaves. Pour chilled water and mix well. Add pomegranate seeds and serve immediately with ice.

name: Mutton Kharda
instructions: Make green paste with green chilli, garlic, ginger and coriander. Marinate mutton with 2 tbsp green paste, salt and turmeric. Heat oil in pressure cooker, fry onions till golden, add mutton and sear 4-5 minutes. Add hot water just below mutton level. Pressure cook 5-6 whistles on medium-high. Dry roast dry coconut till golden. Fry garlic, green chilli, ginger in same pan till golden. Cool ingredients and grind into paste with fresh coriander, mint and fresh coconut. Heat ghee and oil, fry onions, add mutton, kharda paste and 600-700ml stock. Cook 8-10 minutes on high flame till masala coats the mutton. Add remaining green paste and garam masala. Serve with bhakri and rice.

name: Panakam
instructions: Roast cumin seeds and black pepper lightly. Crush cardamom pods. Heat water in a pot, add jaggery and dissolve completely. Add tamarind paste and mix well. Add roasted spices, crushed cardamom and ginger piece. Simmer for 2-3 minutes. Strain and cool. Serve chilled at room temperature.

name: Sannata Raita
instructions: Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Pour hot ghee tadka into whisked curd. Add water to reach desired consistency. Mix in boondi, green chilli and chaat masala. Add salt to taste. Garnish with coriander leaves. Serve chilled or at room temperature.

name: Sabudana Khichdi
instructions: Soak sabudana in water for 30 minutes. Drain well. Roast peanuts and crush coarsely. Boil and cube potatoes. Heat ghee in a pan, add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Add soaked sabudana and stir frequently for 2-3 minutes. Add boiled potatoes, roasted peanuts, green chilli and ginger. Mix well and cook 3-4 minutes till sabudana becomes translucent. Season with salt and black salt. Add lemon juice and toss. Garnish with coriander leaves. Serve hot or warm.

name: Chatpata Laal Batata
instructions: Peel and quarter boiled potatoes. Heat oil until hot, add cumin and mustard seeds until they crackle. Add curry leaves, green chilli and ginger-garlic paste, cook 1-2 minutes. Add tamarind pulp and sugar with water, cook 3-4 minutes. Lower heat and add powdered spices with salt and water, cook until oil separates. Add potato pieces and coat with masala. Taste and adjust salt, add fresh coriander. Serve with toasted pav.

name: Instant Ragi Dosa
instructions: Mix ragi flour, rice flour and salt in a bowl. Add water gradually to make a thin batter similar to crepe batter. Heat a dosa tawa or non-stick pan over high heat, lightly grease with oil. Pour batter onto the hot tawa and spread thin using a ladle. Cook until the bottom is golden brown and crispy, about 2-3 minutes. Flip carefully and cook the other side for 1 minute until light brown. Serve hot with sambar, chutney or curry.

name: Aloo Do Pyaza
ingredients: mustard oil 3 tbsp plus for frying
instructions: Cut potatoes into bite-sized pieces and soak. Heat mustard oil until smoking, cool slightly. Fry potatoes until golden brown and fully cooked. In a separate pan, heat oil and add whole spices until they crackle. Add sliced onions and cook until golden. Add garlic, ginger, green chilli, coriander stems and tomato trimmings with salt and water. Cover and cook until tomatoes are mushy. Mix curd with all powdered spices and add to the pan once tomatoes soften. Cook until oil separates, then grind the mixture into a smooth gravy. In a tawa or deep pan, heat remaining oil. Add cumin seeds, crushed coriander seeds, ginger julienne and green chilli. Toss onion, tomato and capsicum petals for 1 minute. Add fried potatoes and prepared gravy with black salt and chaat masala. Cook 2-3 minutes, adjust consistency with water if needed. Add kasuri methi powder, garam masala and fresh coriander. Serve hot with tandoori roti.

name: Paneer Tawa Masala
ingredients: oil 2-3 tbsp for frying plus 2-3 tbsp for cooking
instructions: Chop onions and tomatoes separately. Make green paste by grinding ginger, garlic, green chilli, fresh coriander and water in a mixer. Cut paneer into rectangular pieces and shallow fry until light golden on both sides. Heat oil on a tawa, add cumin seeds and onions, cook until golden brown. Add green paste and garlic cloves, continue cooking until onions turn dark golden, adding water if the masala becomes too dry. Lower heat and add all powdered spices with water, cook until oil separates. Add tomato and salt, cook until oil separates again. Add kokum agal and cook 2-3 minutes. Adjust consistency to semi-thin gravy. Place fried paneer pieces and flip to coat with masala. Cook few minutes until masala coats paneer and becomes thick. Add melted ghee and fresh coriander. Serve hot with pav, bhakri or rice.

name: Cheese Chutney Chicken Kabab
instructions: Mince chicken finely. Mix minced chicken with ginger-garlic paste, green chilli, onion, coriander powder, garam masala and salt. Divide into portions and stuff each with cheese and green chutney. Shape into kababs. Shallow fry in oil over medium-high heat until golden brown and cooked through, approximately 4-5 minutes per side. Serve hot with sliced onions and lemon wedges.

name: Chicken Kathi Roll
instructions: Cut chicken into medium pieces. Mix yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, garam masala and salt. Marinate chicken for 30 minutes minimum. Thread chicken onto soaked wooden skewers. Grill kababs over high heat for 10-12 minutes, turning frequently until fully cooked and charred edges. For paratha: make dough with flour, salt and water, rest for 20 minutes. Divide into balls, roll each ball, spread ghee and folds, roll into spiral then flatten. Cook on hot tawa with oil until golden and puffed on both sides. To assemble: place warm paratha, spread mint and green chutney, add tossed grilled chicken pieces, roll tightly. Serve hot with sliced onions and lemon.

name: rajgira paratha
instructions: Dry roast peanuts in hot pan until golden brown, cool completely and chop coarsely. Add amaranth flour to bowl with sesame seeds, rock salt, black pepper, cumin seeds, ginger-chilli paste, coriander and grated potato. Mix all ingredients. Gradually add water to make semi-stiff dough, do not knead much as amaranth has no gluten. Cover with damp cloth and rest 10 minutes. Blend coriander, green chilli, roasted peanuts, lemon juice, rock salt, sugar and cumin seeds into fine chutney. Set tawa on medium-high heat. Take small dough portion, flatten and coat with dry amaranth flour, roll into paratha. Place on hot tawa without ghee and cook on high flame until light brown spots appear. Flip and cook similarly on other side until spots appear. Add ghee and cook both sides until golden brown. Serve with green chutney and sweet curd.

name: desi red sauce pasta
instructions: Finely chop onions, garlic and green chilli in chopper and transfer to bowl. Chop tomatoes finely in same chopper. Cut vegetables into small dices. Set pressure cooker over high flame and let get hot, add oil and heat. Add cumin seeds and let crackle, then add chopped onion mixture and cook on high flame until light golden brown. Lower flame and add powdered spices with splash of hot water, stir well and cook for 1-2 minutes. Add tomatoes with pinch of salt, stir and cook until oil separates. Add ketchup, soy sauce and diced vegetables, gently stir and mix. Pour hot water to completely cover ingredients but not too much. Stir, taste and adjust salt. Put lid on and pressure cook on medium flame for 1 whistle. Switch off flame and let cooker depressurize naturally. Remove lid and gently stir pasta. Taste and adjust salt. Add sugar, oregano, chilli flakes, fresh coriander and spring onion greens, stir well. Optionally add processed cheese.

name: eggless mango milk cake
instructions: Add milk to bowl and add vinegar, stir gently and rest 2-3 minutes to make buttermilk substitute. Add melted butter and vanilla essence to milk mixture and whisk well to make lump-free wet mixture. Place sieve over bowl and add refined flour with baking powder and baking soda, sift together. Whisk until smooth lump-free batter without over-whisking. Transfer batter to non-stick fry pan and tap gently to release air bubbles. Place pan on stove, cover with lid and cook on lowest possible flame for 15 minutes. If lid has steam vent, seal with foil. Prepare mango milk: cut mango and add pulp to mixer jar, add few ladles of milk and blend. Strain mixture and combine with remaining milk, add condensed milk and fresh cream, stir well. After 15 minutes of cooking, remove lid and check sponge - sides and edges will be cooked but top may look slightly raw. Switch off flame, cover pan again and let rest 10-15 minutes for residual heat to finish cooking. Check doneness by inserting toothpick which should come out clean. Cool sponge completely before demoulding. Use spatula to lift gently. Flip onto plate and cut into 8 equal slices. Poke holes into sponge using fork. Pour ladles of mango milk over sponge evenly including sides and edges. Cover with lid and refrigerate briefly to soak milk. Whip cream and chop mangoes for topping. Serve decorated with whipped cream and chopped mangoes, pour remaining mango milk around each slice.

name: Chicken Dum Biryani
instructions: Soak rice for 30 minutes. Marinate chicken in yogurt with ginger-garlic paste, biryani masala, and spices for 30 minutes. Boil rice separately. Layer marinated chicken in a heavy-bottomed pot, alternate with boiled rice, onions, and potatoes. Sprinkle biryani masala and herbs between layers. Dot with ghee on top layer. Cook on high heat for 2 minutes until steam forms, then switch to low heat and cook covered (dum) for 45-50 minutes.

name: Tawa Cheese Burst Pizza
instructions: Make pizza sauce by cooking tomatoes with garlic and spices. Prepare cheese sauce with mozzarella and cream. Toss vegetables with minimal seasoning. Place pizza base on heated tawa. Spread pizza sauce, then vegetable toppings, cheese sauce, mozzarella shreds, and chilli flakes. Cover and cook on medium heat until cheese melts and base becomes crispy (about 3-4 minutes). Flip carefully to cook bottom side until golden brown (1-2 minutes).

name: Anda Kolhapuri
instructions: Roast and grind spices (cumin, coriander, cloves, peppercorns, cardamom, cinnamon, chillies) with coconut, sesame seeds, poppy seeds, tomatoes, garlic, and cashews to make a fine paste. Heat oil, temper with cumin seeds, red chilli, bay leaf, then add chopped onions and cook until golden brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chillies, cook further until onions turn dark golden. Add turmeric and kashmiri red chilli powder, stir well. Add the ground spice paste with salt and cook until oil separates (1-2 minutes), cover and cook on medium heat until oil separates completely, stirring at intervals and adding water if too dry. In a separate pan, heat butter and toss capsicum, onion petals, boiled eggs, and spice powders for 1-2 minutes, then add to gravy. Add fresh cream, roasted kasuri methi powder, lemon juice, and fresh coriander. Serve with butter naan or tandoori roti.

name: Jammu Rajma Chawal
instructions: Soak kidney beans overnight, then pressure cook with turmeric and salt until soft (3-4 whistles). Boil rice separately until cooked. For rajma: Heat oil, add cumin seeds and bay leaf, add chopped onions and cook until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chillies, cook briefly. Add turmeric and red chilli powder, stir well. Add boiled beans and tomatoes, mix and cook on medium heat for 3-4 minutes. Make anardana chutney by soaking pomegranate seeds and grinding with spices. Serve rajma over hot rice, topped with anardana chutney and fried paneer pieces.

name: Lauki Ka Cheela
instructions: Peel and grate bottle gourd and carrot into a large bowl. Add onion, green chilli, ginger, carom seeds, sesame seeds, cumin seeds. Mix well. Add flours (gram, rice, sorghum) and all spices. Gradually add water while mixing to make a semi-thick batter similar to dosa consistency. Set pan on high heat and let it get moderately hot. Pour ladle-full of batter and spread thinly. Cook on medium-low heat. Once top sets, add ghee and cook until bottom is set. Flip, add more ghee, and cook until golden brown on both sides. Serve hot with green chutney.

name: Gujarati Dal
instructions: Wash and soak toor dal for 30 minutes to 1 hour. Soak tamarind in hot water. In pressure cooker, add dal, water, turmeric powder, and salt. Place a steel bowl with peanuts on top of dal. Pressure cook on high heat for 4-5 whistles, let de-pressurize. Remove peanuts and blend dal with a whisk or emulsion blender until smooth. Strain soaked tamarind to get pulp. In a deep pan, heat ghee and add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, cloves, cinnamon, red chillies, fenugreek seeds, asafoetida, and curry leaves, cook briefly. Add ginger-chilli paste and tomato, cook until tomato becomes mushy. Add turmeric powder and kashmiri red chilli powder. Add blended dal and hot water to achieve desired consistency. Add tamarind pulp, jaggery, and peanuts, bring to boil. Adjust tamarind and jaggery to balance sweetness and sourness. Cook until right consistency is reached. Add fresh coriander. Serve with hot rice.

name: Gujiya
ingredients: dry fruits cashews almonds pistachios 1 cup mixed chopped
instructions: Make dough: Mix flour, salt, and baking powder. Add ghee and rub until breadcrumb texture. Add milk gradually to make a firm dough. Rest for 30 minutes. Make filling: Roast mawa on low heat until light brown, add powdered sugar, cardamom powder, dry fruits, saffron, and rose water. Mix well and let cool. Roll dough into thin sheets, cut into circles. Place 1 tbsp filling on one half, fold to semicircle, crimp edges with fork. Deep fry in medium-hot oil until light golden on both sides (about 1-2 minutes each side). Remove and place on paper towel. Optionally glaze with sugar syrup. Cool completely before storing.

name: Gobi Aloo Matar
instructions: Cut cauliflower into florets and rinse in salted water. Cut potatoes into medium dice. Heat mustard oil in a kadhai over high flame until smoking, then lower heat slightly. Add cauliflower and potatoes, cook over high flame for 5-6 minutes until 90% cooked. Transfer to a bowl. In the same kadhai, add whole spices and onions, cook over medium-high flame until light golden brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chillies, cook until onions turn completely golden. Lower flame, add powdered spices and gram flour, stir well, add a splash of hot water, cook for 4-5 minutes. Add tomato puree, whisked yogurt and salt, cook over high flame for 12-15 minutes until oil separates. Add green peas, cover and cook for 6-8 minutes. Adjust consistency with hot water. Add cooked cauliflower and potatoes, cook in gravy for a few minutes. Taste and adjust salt, add garam masala, kasuri methi, green chillies, ginger and coriander. Serve with rotis or parathas.

name: Meethi Chutney
instructions: Add deseeded dates and tamarind to a bowl. Pour hot water to fully submerge them, cover and soak for 30 minutes. Transfer soaked fruits to a mixer grinder and blend into smooth paste, adding more water if needed. Strain the paste using a sieve to remove fibrous bits. Add jaggery and all spices to the strained paste in a heavy-bottomed pot. Turn flame to high and stir continuously until jaggery melts completely. Once mixture boils, reduce heat to medium and let simmer, removing scum from surface. Cook for 30-40 minutes, stirring regularly, until chutney gets darker and thicker and coats the back of a spoon. Cool completely (it will continue to thicken as it cools). Store in sterilized airtight jar, refrigerate for up to 2 months or freeze for 6-12 months.

name: Cheesy Paneer Bread Rolls
instructions: Crush paneer into a large bowl and grate cheese using large hole grater. Add all filling ingredients (bell peppers, green chilli, ginger, spring onion greens, coriander, oregano, chilli flakes, dry mango powder, salt) and mix well. Prepare bread slices by trimming off sides (this step is important for proper sealing). Take one bread slice and dip it in water on both sides, then squeeze between your palms to flatten completely and remove excess moisture. Place a handful of filling in the center of the flattened bread, bring the corners together, and using palm and fingers press while rotating to form an oval/egg-shaped roll. Ensure no cracks and rest shaped rolls for 10 minutes. Heat oil in deep pan/kadhai to 175°C (moderately hot). Carefully add 3-4 rolls at a time and fry over high flame until crisp and golden brown. Strain in a sieve and serve hot with ketchup or dip of choice.

name: Cheesy Omelette Butter Masala
instructions: Make puree: blend tomatoes, coriander stems, and boiled cashews into fine puree. Finely chop onions, garlic, ginger and green chilli. Heat oil in kadhai over high heat, add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add chopped onion mixture, cook over medium-high flame until light golden brown. Lower flame, add powdered spices and hot water splash, cook over high flame until oil separates. Add tomato-cashew puree with salt and sugar, cook over high flame until gravy leaves the pan, using as little water as possible. Adjust consistency to semi-thick with hot water. Add butter, cream, kasuri methi, garam masala and fresh coriander. Taste and adjust salt. For omelettes: crack eggs into bowl, add salt, pepper, kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander and onion, and whisk well. Heat butter and oil in a pan, pour eggs and make omelette. Cook and load grated cheese, fold omelette as preferred. To serve: place omelette on plate and pour hot gravy on top.

name: Mangalorean Prawn Curry
instructions: 1. Heat pan and roast byadgi chillies on low flame until puffed, set aside. 2. In same pan roast whole spices (coriander, cumin, peppercorns, carom, fenugreek) until fragrant and golden, cool completely. 3. Grind cooled spices with garlic, ginger, onion, tamarind, fresh coconut, turmeric and water into thick paste. 4. Heat stock pot with coconut oil, add mustard seeds, let crackle, add curry leaves, green chilli and onion, cook until soft. 5. Add prepared paste, stir and cook on high flame 4-5 minutes until glaze appears. 6. Add hot water to make thin curry, simmer 12-15 minutes. 7. Add prawns, cook 5-6 minutes until fully cooked. 8. Adjust consistency and add coconut milk if desired, taste and adjust salt. Serve hot with neer dosa or rice.

name: Mushroom Pepper Fry
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 2 tbsp
instructions: 1. Dry roast black peppercorns, cumin seeds and fennel seeds on low flame until golden, cool and grind into fine powder. 2. Heat oil in pan, add cumin, fennel seeds, garlic and curry leaves briefly. 3. Add onions and cook until light golden brown. 4. Add ginger garlic green chilli paste, cook 1-2 minutes. 5. Lower flame, add turmeric, garam masala, pepper powder and hot water splash, cook 1-2 minutes. 6. Add mushrooms, stir and cook on high flame 4-5 minutes. 7. Cover and cook on low flame 2 minutes until mushrooms release moisture. 8. Uncover and cook on high flame until water evaporates. 9. Add remaining pepper powder, green chilli, curry leaves, lemon juice and coriander, stir well. 10. Taste and adjust salt. Serve hot.

name: Kolambi Bhaat
instructions: 1. Wash rice until water is clear, soak for 30 minutes. 2. Grind green chilli, garlic, ginger and coriander into fine paste. 3. Marinate prawns with salt, turmeric, lemon juice and green paste, refrigerate. 4. Heat oil in flat-bottomed vessel, add whole spices and let crackle. 5. Add sliced onions and cook until golden brown. 6. Add green paste with splash of hot water, cook 1 minute. 7. Add tomato with salt, cook until soft, add water if needed. 8. Lower flame, add red chilli powder, biryani masala and hot water, cook until oil separates. 9. Add marinated prawns with coriander, mint and kokum, cook on high flame 2 minutes. 10. Drain rice and add to masala, stir gently. 11. Add water and coconut milk, bring to simmer. 12. Cover with lid, place weight on lid, cook on low flame 12 minutes. 13. Remove lid and check, rice should be cooked with no liquid remaining. 14. Switch off flame, cover and rest for 20 minutes. 15. Gently stir and serve hot.

name: Vegetable Rava Dhokla
instructions: 1. Mix semolina, sour curd, water, ginger chilli paste, sugar and salt, cover and let rest. 2. Chop carrot and capsicum roughly in a chopper. 3. Heat ghee in small pan, add cumin seeds, add chopped veggies, cook 2 minutes on high flame, cool completely. 4. Set steamer on high flame and grease dhokla trays. 5. Add cooled veggies and sweet corn to batter, mix well, adjust consistency to thick pouring batter. 6. Add eno with water, stir well until completely mixed. 7. Pour batter into dhokla trays evenly, tap gently a few times. 8. Place trays in steamer for 15 minutes. 9. Check with knife, should come out clean. 10. Remove trays and cool. 11. For tempering, heat oil, add mustard seeds, sesame seeds and asafoetida, let crackle. 12. Add curry leaves. 13. Pour tempering evenly over dhokla. 14. Cut as desired and serve with green chutney.

name: Rice Pakora
ingredients: gram flour as required for binding
instructions: 1. Boil and mash potatoes. 2. Chop green chilli and ginger finely. 3. Mix rice, mashed potato, green chilli, ginger, salt, cumin seeds, red chilli powder and coriander. 4. Make a batter by adding gram flour and water to bind. 5. Form small pakora shapes from mixture. 6. Heat oil and deep fry pakoras until golden and crispy. 7. For chutney, grind coriander, curry leaves, green chilli, ginger, peanuts, cumin seeds, black salt and lemon juice into smooth paste with water. 8. Drain pakoras on paper towels and serve hot with chutney.

name: Dudhori
instructions: 1. Wash rice and soak briefly. 2. In a heavy-bottomed pot, bring full cream milk to boil. 3. Add washed rice and 2 tablespoons sugar. 4. Cook rice in milk on medium flame until rice is soft and milk reduces. 5. Add crushed cardamom and saffron strands while rice is cooking. 6. In a separate pan, heat ghee and fry small portions of the cooked rice mixture until golden. 7. In a serving bowl, add remaining 1 cup sugar with 3 cups full cream milk and cardamom. 8. Mix the fried rice pieces into this sweet milk base. 9. Garnish with saffron and cardamom. 10. Serve warm or chilled.

name: Green Moong Modak
instructions: 1. Wash and soak green moong for 30 minutes. 2. Cook moong in pressure cooker or pot until completely soft and cooked, drain excess water if any. 3. Heat 2 tablespoons ghee in a pan, add crushed jaggery and cook until it melts and combines. 4. Add cooked moong to jaggery, mix well. 5. Add crushed cardamom and mix thoroughly. 6. Heat remaining 2 tablespoons ghee in a pan, fry cashews until golden, add to moong mixture and stir. 7. Let mixture cool until it can be handled. 8. Using traditional modak mold or hands, shape mixture into modak shape. 9. Fry each modak in ghee until golden and crispy on outside. 10. Serve warm as prasad or sweet treat.

name: Chandrakala Gujhiya
instructions: Mix refined flour with ghee to form breadcrumb texture. Prepare filling by mixing mawa, coconut, nuts, dried fruits, sugar powder and cardamom. Roll dough, stuff with filling, shape into gujhiya, and deep fry until golden. Store in airtight container.

name: Matar ki Saloni
instructions: Heat oil, temper with fenugreek and cumin seeds. Add ginger and green chilli. Add diced potatoes and cook till partially tender. Add peas and tomatoes. Season with salt, spices, and cook until potatoes are done. Finish with lemon juice and fresh coriander.

name: Coconut Mysore Pak
instructions: Toast desiccated coconut lightly in ghee. Add sugar and mix well. Cook on medium heat, stirring continuously until mixture pulls away from pan. Pour on greased surface, cool slightly, and cut into pieces. Garnish with pistachio flakes.

name: Jowar Kachori
instructions: Make dough with sorghum flour, wheat flour, salt, spices and ghee. Prepare filling by mixing boiled and mashed potatoes with peas, spices, and herbs. Roll dough, stuff with filling, shape into kachori, and deep fry on medium heat until crispy and golden.

name: Ragi Churma Laddu
instructions: Roast ragi flour in ghee until fragrant. Add cream and milk, cook until absorbed. Add roasted nuts and seeds, mix well. Add jaggery powder and cardamom, mix thoroughly. Cool and shape into laddus by hand.

name: Murmura Laddu
instructions: Melt jaggery in ghee on low heat until it reaches soft-ball stage. Add puffed rice, mix quickly but gently to avoid crushing. Shape into laddus by hand using damp hands while still warm. Cool before storing.

name: Khas Khas Halwa
instructions: Soak poppy seeds for 30 minutes, then grind into fine paste. Heat ghee, add poppy seed paste and roast for 2-3 minutes. Add warm milk gradually, stirring continuously. Add sugar and cardamom powder. Cook on low heat, stirring frequently until halwa thickens. Garnish with almond flakes.

name: Bajra Khichdi
instructions: Wash pearl millet and moong dal. Heat ghee, add cumin seeds, green chilli and ginger. Add all vegetables, cook for 2 minutes. Add millet and dal with 1.5 cups water. Bring to boil, then simmer covered on low heat for 15-20 minutes until millet and dal are cooked and water is absorbed. Season with salt and turmeric. Garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Bajra Kadhi Bhat
instructions: Cook bajra and rice together with ghee and salt until done. For kadhi: whisk gram flour with curd, ginger-chilli paste and salt. Heat oil in a pan, temper with cumin and fenugreek seeds. Add the gram flour-yogurt mixture and cook until thickened, stirring occasionally. Add remaining spices. Serve hot kadhi over the bajra-rice.

name: No-Fry Samosa
instructions: Make a stiff dough with flour, oil and salt. Rest for 15 minutes. Prepare filling: mash boiled potatoes, add finely chopped onion, green chilli, ginger, coriander and spices. Roll dough thin between plastic, cut into triangles, fill and fold into samosa shape. Brush with oil, arrange on baking tray and bake at 200°C for 20-25 minutes until golden and crispy.

name: Cauliflower Poori with Cornmeal
ingredients: oil 2 tsp for dough and deep frying
instructions: Grate cauliflower finely and squeeze out excess moisture. Mix with flours, salt and all spices. Add curd and oil. Mix well to form a stiff dough and rest 10 minutes. Add baking soda just before frying. Heat oil in a deep pan. Roll small portions thin on plastic, fry in hot oil until puffed and golden brown on both sides. Serve hot.

name: Semolina Namakpare
instructions: Mix semolina, wheat flour, salt and all seeds in a bowl. Add ghee and mix with fingers until breadcrumb texture. Gradually add warm water and knead into a stiff dough. Knead well for 3-4 minutes. Roll thin on plastic sheet, prick with fork, cut into diamond shapes. Fry in hot ghee at medium-high heat until light golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels.

name: Jowar Dhokle
instructions: Mix sorghum flour, gram flour and all dry spices in a bowl. Add curd, oil and ginger-chilli paste. Mix well to form a thick batter. Add baking soda just before steaming and fold gently. Grease a steamer vessel or mould with oil, pour batter and tap to remove air bubbles. Steam for 20-25 minutes until cooked through. Cool slightly and serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Sama Rice Potato Patties
instructions: Cook sama rice with 1 tsp ghee and rock salt until tender but not mushy. Mash boiled potatoes and mix with rock salt, black pepper, ginger, chopped green chilli, coriander and lemon juice. Form rice patties and place potato filling in center. Heat 1 tsp ghee in a pan, temper with cumin seeds, add patties and pan-fry on both sides until golden. Serve hot with extra coriander and lemon juice.

name: Spicy Bread Karanji
instructions: Mash boiled potatoes and mix with salt, chopped green chilli, ginger, and all spice powders and coriander. Make batter with gram flour, rice flour, salt, carom seeds, chilli, turmeric, sesame and baking soda mixed with water to thick consistency. Cut bread into triangles, coat with potato filling, then dip in batter to coat completely. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy on all sides. Drain on paper towels.

name: Maheri
instructions: Whisk curd in a bowl until smooth and lump-free. In a separate pan, heat 1 tsp ghee and roast pearl millet flour on medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until fragrant and lightly golden (3-4 minutes). Cool the roasted flour for 2 minutes. Add roasted flour and salt to whisked curd, mix well. Heat remaining 1 tsp ghee in a small pan, add cumin seeds and curry leaves, let them crackle for 10 seconds. Pour this tempering over the maheri and mix gently. Serve warm.

name: Coconut Modak
instructions: Mix milk and cream in a pan. Add sugar and dessicated coconut. Cook over medium heat, stirring continuously until the mixture thickens and becomes fudgy (about 5-7 minutes). Add crushed cardamom. Let cool slightly. Divide into portions and shape into modak (dumpling shape). Garnish with almond and pistachio flakes. Serve at room temperature or chilled.

name: Instant Turmeric Ginger Lemon Pickle
instructions: Peel and chop ginger, turmeric, and lemon into small pieces. Mix with salt and let sit for 15 minutes. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, black pepper, carom seeds, and asafoetida. Mix well. Add vinegar and stir thoroughly. Transfer to an airtight container. Ready to eat immediately or can be stored for several days. Serve as a condiment with meals.

name: Jimikand Tikka
instructions: Peel elephant yam and cut into 2-inch pieces. Blanch in salted boiling water for 3-4 minutes until partially cooked. Drain. Season with salt, turmeric, lemon juice, coriander powder, red chilli, cumin powder, mango powder, garam masala, fenugreek, and ginger. Mix gram flour, rice flour, and 2 tbsp curd into a smooth batter. Coat yam pieces in batter. Deep fry in mustard oil until golden and crispy on all sides. For chutney: blend fresh coriander, green chilli, ginger, black salt, mint powder, cumin seeds with 4 tbsp curd until smooth. Serve hot tikka with chutney on the side.

name: Dhokla Veggie Sandwich
instructions: For besan batter: whisk gram flour with 3/8 cup curd, 1/2 tsp ginger, green chilli, sugar, 1/4 tsp salt, and 1/2 tsp eno. Let rest 10 minutes. For suji batter: mix semolina with 1/2 cup curd, remaining ginger, green chilli, salt, and eno. Let rest 10 minutes. For filling: sauté ginger and green chilli, add carrot, capsicum, and tomato. Cook 3 minutes. Add red chilli, turmeric, garam masala, and tomato sauce. Cook 2 more minutes. Oil a round container. Pour besan batter into base, spread suji batter on top. Steam 8-10 minutes until set. Turn out onto a plate. Cut into sandwich slices. Layer with vegetable filling and coriander. Serve warm.

name: Bajra Tikki
instructions: Roast sesame seeds in a dry pan over medium-low heat, stirring frequently until fragrant and lightly toasted (about 3-4 minutes). Set aside. In a heavy bowl, warm ghee slightly. Add pearl millet flour and sugar. Mix thoroughly with the warmed ghee until evenly combined. Fold in roasted sesame seeds and mix well. Divide dough into 8-10 equal portions. Form each into a flat, round patty about 1/2 inch thick. Heat a non-stick griddle or pan over medium heat. Place tikkis on the hot griddle. Pan-fry for 2-3 minutes on each side until golden brown and crispy. Remove to a plate and serve warm. Store cooled tikkis in an airtight container.

name: Bajra Mooli Paratha
instructions: Grate radish using a fine grater. Sprinkle salt over grated radish and let sit for 10 minutes. Squeeze out excess water thoroughly using a clean cloth. In a large bowl, mix pearl millet flour with squeezed grated radish, green chilli, ginger, cumin seeds, carom seeds, salt, and chopped coriander. Knead gently into a soft dough, adding a little water at a time if needed to bring it together (dough should be soft, not sticky). Divide dough into 3-4 equal portions. On a parchment paper, roll each portion into a thin, even roundel. Heat a non-stick griddle over medium-high heat. Apply a small amount of ghee to the griddle. Transfer paratha onto griddle. Cook for 1-2 minutes until the bottom starts to brown lightly. Flip and cook the other side until crispy and golden spots appear (about 2-3 minutes). Apply a dot of ghee on top while cooking if desired. Serve hot, optionally with more ghee.

name: Besan-Sooji Barfi
instructions: Heat 1/2 cup ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat. Add semolina slowly and roast, stirring constantly to avoid lumping, for 3-4 minutes until light golden and fragrant. Add gram flour and roast for another 2 minutes, stirring continuously. In a separate pan, combine sugar with 1/4 cup water and bring to a boil, stirring until sugar dissolves completely. Pour the sugar syrup into the roasted semolina-gram flour mixture, stirring constantly to combine evenly (mixture will be thick). Add 1 tbsp ghee, dessicated coconut, milk powder, and crushed cardamom. Mix thoroughly until well combined. Grease a rectangular tray or baking sheet with remaining 1.5 tbsp ghee. Transfer the mixture onto the tray and spread evenly with a spatula. Let cool for about 10-15 minutes until it sets slightly but is still warm. Cut into squares or diamond shapes using a knife. Allow to cool completely before storing. Keep in an airtight container.

name: Paneer in Roasted Tomato Gravy
instructions: Roast tomatoes over flame until charred. Remove and cool. Grind roasted tomatoes into paste. Heat oil, add cumin seeds, ginger-green chilli, then gram flour and spices. Add tomato paste and salt. Simmer until thickened. Cut paneer into cubes and add to the gravy. Cook on low heat for 2-3 minutes. Finish with fenugreek leaves, ghee, garam masala and fresh coriander. Serve hot with rice or bread.

name: Sama Rice Pancake
instructions: Grind sama rice coarsely. Mix with curd, mashed boiled potatoes, grated ginger, green chilli, black pepper, grated carrot, rock salt and fresh coriander. Heat oil on griddle. Pour batter and cook pancakes until golden on both sides. For chutney, grind coriander, ginger, green chilli, coconut, rock salt with lemon juice. Serve pancakes hot with chutney.

name: Aloo Tikkad Paratha
instructions: Make soft dough with wheat flour, salt, carom seeds, oil and water. Rest for 15 minutes. For filling, mash boiled potatoes and mix with all spices including ginger, green chilli, coriander. Divide dough into 4 parts. Roll each portion, place spiced potato filling in center, seal edges and roll gently. Cook on hot griddle with oil until golden brown spots appear on both sides. Serve hot with yogurt or pickle.

name: Rice Veggie Soup
instructions: Chop beans, carrot, tomato, capsicum into small pieces. Heat butter in a pot. Saute ginger briefly. Make smooth paste of rice flour with water. Add chopped vegetables to butter, stir well. Pour water gradually while stirring to avoid lumps. Add rice flour paste and mix continuously. Simmer on medium heat for 8-10 minutes until vegetables are soft and soup thickens. Season with rock salt, white pepper, cumin powder. Add lemon juice. Garnish with fresh coriander. Serve hot.

name: Stuffed Besan Masala Chilli
instructions: Make a paste by mixing gram flour with salt, coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric, red chilli powder, dry mango powder, garam masala, ginger-chilli paste and fresh coriander. Add water as needed to form thick paste. Slit green chillis lengthwise carefully, keeping stem intact. Remove seeds gently. Stuff each chilli with the prepared gram flour mixture. Heat oil in a pan and shallow fry or deep fry the stuffed chillis until skin turns golden and slightly charred. Drain on paper towel. Serve hot as side dish with dal-rice or bread.

name: Banana Tuk
instructions: Peel raw bananas and cut into thin diagonal slices or small pieces. Season with rock salt and let sit for few minutes. Heat ghee or oil in deep pan until hot. Fry banana pieces in batches until golden and crispy on all sides. Drain on paper towel. For chutney, grind together coriander leaves, peanuts, green chillis, ginger, cumin seeds, rock salt and lemon juice into coarse paste. Serve crispy banana tuk hot with fresh chutney. Best served immediately.

name: Mix Dal Mangode
instructions: Soak all dals separately for 30 minutes then drain completely. Do not grind - keep as dry coarse texture. Mix all drained dals together. Dry roast coriander seeds, fennel seeds, cumin seeds and carom seeds briefly, then crush coarsely. Add roasted seeds to dal mixture along with chopped fenugreek, fresh coriander, turmeric, garam masala, red chilli, ginger-chilli paste, green chilli, salt and asafoetida. Mix well. Shape into small balls or patties. Heat oil in deep pan until hot. Fry mangodes in batches until golden brown and crispy on all sides. Drain on paper towel. Serve hot with tea.

name: Gur Poli
instructions: Make soft dough with wheat flour, refined flour, gram flour, salt, oil and water. Rest for 15-20 minutes. Dry roast sesame seeds until fragrant. Crush peanuts and sesame together coarsely. Heat ghee, add jaggery and melt completely. Add roasted sesame-peanut mixture, coconut and crushed cardamom. Mix well and cool. Divide dough into 4 equal portions. Roll each into thin circle. Place jaggery filling in center, fold edges over and gently roll into thick circle. Cook on hot griddle with oil until light golden brown on both sides, gently pressing with spatula. Serve warm with ghee.

name: Malai Paneer Barfi
instructions: Mix malai paneer with condensed milk until smooth. Roast the mixture in ghee for 5-7 minutes, stirring constantly. Add crushed cardamom. Spread on a greased tray, cool completely, and cut into pieces.

name: Whole Wheat Samosa
instructions: Make dough with wheat flour, semolina, carom seeds, salt, ghee and water. For filling, heat oil, add ginger and green chilli, add peas and boiled mashed potatoes, season with spices. Stuff samosas, shape into triangles, and deep fry until golden brown.

name: Til Ki Chikki
instructions: Heat sugar in a pan until it turns golden. Add toasted sesame seeds and mix well. Spread the mixture on a buttered tray. Cool completely and break into pieces.

name: Til Gud Peanut Laddu
instructions: Roast wheat flour in ghee until fragrant. Toast sesame and peanuts separately. Melt jaggery over low heat. Mix roasted wheat flour with jaggery, add toasted sesame and peanuts, add dry ginger, nutmeg, and cardamom. Fold in almonds and raisins. Cool slightly and roll into laddus.

name: Potato Cluster Fritters
instructions: Boil and mash potatoes. Mix with semolina, gram flour, and all spices including turmeric, red chilli, carom seeds. Add ginger, green chilli, kasuri methi, and fresh coriander. Add baking soda just before frying. Form into clusters and deep fry until golden brown.

name: Nipattu
instructions: Mix rice flour, refined flour, semolina, nuts, and spices. Add curry leaves and asafoetida. Combine with oil and enough water to make a stiff dough. Roll thin, cut into long strips or desired shapes, and deep fry until crispy.

name: Badam Halwa
instructions: Blanch almonds and grind to a fine paste. Heat ghee, add ground almond paste and fry for 2-3 minutes. Add semolina and toast lightly. Gradually add milk, stirring constantly. Add sugar, saffron soaked in warm milk, and crushed cardamom. Cook on low heat until thick and creamy.

name: Gond Pak
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan. Add wheat flour and toast until golden. Add almond flakes, dessicated coconut, and dry ginger powder, frying until fragrant. Add jaggery and nutmeg powder, mixing well. Continue cooking until the mixture turns dark brown. Pour onto a greased tray, cool slightly, then cut into pieces. Store in an airtight container.

name: Sabudana Modak
instructions: Soak sabudana for 30 minutes. Heat ghee and fry soaked sabudana until translucent. Add sugar, cream, dessicated coconut, almonds and pistachios, stirring well. In a separate pan, boil milk and add the sabudana mixture. Cook until thick. Temper with cardamom powder. Pour into modak molds, steam for 5 minutes until set. Cool and remove from molds.

name: Bajra Aloo Paratha
instructions: Boil and mash potatoes. Mix millet flour with salt and a little water to form a dough. Stuff with mashed potato mixture flavored with green chilli, red chilli, ginger, coriander, carom and cumin seeds. Roll out into round flatbreads. Cook on a griddle with ghee until golden brown spots appear on both sides. Serve hot.

name: Atta Besan Laddu
instructions: Heat 1/2 cup ghee in a pan. Add wheat flour and toast over medium heat for 3-4 minutes until light golden. Add gram flour and semolina, continuing to toast for 2-3 minutes. Add cream and mix well, cooking for 1-2 minutes. Remove from heat. Add almond flakes, cashews, pistachio flakes, boora and cardamom powder. Mix until combined. Cool slightly, then shape into laddus using wet hands or an ice cream scoop. Set aside to cool completely. Store in an airtight container.

name: Rice Kheer
instructions: Soak saffron in a little warm milk. In a heavy-bottomed pot, heat ghee and fry cashews, almonds, coconut, fox nuts, chironji and raisins until golden. Set aside. Add rice to the same pot and fry briefly. Pour in full cream milk gradually, stirring continuously. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Simmer for 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until rice is cooked and milk thickens. Add sugar and soaked saffron with its milk. Add cardamom powder. Stir in the fried nuts and dry fruits. Serve warm or chilled.

name: Apple and Pecan Pie
instructions: Prepare pie crust by mixing flour, butter, egg, salt and vanilla. Roll out and line pie pan. Toss sliced apples with cinnamon and sugar-free green sweetener. Spread apple mixture in pie shell. Sprinkle chopped pecans over apples. Top with pie crust or lattice pattern. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 45-50 minutes until golden brown. Cool before serving.

name: Har Gao
instructions: Prepare soft dough with flour, salt and water. Knead well and let rest briefly. Divide into small balls and roll thin into wrappers. Prepare filling with finely chopped vegetables, onion and cabbage. Place filling in center of wrapper, fold and seal edges. Steam dumplings for 5-7 minutes until wrappers become translucent and filling is cooked through. Serve hot.

name: Nachos
instructions: Prepare corn dough and cut into tortilla-sized pieces. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels and season with salt. Layer crispy tortillas on a plate, top with shredded cheese and bake briefly until cheese melts. Garnish with fresh onions, cilantro, jalapeños, and serve with sour cream on the side.

name: Blueberry Gelato
instructions: Blend fresh blueberries until smooth. Mix blueberry puree with heavy cream and whole milk. Add Sugar Free Green sweetener to taste and blend well. Pour mixture into an ice cream maker and churn according to machine instructions, typically 20-25 minutes, until gelato reaches soft-serve consistency. Transfer to a container and freeze for at least 2 hours before serving.

name: aam panna
instructions: Boil raw mangoes until soft. Extract pulp and strain. Mix pulp with sugar, cumin powder, black salt, and pudina powder. Add cumin seeds (crushed or whole as per preference). Dilute with water to desired consistency. Chill and serve cold. Drink to get instant coolness and protection from heat.

name: Dal Tadka
ingredients: garlic 30 cloves (10 for boiling, 20 for cooking)
instructions: Boil dal with 10 garlic cloves, 2 tomatoes, 1 tsp turmeric, salt and 4 cups water until dal is completely soft. In a separate pan, heat mustard oil and add bay leaves, star anise, black cardamom, cumin seeds. When they splutter, add 20 garlic cloves, chopped ginger and green chillies. Fry for 1-2 minutes. Add 1 cup onions and fry until golden. Add 1 cup tomatoes, coriander powder, red chilli powder, and remaining 1 tsp turmeric. Cook for 3-4 minutes until oil separates. Add boiled dal and mix well.
