Add garam masala, black pepper and salt. Simmer for 5 minutes. For tadka: Heat ghee, add 4 dry red chillies and 1 inch ginger, fry until fragrant. Add kashmiri red chilli powder. Pour over dal. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Sheer Khurma
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan, add chopped almonds, pistachios, walnuts, and dates. Roast on low flame until golden brown. Add milk and sugar with cardamom powder. Reduce milk to 30-40% of original amount while dates release their color. In another pan, heat ghee and break vermicelli into half, roast until golden brown. Add roasted vermicelli to reduced milk, stir gently and bring to quick boil. Vermicelli will absorb milk. Add saffron strands if desired. Serve warm.

name: Ukadiche Modak
instructions: Boil water with salt. Mix rice flour and ghee, slowly add boiling water while stirring to form a soft dough. For filling, combine grated coconut, jaggery, cardamom powder, poppy seeds, dry fruits and ghee. Stuff the dough mixture into modak molds, steam for 8-10 minutes until cooked. Garnish with kesar and ghee before serving.

name: Motichoor Laddoo
instructions: Sieve besan twice to make it fine. Heat ghee and oil in a deep pan at medium-high temperature. When oil is hot, add sieved besan slowly and fry continuously while stirring, creating fine pearls (motichoor). Fry until besan turns golden and crispy (about 8-10 minutes). Spread on paper towel to cool and drain excess oil. Make sugar syrup by dissolving sugar in 3/4 cup water, cook for 2-3 minutes until light consistency. Add the fried besan pearls to warm sugar syrup, add crushed cardamom and food colour. Mix well and let cool slightly. Form into balls of desired size while still warm.

name: veg manchow soup
instructions: 1. Heat 4 cups vegetable stock in a pot and bring to boil. 2. Add garlic, ginger, green chilli, and cook for 1 minute. 3. Add shredded cabbage, diced carrot, and mushroom. Cook for 3-4 minutes. 4. Add light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, and chilli paste. Stir well. 5. Mix corn flour with water to make slurry. Add to soup while stirring to thicken. 6. Add vinegar, white pepper powder, and salt. Adjust seasoning. 7. Bring to boil and simmer for 2-3 minutes. 8. Serve hot, garnished with spring onion greens and fried noodles.

name: Litti Chokha
ingredients: chana sattu roasted gram flour 1.5 cups
instructions: For litti: Mix sattu with salt, ajwain, kalonji, green chilli, ginger, coriander, garlic, onion, lemon juice, mango pickle, and mustard oil. Make a dough with wheat flour, salt, ghee, and water. Roll into small balls, stuff with sattu filling, and seal. Roast in pressure cooker at low heat or bake until cooked through. For chokha: Roast baingan and tomatoes directly on flame or in oven until charred. Boil potato and peel. Mash roasted vegetables together with garlic, onion, green chilli, ginger, coriander, black salt, lemon juice, and mustard oil. Serve litti dunked in ghee with chokha on the side.

name: Ragi Masala Roti
instructions: Mix ragi flour with finely chopped vegetables (onion, cucumber, cabbage, capsicum), coriander leaves, cumin seeds and ginger-garlic paste. Knead into a dough with water and salt. Prepare tomato chutney by frying urad dal and chana dal, then adding ginger, garlic, onion, tomato and red chilli, cook until soft. Cook the roti on a hot tawa with oil until both sides are golden. Prepare tadka by tempering mustard seeds and curry leaves in oil.

name: Louki Chana dal Sabzi
instructions: Heat ghee and oil, add cumin seeds and bay leaf. Add soaked chana dal and cook for 8-10 minutes. Sauté onion, garlic, ginger and green chilli until fragrant. Add tomato and cook until soft. Add spice powders (turmeric, red chilli powder, garam masala, coriander powder, black cardamom). Add chopped louki and cook covered until tender (about 15-20 minutes). Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Sooji Halwa with Banana
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan on medium heat. Add sooji and roast until golden and fragrant (about 3-4 minutes), stirring constantly. In another vessel, dissolve sugar in 1 cup water to make a light syrup. Pour the sugar syrup into roasted sooji slowly and stir to avoid lumps. Mash 4 ripe bananas and add to the mixture. Add crushed cardamom powder and mix well. Add chopped dry fruits (cashews, raisins). Cook on low heat for 2-3 minutes, stirring gently until it reaches halwa consistency.

name: Stuffed Bhindi
instructions: Toast coriander seeds, fennel seeds, cumin seeds, black pepper, cloves, and black cardamom. Grind roasted spices with peanuts. Cut okra lengthwise and carefully stuff with spice mixture. Heat oil in pan, add okra pieces, sprinkle turmeric, salt, red chilli powder, dry mango powder, and asafoetida. Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally until okra is tender and golden.

name: Aloo ki Sabzi
instructions: Cut potatoes into small cubes. Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add asafoetida and curry leaves. Finely mince garlic and add to the pan, sauté for 30 seconds. Add the potato cubes and cook for 5-7 minutes on medium heat, stirring occasionally. Add all spice powders (turmeric, red chilli, cumin, coriander, garam masala) and the kashmiri red chilli and mix well. Add chopped tomatoes and salt, stir everything together. Cover and cook on low heat for 15-20 minutes until potatoes are completely tender, stirring occasionally. The potatoes should become soft and absorb all the flavours. Garnish generously with coriander leaves before serving.

name: Dhokli nu Shaak
instructions: For dhokli: Mix besan with 3 cups yogurt, minced ginger, chopped green chilli, 1 tsp cumin seeds, and a pinch of turmeric powder and salt. Let rest for 15-20 minutes. In a pot, bring water to boil with a pinch of turmeric and salt. Pinch off small portions of the dhokli dough and drop into boiling water. Once they float, remove and keep aside. For curry: Heat oil, add mustard seeds and let crackle. Break dry red chilli and add it along with asafoetida and remaining cumin seeds. Add minced garlic and cook briefly. Whisk 3 tbsp yogurt with red chilli powder and pour into the pan. Add cooked dhokli and simmer on low heat for 3-4 minutes. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Tawa Shimla Mirch
ingredients: onion 2 cups total (1 cup cubed plus 1 whole finely chopped)
tomato 2 cups total (1 cup cubed plus 1 whole finely chopped)
instructions: Heat a pan on high heat and add coriander seeds, cumin seeds, fennel seeds, fenugreek seeds, white sesame seeds, and whole black pepper. Roast for about 30 seconds, stirring, until fragrant. Transfer to mortar and pestle and crush into coarse powder, do not make fine. Heat 2 tbsp oil in a tawa and add chopped potatoes. Fry until golden brown and 80% cooked, then remove and set aside. Add 1 tbsp oil to the tawa and add large cubes of onion and capsicum. Sauté on high heat for 1 minute. Add large cubes of tomato and mix. Sauté briefly on high heat to keep vegetables crunchy, then remove and set aside. Add 3 tbsp oil to the tawa, add cumin seeds and slit green chillies. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for a moment. Add finely chopped onion and sauté for 2 minutes until softened. Add finely chopped tomato and salt, cook on low heat for 3-4 minutes until soft. Add turmeric powder and red chilli powder and mix well. Add the fried potatoes to the masala and mix well. Cook on low heat to allow potatoes to fully cook and absorb flavours. Add the sautéed onion, capsicum, and tomato cubes. Add the prepared roasted spice blend and adjust salt. Mix everything well on low-medium heat. Cover and cook for 3-4 minutes on low heat. Uncover and ensure vegetables are cooked but still crunchy. Add lemon juice, mix well and serve hot.

name: Oats Chilla
instructions: Mix rolled oats, besan, grated beetroot, ginger, and green chilli. Add water to make a thick batter. Season with salt. Pour onto a greased pan and cook until golden brown on both sides. Serve hot.

name: Dal Baati Churma
instructions: Cook mixed dals with water and turmeric until soft. In a separate pan, temper cumin seeds in oil, add onions, green chilli, ginger-garlic paste and cook. Add tomato puree, spices (red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala) and salt. Combine with cooked dal. For baati: mix aata, sooji, ghee, baking soda, salt and water to make dough. Shape into balls and bake. For churma: crush baati with ghee, jaggery powder, dry fruits powder, and cardamom powder. Serve dal with baati and churma.

name: Mexican Rice
instructions: Heat butter and oil in a pan. Add chopped garlic and onions, cook until translucent. Add diced capsicums and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add tomato puree, ketchup, schezwan chutney and mix well. Add mixed herbs, oregano, and red chilli flakes. Add boiled rajma and sweet corn. Finally add boiled rice and toss everything together. Season with salt and cook for 2-3 minutes until well combined. Serve hot.

name: Farali Pattice
ingredients: coriander, chilli and ginger paste - 2 tbsp
instructions: Boil and mash potatoes. Mix with arrowroot powder, dry coconut powder, almond-cashew crush, raisins, coriander-chilli-ginger paste, peanut powder, lemon juice, and salt. Shape into patties. Deep fry until golden brown. For green chutney: blend coriander leaves, green chilli, ginger, curd, lemon juice and salt. Serve pattice hot with green chutney.

name: High Protein Laddu
ingredients: mixed dry fruits and berries - 1/2 cup
instructions: Roast all nuts and seeds (almonds, cashews, pista, oats, pumpkin seeds, flax seeds) separately until fragrant. Roast peanuts until golden. Grind all roasted nuts and seeds to a powder consistency. Mix with cinnamon powder. Add honey if desired and mix well. Roll into small laddoo balls. Store in an airtight container. Makes approximately 25 laddoos.

name: Vrat ka Dosa
instructions: Soak sama rice and sabudana separately for 15-20 minutes. Grind them with curd to a thick batter consistency. Add rock salt and water as needed. For green chutney: blend coriander leaves, roasted peanuts, green chilli, lemon juice, ginger, curd and cumin seeds. For aloo masala: heat ghee, add boiled cubed potatoes, green chilli, ginger, curry leaves, roasted peanuts, cumin seeds and rock salt. Cook until fragrant. Pour dosa batter on a greased tawa and cook until edges turn golden. Flip and cook the other side. Serve hot dosa filled with aloo masala and accompanied by green chutney.

name: Chana Chaat
instructions: Boil chickpeas until tender. Dice onion, tomato, and cucumber. Mix cooked chickpeas with diced vegetables. Add lemon juice, chaat masala, salt and pepper. Toss well. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve immediately.

name: Sooji Dosa
instructions: For dosa batter: Mix rawa, besan, curd, salt and water to form a pancake-like consistency. For red chutney: Heat oil, add mustard, cumin, urad dal, chana dal. When they splutter, add ginger, garlic, onion and fry until golden. Add tomato and dry red chili, cook till soft. Blend into smooth chutney. Heat oil on griddle, pour dosa batter, spread thin. Add paneer, schezwan chutney, coriander. Cook until golden and crispy on both sides. Serve hot with red chutney.

name: Jalebi
instructions: Make batter by combining flour, curd, baking soda and salt with water to achieve pourable consistency. Let batter rest for 2 hours for fermentation. Prepare sugar syrup by dissolving sugar in water over medium flame; add lemon juice and let cool slightly. Heat ghee or oil in deep pan to 160-170°C. Fill piping bag fitted with round tip with batter. Pipe spiral shapes directly into hot ghee, frying until golden and crisp on both sides. Remove with slotted spoon and immediately dip into warm sugar syrup. Make sure syrup is warm, not hot, so jalebi absorbs it without becoming too soggy. Sprinkle with cardamom powder and saffron if desired. Arrange on plate and serve warm. The jalebis will crisp up further as they cool.

name: Makhana Coconut Roll
instructions: Crush green cardamom and mix all dry ingredients together: makhana powder, coconut powder, cashew powder, sugar powder, cardamom powder, and coconut milk powder. Add milk and food colour, mix well to form a dough-like consistency. Divide into equal portions and roll into cylinder/log shapes. Decorate with saffron sprigs on top. Refrigerate for 1-2 hours and serve chilled. No cooking required.

name: Soya Pulao
ingredients: 700 gms sella basmati rice soaked 30 minutes
instructions: For soya pulao: in handi add oil and ghee, add sliced onions and saute until golden. Add bay leaf, cinnamon stick, green chillies and tender coriander stems, saute 1 minute. Add ginger garlic paste, curd, degi red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder and salt, cook until oil separates from masala. Add soya chunks and carrots, saute 7-8 minutes until cooked well. Add fresh green peas, 4 cups water, salt, tomatoes and bring to quick boil. Add soaked basmati rice, 1 cup water, ghee, salt and pinch of sugar, mix gently. Cover with lid and cook 12-15 minutes on low to medium heat until rice is tender. Finish with crushed fenugreek leaves and fresh coriander. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with fried onions, mint and coriander. For achaari raita: in pan roast cumin seeds with salt and sesame seeds for 1 minute. Cool, add beaten curd, pickle and grated cheese, mix well. Serve raita alongside pulao.

name: Sitafal Rabdi
instructions: Boil milk in a heavy-bottomed pan. Add milk powder and stir well. Reduce milk by half on medium heat, stirring occasionally (about 30 minutes). Extract custard apple pulp and remove seeds. Add sugar to the reduced milk and stir until dissolved. Fold in custard apple pulp gently. Garnish with grated dry nuts (almonds, cashews, pistachios). Serve warm or chilled.

name: Alu Vadi
instructions: Blanch arbi leaves in water. Grind green chilli, garlic cloves, and mango pickle into a paste. Mix gram flour, rice flour, turmeric, coriander powder, garam masala, salt, and sesame seeds. Add the chilli-garlic-pickle paste and mix well. Spread this mixture on arbi leaves, stack them, and slice. Pan-fry in oil until golden and crispy on both sides. Serve hot.

name: Kathal Sabzi
instructions: Cut jackfruit into chunks and soak in chilled water with salt and turmeric for 30 minutes to remove bitterness. Drain and marinate with salt, turmeric, garam masala, ginger-garlic paste, and mustard oil for 15 minutes. Heat remaining mustard oil in a pan, add dried red chillies and carom seeds. Add chopped garlic, ginger, and green chillies. Sauté for 1 minute. Add chopped onions and cook until golden. Add chilli powder and coriander powder. Stir in marinated jackfruit and pan-fry for 3-4 minutes. Add fresh tomato puree and water. Simmer for 15-20 minutes until jackfruit is tender. Add kasoori methi and chaat masala. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Vegetable Kofta
instructions: Make gravy: Heat oil and butter, add bay leaf, cinnamon, cloves, and cardamom. Add diced onions and cook until golden. Add ginger, garlic, and green chillies. Add cashew nuts. Sprinkle turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, and cumin powder. Add diced tomatoes and coriander. Add water and simmer for 20 minutes. Prepare koftas: Mix mashed potato with chopped vegetables, spices, and bhuna besan. Bind with bread crumbs. Shape into balls. Heat oil and fry koftas until golden. Add fried koftas to the simmering gravy and cook for 10 more minutes. Finish with cream, ginger, green chilli, and fresh coriander. Serve hot.

name: Moong Dal Chilla with Paneer
instructions: Soak moong dal and grind to a paste with cumin, turmeric, heeng, chilli powder, salt, curry leaves, green chilli, ginger, and onion. Add water to make a thick batter. For paneer stuffing: crumble paneer and mix with salt, chilli powder, roasted cumin, coriander, green chilli, and kasoori methi. Heat a non-stick pan, pour batter to make a thin crepe, add paneer stuffing in the center, fold and cook until golden on both sides. Drizzle oil around edges.

name: Mango Cheesecake
instructions: Crush digestive biscuits and mix with melted butter to form base. Press in springform pan and set aside. Whisk hung curd until smooth, add condensed milk and dairy cream, mix well. Dissolve cornstarch in warm milk and add to curd mixture. Pour filling over biscuit base. Bake at 170°C for 35 minutes. Cool completely and refrigerate for 2-3 hours. Peel and slice mangoes into thin strips for garnish. Unmold cheesecake, arrange mango slices on top and garnish with mint sprigs.

name: Onion Bhajia
instructions: Slice onions into rings and separate them. Mix salt, ajwain, ginger, green chillies, chilli powder, turmeric, and coriander into besan with minimal water to make a thick coating paste. Dip onion rings in the besan batter to coat evenly. Heat oil for deep frying, fry bhajia at medium-high heat until golden and crispy. For extra crispiness, remove and fry again briefly. Drain on paper towels. Serve hot with bhuni mirch ki chutney (made with mint leaves, coriander, ginger, garlic, green chillies, lemon juice, chaat masala, hung curd, and iced water blended together).

name: Subz Diwani Handi
instructions: Heat oil and fry sliced onions until golden. Separate onions for garnish and paste. Toast dry fruits (almonds, cashew). Bloom whole spices (bay leaf, caraway, peppercorn, cloves, cardamom). Add ginger garlic paste and cook. Add turmeric, kashmiri chilli, coriander and cumin powders. Add onion paste and cook until thick. Add yogurt and stir. Separately heat oil and butter, add chopped ginger and green chilli. Add prepared vegetables and cook until tender. Add paneer cubes. Combine both mixtures and cook together for 5-7 minutes. Garnish with fried onions, coriander, and spinach.

name: Kanji Vada
instructions: For kanji: Boil water with mustard seeds, yellow mustard, cumin, black salt and salt. Add chilli powder, turmeric and curd. Let cool and ferment overnight. For vada: Soak moong dal for 4 hours. Grind dal with ginger, green chilli and cumin into thick paste. Add salt, chilli flakes and coriander. Heat oil and fry dal by dropping spoonfuls. Fry until golden and crispy. Soak fried vadas in fermented kanji liquid. Serve with mint. Optional: Smoke kanji with cinnamon and asafoetida in hot ghee before soaking vadas.

name: Palak Patta Momos
instructions: Blanch spinach leaves in boiling water for 2 minutes, transfer to ice water, dry completely. Prepare filling: Heat oil, sauté garlic, ginger until fragrant. Add onion and cook until translucent. Add all chopped vegetables and cook until tender. Add mashed paneer, salt and pepper. Cool completely. To make momos: Place 1 tbsp filling on spinach leaf, fold and seal into dumpling shape. Prepare dipping sauce: Mix chilli flakes, garlic, spring onion, coriander, salt, vinegar, oil and soy sauce. Steam momos in steamer for 8-10 minutes until cooked. Serve hot with sauce.

name: Aloo Ka Halwa
instructions: Boil potatoes until completely cooked and tender. Peel and mash potatoes finely. Roast almonds, pista, cashew and walnuts in ghee separately, set aside. Heat 4 tbsp ghee in a pan, add mashed potatoes and roast for 3-4 minutes on high heat. Add warm milk gradually while stirring continuously. Keep roasting until mixture becomes thick and creamy, about 5-7 minutes. Grate jaggery and add to the halwa. Mix well. Add cardamom powder and nutmeg powder. Cook for another 2-3 minutes. Garnish with roasted dry fruits. Serve warm.

name: Nalli Nihari
instructions: Marinate mutton with salt, ginger garlic paste, turmeric and chilli powder for 30 minutes. Brown sliced onions in mustard oil until deep golden. Reserve some for garnish, crush remainder into paste. Cook onion paste with marinated mutton on high heat. Add yogurt and roast khus khus and coconut together, grind into smooth paste and add to meat. Prepare potli masala by toasting and grinding whole spices (coriander, cardamom, cloves, peppercorn, mace, bay leaves, nutmeg, black cardamom, fennel, cumin, rose petals, and other whole spices). Add this potli masala to mutton along with water. Simmer for 45-60 minutes until mutton is tender. Finish with besan paste, cashew paste, coriander powder, and aromatic spices. Garnish with fried onions, fresh coriander and lemon wedges.

name: Paneer Cutlet
instructions: Heat oil, add asafoetida and cumin. Add chopped ginger and green chilli, sauté. Add green peas and cook until tender. Add mashed paneer and spices (chilli powder, coriander powder, chaat masala, black salt). Mix in boiled mashed potato. Add fresh coriander and kasoori methi. Cool mixture completely. Soak bread slices in water and squeeze dry, add to mixture. Divide into 15 equal portions and shape into cutlets. Prepare besan slurry by mixing besan with salt and water. Prepare bread crumb coating by mixing bread crumbs with bhujia. Dip each cutlet in besan slurry, then coat with bread crumb mixture. Deep fry in hot oil until golden brown on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side. Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

name: Watermelon Ki Sabzi
instructions: Peel watermelon rind and dice into small cubes. Heat mustard oil until smoking. Add asafoetida, then add dried red chilli. Add mustard seeds, fennel seeds, cumin and kalonji. Once seeds crackle, add chopped ginger and garlic, sauté until fragrant. Add chopped onion and green chilli, cook until onion turns translucent. Add salt, turmeric, chilli powder and coriander powder. Mix well. Add diced watermelon rind and cook on high heat for 3-4 minutes. Add watermelon juice and cook for another 5-7 minutes. Add amchur powder and kasoori methi. Check seasoning. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot with roti or rice.

name: Methi Dana Ki Sabzi
instructions: Soak methi dana in water overnight or at least 4 hours. Boil soaked methi in the same water for 2-3 minutes, strain and reserve the water. Boil again in fresh water to reduce bitterness, strain and keep water aside. Heat mustard oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds, cumin and coriander seeds. Once seeds crackle, add chopped garlic and roast briefly. Add chopped onion and green chilli, cook until onion turns translucent. Dissolve jaggery in water separately. Add salt, turmeric, chilli powder and dhaniya powder to onion. Stir well. Add prepared methi dana and roast on high flame for 3-4 minutes. Add water and cook for 5-7 minutes. Add tamarind paste and jaggery water. Adjust seasoning. Garnish with fresh coriander. For methi water: Squeeze lemon in a glass, add honey, add cooled methi water that was reserved from boiling. Mix and serve as a health drink.

name: Shami Kebab
instructions: Toast all masala ingredients (bay leaves, black cardamom, peppercorns, mace, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, coriander seeds, caraway, cumin, dry red chillies) until aromatic. In a cooker, heat 2 tbsp ghee and add the toasted masala. Add boneless mutton pieces, mutton nalli, ginger, garlic, green chillies slit, turmeric, salt, onion, and chana dal. Add water and cook in pressure cooker for 2-3 whistles until meat is completely tender. Allow to cool. Pound the cooked mixture by hand (rather than using mixer grinder) to get a better texture. Mix in mint leaves, coriander leaves, chopped green chilli, and beaten egg. Form into round kebab shapes. Shallow fry kebabs in ghee until golden brown on both sides. Serve hot.

name: Aloo Shimla Mirch
instructions: Cut potatoes into medium cubes. Heat mustard oil and add asafoetida and cumin seeds, let them splutter. Add pounded coriander and toast for a minute. Sauté chopped onion, ginger, and green chillies until golden. Add turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, and coriander powder, cook for 1 minute. Add tomato pieces and cook until they are fully softened. Add potato cubes and salt, mix well. Cover and cook on medium heat for 8-10 minutes. Cut capsicum into large pieces and add to the curry along with amchur powder and chilli flakes. Cook until potatoes are tender and capsicum is soft. Finish with kasoori methi and fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot.

name: Aloo Matar
instructions: Cut potatoes into cubes. Heat oil and add asafoetida, black cardamom, and cumin seeds, let splutter. Add green chillies slit. Add ginger and garlic chopped, sauté for 30 seconds. Add chopped onion and cook until translucent and golden. Add turmeric, chilli powder, and coriander powder, cook for 1 minute. Add fresh tomato puree and salt, cook for 7-8 minutes on high heat until oil separates from the masala. Add potato cubes and mix well. Cover with pressure cooker lid and cook until potatoes are half done. Add water and green peas. Simmer for 8-10 minutes until potatoes are fully cooked and soft. Add amchur powder and kasoori methi powder, cook for 1 minute. Sprinkle garam masala and finish with fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot with roti or rice.

name: Paneer Korma
instructions: Soak cashew nuts in warm water for 10 minutes, then drain. Heat ghee in a pan and add sliced onions, sauté until light golden in color. Add ginger slices and garlic cloves, cook for 2 minutes. Add green chillies, garam masala, cardamom, cinnamon piece, mace, and fennel seeds. Toast aromatic spices for 1-2 minutes. Add drained cashew nuts and pour in milk. Simmer on medium heat for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally. Blend the entire mixture to make a smooth, creamy gravy, then strain through a fine sieve. In a separate pan, heat 2 tbsp ghee and shallow fry paneer slices until light golden on both sides. Add fried paneer to the creamy gravy. Simmer for 5-7 minutes until paneer absorbs the flavors. Finish with ghee and serve hot with rice or naan.

name: Korean Chicken Burger
instructions: Marinate chicken thighs with egg, ginger garlic paste, salt, and light soy sauce for 15-20 minutes. For Korean sauce: Boil water in a pan, add schezwan chutney, gochujang paste, and tomato ketchup, mix well and simmer briefly. Transfer to a bowl. For salad: Mix shredded cabbage, sliced cucumber, salt to taste, prepared korean sauce, and mayonnaise in a bowl. Mix refined flour, rice flour, and salt together. Coat marinated chicken pieces thoroughly with flour mixture on all sides. Heat oil for deep frying and fry chicken until golden and crispy. Transfer to absorbent sheet. Toast burger buns lightly in oil on both sides. Assemble burger: Place bottom bun, spread salad, place torn lettuce leaves, add fried chicken piece, drizzle korean sauce, add another fried chicken piece, more sauce, salad, some chips, top with upper bun. Serve hot with extra sauce and salad on the side.

name: Mixed Veg Drumstick Curry
instructions: For dal: Heat ghee in pressure cooker, add ginger slices and cumin seeds, let splutter. Add drumstick pieces, green chilli, soaked moong dal, salt, turmeric, asafoetida, and ghee. Add water and cook in pressure cooker for 2-3 whistles until dal is soft. Cool and transfer to mixer grinder, grind into smooth paste. For spice mixture: Mix water with coriander powder, turmeric, degi red chilli powder, and dry mango powder in a bowl. For curry: Heat ghee and oil in handi, add cumin, chopped ginger, green chillies, asafoetida, let splutter. Add prepared spice mixture and carrots, cook for 5-6 minutes. Add diced potatoes and salt, cover and cook on medium heat until potatoes soften. Add cooked dal paste and water, simmer for 10-12 minutes. Add water, cauliflower florets, and french beans, cook until all vegetables are tender. Finish with ghee, tender coriander stems, and diced tomato, give a quick boil. Garnish with coriander sprigs. Serve hot with steamed rice.

name: Paneer Keema Pav
instructions: Cut paneer into large cubes. Heat oil and deep fry paneer cubes until golden brown on all sides. Transfer to absorbent sheet to cool. Coarsely grind fried paneer in a chopper. Make ginger garlic green chilli paste by grinding ginger, green chillies, and garlic cloves into coarse paste using mortar pestle. For masala: Roast salt, black peppercorns, coriander seeds, fennel seeds, and cumin in a dry pan until aromatic, then grind into fine powder. In a handi, heat ghee and add cumin, add prepared ginger garlic paste, sauté for 1 minute. Add chopped onion and cook for 2-3 minutes until translucent. Add tender coriander stems, degi red chilli powder, turmeric, and coriander powder, cook for 1 minute. Add tomato puree, sugar, and salt, cook for 7-8 minutes until oil separates from masala. Add green peas, fried paneer crumbles, water, and salt, cook until paneer is soft. Add chopped tomato, kasoori methi, and prepared masala powder, add little water if needed, cover and cook for 3-4 minutes on medium flame. Finish with fresh coriander leaves and ghee. For pav: Heat ghee in a pan, sprinkle degi red chilli powder, toast ladi pav on both sides until golden. Serve hot paneer keema with toasted pav, garnish with lemon wedge, green and red chillies, coriander leaves.

name: Garlic Bread
instructions: Prepare garlic butter: heat oil until hot, add 18-20 garlic cloves and green chillies, cook until golden. Grind with coriander leaves, butter, black peppercorns, chilli flakes and oregano into smooth paste. Spread garlic butter on bread slices or dough, add pizza cheese, oregano and chilli flakes. For rolled version: stack slices and roll, place in baking tin, cover with foil and bake at 180°C for 10 minutes. For dough version: roll small dough ball, apply garlic butter, fold and seal edges, apply oil on both sides, cook on tawa over low flames for 10-12 minutes. For pull-apart: stack pav with garlic butter and cheese, bake at 180°C for 12-15 minutes. Garnish with coriander and serve hot.

name: Soya Kofta Curry
ingredients: 1 heaped tsp degi red chili powder
instructions: For cooked soya mixture: in pressure cooker add oil and ghee, add cumin seeds, black peppercorns, crushed garlic and onion, saute until golden. Add kashmiri red chillies, diced potatoes, ginger garlic paste, turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder and fenugreek leaves, cook 1 minute. Add soaked soya chunks and salt, cook on medium heat. Add 4 cups water and bring to boil. Add chana dal with ¾ cup water. Pressure cook for 2-3 whistles until tender. Cool and grind into fine paste. For curry: heat oil, add cumin and fennel seeds, onion and fry until golden. Add green chillies, ginger and tender coriander stems, fry 1 minute. Add curd and cook until oil separates. Add fresh tomato puree, salt and cook well. Add 2 cups water, simmer 7-8 minutes. Add cooked soya mixture, mix well. For koftas: mix cooked soya, fenugreek leaves, salt, dry mango powder and soya powder. Form into balls, coat with soya powder, deep fry until golden brown. Place fried koftas in curry gravy. Serve hot with roti.

name: Chilli Paneer Momos
instructions: Knead refined flour with salt, sugar, water and oil into smooth dough and rest. For stuffing: blanch carrots and cabbage with soy sauce until moisture releases, squeeze dry. Mix with ginger, paneer, coriander and black pepper. Prepare sauce by combining vinegar, soy sauce, chilli sauces, tomato ketchup and water. Shape dough balls, fill with paneer stuffing, seal and pleat into crescent shape. Deep fry in hot oil until crispy and golden. For serving: heat oil, sauté kashmiri chillies, onions and capsicum on high heat, add sauce mixture and fried momos, toss together for 1-2 minutes. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with sesame seeds, spring onions and coriander.

name: Mutton Tahari
instructions: In a handi, heat oil and ghee, fry sliced onions until golden. Add mutton with bones and ginger-garlic paste, sauté 4-5 minutes on high heat. Mix in curd, red chilli powder, turmeric powder and salt, cook 10-12 minutes. Add whole spices (bay leaves, cardamom, cinnamon stick, cloves) with water and cover, simmer until mutton is tender. Add diced potatoes and water. Add coriander stems, green chillies, coriander leaves, basmati rice, water, bhavnagri chillies, tomatoes, salt, mango pickle and more water. Cover and cook until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed. Rest for few minutes before serving. Garnish with fried onions and serve hot with onion salad (onions, green chillies, lemon juice, coriander, salt and red chilli powder).

name: Pav Bhaji Paratha
ingredients: 2 medium boiled potatoes, peeled and grated
1/4 cup fresh coriander leaves, finely chopped
instructions: Knead whole wheat flour with salt, carom seeds, 1/2 tsp oil and water into semi-soft dough, then knead in 1.5 tsp oil. Cover with damp cloth and rest. For stuffing: heat 2 tbsp ghee, add ginger and green chillies and cook 1 minute, add green peas with salt and 1/4 cup water, cover and cook. Add grated carrot and water, sauté 4-5 minutes. Add mashed boiled potatoes, 2 tbsp butter, pav bhaji masala, red chilli powder and salt, mash well. Finish with 1 tbsp butter, fresh coriander and lemon juice. Cool completely. Divide dough into medium balls, create cavity in center, fill with stuffing, seal edges and roll with rolling pin. Heat tawa with 2 tbsp ghee, place rolled paratha and cook evenly on both sides until golden. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with fried chillies and butter. Serve hot.

name: Garlic Lachha Paratha with Matar Masala
ingredients: 1 heaped tsp degi red chilli powder
instructions: For paratha: knead refined flour with salt and water into semi-soft dough, cover and rest 30 minutes. Prepare garlic-flavored oil by cooking chopped garlic in ghee and oil on low heat until golden, set aside. Roll dough into large thin sheet, grease with garlic-flavored oil, fold into pleats with fingers, stretch and roll pleated dough into coil shape, rest 10 minutes. Coat rolled ball in semolina-poppy seed-chilli powder mixture, roll again into thin sheet and apply garlic-flavored oil generously. Heat tawa with garlic-flavored oil, cook paratha on both sides until golden, grease with ghee and crush to form layers. For matar masala: heat mustard oil until smoky hot, add cumin seeds, crushed garlic, kashmiri chillies and soaked white peas, mix well. Add asafoetida, turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder and water, cover and pressure cook 3-4 whistles until peas are tender. Add salt and quick boil, finish with tadka (cumin, fennel, asafoetida and chilli powder fried in ghee), add mango powder and mix. Transfer to serving bowl, garnish with onion, lemon quarters, green chillies, coriander and julienned ginger. Serve hot lachha paratha with matar masala and curd.

name: Aloo Dum Biryani
instructions: Prepare chilli paste by cooking degi red chilli powder with ginger-garlic paste, jaggery and oil for 4-5 minutes until cooked. Soak basmati rice for 20 minutes. Cook rice in water with salt, bay leaves, peppercorns, cardamom, cloves and ghee until 80% cooked, then strain. Heat oil in handi, add curry leaves and cumin seeds. Add chopped garlic, green chillies and ginger, saute until translucent. Add peeled baby potatoes, salt and fried onions, saute for 2-3 minutes. Add beaten curd, cardamom and cloves, cook until potatoes soften. Add prepared chilli paste and water, cover and cook for 10-12 minutes. Layer with cooked rice, fried onions, saffron water, rose water, coriander and ghee. Cover and cook on dum (slow flame) for 15 minutes. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with coriander and mint sprigs. Serve hot with raita.

name: Paneer Crispy
instructions: Deseed and soak kashmiri red chillies in hot water for 1-2 hours, grind into fine paste with water. Heat oil until hot, add chilli paste and cook on medium-high flame for 4-5 minutes until crumbly and oil separates. Add vinegar and cool in bowl. Dust paneer pieces with corn flour using sieve, toss to coat all sides. Fry paneer at 175°C until slightly golden, transfer to sieve. Heat wok over high flame, add oil and heat. Add garlic, ginger, green chillies and saute for 30 seconds. Add capsicum, spring onion, cooked red chilli paste, ketchup, red chilli sauce, soy sauce, vinegar, white pepper and salt. Add hot water and bring to simmer. Make slurry with corn flour and water, add to sauce while stirring to thicken. Lower flame. Flash fry paneer for 20-30 seconds then strain and add to sauce with spring onion greens. Coat paneer with sauce on low flame. Serve hot with ketchup.

name: Aloo Palak
instructions: Wash spinach leaves and pat dry, then chop and transfer to bowl. Heat mustard oil in kadhai over high flame until smoking, lower flame and cool slightly. Add diced potatoes and fry over medium-high flame until 90% cooked, stirring frequently. Transfer cooked potatoes to bowl. In same oil, add red chilli, asafoetida, cumin seeds and onion, cook until onions turn light golden. Add garlic, ginger and green chilli, cook until onions turn completely golden. Lower flame and add all powdered spices with splash of hot water, stir well. Cook on high flame until oil separates. Add tomato, salt and sugar, stir and cook until tomatoes soften and oil separates. Add chopped spinach over masala, cover without stirring and cook on low flame for 3-4 minutes until spinach releases moisture. Uncover and cook stirring until all moisture evaporates. Add hot water to adjust consistency and cook spinach further. Add fried potatoes with lemon juice, stir and taste to adjust seasoning. Add garam masala and fresh coriander. Serve hot as side dish.

name: Mohanthal
instructions: Add gram flour to bowl with 2 tbsp ghee and 2 tbsp milk, rub flour between hands and press down into bowl. Cover and rest for 15-20 minutes. Break set flour and sieve through large hole sieve to create granular texture. Line tray with ghee and butter paper. Heat 0.75 cup ghee in kadhai over high flame, add sieved gram flour and stir well. Once flour soaks ghee, stir continuously on low flame until mixture turns biscuity in color (20-25 minutes). Switch off flame and gradually add 0.5 cup milk spoon by spoon as mixture will bubble. Once bubbling stops, add all remaining milk at once while stirring. Heat on low flame for 4-5 minutes until slight glaze appears. Switch off and stir for 2-3 minutes to cool slightly. In separate pan, combine sugar, water, cardamom powder, saffron and nutmeg, bring to boil over high flame. Lower flame and cook syrup to one-string consistency. Cool syrup for 2-3 minutes while stirring continuously. Add syrup to cooked gram flour and stir for 1-2 minutes until combined. Transfer mohanthal to lined tray and spread evenly. Optionally add slivered almonds and pistachios. Let set for 3 hours or overnight. Remove using butter paper and cut into pieces.

name: Veg Lemon Coriander Soup
instructions: Chop all vegetables into fine dices using chopper or knife. Cut paneer into fine dices. Trim coriander stalks and chop finely, keep separate from leaves. Add water to stock pot with stock cube and stir, bring to boil. Heat oil in wok over high flame. Add garlic, ginger, green chilli and coriander stems, cook for a few seconds. Add stock or hot water and bring to boil. Add chopped vegetables, white pepper powder, sugar, soy sauce, salt and paneer, cook for 2-3 minutes. Make slurry with corn flour and water in separate bowl, add to soup while stirring continuously to thicken. Add chopped fresh coriander leaves and lemon juice, stir well. Taste and adjust salt if required, add more lemon juice if needed. Add spring onion greens and serve hot.

name: Paneer Bhurji Sandwich
instructions: PANEER BHURJI: Heat butter and oil in pan over high heat. Cook gram flour on medium-low flame until golden brown, stirring. Add onion, tomato, green chilli, ginger and salt, cook on high flame for 2-3 minutes. Lower flame, add powdered spices with splash of hot water, cook for 1-2 minutes. Crumble paneer and add with milk, cook for 3-4 minutes, taste and adjust salt. Add kasuri methi, black pepper and fresh coriander. CHUTNEY: Grind fresh coriander, mint, roasted chana dal, green chillies, curry leaves, garlic, chaat masala, salt and ice cube into fine paste. OPEN TOAST: Apply butter and chutney on bread slice, add generous paneer bhurji and spread evenly, top with grated cheese. Heat pan with butter over high flame, place toast and cover. Cook on low flame until bread is light golden. Add splash of water around toast, cover and cook until cheese melts. SANDWICH: Apply butter and chutney on 3 bread slices. Layer first slice with paneer bhurji, add cheese slice, place second slice on top. Add tomato slices, chaat masala, onion and capsicum, top with third bread slice. Toast on gas toaster or pan over medium-high flame until crisp and golden on both sides. Cut as preferred and serve with ketchup and chutney.

name: Prawns Tawa Masala
instructions: Grind garlic, ginger, green chilli and fresh coriander with very little water into fine paste. Add cleaned deveined prawns to bowl with green paste, salt, turmeric powder and kokum agal, mix well and set aside to marinate. Heat tawa over high heat and add oil. Add onions and garlic cloves, stir well and cook over medium-high flame until golden brown. Once onions are light golden, add remaining green paste and cook until onions turn completely golden. Add hot water if masala gets too dry. Lower flame and add powdered spices with splash of hot water, stir well. Cook on high flame for 1-2 minutes. Add grated tomato and salt, cook for 2-3 minutes. Add marinated prawns evenly on tawa. Cook on high flame while stirring at intervals, do not stir too much to preserve texture. Once prawns are cooked, add fresh coriander. Heat ghee in ladle and add to prawns, stir well. Serve hot with pav toasted over flame or with bhakri.

name: Gobhi Ke Danthal Ki Sabzi
instructions: Cut and clean cauliflower stems into 2-3 inch pieces, removing hard outer covering. Boil stems briefly in salted water. Set pressure cooker over high heat, add ghee and oil. Add cumin seeds and whole spices (cinnamon, cardamom, bay leaf), then chopped onions; cook until golden brown. Add ginger-garlic-green chilli paste, cook further. Lower flame, add all powdered spices with a splash of hot water and cook until ghee separates. Add tomatoes and salt, cook until soft. Add boiled stems, stir and cook over high flame for a few minutes. Add water and pressure cook for one whistle, then low flame for 7-8 minutes. After depressurizing, adjust salt. Switch flame to high, add pomegranate powder, fresh coriander, mint, garam masala and dried fenugreek. Serve hot with tandoori roti or bread.

name: Korean Crispy Fried Potato
instructions: Cut peeled potatoes into thick batons. Bring salted water to boil with vinegar, add potatoes and parboil for 3-4 minutes. Remove and cool on sieve. Make batter with refined flour, corn flour, salt, pepper, baking soda and chilled water to semi-thick consistency. Dip cooled potatoes in batter and fry in 165°C oil over medium-high flame until light golden. Rest on sieve. Heat oil separately to 180°C. Make sauce: combine gochujang, mirin, soy sauce, honey, sesame oil, chopped ginger and garlic; bring to boil and simmer until thickened. Flash fry potatoes in hot oil for 30-60 seconds and add to sauce. Toss well without cooking further. Serve hot garnished with sesame seeds and spring onion greens.

name: Kashmiri Laal Paneer
ingredients: kashmiri red chillies 7-8 deseeded and soaked
instructions: Cut paneer into 2-3 cm thick slices. Boil salted water with turmeric, add paneer and simmer for 5-6 minutes. Cool and dry paneer slightly. Heat mustard oil until smoking, cool slightly, then fry paneer slices over medium-high flame until golden brown on both sides; reserve oil. Grind fried onions into paste with water. Grind soaked Kashmiri chillies into fine paste with water. Heat reserved mustard oil in kadhai, add asafoetida, tomato puree, red chilli paste, turmeric powder, whole spices (cardamom, cloves, cinnamon), fennel powder, ginger powder, salt and hot water; bring to boil. Add fried onion paste and cook for 12-15 minutes over medium-high flame until tomato cooks and gravy reduces. Add fried paneer pieces, cover and cook over medium-low flame for 7-8 minutes. Adjust consistency and seasoning. Add roasted cumin powder. Serve hot with rice or bread.

name: Ajay Devgn Mutton Curry
instructions: Marinate mutton with ginger-garlic-green chilli paste, salt and turmeric powder. Dry roast coconut over low flame until golden brown. In pan, heat oil and add onions, ginger, garlic; cook until golden. Add tomato and coriander stems with salt; cook until soft. Cool and grind everything into fine paste with water. Heat oil in kadhai, add cumin seeds and onion, cook until golden. Add curry leaves, the paste, powdered spices (kashmiri chilli powder, agri masala) with water; cook over high flame until oil separates and paste becomes crumbly. Add marinated mutton, stir and cook over high flame for 5-6 minutes. Cover and cook over low flame until mutton releases water. Add hot water as needed and cook covered over low flame until mutton is fully cooked. Adjust seasoning, add lemon juice and fresh coriander. Finish with hot ghee. Serve with bhakri, roti or pav.

name: Kolhapuri Chicken Tambda Pandhra Rassa
instructions: Cut chicken into medium pieces. Prepare chicken stock by boiling chicken with onions, coriander and spices. Prepare Vaatan (paste) for Tambda Rassa: grind onions, garlic, ginger, green chillies, coconut and red chillies into fine paste. Prepare Vaatan for Pandhra Rassa: grind onions, garlic, ginger, coriander, sesame seeds and cashew nuts into paste. Heat oil in pan, add mustard seeds and cumin seeds. Add the pastes and cook until oil separates from the mixture. Add the chicken stock and cooked chicken pieces. For Tambda Rassa (red), add tomato puree, kashmiri chilli powder and jaggery. For Pandhra Rassa (white), add coconut milk and yogurt. Cook sukha chicken separately with additional onions and spices. Combine all components and serve hot with rice or bread.

name: Haldi Ki Sabzi
instructions: Peel fresh turmeric using peeler and wear gloves. Keep peeled turmeric in water, then grate using large-hole grater. Heat ghee in kadhai, add grated turmeric and cook over medium flame until golden brown and moisture evaporates, stirring continuously. Transfer to bowl and squeeze out ghee. Pound garlic, ginger and green chilli into coarse paste. Heat ghee in same kadhai, add cumin seeds and asafoetida. Add chopped onions and ginger-garlic-chilli paste; cook over high flame until onions turn light golden brown. Add cashew nuts and cook for a few seconds. Add tomato and salt; cook over high flame until tomatoes turn mushy. Lower flame, mix powdered spices (kashmiri chilli, spicy chilli, coriander) with curd and whisk well. Add this to kadhai and stir continuously, then increase flame to cook until ghee separates. Add green peas, cooked turmeric and spring onions; cook for 2-3 minutes. Add hot water if needed and cover; cook over low flame for 4-5 minutes. Adjust salt, add garam masala and fresh coriander. Serve hot with bajra rotlo or millet bread.

name: mutton pepper fry
instructions: 1. Dry roast black peppercorns, fennel seeds, and cumin seeds on low heat until aromatic, then grind into powder. 2. Heat oil in pressure cooker on high flame. 3. Add cumin, fennel seeds, garlic, and curry leaves, stir briefly. 4. Add sliced onions and cook until light golden brown. 5. Add mutton, ginger-garlic-chilli paste, turmeric, garam masala, and salt. Cook for 3-4 minutes on high heat. 6. Add water and close the pressure cooker. Cook on high heat until first whistle, then lower flame and cook for 4-5 more whistles (until mutton is 85-90% cooked). 7. Switch off flame and allow natural depressurization. 8. Open lid, turn on high heat, add pepper fry masala, curry leaves, and green chillies. Cook until moisture evaporates and masala sticks to mutton. 9. Add heated ghee and fresh coriander. 10. Serve with parotta.

name: paneer makhani biryani
instructions: 1. Soak basmati rice for 30 minutes. Marinate paneer in yogurt with ginger-garlic paste and spices for 30 minutes. 2. Fry onions until deep golden brown and set aside. 3. Boil water with whole spices and salt. Add soaked rice and cook until 70% done. Drain. 4. Layer in heavy-bottomed pot: rice, then marinated paneer and fried onions, then more rice. 5. Infuse saffron in warm milk and pour over. Dot with butter and cream. 6. Cover with foil and place on high heat for 2-3 minutes until steam forms. Reduce heat to low and cook for 45 minutes. 7. Turn off heat and let rest for 5 minutes without opening. 8. Gently mix with fork and serve garnished with fried onions and fresh coriander.

name: veg schezwan noodles
instructions: 1. Deseed and soak kashmiri red chillies in hot water for 1 hour. Grind with water into a fine paste. 2. Heat oil in kadhai, add red chilli paste and cook on medium-high heat for 7-8 minutes until crumbly and oil separates. Add salt and vinegar. Cool completely and store in airtight container. 3. Boil water with salt and oil. Add noodles and boil for 30 seconds until separated. Cover for 2-3 minutes. Remove with tongs and transfer to sieve. Rinse with cold water and drizzle oil to prevent sticking. 4. Heat oil in kadhai, add red chilli, garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Cook for 30 seconds. 5. Add cooked red chilli paste and light soy sauce. 6. Add all vegetables and cook on high heat for 2 minutes maintaining crunch. 7. Add boiled noodles, white pepper powder, sugar, salt, and spring onion greens. Toss everything using spatulas or tongs over high heat. 8. Serve immediately.

name: moong dal ka cheela
instructions: 1. Wash and soak moong dal for 1 hour until water turns clear. Discard water. 2. Add soaked dal, ginger, green chilli, cumin seeds, salt, and water to mixer. Grind into fine batter. Transfer to bowl and check consistency (should not be too thin). 3. In another bowl, mix chopped vegetables (carrot, cabbage, capsicum, ginger, green chilli), grated paneer, fresh coriander, and spring onion greens. 4. Heat tawa on high heat. Sprinkle water to check temperature (should sizzle and evaporate quickly). 5. Pour ladle full of batter on tawa and spread into dosa shape. Add vegetable-paneer mixture evenly on surface and press gently. 6. Sprinkle salt, black pepper, and ghee on top. Cook on medium flame until bottom turns light golden brown. 7. Flip using spatula and cook other side for 2-3 minutes until vegetables cook. 8. Flip again and roll the cheela. Transfer to cutting board and cut into pieces. 9. Serve with green chutney and sweet tamarind chutney.

name: french beans and potato sabzi
instructions: 1. Wash french beans and pat dry. Cut off tops and bottoms slightly and chop into 1-2 cm thick pieces. 2. Peel potatoes and cut into dices. Make coarse paste of ginger, garlic, and green chilli using mortar and pestle. 3. Heat mustard oil in kadhai until it reaches smoking point. Lower flame and let oil cool slightly. 4. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, red chilli, asafetida, curry leaves, and ginger-garlic paste. Stir and cook briefly. 5. Add diced potatoes, stir well, and cook on high flame for 1-2 minutes. 6. Cover and cook on low flame for 7-8 minutes, stirring at intervals, until potatoes are 90% cooked. 7. Add beans along with salt, black salt, black pepper, and turmeric powder. Stir well. 8. Cover and cook on low flame for 5-6 minutes until beans are cooked (keep slightly crunchy if preferred). Stir 2-3 times to prevent sticking. 9. Add garam masala, lemon juice, and fresh coriander. Stir well. 10. Serve hot.

name: soya chilli dry
instructions: 1. Boil water in stock pot with ginger, green chilli, soy sauce, salt, and sugar. Add soya chunks and boil for 5 minutes. Prepare fresh water in large bowl. 2. Strain soya chunks and transfer to water bowl. Rinse by stirring. Gently squeeze out water (do not squeeze completely). 3. Transfer to large bowl. Add corn flour a couple tablespoons at a time, coating chunks evenly using spatula or spoon. 4. Heat oil to 170°C (moderately hot). Fry coated soya chunks in batches for a few minutes until crispy. Strain into sieve. 5. Heat oil in wok on high flame. Add ginger, garlic, green chilli, fresh coriander stems, and spring onion bulbs. Cook for few seconds. 6. Add light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, hot water, vinegar, sugar, white pepper, and salt. Bring to boil. 7. Mix corn flour with water to make slurry. Add gradually into sauce while stirring to thicken. 8. Add crispy soya chunks with onion petals, bell peppers, spring onion greens, and fresh coriander. Mix well to coat. 9. Serve hot.

name: Veg Chopper Rice
instructions: 1. Cut paneer into cubes and shallow fry until golden; set aside. 2. In the same oil, stir-fry ginger-garlic and chopped vegetables for 2 minutes. 3. Add cooked rice and toss well for 3-4 minutes. 4. Add soy sauce and chili sauce, season with salt and pepper. 5. In a separate pan, make paneer gravy: sauté onions, add tomato paste, paneer, cream, adjust seasoning. 6. Pour gravy over rice or serve on the side. 7. Garnish with spring onion and serve hot.

name: Green Garlic Thecha Paneer Paratha
instructions: 1. Make soft dough with flour, salt, water, and 1 tbsp ghee; rest 30 minutes. 2. Prepare thecha: roughly pound green garlic, chili, ginger, coriander together. 3. Crumble paneer and mix with thecha, add salt. 4. Divide dough into 6-8 portions, roll thin, place paneer-thecha filling in center. 5. Fold into paratha shape, roll gently. 6. Heat griddle with ghee and oil, cook paratha on high heat until golden spots appear on both sides (2-3 minutes per side). 7. Press with cloth to help cook edges. 8. Serve hot with pickle or yogurt.

name: Atte Ki Pinni
instructions: 1. Grind edible gum (gondh) into fine powder. 2. Chop almonds and cashews coarsely. 3. Heat 250g ghee in heavy-bottomed kadhai on medium flame, add flour and mix well. 4. Gradually add remaining ghee as flour dries, achieving wet-sand texture. 5. Roast flour on low flame with lazy-8 stirring motion until light brown/biscuity color (10-15 minutes). 6. Add ground gondh powder, it will crackle; stir until mixed, switch off flame. 7. Add chopped nuts and raisins, stir for 2-3 minutes. 8. Cool slightly, add ginger powder, cardamom powder, powdered sugar; stir and mash. 9. Transfer to large paraat, continue mashing to release ghee (2-3 minutes). 10. Shape into oval pinnis by pressing firmly in fist; decorate with halved fried almond if desired. 11. Cool completely, store in airtight container in cool, dry place.

name: Rava Appe
instructions: 1. In large bowl, mix semolina, sour curd, 1/2 cup water, and salt; cover and rest for 10-15 minutes. 2. While resting, chop finely: ginger, green chili, curry leaves, carrot, capsicum, onion. 3. Heat oil in pan, add chana dal and urad dal; cook until color changes (few seconds). 4. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, and asafoetida. 5. Add chopped veggie mixture with salt; stir-fry over high heat for 2-3 minutes; transfer to bowl and cool completely. 6. Once semolina batter has rested, stir well and add cooled veggie mixture. 7. Add fresh coriander and freshly crushed black pepper; mix again. 8. Gradually add up to 1/4 cup water to achieve idli-batter consistency. 9. Heat appe pan over high flame until moderately hot; grease each mold with ghee. 10. Add baking soda to batter, mix thoroughly. 11. Fill each mold with 1 spoonful batter, cover pan. 12. Cook on medium-low flame for 8 minutes (covered). 13. Remove lid, flip appe using spoon; cook uncovered 2-3 minutes until golden on other side. 14. Scoop out and transfer to plate. 15. Serve hot with coconut chutney and onion-tomato chutney.

name: Til Gud Gajak
instructions: 1. Toast sesame seeds in dry pan over medium heat, stirring constantly for 5-7 minutes until fragrant; cool completely. 2. Grind toasted sesame seeds into coarse powder (not fine); set aside. 3. Heat jaggery in heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat, stirring occasionally until it melts completely (5-7 minutes). 4. Add ghee to melted jaggery and mix well. 5. Test consistency by dropping a small amount in cold water; it should form a hard ball when ready. 6. Remove from heat, quickly add ground sesame seeds and crushed peanuts; stir immediately and vigorously. 7. Immediately pour onto greased surface (first setting) before it hardens completely. 8. Allow to cool slightly (1-2 minutes), then transfer to final setting surface; flatten gently. 9. Once cooled but still warm, cut into bite-sized pieces using oiled knife. 10. Cool completely, store in airtight container at room temperature.

name: Paneer Kaju Masala
ingredients: cashew 65g total (20-25 for soaking, 30-35 for frying)
instructions: 1. Soak 20-25 cashews in hot water. 2. Heat ghee in pan, add remaining 30-35 cashews; fry over medium heat until light golden, transfer to bowl. 3. In same pan, shallow-fry paneer cubes until golden on 2 sides; transfer to bowl. 4. Drain soaked cashews, add to mixer jar with 1 chopped tomato and water; grind into fine puree. 5. Heat remaining ghee and oil in kadhai over high flame; add whole spices (cumin seeds, bay leaf, cardamom, cinnamon). 6. Add chopped onions, stir well, cook until light golden brown over medium-high heat. 7. Add ginger-garlic-green chili paste, cook 2 minutes until golden brown. 8. Lower flame, add all powdered spices with splash of hot water; stir well. 9. Cook over high flame 2-3 minutes until ghee separates. 10. Add coriander stems, stir. 11. Add cashew-tomato puree and whisked yogurt with salt; cook until gravy becomes crumbly and ghee separates (do not add water yet). 12. Add hot water to adjust gravy consistency, cook 2-3 minutes. 13. Add fried paneer cubes, fried cashews, green chili, and ginger julienne. 14. Cook 3-4 minutes; add garam masala and kasuri methi powder. 15. Taste and adjust salt; garnish with fresh coriander. 16. Serve hot with tandoori roti or naan.

name: veg sizzler with garlic pepper sauce
instructions: Make garlic pepper sauce: heat butter until brown, add maida and cook 4-5 min. Gradually add hot water while stirring to avoid lumps. Add stock cube, celery, garlic, all seasonings. Cook on high for 7-8 min until it coats the back of a spoon. Set aside. Char onions: heat oil in pan, add onions and salt, char on high for 2 min each side. Sauté veggies: add oil and butter to pan, cook garlic and mushrooms until dry, add remaining veggies with salt and spices, cook 3-4 min retaining crunch. Assemble on hot sizzler plate: layer cabbage, rice, sautéed veggies, charred onions, corn, french fries. Pour hot garlic pepper sauce over top. Garnish with cheese and serve with extra sauce on side.

name: rajbhog stuffed kesar rasgulla
ingredients: lemon juice as needed for paneer
instructions: Bloom saffron in warm milk. Make chenna (paneer) by curdling milk with lemon juice and separating whey. Knead chenna until smooth. Prepare sugar syrup with water and sugar for cooking. Shape chenna into small balls and stuff with khoya and nuts to make rajbhog. Cook rajbhog in hot sugar syrup until they float and become spongy. Soak in prepared sugar syrup infused with saffron and cardamom. Plate and serve chilled in saffron-scented syrup.

name: vatidal khaman
instructions: Soak chana dal for 5-6 hours. Grind soaked dal with water into thick semi-fine batter. Transfer to bowl, add gram flour and citric acid, whisk 2-3 min. Ferment in warm place for 8-10 hours until doubled. After fermentation, gently mix to release air. Add ginger and green chilli paste, oil, asafoetida, turmeric, sugar. Whisk well. Divide batter. Grease steamer trays. Add salt and 0.5 tsp eno with 1 tsp water to half the batter and mix. Pour into greased tray and steam for 15 min without opening. Repeat with remaining batter. Cool completely. Cut khaman and remove from tray. Make tempering: heat oil, add green chillies, mustard seeds, curry leaves, water and salt. Pour tempering over khaman with fresh coriander. Make chutney from fresh herbs, khaman pieces, cumin, sugar, lemon juice and ice. Serve khaman with chutney.

name: coconut kulfi
instructions: Pour full cream milk into a heavy-bottomed pan. Add custard powder mixed with a little milk to avoid lumps. Heat milk on medium flame, stirring occasionally. Add grated coconut and sugar, stir well to combine. Continue heating and stirring until milk thickens and reduces to about 1/3 of original volume (this concentrates flavors). Add crushed cardamom at the end and stir. Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Pour into kulfi moulds or small containers. Refrigerate for at least 4-6 hours or freeze overnight. Dip moulds in warm water for a few seconds to unmould. Serve cold.

name: pineapple sharbat concentrate
instructions: Peel and chop fresh pineapples. Extract juice by blending and straining through a fine sieve to remove pulp. In a heavy-bottomed pan, combine pineapple juice with sugar (1:1 ratio). Heat over medium flame, stirring constantly to dissolve sugar completely. Continue cooking until the mixture reaches a thick concentrate consistency that coats the back of a spoon (about 1-1.5 hours). Meanwhile, bloom saffron strands in a small amount of warm juice. Squeeze lemon juice and strain to remove pulp. Add saffron and lemon juice to the concentrate near the end of cooking. Stir well and let cool. Pour concentrate into sterilized glass bottles. This concentrate keeps for months at room temperature. To serve, mix 1-2 tbsp concentrate with 1 glass of water or soda.

name: kantola sabzi
instructions: Wash and chop kantola into medium pieces. Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds and cumin seeds for tempering. Add chopped onions and cook until golden. Add ginger and garlic paste. Add green chillies (slit). Add chopped kantola and stir well. Add tomato pieces and cook until they soften. Add turmeric, chilli powder, and coriander powder. Stir well and cook on medium-high heat for 5-7 minutes until kantola is soft but still holds its shape. The vegetable will release its own moisture which helps in cooking. Taste and adjust salt. Garnish with fresh coriander. Serve hot with roti, paratha, or rice.

name: Dahi Wali Mirch
instructions: Heat oil and add mustard seeds and cumin seeds until they splutter. Add asafoetida and green chillies, cook briefly. Add turmeric powder, coriander powder, fennel powder, red chilli powder, and salt. Add yogurt and cook on low heat, stirring gently until well combined. Finish with dry mango powder. Serve hot.

name: Mangalore Banana Bun
instructions: Mash banana with yogurt and sugar. Mix in salt, cumin seeds, and refined flour. Add baking soda just before cooking. Make small balls and cook on griddle or pan with ghee until golden brown and cooked through.

name: Melon Seeds Katli
instructions: Roast melon seeds until fragrant. Crush cardamom pods and grind with roasted seeds into powder. Heat ghee in pan, add sugar, stir until melted and light brown. Add seed powder and mix well. Pour onto greased surface, press with ghee-coated hands, cut into pieces while warm. Let cool and set completely.

name: Sweet Corn Paneer Paratha
instructions: Make dough with wheat flour, gram flour, salt, ghee, carom seeds, and fenugreek leaves. Let rest 15-20 minutes. For filling: heat oil, add cumin seeds, ginger, green chilli, cook briefly. Add turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli powder, garam masala, dried mango powder. Add capsicum, cook until soft. Mix in paneer and coriander leaves, cool. Divide dough into balls, roll thin, place filling, fold and roll. Cook paratha on hot griddle with ghee until golden and crispy on both sides.

name: Malai Semiya Kheer
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan and roast vermicelli until golden brown. Add milk and bring to a boil, stirring occasionally. Add crushed cardamom and sugar. Simmer, stirring regularly, until vermicelli is cooked and milk thickens (about 15 minutes). Stir in cream. Garnish with chopped nuts and serve warm or chilled.

name: Banana Ice Cream
instructions: Boil milk with sugar powder and corn flour, stirring continuously until thickened. Cool completely. Mash ripe bananas and add to cooled milk mixture. Whip fresh cream until soft peaks form, then fold gently into banana mixture. Pour into container and freeze for 4-5 hours, stirring every 1-2 hours to prevent ice crystals. Serve when frozen solid.

name: Sattu Makhana Laddu
instructions: Roast fox nuts in a dry pan over medium heat until crispy (2-3 min). Dry roast sattu for 2-3 minutes until fragrant. Lightly pound roasted fox nuts to break them into coarse pieces. Mix sattu, fox nut pieces, powdered sugar, and crushed cardamom in a bowl. Heat ghee and mix into dry ingredients while warm. Let cool for 5 minutes. Roll into laddus of desired size. Store in airtight container for several months.

name: Butterscotch Ice Cream
instructions: Heat butter with brown sugar in a pan over medium heat, stirring until caramelized into butterscotch sauce (3-4 min). Cool completely. Boil milk with sugar, then cool. Whip fresh cream to soft peaks. Combine cooled milk, whipped cream, and butterscotch sauce. Mix gently until uniform. Pour into container and freeze for 4-5 hours, stirring every 1-2 hours to achieve creamy consistency.

name: Kabuli Chana Kachori
instructions: Mix wheat flour, semolina, salt, carom, cumin, chilli flakes, sesame, and fenugreek. Add 2 tbsp oil and water to make stiff dough. Knead well and rest 20 minutes. For filling: roast coriander, fennel, and cumin seeds, then grind coarsely. Cook boiled chickpeas with minced ginger, green chilli, and ground spices with salt, turmeric, red chilli, garam masala, amchur, and baking soda. Divide dough into 8-10 portions. Flatten each, add spoonful of filling, seal completely. Deep fry in oil until golden and crispy. Serve hot with mint chutney.

name: Malai Kofta Curry
instructions: For koftas: Mix grated paneer, boiled mashed potato, refined flour, salt, chopped green chilli, grated ginger, black pepper, and coriander. Form into smooth balls and deep fry in oil until golden brown. For curry: Blanch tomatoes and make smooth puree. Grind roasted coriander, fennel, cumin, black pepper, cinnamon, bay leaf, clove, and black cardamom into fine paste. Heat oil, add cumin seeds, then tomato puree, ginger, green chilli. Cook until oil separates (3-4 min). Add spice paste, turmeric, and cook 2-3 minutes. Stir in muskmelon seed and cashew paste. Simmer 5 minutes. Add salt, asafoetida, coriander powder, red chilli powder, fenugreek leaves, and sugar. Gently add fried koftas. Pour cream over top and simmer 10-15 minutes. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Lasoda Sabji
instructions: Heat oil and add cumin, fenugreek, carom and mustard seeds along with asafoetida. Add prepared lasoda pieces and salt. Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally. Add raw mango pieces to enhance flavor. Add turmeric, coriander, fennel, kashmiri red chilli and garam masala. Cook until lasoda is tender and flavors blend well. Serve hot.

name: Coconut Rice Cake
instructions: Mix rice flour, curd, and sugar powder together. Add grated coconut and crushed cardamom. Stir in Eno baking soda just before cooking. Pour into greased container and steam for 20-25 minutes until cooked through. Garnish with almond flakes. Cool before serving.

name: Besan Ganthia
instructions: Mix gram flour, salt, black pepper, carom seeds and papad khar. Add oil gradually and mix to form a stiff dough. Knead well. Heat oil for deep frying. Form small portions and deep fry until golden and crispy on both sides. Drain on absorbent paper. Store in airtight container.

name: Crispy Bhindi
instructions: Cut ladyfingers into desired pieces, removing seeds if preferred. Dry them completely to avoid moisture. Mix all spices including turmeric, coriander, red chilli, garam masala and chat masala. Add rice flour and gram flour to the spice mix. Coat ladyfinger pieces evenly in the flour-spice mixture. Deep fry in hot oil until golden brown and crispy.

name: Pumpkin Sabji
instructions: Cut pumpkin into chunks and raw mango into pieces. Heat mustard oil and add fenugreek seeds and asafoetida. Add ginger chilli paste and cook briefly. Add pumpkin pieces and raw mango. Mix in spices: turmeric, coriander powder, chilli powder and salt. Add whole spices: bay leaf, cloves, black pepper, black cardamom and cinnamon stick. Cover and cook on medium heat until pumpkin is tender. Garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Bardoli Patra
instructions: Clean colocasia leaves. Mix gram flour, rice flour, salt and all spices. Add ginger chilli paste, tamarind paste, jaggery and oil. Mix to form a thick batter. Spread batter on colocasia leaves, layer another leaf on top, and apply more batter. Roll tightly. Cut into cylindrical pieces. Stir in baking soda just before steaming. Steam patra rolls for 15-20 minutes until cooked. Cool and slice. Deep fry until crispy golden.

name: kesar peda
instructions: Soak saffron in 1 tbsp milk. Heat ghee in pan, add milk powder and roast lightly. Add full cream milk and mix. Add sugar powder and cook on medium heat stirring constantly until thickened. Add crushed cardamom and saffron with milk. Cool slightly. Roll into balls and shape. Garnish with pistachio flakes. Allow to set. (Note: Video also shows coconut laddu variant made similarly with dessicated coconut)

name: cheese bread vada
instructions: Mix gram flour with salt, carom seeds, turmeric, chilli powder and baking soda. Add water gradually to make thick batter. Cut bread slices in halves. Place cheese slice between two bread pieces. Dip in gram flour batter to coat well. Deep fry in hot oil until golden brown on both sides. Drain on paper towels. Serve hot.

name: mango chutney
instructions: Wash and cut raw mangoes into pieces, removing the stone. Mix mangoes with sugar and let sit 1-2 hours. Add black salt, kashmiri chilli powder and garam masala. Roast cumin seeds and add powder. Add turmeric powder and crushed cardamom. Mix well and let rest. Store in airtight containers for long shelf life.

name: moong dal mangodi
instructions: Wash and soak moong dal for 30 minutes. Drain completely. Chop green chillies and grate ginger finely. Mix soaked dal with ginger, chillies, salt, cumin seeds, chilli powder, coriander and asafoetida. Grind to thick paste retaining some texture. Shape into small dumplings. Dry in sun or dehydrator until completely hard (2-3 days). Store in airtight container for long-term use in curries and dal.

name: sattu khaskhas laddu
instructions: Dry roast poppy seeds in pan for 1-2 minutes until fragrant. Chop almonds into small pieces. Grate jaggery finely. Heat ghee in pan and add roasted chickpea powder. Mix well. Add poppy seeds and mix. Add grated jaggery and stir until combined. Add almonds and mix well. Cool until comfortable to handle. Roll into small balls and store in airtight container.

name: sakrori
instructions: Heat milk in heavy-bottomed pan. Add saffron strands to infuse. Fry fox nuts in ghee until golden and puffed. Fry mixed dry fruits in ghee until aromatic. Fry boondi pearls in ghee until golden. Add sugar to hot milk and stir until dissolved. Add fried fox nuts, dry fruits, and boondi. Add crushed cardamom and raisins. Reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes. Add cream and mix well. Cook for 2-3 more minutes. Serve warm or chilled.

name: raw mango jam
instructions: Wash and chop raw mangoes into small pieces, removing stones. Mix mangoes with sugar and salt. Let sit for 2-3 hours for juice to release. Transfer to heavy-bottomed pan. Cook on medium heat, stirring frequently, until mixture thickens and reaches jam consistency (approximately 45-60 minutes). Add cardamom powder near the end. Skim foam if necessary. Test consistency by dropping small amount on cool plate - should wrinkle when pushed. Cool and transfer to sterilized jars. Seal well for long shelf life. Store in cool dark place.

name: moong dal oats idli
instructions: Soak moong dal for 4 hours. Grind coarsely with oats and curd. Add chopped vegetables, ginger, green chilli, and salt. Mix well. Heat oil in pan, add mustard seeds, urad dal, curry leaves until seeds crackle. Add to batter. Pour into greased idli molds, add eno just before steaming. Steam for 10-12 minutes. For chutney: grind peanuts, coconut, green chilli, ginger, coriander with lemon juice. Temper with mustard seeds and curry leaves.

name: brown rice pulao
instructions: Rinse brown rice. Heat ghee and oil in a pressure cooker or heavy-bottomed pan. Add cumin seeds, cloves, bay leaf, cinnamon, black pepper, cardamom. Let them crackle. Add ginger paste. Add all chopped vegetables. Stir for 2 minutes. Add rice and salt. Mix well. Toast for 2 minutes. Add 1 cup water. In pressure cooker: cook for 2 whistles. In regular pan: cover and simmer for 30-35 minutes. Fluff with fork. Garnish with fresh coriander.

name: dal pakwan
instructions: For dough: Mix wheat flour, carom seeds, salt, and oil with water to form stiff dough. Let rest 30 minutes. For filling: Soak and cook chana dal until soft. Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and asafoetida. Add ginger, green chilli (chopped), tomato (crushed). Add cooked dal and spices (turmeric, coriander, red chilli, garam masala, salt). Cook 5 minutes. Add baking soda just before using. Roll small balls of dough, stuff with dal mixture, flatten slightly. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy on both sides.

name: chocolate gujiya
instructions: For dough: Rub ghee into refined flour until breadcrumb texture. Add salt and water to form smooth dough. Let rest 30 minutes, covered. For filling: Heat mawa in a pan with cocoa powder. Add chocolate chips, mix. Add crushed dry fruits, chironji, grated coconut, boora. Mix well and cool. Divide dough into 8-10 portions. Roll each into circle, place filling on one half, fold in semicircle, seal edges with water. Deep fry in hot ghee/oil until golden brown. Cool on paper towel.

name: chana dal poori with aloo bhaji
instructions: For poori dough: Dry roast chana dal and semolina separately. Cool. Mix with wheat flour, salt, carom seeds, cumin seeds, asafoetida, fenugreek leaves, ginger chilli paste, red chilli, coriander powder, turmeric. Add oil and water to form stiff dough. Let rest 20 minutes. For aloo bhaji: Heat oil, add cumin seeds. Add ginger, green chilli, tomato. Cook until tomato softens. Add mashed boiled potatoes, salt, turmeric, coriander powder, kashmiri chilli, gram flour. Cook until well mixed, add garam masala. Roll small portions of poori dough into discs. Deep fry until puffed and golden. Serve hot with aloo bhaji.

name: mango papad
instructions: Extract pulp from raw mangoes by boiling and mashing or grating. Mix pulp with sugar, salt, cumin powder, red chilli powder, and ginger powder. Mix well. Spread thin layer (approximately 1/8 inch) on a clean cloth, banana leaf, or parchment paper. For quick version: Dry in oven at low temperature (100-120°C) for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally until completely dry. For traditional: Sun dry for 5-7 days, turning daily. Once completely dry and crispy, break into pieces. Store in airtight container.

name: moringa stuffed paratha
instructions: For dough: Mix wheat flour with salt and water to form smooth dough. Knead well. Cover and let rest 30 minutes. For filling: Boil and chop moringa/drumstick pods finely. Heat oil, add cumin seeds. Add ginger, green chilli. Add chopped moringa pods, salt, turmeric, coriander powder. Mix well and cook for 2 minutes. Cool slightly. Divide dough into 6-8 portions. Roll each portion into thin disc. Place moringa filling in center, fold edges and seal. Roll gently to maintain thickness. Cook on preheated griddle/tawa with ghee until golden brown spots appear on both sides. Flip and cook until done.

name: Sama Rice Paratha with Aloo Sabzi
instructions: Boil potatoes with rock salt. Mix cooked sama rice with ghee, rock salt, black pepper, chopped green chilli, coriander, and crumbled boiled potatoes. Form and pan-fry parathas. For sabzi: heat ghee, add cumin seeds, fry ginger-green chilli-tomato paste, add turmeric and coriander powder, add boiled potato cubes, season with salt and coriander.

name: Palak Pakora
instructions: Method 1 - Flat fritters: Mix besan, rice flour, ginger-chilli paste, carom, kasuri methi, red chilli, coriander powder, salt, baking soda. Dip fresh spinach leaves one at a time and deep fry until golden. Method 2 - Round fritters: Mix chopped spinach with besan and rice flour, ginger-chilli paste, red chilli, carom, salt. Form into balls and deep fry until crispy.

name: Paneer Mungodi Sabji
instructions: Soak moong dal mangodi in water. Blend tomato, ginger, green chilli into paste. Heat oil, add whole spices (cumin, cloves, black pepper, cinnamon, black cardamom, bay leaves). Add tomato paste, simmer 2-3 minutes. Add turmeric, kashmiri chilli, coriander powder. Add soaked mangodi and cook until soft. Stir in cream and crumbled paneer. Finish with butter and kasuri methi. Garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Sarasiya Khaja
instructions: Mix refined flour with salt, turmeric powder, oil, and lemon juice. Knead into a smooth dough. Roll out thin sheets. Sprinkle crushed black and white pepper with salt on top. Roll up tightly, then coil the roll. Flatten slightly into a disc. Deep fry in oil until golden brown and crispy on both sides. Serve hot.

name: Malai Ghevar
instructions: Roast wheat flour in ghee on low heat for 5-7 minutes. Add milk and sugar gradually, stirring constantly. Crush cardamom and add. Let cool slightly and form ghevar dough. Shape into traditional ghevar pattern or flat rounds. Heat oil-ghee mixture for frying. Deep fry ghevar until golden brown. Prepare sugar syrup by dissolving sugar in water. Dip hot ghevar into warm syrup. Top with rabri, almond flakes, pistachio flakes, and saffron strands.

name: Moong Dal Laddu
instructions: Roast hulled moong dal in ghee over medium-low heat until golden and fragrant, stirring continuously. Cool completely. Grind roasted dal with cardamom into a fine powder. Toast and powder cashews separately. Mix moong dal powder with powdered cashews and melon seeds. Add malai (cream) and boora, mix well to form a dough-like consistency. Roll into round laddoos using your hands. Store in airtight container.

name: Chicken Shawarma
instructions: Marinate chicken breast in yogurt, garlic paste, onion paste, paprika, cumin, coriander, cayenne, lemon juice, and salt for at least 2 hours. Cook marinated chicken on skewers or grill over high heat until charred outside and cooked through. Warm pita bread. Assemble shawarma: spread tahini sauce on pita, add shredded lettuce, top with cooked chicken pieces, add fresh tomato and onion slices. Roll tightly and serve hot.

name: Chocolate Cream Éclairs
instructions: Make choux pastry: heat butter and water until boiling, remove from heat, stir in flour and salt. Cool slightly. Beat in eggs one at a time. Transfer to piping bag. Pipe 4-inch long eclairs on baking sheet. Bake at 200°C for 30-35 minutes until puffed and golden. Cool completely. For filling: melt chocolate, mix with whipped cream and sugar-free sweetener. Fill cooled eclairs with chocolate cream using a small piping tip or slit. Optional: drizzle tops with chocolate ganache.

name: Chocolate Cream Eclairs
instructions: 1. Heat water and butter, bring to boil. 2. Remove from heat and mix in flour and salt until smooth dough forms. 3. Beat eggs and gradually add to dough, beating well. 4. Transfer to piping bag and pipe éclair shapes onto baking tray. 5. Bake at 180°C for 35-40 minutes until puffed and golden. 6. Cool completely. 7. Whip cream with sugar-free sweetener and cocoa powder for filling. 8. Fill éclairs using piping bag. 9. Make chocolate glaze and dip tops. 10. Garnish with sliced almonds and chill.

name: Tiramisu
