instructions: 1. Brew strong espresso and let cool to room temperature. 2. Beat egg yolks with sugar-free sweetener until pale and fluffy. 3. Fold in mascarpone cheese gently. 4. Dip ladyfingers briefly in espresso mixture and arrange in single layer in dish. 5. Spread half the mascarpone mixture over ladyfingers. 6. Repeat with another layer of dipped ladyfingers. 7. Top with remaining mascarpone cream. 8. Cover and refrigerate for at least 6 hours. 9. Dust generously with cocoa powder before serving.

name: Mango Cake
instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 180°C and grease a round cake pan. 2. Cream butter and sugar together until light and fluffy. 3. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. 4. Sift flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. 5. Alternate adding dry mixture and milk to the butter mixture, starting and ending with dry ingredients. 6. Add vanilla extract and fold in diced mango. 7. Pour batter into prepared pan. 8. Bake for 30-35 minutes until a toothpick inserted in center comes out clean. 9. Cool in pan for 10 minutes, then turn out onto cooling rack.

name: Schezwan Fried Rice
instructions: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a wok on high heat. Add minced garlic and chopped red chili, stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant. Add chopped onions and stir-fry for 1 minute. Add carrots, cabbage, and bell peppers; toss continuously for 2-3 minutes. Pour in remaining 2 tbsp oil, add cooked rice and break any lumps. Add soy sauce, schezwan chili sauce, vinegar, and white pepper. Toss continuously on high heat for 3-4 minutes, creating a smoky wok flavor. Garnish generously with spring onion greens. Serve hot immediately.

name: Pav Bhaji
instructions: Pressure cook chopped vegetables with water, salt, and pav bhaji masala. In a separate pan, temper oil and butter, fry onions and ginger-garlic paste, add spices. Add tomatoes and cook until soft. Mix in cooked vegetables and mash. Simmer and add kasoori methi. Toast pav with butter and masala. Serve bhaji with pav, garnished with onion, lemon, and fried chillies.

name: Rava Laddu
instructions: Add semolina to oil in large bowl and massage together until it binds when held in a fist. Add warm water gradually and knead into stiff dough. Take small portions, flatten between fists. Fry in 165°C oil for 12 minutes until light golden brown. Transfer to sieve to cool. Break cooled fried pieces roughly. Grind mixture to coarse semolina texture in batches. Add powdered sugar and cardamom powder, mix well. Melt 3 tbsp ghee in small pan, add gram flour, cook on low heat while stirring until biscuity in colour. Add gram flour mixture to semolina mixture. Set pan over medium heat with remaining ghee, add nuts and raisins, fry until light golden brown. Add entire mixture to semolina, stir with spatula. Once warm enough to touch, mix with hands ensuring ghee is incorporated well. Shape small portions into laddus by hand.

name: Salt and Pepper Crispy Corn
instructions: Boil sweet corn kernels for 3-4 minutes, drain and cool spread out. Add salt, ginger-garlic paste, black pepper powder, mix well. Coat with maida and 3 tbsp corn flour lightly. Add splash of water and recoat with corn flour multiple times (3-4) for even coating. Let rest. Sift coated corn to remove excess flour. Deep fry at 165C in batches on medium heat for 1 minute covered, then uncovered until crisp. Drain in strainer. Heat wok with oil, sauté onion, ginger, garlic, bell peppers, green chilli for 1 minute. Add crispy corn and remaining ingredients, toss on high heat 1 minute. Serve hot.

name: Sabudana Thalipeeth
instructions: Wash sabudana 2-3 times until water turns clear, then discard water and add measured water. Cover and soak 3-4 hours until water is fully absorbed and pearls are translucent. In hot pan, add unsalted peanuts and dry roast over low flame while stirring continuously until golden brown, then transfer to bowl and cool completely. Chop 70% of roasted peanuts coarsely. Mix soaked sabudana with all chopped peanuts, boiled potato, fresh coriander, curry leaves, green chilli, lemon juice and sendha namak until mixture combines and forms roundels. You can skip fasting-restricted ingredients if observing vrat. Prepare green chutney: grind fresh coriander, roasted peanuts, fresh coconut, ginger, green chilli, lemon juice, dahi, sugar, cumin seeds and sendha namak in mixer jar using very little water. To shape thalipeeth: place square parchment paper and apply few drops of oil. Take portion of mixture and form roundel, place on oiled paper and press with palm. Wet hand and shape into round chapati by pressing and spreading with fingers, make small hole in center. Flip carefully onto hot tawa and peel parchment paper. Add few teaspoons oil in center and cook on one side 2-3 minutes over medium-high flame until golden and crisp. Flip and add more oil, cook until crisp from other side as well. Serve delicious sabudana thalipeeth with special green chutney and dahi.

name: Gajar Ka Halwa
instructions: Grate fresh red carrots. Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan and add grated carrots. Cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes. Add milk and bring to a boil. Simmer on low heat, stirring occasionally, until carrots are fully cooked and milk is absorbed (about 20-25 minutes). Add sugar and mix well. Separately roast almonds, walnuts, and pistachios in ghee until golden. Add the roasted nuts to the carrot mixture. Add cardamom powder and optional khoya. Continue cooking until ghee separates from the halwa. Serve hot or warm.

name: Chole with Soya Chunks Tiffin
instructions: Boil chole and soya chunks together until tender. Prepare shimlamirch pyaz ki sabzi (bell pepper and onion curry) in a separate pan with oil and seasonings. Cook rice separately. Combine all three items for a balanced lunch box: protein from chole and soya, vegetables from the sabzi, and carbs from rice.

name: Healthy Veg Wrap
instructions: Mix multigrain flour with salt, oil, and water to make dough. Rest for 10 minutes. Roll out thin wraps and grill with butter. For filling, combine sautéed onion, cabbage, capsicum, beetroot, tomato. Mix yogurt with garlic, chili, and black pepper for the spread. Assemble wraps with filling and yogurt spread. Slice avocado on top. Grill until edges are crispy.

name: Beetroot Oats Dosa
instructions: Blend ginger and green chili with water to make a paste. Mix rolled oats and besan together. Add grated beetroot to the mixture. Add ginger-chili paste and salt. Mix well to form a smooth batter, adjusting water as needed for consistency. Heat a non-stick skillet or tawa. Pour batter and spread thinly. Cook until golden on both sides, about 2-3 minutes per side. Serve hot.

name: High Protein Paneer Sandwich
instructions: For mayonnaise: Blend paneer, garlic cloves, green chili, and curd until smooth. Add salt, chili flakes, oregano, and mixed herbs. Mix well to create a creamy spread. For sandwich: Toast bread lightly. Spread paneer mayonnaise on one slice. Layer chopped vegetables and grated paneer on top. Add another slice of bread. Grill the sandwich with ghee until golden and crispy on both sides. Cut diagonally and serve hot.

name: Chilli Garlic Pasta
instructions: Boil pasta in salted water until al dente. Drain and set aside. Crush dry chili and mince garlic cloves. Heat butter in a pan, add garlic and chili, cook until fragrant. Add chopped onion, capsicum, red and yellow peppers. Cook for 2-3 minutes. Add green peas and carrot. Add boiled pasta to the pan. Mix in tomato ketchup, oregano, black pepper, and mixed herbs. Toss everything together. Add grated cheese and mix well. Season with salt and serve hot.

name: Chicken Biryani
instructions: Marinate chicken in yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, green chili, and salt for 30 minutes. In a pressure cooker, heat ghee, add bay leaves, cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, and peppercorns. Add sliced onions and cook until golden. Add marinated chicken and cook on high heat for 5 minutes. Separately, parboil rice with whole spices. Layer the marinated chicken in the cooker, then add parboiled rice on top. Sprinkle coriander and mint leaves. Cover and pressure cook for 15 minutes on high pressure. Allow natural release for 10 minutes before opening.

name: Vrat ki Thali (Fasting Meal Platter)
instructions: For aloo ki sabzi: Boil and cube potatoes. Heat 1 tbsp ghee, add 1 tsp cumin seeds. Add diced potatoes, chopped ginger, green chili, and coriander leaves. Cook for 5 minutes on low heat with sendha namak. For fasting rice: Heat 1 tbsp ghee, add 1 tsp cumin seeds and curry leaves. Add sama rice, cook for 2 minutes. Add water (1 cup for 1/2 cup rice) and cook until tender. For poori: Mix rajgira aata with sendha namak and water to form a stiff dough. Knead well. Roll into small balls and flatten. Deep fry in ghee until they puff up and turn golden. Serve all three components together as a complete fasting meal.

name: Makhana Chaat
instructions: For green chutney: Blend coriander leaves, ginger, green chili, yogurt, and roasted peanuts into a smooth paste. Season with sendha namak. For chaat: Combine roasted makhana, peanuts, diced boiled potatoes, chopped tomatoes, and cucumber in a bowl. Add minced green chili and sendha namak. Mix well. Top with green chutney and toss everything together. Serve immediately while makhana is still crispy. Garnish with extra coriander leaves if desired.

name: Paneer Masala
instructions: Heat ghee and oil. Add cumin seeds, bay leaves, and black cardamom. Add ginger and green chilli. Add tomatoes and cook until soft. Add all spices: turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, and garam masala. Add paneer pieces and cashews with melon seeds. Simmer until paneer is cooked and masala is well combined. Adjust salt and serve hot.

name: Schezwan Paneer Samosa
instructions: Make dough with flour, carom seeds, oil, and salt. Let rest. For filling: sauté ginger, garlic, and onion in oil. Add chopped paneer, capsicum, carrot, and cabbage. Season with black pepper, salt, and schezwan chutney. Add spring onion. Roll dough thin, cut into squares, fill with mixture, fold into triangles. Deep fry until golden and crispy.

name: Akki Roti
instructions: Mix rice flour with grated vegetables (lauki, carrot, cabbage, onion), soya leaves, coriander leaves, green chilli paste, garlic, ginger, cumin seeds, and salt. Make dough. Roll between plastic sheets and cook on hot tawa until golden. For chutney: sauté mustard seeds and curry leaves in oil, add roasted chana dal and urad dal, add ginger, garlic, onion, and tomatoes. Grind with red chilli. Serve roti hot with chutney.

name: Crispy Karela
instructions: Slice karela into thin chips. Soak in salt water briefly to reduce bitterness, then drain and pat dry. Mix cornflour, red chilli powder, black pepper powder, chaat masala powder, and salt. Coat karela slices with this mixture. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy on both sides. Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

name: Veg Manchurian
instructions: Mix grated cabbage and carrot with corn flour and all purpose flour. Add chopped onion, spring onion, green chilli, ginger, and green bell pepper. Add soy sauce, red chilli sauce, and salt. Make small balls and deep fry until golden. For sauce: mix soy sauce, green chilli sauce, red chilli sauce, tomato ketchup, white vinegar, black pepper, and salt. Toss fried manchurian balls in sauce and serve hot.

name: Paneer Beetroot Cutlet
instructions: Grate or finely chop paneer, beetroot, carrot, cabbage, and bell pepper. Mince green chilli and ginger. Mix all ingredients with salt and black pepper. Form into patties or cutlets. Pan-fry with minimal oil until golden on both sides, or air-fry at 180C for 12-15 minutes until crispy. Serve hot with green chutney or dip.

name: Cheese Corn Paratha
instructions: Make dough with atta, salt, and oil. Let rest for 15 minutes. For filling: heat oil, sauté green chilli and garlic, add corn and onion, sprinkle mix herbs and black pepper, cool. Mix corn mixture with grated cheese. Divide dough into balls, roll thin, place filling in center, fold edges, roll into flat paratha shape. Cook on hot tawa with oil until golden on both sides. Serve hot with butter or yogurt.

name: High Protein Paneer Cutlet
instructions: Mix grated paneer with finely chopped vegetables (green chili, onion, capsicum, carrot, beetroot, cabbage). Add salt, lemon juice, chili flakes, mixed herbs and besan. Mix well to form a dough. Shape into cutlet form. Coat with sesame seeds. Shallow fry until golden brown on both sides until crispy.

name: Moong Dal Pakoda
instructions: Soak moong dal for 30 minutes. Drain well. In a bowl, add moong dal, finely chopped ginger, garlic, onion, coriander leaves. Add turmeric powder, red chili powder, coriander powder, garam masala and salt. Mix well to combine. Heat oil in a deep pan. Drop spoonfuls of batter into hot oil. Deep fry until golden and crispy on all sides. Drain on paper towels and serve hot with chutney.

name: Lasaniya Batatana Bhajiya
ingredients: red garlic chutney (spices, garlic, red chilies)
instructions: Make red garlic chutney by roasting and grinding spices with garlic and red chilies. Make green chutney by blending coriander, green chilies and ginger. Slice potatoes thinly. Mix gram flour with turmeric, red chili powder, salt and water to form a thick batter. Spread red chutney between two potato slices. Dip in gram flour batter. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Serve hot with green chutney.

name: Orange Tea Cake
instructions: Preheat oven to 175°C. Sift together all purpose flour, milk powder, powdered sugar, baking soda and baking powder. In another bowl, mix milk, oil, vanilla essence and orange food color. Combine wet ingredients with dry ingredients until just mixed. Fold in orange zest. Pour into greased baking pan. Bake for 25-30 minutes until a toothpick inserted comes out clean. Cool and serve.

name: Rava Idli
instructions: For idli: Mix sooji with curd and salt. Let sit for 15 minutes. Add eno fruit salt just before cooking to make batter fluffy. Pour into idli maker and steam for 8-10 minutes. For peanut chutney: Grind roasted peanuts with green chili, garlic, ginger and salt. Add water to reach desired consistency. For red chutney: Dry roast chana dal, urad dal, peanuts and red chilies. Grind with tomato, ginger, garlic and salt. Temper with mustard seeds, curry leaves in oil. Serve idli hot with both chutneys.

name: Dahi Ke Sholay
instructions: Hang curd in muslin cloth for 2-3 hours to remove excess whey. Mix hung curd with chopped onion, capsicum, ginger, green chili, coriander, salt, black salt, grated cheese, roasted cumin powder and black pepper. Spread this mixture on bread slices. Cut into desired shapes (triangles or rectangles). Heat oil and deep fry until golden and crispy on all sides. Drain on paper towels. Serve hot with tamarind-onion chutney.

name: Paneer Pasanda
instructions: Prepare paneer stuffing with nuts, spices, and coriander. Stuff paneer cubes and keep aside. Make spiced curry by tempering oil and butter with spices, frying onions, adding ginger-garlic paste, and cooking tomatoes into a thick base. Grind the curry to a paste. Add cream and temper with butter and ginger-chilli. Roast stuffed paneer in ghee. Add paneer to the curry just before serving.

name: Subz Tehri
instructions: Toast dry spices for tehri masala in a pan and grind. Temper ghee with whole spices. Add sliced onions and fry until golden. Add vegetables and slit green chillies. Add curd mixed with powder spices. Cook vegetables partially. Add rice and water. Pressure cook or simmer until rice is cooked. Garnish with coriander and serve with tomato-coriander chutney.

name: Coconut Barfi
instructions: Reduce milk in a heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat, stirring occasionally. Prepare mould lined with parchment. Add desiccated coconut to reduced milk and mix well. Add rose water. Pour mixture into prepared mould and press down. Let cool and set. Melt dark chocolate and add cream to it. Cut coconut barfi into pieces. Pour molten chocolate over barfi and let set.

name: Potato Smiley with Cucumber Curd Dip
instructions: Peel and cut potatoes into pieces. Boil until soft, then mash thoroughly. Mix mashed potatoes with butter, garlic, maida, cornstarch, spring onion, and salt to form a dough. Shape into balls and flatten into smiley shapes. Deep fry until golden and crispy. For dip: grate cucumber and squeeze out excess water. Mix with hung curd, add black pepper powder, chopped coriander, roasted cumin powder, black salt, and salt. Serve warm crispy smileys with chilled cucumber dip.

name: Goan Fish Curry
instructions: Extract fresh coconut and grind into paste. Cut pomfret fish into large steaks. Marinate fish with salt and turmeric. Toast kashmiri chillies, coriander seeds, peppercorns, clove, cinnamon, and cumin in a dry pan. Grind toasted spices with ginger and garlic cloves into a fine paste. Fry the spice paste in oil until fragrant. Add fresh coconut paste and water to create curry base. Simmer for a few minutes then add marinated fish pieces. Cook until fish is done. Season with sliced onion, green chilli, kokum, and tamarind water. Adjust salt and simmer until flavors meld.

name: Crispy Fried Lauki
instructions: Peel lauki and cut into half-inch thick rings. Mix salt, black salt, turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder, roasted cumin powder, garam masala, chaat masala, kasoori methi powder. Marinate lauki rings in this spice mixture and lemon juice for 30 minutes. Prepare batter by whisking gram flour with salt, chilli powder, kasoori methi powder, chaat masala, and water to thick consistency. Dip marinated lauki rings in batter and coat with bread crumbs. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. For dip: mix thick curd with beetroot puree, chopped green chillies, black salt, pepper powder, garlic, and spring onion. Serve hot crispy lauki rings with cold beetroot curd dip.

name: Gajar Matar Sabji
instructions: Heat mustard oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Add cumin seeds and allow them to splutter. Add chopped onions and fry until golden brown. Add sliced carrots and green peas. Sprinkle salt, turmeric powder, and chilli flakes. Stir well to coat vegetables with spices. Cover and cook on medium heat for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until carrots are tender and peas are fully cooked. Add chopped coriander leaves and a pinch of kasoori methi. Adjust salt to taste and serve hot.

name: Cabbage Paratha
instructions: For paratha dough: Mix wheat flour with salt, ajwain seeds, ghee, and water to form smooth dough. Rest for 15 minutes. For filling: Heat oil, add heeng and cumin seeds. Toast coriander seeds. Add ginger and green chilli. Fry onion until soft. Add grated carrot, then finely chopped cabbage with salt, turmeric, chilli powder, chaat masala, and kasoori methi. Cook until cabbage is tender. Divide dough into 6 portions. Roll thin, place cabbage filling in center, fold edges, and roll into paratha. Cook on hot griddle until golden spots appear on both sides. For bathua raita: Blanch bathua and chop finely. Heat ghee with green chilli and garlic clove. Mix into curd with salt, chopped spring onions, and roasted cumin. Serve hot parathas with cool raita.

name: Chutney Pulao
instructions: For pulao: Heat oil and add black cardamom and cumin. Add chopped garlic and bay leaf. Add chopped onion and fry until golden brown. Sprinkle kasoori methi and salt. Pour water and bring to boil. Add basmati rice, stir gently once, cover, and cook on low heat until water is absorbed and rice is tender. For chutney: Blend coriander leaves, mint leaves, green chillies, ginger, salt, black salt, lemon juice, tomato pieces, onion pieces, roasted cumin powder, and garlic cloves with crushed ice and a dash of iced water into smooth paste. When rice is done, heat butter with heeng and mix into rice. Gently fold in fresh chutney and serve immediately.

name: Eggless Strawberry Cashew Cake
instructions: Preheat oven to 180C. Cream together unsalted butter and fine grain sugar until light and fluffy. Add vanilla essence and mix well. Fold in curd until well combined. Blend bananas with cashew nuts into a smooth puree and fold into butter mixture. Sift together all purpose flour, baking powder, and baking soda. Gently fold dry ingredients into wet mixture until just combined. Chop strawberries and fold in. Pour batter into greased heart-shaped mold or cake pan. Bake for 45 minutes until a toothpick inserted in center comes out clean. Cool completely in pan. For cream cheese icing: Beat cream cheese with powdered sugar until smooth and fluffy. Gently fold in whipped cream. Spread frosting over cooled cake. Garnish with fresh strawberries and sugar balls.

name: Palak Paneer Dosa
instructions: Soak moong dal and rice for 2-3 hours. Blanch spinach leaves and drain. Grind dal and rice with blanched spinach, curry leaves, ginger, green chilli, cumin, and peppercorn with water into smooth batter. Add onion, salt, and let ferment for 2 hours. For paneer filling: Heat oil, add dry red chilli, mustard seeds, and urad dal. Add curry leaf sprig. Add chopped onion and green chilli, fry until soft. Add ginger and mashed paneer with salt. Mix well and cook through. For onion tomato chutney: Heat oil with heeng. Add dry red chilli and mustard seeds. Add urad dal and cumin. Add ginger, garlic cloves, green chilli, and curry leaves. Add roughly cut onion and tomato. Cook until soft. Add salt and tamarind pulp. Grind to desired consistency. To make dosas: Heat griddle lightly oiled. Pour fermented batter in circular motion, spread thin. Drizzle oil on edges. Cook until golden. Fill with paneer mixture, fold into half and serve with hot onion tomato chutney.

name: Rara Chicken
instructions: Toast whole spices to make rarha masala. Marinate chicken pieces in salt, masala, and curd. In oil, temper bay leaves, cardamom, clove, and cinnamon. Add chopped onions and fry until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and fry. Add marinated chicken and cook. Add chicken mince separately cooked with spices. Add chopped tomatoes and water. Simmer until chicken is tender. Add kasuri methi and garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Aloo Methi
instructions: Heat mustard oil in a pan. Add diced potatoes and cook until they become tender. Add chopped methi leaves and mix well. Season with salt and chilli flakes. Add garam masala and stir. Cook on medium heat for 5 minutes until flavors combine. Serve hot.

name: Doodh Wali Bread
instructions: Toast bread slices lightly in butter. Mix custard powder with a little milk to form a paste. Boil remaining milk with sugar until sugar dissolves. Pour the hot milk mixture over the toasted bread. Mix the custard slurry into the milk. Let soak for 2-3 minutes. Garnish with tooti fruity and fresh mint leaves. Serve warm.

name: Makki Ka Halwa
instructions: Prepare sugar syrup by boiling sugar with water. In a heavy-bottomed pan, heat ghee and roast semolina and gram flour until fragrant. Add cornflour and roast on low heat until golden brown, stirring continuously. Add chopped nuts and roast for a minute. Pour the hot sugar syrup slowly while stirring constantly. Add cardamom powder and rose water. Continue cooking and stirring until ghee separates from the mixture. Serve warm.

name: Cabbage Apple Salad
instructions: Shred cabbage and slice apple. Sprinkle salt and squeeze out excess water. Place in a bowl. In a pan, heat oil and temper mustard seeds, dry chillies, urad dal, and chana dal until they crackle. Add chopped ginger, green chilli, and curry leaves. Fry for 30 seconds. Mix yogurt with the vegetables. Pour the hot tempering over the salad. Garnish with fresh coriander. Mix well and refrigerate for 15 minutes before serving.

name: Asian Cucumber Salad
instructions: Slice cucumbers thinly, retaining or removing skin as preferred. In a bowl, add jaggery and salt. Pour lemon juice and mix until jaggery dissolves. Grate ginger into the mixture. Add light soya sauce and sesame oil. Stir well until all ingredients combine. Dry roast sesame seeds lightly and add to dressing. Add chopped red capsicum. Place sliced cucumbers in a serving bowl and pour the dressing over them. Mix gently. Refrigerate for at least 15 minutes for flavors to meld. Serve chilled.

name: Makki Ki Roti with Lehsun Chutney
instructions: For Makki Ki Roti: Boil water with salt and ghee. Remove from heat and add makki atta, stirring well. Cover with a lid and let rest for 10 minutes. Scoop onto a surface and knead with oil for 7-10 minutes until smooth. Divide into equal-sized dough balls. Dust counter with dry makki atta and roll out like a chapati. Cook on both sides on a hot tawa. Then cook on an open flame to puff up. Smear ghee generously. For Lehsun Ki Chutney: Heat mustard oil on low heat and fry dry red chillies until fragrant, then remove. To the same oil add hing, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, peeled garlic, and chopped ginger. Cook on low heat for 3-4 minutes until garlic becomes tender. Add amchur powder and salt. Mix well. Serve chutney alongside hot makki roti.

name: Gur Makhana
instructions: 1. Heat ghee in a pan and roast makhana until light and crunchy. 2. In another pan, heat ghee and melt gur, then add baking soda. 3. Add roasted makhana to the melted gur and mix well. 4. Add sesame seeds, fennel seeds, salt, black pepper, ginger powder, and black salt. 5. Toss everything together until evenly coated. 6. Spread on a plate to cool and harden. 7. Once cooled, break into pieces and store or serve immediately.

name: Achari Paneer Masala
instructions: 1. Heat mustard oil and add bayleaf, nigella seeds, fennel seeds, mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds, and cumin. Let them splutter. 2. Add chopped onions and sauté until golden. 3. Add ginger, garlic, and green chilli, cook for 1 minute. 4. Add turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, coriander powder, and cumin powder. 5. Add water and cook the masala for 2-3 minutes. 6. Add chopped tomatoes and cook for 5-7 minutes until soft. 7. Add water and bring to simmer. 8. Add cashew paste (if using). 9. Add red chilli pickle and mixed pickle. 10. Add paneer pieces and simmer for 8-10 minutes. 11. Finish with kasoori methi and coriander. 12. Serve hot with roti or rice.

name: South Indian Dal Tadka
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds, garlic cloves, and curry leaves. Let them splutter. 2. Add ginger, dry kashmiri red chillies, and sauté for 1 minute. 3. Add chopped onions and tender coriander stems, cook until onions are golden. 4. Add salt and mix well. Keep this tadka aside. 5. In another pan or handi, add the prepared tadka along with cooked toor dal and chana dal. 6. Add water to reach desired consistency and simmer for 10-12 minutes on medium flame. 7. Add sugar, curry leaves, and ghee. 8. Mix well and serve hot with steamed rice.

name: White Dal Makhani
instructions: 1. Rinse and soak whole urad dal overnight. 2. In a handi, add ghee, crushed garlic, soaked urad dal, and water. Simmer until half cooked. 3. Add more water, cover with lid, and cook until dal is completely soft. 4. Mash the cooked dal with a spatula. 5. Prepare dry masala: dry roast cumin seeds, black cardamom, cloves, black peppercorns, and dry fenugreek leaves. Grind to coarse powder. 6. For tadka: heat oil and ghee, add green chillies and let splutter. Add yogurt, bay leaf, black cardamom, cinnamon stick, and ginger garlic paste. Simmer until cooked. 7. Add tender coriander stems and the cooked dal to the tadka. Cook until mushy. 8. Finish with fresh cream and butter, bring to boil. 9. Place hot coal on fire, once smoky, place in steel bowl. Put bowl in dal, add cloves and butter, cover with lid for smoking. 10. Serve hot garnished with cream, dry fenugreek, masala powder, chilli oil, ginger juliennes, and coriander.

name: Kerala Pepper Chicken Masala
instructions: 1. In a bowl, combine eggs, cornstarch, degi red chilli powder, turmeric, garam masala, black pepper, salt, and curry leaves. 2. Add chicken legs and marinate well. 3. Dry roast cinnamon, coriander seeds, cumin seeds, green cardamom, and black peppercorns for 2-3 minutes. Grind into fine powder. 4. Heat oil for frying and fry marinated chicken until tender. Set aside. 5. In a handi, heat oil, add curry leaves and chopped onions, sauté until translucent. 6. Add green chillies, salt, and ginger garlic paste. Cook for 1 minute. 7. Add degi red chilli powder and fresh tomato puree, cook for 5-6 minutes. 8. Add the prepared dry masala powder and water. Mix well. 9. Add fried chicken and let simmer on medium flame until cooked well. 10. Finish with green chillies, curry leaves, lemon juice, and black pepper powder. 11. Serve hot with parotta or steamed rice.

name: Egg Pollichathu
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a handi, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, and curry leaves. Let them splutter. 2. Add sliced onions and sauté for 2-4 minutes until translucent. 3. Add ginger garlic paste and sauté well. 4. Add turmeric powder, degi red chilli powder, chopped tomatoes, and green chillies. 5. Sauté on medium flame for 5-6 minutes. 6. Add water and salt, cook for 4-5 minutes. Finish with coriander leaves. 7. In a bowl, whisk eggs with salt and oil. 8. In a pan, add oil and make a thin omelette from the egg mixture. 9. Place omelette on a plate, add prepared masala, grated cheese, and grated boiled egg. 10. Add more masala and seal from sides. 11. Soften banana leaf on fire for 2-3 seconds. 12. Take a large piece of banana leaf, add masala in center. 13. Place omelette on it, add tomato slices, green chillies, degi red chilli powder, and salt. 14. Fold banana leaf to make a pocket. 15. In a pan, heat oil and place the pocket, cook for 5 minutes on low flame from both sides. 16. Serve hot.

name: Chicken Spring Roll
instructions: Marinate chicken with soy sauce, green chilli sauce, ginger-garlic paste and salt. Sear in hot oil for 12-15 minutes, then shred. Pickle cabbage and carrots with salt, vinegar, sugar. Make batter with refined flour, cornstarch, salt, egg, water, oil and cook thin sheets on pan. Mix pickled vegetables with shredded chicken, coriander leaves, and julienned ginger. Prepare slurry with batter and cornstarch. Fill spring roll sheets with stuffing, apply slurry, seal edges, coat with batter and fry until golden. For sauce: heat oil with garlic and vinegar, add red chilli sauce and tomato ketchup. Serve hot with garlic sauce.

name: Jodhpuri Mirchi Vada
ingredients: ½ tsp degi red chilli powder
instructions: Grind ginger, garlic, and green chillies into coarse paste. Roast coriander seeds 2-3 minutes and crush coarsely. Make batter with gram flour, carom seeds, red chilli powder, asafoetida, oil, salt, sugar, water. Add oil and vinegar, rest for minutes, then add baking soda. For stuffing: heat oil, add cumin and fennel seeds, add ginger-garlic paste and cook 1 minute. Add boiled potatoes, roasted coriander seeds, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, salt, cook 5-6 minutes. Add sugar, dry mango powder, citric acid and coriander leaves. Deseed bhavnagri chillies, sprinkle salt, stuff with potato mixture, seal edges. Dip in batter and fry until golden. Serve hot with green and tamarind chutneys.

name: Fauji Paneer Masala
instructions: Grind ginger, garlic, green chillies into coarse paste. Blend spinach, coriander stems, green chillies, coriander leaves, tomatoes into smooth paste. Heat oil, add cumin seeds, bay leaves, black and green cardamom, cloves, ginger-garlic paste and cook 2-3 minutes. Add chopped onions and saute until golden. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder and cook 1 minute. Add spinach-tomato paste and cook until oil separates. Add water, ghee, salt and bring to boil. Adjust consistency, add sugar, diced tomato, green chillies. Fry paneer cubes in oil until golden on all sides. Add fried paneer to gravy and simmer 4-5 minutes on medium flames. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with fresh cream and coriander sprig. Serve hot with roti or naan.

name: Lucknowi Khasta Kachori
ingredients: ½ tsp degi red chilli powder
instructions: Dry roast cumin seeds, coriander seeds, black peppercorns, cloves, black cardamom, fennel seeds for 2-3 minutes until aromatic, then grind into fine powder. Heat oil, add finely chopped ginger and green chillies, saute 1 minute. Add chopped boiled potatoes and dry masala powder, cook 5-6 minutes on medium flames. Finish with ghee and dry mango powder, mix well. Make slurry with refined flour, salt, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, water. Cut bread slices into shapes using cutter, apply water on bread edges, stuff with potato mixture. Apply slurry on all sides and seal edges properly. Coat thoroughly with breadcrumbs on all sides. Heat oil in kadai and fry kachoris until golden and crispy. Transfer to absorbent sheet. Serve hot with mint and tamarind chutneys.

name: Creamy Garlic Chicken
instructions: Boil chicken with water, salt, garlic, black cardamom, peppercorns, bay leaves for 7-8 minutes until half cooked. Boil cashews for 3-4 minutes and strain. Boil mushrooms for 2-3 minutes and strain. Grind boiled cashews with milk into smooth paste. Heat oil, add crushed garlic and butter, saute 1 minute. Add water and crushed black cardamom, simmer on medium flames. Add cashew-milk paste, butter, water, boiled mushrooms, boiled chicken, fresh cream, simmer 5-6 minutes until cooked. For tadka: heat oil, add chopped garlic and saute until golden, add deseeded kashmiri red chillies and coriander leaves, add butter, finish with lemon juice. Add prepared tadka to curry and mix well. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with coriander sprig. Serve hot with roti or steamed rice.

name: Achari Sattu Bhindi
instructions: In a bowl, coat lady finger with salt, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, asafoetida and keep aside. Mix sattu with mango pickle, coriander leaves, red chilli powder, coriander powder, dry mango powder, salt, pickle oil to make sattu mixture. Heat mustard oil until smoky hot, add onion seeds, fennel seeds, cumin seeds, mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds and let splutter. Add sliced onions and salt, saute until translucent. Add green chillies and coated lady fingers, stir well and cook 7-8 minutes on medium flames. Add sattu mixture, mix well and cook another 4-5 minutes. Finish with fresh coriander leaves and ghee, mix well. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with sattu mixture and coriander sprig. Serve hot with roti, steamed rice, and dal.

name: Bel Sharbat
instructions: Mix bael pulp with water. Add sugar and salt to taste. Stir in roasted cumin powder. Chill and serve cold.

name: Tomato Rice
instructions: For paste: Heat oil, add garlic cloves, dry kashmiri red chillies, tender coriander stems, quartered tomatoes and saute 1 minute. Add curry leaves, salt, sugar, turmeric powder, red chilli powder and water. Cover and cook until well done. Transfer to grinder and grind to smooth paste. For tadka: Heat oil, add urad dal, fenugreek seeds, mustard seeds, curry leaves - let splutter well. Add sliced onions, ginger garlic paste, cook until translucent. Add ghee, chopped tomatoes, salt, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cinnamon stick, cook 2-3 minutes. Add black peppercorns, prepared paste, water, salt and bring to quick boil. Add soaked sella basmati rice and ghee, cover and cook 8-10 minutes until done. Turn off flames and rest. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with grated cheese, coriander sprig and tomato slice. Serve hot with raita.

name: Himachali Khatta Kaddu
ingredients: mustard oil 3 tbsp for first tadka
mustard oil 2 tbsp for second tadka
green chillies 4 less spicy roughly chopped
instructions: First tadka: Heat mustard oil until smoky hot, add crushed coriander seeds and cumin seeds, let splutter. Add diced pumpkin, salt to taste, little water. Cook 7-8 minutes on low flames. Turn off and set aside. Garlic water: In mortar pestle, grind crushed garlic into coarse paste. Transfer to bowl, add water and mix well. Second tadka: Heat mustard oil until smoky hot, add fenugreek seeds, black cardamom, green cardamom, cloves, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, green chillies and saute 1 minute. Add onions and cook until translucent. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, cooked pumpkin and cook 2-3 minutes. Add first tadka mixture and salt, mix well. Finish with fresh coriander leaves and mix. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with coriander sprig. Third tadka: Heat ghee, add dry kashmiri red chillies and smoke 1 minute. Add prepared garlic water, tamarind pulp, water, salt to taste, jaggery and simmer 4-5 minutes. Serve hot with roti.

name: Veg Dhansak
ingredients: bottle gourd 1/2 medium peeled roughly diced
instructions: Cook dal and vegetables: In pressure cooker add soaked toor dal, masoor dal, pumpkin, brinjals, bottle gourd, water, salt, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, ghee and mix well. Cover and cook 3-4 whistles until cooked well. Make dhansak masala: In pan add salt, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, fennel seeds, kashmiri red chillies, black peppercorns, cloves, black cardamom, green cardamom, mace, nutmeg, bay leaf, cinnamon stick and roast 2-3 minutes until aromatic. Transfer to mixer grinder and grind into fine powder. Brown rice: Heat oil, add onion, cinnamon stick, bay leaf, saute until onion translucent. Add sugar and let caramelize on medium flames until brown colour. Add water, salt to taste and bring to quick boil. Add soaked sella basmati rice and cook 10-12 minutes on medium flames. Make paste: In mortar pestle add garlic, cumin seeds, fresh coriander leaves with stems and grind into coarse paste. For veg dhansak: Heat oil, add cumin seeds and onion, saute 2-3 minutes on high flames. Add fresh fenugreek leaves and prepared paste and cook for a while. Add cooked dal and vegetables, prepared dhansak masala and simmer 10-12 minutes. Add tamarind pulp, salt to taste and bring to quick boil. Finish with ghee and mix well. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with fried onions, lemon wedge and coriander sprig. Serve hot with prepared brown rice.

name: Andhra Egg Pulusu
ingredients: green chillies 4 less spicy cut into half
degi red chilli powder 2 heaped tsp
instructions: Saute eggs: Heat oil, add boiled eggs, curry leaves, salt to taste, red chilli powder and fry 1 minute. Turn off and keep aside. For egg pulusu: Heat oil in handi, add fenugreek seeds and cumin seeds, let splutter well. Add onions, green chillies, ginger and cook until onions translucent. Add tomato, water, salt to taste and let cook for a while. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder and saute 1 minute. Add water, tamarind pulp, sugar and let simmer 7-8 minutes. Add water and sauteed eggs and cook for few minutes on medium flames. Finish with fresh coriander leaves and mix well. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with coriander sprig. Serve hot with steamed rice.

name: Surti Aloo Puri
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 1 tbsp
instructions: Make a semi-soft dough with flour, oil, salt and water. Let it rest covered for 30-40 minutes. Grind green chutney ingredients into fine paste. Make kokum chutney by boiling kokum with water, spices and jaggery for 10-12 minutes, then grind. Soak white peas overnight, pressure cook with diced potato for 3-4 whistles. Temper cumin seeds and ginger-garlic-chilli paste, add all powdered spices and boiled peas-potato mixture, cook until thickened. Roll dough into thin sheets, cut 3-4 inch puris using cookie cutter, fry briefly in 180°C oil (they should not puff much and remain soft). Assemble by placing puri, adding spoonful of ragda, topping with green chutney, kokum chutney, sliced onions, papdi, sev, chaat masala and fresh coriander.

name: Dahi Waali Bhindi
instructions: Wash and wipe lady fingers thoroughly, cut off tips, cut in half. Mix all powdered spices, salt and gram flour into yogurt until smooth. Heat mustard oil in kadhai, add cumin seeds, asafoetida, onion, garlic, ginger and green chilli. Cook until onions turn golden brown. Lower flame, add yogurt mixture, stir continuously for 1 minute, then increase flame to cook until oil separates. Add hot water to adjust consistency (semi-thin gravy). In separate pan, heat oil and cook lady fingers with ginger and green chilli for 3-4 minutes, add lemon juice. Pour gravy over cooked bhindi, increase flame, add kasuri methi, fresh coriander and sugar, cook 1-2 minutes more.

name: Dosa
ingredients: oil for cooking 1 tbsp per dosa
instructions: For fermented batter: Soak rice and urad dal separately for 4-6 hours. Grind urad dal to fluffy paste by adding water gradually. Grind rice to coarse paste. Mix both, add salt, let ferment for 8-12 hours. For instant batter: Combine rice flour and urad dal flour with salt and water to make smooth batter. To season dosa tawa: Heat tawa on high, rub onion cut vertically, then wipe with oil-soaked cloth. Heat until smoking. To cook: Spread thin batter quickly in spiral motion from edges to center (fermented batter) or pour and spread with spatula (instant batter). Cook until golden and crispy, fold and serve with sambar and chutney.

name: Tikki Chole
instructions: Chole: Soak chickpeas overnight, discard water. Pressure cook with salt, whole spices, baking soda and water for first whistle then 4-5 more whistles on medium. Let depressurize naturally. Make ginger-chilli paste. Heat ghee, add cumin seeds, asafoetida and paste, cook 1 minute. Add cooked chickpeas, all powdered spices, tamarind water, cook 7-8 minutes. Mash some chickpeas, add ginger, kasuri methi, coriander. Aloo Tikki: Grate boiled potatoes, add salt and corn flour gradually, mix into dough. Form 80-90g roundels, press into thick tikki, dust with corn flour. Fry in ghee on medium heat until light golden, mash to flat discs using potato masher, fry again until golden and crispy. Green Chutney: Grind mint, coriander, ginger, green chilli, salt, dry mango powder, black salt, cumin powder, ice and water into paste. Amchur Chutney: Gradually mix dry mango powder with water, add jaggery and spices, cook on high until jaggery melts, simmer 15 minutes. Assembly: Layer chole, tikki, chutneys, toppings.

name: Laal Maas
instructions: Marinate mutton pieces in yogurt with salt for 30 minutes. Heat ghee in heavy-bottomed pot over high heat. Sauté onions until golden brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and chopped red chillies, cook until fragrant. Add marinated mutton, cook on high heat for 5-7 minutes. Add tomatoes and all powdered spices (coriander, red chilli, turmeric, garam masala). Stir well and cook on high heat for 10 minutes. Lower heat to medium-low, cover and simmer for 45-50 minutes until mutton is tender and oil separates from gravy. The gravy should be rich red in color (from red chillies) and well-reduced. Adjust salt to taste. Serve hot with roti or rice.

name: Basundi
instructions: Add milk to heavy-bottomed kadhai, bring to boil on high heat while stirring at intervals. Add saffron strands, lower flame to medium. Let milk simmer while stirring occasionally for 30-35 minutes until it reduces to half its original quantity (make sure malai layer doesn't form on surface). Add sugar, nuts and cardamom powder, stir well. Increase flame slightly and let simmer for another 10 minutes. Turn off flame when basundi reaches slightly thick consistency (not too thick). Basundi is ready and can be served hot, warm or chilled. When it cools completely, a layer of malai (cream) will form on the surface which can be mixed in or served separately. Garnish with slivered nuts and rose petals. Can be served as trifle by layering with vanilla sponge, fruits and rose syrup.

name: Chicken Pepper Fry
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 2 tbsp
instructions: Dry roast black peppercorns, cumin seeds and fennel seeds over low flame until fragrant, cool completely then grind into fine powder. Heat oil in kadhai over high flame, add cumin seeds, fennel seeds, garlic and curry leaves, stir and cook briefly. Add onions and cook over high heat until light golden brown, then add ginger garlic chilli paste and cook 1-2 minutes. Lower flame, add turmeric powder and garam masala, stir well. Add chicken with salt, stir and cook over high flame for 4-5 minutes. Add a splash of water, cover and cook on low flame until chicken is cooked. Remove lid, add pepper fry masala powder along with curry leaves and green chilli, cook over high flame while stirring gently until all moisture evaporates. Taste and adjust salt, add lemon juice and fresh coriander, stir well. Serve hot with malabar parotta.

name: Rasam with Vada
instructions: Pressure cook washed soaked tur dal with turmeric for 3-4 whistles over high flame. After natural depressurization, whisk dal well until completely smooth and watery. Heat oil in stock pot over high heat, add red chillies, mustard seeds, curry leaves, green chilli and garlic, stir and cook briefly. Add asafoetida, tomato, salt and coriander stem, stir and cook until tomato becomes mushy. Add turmeric powder and tamarind pulp with jaggery and hot water, stir and cook 3-4 minutes until raw tamarind smell is gone. Add cooked tur dal and hot water to adjust consistency. Let rasam simmer 7-8 minutes. Mix rasam powder with water to remove lumps, add to rasam and stir well. Taste and adjust salt, add crushed black pepper and fresh coriander, cook 2-3 minutes. For vada: wash and soak urad dal 3-4 hours, drain excess water and grind coarsely with minimal ice-cold water until neither completely smooth nor coarse. Transfer to bowl and whisk by hand 3-4 minutes until light and fluffy. Test readiness by dropping small portion in water - should float. Gently add salt, ginger, green chilli, asafoetida and curry leaves and mix very gently. Heat oil in kadhai to moderately hot (170°C). Dip hand in cold water, scoop bite-sized portion of batter and carefully drop into hot oil. Splash hot oil over vada so it puffs up, then fry on medium flame flipping at regular intervals until light golden brown. Transfer to sieve using spider. Serve vadas in hot rasam.

name: Patra
ingredients: water 1/4 cup plus 2 tbsp added gradually
instructions: Soak tamarind and jaggery in hot water and set aside. Prep colocasia leaves by flipping each one, trimming bottom veins running from center to tips, trimming center vertical vein, then gently flattening using rolling pin. Wash each leaf gently on both sides, pat dry with kitchen towel. Make batter by combining gram flour, rice flour and all spices in a large bowl. Mash soaked tamarind and jaggery in bowl and pass through sieve into batter. Add remaining water gradually while whisking to make thick lump-free paste. Add oil and baking soda and mix. To shape: place leaf vein-side up, apply thin batter layer. Place second leaf on top vein-side up with tip facing opposite direction, apply thin batter. Fold sides toward center so edges meet in middle, apply more batter. Starting from bottom, fold tips inward toward center and roll upwards tightly. Apply more batter as you reach center, continue rolling. When reaching top tip, fold inward, apply batter and seal last roll. Secure sides by pressing inward while rolling. Place on greased steamer plate and steam over medium flame 20-25 minutes until knife inserted comes out clean. Cool slightly, can refrigerate or freeze at this stage. Cut into 1-1.5 cm thick pieces. Heat oil in pan, add mustard seeds, sesame seeds, curry leaves and asafoetida. Shallow fry pieces on both sides until crisp and golden brown, or deep fry if preferred. Serve hot with chai or chutney, garnish with fresh coriander and coconut.

name: Baingan Aloo
instructions: Wash eggplant and cut into large dices. Peel potatoes and cut into wedges. Chop tomatoes roughly. Grind ginger and green chillies in mortar into coarse paste. Heat ghee in pressure cooker over high flame. Add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add ginger-chilli paste, stir and cook over high flame 30 seconds. Add tomatoes and cook 1-2 minutes over high flame. Add eggplant, potato, salt and all powdered spices, stir well and add a splash of water. Pressure cook over medium-low flame for one whistle, then switch off flame and let cooker depressurize naturally. Open lid, stir well and cook over high flame until desired consistency is achieved. Taste and adjust salt if required. Add garam masala and fresh coriander, stir well. Serve hot with rice or bread.

name: Kaala Chicken
ingredients: fresh coriander large handful for vatan
instructions: Make fine paste of fresh coriander, ginger, garlic and green chilli using very little water. Add chicken to paste along with salt and turmeric powder, mix well and marinate. Set large stock pot or handi over high flame, add oil and heat. Add chopped onion and cook over high flame until light golden brown. Add marinated chicken, stir well and cook over high flame 4-5 minutes while stirring until chicken starts releasing water. Place large parat or plate over vessel, add water on top and cook chicken over low flame. Once water on top gets hot, remove lid and stir well, add this heated water to chicken, replace lid and pour more water on top. Continue this process until chicken is 90% cooked and good stock is prepared. Strain chicken into bowl - use boiled chicken for sukkha and stock for rassa. For wet vatan: place papad jaali on flame, add onions (with tops and roots trimmed, slits made), dry coconut and green chillies. Roast over direct flame until charred (fan coconut as it catches flame). Let cool few minutes, roughly chop and grind with fresh coriander, garlic, ginger and garam masala using minimal water into smooth paste. Heat oil in iron kadhai, add red chilli powder and stir quickly, then immediately add vatan and salt. Stir continuously over high flame until crumbly and dark in color, oil will separate. Take 30% of vatan for rassa base, add 70% of chicken stock and mix well, bring to boil. Meanwhile add remaining chicken to iron kadhai with remaining vatan and stock to make chicken sukkha, cook until fully done and consistency thickens. Adjust salt in both preparations, add lemon juice. Heat ghee in separate tadka pan and pour half on chicken sukkha and half in rassa. Stir both well and garnish with fresh coriander. Serve hot kaala chicken with rassa along with jowar bhakri, rice and solkadhi.

name: Corn Pattice
instructions: Boil sweet corn for 5 minutes, drain and cool. Make paste with handful of corn, coriander, green chilli, garlic, ginger. Heat oil, add cumin seeds, paste, cook 1-2 minutes. Add remaining corn and seasoning, cook and cool. Boil and mash potatoes, add salt and corn flour gradually to form dough. Stuff dough with corn filling, seal and flatten into disc. Coat with flour slurry then bread crumbs. Deep fry at 165C until golden and crispy on all sides. Serve with mint chutney and ketchup.

name: Cream of Mushroom Soup
instructions: Wash and dry mushrooms, slice roughly. Heat stockpot, add butter and olive oil, sauté onions over medium-low heat until caramelized and brown (8-10 minutes). Add garlic, celery, and mushrooms, cook on high until mushrooms turn golden and release moisture, cook until evaporated. Add wine or hot water, scrape fond from bottom, cook until moisture evaporates. Add refined flour and olive oil, cook 2-3 minutes. Dissolve stock cube in hot water and add, simmer 5-6 minutes. Blend with hand blender until smooth. Add fresh cream, taste and adjust salt. Serve hot.

name: Achari Paneer
instructions: Toast whole spices over low flame until fragrant, cool completely, grind with powdered spices into coarse achari masala. Heat mustard oil until smoking, add cumin, kalonji, garlic, green chilli, then paneer, tomato, capsicum, onion with salt, turmeric, red chilli, achari masala. Cook 2-4 minutes, transfer to bowl. In same pan, heat more mustard oil, add cumin, kalonji, mustard seeds, onions, cook until golden. Add ginger-garlic-green chilli paste, cook until golden. Add powdered spices and achari masala, stir well with splash of water until oil separates. Add tomato puree and yogurt gradually, cook until oil separates. Adjust gravy consistency to semi-thin with hot water. Add paneer, vegetables, fresh cream, ginger, kasuri methi, garam masala. Taste and adjust seasoning. Garnish with coriander. Serve with tandoori roti, paratha, or naan.

name: Veg Kadhai
instructions: Toast whole cumin seeds in oil, add chopped onions and cook until golden. Add ginger-garlic-green chilli paste, cook until onions turn deep golden. Add all powdered spices and salt, stir well. Add chopped tomatoes and cook on high heat until tomatoes soften and oil separates. Add paneer cubes, capsicum, remaining onion, and cook on high heat for 2-3 minutes. Adjust seasoning, add black pepper, sugar, and kasuri methi powder. Toss well, cook 1-2 minutes more. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Puneri Misal Pav
instructions: Boil matki for 10-15 minutes until soft but not mushy. For vatan: dry roast grated coconut until golden. In oil, sauté onions until golden, add ginger, garlic, tomato, salt, cook until soft. Combine with roasted coconut, cool, and grind into fine paste. For usal: boil and mash potatoes. Heat oil, add cumin, asafoetida, curry leaves, onion. Add spices with splash of water, stir well, cook until oil separates. Add vatan paste, cook until crumbly and oil separates. Add matki, adjust consistency with hot water, simmer 5-6 minutes. Adjust salt, add lemon juice and fresh coriander. Serve hot with toasted pav and puffed rice on the side.

name: Sev Bhaji
instructions: Dry roast grated coconut over low heat until golden, set aside. Heat oil, sauté onions until golden, add ginger, garlic, cook well. Add tomato with salt, cook covered on low heat until soft. Combine with roasted coconut, cool completely, grind into fine paste. Heat oil in kadhai with cumin, asafoetida, curry leaves, onion until onion turns golden. Add all powdered spices with splash of water, stir well, cook on high until oil separates. Add prepared paste, cook on high heat until crumbly and oil separates, keep stirring. Add hot water to adjust consistency (keep semi-thin), simmer 5-6 minutes on medium heat. Taste and adjust salt, add sev, lemon juice, and fresh coriander, stir gently to combine. Serve hot with bhakri, roti, or toasted pav.

name: Panki
instructions: Wash and dry banana leaves, cut into squares. Mix rice flour with curd, ginger-chilli paste, asafetida, turmeric, salt, and oil. Add water gradually to make a lump-free batter slightly runnier than dosa batter. Make green chutney by grinding coriander, green chilli, ginger, peanuts, cumin, curd, sugar, lemon juice, and salt. Heat tawa on high flame. Grease two banana leaf squares with oil, pour ladle of batter on one leaf, place other leaf on top with smooth side touching batter. Once tawa is hot, apply oil and place leaf on tawa, press gently. Cook on medium-high flame until leaf turns light brown (2-3 minutes), flip and cook similarly. Peel leaf to check if panki doesn't stick. Serve hot with banana leaf and chutney.

name: Dahi Ke Kebab
instructions: Hang curd in muslin cloth over sieve for 8-12 hours until excess water drains. Heat oil and fry sliced onions until light golden brown, remove and place on tissue paper. In same oil fry cashews until light golden brown. Cool and chop onions and cashews. Mix hung curd with paneer, processed cheese, raisins, ginger, chilli, mint, coriander, cardamom, cumin, black salt, chaat masala, sugar, pepper, salt, and garam masala. Add gram flour for binding if needed. Form mixture into roundels, press flat, coat with breadcrumbs. Fry kebabs in hot oil (175-180°C) for 2-3 minutes until crisp and golden brown. Transfer to tissue paper-lined sieve to drain excess oil. Serve hot with green chutney.

name: Crispy Butter Garlic Mushroom
instructions: Wash mushrooms, trim stems, cut medium mushrooms in half, leave small ones whole. Blanch in boiling water for 30 seconds, immediately submerge in ice cold water. Coat blanched mushrooms with corn flour and refined flour mixture in two stages. Heat oil to 180°C, fry mushrooms until coating holds. Set aside. Melt butter in wok over high heat, add garlic, green chilli, coriander stems, cook for 2-3 minutes on medium flame without browning. Add hot water, salt, soy sauce, vinegar, white pepper, sugar. Stir well, cook for 2-3 minutes. Make corn flour slurry with water, add to sauce to thicken. Flash fry mushrooms for 30 seconds in hot oil, add to sauce with lemon juice, zest, and spring onions. Toss well to coat. Serve hot with spring onion garnish.

name: Chicken Gyoza
instructions: Mix refined flour, salt, and sesame oil. Add water gradually and knead into smooth dough. Cover and rest 15 minutes, then knead for 8-10 minutes, rest 30 minutes. Chop chicken and mince. Blanch cabbage in boiling water 2-3 minutes, cool in ice water, chop finely. Mix minced chicken with ginger, garlic, both peppers, salt, soy sauce, sesame oil. Add chopped cabbage, spring onions, and greens. Divide rested dough into 4 portions. Roll each very thin, dust with corn flour. Cut 4-5 inch circular wrappers using cookie cutter. Place spoonful of filling in wrapper center, wet edge, fold into taco shape, make pleats from one end to another, pinch and seal. Heat oil in nonstick pan on high heat, place gyozas, cook on medium until light golden on bottom. Mix refined flour and water into slurry, add to pan, spread evenly, cover, cook on low heat 5-6 minutes. Uncover and cook until moisture evaporates and crispy crust forms. Invert onto plate. Serve hot with soy and chilli oil.

name: Chakli
instructions: Wrap refined flour in clean towel and steam for 15-20 minutes. Cool slightly, remove, break up clumps, sieve flour to return to powder. Wash moong dal, pressure cook with water for 3-4 whistles on medium flame, let depressurize naturally, mash into smooth paste, cool. Mix dal into flour with all flavoring ingredients. Add water gradually to knead semi-soft dough resembling clay, not too hard or soft. Cover with damp cloth. Heat oil in kadhai to 165-170°C. Lightly grease chakli mold, take portion of dough, massage between palms, add to mold. Hold mold over tray, apply gentle pressure to handle, make circles from center spiraling outward to create round spiral shape. Pinch and stick end of chakli. Gently slide each chakli into hot oil one at a time. Fry on medium flame until crispy and golden brown. Don't stir initially; chaklis stop bubbling when fried. Remove when crispy.

name: Matkichi Usal
instructions: Heat oil in kadhai, add mustard seeds, cumin, curry leaves and onion, cook until translucent. Add garlic, ginger and green chilli, cook 1-2 minutes. Add asafoetida, turmeric, tomato and salt, cook 2-3 minutes. Lower flame, add masalas and hot water, cook spices 1 minute. Add sprouted matki and moong, cook 1-2 minutes on high flame. Add hot water, cover and cook on low flame 12-15 minutes until beans are cooked. Remove lid, adjust salt, add lemon juice and fresh coriander. Serve hot with chapati or bhakri.

name: Turai Ki Sabzi
ingredients: kashmiri red chilli powder a pinch
black pepper a pinch freshly crushed
instructions: Wash, peel and cut sponge gourd into dices. Heat ghee in kadhai, add onions and cook until translucent. Add turmeric and red chilli powder. Add tomatoes and salt, cook until slightly mushy. Add diced turai, stir and cover. Cook on low flame 15 minutes, stirring at intervals until turai releases moisture. Remove lid and cook on high flame 7-8 minutes, stirring until all moisture evaporates and ghee separates. Adjust salt, add black pepper, garam masala and fresh coriander. Serve hot with fulkas or khichdi.

name: Egg Toast
instructions: Version 1: Make chutney by grinding coriander, mint, onion, ginger, garlic, green chilli, black salt and amchur. Apply chutney on one bread slice and ketchup on other, place cheese slice on one. Apply egg mixture ingredients beaten together, dip bread triangles in egg mixture coating all sides. Cook in ghee on medium flame until golden brown on all sides. Version 2: Make egg filling with mayo, hung curd, seasonings and boiled eggs. Spread on bread, dip in egg mixture and cook in ghee until golden.

name: Garlic Pepper Chicken
instructions: Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces, coat with salt, pepper, vinegar, soy sauce, ginger-garlic paste and egg white, let marinate. Fry chicken in oil at 170-175C until light golden brown, 3-4 minutes in batches, transfer to sieve. Heat oil in wok, add onion, ginger, garlic, green chilli and saute 1 minute. Add capsicum and stir. Add hot water, ketchup, red chilli sauce and dark soy sauce, cook 1 minute. Add salt, pepper, vinegar and corn flour slurry to thicken. Add fried chicken and spring onion greens, toss to coat without cooking further.

name: Veg Wontons
instructions: Make dough with flour, salt, oil and water gradually, rest 15 minutes covered. Knead smooth, rest 1 hour covered. For filling, chop garlic, ginger, chilli and vegetables finely. Drain excess moisture with cloth. Heat oil, add veggie mixture with peppers, vinegar, sugar, cook 1-2 minutes. Add soy sauce and red chilli sauce. Cool slightly, add coriander and spring onion. Make chilli oil by heating oil with spices and pouring over chilli flakes. Roll dough very thin, cut 5x5 inch squares, place spoonful filling in center, fold diagonally and seal, bring edges together and seal. Fry in oil at 160-165C until golden or boil 5-6 minutes. Serve fried with sweet chilli sauce or in bowl with chilli oil, soy sauce, vinegar, sesame oil and water.

name: Cheese Chilli Corn Bread Pakora
instructions: Mix gram flour with baking soda, salt and carom seeds. Whisk in water gradually to make thin batter, rest until chutney is made. Blend coriander, mint, green chilli, garlic, chaat masala and ice cube into fine chutney. Mix onion, capsicum, sweet corn, cheese, green chutney and chaat masala for filling. Heat oil in kadhai. Apply filling on bread slice, place another slice on top, cut diagonally into triangles. Add tablespoon hot oil to batter and mix. Dip bread triangles in batter, coating all sides. Deep fry in hot oil at medium-high heat until crisp and golden brown. Transfer to sieve. Serve hot with green chutney or ketchup.

name: Paneer Burrito
instructions: Mix all powdered spices for Mexican seasoning. Heat oil, add onion, bell peppers, paneer and seasoning, cook 2-3 minutes. Pressure cook soaked rajma with cinnamon stick 5 whistles. Heat oil, add onion, garlic, jalapeno, cook until golden. Grate tomato, add with seasoning and salt, cook a few minutes. Add cooked rajma and simmer until thickened. Heat butter, add rice, coriander and lemon juice, cook 2-3 minutes. Mix all pico de gallo ingredients. Blend curd, ketchup, red chilli sauce, lemon juice, seasoning and garlic for sauce. Assemble burrito on tortilla with rice layer, beans, lettuce, paneer-veggie, pico de gallo, avocado and sauce. Fold sides in, roll from bottom, keeping fillings tucked, fold top edges inward. Toast on hot pan until golden on both sides. Alternatively, serve as burrito bowl in a bowl.

name: Poha Ka Halwa
instructions: Roast cashews and almonds in 1 tsp ghee and set aside with raisins. Heat 1/2 cup ghee in heavy-bottomed pan, add poha and roast until golden and fragrant, about 10-15 minutes on low flame. Add grated coconut and continue roasting 2-3 minutes. Pour milk gradually while stirring constantly. Add jaggery and stir well until dissolved. Add crushed cardamom and cinnamon powder. Cook on low flame until halwa thickens and leaves the sides of the pan, about 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add roasted dry fruits, mix well. Transfer to serving dish. Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Apple Murabba
instructions: Wash and cut apples into halves. In a heavy-bottomed pan, mix apples with sugar. Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally. Add crushed cardamom, cinnamon, saffron soaked in water, and lemon juice. Continue cooking until apples become translucent and syrup thickens (approximately 1-2 hours). Stir frequently to prevent burning. When apples are soft and preserve reaches jam-like consistency, transfer to clean jars and seal.

name: Dumbu
ingredients: oil (1 tsp for dough + 2-3 tsp for frying)
instructions: Mix wheat flour, salt, curd, and baking soda with minimal oil to form a soft dough. Knead well and let rest for 15 minutes. In a pan, heat oil with mustard seeds, add chopped green chilli and tomato. Add spice powders and salt. Mix to combine. Divide dough into portions, flatten each piece, add the tomato mixture to center, fold into balls, and flatten again. Shallow-fry or deep-fry in oil until golden brown on both sides. Serve hot.

name: Tilkut Laddu
instructions: Dry roast sesame seeds in a pan on medium heat for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly until fragrant. Crush jaggery into powder or break into small pieces. Heat ghee in the same pan, add crushed jaggery and melt on low heat. Add roasted sesame seeds and dry ginger powder. Mix thoroughly until well combined. Let cool slightly, then shape into laddus (balls) by oiling your palms and rolling the mixture. Cool completely on a plate before storing.

name: Amla Badam Pak
instructions: Remove seeds from amla and cut into pieces. Roast long pepper, nutmeg, and cardamom in ghee until fragrant. Add amla pieces and cook until soft. In a separate pan, roast almonds in ghee until golden, then grind into powder. Add almond powder to the cooked amla mixture. Add dry ginger powder and cook for 2-3 minutes. Heat remaining ghee, add sugar and melt completely. Combine the amla-almond mixture with the melted sugar. Mix well and transfer to a greased plate. Cool completely, cut into pieces, and garnish with almond flakes.

name: Saffron Kalakand
instructions: Crumble paneer into a mixing bowl using your fingers to make breadcrumb-like texture. Heat ghee in a pan, add crumbled paneer and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until slightly golden. Add condensed milk and mix thoroughly. Soak saffron threads in warm milk and add to the mixture along with crushed cardamom. Cook on medium heat, stirring constantly for 3-5 minutes until the mixture leaves the sides of the pan. Transfer to a greased plate, smooth the top, and cool. Cut into squares when set.

name: Bajra Churma Laddu
instructions: Dry roast sesame seeds in a pan on medium heat for 2-3 minutes until fragrant. Set aside. Roast millet flour in ghee (1 tbsp) on low heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring continuously until light golden in color. Crush jaggery into powder. Heat ghee (2 tbsp) in a pan, add crushed jaggery and melt on low heat. Add roasted millet flour, sesame seeds, nutmeg, ginger powder, and cardamom. Mix thoroughly until well combined. Cool slightly, then shape into laddus by oiling your palms.

name: Besan Burfi
instructions: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan on medium heat. Add gram flour gradually, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Continue roasting for 8-10 minutes, stirring frequently until the flour becomes light brown and fragrant. Add powdered sugar and cardamom powder, mixing thoroughly. Pour the mixture onto a greased plate or in a greased pan, smooth the top, and let cool completely. Cut into squares or rectangles and garnish with almond flakes. Store in an airtight container.

name: Chana Kabab
instructions: Mash boiled chickpeas coarsely. Mix in finely chopped onion, garlic, green chilli, ginger, and fresh coriander. Season with salt and black pepper. Mix well until combined. Divide into 6 equal portions and shape into oval patties. Brush lightly with oil on both sides. Place on a microwave-safe tray and cook using the microwave's convection setting for chana kebab (approximately 8-10 minutes at 200 degrees Celsius), or shallow-fry in a pan over medium heat for 3-4 minutes on each side until golden brown.

name: Amla Candy
instructions: Boil gooseberries until soft and tender. Dry them completely in sun or oven. Boil sugar candy in minimal water to make syrup, add lemon juice. Dip dried gooseberries in syrup, then roll in powdered sugar candy. Dry completely on cloth in shade for 2-3 days until they become candy-like. Store in airtight container.

name: Bajra Pancake Chila
instructions: Mix pearl millet flour with curd and let rest 15 minutes. Add salt, cumin, carom seeds, turmeric, red chilli, ginger-chilli paste, asafoetida and sesame. Finely chop spinach and fresh coriander, add to batter. Just before cooking, add eno fruit salt to make batter fluffy. Heat oil-greased griddle, pour batter and cook like pancakes until golden brown on both sides. Serve with green chutney made from radish leaves, coriander, green chilli, ginger, cumin, and dry mango powder.

name: No-Fry Moong Dal Pakodi Curry
instructions: Soak moong dal 2-3 hours, grind with ginger, green chilli, asafoetida, salt and coriander to a thick paste. Shape into pakodi and boil in water until they float and sink again, then remove. In a pot, temper oil with bay leaf, black pepper, cloves, black cardamom and cumin seeds. Add chopped tomato, cook until soft. Add coriander powder, turmeric, kashmiri red chilli. Stir in curt, add salt. Gently add boiled pakodi to gravy. Simmer 5-10 minutes. Garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Milona Roti
instructions: Mix wheat flour, gram flour, salt, carom seeds, black cumin with ghee until breadcrumb texture. Add water slowly to make soft dough. Cover and rest 30 minutes. For filling: Boil and mash potatoes. Temper mustard oil with cumin seeds, asafoetida, add chopped tomato, ginger, green chilli. Add mashed potato, turmeric, coriander powder, kashmiri chilli, garam masala, salt, coriander leaves. Cook filling until aromatic. Divide dough into balls, flatten, add potato filling, roll out. Cook on griddle or tawa until golden brown on both sides with ghee.

name: Moong Dal Dahi Bhalla
instructions: Soak moong dal for 4-6 hours. Grind to a smooth paste without adding water (texture should be thick and airy). Add grated ginger, chopped green chilli and salt, mix well. Heat water in a pot and bring to gentle boil. Using wet hands, drop small portions of batter into water. Once bhalla float and cook for 2-3 minutes more, remove with slotted spoon and place on cloth to drain. Just before serving, whip curd with little salt to make it creamy, add cooked bhallas to it. Top with sweet chutney, chilli chutney, pomegranate seeds, red chilli powder, black salt and roasted cumin powder. Serve immediately.

name: bhajni chakli
instructions: Grind rice, chana dal, urad dal, and moong dal into a fine powder and sift. Dry roast coriander seeds, cumin seeds, sesame seeds, and carom seeds, then grind into powder. Mix all powders with salt, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, asafoetida, and 1 tbsp oil to form a stiff dough. Using a chakli maker, press dough into hot oil to form spirals. Deep fry until golden and crispy on both sides.

name: sepu vadi
ingredients: urad dal with skin - 1/2 cup (100 grams)
instructions: Soak and grind urad dal with coriander, cumin, fennel, clove, and black pepper into a batter. Mix with asafoetida and salt. Blanch spinach, chop finely, and mix into batter. Deep fry spoonfuls of mixture into cylindrical dumplings (vadi). For curry: Heat oil and ghee, add bay leaf, cinnamon, black cardamom, and remaining cumin/pepper. Add ginger-chilli paste and fry. Add tomatoes and cook until soft. Add turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli powder. Simmer fried vadis in curry, add kasuri methi, and cook for 5 minutes until flavors blend.

name: magdal
ingredients: moong dal split and husked - 1 cup (200 grams)
instructions: Clean moong dal using a damp cloth. Grind into fine powder and sift through. Melt ghee over medium heat in a heavy pan. Add moong dal powder and stir continuously for 20-25 minutes until light brown. Cool completely. Mix in sugar powder. Whip mixture with hands until fluffy and light in color. Grease hands with ghee and shape into small round or oval pieces. Garnish each piece with pistachio flakes, pressing gently to adhere.

name: chole palak
instructions: Soak chickpeas overnight. Boil with bay leaf, cinnamon, black pepper, clove, black cardamom, salt, and baking soda for 45 minutes until tender. Drain and set aside. Heat oil, add cumin seeds. Add ginger-chilli paste and fry briefly. Add tomatoes and cook until soft. Add turmeric, kashmiri red chilli, and coriander powder. Add spinach and cook until wilted. Add boiled chickpeas and kasuri methi. Simmer for 5-10 minutes. Finish with ghee, extra green chillies, and kashmiri red chilli.

name: green moong burfi
instructions: Dry roast green moong until lightly colored and fragrant. Separately roast sesame seeds, coconut, pumpkin seeds until crispy. Roast edible gum until it puffs up. Roast peanuts until aromatic. Heat ghee in heavy-bottomed pan and add roasted moong, frying until dark brown (15-20 minutes). Add roasted sesame, coconut, pumpkin seeds, peanuts, edible gum, and ground spices (nutmeg, dry ginger, crushed cardamom). Mix well. Fold in jaggery and stir until all ingredients are coated and uniform. Spread on greased surface, cool completely, and cut into pieces.

name: moong dal mangode
instructions: Soak moong dal for 30-45 minutes, then drain thoroughly. Grind coriander seeds, fennel seeds, and cumin seeds coarsely. Mix drained dal with ground spices, carom seeds, salt, green chilli, ginger, kasuri methi, coriander leaves, and asafoetida to form a thick batter without water. Drop spoonfuls into hot oil and deep fry until golden and crispy on all sides. For chutney: Blend mint, coriander leaves, green chilli, ginger, black salt, and cumin seeds. Add asafoetida and lemon juice. Serve hot mangode with chutney.

name: Gobi Masala Paratha
instructions: Knead wheat flour, gram flour, and spices with water into a dough. Make cauliflower filling with grated cauliflower, green chilli, ginger, spices and coriander. Roll dough, stuff with filling, and fry in hot oil until golden brown.

name: Khaskhas Almond Burfi
instructions: Soak poppy seeds and almonds. Grind them finely with milk. Heat ghee, add the paste, and cook until golden. Add sugar and cook until burfi consistency. Add crushed cardamom and saffron. Pour into a greased pan and garnish with almond and pistachio flakes. Cut into pieces when cool.

name: Corn Dhebra
instructions: Combine corn flour, wheat flour, and all dry spices. Mix in fenugreek leaves, coriander, and ginger-chilli paste. Add curd and ghee. Gradually add water to form dough. Rest for 15-20 minutes. Roll into flatbread shapes. Cook on medium heat with ghee until golden brown on both sides.

name: Jaggery Thekua
instructions: Mix wheat flour with ghee, fennel seeds, and cardamom powder. Powder the jaggery and mix it in along with grated coconut. Gradually add small amounts of water to form a dough. Form into small balls or use a mould to shape. Deep fry in hot oil until golden brown on all sides.

name: Radish Leaves Bhujiya
instructions: Wash and chop radish leaves finely. Heat mustard oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds and asafoetida, let them splutter. Add chopped green chilli. Add radish leaves and stir-fry. Add turmeric powder and salt. Cook until leaves are tender. Garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Whole Wheat Jaggery Cake
instructions: Mix jaggery with curd and olive oil. In another bowl, combine wheat flour, semolina, baking powder, baking soda, nutmeg, cinnamon, and cardamom. Alternate adding dry ingredients and milk to the wet mixture, stirring gently. Fold in chopped walnuts. Pour into a greased cooker-safe vessel. Place on a stand or trivet in pressure cooker with vent open. Cook on high heat until steam comes steadily, then reduce. Bake for 35-40 minutes. Let cool before serving.

name: Jaggery Coconut Laddu
instructions: Roast shredded coconut with ghee over medium heat until lightly golden. In separate pan, melt jaggery on low heat. Mix roasted coconut with melted jaggery and crushed cardamom. Cool slightly, then shape into round balls while still warm. Store in airtight container.

name: Kadhai Mushroom
instructions: Heat oil in a kadhai or wok. Add cumin seeds and asafoetida. Add mushroom and capsicum, stir-fry for 3-4 minutes. Add tomato pieces, ginger, and green chilli. Cook until tomato is soft. Mix curd with gram flour and add to the pan. Add turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, fenugreek leaves, and salt. Simmer on low heat for 5-7 minutes. Finish with garam masala and garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Mawa Stuffed Anarsa
instructions: Soak rice for 30 minutes and grind to a smooth paste using milk. Prepare filling: mix mawa with sugar powder and crushed cardamom. Take small portions of rice paste, flatten, place filling in center, seal and shape into oval or twisted form. Roast sesame seeds and coat the anarsa or sprinkle on top. Can be deep-fried until golden (traditional) or baked at 180C for 15-20 minutes.

name: Bajra Idli
instructions: Mix bajra flour, semolina, curd, salt, green chilli, ginger, and green peas. Add eno fruit salt just before steaming and mix gently. Pour batter into greased idli molds and steam for 8-10 minutes until cooked. For chutney: heat oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, and red chilli. Add peanuts, coconut, green chilli, and ginger. Cook briefly then grind coarsely. Add lemon juice, black salt, and curry leaves. Serve idli with chutney.

name: Coconut Cookies
instructions: Cream ghee and sugar powder together until light and fluffy. Add refined flour, baking powder, and desiccated coconut. Mix well. Gradually add milk and knead into a stiff dough. Shape into round cookies. Option 1: Bake in preheated oven at 180C for 12-15 minutes until golden. Option 2: Place cookies on a low-heat griddle (tawa) and cook covered on low heat, turning occasionally until golden and crispy. Cool completely before storing.

name: Methi Dal Vada
ingredients: cumin powder 0.5 tsp roasted for chutney
instructions: Soak chana dal, urad dal, and moong dal for 3-4 hours. Drain well and grind coarsely (not into smooth paste). Add ginger, green chilli, salt, cumin seeds, asafoetida, fenugreek leaves, and curry leaves. Mix well. Heat oil for deep frying. Shape mixture into balls or vada shape and deep-fry until golden brown on all sides. For chutney: heat oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, red chilli. Add spinach, coriander, ginger, green chilli, cook briefly. Grind coarsely. Mix in lemon juice, black salt, asafoetida. Serve vada hot with chutney.

name: Methi Masala Bati
ingredients: ginger chilli paste 1 tsp for filling
red chilli powder 0.25 tsp for filling
dry mango powder 0.25 tsp for filling
instructions: Knead dough with wheat flour, semolina, salt, carom seeds, baking soda, finely chopped fenugreek leaves, and ghee. Let rest for 15-20 minutes. Prepare filling: boil and peel potatoes, mash with oil, cumin seeds, asafoetida, ginger-chilli paste, salt, turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, and dry mango powder. Mix well. Divide dough into 6 portions. Flatten each, add 2 tbsp filling in center, seal edges. Shape into round balls. Bake in preheated oven at 200C for 20-25 minutes until golden, or cook on preheated griddle until puffed and golden. Serve hot.

name: Paneer Methi Matar Malai
instructions: Make paste of cashews, green chilli, and ginger using 2 tbsp oil. Blanch fenugreek leaves briefly and chop. Heat 2 tbsp ghee and 1 tbsp oil, add cumin seeds, clove, black pepper, cinnamon, and black cardamom. Add chopped tomato and fenugreek leaves, cook until soft.
