Add cashew paste and cook for 2-3 minutes. Cut paneer into cubes and add with peas. Add coriander powder, kashmiri red chilli, asafoetida, and salt. Simmer for 5-7 minutes. Pour in cream and stir gently. Finish with remaining 1 tbsp ghee. Simmer 2-3 minutes more. Serve hot.

name: Sindhi Khorak
ingredients: dry fruits (dates, raisins, almonds, cashews, walnuts)
instructions: Dry roast gum arabic and goond until they puff and turn golden. Soak and chop dry fruits into small pieces. Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot and lightly roast the dry fruits until fragrant. Add the roasted puffed ingredients and mix well. Add crushed cardamom and nutmeg powder. Stir in sugar or melted jaggery until well combined. Spread on parchment paper to cool completely. Break into desired pieces and store in airtight container.

name: Anarsa
instructions: Soak and grind rice into a fine paste. Dry roast poppy seeds and grind coarsely. Crush cardamom pods into powder. Mix rice paste with ground poppy seeds, sugar, and cardamom powder. Mix well to form a soft dough. Heat ghee for deep frying. Shape small portions of dough into flat discs or oval shapes. Deep fry in hot ghee until golden brown on both sides. Drain on absorbent paper. Store in airtight container for up to 2 weeks.

name: Methi Masala Mathari
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp ghee, temper with cumin seeds and asafoetida, add chopped methi leaves and cook until dry. Mix refined flour and semolina in a bowl. Add the methi tempering, 3 tbsp ghee, carom seeds, black pepper, chilli flakes, sesame seeds, and salt. Add 4 tbsp flour mixed with 2 tbsp ghee to form a crumbly dough. Knead into a firm dough adding water if needed. Divide into portions and roll thin between parchment paper. Cut into desired shapes. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy on both sides. Drain on paper towels. Store in airtight container.

name: Sabudana Paratha
instructions: Soak sabudana for 2-3 hours until softened. Boil and mash potatoes. Mix soaked sabudana with mashed potatoes, ginger-chilli paste, black pepper, salt, coriander, and cumin seeds. Knead into a soft dough. Divide into balls and flatten into parathas. Heat ghee on a griddle and cook paratha on medium heat, turning occasionally until golden and cooked through. Serve hot. Can also be shaped and deep fried as puri.

name: Til Badam Laddu
instructions: Dry roast sesame seeds, almonds, and peanuts separately until fragrant and lightly golden. Cool completely. Chop or coarsely powder the roasted nuts and seeds. Heat 3-4 tbsp ghee in a heavy pan, add the nut-seed mixture and cook on low heat for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly. Add desiccated coconut and mix well. Melt jaggery in separate pan or add in small pieces to the nut mixture, mix thoroughly. Add nutmeg and crushed cardamom, stir well. Remove from heat and let cool until handleable. Roll into laddu balls while still warm. Store in airtight container.

name: Methi Gatta Curry
instructions: For Gatta: Mix gram flour, salt, turmeric, red chilli, coriander powder, green chilli, yogurt, oil, and finely chopped methi. Add baking soda and knead into a stiff dough. Shape into cylindrical logs and steam for 15 minutes. Cool and cut into pieces. For Curry: Heat oil, temper with cumin and asafoetida. Add chopped tomato and cook until soft. Add ginger-green chilli paste, turmeric, coriander, and kashmiri chilli. Cook for 2 minutes. Add gatta pieces, fenugreek leaves, yogurt, salt, and garam masala. Simmer for 8-10 minutes. Serve hot with rice or bread.

name: Melon Seeds Chikki
instructions: Lightly roast all the seeds separately until they release aroma and become slightly darker. Mix all roasted seeds in a bowl. Grease a baking sheet or marble slab with ghee. Heat jaggery in a heavy-bottomed pan on medium heat, stirring occasionally until it melts and reaches hard-ball stage (test by dropping small amount in water - should become brittle). Add roasted seed mixture, nutmeg, and crushed cardamom to melted jaggery. Mix quickly and thoroughly. Immediately pour onto greased surface and spread evenly. Let cool for 2-3 minutes until just set but still flexible. Cut into desired pieces with a greased knife. Cool completely before storing in airtight container.

name: Gobi Matar
instructions: Coat cauliflower florets with spices (turmeric, red chilli, coriander, cumin, salt) and gram flour, then deep fry until golden. In a separate pot, heat oil and temper with cumin seeds, bay leaf, cinnamon, cloves, black pepper, and cardamom. Add ginger and tomatoes, cook until soft. Stir in curd and spice powders (coriander, turmeric, red chilli). Add fried cauliflower and green peas, simmer until peas are cooked. Finish with fenugreek leaves and fresh coriander.

name: Mixed Fruit & Nut Frozen Yogurt
instructions: Combine yogurt with sugar-free sweetener and mix well. Chop apricots into small pieces. Roughly chop or slice almonds and pistachios. Fold fruits and nuts into the sweetened yogurt, reserving some for topping. Pour into freezer-safe container and freeze until partially set. Stir occasionally to break ice crystals. Freeze completely for 4-6 hours. Scoop into bowls and top with reserved nuts and fruit pieces.

name: mix veg makhani
instructions: Heat butter in a pan, add finely chopped onions and cook until golden brown. Add minced garlic and ginger, sauté for 30 seconds. Add tomatoes and cook until soft and mushy. Add the mixed vegetables and cook for 5-7 minutes until partially tender. Pour in cream and makhani sauce, mix well. Simmer for 5 minutes until vegetables are cooked through and gravy reaches desired consistency. Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot with rice or bread.

name: sanjori
instructions: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat. Add chopped nuts and dry fruits, roast until fragrant and light brown. Add gram flour gradually, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Roast the mixture for 8-10 minutes until it turns golden and fragrant. In a separate pan, warm jaggery with 2 tablespoons water until melted and syrupy. Add the roasted gram flour mixture to the jaggery, mixing quickly and thoroughly. Add cardamom powder and salt, mix well. Pour onto a greased surface and spread evenly. Allow to cool for a few minutes, then cut into desired shapes using a knife or mold. Store in an airtight container.

name: matcha tea cake
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Sift matcha powder with flour and baking powder to remove lumps. In a mixing bowl, cream softened butter with sugar-free sweetener until light and fluffy, about 3-4 minutes. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Alternate between adding the matcha-flour mixture and milk, starting and ending with flour mixture. Mix until just combined. Pour batter into a greased and lined cake pan. Bake for 30-35 minutes until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool in pan for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a wire rack to cool completely. Serve plain or with a light glaze.

name: Mango Pickle
instructions: Wash and cut raw mangoes into pieces. Roast fennel seeds, fenugreek seeds, cumin seeds, mustard seeds, black pepper, black cardamom and cloves in a pan until fragrant. Grind into coarse powder. In large bowl, combine mango pieces with roasted spice powder, sesame oil, additional fennel seeds, asafoetida, black salt, salt and turmeric. Mix well. For sweet variation: Add jaggery and kashmiri red chilli, mix thoroughly. For sugar variation: Add sugar instead of jaggery and kashmiri red chilli. Add chopped dates to both variations. Mix gently. Transfer to glass jars, cover and allow flavors to meld for 3-4 days before serving. Stir occasionally.

name: Rasmalai
ingredients: milk 1 litre full fat for ras
milk 1 litre full fat for chena
instructions: For flavoured milk: Boil 1 litre full fat milk, add sliced almonds, pistachios, cardamom powder, saffron and sugar. Simmer and let cool. For chena: Heat 1 litre full fat milk with 2 tbsp water, bring to boil. Add 2 tbsp vinegar and stir until milk curdles. Strain through muslin cloth and press to remove water. Mix chena with 1 tsp refined flour and ice cold water to form smooth paste. For cooking: Prepare sugar syrup with 1.5 cups sugar and 6 cups water, bring to boil. Shape chena mixture into balls, place in boiling syrup and cook until they float. Remove and soak in flavoured milk for at least 30 minutes before serving. Chill and serve cold.

name: Thatte Idli
instructions: Soak rice, urad dal, and fenugreek seeds separately for 4-6 hours. Grind urad dal with some water to make a fluffy batter. Grind rice with water to a coarse paste. Mix both batters. Add salt and let it ferment overnight. Pour batter into greased bowl or pan. Steam in boiling water for 15-20 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean. Serve warm.

name: Green Chilli Pickle
instructions: Wash and dry green chillies completely. Crush mustard seeds coarsely. In a bowl, combine salt, turmeric powder, fennel powder, kashmiri red chilli powder, and asafoetida. Add whole green chillies to spice mixture and toss to coat. Pour mustard oil and vinegar over chillies and mix gently but thoroughly. Transfer to glass jar and let sit for 30 minutes before serving. No cooking required - instant pickle ready to eat immediately.

name: Khasta Kachori
instructions: Make pastry dough: Mix refined flour, salt, carom seeds, and baking soda. Rub in ghee until breadcrumb texture. Add water gradually and knead into a smooth dough. Rest for 30 minutes. Make filling: Soak and grind urad dal finely. Heat oil, add gram flour, and fry until light brown. Add ground urad dal, all spice powders, salt, asafoetida, fennel seeds, dried mango powder, and garam masala. Cook until dry. Cool completely. Assemble: Divide dough into lemon-sized balls. Flatten each, place filling in center, seal edges, and shape into a round. Deep fry in hot oil until puffed and golden brown on both sides. Drain on paper towels. Serve hot with potato curry and tamarind chutney.

name: Mango Sago Drink
instructions: Boil water and soak sago pearls until translucent and cooked. Peel and blend ripe mangoes with sugar to make a smooth puree. Mix mango puree into cold milk. Add the cooked sago pearls and stir well. Chill thoroughly in refrigerator. Serve cold in glasses.

name: Indian Style Korean Noodles
instructions: Boil Korean noodles according to packet instructions and drain. Heat oil in a wok or large pan. Add minced garlic and stir briefly. Add onion and sauté until translucent. Add capsicum, bell peppers, tomato, carrot, and peas. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes on high heat. Add cooked noodles and toss everything together well. Season with salt and black pepper. Add water if needed for desired consistency. Garnish with green onion and serve immediately.

name: Idli Shakshouka
instructions: Heat oil in a large pan and add mustard seeds. When they crackle, add curry leaves and chopped green chilli. Add minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds. Add diced onion and sauté until translucent. Add tomato and pepper pieces, cook until soft. Stir in turmeric, red chilli, coriander powder, and garam masala. Add schezwan chutney and tomato ketchup. Pour in the idli batter and mix well to combine. Cook on medium heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the batter is fully cooked and has a thick curry-like consistency. Serve hot.

name: Bihari Mustard Fish Curry
instructions: Grind big mustard seeds, coriander seeds, cumin seeds, ginger, garlic, and green chillies into a fine paste. Clean and cut fish into pieces. Marinate fish with 1/2 tsp turmeric, salt, and lemon juice for 15 minutes. Heat oil in a pan and fry the marinated fish until golden on both sides. Remove fish and set aside. In the same oil, add the prepared spice paste and cook for 2-3 minutes over medium heat. Add tomato paste and cook until oil separates. Add 1 tsp turmeric, kashmiri red chilli, 1/2 tsp cumin seeds, and water. Bring to a boil. Add the fried fish back into the curry. Reduce heat and simmer for 12-15 minutes until the curry reaches desired consistency. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot with rice.

name: Pakhala Bhat
instructions: Prepare fermented rice: Mix cooked rice with water and curd, stir well and let ferment overnight. Prepare red saag curry by heating mustard oil, adding cumin seeds and curry leaves, then garlic and green chillies, then saag with salt. For fried eggplant, cut into pieces, coat with turmeric and red chilli powder, fry in oil. For aloo bharta, mash boiled potatoes and mix with diced onion, coriander, salt, mustard oil and dry red chillies. Prepare tomato chutney by cooking tomatoes with garlic, green chilli, mustard oil and coriander. For bharwa bhindi, cut okra, mix with cumin powder, red chilli, coriander powder, garam masala, salt and aamchur, then lightly fry. Deep fry moong dal wadi until crispy. To serve, place fermented rice in center of plate with onion rings, green chilli, coriander and lemon on top. Arrange all cooked vegetables around it.

name: Cheese Chilli Paratha
instructions: Knead wheat flour with water and salt to make a soft dough, let rest for 15 minutes. Mix mozzarella cheese with finely chopped capsicum, green pepper, onion, chilli flakes, mixed herbs, and oregano to make the filling. Divide dough into 4 equal portions. Roll each portion into a circle, place cheese mixture in the center, fold edges and seal properly. Gently flatten into a paratha shape. Cook on a hot griddle or tawa with ghee or oil, pressing gently with a spatula, cook until golden brown spots appear on both sides. Serve hot with yogurt or pickle.

name: Vegetable Pizza
instructions: Prepare pizza dough: Mix all purpose flour, salt, and sugar. Dissolve yeast in warm water for 10 minutes until foamy, then add to dry ingredients with oil. Knead to a soft dough and let rise for 1-2 hours until doubled in size. Prepare pizza sauce: Blend tomato with dry chilli, garlic, mixed herbs, oregano, soya sauce, oil, and salt. Roll dough to desired thickness and place on a baking tray or pizza pan. Spread pizza sauce evenly. Arrange toppings: sliced green, yellow, and red peppers, sweet corn, capsicum, onion, and cheese. Sprinkle chilli flakes and oregano. Bake in preheated oven at 200°C for 15-20 minutes until cheese melts and crust is golden brown. Alternatively, cook in cooker or covered pan on stovetop.

name: Mango Sevai Custard
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan and roast vermicelli until golden brown and fragrant. In another pan, bring milk to a boil, add roasted vermicelli and cook until soft and well blended. Mix custard powder with a little cold milk to make a smooth paste. Pour this paste into the hot milk mixture while stirring continuously to avoid lumps. Add sugar and mango pulp, stir well. Add mixed fruits (fresh or canned). Cook for another 2-3 minutes. Pour into serving bowls and garnish with dry fruits like cashews, almonds, and pistachios. Serve chilled or warm.

name: Instant Mango Pickle
instructions: Wash raw mangoes thoroughly and cut into small pieces or grate them. Finely mince garlic and green chilli. Mix all spice seeds (fenugreek, mustard, fennel, nigella, carom) together. Add asafoetida, red chilli powder, and turmeric powder to the spice mixture. In a large sterilized jar, layer mango pieces with the spice mixture, minced garlic, and green chilli. Pour mustard oil over the layers and add salt to taste. Mix everything thoroughly. Store in an airtight glass container. This instant pickle is ready to eat immediately without any fermentation time. Store in a cool, dry place and consume within 2-3 months.

name: Cone Chat
instructions: Make cone shells from maida dough by frying in cone molds. Boil and dice potatoes and chickpeas. Dice onions and tomatoes finely. Mix all filling ingredients with spices, chaat masala, lemon juice and salt. Fill cones with this mixture just before serving. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Bhindi Do Pyaza
instructions: Wash and cut bhindi into long pieces. Deep fry onion slices until golden and crispy, set aside. In remaining oil, add cumin seeds. Add green chillies, garlic, and curry leaves. Add one portion of onions and tomatoes. Add all spices and turmeric. Fry bhindi pieces separately until tender. Add fried bhindi to the gravy, mix with aamchur powder and reserved crispy onions. Finish with coriander leaves.

name: Samosa
instructions: Mix maida flour with salt and oil. Add water gradually to make a stiff dough. Let rest for 30 minutes. Boil and mash potatoes, mix with peas, green chillies, ginger, cumin seeds, coriander leaves, amchur and garam masala. Roll dough into thin sheets, cut triangles, fold into cone shape. Fill with potato mixture and seal edges. Deep fry until golden brown and crispy.

name: Healthy Granola Bar
instructions: Soak dates in warm water for 10 minutes, then blend into paste. Chop almonds, pistachios, cashews finely. Mix with sunflower seeds, pumpkin seeds, flax seeds, and oats. Add date paste, honey, and cinnamon. Mix well. Press into parchment-lined tray. Melt dark chocolate with ghee, spread on top. Refrigerate for 2 hours, cut into bars.

name: Drumstick Masala Curry
instructions: Cut drumsticks into 2-inch pieces. Fry in oil until light brown. In separate pan, heat oil and add cumin, mustard, and fennel seeds. Add green chillies and fry. Add onions and cook until golden. Add ginger-garlic-chili paste and cook. Add all dry spices and turmeric. Add fried drumsticks and tamarind paste. Add water and simmer until drumsticks are tender. Season with salt. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Sattu Drink
instructions: Add sattu to a bowl and mix with a little water to make a smooth paste. Chop onions, mint, coriander leaves, and green chillies. Grate raw mango or chop finely. Add all ingredients to sattu paste. Add roasted cumin powder, black salt, and plain salt. Add remaining water and mix well. Stir thoroughly to combine. Serve chilled over ice.

name: Dhaba Style Egg Curry
instructions: Boil eggs and peel them. Heat oil and add cumin seeds, bay leaf, cardamom, star anise, and mace. Add chopped onions and cook until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft. Add turmeric, red chili, coriander, and cumin powders along with garam masala. Add water and bring to boil. Add boiled eggs and simmer until gravy thickens. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Mango Rice
instructions: Heat oil and add mustard seeds. When they crackle, add urad dal and let it turn golden. Add curry leaves, garlic, and green chili. Saute briefly. Add grated raw mango and turmeric. Mix well. Add the cooked rice and mix everything together until well combined. Garnish with peanuts.

name: Bharwa Lal Mirch Ka Achar
instructions: Slit red chillies and remove seeds. Dry roast coriander, cumin, fenugreek, fennel seeds and dry red chillies. Grind roasted spices to powder. Mix turmeric, carom, nigella seeds, hing, red chili powder and amchur powder. Stuff this spice mixture inside slit red chillies. Heat mustard oil and place stuffed chillies in it. Layer with remaining spice powder. Cover and let it sit for 1-2 weeks before serving.

name: Amritsari Nutri Kulcha
instructions: Boil nutri nuggets and keema with water, salt, green chili and ginger. Make nutri masala by roasting and grinding all the spices. Heat ghee and add hing. Saute onions until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste. Add tomato puree. Add all spice powders including the nutri masala. Add boiled and drained nutri. Add water and simmer until thick. Finish with kasoori methi. Brush kulchas with butter. Serve hot nutri curry with kulchas.

name: Shezwan Chilli Potato
instructions: Boil potatoes and shape into mushroom-like balls. Make chilli paste by grinding dried red chillies with spices, garlic and ginger. Make schezwan sauce by heating oil, adding garlic, ginger, schezwan pepper and onions. Add bell peppers. Add ketchup, soya sauce, vinegar and water. Add cornstarch slurry to thicken. Season with salt and sugar. Add cooked potatoes to sauce and simmer. Garnish with spring onions and serve.

name: Veg Kimchi
instructions: Soak dried chillies, then grind with garlic, ginger, water to make chilli paste. Add vinegar, ketchup, and sugar. Chop napa cabbage, regular cabbage, radish, carrots, and spring onions. Salt the vegetables and let rest. Wash out salt with chilled water. Make rice flour slurry with water, soy sauce and sesame seeds. Mix slurry with vegetables and chilli paste. Mix well and serve immediately or let ferment for 2-3 days for tangy flavor. Plate with remaining sauce.

name: Bharwan Shimla Mirch
instructions: Heat oil and temper with kalonji. Add ginger, garlic, onion, and green chillies. Add boiled corn and potato. Season with salt, pepper, and chilli flakes. Add crumbled paneer and coriander. This is the stuffing. For the gravy, heat oil, add bay leaf, black pepper, black cardamom, cumin, and coriander seeds. Add onion, ginger, and garlic. Add turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, coriander powder, and black pepper. Add tomatoes and cook until soft. Blend smooth or leave chunky as preferred. Cut capsicums in half, deseed them, and fill with the paneer-potato mixture. Heat butter on tawa/griddle and place stuffed capsicums. Roast until golden. Serve with the tomato gravy, garnished with kasoori methi and fresh coriander.

name: Cheese Bread Pakora
instructions: Make besan batter with red chilli powder, salt, turmeric, kasoori methi, jeera powder, and water. Keep batter smooth and consistent. For stuffing, heat oil and add mustard seeds. Add green chilli, ginger, garlic, curry leaves, onion, carrots, and capsicum. Stir-fry until vegetables are cooked. Add mashed potato and coriander. Mix well and let cool. Take bread slices and cut edges. Place a spoonful of potato-vegetable mixture in the center. Top with a cheese stick. Roll the bread tightly and seal edges with water. Dip the rolls in besan batter ensuring complete coverage. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy on all sides. Drain on paper towel and sprinkle chaat masala. Serve hot.

name: Tawa Paneer Masala
instructions: For gravy: Roast tomatoes and onions with black cardamom, cloves, green cardamom, cumin, ginger, and garlic. Add kashmiri chilli powder, turmeric, and water. Cook until soft. Blend smooth. Strain if needed. For tawa masala: Heat oil, add cumin, ginger, green chilli, and garlic. Add butter and diced onion. Stir-fry until onion is translucent. Add yellow, green, and red capsicum. Cook for 2-3 minutes. Add kashmiri chilli powder, turmeric, coriander powder, and garam masala. Dice paneer and add to the tawa masala. Cook for 2 minutes. Add 1/2 cup gravy mixture and kasoori methi. Stir gently. Squeeze lemon juice and sprinkle chaat masala. Garnish with fresh coriander and optional cream. Serve hot on a tawa or plate.

name: Aloo 65
instructions: Grate potatoes and soak in iced water for 30 minutes. Drain and squeeze out excess water. In a bowl, mix grated potatoes with salt, kashmiri chilli powder, pepper powder, coriander powder, garam masala, green chillies, curry leaves, coriander, ginger-garlic paste, and lemon juice. Mix cornflour and all-purpose flour together. Add to potato mixture and mix well to form balls. Deep fry potato balls in hot oil until golden and crispy. Remove and drain on paper towel. For tadka: heat oil in a pan, add dry red chillies, ginger, garlic, curry leaves, and green chillies. Toss the fried aloo balls in this tadka until well coated. Serve hot and crispy.

name: Suji Aloo Nuggets
instructions: For nuggets: Heat oil and add asafoetida, cumin, and coriander. Add green chilli, ginger, turmeric, chilli powder, and coriander powder. Roast suji in this mixture until light brown. Add water slowly while stirring to avoid lumps. Cook until thickened. Mix in mashed potato, salt, kasoori methi, black salt, and chaat masala. Let cool completely and shape into balls or nuggets. For tomato chutney: Heat oil, add kalonji and fennel seeds. Add green chilli and kashmiri chilli powder. Add chopped tomato and cook until soft. Add sugar and black salt. Add tomato puree and pepper powder. Add cinnamon stick and cook until thick. Finish with ghee and lemon juice. Strain if desired. Deep fry the suji nuggets in hot oil until golden. Serve hot with smokey tomato chutney.

name: Langarwali Dal
instructions: Soak urad sabut and chana dal overnight. Drain and add to pressure cooker with 5 cups water and salt. Pressure cook until dal is soft and creamy. For first tadka: Heat oil or ghee and add cumin seeds. Add garlic, ginger, onion, and green chillies. Stir-fry until onion is golden. Add tomato and cook until soft. Add turmeric and kashmiri chilli powder. Pour this mixture into the cooked dal and stir well. Add butter and fresh coriander. For second tadka: Heat oil or ghee in a separate pan and add kashmiri chilli powder. Pour this hot tadka over the dal for extra flavor. Serve hot with rice or bread.

name: Aloo Pyaaz Ke Pakore
instructions: For potato stuffing: Heat oil, add heeng, mustard seeds, green chilli, ginger, garlic, and curry leaves. Add turmeric, salt, and chilli powder. Stir in mashed potato and mix well. Set aside. For pakora: Slice onions into rings. Fill the center of each onion ring with potato mixture and diced cheese. Prepare batter with besan, salt, turmeric, chilli powder, ajwain, green chilli, ginger, kasoori methi, garam masala, jeera powder, and water. Add coriander and chaat masala. Dip filled onion rings in batter ensuring complete coverage. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towel. For tandoori mayo dip: Mix mustard oil, kashmiri chilli powder, hung curd, mayonnaise, lemon juice, ginger-garlic paste, coriander, black salt, salt, and kasoori methi. Serve hot pakoras with tandoori mayo dip on the side.

name: Modak
instructions: Classic modak: Mix coconut, jaggery, cardamom, cashew, pista, nutmeg with ghee for filling. Make rice flour dough with water and salt. Use mould to shape, or hand-form into rounded dumplings with pointed tops. Steam in water for 10-12 minutes until set. Jim-jam modak: Crush glucose biscuits, mix with butter and milk to form paste. Sandwich with fruit jam, shape into modak form, and steam for 5 minutes.

name: Veg Jalfrezi
instructions: Heat ghee, add cumin and roast. Add chopped onions, stir-fry on high heat. Add ginger-garlic and green chillies. Add chopped tomatoes and tomato puree. Add turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, and garam masala. Cook on medium-high heat until tomatoes soften. Add cauliflower first (longest cooking), then beans, carrots, and cabbage with a splash of water. Cover and cook on low heat to tenderize. On high heat, add all three capsicums, paneer, coriander, slit chillies, sliced onions, and lemon juice. Toss gently with spatula. Serve with paratha or roti.

name: Kimchi Fried Rice
instructions: Make kimchi: Mix radish, cabbage, carrots, cucumber, spring onions with salt. Make slurry of rice flour and water, boil until thick. Mix in chilli powder, garlic, ginger, sugar, salt, vinegar, soya sauce, sesame oil, sesame seeds, and ketchup. Pour over vegetables and mix well. Let rest for 30 minutes. For fried rice: Heat oil in wok, add instant kimchi and toss. Add boiled rice and mix thoroughly on high heat. Season with salt, add spring onions and sesame oil. Garnish with toasted sesame seeds.

name: Dalia Khichdi
instructions: Boil dalia with ghee, moong dal, turmeric, and water until soft. For tempering: heat ghee, add heeng and cumin. Add ginger, green chilli, onion, and sauté. Add carrot, beans, cauliflower, and tomato. Add turmeric and red chilli powder. Cook until vegetables soften. Add water to achieve desired consistency. Add green peas and kasoori methi. For instant garlic aachar: heat mustard oil, add methi dana and mustard seeds (they will pop). Add saunf, kalonji, dry red chillies, curry leaves. Add peeled garlic cloves and fry until light brown. Add salt, turmeric, red chilli powder, and vinegar. Cool and store. Serve khichdi hot with aachar, yogurt, and papad.

name: Soya Chunks Biryani
instructions: Boil and soften soya chunks in salted water with green chillies. Prepare biryani masala: fry onions until golden brown in oil, remove and set aside. In remaining oil, add cinnamon, cardamom, pather phool, shahi jeera. Add sliced onions and fry till brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and fry. Add turmeric, chilli powder, cardamom powder, mace powder. Cook briefly, add curd and soya chunks with water. Add mint leaves, kewra water, rose water, and kasoori methi. Cook covered on low heat for 15-20 minutes. Boil rice separately in salted water with cardamom, chilli, rose water, and kewra water until 70% cooked. Layer rice over masala, top with fried onions and brown onion oil. Cover and cook on high heat for 2-3 minutes until steam forms, then low heat for 30-40 minutes (dum pukht).

name: Tandoori Onion Salad
instructions: Place whole onions directly on flame or tawa and dry-grill until charred on all sides and softened inside. Let cool slightly. Carefully separate onion layers, keeping them intact. Mix mustard oil, chopped garlic, black salt, salt, red chilli powder, and chaat masala. Drizzle lemon juice into the spiced oil. Arrange grilled onion layers on a plate and pour the spiced dressing over them. Toss gently. Garnish with fresh chopped spring onions. Serve immediately.

name: Pink Sauce Pasta
instructions: Make dough: Mix maida with water and knead into a stiff dough. Knead well until smooth and elastic. Let rest for 10 minutes. Roll out into thin sheet and cut into ribbons (tagliatelle). Dust with flour to prevent sticking. Boil water with salt and oil. Boil pasta ribbons for 5-6 minutes until al dente. Drain and set aside. Make pink sauce: Boil beetroot with skin until soft, peel and puree. Heat butter, add garlic and onion, sauté until soft. Add maida flour and cook for 1 minute. Gradually add milk while stirring to avoid lumps. Add beetroot puree, cheese slices, thyme, oregano, italian seasoning, salt, and pepper. Cook on low heat until thickened and smooth. Toss cooked pasta with pink sauce. Garnish with fresh basil and serve immediately.

name: paneer pakora
instructions: Marinate paneer cubes with salt, turmeric, chilli powder, chaat masala, black salt, coriander and kasoori methi for 15-20 minutes. Prepare besan batter with besan, salt, turmeric, chilli powder, chaat masala, green chilli, ajwain, ginger and garam masala mixed with water to a smooth consistency. Dip marinated paneer in batter and coat generously with crushed cornflakes mixed with chaat masala. Deep fry in oil until golden and crispy. Prepare vegetable dip by mixing mayonnaise with finely chopped onion, tomato, cucumber, salt, coriander and green chilli. Serve hot paneer pakora with chilled vegetable dip.

name: veg thukpa
instructions: Prepare vegetable stock by simmering water with bayleaf, peppercorns, garlic, ginger, coriander stems, onion, beans trimmings, carrot, cauliflower and tomato for 20-30 minutes, then strain. Toast spices (peppercorns, coriander seeds, cumin) and grind. Make tibetan chilli paste by heating oil, adding dried chilli flakes and ground spices, then cooking until fragrant. Heat butter in a pot, add garlic and ginger, sauté onions until translucent, add green chilli and mix. Add tomatoes with turmeric, chilli powder, cumin powder and coriander powder. Pour in the strained vegetable stock and bring to boil. Add carrots, mushrooms, beans and spring onions. Separately steam cabbage, carrots and onions. Boil noodles separately. Assemble by placing noodles in bowl, add steamed vegetables, pour hot thukpa soup, top with chilli paste, garnish with coriander and serve with lemon wedge.

name: mexican fried rice
instructions: Heat oil in a kadhai, add chopped garlic and sauté. Add chopped onions and carrots, stir-fry until softened. Add corn, rajma and capsicum. Sprinkle oregano, coriander powder, cumin powder and kashmiri chilli powder over vegetables. Pour in tomato puree and salt, mix well. Add boiled rice and stir continuously to break clumps and mix evenly with spices. Add fresh tomato pieces, coriander, sugar, olives and jalapenos. Toss everything together and cook for 2-3 minutes. Finish with lemon juice and coriander. For salsa, finely chop onion and green chilli, mix with chunky tomato puree, salt, coriander, tomato ketchup and lemon juice. Serve hot Mexican fried rice with Mexican tomato salsa on the side.

name: korean momos
instructions: Make dough by combining all purpose flour, salt and oil, then gradually add warm water to form smooth dough. Knead well and let rest for 15 minutes. Prepare filling by combining chopped cabbage, spinach, mushroom, spring onion, ginger, garlic, green chilli and pepper powder. Add coriander stems and mashed paneer. Season with soy sauce, vinegar and sesame oil. Remove excess water from filling. Roll dough into thin circles, fill with 30g filling, fold and pleat edges to form momo shape. Steam in steamer basket for 8-10 minutes until cooked. For Korean sauce, heat oil and add chopped garlic, cook until fragrant. Add chilli flakes and stir. Pour in vinegar, soy sauce and water. Add sugar and spring onion. Simmer for 1-2 minutes. Garnish sauce with toasted sesame and fresh red chillies. Serve hot steamed momos with Korean dipping sauce.

name: ghugni
instructions: Soak white peas overnight. Boil peas with onion quarter, 1 green chilli, turmeric and salt in 2.5 cups water until soft, about 30-40 minutes. Toast cardamom, fennel seeds, cumin and coriander seeds to prepare bhaja masala. Heat mustard oil in a large pan, add dried red chillies and cardamom from toasted spices. Add cinnamon stick, bay leaves and cloves, sauté for a minute. Add sliced onions and fry until golden. Add chopped green chilli and ginger garlic paste, stir well. Add cubed potatoes and cook until partially tender. Add turmeric, chilli powder, cumin powder and coriander powder. Pour in diced tomatoes and cook until soft. Add the boiled white peas with their liquid. Simmer for 10 minutes and add bhaja masala powder. Season with salt and a pinch of sugar. Add slit green chilli. Finish with ghee. Serve hot ghugni topped with tamarind water, chopped onion, green chilli, coriander, sev and lemon juice.

name: tomato fried rice
instructions: Heat coconut oil in a heavy-bottomed pot. Add hing, whole red chillies, cinnamon, cloves, bay leaf and cardamom, let them crackle for 30 seconds. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, chana dal and urad dal. When seeds splutter and dals turn golden, add chopped garlic, ginger and green chilli. Sauté until fragrant. Add cashew nuts and curry leaves. Add chopped onions and fry until translucent. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft, breaking them down. Add turmeric, red chilli powder and coriander powder. Stir well. Pour in water and bring to boil. Add soaked rice and stir. Cover and cook on medium-high heat until rice is cooked and water is absorbed, about 15-20 minutes. Garnish with fresh mint and coriander stems. For tadka raita, whisk curd with salt and black pepper, stir in chopped onion. Heat oil in a small pan, add mustard seeds and let splutter. Add chopped ginger, green chilli and curry leaves, then pour the tempering over raita. Serve hot tomato rice with chilled tadka raita on the side.

name: Masala Onion Rings
instructions: 1. Slice large onions into rings. 2. Mix besan with salt, red chilli powder, turmeric, cumin powder, kasoori methi, coriander, chaat masala, and water to form a smooth batter. 3. Coat onion rings in batter, then roll in bread crumbs. 4. Deep fry at medium-high heat until golden and crispy. 5. For sweet chilli sauce: Combine water, vinegar, sugar, chilli flakes, garlic, and salt. Simmer 3-4 minutes. 6. Mix cornstarch with 2 tbsp water and add to sauce, stir until thickened. 7. Serve crispy onion rings hot with sweet chilli sauce.

name: Imli Bada
instructions: 1. For imli water: Add soaked tamarind to water with coriander seeds, gur, roasted cumin, black salt, salt, red chilli powder, green cardamom powder, and pepper. Simmer over low-medium heat until reduced. 2. For bada: Soak moong dal and urad dal overnight. Grind dals with green chilli and ginger without water into a coarse paste, blitzing and moving dal around. 3. Add water gradually while grinding to make smoother paste. 4. Whip the batter in a bowl or paraat by hand until it changes color and becomes lighter (test by holding bowl upside down). 5. Heat oil and drop small portions of batter, fry until golden brown. 6. Drain on kitchen towel. 7. Strain tamarind water into ice in a jar. 8. Once ice melts and volume rises, add badaes, boondi, and mint. 9. Serve chilled.

name: Pyaz Tamatar ki Sabji
instructions: 1. Heat oil, add black cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, cumin. 2. Add green chilli and chopped onions, sauté until golden. 3. Add minced garlic and ginger, cook 2 minutes. 4. Add butter and then turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, coriander powder. Cook 2 minutes. 5. Add garam masala, salt, black salt, chaat masala. 6. Stir in tomatoes and kasoori methi. 7. Add water and simmer 15-20 minutes until tomatoes soften. 8. For finishing: Char onions and tomatoes separately on high heat. 9. Add charred vegetables to the sabzi. 10. Add butter and more water to adjust consistency. 11. Garnish with fresh coriander, green chilli, kasoori methi, and green onion. 12. Serve hot.

name: Besan Cupcakes
instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 350F. 2. Cream butter and powdered sugar together. 3. Add curd and mix well. 4. In separate bowl, combine besan, baking powder, baking soda, cardamom powder. 5. Mix dry ingredients into wet ingredients until smooth. 6. Add vanilla essence. 7. Pour batter (100gm each) into cupcake molds. 8. Bake for 20 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean. 9. Cool completely. 10. For chocolate ganache: Heat whipped cream, chop chocolate finely, pour hot cream over chocolate and let sit 2 minutes, whisk until smooth. 11. For buttercream: Beat butter, mix in powdered sugar, add whipping cream and vanilla essence. 12. Pipe or spread icings on cooled cupcakes. 13. Serve or store in airtight container.

name: Rice Aloo Uttapam
instructions: 1. Mix rice flour with mashed potato and water to form a smooth batter. 2. Add salt and let rest 30 minutes. 3. Mix in chopped onion, tomato, green capsicum, grated carrots, red capsicum, green chilli, ginger, pepper powder, curry leaves, and coriander. 4. Just before cooking, add eno and mix gently until frothy. 5. Heat oil on tawa. 6. Pour batter and spread into pancake shape. 7. Cook on both sides until golden brown and crispy. 8. For tadka dip: Mix yogurt and mayo with salt. 9. Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaf until they splutter. 10. Pour tempering over yogurt-mayo mixture and stir. 11. Add chilli powder. 12. Serve hot pancakes with tadka dip.

name: Moong Dal Pakora Chaat
instructions: 1. Soak all dals overnight. Grind together with cumin, ginger, curry leaves, green chilli until coarse paste. 2. Mix in turmeric, chilli powder, black pepper, coriander, onion, baking powder, salt. 3. Heat oil and fry spoonfuls of batter until golden brown and crispy pakora. Drain on paper towel. 4. For sweet curd: Mix yogurt, sugar, black salt. 5. For amchur chutney: Combine water, amchur powder, roasted cumin powder, chilli powder, black salt, sugar, salt. Simmer until syrupy. 6. For mint chutney: Blend coriander, mint leaves, garlic, ginger, green chilli, salt, black salt, chaat masala, lemon juice. Add ice and water. 7. Assemble: Place pakora in bowl, top with sweet curd, pour amchur chutney and mint chutney. 8. Garnish with pomegranate, coriander, mint leaves, julienned ginger, peanuts, matar namkeen. 9. Serve immediately.

name: Purani Delhi Style Butter Chicken
instructions: 1. Marinate chicken with salt, degi chilli powder, ginger-garlic paste, oil, lemon juice for 30 minutes. 2. For gravy: Heat oil, add bay leaf, sliced onions, sauté 1 minute. 3. Add tomatoes, salt, cashew nuts, degi chilli powder, coriander stems, ginger-garlic paste, coriander powder. Cook 5-6 minutes. 4. Add water and beaten curd, simmer 10-12 minutes. 5. Grind to smooth paste and set aside. 6. For masala: Dry roast green cardamom, black cardamom, mace, cloves, black peppercorns 2-3 minutes until aromatic. Grind to fine powder. 7. Sauté marinated chicken in oil for 5-6 minutes on both sides. Add butter and cook 1 more minute. 8. For smoking: Place chicken in bowl, add hot coal to steel bowl with cloves and ghee. Cover and let smoke. 9. In handi, melt butter, add degi chilli powder, water, simmer 2 minutes. 10. Add prepared gravy, water, prepared masala, butter. Simmer until cooked. 11. Add kewra water, salt, sugar, sautéed chicken. Cook until tender. 12. Finish with kasoori methi, fresh cream, butter. Mix well. 13. Garnish with coriander sprig, kasoori methi, fresh cream. 14. Serve hot with naan and salad.

name: Lauki Aur Tinde Ka Bharta
instructions: Stuff 1 bottle gourd with garlic clove, brush with oil and roast until charred and cooked. Peel skin, chop well and set aside. Roast round gourd, onions and tomatoes until charred. Peel, chop and set aside. Grind 5-6 garlic cloves, 1 inch ginger, 3 green chillies and salt into coarse paste with mortar pestle. Heat 2-3 tbsp mustard oil until smoky, add 2-4 fenugreek seeds and 1 tsp cumin seeds, add ginger-garlic paste and cook 1 minute. Add 1 tsp turmeric powder, 1.5 tsp chili powder and all roasted vegetables. Saute 7-8 minutes on medium heat. Finish with salt and 1 tbsp fresh coriander leaves. Garnish with fried onions, green chillies slices, julienned ginger and coriander sprig. Serve hot with roti.

name: Chilli Potato
ingredients: 5 large frying potatoes peeled and diced
3 tbsp oil for coating and frying
instructions: Boil diced potatoes in water with salt and 2 tbsp vinegar for 7-8 minutes until half cooked. Drain and cool. Coat boiled potatoes with 0.33 cup cornstarch mixed with salt and little water. Deep fry coated potatoes in oil until golden and set aside on absorbent sheet. For chilli potato gravy: heat 2 tbsp oil, add 4-5 dry kashmiri red chillies deseeded, 0.5 inch chopped ginger, 1 green chili, and 2 medium diced onions, toss on high heat for 1 minute. Mix sauce: 2 tsp soy sauce, 2 tsp red chili sauce, 1 tsp green chili sauce, 2 tsp vinegar, 1.5 tbsp tomato ketchup. Add sauce mixture to wok, add 2 large diced capsicums and 0.25 cup spring onion bulbs, toss on high heat. Add fried potatoes and 1.5 tsp chili oil, mix well. For gravy version: take remaining chilli potato, add 2 fresh red chillies chopped, 0.5 large capsicum diced, 1 tsp chili oil and 0.33 cup water, bring to boil. Add cornstarch slurry and 2 tbsp onion diced, cook 1 minute on high heat. Garnish with spring onion and coriander sprig. Serve hot.

name: Dhaba Style Keema
ingredients: 1 heaped tsp degi red chili powder
instructions: Make marination: combine 1 kg mutton mince with bones, 2 cups curd, 2 tbsp ginger garlic paste, mint leaves, 2 tbsp coriander leaves, 1 tsp turmeric powder, 1 heaped tsp chili powder, 2 tbsp coriander powder, salt and 2 tbsp ghee. Mix well and marinate. Pressure cook: heat 3-4 tbsp mustard oil until smoky, add 1 bay leaf, 1 inch cinnamon stick, marinated mutton and mix. Cover and pressure cook for 6-7 whistles until cooked. Let depressurize naturally. For curry: heat 2 tbsp mustard oil and 2 tbsp oil until smoky, add 2-4 cloves, 2-3 dry red chillies and 1 tsp cumin seeds to splutter. Add 3 large chopped onions and saute 2-3 minutes until translucent. Add 1 heaped tbsp ginger garlic paste and saute briefly. Add 1 heaped tbsp tender coriander stems, 3-4 green chillies and 2 cups fresh tomato puree, cook 1 minute. Add 1.5 tbsp coriander powder and 1 tsp chili powder, cook until oil separates. Add cooked keema and 0.5 cup water to adjust consistency. Cover and cook 7-8 minutes on low heat. Finish with 1.5 tsp crushed fenugreek leaves and 2 tbsp coriander leaves, mix well. Garnish with coriander sprig, boiled egg slices and lemon wedge. Serve hot with pav.

name: Anda Malai Makhani
instructions: Boil melon seeds and cashews separately. In a handi, heat ghee and cook sliced onions until translucent (2 min). Add ginger-garlic paste (1 min). Add boiled melon seeds, cashews, bay leaf, cinnamon, yogurt and cook 7-8 min until oil separates. Add green chilli and water, bring to boil. Add warm water, cover and cook 7-8 min more. Grind gravy into smooth paste and set aside. Heat ghee in pan, add kashmiri chillies, tomato, capsicum, onions, salt and toss 2-3 min. Dry roast black peppercorns, cumin, coriander, cloves, salt until aromatic. Add fenugreek leaves, roast 1 min. Grind into powder. In a pan with oil, add cumin seeds and hard boiled eggs, cook 1 min. Add water, prepared gravy, salt. Cook 4-5 min. Add butter and sauteed vegetables, boil. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with masala powder, fresh cream, chilli oil, and coriander.

name: Chicken Chapli Kabab
instructions: Mix chicken mince with coriander stems, onions, tomato, green chillies, ginger, coriander leaves, egg, salt, boiled eggs and gram flour. Wet hands with water, take portion of mixture, place tomato slice on one side and shape into kababs. Heat oil in pan and cook kababs 4-5 min until golden on both sides. Transfer to serving platter and garnish with coriander and pomegranate pearls. Serve hot with onion salad and green chutney. For onion salad: mix sliced onion with roasted cumin, salt, lemon juice and degi chilli powder. For green chutney: blend onion, green chillies, coriander leaves, mint, tamarind pulp, salt, degi chilli powder and ginger into smooth paste.

name: Kacche Aam Wale Aloo
instructions: In mortar pestle, grind green chillies, ginger and salt into coarse paste. In a handi, heat mustard oil until smoky, add fenugreek seeds, fennel, cumin, nigella, mustard seeds and prepared ginger-green chilli paste. Saute 1 min. Add peeled and diced potatoes, mix well, cover with lid and cook 7-8 min on medium heat. Prepare curd mixture: mix water, yogurt, turmeric, degi red chilli powder, coriander powder and asafoetida. Add curd mixture to potatoes and cook until oil separates from masala. Add peeled and chopped raw mangoes, water and jaggery. Simmer 5-6 min more. Finish with salt and additional jaggery. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with diagonally cut green chillies, julienned ginger and coriander leaves. Serve hot with roti.

name: Easy Chicken Masala
instructions: Grind onion, coriander leaves, coriander stems, green chillies, ginger and garlic into smooth paste. In pressure cooker, heat mustard oil until smoky, remove from flames. Add cloves and splash of water to remove impurities. Add sliced onions and saute until golden. Add chicken legs, salt, turmeric, degi red chilli powder and little water. Saute 7-8 min. Add prepared paste and tomatoes, cook 2-3 min until oil separates from masala. Add water to adjust consistency and bring to boil. Cover with lid and cook 2-3 whistles until chicken is cooked well. Let depressurize. Finish with ghee and simmer until gravy thickens. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with coriander leaves. For chutney: roast onion, tomato and green chillies on gas range 4-5 min until charred, deskin vegetables and grind into coarse paste. Serve hot with roti, steamed rice and chutney.

name: Chettinad Chicken Curry
instructions: Roast black peppercorns, cloves, coriander, cumin, black cardamom, green cardamom, cinnamon, stone flower and kashmiri chillies until aromatic. Set aside. Roast fresh coconut on low-medium heat 5-6 min until golden. Transfer spices and coconut to cool, then grind into smooth paste. Marinate chicken legs with salt, turmeric and degi red chilli powder. Heat oil in earthen pot, add curry leaves, bay leaf, fennel seeds and let splutter. Add shallots and coriander stems, saute until golden. Add tomatoes and salt, cook 2-3 min. Add marinated chicken, cover and cook 5-6 min on medium heat. Add prepared spice paste, cook until oil separates from masala. Add hot water and simmer 4-5 min. Finish with sugar and tadka (oil tempering with fried shallots and curry leaves). Transfer to serving dish and garnish with tadka. Serve hot with malabar parotta.

name: Paneer Kolhapuri
instructions: Roast 2 large onions on gas range 2-3 min until roasted. Trim edges and skin, scrape off burnt parts. For vatan masala: dry roast cumin, coriander, black cardamom, green cardamom, star anise and poppy seeds 1 min. Add dry coconut, cashews, salt and bedgi chillies, roast until aromatic. Transfer to bowl with roasted onions. Grind spice mixture and onions into smooth paste. Grind tomatoes into smooth puree. Fry paneer cubes in oil until golden and set aside. Heat oil in handi, add bay leaf and chopped onion, saute until translucent. Add prepared vatan paste and saute until oil separates from masala. Add tomato puree and coriander stems, cook 7-8 min. Add water and jaggery, bring to boil. Add water to adjust consistency, fried paneer pieces and simmer 5-6 min on medium heat. Finish with coriander leaves, diced capsicum and diced onion. Season with salt and mix well. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with coriander leaves. Serve hot with roti or bhakri.

name: Thecha Chicken
instructions: Marinate chicken legs with salt and lemon juice. Dry roast green chillies and garlic for 4-5 minutes. Grind with roasted peanuts and coriander into a coarse paste to make thecha. Heat oil in a handi, add cumin seeds, cloves, bay leaf and sautéed onions until translucent. Add marinated chicken and prepared thecha, cook for 12-15 minutes on medium flame. Add beaten curd, salt and water, cook for another 5 minutes. Finish with fresh coriander leaves. Fry some garlic and green chillies separately in oil and use as garnish. Serve hot with roti or bhakri.

name: Chicken Tawa Fry
instructions: Grind garlic, ginger, green chilli and fresh coriander with water into a fine green paste. Cut chicken breast into flat slices and marinate with salt, turmeric powder and green paste. Heat a tawa over high heat, add oil and get it hot. Add chopped onions and cook over medium-high flame until golden brown. Add remaining green paste and garlic cloves, continue cooking until onions turn golden brown. Add powdered spices and a splash of hot water, cook for 1-2 minutes on high flame. Add chopped tomato and salt, cook until tomatoes turn mushy. Add kokum agal and place marinated chicken pieces evenly on the tawa. Cook for 2-3 minutes then flip and cook for another 2-3 minutes until chicken is cooked through. Coat the chicken nicely with the masala, add ghee and fresh coriander. Serve with pav or bhakri.

name: Bharwan Bhindi
instructions: Wash and pat dry ladyfingers, place below fan for 4-5 minutes to dry completely. Cut tops and bottoms, make a center slit for stuffing. Toast gram flour over low flame for 3-4 minutes, transfer to bowl. Add carom seeds, fennel seeds, spices, salt and 1 tsp mustard oil, mix well until binding forms. Fill ladyfingers with filling inside and outside. Heat 2 tbsp mustard oil in kadhai until smoking, reduce flame. Add cumin seeds, carom seeds, asafoetida, stuffed bhindi and stir. Add turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder and salt. Cover and cook over low flame until bhindi is tender, stirring every 2-3 minutes. Once cooked, uncover and set flame to high for 2-3 minutes to evaporate excess moisture. Add fresh coriander and lemon juice, stir well. Serve hot.

name: Cheese Burst Garlic Bread
instructions: Make garlic butter: mix softened butter with chopped garlic, coriander, and green chilli. For cheese sauce: set double boiler with boiling water, add processed cheese, butter, and milk, whisk for 5 minutes until smooth and creamy, cool down. Cut bread into 1.5 inch thick pieces, make small cavity in center. Apply garlic butter to bread, add spoonful of cheese sauce in cavity, top with mozzarella, green chillies, oregano and red chilli flakes. To cook on tawa: heat moderately hot tawa, add garlic butter, place garlic bread, lower flame, cover with lid until light golden brown on bottom. Open lid, add water splash, cover again immediately, cook until cheese melts. For oven method: preheat to 200°C, spread garlic butter on baking tray, place breads, bake for 6-7 minutes until cheese melts. Serve with ketchup.

name: Tari Waale Chane
instructions: Soak black chickpeas in water for 6-7 hours or overnight. Chop onion, garlic, ginger and green chilli roughly. Chop tomatoes similarly. Drain soaked chickpeas and add to pressure cooker with onion mixture and tomatoes. Add powdered spices and enough water to create thin gravy (about 2 inches above chickpeas). Pressure cook over medium-high flame for 3 whistles, then reduce to low and cook for 10 minutes. Turn off flame and let depressurize naturally. Open lid and stir, check if chickpeas are cooked, cook 5-10 minutes more if needed. Strain cooked chickpeas, reserve stock. Heat ghee in cooker or stock pot over high flame. Add asafoetida and drained chickpeas, stir and cook over high flame for 4-5 minutes until onions and tomatoes are homogenous. Add reserved stock and stir well, adjust consistency with hot water as needed for thin gravy. Bring to boil and simmer for 4-5 minutes. Taste and adjust salt, add garam masala, roasted kasuri methi powder and fresh coriander. Serve hot with rotis and steamed rice.

name: Mango Pulissery
instructions: Peel and cut mangoes into large pieces, transfer to stock pot with pit. Add curry leaves, green chilli, jaggery, salt, red chilli powder, turmeric powder and water (level just below mangoes). Bring to simmer, cover and cook over medium-low flame for 7-8 minutes until mango pieces turn slightly soft (not mushy). Meanwhile, grind fresh coconut and cumin seeds in mixer with water to make thick fine paste. Once mangoes are soft, add coconut paste and stir well to mix. Cook for 3-4 minutes. In separate bowl, whisk curd. Set flame to low or off, add whisked curd and stir well. For tempering: heat small pan over high flame, add coconut oil until hot. Add mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds, red chillies, curry leaves and asafoetida, immediately pour into curry and cover for one minute. Open lid and stir well to mix tempering. Adjust consistency with hot water if needed. Serve hot with rice.

name: Bengali Tok Dal
instructions: Wash dal and add to pressure cooker with water (1 inch above dal), green chilli and salt. Pressure cook over high flame for only one whistle. Turn off flame and immediately depressurize by lifting whistle with spoon. Open lid and whisk dal lightly with whisk or spatula (some texture should remain, not smooth). For tempering: heat kadhai over high flame, add mustard oil until it reaches smoking point, then lower flame to cool slightly. Add all whole spices (mustard seeds, radhuni, kalonji, fennel seeds, fenugreek seeds, cumin seeds, red chilli), raw mango slices and turmeric powder. Stir well over high flame for 1-2 minutes, cover and cook over low flame for 1 minute. Once raw mango turns soft, mash 1-2 pieces. Add boiled dal, hot water to adjust consistency and sugar. Stir well and cook for 2-3 minutes. Adjust consistency as preferred. Taste and adjust salt if needed. Serve hot with rice.

name: Bisi Bele Bath
instructions: For Bisi Bele Bath Powder: Heat pan over high flame, reduce to low. Dry roast chana dal and urad dal while stirring until golden and fragrant, transfer to bowl. Dry roast coriander seeds until golden brown, transfer. Dry roast poppy seeds until golden, transfer. Dry roast remaining whole spices (fenugreek, peppercorns, bay leaf, cinnamon, marathi moggu) until fragrant, transfer. Dry roast coconut until golden brown, transfer. Add oil to pan, roast red chillies for few minutes, transfer. Add asafoetida directly to bowl. Cool mixture to room temperature and grind into fine powder. Store in airtight container. For Bisibelebath: Wash rice and dal together until water runs clear, add to pressure cooker with all vegetables, water, turmeric powder and oil. Pressure cook over medium-high flame for 4 whistles, then switch off and let depressurize naturally. Check if rice is cooked, cook 1 more whistle if needed. Heat ghee in large stock pot over high flame. Fry cashews and peanuts until golden brown, remove to bowl. Add mustard seeds, red chilli, curry leaves and asafoetida in ghee, let mustard seeds crackle. Add tomato, peas and salt, stir and cook until tomatoes turn mushy. Add capsicum, cook over high flame for 1 minute. Add tamarind water (strained through sieve), mash pulp, add jaggery and stir. Add cooked rice mixture, hot water and keep slightly watery. Dissolve bisi bele bath powder in water separately and add to stock pot, stir well. Adjust consistency as needed. Taste and adjust salt. Add fried nuts and fresh coriander. Serve hot with khara boondi, cucumber raita, papad and pickle.

name: Sindhi Koki
instructions: In a large bowl, add wheat flour and ghee, mix well by rubbing between hands to incorporate shortening. Add carom seeds, anardana powder, green chilli, onion, cumin seeds, black pepper, and coriander. Gently knead dough with water in batches to form a stiff, uneven dough. Divide into 6 portions and form rough roundels. Cover with towel. Heat tawa on high. Take dough ball, press gently and roll into thick paratha about 1 cm thick. Make incisions with a floured knife. Place on hot tawa over low flame until golden brown spots appear on one side, flip and repeat. Once brown on both sides, add ghee and cook until crisp on both sides.

name: Egg Dum Biryani
instructions: Wash rice 2-3 times and soak. Dice onions, green chilli, ginger and add to chopper with garlic cloves. Heat oil in kadhai and add chopped onion mixture, cook until golden brown (10-12 minutes). Add grated tomato and salt, cook 1-2 minutes. Lower flame, whisk curd and add with biryani masala, cook on low while stirring for 1 minute, then high for 2-3 minutes until oil separates. Add boiled eggs and adjust with hot water to semi-thick consistency. Add coriander and mint. Boil water separately with salt and whole spices. Add soaked rice and cook 4-5 minutes until partially cooked. Strain rice and spread over egg base. Add coriander, mint and ghee on top. Cover kadhai lid with wet towel and cook on low heat for 10 minutes. Switch off and rest for 10-15 minutes. Serve with flat spatula.

name: Aam Ka Murabba
instructions: Cut raw mango into medium pieces. Boil water in stock pot. Add chopped mango with salt and turmeric, boil for 3-4 minutes until slightly soft (do not make mushy). Strain and reserve water. In kadhai, add sugar and water (level with sugar surface) and cook over high flame until sugar dissolves. Continue cooking over medium-high heat until one-string consistency is reached. Check by cooling a few drops on a plate and stretching between thumb and finger. Add boiled mango, lemon juice, whole spices and saffron to syrup. As mangoes release moisture, the syrup will become watery. Cook over medium-high again until one-string consistency is reached. Add chopped almonds and cashews, stir and switch off. Let cool completely. Transfer to sterilized glass jar when cooled.

name: Paneer Koliwada
instructions: To make chutney, add all ingredients to mixer grinder jar and grind into fine paste. Cut paneer into thick cubes. Grind green chilli, ginger, garlic with little water to make paste. In a large bowl, add oil and all powdered spices, carom seeds, ginger-garlic-chilli paste, lemon juice and salt. Mix using fork or whisk. Add curd and baking soda, mix and taste, adjust salt. Add gram flour and corn flour to make thick paste (not runny). Add paneer pieces and gently coat with batter. Heat oil to 170°C. Carefully drop coated paneer into hot oil and fry over medium-high flame until crisp. Remove from oil using sieve and immediately sprinkle chaat masala. Serve hot with chutney.

name: Dhaba Style Dal Fry Tadka
instructions: Add dal to bowl and wash 3-4 times until water runs clear. Soak for 20-25 minutes. While soaking, make birista: heat oil to 150°C and carefully add sliced onions. Fry over medium flame until light golden brown, remove with sieve and squeeze excess oil. Transfer to bowl and separate using forks, let cool. Discard water from soaked dal and add to pressure cooker with water, turmeric powder, ghee and ginger. Pressure cook on high for 1 whistle, then low for 2-3 more whistles. Switch off and let depressurize naturally. Open and whisk dal while hot, add hot water to adjust consistency. In separate kadhai, add ghee and heat. Add cumin seeds and let crackle. Add curry leaves, green chilli, ginger, asafoetida and cook. Add cooked dal on high flame so it sizzles. Mix with tempering, add salt and bring to boil. Discard any scum. Make second tempering in a pan: melt butter over medium flame, add cumin seeds, green chilli and birista. Add red chilli powder and pour over dal. Mix well.

name: Raw Mango Rasam
instructions: Place raw mango and green chillies on a roti grill over high flame. Char the green chillies until completely charred and remove to a bowl. Reduce flame to medium and char raw mango, rotating with tongs to cook evenly on all sides. Transfer charred mango to bowl and cover for 5-10 minutes to rest. Peel the charred mango and cut pulp, scraping the seed and peel to get maximum pulp. Transfer pulp and chillies to mixer grinder with water and grind into fine puree. In a stock pot, add groundnut oil and heat. Once hot, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, red chilli, curry leaves, asafoetida and turmeric powder. Stir immediately then strain and add mango puree. Stir well. Add hot water to adjust to watery consistency. Simmer and reduce for 8-10 minutes. Taste and adjust salt. Add jaggery to balance flavors. Add fresh coriander and stir. Serve hot.

name: Punjabi Dum Aloo
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 2 tbsp
instructions: Peel and rinse potatoes, poke with fork. Heat ghee and oil in cooker, fry potatoes until light golden, remove. Add whole spices to ghee, then add chopped onions and cook until golden. Add ginger-garlic-chilli paste and cook until onions turn golden. Lower flame, add powdered spices and hot water, cook briefly. Add tomato puree and salt, cook 1-2 minutes. Whisk in curd and cook 2-3 minutes until oil separates. Add fried potatoes, coat with masala, add hot water for consistency. Pressure cook on high for 1 whistle, then low flame for 7-8 minutes. Let depressurize naturally. Adjust consistency by adding water or cooking down. Taste and adjust salt, add garam masala, kasuri methi, and fresh coriander.

name: Gobi 65
ingredients: black pepper powder 1 tsp freshly crushed
green chilli 3-4 nos sliced for tempering
instructions: Cut cauliflower into bite-sized florets and rinse with salted water for 3-4 minutes, discard salt water and rinse with fresh water. Grind ginger, garlic, green chilli with water to make paste. Mix rice flour, corn flour, powdered spices, salt, green paste, curry leaves, curd in a bowl using whisk. Gradually add water to make semi-thin batter, add hot oil and mix. Coat cauliflower pieces in batter. Fry in 170°C oil over medium flame until light golden, transfer to sieve and rest. Heat oil further, flash fry for 30 seconds until crispy. In a hot wok, add oil and let heat, add garlic, green chilli, curry leaves, cook 1-2 minutes over high flame. Add fried cauliflower and crushed black pepper, toss well.

name: Egg Triple Schezwan Fried Rice
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 1 tbsp
schezwan sauce 2 tbsp for fried rice
red chilli sauce 1 tbsp for fried rice
light soy sauce dash for fried rice
instructions: Dice carrot, capsicum, french beans. Chop spring onion keeping whites and greens separate. Roughly chop ginger, garlic, and green chilli. Heat wok, add oil and ginger-garlic-chilli paste, add half of spring onion whites and toss. Add half of chopped veggies and cook 2-3 minutes over high flame. Push veggies to side, add oil on empty side, pour whisked egg and scramble over high flame, stir with veggies. Add all sauces, stir, add rice with sugar, white pepper, salt and spring onion greens. Cook 2-3 minutes. For omelette: cook whisked salted eggs in hot oiled pan, pull edges with fork to cook evenly, flip and cook other side. Cool, roll, and cut into 2-3 cm strips. For gravy: heat wok with oil, add ginger-garlic-chilli paste and remaining spring onion whites, cook briefly. Add remaining veggies and cook 2-3 minutes. Add sauces, salt, pepper, sugar and cook 1-2 minutes. Add hot water and bring to boil. Make corn flour slurry, add slowly to boiling gravy while stirring. Adjust seasoning, add omelette strips and spring onion greens. Serve fried rice topped with gravy.

name: Eggless Churros
instructions: Mix caster sugar and cinnamon powder in a flat bowl using fork. Prepare piping bag with star nozzle inside another piping bag, place in tall glass. Ready parchment-lined tray. In stock pot, add water, butter, sugar and vanilla essence, stir well and heat on high flame. Once butter melts and water boils, turn off flame and add refined flour, stir well with spatula to form dough. Let rest 1-2 minutes until warm to touch, transfer to piping bag and push down, twist to seal. Pipe 4-5 inch churros onto tray and cut ends with knife. Fry in 165°C oil over medium flame until crispy and golden brown. Transfer to sieve to drain excess oil, rest 1-2 minutes and coat in cinnamon sugar. Optional: make cavity with straw and pipe chocolate spread or cream cheese. Serve warm with whipped cream and chocolate spread.

name: Sattu Ka Paratha
instructions: Add wheat flour, kalonji, carom seeds and salt in bowl, mix well. Add water gradually and mix with hands until dry flour combines. Knead into semi-soft dough, add oil and knead again until incorporated. Cover with damp cloth and rest 30 minutes. For filling: mix sattu with garlic, ginger, green chilli, carom seeds, kalonji, black pepper, lemon juice, salt and mango pickle masala. Add mustard oil and mix, then add onion and fresh coriander, mix until filling binds together. To shape: take dough portion, form into roundel and press slightly. Create bowl shape using thumbs and rotating. Fill generously with sattu filling and pinch edges together to seal. Dust with wheat flour and roll into semi-thin paratha. Heat tawa well, add ghee and wipe with napkin. Place paratha on tawa and press while rotating, cook both sides over high flame. Apply ghee and cook briefly over medium-low flame until light golden brown. Serve hot with yogurt, chutney or chokha.

name: Thandai Malai Kulfi
instructions: Add all thandai masala ingredients (almonds, pistachios, peppercorns, fennel seeds, cardamom, melon seeds, poppy seeds, saffron, rose petals, ginger powder, nutmeg) to mixer grinder and grind into powder using pulse mode, do not over-grind or nuts will release oils. Set heavy-bottomed kadhai over high flame and add 2 litres full fat milk. Bring to boil while stirring regularly. Once boiling, reduce to medium flame and simmer until milk reduces to half its quantity (approximately 45 minutes). After simmering 15 minutes, add thandai masala and stir, continue cooking for 15 more minutes ensuring no lumps remain. Add sugar and stir, continue cooking for 10 more minutes until milk thickens and turns grainy but remains smoothly flowing. Turn off flame and transfer to bowl, let cool completely. Pour cooled base into kulfi moulds, cover with foil, make slit with knife and insert ice cream stick. Alternatively use paper cups with toothpicks. Freeze for 4-5 hours or overnight. To serve from moulds: dip in room temperature water and roll gently in palms 2-3 times then pull out. From cups: cut with scissors and quarter the kulfi. Garnish with pistachio powder and dry rose petals.

name: Chicken Butter Masala
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 1 tbsp
instructions: Cut chicken into thin slices and marinate with salt, lemon, ginger-garlic paste, yogurt, turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder and garam masala for 1-2 hours or use directly. Sear chicken pieces in hot oil on both sides until partially cooked, set aside. Grind tomatoes, garlic, ginger, green chilli with coriander stems and soaked cashews into fine puree. Heat butter and oil in pan, add cumin seeds, cinnamon, green cardamom, chopped onion mixture and cook until golden brown. Add turmeric and kashmiri chilli powder with splash of water. Add tomato puree and salt, cook on medium-high heat 10-12 minutes until gravy thickens and separates from oil. Add hot water to adjust consistency to semi-thick, add cooked chicken and cook 4-5 minutes more. Finish with fresh cream, butter, kasuri methi, garam masala and fresh coriander.

name: Veg Soya Matar Pulao
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 1 tbsp
instructions: Boil soya chunks in salted water for 5-6 minutes, then rinse thoroughly in cool water to remove smell and squeeze dry. Soak basmati rice in water and drain. Heat ghee in pressure cooker, add whole spices with sliced onions and cook until onions turn light golden. Add ginger-garlic-chilli paste, cook 1 minute. Add chopped tomato with salt and cook until mushy. Mix yogurt with turmeric, kashmiri chilli, spicy chilli, coriander powder and biryani masala, whisk well. Add whisked yogurt to cooker, stir continuously 1 minute. Add green peas, boiled soya chunks, fresh coriander and mint. Add soaked rice with water and kasuri methi, stir well. Pressure cook for 1 whistle on high heat, then switch off and let depressurize naturally. Rest 10-15 minutes before opening and fluffing with spatula.

name: Veg Kheema Masala
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 2 tbsp
instructions: Boil cauliflower florets and green peas in salted turmeric water for 3-4 minutes, drain and cool. Transfer to chopper and chop coarsely. Add chopped carrot, capsicum and french beans, chop coarsely as well. Heat ghee in deep pan, add cumin seeds and chopped onions, cook on high heat until light golden. Add ginger-garlic-chilli paste and continue cooking until onions turn golden. Lower flame and add all powdered spices with splash of hot water to prevent burning. Add tomato puree and salt, cook until ghee separates. Add optional cashew paste and cook similarly until ghee separates. Add chopped vegetables and mix well, add hot water to adjust consistency to semi-thick. Cook vegetables in gravy 7-8 minutes. Taste and adjust salt, add kasuri methi, ginger julienne, green chilli and fresh coriander. Serve hot.

name: Soy Basil Paneer Chilli
instructions: Lightly coat paneer pieces with cornflour by tossing in a sieve. Deep fry paneer in oil heated to 170°C until light golden brown in batches, transfer to sieve and drain. Heat oil in wok over high flame, add ginger, garlic, green chilli and spring onion whites, saute for few seconds. Add capsicum, red pepper and onion petals, toss over high heat for few seconds. Add sugar, dark soy sauce, light soy sauce, white pepper, vinegar, salt and hot water to make sauce, stir and bring to simmer. Mix cornflour with water to make slurry, add to simmering sauce while stirring to thicken. Lower flame, add fried paneer and basil leaves torn by hand, coat paneer with sauce without overcooking. Finish with spring onion greens and serve hot.

name: Chicken Satay
instructions: Grind kashmiri and spicy red chillies, lemongrass, galangal, shallots and garlic with water into fine thick red paste. Cut chicken into thin bite-sized pieces. Mix marinade: salt, coriander powder, turmeric, cumin powder, white pepper, light brown sugar, light soy sauce, 1 tbsp red paste and 4 tbsp coconut milk. Marinate chicken 1-2 hours or use directly. For peanut sauce: roast peanuts in dry pan over medium heat until golden, cool and coarsely chop. Heat remaining coconut milk, cook until it thickens. Add remaining red paste, cook until it turns crumbly and separates from pan. Add remaining coconut milk, light soy sauce, tamarind pulp, light brown sugar and salt, bring to simmer. Add roasted peanuts, cook 1-2 minutes, adjust seasoning. To cook chicken: skewer on bamboo sticks or grill directly. Heat oil in pan or on grill until hot. Place skewers and cook over medium-high heat 4-5 minutes per side for thighs, 3-4 minutes for breast until golden and slightly charred. Serve hot with peanut sauce.

name: Mutton Tawa Bhuna Masala
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 4 tbsp
instructions: Wash and cut boneless mutton into small bite-sized pieces. Grind tomato and soaked cashews with water into puree. Set pressure cooker over high heat with oil, add cumin seeds and chopped onions, cook until light golden. Add ginger-garlic-chilli paste and saute briefly. Lower flame, add fresh coriander stems and all powdered spices with splash of hot water. Increase to high heat and cook until oil separates (2-3 minutes). Add tomato-cashew puree and whisked yogurt with salt, stir well and cook on high 5-6 minutes until oil separates. Add mutton pieces, stir with masala and cook 7-8 minutes on high heat. Add hot water to barely reach level of mutton without fully submerging. Pressure cook 1 whistle on high, then medium-low for 10 minutes depending on mutton quality. Switch off and let depressurize naturally. Check if cooked; if underdone cook a few more minutes until semi-thick consistency. Transfer to tawa or continue in cooker. Heat ghee over high flame, add garlic, green chilli and ginger, saute 1-2 minutes. Add mutton and cook until gravy reduces to thick coating. Adjust seasoning, add garam masala, kasuri methi, black pepper and fresh coriander. Serve with rumali roti or pav.

name: Karela Aloo Ki Sabzi
instructions: Wash bitter gourds and remove scales with peeler or knife without removing all skin. Use smaller gourds; for larger ones cut in half. Check seeds inside with a knife - should not be orange or red. Add to bowl with salt, rubbing salt inside slits, rest for 1 hour. After resting, transfer gourds to sieve and wash well under tap water. Squeeze and remove all moisture, optionally wipe with clean kitchen towel. Cut potatoes into thick wedges and onions into thick slices. Heat mustard oil in kadhai until hot. Add bitter gourds and fry over medium-high heat until golden brown, stirring at intervals. Add potatoes and onions with salt, stir and cook on high flame 2-3 minutes. Lower flame and continue cooking until onions caramelize and potatoes are fully cooked (15-20 minutes). Stir occasionally to prevent burning. Once onions are caramelized and potatoes cooked, add all powdered spices and stir well. Switch off flame and cover with lid for 2-3 minutes. Remove lid and stir again. Serve hot.

name: Veg Creamy Pasta in Broccoli Sauce
instructions: Boil water in stock pot and season with large pinch of salt. Add broccoli florets, almonds and garlic, boil until broccoli becomes soft. Strain and transfer to bowl to cool. Once cooled, add to mixer grinder with some cooking water and ice cube, grind into fine puree without making it too thin. In same stock pot of boiling water add pasta, sweet corn, 1 tsp oil and salt, boil 8-10 minutes until cooked. Test by biting between teeth. Strain pasta and corn, transfer to bowl and add oil so pasta doesn't stick. Reserve pasta water. In heated pan add 1 tsp olive oil and let heat. Add mushroom and bell pepper, toss over high flame 1 minute and transfer to bowl. Add more olive oil to same pan with chopped garlic, oregano and red chilli flakes, stir and cook garlic 30 seconds. Add broccoli puree and stir well. Add salt, sugar and black pepper, cook 1-2 minutes. Add boiled pasta and corn, stir well and add pasta water to adjust consistency. Cook 1-2 minutes more. Taste and adjust salt, add lemon juice and grated nutmeg. Serve hot.

name: Gujarati Rasse Waale Aloo
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 1 tbsp
instructions: Peel and dice potatoes, tomatoes and onions. Heat oil in pressure cooker, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, asafoetida and onions, cook until translucent. Add ginger-garlic-chilli paste and tomatoes, cook for 1 minute. Add diced potatoes, salt, turmeric and spice powders, cook for 2-3 minutes. Add water (equal to potato level), sugar and pressure cook on high for 3 whistles. Let depressurize naturally, open lid, adjust consistency and salt. Finish with garam masala and fresh coriander.

name: Creamy Mushroom with Lemon Coriander Rice
instructions: For rice: Heat butter and oil in wok, add oregano, chilli flakes, garlic and onion, cook until golden. Add coriander and cooked rice, add lemon zest and juice, salt, and cook for 3-4 minutes. For mushroom: Heat olive oil, add garlic and green chilli, cook briefly. Add mushrooms and salt, cook over high heat until golden brown and moisture evaporates. Transfer mushrooms to bowl. In same pan, add butter and melt, stir in refined flour until sandy. Add milk in batches while whisking to avoid lumps. Add cooked mushrooms, oregano, chilli flakes and black pepper, cook for 1-2 minutes. Serve mushroom alongside rice.

name: Instant Vrat Ka Dosa
instructions: Dry roast sabudana over low-medium heat for 2 minutes, cool completely. Transfer to mixer grinder with sama rice, diced potato, green chilli, ginger, curd, sendha namak and water. Grind into fine batter, transfer to bowl and cover to rest. After resting, adjust consistency with water if thickened. Add cumin seeds, black pepper and coriander, stir well. Heat dosa tawa on high heat until very hot. Pour batter and spread to cover surface - it will form net-like structure. Lower flame, add ghee on surface, cook until edges turn golden brown. Peel off and fold. Serve hot.

name: Chicken Cheese Chutney Kabab
instructions: Place onion on muslin cloth with salt, squeeze out moisture after 1-2 minutes. Grind chicken breast into fine mince. Mix mince with ginger, garlic, green chilli, coriander, mint, salt, black salt, amchur, spice powders, crushed pepper and lemon juice. Knead slightly and refrigerate. Grind chutney ingredients into fine paste. Place plastic sheet, add spoonful of chicken mixture, flatten into sheet. Place cheese slice and spread chutney. Fold sides and roll into log, seal ends. Coat egg in flat bowl and panko breadcrumbs in another bowl. Transfer kabab through egg then breadcrumbs, ensuring full coating. Place on plate and shape remaining mixture. Freeze for 10-15 minutes. Fry in moderately hot oil (170°C) until crisp and golden brown, keeping high flame initially then medium. Transfer to sieve to drain oil.

name: Smoked Creamy Paneer Roll
instructions: Heat wok over high heat, add butter and oil. Add diced paneer, salt and pepper, toss for 2-3 minutes and transfer to bowl. Grind sugar and mustard seeds into fine powder. In bowl, whisk mayonnaise, curd, salt, pepper, vinegar, grated garlic and sugar-mustard powder. Add tossed paneer and mix well. Place bowl with small dish inside holding live charcoal, add spoonful of ghee, cover with lid and let smoke for 3-4 minutes. Remove charcoal carefully and mix gently. For birista: Heat oil in deep pan, add sliced onions and stir well. Fry until light golden brown with frequent stirring, adjusting flame to avoid burning. Remove with spider and squeeze excess oil on spatula. Spread on tissue paper and separate with forks, dab with tissue to remove remaining oil. To assemble: Cut hot dog bun open, apply soft butter, layer with lettuce, smoked paneer filling, crispy birista and spring onion greens.

name: Moong Dal Malpua
instructions: Rinse moong dal and soak for 1 hour. Drain water. Add soaked dal to blender with 2 tbsp milk and grind to coarse paste. Add more milk if needed. Transfer to bowl.
