Add semolina, 2 tbsp sugar and 3 crushed cardamom pods, mix well. Gradually add 1 cup milk while stirring to achieve pouring consistency. In separate saucepan, combine 1.5 cups sugar with 1.5 cups water and heat over medium heat until sugar dissolves. Add 3-4 crushed cardamom pods and saffron strands, simmer for few minutes until slightly sticky, keep warm. Heat oil or ghee in frying pan over medium heat. Pour ladleful of batter to form round shape. Fry on medium-low heat until golden brown on both sides, flipping once for even cooking. Remove fried malpua and drain excess oil. Immediately transfer to warm sugar syrup and soak for 1-2 minutes per side. Remove from syrup and place on serving plate. Garnish with chopped cashews and pistachios. Serve warm.

name: Mawa Semiya Halwa
instructions: Chop cashews and almonds into pieces. Heat ghee in heavy-bottomed pan or wok over medium heat. Add roasted vermicelli and fry until golden and fragrant, stirring frequently. Add fox nuts and fry until they puff up slightly. Add chopped cashews, almonds, grated dry coconut and raisins, stir and cook for 1-2 minutes. Add mawa (condensed milk solids) and sugar, mix well, breaking up mawa into smaller pieces. Continue cooking over medium heat, stirring frequently to prevent sticking and burning, until halwa becomes thick and pulls away from sides of pan. Add saffron strands and crushed cardamom, stir well. Transfer to serving plate. Garnish with additional dry fruits if desired. Serve warm.

name: Malai Sandwich
instructions: Layer bread slices with malai or cream filling. Toast or serve as is. Two preparation methods shown in video.

name: Ginger Chilli Pickle
instructions: Roast and grind the spices (fennel, coriander, cumin, carom, fenugreek seeds). Chop green chilli and ginger. In mustard oil, add mustard seeds and roasted spice powder. Add chopped chilli and ginger. Season with turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, asafoetida, salt and vinegar. Mix well and store in a jar. Ready to serve as an instant pickle.

name: Aloo Bonda Vada Pav
instructions: Make semolina batter with curd, green chilli, ginger, cumin seeds, and baking soda. Prepare potato filling by tempering cumin in oil, adding spices and mashed potato. Make green chutney by grinding coriander, green chilli, amchur, cumin, asafoetida, salt and peanuts. Stuff potato filling into semolina batter balls. Deep fry until golden brown. Serve with chutney and pav/bread.

name: Drumstick Potato Curry
instructions: Cut drumsticks into pieces and peel potatoes. In oil, temper cumin seeds. Add turmeric powder and salt. Add chopped tomatoes with ginger and green chilli. Add coriander powder, asafoetida, and kashmiri chilli powder. Once tomatoes are cooked, add drumstick pieces and potato cubes. Cook covered until vegetables are tender. Finish with garam masala and kasuri methi leaves.

name: Coriander Panjiri
instructions: Dry roast coriander seeds until fragrant. Roast fox nuts in ghee until crispy. Toast almonds, cashews, and muskmelon seeds separately in ghee. Grind roasted coriander seeds to powder. Mix all roasted nuts, seeds, grated coconut, raisins, crushed cardamom, and white pepper. Add sugar powder and mix thoroughly. Shape into balls or halwa form.

name: Rice Coconut Burfi
instructions: In a pan with ghee, add rice flour and roast for 2-3 minutes. Slowly add milk while stirring to avoid lumps. Keep stirring constantly until mixture thickens and starts to leave the sides of the pan. Add sugar, dessicated coconut, and crushed cardamom. Mix well and cook for 2-3 more minutes. Pour onto a greased tray, flatten, and cut into pieces. Garnish with pistachio.

name: Chana Gur Ki Burfi
instructions: Roast fox nuts in ghee until crispy and set aside. Grind roasted chickpeas coarsely. In a pan, melt jaggery over low heat, stirring frequently. Once melted, add ground chickpeas and roasted fox nuts. Add crushed cardamom, nutmeg powder, and dry ginger powder. Mix in dessicated coconut. Stir well and cook for 2-3 minutes. Pour onto a greased tray or in a mold, press down firmly, and cut into pieces once cooled.

name: Jackfruit Pickle
instructions: Wash and dry jackfruit, cut into bite-sized pieces. Toast fennel, coriander, fenugreek, and carom seeds separately until fragrant. Crush mustard seeds coarsely. Combine all dry spices including black pepper, turmeric, salt, kashmiri chilli, asafoetida, and black cumin. Layer jackfruit pieces with salt and let sit for 2-3 hours. Mix spiced mixture with jackfruit. Heat mustard oil until warm, pour over jackfruit mixture. Add vinegar and mix well. Store in glass jar for shelf stability.

name: Aloo Paratha
instructions: Make a soft dough with wheat flour, water, salt, and oil. Rest for 15 minutes. Grate raw potatoes and mix with salt, red chilli, cumin, ginger, green chilli, and fenugreek leaves. Divide dough into 3 balls. Roll out each, place potato filling in center, seal edges, and roll gently into paratha. Cook on tawa with oil on both sides until golden and crispy. For raita, chop cucumber and mix with curd, salt, black pepper, and coriander. Heat oil with cumin and asafoetida, pour over raita.

name: Aloo Papad
instructions: Boil potatoes until completely cooked. Peel and mash finely to smooth consistency. Add salt, roasted cumin seeds, red chilli powder, and oil. Mix thoroughly until uniform. Spread mixture on clean cloth in thin layer (about 0.25 inch thickness). Let dry in sun for 3-4 days until completely hardened. Once fully dried and brittle, carefully peel from cloth and break into papad sheets. Store in airtight container. Fry in hot oil until crispy and golden just before serving.

name: Moong Dal Dhokle
instructions: Soak moong dal for 1-2 hours, then crush lightly. Mix wheat flour, soaked moong dal, curd, and salt in a bowl. Add turmeric powder, coriander powder, red chilli powder, grated ginger, ajwain, cumin seeds, green chilli, and fresh coriander leaves. Mix well to combine all spices. Just before steaming, add baking soda and mix gently. Grease a baking pan or dhokle mold with oil. Pour batter into mold. Place in steamer with water and steam for 40-45 minutes until cooked through and a skewer comes out clean. Cool for 10 minutes, turn out, and cut into pieces. Optional: temper with ghee and seasoning (oil, cumin, asafoetida) before serving.

name: Stuffed Parwal
instructions: Wash parwal and slice lengthwise. Gently scoop out the center slightly using a small spoon to create space for filling, leaving walls intact. Crush peanuts coarsely. Crush namkeen sev into coarse pieces. Mix crushed peanuts and sev with salt, turmeric, coriander powder, fennel powder, dry mango powder, kashmiri red chilli, and garam masala. Stuff each parwal piece generously with this mixture. Heat oil in a large pan or kadai. Add cumin seeds and let them crackle, then add asafoetida. Carefully place stuffed parwal pieces in the pan. Cover with lid and cook on medium heat for 12-15 minutes, stirring occasionally to ensure even cooking on all sides, until parwal is tender. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Moongre
instructions: Mix moong dhuli dal and gram flour together. Add baking soda. Prepare a thick batter by adding minimal water. Deep fry spoonfuls of the batter in hot oil until golden and crispy. These fritters can be stored for months and used later to make various dishes like chaat, desserts, vegetables, or kadhi.

name: Balushahi
instructions: Mix refined flour with baking powder. Rub in ghee until mixture resembles breadcrumbs. Knead gradually with water into soft dough. Rest dough 15-20 minutes. Prepare sugar syrup by heating 2 cups sugar with 1 cup water, add crushed cardamom and saffron strands. Pull and fold dough repeatedly, cut into pieces, shape into smooth balls, flatten with thumb indent in center. Fry gently in ghee on low heat until golden brown. Immediately soak in warm sugar syrup for 5-8 minutes. Garnish with pistachio flakes while still wet.

name: Moong Dal Pakodi Curry
instructions: Soak moong dal. Make pakodi mixture: combine soaked moong dal, tomatoes, green chilli, ginger, salt, red chilli, asafoetida, fresh coriander and spinach. Deep fry spoonfuls in oil until golden. For curry: heat oil, add cumin seeds, asafoetida, green chilli, ginger. Add turmeric, coriander powder, kashmiri red chilli powders. Mix in curd carefully. Add fried pakodis to curry. Simmer on low heat. Finish with garam masala and fresh coriander.

name: Paneer Yakhni
instructions: Heat ghee, add cumin seeds, bay leaf, cinnamon, black pepper, cloves, cardamom, black cardamom. Toast spices briefly. Add soaked cashews and green chilli. Cook until cashews soften. Grind this into a fine paste. Mix paste with curd and remaining spice powders. Cut paneer into cubes, add to yogurt-spice mixture. Simmer gently on low heat for 5-10 minutes without letting it boil. Season with salt and red chilli. Garnish with fresh coriander if desired.

name: Bun Parotta
instructions: Combine refined flour and baking powder in a bowl. Add salt and sugar powder. Gradually add milk while mixing. Knead with oil into a soft, slightly sticky dough. Do not over-knead. Cover and rest the dough for 15-20 minutes. Take portions and pull the dough lengthwise, then fold it back. Repeat pulling and folding 8-10 times to create layers. Roll out the folded dough into a flat parotta. Fry or cook on a hot griddle/skillet with oil or ghee until golden brown and crispy on both sides.

name: Sweet Lemon Pickle
instructions: Wash and dry lemons thoroughly. Cut each lemon into quarters. In a clean glass jar, layer the lemon pieces with salt. Mix all spices together: turmeric powder, fennel seeds, cumin seeds, carom seeds, black pepper, cloves, black cardamom, black salt, kashmiri chili powder, and asafoetida. Layer lemons and spice mixture alternately in the jar. Pour sugar powder over everything. Close the jar and shake well daily for 3-4 days until sugar dissolves and lemons release their juice. The pickle will be ready to eat after a few days. Store in a cool, dry place. It keeps for months or even years.

name: Peanut Aloo Vadi
instructions: Boil and mash potatoes. Mix gram flour with salt, turmeric powder, red chilli, and coriander powder. Heat 1 tbsp oil, add cumin seeds, green chilli, ginger, asafoetida. Mix this tempering with the gram flour mixture. Add mashed potatoes and crushed peanuts. Knead gently into a soft mixture. Shape into vadis (oval or round pieces). Deep fry in 4 tbsp oil until golden brown on all sides. For chutney: grind together fresh coriander, green chilli, ginger, roasted cumin powder, asafoetida, salt, black salt, lemon juice, and peanuts into a smooth paste. Serve hot vadis with the peanut-coriander chutney.

name: Cheese Coin
instructions: Mix refined flour, salt, baking powder, oregano, carom seeds, and chilli flakes in a bowl. Add diced cheese pieces. Rub in ghee with fingertips until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs. Add minimal water (just a few tablespoons) and gently knead into a stiff dough. Roll out the dough thin on a surface. Cut into circles or coin shapes using a glass or cutter. Deep fry in hot oil until golden brown and crispy on both sides. Drain on paper towels. Serve hot as a tea-time snack or appetizer.

name: Parwal ki Mithai
instructions: Clean and halve pointed gourds, remove seeds carefully. Prepare filling by mixing mawa, powdered sugar, crushed cardamom, almond and pistachio flakes. Stuff the gourd halves. Make sugar syrup with 450g sugar and water, add baking soda and stir. Place stuffed parrwals in boiling syrup. Cook on low-medium heat for 15-20 minutes until translucent and fully cooked. Remove and cool on paper towels.

name: Semiya Custard
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan, add vermicelli and roast until light golden. In another pan, warm milk and dissolve custard powder and sugar in it, stirring well. Add roasted vermicelli to the milk mixture. Cook on low heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally until custard thickens slightly. Add chopped cashews, almonds, pistachios, and raisins. Mix well. Serve warm or chilled in bowls, garnished with almond and pistachio flakes.

name: Mathura Peda
instructions: Pour milk in a heavy-bottomed pan. Heat on high flame, stirring frequently and scraping the sides as milk thickens and darkens. Continue until milk solids separate and turn light brown, about 30-40 minutes. Add sugar and cook for another 2-3 minutes. Cool slightly, then mix in ghee, coarsely ground cardamom, and powdered sugar until it becomes a soft moldable paste. Shape small portions into smooth round balls. Dust with powdered sugar. Cool completely and store in an airtight container.

name: Rice Potato Tikki
instructions: Peel and grate potatoes, squeeze out excess moisture. Mix grated potatoes with rice flour, salt, oil, ginger, green chilli, chilli flakes, cumin powder, and chopped coriander leaves. Form into compact oval or round patties, about 1/2-inch thick. Heat oil in a shallow pan on medium-high heat. Place tikkis carefully and shallow fry 3-4 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy. For chutney: grind coriander leaves, green chillies, ginger, yogurt, and salts into smooth paste. Serve tikkis hot with coriander chutney.

name: Rice Papad
instructions: Soak rice for 30 minutes, drain. Grind soaked rice with salt and chilli flakes to a smooth thick batter, adding minimal water. Dust a non-stick pan or griddle lightly with oil. Spread rice batter very thin and even, about 1-2mm thick, using the back of a spoon. Cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes until edges start to lift and the center is cooked. Carefully lift edges and flip. Cook the other side for 1-2 minutes until crispy. Transfer to paper towels to cool. Store in airtight container.

name: Dalmoth Namkeen
instructions: Soak moth beans overnight, drain thoroughly. Toss beans with baking soda and dry in sun or oven until completely dry. Mix gram flour, salt, turmeric, and oil to form a thick coating paste. Coat the dried moth beans with this paste, ensure all beans are covered. Add muskmelon seeds to the mixture. Heat oil for deep frying to 170°C. Fry coated beans in batches, stirring occasionally, for 8-10 minutes until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels. Once cooled, temper separately: heat 1 tsp ghee, add red chilli, garam masala, cumin powder, amchur, coriander, and asafoetida. Toss this tempering with cooled fried beans and black salt. Mix well and store in airtight container.

name: Mango Launji Pickle
instructions: Peel and cut raw mangoes into pieces. Heat mustard oil and temper with mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds, and whole red chillies. Add cut mangoes to the oil and mix well. Add all dry spice powders (turmeric, red chilli, fennel powder) and salt. Mix thoroughly and store in sterilized jars. The pickle will be ready within a few days and can last for up to a year.

name: Chana Peanut Sundal
instructions: Soak desi chana and peanuts for 4-6 hours. Pressure cook them together with salt until soft (about 5-6 whistles). Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds, let them crackle. Add red and green chillies, curry leaves, and asafoetida. Add the cooked chana and peanuts mixture. Mix in grated coconut and ginger. Season with salt and finish with fresh coriander and lemon juice. Serve warm.

name: Mango Fried Rice
instructions: First boil rice separately with salt and a little oil, then spread to cool. Heat 2-3 tbsp oil in a pan and add mustard seeds, let them crackle. Add urad dal and chana dal, fry until golden. Add peanuts and fry till light brown. Add asafoetida, red chillies, and curry leaves. Add grated raw mango and turmeric powder, stir well. Add the cooked rice and mix thoroughly. Season with salt. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Dal Bati Churma
instructions: For bati: Mix wheat flour, semolina, 1 tbsp salt, baking soda, and 2 tbsp ghee. Knead with water to a stiff dough. Divide into balls, stuff with a small piece of jaggery, seal, and bake at 200°C for 25-30 minutes until golden. For dal: Heat 2 tbsp ghee, add spices (cumin, pepper, cloves, cardamom, asafoetida), add tomato, ginger, chilli. Add all 5 dals and water, pressure cook until soft. Season with salt. For churma: Crumble baked batis, heat 7 tbsp ghee, add crumbled batis, fry until golden. Add brown sugar, nuts, and cardamom. Mix well. Serve dal hot with bati pieces and churma on the side.

name: Cajun Potato
instructions: Wash and soak potatoes for 15 minutes. Pressure cook potatoes with salt for 3 minutes, cool, and gently flatten with palm. Mix refined flour, rice flour, salt, chilli flakes, oregano, and black pepper with water to make a batter. Coat flattened potatoes in batter. Heat oil and deep fry potato wedges until golden brown and crispy, stirring occasionally. Drain on paper towels. In a bowl, mix mayonnaise, chilli flakes, oregano, black pepper, kashmiri chilli powder, red chilli sauce, salt, and milk to make dipping sauce. Serve fried cajun potatoes with sauce drizzled on top, garnished with chilli flakes and fresh coriander.

name: Eggless Baklava
ingredients: ghee 0.5 cup total (for dough and layering)
instructions: Make pastry dough: Mix refined flour, salt, and baking powder. Rub in 0.25 cup ghee until breadcrumb texture. Add milk gradually and knead into a smooth dough. Rest for 20 minutes. Make filling: Prepare a mixture of flour (0.5 cup refined + 0.5 cup corn flour) with chopped mixed dry fruits. Layer: Roll out a thin sheet of dough, place in greased baking pan. Brush with melted ghee, sprinkle dry fruit-flour mixture. Repeat layers, ending with dough on top. Brush top with remaining ghee. Mark into square pieces. Bake at 180°C for 30-40 minutes until golden. Prepare sugar syrup: Boil sugar with water and lemon juice until syrupy. Cool slightly. Pour warm syrup over hot baked baklava as soon as it comes out of the oven. Let it soak completely. Cut along marked lines and serve.

name: Badam Poori
instructions: Soak saffron strands in 0.25 cup warm water. Mix refined flour with crushed cardamom and saffron-soaked water. Add ghee and knead into a smooth dough. Let rest for 15-20 minutes. Prepare sugar syrup: Heat 1 cup water with 2 cups sugar until it reaches one-string consistency, add a few saffron strands. Keep warm. Roll out dough portions into thin, oval shapes (like poori). Heat oil in a deep pan. Carefully slide each poori into hot oil. As it rises and puffs, gently press it with the back of a spoon to ensure even puffing. Fry until light golden brown on both sides (about 1-2 minutes). Remove with a slotted spoon and immediately dip into warm sugar syrup, ensuring it soaks well. Place on a serving plate. Garnish with almond flakes and desiccated coconut. Serve at room temperature or slightly warm.

name: Hara Bhara Kabab
instructions: 1. Blanch spinach and peas in boiling salted water for 2 minutes, drain and cool in cold water. Squeeze dry and grind into paste. 2. Heat ghee, sauté cumin seeds, green chilli, ginger, and capsicum for 2-3 minutes. 3. Add spinach-peas mixture and dry on low flame. 4. Combine with mashed potatoes and add all spices, mint, coriander, and roasted gram flour. Mix well. 5. Shape into oval kababs and place half cashew on top. 6. Heat ghee in pan and fry kababs until golden brown and crisp on both sides. 7. Serve hot with dahi coriander chutney.

name: Vella Seedai
instructions: 1. Crush jaggery and mix with butter. 2. Add rice flour, urad dal flour, dessicated coconut, sesame seeds, and crushed cardamom. Mix well to form dough. 3. Heat oil in a deep pan to medium-high heat. 4. Shape mixture into small round balls carefully. 5. Slide balls into hot oil and fry until golden brown and crispy. 6. Remove with slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. 7. Serve at room temperature.

name: Gobhi ke Fara
instructions: 1. Mix flour, semolina, salt, and 1 tsp oil. Knead with water to make soft dough. Rest for 15 minutes. 2. Finely chop cauliflower and cook with 2 tsp oil over medium heat until tender. 3. Add ginger, green chilli, turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli, amchur, garam masala, kasuri methi, and salt to cauliflower. Mix well. 4. Divide dough into small balls. Flatten each, place cauliflower filling in center, and seal edges to form dumplings. 5. Heat 2-3 tbsp oil in pan. Add mustard seeds, cumin, curry leaves, asafoetida, and chilli flakes. 6. Place dumplings in pan and cook until light golden on all sides, turning occasionally. 7. Serve hot with chutney.

name: Chocolate Wontons
ingredients: nutralite activ crunchy peanut butter 2 tbsp
instructions: Layer wonton wrappers into cupcake mold. Brush with Doodhshakti butter and oil. Bake at 180C for 8-10 minutes until golden and crispy. Fill with chocolate spread and peanut butter mixture. Serve as a sweet, crunchy snack.

name: Baklava Rolls
ingredients: mixed nuts pistachios and walnuts 1 cup chopped
instructions: Layer phyllo sheets brushing each with melted butter. Sprinkle chopped nuts mixed with cinnamon on each layer. Roll tightly from one end, cut into 2-inch rolls. Arrange in baking pan, brush with remaining butter. Bake at 180C for 25-30 minutes until golden and crispy. Meanwhile, prepare syrup by dissolving sugar-free sweetener in water, add cinnamon. Pour warm syrup over hot baklava rolls immediately after baking. Cool completely before serving.

name: Corn Cheese Rawa Paniyaram
instructions: Mix rawa, yogurt, and milk to form a smooth batter. Add corn, grated cheese, ginger-chili paste, and salt. Stir in Nutralite Cheesy Garlic Mayo and Nutralite Premium Table Spread. Heat oil in a paniyaram mold or non-stick pan with round cavities. Pour prepared batter into each cavity until full. Fry on medium heat until the bottom is golden and crisp. Flip and fry the other side until golden. The paniyaram should be crisp on the outside and soft on the inside. Remove with a fork or skewer. Serve hot.

name: Malai Kofta
instructions: Heat oil with whole spices and sliced onions until light golden. Add tomato, green chilli, garlic, coriander stems, cashew and powdered spices, cook 1-2 minutes. Add hot water and cook covered 10-12 minutes. Cool, grind into fine puree. For kofta: mash paneer, add mawa and boiled potato, mix with refined flour and seasonings. Shape into roundels, coat with corn flour. Fry in 150-155°C oil until golden. For gravy: melt butter, add chilli powder and sieved gravy puree, add hot water to adjust consistency, add sugar, cream, garam masala and kasuri methi. Serve koftas with hot gravy separately.

name: Gochujang Paneer
instructions: Cut paneer into cubes. Mix with corn flour, maida, and black pepper. Deep fry or air fry until golden brown. In a pan, heat gochujang paste with minced garlic and ginger. Toss fried paneer in the gochujang sauce. Garnish with spring onion and sesame seeds. Serve hot.

name: Custard Sharbat
instructions: Boil milk and let it cool to room temperature. Mix custard powder and condensed milk with a little warm milk to create a smooth paste, avoiding lumps. Add this mixture to the cooled milk and whisk well until combined. Add chopped seasonal fruits of your choice. Refrigerate for 30 minutes. Stir well and serve chilled.

name: Healthy Nachos with Avocado Dip
instructions: Mix maize flour, wheat flour, black pepper, mixed herbs, and salt. Add water gradually and knead into a soft dough. Roll out thin and cut into nacho shapes. Air fry at 180C for 8-10 minutes until crispy. For dip: peel avocado and mash well. Mix with finely chopped onion, minced green chilli, and lemon juice. Add salt to taste. Serve warm nachos with fresh avocado dip.

name: Jackfruit Bhuna Masala
instructions: Heat mustard oil in a pressure cooker on medium heat. Add cumin seeds and bay leaves and let them crackle. Add ginger and garlic paste, sauté for 1-2 minutes. Add chopped jackfruit and mix well for 2 minutes. Add chopped onions and cook until translucent. Add tomato chunks, all remaining spices, and yogurt. Stir well to combine. Close the pressure cooker and cook on high heat until 3 whistles. Let pressure release naturally. Open cooker, garnish with fresh coriander leaves, and serve hot with rice or bread.

name: Sabudana Sharbat
instructions: Soak sabudana in water for 2-3 hours until soft. In a pot, boil water with ginger slices and crushed cardamom. Add soaked sabudana and cook until translucent, about 10 minutes. Add sugar and stir until dissolved. Let cool completely. Add fresh lemon juice and chopped mint leaves. Stir well and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes. Serve chilled with ice cubes.

name: Lemon Garlic Chicken
instructions: Pat dry chicken breast and season with salt and black pepper. Mince garlic and crush lemon wedges. Heat olive oil and butter in a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken for 4-5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through. Add minced garlic and lemon wedges near the end, squeeze lemon juice over the chicken. For the salad: Cut broccoli into florets, dice zucchini, peppers, and carrot. Sauté in butter with salt and pepper until tender-crisp (about 5-7 minutes). Serve chicken alongside the sautéed vegetable salad.

name: Mixed Dal Dhokla
instructions: Soak mixed dals overnight. Grind soaked dals into smooth batter. Add salt and optional spices. Ferment for 2-3 hours. Add eno just before steaming. Pour into greased steamer plate. Steam for 15-20 minutes until cooked through. Cool slightly and cut into pieces. Serve warm.

name: Paneer Cheese Ball
instructions: Crumble paneer and mix with finely chopped vegetables. Add garlic, green chilli, chilli flakes, mixed herbs and salt. Mix well. Shape into balls. Make a batter with maida, corn flour, water and salt. Coat balls in batter, then roll in bread crumbs. Deep fry in hot oil until golden brown. Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

name: Chicken Kabab
instructions: Mince or finely chop chicken. Mix with finely minced onion, ginger, garlic, and mint leaves. Add all spice powders, salt and butter. Mix well and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Shape into kabab form. Grill over charcoal or pan-fry in oil until cooked through, about 8-10 minutes. Serve with chutney or as appetizer.

name: Kada Prasad
instructions: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan or kadai. Add wheat flour gradually while stirring continuously to avoid lumps. Roast the flour in ghee until it turns golden brown and releases a nutty aroma, about 10-15 minutes. Slowly add water while stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Add sugar and mix well. Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and the ghee starts separating from the sides. Cook until the consistency is thick and halwa pulls away from pan. Serve hot.

name: Thandai Gujiya
instructions: For dough: Mix flour with ghee and powdered sugar until crumbly. Add chilled water gradually and knead into a stiff dough. Rest for 30 minutes. For Thandai masala: Dry roast almonds, cashews, pistachios, peppercorns, cinnamon, fennel, cardamom, nutmeg, melon seeds and poppy seeds. Cool and grind to a powder. Add saffron. For filling: Mix semolina, coconut, khoya, Thandai masala and sugar. Roll dough into circles, place filling in center, seal and give gujiya shape. Bake in a preheated oven at 180C for 30-35 minutes until golden. Cool on a wire rack.

name: Potato Chips
instructions: Select firm potatoes and wash thoroughly. Peel and slice potatoes into thin, even slices (1-2mm thick). Soak slices in water for 30 minutes to remove starch. Pat dry with a cloth. Heat oil to medium-high temperature. Fry potato slices in batches until they turn golden and crispy. Remove with a slotted spoon onto paper towels. Sprinkle salt while still warm. Cool completely and store in airtight containers.

name: Watermelon Sharbat
instructions: Scoop watermelon flesh and blend with milk until smooth. Strain through a fine sieve if desired. Add sugar and rose syrup to the watermelon-milk mixture. Stir well to combine. Pour into glasses and top with chia seeds and jelly pieces. Chill in refrigerator for at least 30 minutes. Serve cold with a straw.

name: Gond Katira Sharbat
instructions: Soak gond and katira seeds separately in 1 cup water for 15-20 minutes until they swell and become gelatinous. In a serving glass, add swollen gond and katira. Pour remaining water, add sugar and rose water. Stir well to combine. Add ice cubes and serve immediately.

name: Lobiya Curry
instructions: Soak chowli overnight and boil until soft with cardamom and tej patta. In a pan, heat oil and temper with cumin seeds and hing. Add chopped onions and fry until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and fry briefly. Add tomato puree and cook until oil separates. Add turmeric, chili, coriander, cumin and garam masala powder. Mix well. Add boiled chowli with some water to make gravy. Stir in curd and salt. Simmer for 5-7 minutes until flavors combine. Garnish with ghee and fresh coriander leaves.

name: Korean Cheese Corn Dog
instructions: Prepare yeast dough with maida, yeast, salt, and sugar. Cut mozzarella cheese and potato into strips. Coat strips with the prepared dough. Mix maida and bread crumbs for breading. Roll coated strips in the breadcrumb mixture. Deep fry until golden brown. Serve hot.

name: Stuffed Karela
instructions: Wash and cut karelas lengthwise, removing seeds and bitter parts. Soak in water with salt to reduce bitterness. Sauté onions with cumin seeds and asafoetida. Add all spices and tamarind pulp to make filling. Stuff the prepared karelas with this spice mixture. Heat oil and cook the stuffed karelas on medium heat until tender, stirring occasionally. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Rajwadi Chai
instructions: Crush ginger, black pepper, cloves, cardamom, cinnamon, and fennel seeds. In a teapot, melt sugar with a little water until dark caramel forms. Pour water into the caramelized sugar and bring to boil. Add crushed spices and boil for 1-2 minutes. Add tea leaves and sugar as desired. Pour in milk and boil again. Add saffron strands for royal touch. Strain and serve hot in traditional cups.

name: Orange Malpua
instructions: Mix maida, rava, sugar, fennel seeds, dry fruits, and cardamom powder. Add orange zest to the dry mixture. Add water to form a batter. Prepare orange sugar syrup by dissolving sugar in water and orange juice. Heat oil for frying. Drop small portions of batter into hot oil and fry until light golden. Remove and immediately dip the fried malpuas into the warm orange sugar syrup. Let them soak for a minute and serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Mix Dal Pakoda
instructions: Soak moong dal, chana dal, and urad dal for 1-2 hours. Grind soaked dals roughly with ginger, garlic, and green chilli to make a coarse paste. Add grated mooli and salt, mix well. Prepare green chutney by grinding mooli leaves, coriander leaves, green chilli, and garlic with lemon juice. Drop small portions of dal mixture into hot oil and fry until golden brown. Serve hot with green chutney and sev bhujiya.

name: Lemon Pickle
instructions: Wash and cut lemons into small pieces, removing seeds. Mix lemon pieces with salt and jaggery in a clean container. Let it sit for 2-3 hours. Heat oil in a pan and add cumin seeds, fennel seeds, and mustard seeds. Once they splutter, add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, and asafoetida. Pour this tempering over the lemon mixture and mix well. Add sugar and mix thoroughly. Store in an airtight glass container. The pickle is ready to consume immediately and tastes better as it sits.

name: Iftaar Energy Drink
instructions: Soak dates in warm water for 10 minutes to soften. Blanch almonds and remove skin if desired. Grind soaked dates, cashews, almonds, and pistachios to a smooth paste. Add cinnamon powder and sugar to the paste. Heat milk until warm. Pour the nut-date paste into warm milk and mix well until fully combined. Stir until smooth and creamy. Pour into serving glasses and garnish with chopped pistachios. Serve immediately while warm.

name: Dahi Vada
instructions: Soak and grind urad and moong dal together with ginger and green chilli to make a smooth batter. Beat the batter to make it light and fluffy. Deep fry spoonfuls of batter until golden brown. Soak the fried vadas in warm water for a few minutes, then gently squeeze out water. Prepare a sweet yogurt sauce with yogurt, sugar, roasted cumin powder and salt. Dip the vadas in the yogurt sauce and garnish with chilli powder, dry nuts and pomegranate seeds.

name: Panna Cotta
instructions: Heat cream and milk with sugar until warm and sugar dissolves. Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Dissolve gelatin or agar-agar according to package instructions and mix with the cream mixture. Pour into glass serving cups or molds. Refrigerate until set, approximately 4 hours. Top with strawberry gel or fresh strawberries before serving.

name: Moringa Palak Paneer
instructions: Clean and blanch moringa and spinach leaves. Squeeze out water and grind to a smooth paste. Heat butter and oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add chopped garlic, ginger, green chilli and onion, saute until translucent. Add the ground moringa-spinach paste and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add chilli powder and cumin powder, mix well. Add paneer cubes and cream, stir gently. Season with salt and cook for another 2-3 minutes until the paneer is coated with the gravy. Garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Malai Soya Chaap
instructions: Boil soya chaap with water, ginger, green chilli, peppercorns and salt until soft. Remove and let cool. Prepare cold marination: mix grated cheese, finely chopped ginger, green chilli, hung yogurt, cardamom powder, white pepper, nutmeg, corn flour and cream. Deep fry boiled chaap pieces until golden. Thread fried chaap and coloured peppers, onions on skewers alternately. Coat skewers with the malai marination. Grill on tawa or tandoor until golden and cooked through. Brush with melted butter and finish with chaat masala and coriander.

name: Bedmi Poori with Aloo Sabzi
instructions: For Bedmi Poori dough: Mix wheat flour, all-purpose flour, semolina, salt, carom seeds and dried fenugreek. Add oil and ghee and make a crumbly mixture. Add chilled water and knead into a stiff dough. Rest for 20 minutes. For Bedmi stuffing: Soak urad dal, then grind with salt, black salt, fennel powder, asafoetida, green chilli, chilli powder, mango powder, coriander powder, garam masala and turmeric. For Aloo Sabzi: Heat oil, add asafoetida, bay leaf, cloves and cumin. Add chopped ginger and green chilli, saute. Add gram flour and turmeric, stir. Add tomato puree and boiled potatoes, add water and cook until curry reaches desired consistency. Season with spices. For Pooris: Roll dough into circles, place filling in center, seal and roll gently. Deep fry until golden and puffy.

name: Vegan Fruit Custard
instructions: For oats milk: Blend oats with chilled water, soaked cashews and almonds until smooth. Strain through a fine sieve to get oats milk. For custard: Heat oats milk in a pan and bring to a boil. Mix custard powder with a little water to make a paste and add to hot milk, stirring continuously to avoid lumps. Add sugar, yellow colour and vanilla extract. Cook until custard thickens to desired consistency. Cool slightly and pour into serving bowls. Garnish with corn flakes, sliced banana, grapes, mango slices, sliced almonds and tutti frutti.

name: Oats Chilla with Tamarind Chutney
instructions: For chilla batter: Soak oats in water for 10 minutes. Mix with gram flour, salt, chopped onion, green chilli, ginger, carom seeds, cumin, black salt, mango powder, pepper, fenugreek powder, turmeric and chilli powder. Add water as needed for pancake batter consistency. For paneer stuffing: Mix paneer, salt, fenugreek powder, coriander, ginger and green chilli. For tamarind chutney: Mix tamarind water, spring onion, onion, salt, black salt, roasted cumin, chilli powder, ginger, green chilli, mashed potato and sugar. Heat oil on a tawa, pour batter and spread into a circle. Add paneer filling in the center, fold chilla. Cook until golden on both sides. Serve hot with tamarind chutney.

name: goli idli
instructions: Prepare rice flour dough by mixing with water and salt. Divide into portions and steam in idli molds. Serve with various tempering options: masala tadka with spices, or butter garlic preparation with corn and spring onion.

name: tomato ketchup
instructions: Quarter 2.5kg tomatoes and cook with diced onion, ginger, garlic, dried red chillies, and spices. Grind the mixture into puree after spices are bloomed in oil. Boil the ketchup for extended time to concentrate flavors. Add vinegar and sugar for balance. Store in bottles for months.

name: melon malai kulfi
instructions: Boil 1 liter full-fat milk until reduced. Dice 400gm musk melon and reduce to puree over heat. Mix reduced melon puree with milk and custard powder slurry. Blend the mixture until smooth. Pour into kulfi molds and freeze for several hours until set. Demold and serve.

name: green chilli sauce
instructions: Boil diced potatoes in salted water until tender. Prepare green chillies by deseeding. Bloom coriander seeds in oil, then grind with garlic, ginger, green chillies, and cooked potatoes to a paste. Boil the chilli paste in water, add vinegar and sugar for balance. Strain and bottle for storage.

name: biryani raita
instructions: Heat oil in a pan over low flame. Add chopped garlic and fry while stirring continuously until it turns brown. Transfer browned garlic to a bowl and let cool completely. Mix cooled garlic with yogurt, salt, black salt, and chilli powder. Allow to mature for 15 minutes. Transfer to serving container and garnish with browned garlic.

name: Masala Jeera Pulao
instructions: Heat oil and butter in a pan. Add bay leaf, black cardamom, and cumin seeds. Sauté briefly. Add green chilli, ginger, and garlic and cook for 30 seconds. Add sliced onions and cook until golden. Add chilli powder, turmeric, and coriander powder and stir well. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft. Add salt to taste. Pour in water and bring to a boil. Add basmati rice and kasoori methi. Stir, cover, and cook on medium heat for 10-12 minutes until rice is tender and water is absorbed.

name: Masala Laccha Paratha
instructions: Prepare dough with both flours, salt, ajwain, kasoori methi and water. Let rest. For filling, dry roast urad dal and mix with fennel seeds, asafoetida, turmeric, chilli powder, black salt, salt, coriander powder, garam masala and amchur. Divide dough into small balls. Roll thin, apply butter and spice mixture in layers. Re-roll carefully maintaining layers. Cook on hot tawa with butter until golden and crispy on both sides.

name: Lauki Ka Bharta
ingredients: urad dal vadi 1 broken in pieces
instructions: Char bottle gourd over open flame, peel and mash. Heat mustard oil and add urad dal vadi and cumin seeds. Add ginger and onions, cook until soft. Add tomatoes and cook until mushy. Add all spice powders and salt. Fold in mashed lauki and cook for 5-7 minutes on medium heat. Finish with kasoori methi and fresh coriander leaves.

name: Ragda Pattice
ingredients: dried white peas (सफेद मटर) soaked 1 cup
instructions: For ragda: soak white peas overnight, boil with turmeric, salt and baking soda until tender. For pattice: mash boiled potatoes with salt, spices, bread crumbs and chopped vegetables. Form into patties and pan-fry until crispy. For green chutney: grind coriander, mint, green chilli, ginger with salt and lemon juice. For tamarind chutney: mix tamarind water with sugar, spices and salt. Assemble by topping pattice with ragda, both chutneys, and garnish with onion, tomato, pomegranate and sev.

name: Hyderabadi Mutton Haleem
instructions: Soak grains and lentils for 30 minutes then boil with almonds and cashews until soft. Marinate mutton with salt, turmeric, chilli powder, garam masala, black pepper, ginger-garlic paste and curd for 30 minutes. Heat ghee and add whole spices (cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, peppercorn, allspice, caraway). Add marinated mutton and cook for 20 minutes. Add green chillies, onions, coriander and mint. Pour in water and simmer until mutton is very tender. Remove bones and combine with cooked grain mixture. Cook together for 15-20 minutes until well amalgamated and rich. Garnish with fried onions, fried cashews and fresh herbs.

name: Rabri Falooda
instructions: For rabri: reduce full fat milk by 80% on high heat, stirring occasionally, until thick and condensed. Add sugar and cardamom powder, cool in ice bath. For falooda: mix corn flour with powdered sugar and water to smooth paste. Cook while stirring until thick and translucent, add rosewater if using. Extrude through special falooda press into ice water to cool. Assemble: layer falooda in glass, add chilled rabri, pour rose syrup, top with crushed ice, garnish with pista, cashew, tutti frutti, cherry and sabza. Add vanilla ice cream scoop on top.

name: Dal Moth Chaat
instructions: Soak moth dal for 2 nights and allow to sprout. Boil sprouts with turmeric and salt until tender. Prepare amchur chutney by mixing dry mango powder with salt, black salt, sugar, chilli powder, roasted cumin and water. Cut cucumber lengthwise and scoop out center to make boats. Boil potatoes and cut into chunks. Mix boiled moth sprouts with black salt, chaat masala, sugar and roasted cumin. Layer in cucumber boat with boiled potato pieces, amchur chutney, onion, tomato, green chilli, pomegranate seeds and coriander. Top with matthi and serve immediately.

name: bharwa bhindi masala
instructions: Clean bhindi and slit vertically without cutting completely. Prepare stuffing by mixing ground spices, raw mango, mustard oil and salt. Fill each bhindi piece with prepared masala. Heat oil in pan and temper with dry red chilli and asafoetida. Add whole onions and cook until golden. Add stuffed bhindi and cook on medium-high heat, tossing occasionally. Continue cooking until bhindi is tender and outer skin is golden. Toss with chopped raw mango and finish. Plate and serve hot with roti or paratha.

name: banarasi tamatar chaat
instructions: Prepare sugar syrup by dissolving sugar in 1/2 cup water and boiling until slightly thickened. Heat ghee in pan and temper with cumin seeds. Add chopped ginger and green chilli, saute. Add diced tomatoes and cook on high heat until they break down. Add cooked potatoes and dried peas. Season with salts, garam masala and chaat masala. Add water and simmer for 5-10 minutes. Finish with lemon juice. Transfer to serving bowl, pour sugar syrup over top, drizzle ghee, and garnish with roasted cumin, fresh coriander and namak para. Serve hot.

name: rajasthani kadhi
instructions: Whisk yogurt and besan together to make a smooth paste. Add salt, turmeric and chilli powder. Dilute with 5 cups water and strain to remove lumps. Heat oil in large pan and add dry chillies, tej patta and fenugreek seeds. Add mustard seeds, cloves, coriander seeds, cumin and fennel seeds. Add curry leaves, green chillies and ginger. Pour this hot tempered oil into yogurt-besan mixture. Bring mixture to boil on high heat, then reduce to low and simmer for 20-25 minutes until kadhi thickens slightly. Optionally finish with second tempering of ghee and kashmiri chilli powder. Serve hot with rice or flatbread.

name: aachari masala tinde
instructions: Clean and peel tinda, cut in half lengthwise and scoop out center portions. Prepare dry spice mixture with turmeric, chilli powder, coriander, kasoori methi, amchur and cumin powders. Mix with mustard oil and salt to form thick paste. Stuff each tinda half with prepared masala mixture. Heat oil in pan and temper with cumin seeds. Add chopped onions and cook until golden brown. Add garlic, ginger and green chilli, cook for 1 minute. Add mango pickle and stir. Add tomato puree with remaining spices, simmer. Add prepared tinda pieces and cook covered on medium heat until tinda is tender (15-20 minutes). Finish with fresh coriander. Serve hot with roti or rice.

name: jaljeera masala soda
instructions: Toast cumin, black pepper, cloves, anaradana, dried ginger, asafoetida, amchur and salt in a dry pan for 2-3 minutes until aromatic. Cool and grind all toasted spices into fine powder with mint leaves powder. Store this jaljeera masala for future use. To prepare classic jaljeera drink: take 1 tbsp jaljeera masala powder, mix with 1 cup chilled water in glass. Add lemon juice, stir well. Add ice cubes and top with boondi. Serve immediately. For cucumber-mint variation: muddle cucumber and fresh mint leaves, prepare base as above, add to the mixture, finish with lemon juice, ice and boondi. Stir and serve chilled.

name: bhandare wale kale chane
instructions: Soak kala chana overnight. Boil with water, salt, baking soda, black cardamom and cloves until tender. In a separate pan, heat oil and temper with cardamom, cloves, bay leaf and black pepper. Add asafetida, then roast cumin and add ginger and green chilli. Saute onion until golden. Add spice powders, tomato puree and boiled chana with salt and black salt. Simmer until flavors meld. Top with fried green chillies, potatoes, amchoor powder, paneer cubes, kasoori methi and fresh coriander.

name: sabudana aloo paratha
instructions: Soak sabudana in water for 4 hours until plump. Grind soaked sabudana pearls. Mix ground sabudana with mashed boiled potato, rock salt, roasted cumin, green chilli, crushed peanuts and coriander. Divide mixture into balls and flatten between butter paper into paratha shape. Cook on griddle with light oil on both sides until golden and crispy.

name: mango ice cream
instructions: Reduce full-fat milk on medium heat for 5-7 minutes. Grind ripe mango until smooth puree. Mix mango puree with reduced milk and condensed milk. Mix custard powder with 0.25 cup milk and add to mango mixture. Add dairy cream and grind entire mixture until smooth and aerated. Freeze in covered container, stirring occasionally until set.

name: aam panna chuski
instructions: For Aam Panna: Boil ripe and raw mango until soft, strain pulp. Make sugar syrup with 200g sugar and water, let cool. Mix mango pulp with sugar syrup, black salt, roasted cumin and black pepper powder. For Chuski: Crush ice finely. Pack ice into glass around ice cream stick. Pour Aam Panna syrup over ice and top with chat masala. Alternatively make Shikanji syrup by mixing sugar syrup, lemon juice, roasted cumin and mint leaves for a tangy chuski.

name: toor dal dosa
instructions: Soak rice and toor dal overnight. Grind with ginger, garlic, dry red chilli, curry leaves, black pepper, cumin seeds, asafetida, salt, turmeric and water to make smooth batter. Let ferment if time permits. Heat griddle, pour batter thin, spread evenly. Cook paneer filling by tempering with mustard and bengal gram, adding onion, tomato, green chilli and paneer curry. Fill dosa, fold and serve. Make chana dal chutney by tempering chana dal with spices and grinding with coconut, tamarind and water.

name: chawal ke kurkure
instructions: Dry roast rice in pan until light brown, cool completely. Boil water with turmeric, salt and sugar. Mix roasted rice with cumin seeds and black sesame seeds. Add boiling water slowly to rice mixture, stirring constantly to form sticky dough. Let cool slightly. Using wet hands, shape small portions into elongated rounds or rods. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy on all sides. Drain on paper towels. Mix chat masala, raw mango powder, mint powder and red chilli powder and toss with hot kurkures. Serve with cucumber-mint dip.

name: paneer kulcha roll
instructions: Knead dough with all-purpose flour, whole wheat flour, salt, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, curd, milk, water and oil. Let rest 30 minutes. For paneer filling: Heat butter and oil, temper with cumin. Saute ginger, garlic and green chilli. Add onion until soft. Add all capsicum colors and tomato. Add turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder and chaat masala. Add mashed or cubed paneer and kasoori methi. Mix until well coated. Divide dough into portions and roll into kulcha shape (oval flat bread). Cook on tawa with oil until puffed and golden. Mix mayonnaise with mint chutney for mint mayo. To assemble: Place kulcha on butter paper, spread mint mayo, add paneer filling, layer with onion slices, tomato slices and pickled vegetables. Roll tightly in butter paper.

name: Chicken Pie
instructions: Make pie dough by mixing maida, butter, sugar and chilled water until crumbly, then knead lightly. Prepare filling by sauteing onions, garlic, celery in oil and butter, add chicken and brown it, then add flour to make roux, add vegetables, cream and cheese, season with salt and spices. Assemble in 9-inch pie mould by placing dough as base, add filling, create lattice pattern with dough strips on top, brush with egg-milk wash, bake until golden brown.

name: Dal Ki Dulhan
instructions: Soak arhar dal and boil with water, turmeric and salt until cooked. Prepare dough for pitthi/dhokli by mixing wheat flour, ghee and water. Make tadka by heating ghee and adding cumin, mustard seeds, asafoetida, then add garlic, ginger, green chilli, onion and cook until golden. Add tomato and turmeric and red chilli powder. Pour into boiled dal. Drop small portions of dough into the dal and simmer until they float and cook through. Finish with garnish.

name: Paneer Makhni Pizza
instructions: Prepare makhni sauce by cooking tomatoes with ginger, garlic, green cardamom, cashew and green chilli until soft, then blend smooth. Heat butter and add the sauce, simmer until thick. Finish by adding ginger, green chillies, garam masala, fenugreek leaves and paneer cubes. Prepare pizza dough by mixing maida, semolina, salt, sugar, baking powder, oil and water until soft. Knead well and let rest. Roll dough thin and place on heated tawa. Cook until base is firm, then spread makhni sauce, add paneer pieces, top with mozzarella cheese, onions, tomatoes, coriander and olives. Cook until cheese melts.

name: Upma
instructions: For classic upma: Heat ghee and roast rava until fragrant. In another pan, heat ghee, add mustard seeds, urad dal, chana dal, allow to crackle, then add curry leaves, green chilli, ginger. Add onion and cook until translucent, add carrot and peas, then add roasted rava, stir well. Pour water gradually, stirring constantly to avoid lumps, cook until water absorbs and upma is fluffy. Finish with ghee, coriander and lemon juice. For cheese upma: Toast rava separately, heat butter and add garlic, all capsicums, oregano, basil. Add rava and stir, then pour water/milk gradually, cook until fluffy, finish with cheese and chilli flakes.

name: Afghani Paneer
instructions: Marinate paneer cubes in salt, ginger-garlic paste and lemon juice for 15 minutes. Prepare second marination by blending coriander leaves, mint, onion, green chillies, cashews and cheese into a paste, mix with curd and cream, add salt, pepper, kasuri methi, chaat masala, garam masala, cumin powder and water. Marinate paneer again in this paste for 20 minutes. Grill or bake the marinated paneer until cooked. In a separate pan, heat oil and butter, add bay leaf, black cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, green chilli and ginger. Add the marination paste and simmer until it becomes a curry consistency. Combine grilled paneer pieces with the curry. Garnish with masala onions and coriander sprigs.

name: Peri Peri Paneer Tikka
instructions: Prepare peri-peri sauce by soaking dried red chillies in water, then blend with ginger, garlic, cumin, cloves, cinnamon and salt to make a paste. Mix in lemon juice, tomato ketchup and oil to make a pourable sauce. Salt the paneer cubes and let rest for 5 minutes. Soak wooden skewers in water for 30 minutes. Thread paneer cubes and vegetable chunks alternately on skewers, starting and ending with vegetables. Brush the skewers generously with peri-peri sauce. Heat oil in a pan or grill, cook the skewers on medium-high heat until paneer has grill marks and vegetables are slightly charred. Serve hot with lime wedges and additional sauce.

name: Kashmiri Rajma
instructions: Rinse and boil rajma with bay leaves, black cardamom, asafoetida, fenugreek seeds, cloves and salt until fully cooked and tender. In a separate pan, heat mustard oil or ghee, add caraway seeds and let them crackle. Add coarse onion paste and cook until golden and oil separates. Add ginger and garlic paste, cook for 2 minutes. Add fresh tomato puree and cook until reduced and oil separates, about 10 minutes. Add kashmiri chilli powder, turmeric, fennel powder, dry ginger powder and coriander powder. Add curd and stir well to combine. Add cooked rajma with its cooking liquid to the masala, simmer for 15 minutes. Finish with ghee and serve hot with rice or bread.

name: Aloo Tikki
instructions: Prepare chana dal stuffing by heating oil, adding asafoetida, cumin, fennel, then add ginger and green chilli, cook briefly. Add boiled chana dal with salt, turmeric, chilli powder and coriander powder, mix well and let cool. Prepare mashed potato mixture by mixing boiled mashed potatoes with salt, cornstarch and chopped coriander. Take a portion of potato mixture, flatten in palm, place a spoonful of chana dal filling in center, wrap with more potato mixture to enclose filling, form into patty. Heat oil in pan and shallow fry tikkis until golden and crispy on both sides. Prepare sweet curd by mixing curd with sugar powder, black salt and a pinch of regular salt. Place cooked aloo tikkis on plate, top with sweet curd, tamarind chutney, mint chutney, beetroot and carrot juliennes, pomegranate and sev. Serve hot.

name: Mutton Kabuli Pulao
instructions: Cook mutton with ghee, oil, bay leaf, cinnamon, onions, salt and caraway seeds in water for 35-40 minutes. Prepare Afghani chaar masala by roasting and grinding cumin, black cardamom, cloves and cinnamon. Layer cooked mutton with carrots, raisins and masala in a handi. Add soaked basmati rice with hot water, seal edges with dough and cook on low heat for 15-20 minutes. Rest and serve hot with garlic curd raita and mint garnish.

name: Punjabi Kadhi Pakoda
instructions: Grind ginger, coriander leaves and green chillies into paste. Mix paste with curd, water, turmeric, red chilli powder, asafoetida, gram flour, salt and jaggery. For pakoda: mix grated onions, green chillies, coriander, ginger, carom seeds, masala, turmeric, chilli powder and gram flour. Fry pakoda balls until crispy. For final dish: heat mustard oil until smoky, add fenugreek seeds and kashmiri chillies, add curd mixture and water, simmer 15-20 minutes, add fried pakoras and boil. Garnish with coriander sprig and serve hot with rice.

name: Kothimbir Vadi
instructions: Mix roughly chopped coriander, besan, salt, chilli powder, turmeric, garam masala, green chilli, cumin powder, sugar, asafoetida, sesame seeds, lemon juice and rice flour with water to form dough. Steam mixture in a lined bowl until cooked. Demould and cut into pieces. Deep fry until crispy and golden. For accompaniment, blend spring onions, mint, coriander, green chilli, ginger with black salt and chaat masala into a chutney. Serve crispy vadi with chutney and fresh vegetable toppings.

name: Wayanadan Chicken Curry
instructions: Marinate chicken with salt, garlic, ginger, green chillies, coriander powder, chilli powder, garam masala, meat masala and black pepper. Heat coconut oil and temper with mustard seeds and dry red chillies. Add marinated chicken and cook briefly. Add sliced onions and tomatoes, curry leaves and water. Simmer until chicken is nearly cooked. Add thick coconut milk and simmer further until chicken is tender and curry reaches desired consistency. Serve hot with steamed rice or flatbread.

name: Kala Chana Chaat
instructions: Boil kala chana with salt and water until tender. Prepare tadka: heat oil with tej patta, asafoetida, black cardamom, cloves and black pepper. Add chopped ginger and green chilli, kashmiri chilli powder, turmeric and coriander powder, fenugreek powder and salt. Cook until fragrant. Mix boiled chana with tadka. Toss with diced boiled potatoes, raw onions, cucumber and tomato. Season with black salt, roasted cumin, chaat masala, amchur powder and red chilli powder. Finish with fresh lemon juice, coriander leaves and pomegranate seeds. Serve immediately.

name: Aloo Puri
instructions: Prepare masala by dry roasting black peppercorns, cumin seeds, black cardamom, cloves, coriander seeds, green cardamom, salt, and cinnamon stick. Grind into fine powder. Heat mustard oil in kadai, add bay leaf, cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds, fennel seeds, green chillies, ginger; let splutter for 1 minute. Add diced potatoes, salt, little water; cook 10-12 minutes until tender. Finish with prepared masala, dry mango powder, fresh coriander leaves; mix well. For raita: fry green chillies in mustard oil until light golden, mash in bowl. Mix mashed chillies with beaten curd, salt, crushed black peppercorns. For puri: divide dough into lemon-sized balls, flatten, add pinch of red chili powder. Fry in hot oil until golden. Serve hot puri with aloo sabji and raita.

name: Meethe Chawal
ingredients: sella basmati rice 2.5 cups soaked 20 minutes
instructions: Heat 3 tbsp ghee in kadai, add soaked sella basmati rice and sauté for 1 minute. Add apricots, 4 cups water, saffron water, 1 cup sugar, cloves, green cardamom, 1/2 cup water and mix well. Cover with lid and cook on low flame for 12-15 minutes until cooked. Meanwhile, prepare fried nuts: heat 2 tbsp ghee, fry pistachios, almonds, dry coconut, cashew nuts until golden. Add fennel seeds and crushed peppercorns; mix. Finish cooked rice with fried nuts, 3/4 cup sugar, little water; mix well. Transfer to serving bowl, garnish with mint sprig and fried nuts. Serve hot.

name: Lucknowi Chicken Stew
ingredients: tender coriander stems with roots 1 tbsp finely chopped
instructions: Marinate chicken: Combine drumsticks and thighs, salt, ginger garlic paste, green chilli, crushed black peppercorns, 1 tbsp oil in bowl; set aside. Heat 3-4 tbsp oil in handi, add dry red chillies, black peppercorns, black cardamom, sliced onions, salt and sauté until translucent. Add little water and marinated chicken; cook 5-6 minutes until tender. Add coriander seeds, cumin seeds, clove, black peppercorns, coriander powder, tender coriander stems with roots; mix well. Add beaten curd and fresh cream, cover lid and cook 10-12 minutes on medium flame until cooked. Finish with ghee and crushed black peppercorns; simmer 1 minute. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with coriander sprig. Serve hot with khameeri roti or ladi pav.

name: chicken koliwada
instructions: Make green paste: grind garlic, ginger, green chilli, coriander with water until smooth. Cut chicken into slices, add salt, lemon juice, green paste, all spice powders, carom seeds, baking soda, oil; mix to coat. Add egg white and optional red food colour. Add corn flour and gram flour in 3 batches, mixing well after each; adjust consistency with water/flour as needed. Fry at 170°C for 3-4 mins in batches (don't overcrowd). Rest 4-5 mins, then flash fry at 185°C for 30 secs until crisp. Transfer to sieve to drain, sprinkle generous chaat masala. Serve with chutney.

name: Nagpuri Tari Wale Pohe
ingredients: 2 heaped tbsp degi red chili powder
instructions: Grind onions, dry coconut, ginger, garlic, green chili with red chili powder, coriander powder, turmeric, oil, sesame seeds into smooth vatan paste. Pressure cook soaked chickpeas with water, salt, kashmiri red chillies for 3-4 whistles. Wash and soak poha gently. Prepare tadka: heat oil, add peanuts, mustard seeds, cumin seeds, let splutter, add potato, onion, green chili, ginger, salt, cook 3-4 minutes until translucent, finish with curry leaves and turmeric. Prepare tarri: heat oil in handi, add bay leaf, cardamom, cloves, peppercorns, cumin, onion, saute, add vatan paste, cook 7-8 minutes. Add pepper powder, chickpeas, water, simmer, finish with tomatoes, coriander, garam masala, mango powder, salt and boil. Serve soaked poha mixed with tadka, topped with tarri and coriander.

name: Kashmiri Chicken Dalcha
instructions: Boil chicken legs with water, salt, turmeric on low heat for 4-5 minutes until cooked. In another handi, add water, salt, soaked chana dal, turmeric, cinnamon stick, bay leaf and cook 20-25 minutes on medium heat until dal is soft. Prepare curd mixture by mixing curd, fennel powder, dry ginger powder, degi red chili powder, turmeric, water. Heat mustard oil in handi until smoky hot, saute sliced onions until golden, add bay leaf and cinnamon stick, then add curd mixture and saute. Add boiled chicken and cooked dal, simmer on low-medium heat for 12-15 minutes. Finish with butter and lemon juice, mix well. Serve hot with steam rice, garnished with coriander leaves and green chillies.

name: Lucknowi Machhi Ka Salan
ingredients: 2 tbsp tender coriander stems with roots
instructions: Marinate salmon pieces with turmeric powder, degi red chili powder, salt, and curd. Grind onions, turmeric powder, degi red chili powder, coriander powder, and coriander stems into smooth paste. Heat mustard oil in pan until smoky hot, place marinated fish and cook 2 minutes per side, transfer to tray. In handi, heat remaining mustard oil, add fenugreek seeds and green chillies, then add prepared paste and cook for a while. Add water and let boil, adjust consistency with more water if needed, add salt and simmer for 5-6 minutes. Add fried fish and cook for 1 minute. Finish with ginger, fresh coriander leaves, and lemon juice, mix well. Serve hot with roti and steam rice, garnished with coriander sprig.

name: Purani Dilli Chole Kulche
instructions: Pressure cook soaked chickpeas with turmeric, garlic, bouquet garni (cardamom, peppercorns, tea, cloves, chillies wrapped in muslin), water, and ghee for 3-4 whistles. Open lid, add salt, tamarind pulp, jaggery, prepared masala, crushed peppercorns, and chickpeas water, simmer 10-12 minutes until thickened. Dry roast masala spices then grind to powder. For carrot pickle: boil carrots and green chili for 7-8 minutes, add vinegar, jaggery, and spices, simmer 4-5 minutes. For ginger-chili paste: mortar pestle ginger and green chillies into coarse paste. For tira: heat oil and ghee, add paste and asafoetida, cook 2-3 minutes, add red chili powder, cook 1 minute, finish with ghee. Finish chole with prepared tira and coriander. For kulcha: heat ghee in pan, place kulchas, pour tira, sear both sides until golden. Serve hot chole with seared kulcha, garnished with coriander, green chili, lemon.

name: Tari Wala Chana
instructions: Grind tender coriander stems, ginger, garlic, green chillies, kashmiri red chillies, salt into smooth paste. Dry roast masala spices for 1 minute until aromatic, then grind to fine powder. Pressure cook soaked black chickpeas with salt, bay leaf, cinnamon stick, kashmiri red chillies, water, turmeric powder, degi red chili powder for 2-3 whistles. In handi, heat mustard oil and oil until smoky hot, add cumin seeds, bay leaf and let splutter. Add onions and saute until golden, add prepared paste, tomatoes, potatoes, salt and saute 1 minute. Add turmeric powder, degi red chili powder, water and cook 10-12 minutes until potatoes half-cooked. Add cooked chickpeas, water, sugar, salt and simmer. Add prepared masala, water and boil. Finish with ghee and fresh coriander leaves, mix well. Serve hot with pav, tandoori roti or steam rice, garnished with coriander.

name: Dry Fruit Mohanthal
instructions: For crumbled gram flour mixture: heat milk and ghee until it boils, add gram flour and mix well, then rub with hands until crumbly in texture, rest for 10-15 minutes, sieve through strainer. For sugar syrup: in handi, add sugar and water, simmer until thickens, cook until one string consistency. For mohanthal: heat ghee in kadai until hot, add prepared crumbled mixture and cook 2-3 minutes until golden, add milk and saute 4-5 minutes until golden brown, add prepared sugar syrup and cook well until mixture thickens. Finish with cardamom powder and nutmeg powder, mix well. Transfer to tray and spread evenly. In pan, dry roast cashews and almonds for 1 minute, top onto mohanthal. Let set for a while, cut into desired shapes. Garnish with golden vark and rose petals. Serve warm.

name: Mango Lassi
instructions: Mix mango concentrate with yogurt. Add water or milk to desired consistency. Sweeten with sugar. Blend with ice cubes. Serve chilled.

name: Besan Tofu
instructions: Mix gram flour with turmeric, chilli powder, asafoetida, salt, water and yogurt. Pour into greased mold. Steam for 12-15 minutes until set. Cool and demold. Cut into desired shapes. Can be used in curries or fried.

name: Burmese Aloo Curry
instructions: First prepare tohu (besan tofu): Mix besan with turmeric, red chilli powder, asafoetida, salt, water and yogurt. Pour into greased steaming mold and steam for 12-15 minutes. Cool and demold, cut into cubes. For curry: Heat oil, add bay leaf, cinnamon, button chillies and sautéed sliced onions. Add diced potatoes and ginger-garlic paste. Cook for a minute. Add turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder and salt. Add water and prepared tohu cubes. Simmer for 5-6 minutes. Finish with coriander leaves and green chillies. Serve hot with roti.

name: Dhaba Style Kadhai Paneer
instructions: Make gravy: Heat oil, add kashmiri chillies and garlic, sauté 1 minute. Add onions and ginger-garlic paste, cook until onions are translucent. Add tomatoes, salt, sugar, chilli powder, turmeric and water; cook 5-6 minutes. Add coriander powder, cashew nuts and butter; cover and cook 10-12 minutes. Blend into smooth paste. Make kadhai masala: Dry roast peppercorns, coriander seeds, cumin seeds, fennel seeds and salt; grind with chilli flakes. Marinate paneer slices with salt and chilli powder. Fry paneer until golden. Sauté vegetables separately with oil, onions, capsicum, peppers, tomatoes, butter, chilli powder and kadhai masala. In kadhai, melt butter, add onions, prepared gravy, chilli powder and water; simmer 7-8 minutes. Add fried paneer, sautéed vegetables, kadhai masala. Finish with coriander leaves, ginger juliennes, lemon juice. Serve hot with roti or naan.

name: Chicken Malai Seekh Biryani
instructions: Prepare seekh: Mix ground chicken thighs and breast with salt, black salt, pepper, garam masala, garlic, ginger, green chillies, butter, mint, coriander, lemon juice and onion. Shape onto seekhs and smoke with charcoal and ghee. Prepare birista: Deep fry sliced onions until golden and crispy. Make gravy: Blend soaked cashews, melon seeds with green chilli paste, ginger-garlic paste and 80% of birista with water and salt; add biryani masala, birista oil and fresh cream. Cook rice: Boil rice with bay leaves, star anise, cinnamon, green cardamom, cloves, peppercorns, caraway seeds, green chilli and lemon juice until 70% done. Layer: Spread rice in handi, add gravy mixture with fried seekhs, top with remaining rice, mint, coriander, remaining birista and ghee. Seal with dough and dum cook for 45 minutes. Garnish with saffron soaked in milk. Serve hot.

name: instant sponge dosa
instructions: For dosa: Grind curd and semolina with washed poha, powdered sugar, water and salt into fine batter. Add eno and mix well. Heat tawa on high flame until moderately hot. Pour 1.5 ladles batter on tawa and let spread on its own. Cook on high flame until bubbles form, then lower flame until set on surface. Remove from tawa using spatula. For potato masala: Heat oil in pan, add mustard seeds, urad dal and chana dal until golden. Add cashews, ginger, garlic, green chilli, curry leaves and asafoetida, cook 1-2 minutes. Add sliced onions, cook until translucent. Add hot water and boiled potatoes (roughly mash while adding), sugar, turmeric powder and salt. Cook until water evaporates while lightly mashing potatoes, then add fresh coriander. For coconut chutney: Grind coconut, roasted chana dal, fresh coriander, mint, green chilli, tamarind, cumin seeds and black pepper into fine paste. Temper with oil, chana dal, urad dal, mustard seeds, red chillies and curry leaves fried until golden, then pour tempering over chutney and mix. Serve dosa with masala and chutney.

name: sweet potato fries
instructions: Wash, peel and cut sweet potatoes into 1cm x 1cm french fries. Wash cut fries in cold water until water turns clear. Boil salted water with vinegar and blanch fries for 3 minutes, then strain and cool to room temperature. Deep fry in 185-190°C oil for 1 minute, transfer to sieve and cool to room temperature again. For second fry, heat oil to 180-185°C and fry over medium to low heat until golden brown and crisp. Drain on paper towels. Sprinkle salt and black pepper to taste. Optional: add kashmiri chilli powder, chaat masala, garam masala and lemon juice for masala fries. For garlic dip: mix curd, mayonnaise, vinegar, powdered sugar, black pepper, salt, fresh coriander and grated garlic. For tangy mayo dip: mix mayonnaise, ketchup, red chilli sauce and mustard sauce. Serve fries with dips.

name: jhol momo soup
instructions: Toast soybeans on low flame while stirring until slightly light brown, transfer to bowl. Toast sesame seeds until light brown in same pan, transfer to same bowl. Toast peanuts until light brown, transfer to bowl and set aside to cool. Heat oil in deep pan on high flame, add cumin seeds, sichuan peppercorns, garlic, ginger, green and red chilli. Stir well and add sliced onions, cook on high flame until soft and translucent. Lower flame, add turmeric powder and stir well. Add tomatoes and coriander stem with salt, stir and cook for 3-4 minutes. Add hot water as required, stir well, cover and cook on low flame until tomatoes become soft and mushy. Switch off flame and cool to room temperature. Add tomato mixture to mixer grinder jar along with toasted soybeans, sesame seeds and peanuts, grind into fine puree using water as required for consistency (should be free-flowing but not too liquidy). Transfer puree to deep pan, add more water to adjust consistency and fresh chopped coriander, stir well. Taste jhol achar and adjust seasoning, add lemon juice and stir. For jhol soup, turn flame to high, add more water to make soupy, add sugar, stir and bring to boil. Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve hot with steamed momos.

name: gujarati khichu
instructions: In non-stick kadhaiAdd water, carom seeds, green chilli, cumin seeds and salt. Cover and bring to boil. Once water comes to boiling, add fresh coriander and groundnut oil, let boil for 3-4 minutes. In separate bowl sieve rice flour. Add papad khar to water, then gradually add sieved rice flour while mixing with rolling pin. Keep stirring vigorously until all flour combines with no lumps, using spatula to ensure. Cook over low flame for 2-3 minutes until everything comes together like a dough. Taste and adjust salt if required. Switch off flame and cover khichu, set aside. Oil steamer plate and transfer khichu to it, spread unevenly on plate. Place in steamer and steam for 8-10 minutes. Once steamed, serve hot topped with methi masala and groundnut oil.

name: kala jamun
instructions: Make chaasni: Add sugar and water to deep pan on high flame, stir until sugar dissolves. Add saffron, rose water, green cardamom and lemon juice, boil for 4-5 minutes until slightly sticky when pressed between fingers (should not become too thick). Set aside and keep warm. Cream mawa by beating with palm base for at least 15 minutes until smooth with no grains. Grate malai paneer and cream similarly until smooth texture. Combine creamed mawa and paneer together. Add powdered sugar, baking powder and refined flour in batches (quantity varies with paneer moisture), mixing well until it forms dough that leaves the pan. Do not over-knead or it will become greasy. Cover and rest dough for 10-15 minutes. Divide into equal portions (22g each for 16 jamuns). Roll portions into smooth, crack-free balls, keeping covered with damp cloth. Heat oil in kadhaiito 150-155°C. Verify chaasni is warm (50°C). Create whirlpool in oil with large spoon carefully without spilling. Add jamun balls without spoon touching them, fry in batches without overcrowding on low flame while stirring for even cooking. Once light golden brown, continue stirring and frying until brown. Increase flame to medium-high and fry until dark brown almost blackish. Remove with large spider and dunk into warm chaasni, gently press down. Repeat with remaining batches. Soak in chaasni for at least 1 hour, preferably 3-4 hours (will grow bigger as they soak). Serve with or without chaasni.

name: eggless donuts
instructions: In a bowl add milk, water and 1 tsp powdered sugar, stir well then add yeast and dissolve, cover with cling wrap and set aside 5-10 minutes. In a large bowl sieve flour, remaining powdered sugar and salt, mix well. Add curd and mix to incorporate. Add the yeast mixture and combine using hands to form dough. Transfer to flat surface and knead until smooth and soft. Add softened butter in 2 batches and knead well for 10-12 minutes until dough becomes super soft and smooth with a gluten window. Form into a doughball, transfer to greased bowl, drizzle oil, cover with cling wrap and let ferment in warm place until doubled in size (1-1.5 hours). After doubling, puncture dough, transfer to flour-dusted platform and knead slightly. Roll into 1.5cm thick sheet. Cut donuts using donut cutter or cookie cutters. Place on parchment lined sheet, cover with damp cloth. Knead extra dough and cut more donuts. Place all donuts on parchment tray, cover with damp cloth and place in warm place for 45 minutes to 1 hour (second proofing). For glaze, sieve powdered sugar into bowl with milk and vanilla essence, whisk well. For chocolate glaze, add cocoa powder to the sugar mixture. Heat oil in deep pan to 175°C. After donuts have risen, carefully drop into hot oil, splash hot oil over donuts until they puff up nicely. Fry 3-4 at once. Flip when light golden brown and fry until golden on other side. Transfer to wire rack and cool slightly. Dip into glaze of choice while warm. Add sprinkles if desired. Fry and glaze all donuts similarly.

name: matar ka nimona
instructions: Take 500g peeled fresh green peas and remove a handful and keep in separate bowl. Add remaining peas to mixer grinder and grind into semi-fine paste without water. In small mixer grinder jar add fresh coriander, green garlic, ginger, green chilli, coriander seeds and little water, grind into coarse paste keeping it thick. Set handi over high flame, add mustard oil until smoking point. Lower flame and add vadis, fry until golden brown and transfer to bowl. Increase flame to high and add potato wedges, fry until light golden brown and transfer to same bowl. Remove few tablespoons oil from kadhai and add green pea paste, stir and cook over medium to low until paste turns crumbly and darkens. Remove cooked pea paste to separate bowl. Add removed oil back to kadhai, once hot add cumin seeds, red chilli, asafetida and green ginger-garlic paste, stir and cook over high flame 2-3 minutes. Add tomatoes, reserved fresh green peas, salt, stir and cover, cook over medium to low flame 8-10 minutes until tomatoes turn mushy, stirring once or twice. Once tomatoes cooked add turmeric and red chilli powder, stir and cook spices briefly. Add fried potatoes and vadis, stir and coat well with masala, add cooked green pea paste and stir well. Add hot water to adjust consistency, bring to boil. Once boiling taste, add salt if required, add garam masala and stir well.

name: shegaon kachori
instructions: Add flour to bowl with salt and mix lightly, add oil and start adding water gradually to bring dry flour together. Once dry flour comes together transfer to platform and knead 10-12 minutes until dough is smooth and soft. Place dough in bowl, drizzle few drops oil and coat doughball, cover with damp cloth and rest 1 hour minimum. Add coriander, fennel and cumin seeds to mortar or small mixer grinder and grind into coarse powder. In mixer grinder jar add green chilli, garlic, ginger and fresh coriander with water and grind into thick coarse paste. Add roasted chana dal to dry clean mixer grinder and grind into fine powder, then sieve. Set deep pan over high heat, add oil and let get hot. Add coarsely ground spices, stir and cook 1 minute. Add green paste, stir and cook over high flame 2 minutes. Lower flame and add all powdered spices, give a stir and add hot water, stir well and cook spices over high flame 1 minute. Lower flame again and add kasuri methi with powdered roasted chana dal, stir ensuring no lumps, cook 3-4 minutes until mixture becomes slightly dry. Add splashes of water, stir and transfer to bowl, cool completely. Mixture should come together if pressed between hands but not completely dry. To shape kachori take portion of dough, form into bowl shape using thumb and fingers, take slightly smaller portion of filling, place in dough and seal completely. Shape kachoris using rolling pin or palms. Fry in hot oil at 180°C until crisp and golden brown. Serve hot with fried salted green chillies.

name: palak chicken
instructions: Add chicken to large bowl, cut tendons of drumsticks, add salt, lemon juice, ginger garlic paste and kashmiri red chilli powder, mix well and set aside to marinate. Bring water to roiling boil in stock pot, add salt and sugar, keep bowl of ice cold water ready. Once water boils add spinach and fresh coriander, leave 15 seconds then transfer to ice cold water using tongs. Squeeze excess water from blanched spinach and coriander, add to mixer grinder and grind into fine paste. Set large handi over high flame, add ghee and oil, let get hot. Add all whole spices and sliced onions, stir and cook over high flame until onions turn golden brown. Add green chillies and ginger garlic paste, stir and cook over high flame 1 minute. Lower flame and add all powdered spices, add splash of hot water and cook over medium to high flame 3-4 minutes. Add tomatoes with salt and cook over high flame 2-3 minutes, cover with lid and cook over medium flame until tomatoes turn mushy, stirring at intervals, adding hot water splashes if too dry. Once tomatoes soft and mushy add hot water, stir and cook over high flame until oil separates. Add chicken, stir to coat with masala and cook over high flame 4-5 minutes. Add spinach puree, stir and cook until puree changes colour and becomes dark (10-12 minutes). Add hot water to adjust gravy (not too thick or thin) and cook until chicken fully cooked. Taste and adjust seasoning. For tempering heat small pan over high flame, add ghee until hot. Add garlic and fry until light golden brown, switch off flame and add asafetida and kashmiri red chilli powder, stir quickly and pour tempering over chicken. Add ginger juliennes, lemon juice, roasted kasuri methi powder, garam masala and fresh coriander, stir well. Serve with laccha parathas.

name: besan ke laddu
instructions: Add almonds and cashews to chopper and make coarse powder, don't grind into fine powder. Set large heavy bottomed kadhai over high flame and add ghee, let melt without heating too much. Lower flame and add all besan, stir well and cook over low flame until ghee separates, keep mashing and breaking lumps of besan as you cook, mixture will keep loosening and start binding (25-30 minutes). Once ghee separates add nuts and cardamom powder, add additional ghee gradually using tablespoon, keep stirring 2-3 minutes then add next tablespoon ghee and cook 2-3 more minutes over slightly higher flame until besan turns brownish in colour.
