Immediately transfer mixture to large plate, keep stirring 2-3 minutes after transferring so mixture drops in temperature and doesn't get darker, let mixture come to room temperature. Add boora/tagar and mix well, mixture will come together as sugar absorbs all ghee, after mixing let mixture set 20-25 more minutes before shaping, then shape laddus. Garnish with pistachio powder.

name: chicken fry
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 1 tbsp
instructions: Add curry cut chicken pieces to bowl with ginger garlic green chilli paste, kashmiri red chilli powder, turmeric, lemon juice and salt, mix well so every piece evenly coated, set aside. Slice onions thin. Cut each onion in half, lay flat and slice through, once halfway drop onion and slice remaining half for even slices and finger safety. Heat kadhai on high flame, add oil and let get hot. Add bay leaves, black peppercorns, black cardamom, cinnamon and cumin seeds and let crackle. Add sliced onions and cook on high flame stirring regularly until golden brown, add curry leaves and let crackle in oil. Add marinated chicken and stir well on high flame 2 minutes. Add salt, turmeric, kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, spicy red chilli powder, garam masala, ginger garlic green chilli paste and whole garlic cloves, mix well and cook 1-2 minutes. Add splash of hot water and stir, cover and cook on lowest flame until chicken fully cooked (6-7 minutes), open once between to stir if needed. Once done remove lid, you will see thick clingy masala coating every piece of chicken, switch to high flame and stir so any remaining water dries completely. Taste and adjust salt, add garam masala, freshly crushed black pepper, chopped coriander stems, slit green chillies, lemon juice and warm ghee, mix through well. Serve as is or with rice bhakri or pav.

name: KFC Style Chicken Popcorn
instructions: Cut chicken breasts into long strips then into bite-sized cubes. Marinate chicken with salt, vinegar, ginger garlic paste, and peri peri spice mix for 10 minutes. Coat marinated chicken in seasoned flour, dip in ice-cold water, coat again in flour. Fry in 170°C oil over high flame until golden brown and crisp. Drain excess oil and sprinkle peri peri spice mix over fried chicken.

name: Nylon Khaman
instructions: Mix water, citric acid, sugar, salt and groundnut oil, whisk well. Sieve gram flour and whisk for 3-4 minutes. Rest batter 10 minutes. Add baking soda with water and whisk until puffed. Transfer to greased tray and steam 20-25 minutes. Cool to room temperature. Heat oil for tempering, add mustard seeds, green chilli, and remaining ingredients, boil 2-3 minutes. Demold khaman, cut into squares, pour tempering over it. Garnish with coriander and coconut.

name: Cheese Burst Pizza Sandwich
instructions: Make cheesy sauce: set up double boiler with processed cheese, butter and milk, whisk until smooth and creamy for 5 minutes, cool. Prepare filling: mix paneer, sweet corn, capsicum, onion, green chilli, pizza sauce, ketchup, oregano, red chilli flakes and cheese. Assemble sandwich: butter one bread slice, add even layer of filling, apply cheesy sauce on another slice leaving 1-2 cm empty, place sauced slice on filling upside down, top with third bread slice buttered on surface. Toast in hand toaster over medium-high flame until crisp and golden brown on both sides.

name: Chicken Kofta Curry
ingredients: lemon juice of half a lemon
instructions: Mince chicken finely. Squeeze water from chopped onions. Mix chicken with onions, green chillies, ginger, spices and herbs, knead for 3-4 minutes. Add ghee to mixture. Shape 30-35g portions into roundels and fry in moderately hot oil over medium-high flame until light golden. For gravy: heat oil and ghee, fry whole spices and chopped onions until light golden. Add ginger garlic paste and cook until deep golden. Add powdered spices with hot water, cook 2-3 minutes. Add curd, then tomato puree, cook covered 25-30 minutes until oil separates, stirring regularly. Add fried koftas with green chilli and ginger, adjust consistency with hot water, cook 3-4 minutes. Add cream, kasuri methi, garam masala and coriander. Serve with rotis or naans.

name: Gujarati Handvo
instructions: Wash rice and dals, soak 5-6 hours. Mix sour curd with water, grind soaked rice and dals with buttermilk into semi-fine paste. Ferment in warm place 10-12 hours. After fermentation, mix grated bottle gourd, carrot, peanuts, green chilli, ginger, coriander, asafetida, sugar, salt, turmeric powder and groundnut oil. Add water to adjust to idli batter consistency. Add baking soda to portion of batter. Heat thick-bottomed pan, add oil and temper with mustard seeds, cumin seeds, sesame seeds and curry leaves. Pour batter into pan, cover and cook over low flame 8 minutes. Flip and cook other side 4-5 minutes. Serve hot with chutney.

name: Lacchedar Rabdi
instructions: Pour milk into large heavy-bottomed kadhai. Bring to boil over high flame, stirring at regular intervals. Lower flame to medium once boiled. Wait for malai layer to form on surface. Pull malai layer towards side and stick to kadhai surface. Repeat this process without stirring milk until reduced to 1/3 quantity. Add sugar and stir to dissolve. Scrape malai from sides and add into milk, avoiding browned parts. Mix malai gently and cook 2-3 minutes over high flame. Transfer to bowl and refrigerate. Serve chilled, garnished with nuts and rose petals.

name: Veg Pan Fried Noodles
ingredients: spring onion white handful roughly chopped
hot water or vegetable stock as required
instructions: Boil salted water, add noodles and boil until separated, cover and rest 2 minutes. Drain and rinse with cold water, coat with oil. Heat wok, add oil for frying, spread noodles to cover surface. Fry over high flame until light brown and crisp on one side, flip and repeat. Transfer fried noodles to plate. For sauce: heat wok with oil, add garlic, ginger and green chilli, cook 30 seconds. Add spring onion whites and all vegetables, stir-fry 2-3 minutes over high flame. Add hot water/stock, both soy sauces, oyster sauce, sugar, white pepper, vinegar and salt. Make cornstarch slurry and stir into sauce to thicken. Adjust seasoning and add spring onion greens. Serve fried noodles with hot gravy poured over, mix well.

name: Masala Milk
instructions: Set a pan on high heat and lower flame. Add almonds, cashew, and pistachio; dry roast over low heat for a couple minutes until golden brown, stirring constantly. Transfer to bowl and cool completely. In a chopper or mixer grinder, add roasted nuts, green cardamom seeds only, black pepper powder, ginger powder, nutmeg, saffron, and turmeric powder; grind into coarse powder. Store in airtight container and refrigerate. For the drink: Set a large thick bottomed vessel and add milk; bring to boil on high heat, stirring regularly. Lower heat to medium/low and add 4 tbsp masala milk powder; stir well and cook for 8-10 minutes until milk thickens. Add sugar and stir well; simmer for 2 minutes. Serve hot or chill for cold version.

name: Matar Paneer Pulao
instructions: Wash basmati rice gently 2-3 times until water runs clear; soak for 25-30 minutes. Coat paneer pieces with corn flour in a large bowl. Heat oil in pan over high heat; shallow fry paneer until golden brown on both sides; transfer to sieve and set aside. Set pressure cooker over high flame; add oil with ghee and let ghee get hot. Add all whole spices, green chilli; stir and cook over low flame for 1 minute. Add onions; cook over high flame until translucent. Add halved cashews; continue cooking until onions and cashews turn slightly golden brown. Add green peas; stir well and cook over high flame for 1 minute. Discard water from soaked rice; add gently to cooker and stir very gently. Add water with salt, lemon juice, and mint leaves; stir well and check seasoning. Place fried paneer pieces on top; cover with lid. Cook over high flame for just one whistle; switch off flame and let cooker depressurize naturally. Open lid and gently stir pulao using spatula, bringing rice from bottom to top to release excess steam. Serve hot.

name: Lauki Ki Sabzi
instructions: Chop onions and tomatoes; peel and dice bottle gourd. Set cooker over high flame; add ghee and let it get hot. Add onions, tomatoes, and bottle gourd with salt, turmeric powder, red chilli powder; stir well and put lid on. Cook over high flame for one whistle; lower flame to medium and cook for 3-4 whistles; set flame to low and cook for 8-10 minutes. Switch off flame and let cooker depressurize naturally. Switch on flame to high again; cook while stirring until all water evaporates and ghee separates; keep mashing the veggies until consistency is like a bharta. Taste and add salt if required; add black pepper powder, garam masala, fresh coriander, and stir well. Serve hot.

name: Mushroom Chilli
instructions: Cut bell peppers and onion into petals and set aside. Wash mushrooms to remove dirt; pat dry using kitchen towel. Trim stem from bottom and cut mushrooms into halves if medium, leave small ones whole. Transfer cut mushrooms into boiling water and cook for 30 seconds only; immediately transfer and submerge in ice cold water. Heat oil to 180C. Transfer blanched mushrooms to large bowl; add 3 tbsp each corn flour and refined flour; mix well to coat. Add remaining 2 tbsp each corn flour and refined flour; mix well and coat again. Add mushrooms into hot oil and fry for a few minutes until coating holds; transfer to sieve and keep oil hot for double frying. Set wok over high heat; add oil and let it get hot. Add garlic, ginger, green chillies, and spring onion bulbs; stir well and cook over high heat for 1 minute. Add dark soy sauce, light soy sauce with splash of water, vinegar, sugar, white pepper powder, and salt; stir well and bring to boil. Make slurry by mixing corn flour with water; add gradually to sauce while mixing continuously to thicken. Lower flame and second fry mushrooms until light golden brown and crisp. Immediately add fried mushrooms to sauce with veggies, spring onion greens, and fresh coriander; coat mushrooms with sauce. Serve hot.

name: Eggless Chocolate Chip Cookies
instructions: Add melted butter, caster sugar, and light brown sugar into large bowl; mix until combined. In separate bowl, whisk water, oil, and baking powder; add to butter and sugar mixture with vanilla essence; whisk well until combined. This mixture substitutes for egg. Sieve in refined flour, corn flour, baking soda, and salt; mix well using spatula until all ingredients combine. Roughly chop and add dark chocolate chunks into cookie dough; mix well. Portion out dough using scooper or spoon into 60 gram dough balls; adjust size as preferred. Cover shaped dough balls with cling film and set in fridge for 45 minutes to 1 hour. Preheat oven to 175C. Place chilled dough balls onto butter paper lined baking tray; space 2-3 inches apart as they will spread. Bake for 10-12 minutes. Remove tray from oven; sprinkle flakey salt and let cookies cool for 8-10 minutes; do not move while hot or they will collapse. Serve.

name: Egg Pepper Fry
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 1 tbsp
instructions: Set small pan over high heat; once hot, lower flame and add fennel seeds, cumin seeds, and black peppercorns; keep stirring and dry roast over low flame until cumin seeds turn light brown. Transfer to bowl; cool completely and grind into fine powder. Set pan over high heat; add oil and let it get hot. Add cumin and fennel seeds, garlic, and curry leaves; stir well and cook until garlic turns slightly golden brown. Add onions; stir and cook over high heat until onions turn light golden brown. Add ginger garlic and green chilli paste; stir and cook for 1-2 minutes. Add hot water and stir; add spice powder, then add more hot water to adjust consistency. Add salt to taste and cook over high flame until oil separates. Taste and adjust seasoning if required; pepper fry masala is ready. Set tawa/pan over high flame; once hot, add oil and let it heat up. Place boiled and halved eggs in pan; add salt, turmeric powder, and black pepper powder; fry for 1-2 minutes on one side then flip and fry similarly on other side. Lower flame and add pepper fry masala on top of eggs evenly with curry leaves, fresh coriander, lemon juice, and green chillies. Flip eggs carefully to coat with masala, then flip again and add more fresh coriander. Serve hot with Malabar parotta.

name: mooli ka paratha
instructions: Make dough: combine wheat flour, salt, water, and oil; knead until soft; rest 30 minutes. Make filling: grate radish, squeeze excess water, mix with ginger, green chilli, coriander, spice powders. Shape: form dough ball into cup shape, fill with radish mixture, seal edges, roll into semi-thin disc. Cook: heat tawa with ghee, place paratha, press while rotating, cook both sides until golden. Serve hot with butter, pickle, and curd.

name: matar kachori
instructions: Make dough: mix flour, salt, carom seeds, ghee; rub for shortening; add water gradually and knead using stretch-fold method; rest 2 hours. Make filling: dry roast cumin, coriander, fennel, peppercorns; grind to powder. Grind green chilli, ginger, coriander into paste. Heat ghee, cook peas 3-4 minutes, add roasted spices, green paste, salt, sugar, dry mango powder; mash and cool. Shape: form dough cup, fill generously, seal, flatten with palms. Fry: heat oil to 160°C, fry kachoris until golden, drain on sieve. Serve with tamarind and green chutney.

name: paneer tikka dum biryani
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 1 tbsp
instructions: Prepare marinade: mix mustard oil, spice powders, ginger-garlic paste, hung curd, lemon juice, salt. Add cubed paneer, coat well, smoke with charcoal over ghee for 3-4 mins. Soak rice 45 mins. Cook paneer: heat tawa with ghee, cook paneer pieces until light golden both sides. Cook veggies in leftover marinade briefly, keep crunchy. Boil water with spices, add pre-soaked rice, cook 4-5 mins (80-85% done). Layer: spread half-cooked rice on paneer-gravy base, add coriander-mint, then remaining rice, saffron water, ghee. Cover with muslin cloth, place tawa under, weight lid. Dum cook on high 10 mins, then low 10-15 mins. Rest 30 mins before opening.

name: sweet corn soup
instructions: Boil sweet corn 4-5 mins, divide into two portions. Grind one portion into puree with water. Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces, coat with salt, black pepper, vinegar, 1 tsp corn flour. Whisk 2 eggs, add 2 spoonfuls to chicken. Heat oil, cook chicken 3-4 mins on high, transfer to bowl. Add corn puree to wok with hot water, bring to boil. Skim scum until clear. Add salt, sugar, white pepper, soy sauce, vinegar, remaining boiled corn; boil 1-2 mins. Mix 2 tbsp corn flour with water, add slurry to thicken while stirring. Pour remaining whisked eggs in thin stream, stir gently. Add cooked chicken and spring onion greens, cook 1 min. Taste and adjust seasoning.

name: schezwan paneer dry
ingredients: kashmiri red chilli 20 nos deseeded soaked
spring onion whites 4 nos roughly chopped
instructions: Make red chilli paste: deseed and soak kashmiri red chillies in hot water 1-2 hours, grind with water to fine paste. Cook chilli paste: heat oil, add red chilli paste, cook on medium-high 4-5 mins until crumbly and oil separates, add vinegar, cool and store. Coat and fry paneer: dust cut paneer pieces with corn flour using sieve, toss gently (add flour in batches), fry at 175°C until slightly golden, drain on sieve. Make sauce: heat wok with oil, add ginger, garlic, coriander stems, spring onion whites, green chillies; cook 1 min on high. Lower flame, add red chilli paste, red chilli sauce, ketchup, soy sauce, vinegar; cook 1-2 mins. Add hot water, white pepper, sugar, salt. Make corn flour slurry, add gradually while stirring to thicken. Add veggies and fried paneer with spring onion greens; toss gently. Serve hot.

name: mango methi dana pickle
instructions: Chop raw mango into bite-sized pieces. Mix chopped mango with salt and let rest. In a large jar or container, layer raw mango pieces alternating with salt, mustard oil, turmeric, fennel powder, kashmiri red chilli powder, and asafoetida. Add vinegar and mix well. Store in airtight container for 2-3 days before serving to allow flavours to develop. Serve as condiment with meals.

name: chili baby corn
instructions: Rinse and pat dry baby corn, cut into bite-sized pieces. Boil baby corn in salted water 5-7 mins, drain well. Make coating: mix 1/4 cup corn flour, 1/4 cup refined flour, 1/2 tsp salt. Coat boiled baby corn with flour mixture, add water gradually until well coated. Heat oil in pan, fry coated baby corn over medium heat until light golden brown, drain on paper. In same pan, add 1 tbsp fresh oil, sauté green chilli, ginger, coriander stem 1 min. Add chopped capsicum, sauté 2-3 mins. Add red chilli powder, black pepper, soy sauce, white vinegar, tomato ketchup, 1/4 tsp salt; mix well. Make corn flour slurry with 1/4 cup water, add to pan, mix until sauce thickens. Add fried baby corn to pan, mix well, cook 2-3 mins. Garnish with coriander leaves, serve hot.

name: Gajar Gobhi Shalgam Sweet Pickle
instructions: Cut carrot, cauliflower, and turnip into small pieces. Heat mustard oil in a large pan and add crushed black cardamom and cloves. Once they release aroma, add cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds, fennel seeds, and mustard seeds. Let seeds crackle and splutter. Add grated ginger and asafoetida. Add all chopped vegetables and stir well. Add salt, black pepper, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, and black salt. Mix thoroughly. Pour in vinegar and cook vegetables on medium heat for 5-7 minutes until they soften slightly. Add jaggery and raisins, stir to combine evenly. Cool completely and store in sterilized glass jars.

name: Masala Gur
instructions: Roast jaggery in a heavy-bottomed pan on low heat. Separately, dry roast carom seeds and nutmeg powder for fragrance. Add ghee to the jaggery and stir constantly. Once jaggery reaches soft-ball consistency (check with water drop), add the dry roasted spices, black pepper, roasted peanuts, almond flakes, and grated coconut. Mix thoroughly. Pour onto a greased surface and allow to cool. Once set, cut into bite-sized pieces and store in an airtight container.

name: Mirchi Bonda
instructions: Boil and peel potatoes. Heat mustard oil and add cumin seeds, asafoetida. Add ginger, chopped green chilli, coriander powder, turmeric powder, salt, red chilli powder, and dry mango powder to potatoes. Mix well and add fresh coriander leaves. Roll into small balls and refrigerate. Make a thick batter with gram flour, turmeric, carom seeds, red chilli, salt, and baking soda. Carefully slit large green peppers lengthwise without cutting completely. Stuff each pepper with the cooled potato filling. Dip each stuffed pepper in gram flour batter, ensuring full coverage. Heat oil and deep fry on medium heat until golden brown. Drain on paper towels and serve hot with chutney.

name: Matar Masala Poori
instructions: Make soft dough with wheat flour, salt, carom seeds, and 2 tsp oil. Cover and rest for 15 minutes. For filling, heat 2 tbsp oil and add cumin seeds. Add chopped ginger and green chilli, cook briefly. Add green peas and cook until tender. Season with turmeric, coriander powder, fennel powder, dry mango powder, red chilli, salt, and garam masala. Cool the filling completely. Divide dough into equal balls. Roll each ball into a thin disc. Place filling in center, fold edges to seal, and gently roll out while keeping filling intact. Heat oil for deep frying. Fry poori on medium heat until puffed and golden on both sides. Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

name: Jowar Laddu
instructions: Roast sorghum flour in a heavy pan with 1 tbsp ghee on medium heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring constantly until golden and fragrant. Add remaining ghee to the roasted flour. Add malai and mix thoroughly. Add desiccated coconut and almond flakes. Mix well. Add powdered sugar and crushed green cardamom seeds. Mix until well combined. Allow to cool for 5 minutes. Take small portions and roll between palms to form smooth balls. Store in an airtight container in a cool place. Best consumed within 2 weeks.

name: Til Mawa Rolls
instructions: Dry roast sesame seeds in a pan on low heat for 2-3 minutes until fragrant and nutty. Crush cardamom pods to extract seeds and powder finely. In a bowl, combine crumbled mawa, roasted sesame seeds, powdered sugar, and powdered cardamom. Add 1 tbsp ghee and mix thoroughly until well combined. Divide mixture into equal portions. Roll each portion into a cylindrical log on a clean surface. Chop pistachio, almond, and cashew finely. Mix them together. Roll each log in the nut mixture to coat generously on all sides. Cut into 2-inch pieces. Store in an airtight container in a cool place.

name: Matar Daliya Pulao
instructions: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot. Add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add broken wheat and roast for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally until light golden. Add fresh green peas, chopped green chilli, and grated ginger. Stir well. Sprinkle turmeric powder and add salt. Pour in water and bring to a boil. Cover the pot with a tight lid, reduce heat to low, and cook for 15-20 minutes until broken wheat is completely cooked and water is absorbed. Do not stir during cooking. Once done, gently fluff with a fork and serve hot.

name: Paneer Manchurian
instructions: Cut paneer into cubes. Mix corn flour, refined flour, ginger, salt, red chilli powder, garam masala, and soya sauce. Coat paneer pieces and deep fry until golden. For gravy: heat oil, add ginger, green chilli, capsicum, cabbage, coriander, tomato sauce, soya sauce, white vinegar, black pepper, red chilli powder mixed with corn flour and water. Cook until thickened. Add fried paneer pieces to gravy and serve hot.

name: Aloo Sooji Tikki and Dahi Bhalla Chaat
instructions: Boil and mash potatoes. Mix with semolina, cumin seeds, green chilli, ginger, peas, turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli, amchur, garam masala, salt, and oil. Form into patties and deep fry until golden and crispy. Serve tikkis topped with yogurt, sweet and spiced chutneys, boondi, roasted cumin powder, and black salt.

name: Rajasthani Masala Tikkar with Mixed Dal
instructions: Mix wheat flour, semolina, cumin seeds, carom seeds, asafoetida, ghee, and salt. Knead into dough and deep fry into crispy tikkar pieces. Soak mixed dals in water. Cook dals with tomato, green chilli, ginger, turmeric, coriander powder, kashmiri chilli until soft. Season with salt. Prepare tadka with ghee, cumin seeds, dry red chilli, kashmiri chilli. Pour tadka over dal and serve with tikkar.

name: Multigrain Millet Methi Paratha
instructions: Mix all flour types with fenugreek leaves, cumin seeds, asafoetida, ginger-chilli paste, spices, and salt. Add yogurt to form dough. Knead well and let rest. Roll dough portions on floured surface into thin circles. Cook on hot griddle, brushing ghee on both sides until golden spots appear. Serve with yogurt, boondi, chutneys, and roasted cumin powder.

name: Orange Candy
instructions: Combine orange juice and sugar in a heavy-bottomed pan. Heat over medium-high heat, stirring until sugar dissolves. Once boiling, stop stirring and cook until mixture reaches hard-candy stage (300-310F). Add orange food colour if desired. Pour onto parchment paper. Let cool completely until hardened. Break into pieces and store in airtight container.

name: Orange Jam
instructions: Wash and cut oranges into quarters. Remove seeds and core. Finely chop or mince orange pieces. Combine with sugar and cinnamon sticks in a heavy-bottomed pan. Heat over medium flame, stirring occasionally, until mixture comes to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 45-60 minutes, stirring frequently, until mixture thickens and reaches jam consistency. Add orange food colour if desired. Cool slightly and transfer to sterilized jars. Refrigerate after opening.

name: Amla Jam with Jaggery
instructions: Wash and remove seeds from amlas without breaking. Cut into pieces if desired. Combine amla with choice of sugar or jaggery, cinnamon sticks, and green cardamom pods. Add lemon juice. Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until amla softens and mixture thickens to jam consistency, about 45-60 minutes. Remove cardamom pods and cinnamon sticks. Cool and transfer to sterilized jars. Store in cool, dry place.

name: Besan Cheela Sabji
instructions: Make besan batter: combine gram flour, salt, grated ginger, chopped green chilli, coriander leaves, and water to make pouring-consistency batter. Heat non-stick pan, lightly grease with oil. Pour batter and spread evenly, cook 2-3 minutes until set. Drizzle oil around edges and flip, cook 1 minute more until lightly browned. Remove and cut into pieces. Make curry: heat oil in pan, add cumin seeds. Add tomato-ginger-chilli paste, cook 2-3 minutes. Add turmeric, coriander powder, asafoetida, kashmiri chilli, fenugreek leaves. Cook until oil separates. Add yogurt slowly, mix well. Cook 3-4 minutes. Add water, ghee, salt, garam masala. Bring to boil. Add cheela pieces and simmer 2-3 minutes. Garnish with coriander and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Pearl Millet Flour Toast
instructions: Mix pearl millet flour with curd, salt, green chilli, ginger, capsicum, carrots, and coriander leaves. Add eno powder and mix well. Heat ghee on a tava/griddle, spread the mixture and cook until golden and crispy on both sides. Serve hot.

name: Dry Fruit Laddu
instructions: Dry roast pumpkin seeds, melon seeds, walnuts, and cashews separately. Grind dates into paste. Mix all seeds, nuts, and raisins with date paste. Add ghee and mix well. Shape into balls. Serve cold.

name: Layered Palak Paneer
instructions: Blanch and puree spinach. Cook tomatoes and blend. Layer spinach puree, tomato puree, and paneer chunks in a baking dish. Season each layer with spices, malai, and cheese. Bake until layered and cooked through. Garnish with chilli flakes and coriander leaves.

name: Green Moong Bedmi Puri
instructions: Soak green moong for 2 hours. Grind coarsely and mix with wheat flour, spices, ginger, and herbs. Knead to stiff dough. Rest 30 minutes. Roll small puris and deep fry until golden. For aloo bhaji: cumin seeds, cooked potatoes, tomatoes, and spices. Serve hot puri with aloo bhaji.

name: Ragi Laddu
instructions: Dry roast ragi flour with 1/4 cup ghee until fragrant. Roast sesame seeds and peanuts separately. Powder jaggery. Mix roasted ragi, sesame, peanuts, jaggery powder, and spices. Add remaining ghee if needed. Shape into balls. Cool and store.

name: Methi Bedmi Poori
instructions: Mix semolina, wheat flour, chickpea flour, and salt. Add spices, cumin, carom seeds, and asafoetida. Chop and add fresh fenugreek leaves. Knead with 4 tbsp oil and water to stiff dough. Rest 20 minutes. Roll small puris and deep fry until puffed and golden. Serve hot.

name: Farali Dahi Vada & Masala Vada
instructions: Boil and mash raw bananas. Mix with rock salt, spices, green chilli, coriander. Form into vada shapes. For dahi vada: deep fry and soak in curd yogurt. For masala vada: add peanut powder to mixture before frying. Serve with chutney, curt, and pomegranate garnish.

name: rice flour kachori
instructions: Make dough by mixing rice flour, 1 tsp oil, and salt. Knead until smooth. Prepare filling: boil and cube potatoes, mix with peas, carrot, capsicum, cabbage, spices, tomato sauce, and coriander leaves. For each kachori: roll dough, stuff with filling, shape into round, and bake or shallow-fry with oil until golden brown.

name: urad dal halwa
instructions: Roast urad dal in ghee until golden. Add poppy seeds and roast briefly. Pour milk and bring to boil. Add sugar, coconut, almonds, cashews, and cardamom. Cook on medium heat, stirring continuously until halwa thickens and ghee separates from the mixture (about 20-30 minutes). Serve warm.

name: methi aloo
instructions: Cube boiled potatoes. Blanch and chop fenugreek leaves to remove bitterness. Heat mustard oil and add cumin seeds and asafoetida. Add ginger-chilli paste and red chillies, sauté briefly. Add tomato paste and cook until oil separates. Add cubed potatoes, turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, salt, and kasuri methi. Mix gently and cook on low heat for 5-7 minutes. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot with roti.

name: bathua masala paratha
instructions: Blanch and finely chop bathua leaves. Make dough: mix wheat flour, salt, 2 tsp oil, and knead with water to form soft dough. For filling: mix mashed potatoes, chopped bathua, cumin seeds, asafoetida, green chilli, ginger, turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander leaves, and dry mango powder. Roll dough into round, place filling in center, seal and roll out into paratha. Cook on griddle with ghee until golden brown on both sides. Serve hot with butter or pickle.

name: coconut laddu
instructions: Boil milk and add desiccated coconut. Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally until milk evaporates and mixture becomes dense and thick (15-20 minutes). Add sugar and cardamom powder, mix well. Cook for another 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly. Spread mixture on a plate to cool slightly. When cool enough to handle, roll into balls (laddus) with wet hands. Cool completely before storing in an airtight container.

name: cheesy potato bread rolls
instructions: Boil and mash potatoes finely. Mix with salt, chilli flakes, black pepper, coriander powder, dry mango powder, chaat masala, oregano, ginger, green chilli, capsicum, coriander leaves, and grated cheese. Make balls from the mixture. Remove crusts from bread slices and flatten with a rolling pin. Wrap each flattened bread around a potato-cheese ball, seal edges with water. Shallow or deep fry in oil until golden brown and crispy. Serve hot with ketchup, mayo, or any sauce.

name: Vettu Cake
instructions: Mix refined flour, semolina, powdered sugar, baking powder, and powdered green cardamom. Add ghee and rub until crumbly. Gradually add milk and knead into smooth soft dough. Rest for 10-15 minutes. Roll dough into log shape, cut into 1-inch pieces, and make a cross-cut in the middle of each piece. Heat oil and fry at low-medium heat until golden brown and crispy. Remove and place on paper towel to remove excess oil.

name: Orange Halwa
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan over medium heat. Add corn flour and roast until fragrant and light golden, stirring continuously. Gradually add orange juice while stirring to avoid lumps. Keep stirring constantly as the mixture thickens. Add powdered sugar and orange food colour, mix well. Continue cooking until the mixture leaves the sides of the pan and becomes a thick, glossy halwa. Garnish with pistachio flakes and chopped cashews. Serve warm.

name: Amla Pickle
instructions: Wash and prick whole amlas with a fork. In a bowl, combine fennel seeds, fenugreek seeds, mustard seeds, black pepper, cloves, turmeric powder, carom seeds, kalonji, asafoetida, and red chilli powder. Heat mustard oil until it starts to smoke. Pour hot oil into the spice mixture. Add salt and vinegar. Mix well. Place amlas in a glass jar and pour the spiced oil over them. Seal and let it cure for 7-10 days before consuming. Shake the jar occasionally.

name: Shahi Navratan Vegetable Kurma
instructions: Soak cashews in warm water for 10 minutes. In a pan, heat ghee and roast the whole spices (bayleaf, cinnamon, mace, cardamom, cloves, black pepper) until fragrant. Grind soaked cashews with yogurt to make a smooth paste. Add the cashew paste to the pan and cook for 2 minutes. Add ginger, green chilli, coriander powder. Cut paneer and all vegetables into uniform pieces. Add vegetables to the curry and cook for 8-10 minutes until tender. Stir in cream, dried fenugreek, tomato, sugar, and salt. Cook for another 5 minutes. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve.

name: Sweet Khakhra
instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine wheat flour, desiccated coconut, crushed cardamom, and sugar powder. Mix well. Add ghee and mix with fingers until the mixture becomes crumbly and resembles breadcrumbs. Gradually add water (as needed) and knead into a soft dough. Divide into small portions and roll each very thin between two sheets of parchment paper. Carefully transfer to a griddle or tawa and cook on medium heat until light golden brown spots appear on both sides, turning gently. Sprinkle sesame seeds while cooking if desired. Cool completely before storing.

name: Papaya Halwa
instructions: Peel the raw papaya and grate it finely. Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan and add grated papaya. Fry for 3-4 minutes. Add milk and bring to boil. Lower heat and simmer, stirring frequently, until the mixture becomes thick and the milk is mostly absorbed (about 25-30 minutes). Add sugar and crushed cardamom, mix well. Continue cooking for another 5 minutes until the halwa thickens further and pulls away from the sides of the pan. In a separate pan, heat 1 tbsp ghee and fry almond flakes, cashew pieces, and raisins until golden. Pour the fried nuts and ghee into the halwa and mix well. Transfer to a serving dish and serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Saag Vadi
instructions: Chop spinach, bathua, fenugreek and coriander leaves finely. Mix with besan and rice flour. Chop ginger and green chilli, add to mixture. Add all spices and salt. Mix well, adding minimal water to form stiff dough. Shape into small balls or patties. Deep fry in hot oil until golden brown and crispy. Serve hot with chutney or as breakfast snack.

name: Sooji Milk Peetha
instructions: Roast semolina in ghee over medium heat until fragrant and light golden, about 3-4 minutes. Add 400 ml milk and cook, stirring constantly until mixture thickens into a paste. Let cool slightly and shape into small balls about 1 inch diameter. In a heavy-bottomed pan, bring 1 litre milk to boil with sugar. Add cardamom powder and saffron strands. Gently drop semolina balls into boiling milk. Once they float, reduce heat and simmer for 2-3 minutes. Serve warm, garnished with almond flakes.

name: Jackfruit Biryani
instructions: Cut jackfruit into pieces. Mix yogurt with spices. Marinate jackfruit in yogurt-spice mixture for 15-20 minutes. In a heavy pot, heat oil and ghee, add cumin seeds and whole spices. Fry jackfruit pieces until edges are golden, about 3-4 minutes. In another pot, boil salted water with whole spices. Add rice and cook until 60-70% done. Layer cooked jackfruit at the bottom of a heavy biryani pot. Spread half-cooked rice over jackfruit. Sprinkle mint, cilantro, saffron soaked in milk. Cover with foil and lid. Cook on high heat for 2-3 minutes until steam forms, then reduce to low heat and cook for 15-20 minutes. Turn off heat and let rest for 5 minutes. Fluff gently with fork before serving.

name: Sorghum Flour Khasta Snacks
instructions: Boil potatoes and mash well. Chop spinach, capsicum, green chilli and ginger finely. Mix mashed potato with chopped vegetables and fenugreek leaves. Add sorghum flour and all spices. Mix 2 tbsp oil into mixture and knead into stiff dough using minimal water. Let rest for 5 minutes. Shape dough into desired forms like patties or balls. Deep fry in hot oil over medium-high heat until golden brown and crispy on all sides. Drain on paper towels. Serve hot with chutney or as tea-time snack.

name: Soya Peas Pulao
instructions: Soak soya nuggets in warm water for 5 minutes and drain. Rinse rice under running water. Heat oil and ghee in pressure cooker, add whole spices. Add cumin seeds and allow to crackle. Add ginger and green chilli, fry for 30 seconds. Add rice and stir-fry for 2 minutes. Add tomato and yogurt, stir well and cook for 2-3 minutes until mixture becomes paste-like. Add turmeric and red chilli powder. Add drained soya nuggets, green peas and salt. Pour 2 cups water. Stir well. Close pressure cooker lid. Cook on high heat until first whistle, then reduce to low heat. Cook for 1 whistle. Turn off heat and let pressure release naturally for 2-3 minutes. Open lid, fluff pulao gently with fork. Serve hot with pickle or raita.

name: Eggless Chocolate Cake
instructions: Mix milk with vinegar and let sit for 5 minutes to curdle. In a bowl, whisk together olive oil and brown sugar until combined. Add curdled milk mixture to oil-sugar mixture and whisk well. Sift together refined flour, cocoa powder, baking powder and baking soda in another bowl. Gently fold dry ingredients into wet ingredients until just combined. Pour batter into greased and floured 8-inch pan. Cover pan with lid or aluminum foil. Place on stovetop over low-medium heat. Cook for 15-20 minutes until a toothpick inserted in center comes out with few moist crumbs. Remove from heat and let cool. For chocolate spread: whisk together sugar powder, cocoa powder, milk and olive oil until smooth. Spread over cooled cake. Top with chocolate chips. Slice and serve.

name: Mushroom Soya Curry
instructions: Soak soya vadi in hot water for 5 minutes and drain. Clean and slice mushrooms. Soak cashews in hot water for 10 minutes. Heat oil and ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan. Add whole spices and bay leaves. When spices crackle, add ginger and green chilli, fry for 30 seconds. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft and mushy, about 5-7 minutes. Grind cashews with 1/4 cup water to make paste. Add tomato-spice mixture to a blender or use hand blender to make smooth paste. Return paste to pan. Add turmeric, coriander and red chilli powders. Add soya vadi pieces and mushrooms. Add 1 cup water and salt. Simmer on medium heat for 7-8 minutes until mushrooms are cooked and curry thickens. Add cashew paste and mix well. Add cream and dried fenugreek leaves. Sprinkle garam masala. Simmer for 2 more minutes. Garnish with fresh cilantro. Serve hot with rice, roti or naan.

name: vegetable burritos
ingredients: nutralite activ plant based buttery spread
instructions: Prepare fresh vegetables by chopping. Warm tortillas or burrito wraps. Spread Nutralite plant-based buttery spread and veg mayo on the tortillas, add herbs for flavor. Fill with fresh mixed vegetables, distributing evenly. Roll tightly and serve warm.

name: Italian Vegetable Casserole
instructions: Chop mixed vegetables including bell peppers, zucchini, broccoli, and tomatoes. Layer vegetables in a casserole dish with nutralite butter spread and cheesy garlic mayo. Season with salt, pepper, and italian herbs. Top with cheese and bake until vegetables are tender and top is golden brown.

name: Dum Aloo
instructions: Boil potatoes in pressure cooker with plenty of water and salt for 1 whistle, switch off and let cool naturally. Peel cooled potatoes and poke holes with fork or toothpick. Heat mustard oil in kadhai until smoking point, lower flame and cool slightly. Add potatoes and fry without stirring initially until golden on bottom, then stir and fry on medium-high until golden and crispy on all sides. Transfer to sieve to cool, then poke potatoes again with fork. Pound black cardamom, green cardamom and cinnamon stick slightly to break open pods and stick. Remove some oil from kadhai and heat fresh oil. Add cumin seeds, bay leaf, cloves and crushed spices, mix kashmiri chilli powder with hot water into paste and add. Stir and cook 1-2 minutes on low heat. Add asafoetida, fennel powder and ginger powder, cook 3 minutes on medium heat. Add fried potatoes with salt and stir well, cook 3-4 minutes on medium-high. Add hot water until potatoes are covered and bring to boil. Cover with lid and place weight on top (or use foil), cook on low heat for 20-25 minutes. Remove lid, adjust gravy consistency as preferred, taste and adjust seasoning. Serve hot with rice.

name: Sevai Upma
instructions: Roast vermicelli lightly in oil until golden. In the same pan, heat remaining oil and add cumin seeds and mustard seeds. Add curry leaves and green chilli. Add finely chopped onion and sauté. Add all diced vegetables and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add roasted vermicelli and mix well. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder and garam masala. Pour water and bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer until vermicelli is cooked and water is absorbed. Serve hot.

name: Wheat Bread
instructions: Mix whole wheat flour, salt, sugar and yeast in a bowl. Add oil and warm water gradually while kneading to form a soft dough. Knead for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Place in greased bowl, cover and let rise for 1-2 hours until doubled. Punch down dough, shape into a loaf and place in greased bread pan. Cover and proof for 45 minutes. Brush with butter. Bake in preheated oven at 180°C for 35-40 minutes until golden brown. Cool before slicing.

name: Moong Dal Pakoda Sabzi
instructions: Grind soaked moong dal with green chilli, 14-15 garlic cloves and salt to form a paste. Heat 4 tbsp oil and fry the dal mixture in batches until golden, then set aside. Grind onion, tomato, 12 garlic cloves and ginger into a paste. Heat remaining 4 tbsp oil and add cumin seeds, bay leaves and asafoetida. Add the onion-tomato paste and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add all powdered spices and cook. Add curd and mix well. Add water and bring to boil. Add the fried dal pakodas. Simmer on low flame for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Poha Cutlet
instructions: Mix poha with grated beetroot, capsicum, onion and carrot in a bowl. Add chilli flakes, oregano and salt to taste and mix well. Form mixture into cutlet shapes with your hands. Heat oil in a pan and shallow fry the cutlets on both sides until golden brown and crispy. Serve hot with green chutney or ketchup.

name: Sattu Laddoo
instructions: Heat ghee in a large pan and add chana sattu. Toast for 14-15 minutes on medium flame, stirring constantly. Add grated almonds and cashews and cook for 2-3 minutes. Remove from heat and add cardamom powder. Add grated jaggery and mix well. Chop cashews, almonds, walnuts and seeds and add to the mixture. Mix well and let cool for 5-10 minutes. Oil your palms lightly and form laddoos of desired size from the mixture. Store in an airtight container.

name: Louki Chana Dal
instructions: Soak and cook chana dal until tender and set aside. Heat mustard oil in a pan and add bay leaves and black cardamom. Add cumin seeds and asafoetida. Grind ginger, garlic, green chilli and onion into a paste and add to the pan. Cook for 3-4 minutes. Add all powdered spices. Dice louki into chunks and add to the pan along with chopped tomatoes. Cook for 8-10 minutes. Add cooked chana dal and mix well. Add water as needed and simmer for 15 minutes until louki is tender. Finish with ghee and garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Oats Poha
instructions: Heat oil and add mustard seeds and cumin seeds. Add curry leaves for few seconds. Add green chilli and onion, fry for 2 minutes. Add carrot, capsicum and french beans, fry for 2-3 minutes. Sprinkle turmeric powder. Add oats and mix well. Sprinkle water over the mixture and cook on low flame for 3-4 minutes. Add lemon juice and turn off flame. Garnish with coriander leaves. Serve hot.

name: Chinese Bhel
instructions: Boil noodles for 1-1.5 minutes, drain and rinse. Toss with oil and corn flour. Deep fry noodles at 175°C until golden, break into pieces. Heat wok, add oil, cook garlic, ginger, green chilli, onion for 1 minute. Add cabbage, carrot, capsicum, spring onion, salt, pepper, cook 30 seconds on high heat. Add schezwan sauce, red chilli sauce, ketchup, soy sauce and stir. Add fried noodles, coriander, toss well and serve immediately.

name: Aloo Pyaz Sabzi
instructions: Cut potatoes into chunks and onions into slices. Heat oil and add cumin and mustard seeds. Add onions and fry until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for a minute. Add potatoes and mix well. Add turmeric, chilli powder and coriander powder. Cook covered on medium flame for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until potatoes are soft. Add garam masala and salt to taste. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve.

name: Masala Kathal
instructions: Heat oil and temper with mustard and cumin seeds. Add chopped onions and fry until brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 2 minutes. Add green chilli and tomatoes, cook until soft. Add turmeric, red chilli and coriander powder. Add jackfruit pieces and mix well. Add water and cook on medium flame for 25-30 minutes until jackfruit is tender and flavors are absorbed. Finish with garam masala. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Wheat Flour Samosa
instructions: Make dough with wheat flour, carom seeds, salt, ghee and water. Let rest for 15 minutes. Dry roast cumin, coriander seeds and black pepper, then powder. Heat 2 tbsp oil, add 1 tsp cumin seeds. Add crushed green chilli and boiled potato pieces. Mix in spice powder, turmeric, chilli, garam masala and amchur. Add coriander leaves. Roll dough into thin sheets, cut triangles, fill with potato mixture and seal edges. Deep fry until golden brown. Serve hot.

name: street style chatpata burger
instructions: Boil potatoes and mash them. Mix mashed potatoes with finely chopped onion, tomato, green chilli, coriander leaves, pav bhaji masala, turmeric powder, and red chilli powder. Heat oil and butter on a tawa. Add the potato mixture and cook until golden and heated through, about 5-7 minutes. Toast burger buns lightly on the tawa. Assemble burgers by placing the potato filling on the bun with additional onion and tomato slices. Serve hot.

name: punjabi bharwa karela
instructions: Wash karela and make lengthwise slits using a small knife, being careful not to cut completely through. Finely chop onions, green chilli, garlic, and ginger. Dry roast coriander seeds and cumin seeds, then grind. Mix onion, green chilli, garlic, ginger, ground spices, coriander powder, garam masala, anardana powder, dry mango powder, coriander leaves, and salt. Carefully stuff each karela piece with this mixture. Heat mustard oil in a heavy-bottomed pan. Add stuffed karela and cook on medium heat with lid covered, stirring occasionally, for 30-40 minutes until karela is tender and oil releases at the edges. Serve hot.

name: mooli mirchi ka achar
instructions: Wash mooli and green chilli. Cut into desired pieces. Spread on a cloth in sunlight to dry for 2-3 hours. Dry roast coriander seeds, cumin seeds, fennel seeds, carom seeds, and nigella seeds in a large pan until fragrant. Let cool. In a heavy-bottomed pan, heat mustard oil until it reaches smoking point. Let cool completely. Mix dried mooli, green chilli, and all roasted seeds. Add asafoetida, turmeric powder, chilli powder, dry mango powder, and salt. Mix well and transfer to sterilized glass jars. Seal tightly. Let the pickle rest in sunlight for 7 days before consuming, stirring occasionally.

name: murmura poha
instructions: Heat oil in a large pan or kadai over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and mustard seeds and let them splutter and crackle. Add finely chopped green chilli and let it sizzle. Add diced onion and curry leaves, cook until onion turns translucent, about 2-3 minutes. Add diced carrot, capsicum, and green peas. Cook for 2-3 minutes. Add diced tomato and cook for another 1-2 minutes. Add murmura and roasted chana, gently mix to combine. Sprinkle turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder, and cumin powder. Toss everything together carefully. Turn off heat. Add fresh coriander leaves and lemon juice. Mix gently and serve hot.

name: Banana Walnut Cake
instructions: Mash ripe bananas with powdered sugar. Add melted butter/ghee and vanilla essence. In a separate bowl, sift together wheat flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, baking soda, and cinnamon powder. Mix dry ingredients into wet mixture. Fold in milk until smooth batter forms. Mix in chopped walnuts. Pour into greased baking pan. Bake at 350°F for 30-35 minutes until golden and a toothpick inserted comes out clean. Cool before slicing.

name: Corn Chivda
instructions: Deep fry corn poha in batches until crispy and drain. Separately fry roasted chana dal until fully dried. Fry dry coconut pieces until golden. Deep fry raw peanuts until light brown. In a large bowl, combine all fried ingredients and curry leaves. Sprinkle red chilli powder, dry mango powder, powdered sugar, and salt. Toss well to evenly distribute seasonings. Cool completely before storing in an airtight container.

name: Chana Dal Pulao
instructions: Wash rice and soak chana dal for 4 hours beforehand. Heat oil in pressure cooker. Add peppercorns, mace, bay leaves, cinnamon stick, black cardamom, cumin seeds, and hing. Let seeds crackle. Add green chilli and ginger-garlic paste. Add chopped onion and cook for 2 minutes. Add chopped tomato and cook for 4 minutes. Add soaked chana dal and cook for 2 minutes. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, and garam masala. Mix well and cook for 2 minutes. Add washed rice and mix. Add 2.5 glasses of water and salt to taste. Close the lid and cook on medium flame for 2 whistles. Turn off heat and let pressure release naturally. Open carefully and garnish with coriander leaves. Serve with chopped onion and yogurt.

name: Lahsooni Dal Palak
instructions: Heat ghee and oil in a pot. Add cumin seeds, big cardamom, dry chillies, and bay leaves. Let spices crackle for a few seconds. Add minced garlic and chopped green chilli. Add chopped onion and cook until softened. Add moong dal and water with turmeric powder. Bring to boil and simmer until dal is soft (about 20 minutes). Wash spinach thoroughly and blend into a smooth puree. Stir spinach puree into the cooked dal. Add garam masala powder and salt to taste. Simmer for another 5-10 minutes until well combined. Serve hot.

name: Healthy Multigrain Hakka Noodles
instructions: Boil water in a pot with salt. Add multigrain noodles and boil for 2-3 minutes until cooked. Drain water completely. Heat oil in a large pan or wok. Add chopped green chilli and minced garlic. Add chopped onion and cook for 1 minute. Add all chopped vegetables (peppers, carrot, french beans, beetroot) and cook for 2 minutes on high heat. Season with salt and cook for another minute. Add boiled noodles and black pepper. Toss everything together well. Cook for another minute. Add tomato ketchup if desired and mix. Toss well one more time and serve hot immediately.

name: Besan Laddu
instructions: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan on medium heat. Add gram flour slowly while stirring continuously. Roast the mixture for 5-7 minutes, stirring constantly, until it becomes golden brown and fragrant. Remove from heat and let cool for 2-3 minutes. Add powdered sugar and cardamom powder. Mix well. Let cool a bit more until it's comfortable to handle. Take small portions and roll between your palms into round balls. Store in an airtight container at room temperature.

name: High Protein Dates Laddoo
instructions: Soak dates in warm water for 10-15 minutes until soft. Remove pits and blend into a smooth paste. Lightly toast oats in a pan for 1-2 minutes and set aside. Coarsely chop all dry fruits (cashews, almonds, pistachios, walnuts) and all seeds. Heat ghee in a pan and add toasted oats. Fry for 2-3 minutes. Add chopped dry fruits and seeds. Mix well. Add dates paste and combine thoroughly until well mixed. Let cool. Form into laddoos by rolling in your palms. Refrigerate in an airtight container.

name: Moong Dal Vada
instructions: Soak moong dal for 4-6 hours until soft. Drain completely. Add soaked dal to a food processor along with green chillies, ginger piece, and garlic cloves. Pulse until coarsely ground (not a smooth paste). Transfer to a mixing bowl. Add chopped onion and coriander leaves. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala powder, cumin seeds, and salt. Mix thoroughly. The mixture should be thick and hold together. Heat oil in a deep pan for deep frying. Take small portions of the batter and shape into balls or patties. Carefully place in hot oil and fry until golden brown on all sides. Drain on paper towels. Serve hot with mint chutney or tamarind chutney.

name: Sooji Besan Nasta
instructions: Mix gram flour, semolina, curd and salt with water. Rest for 15 minutes. Add chopped onion, carrot, capsicum, cabbage, ginger, chilli, garlic. Add baking soda and mix well. Heat tawa with oil. Add batter and sprinkle chilli powder and coriander leaves. Cook for 2 minutes on low flame. Flip gently and cook till done. Heat oil, add mustard seeds, green chilli, curry leaves, sesame seeds and cook for a few minutes. Pour this tadka over the cooked cake and serve hot.

name: Palak Saag
instructions: Dry roast sesame seeds and peanuts for 30 seconds. Take out and coarsely grind. Dry roast gram flour for 1 minute and set aside. Heat oil, add asafoetida and cumin seeds. Add garlic and green chilli, fry for a few seconds. Add onion and fry for 3 minutes till soft. Add spinach and mix well. Cook for 3 minutes. Continue cooking for another 4-5 minutes. Add turmeric, red chilli, coriander and garam masala powders. Mix well and cook for 30 seconds. Add roasted gram flour and peanut-sesame powder. Mix with salt. Add 1 cup water and mix well. Cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add amchur powder and butter. Turn off heat and serve.

name: Bihari Dal Bhaat Bhujiya
instructions: Boil rice in water with salt until cooked. Pressure cook arhar dal with turmeric and salt. Heat oil/ghee in a pan, add cumin seeds and asafoetida. Add onion, garlic and green chilli, cook well. Add tomato and cook till soft. Add cooked dal and stir. Season with turmeric powder and garnish with coriander leaves. For aloo bhujiya: Heat oil, add cumin seeds, garlic and dry red chillies. Add sliced potatoes and cook with turmeric and salt, stirring frequently until potatoes are crispy on edges. Serve plain rice topped with dal and aloo bhujiya on the side.

name: Besan Drumstick Sabzi
instructions: Peel and cut drumsticks into bite-sized pieces. Wash well. Boil in hot water with salt for 3 minutes. Strain and reserve the water. Dry roast gram flour in a dry pan for 1 minute till light brown. Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and asafoetida. Add boiled drumstick pieces and cook for 2 minutes. Add turmeric, red chilli, coriander and cumin powders along with garam masala and salt. Mix well and cook for 1 minute. Add roasted gram flour and mix. Add dry mango powder and stir. Cook for 3-4 minutes, adding a little water if needed. Stir well and cook for another minute. Turn off heat and serve hot.

name: Oats Dalia Khichdi
instructions: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot or pressure cooker. Add cumin seeds, asafoetida, green chilli and curry leaves. Add garlic cloves and cook briefly. Add chopped onion and cook till translucent. Add all chopped vegetables (carrot, capsicum, beans, peas, tomato) and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add oats dalia and moong dal, stir well to coat with ghee. Add turmeric, red chilli and garam masala powders. Mix and add salt. Pour in 3 cups water and bring to boil. Cook till all ingredients are tender and water is fully absorbed. Add lemon juice and mix. Serve hot.

name: Masala Bhaat
instructions: Heat oil and toast whole spices (cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, star anise, bay leaves). Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilli. Sauté chopped vegetables (onion, potato, cauliflower, beans, capsicum, carrot, tomato) until partially cooked. Add turmeric, cumin, and coriander powder. Add washed rice and mix well. Pour water (2 glasses for 1 glass rice) and bring to boil. Cover, reduce heat, and pressure cook for 2 whistles in a pressure cooker. Let it rest, then open and garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Khasta Mini Kachori
instructions: For dough: Mix maida with salt and ghee. Add water gradually and knead into a stiff dough. Let rest 15 minutes. For stuffing: Toast cumin and coriander seeds lightly. Crush coarsely with sesame seeds and black pepper. Mix in sev gathiya, sev bhujiya, masala chana, chilli powder, dry mango powder, and salt. Heat 4-5 tbsp oil, mix into spice mixture and let cool. Roll dough into thin sheets, cut into circles, place stuffing in center, fold and seal edges. Deep fry in hot oil on medium heat until golden and crispy.

name: Soya Malai Chaap
instructions: Soak soya chaap in warm water for 15 minutes, squeeze and set aside. Heat oil and add bay leaves, black cardamom, green cardamom. Add sliced onions and sauté until golden. Add ginger-garlic-green chilli paste and cook 1 minute. Add cumin powder, coriander powder, black pepper. Grind cashew nuts and melon seeds into a smooth paste, add to gravy and cook 2 minutes. Add soya chaap, mix gently. Pour cream mixed with fenugreek leaves. Cook on low heat for 5-7 minutes. Add butter and adjust salt.

name: Vada Pav
instructions: For vada filling: Mash boiled potatoes. Heat oil with mustard seeds, cumin seeds, asafoetida, curry leaves. Mix with potatoes, turmeric, salt. Shape into balls. For batter: Mix gram flour with water, turmeric, salt to thick consistency. Dip potato balls in batter and deep fry in hot oil until golden. For peanut chutney: Grind roasted peanuts with garlic, fried besan, chilli powder, cumin seeds, salt, and water to paste consistency. For green chutney: Blend mint, coriander, garlic, green chilli, salt, lemon juice, and water. Serve vada with both chutneys.

name: Murmura Chivda
instructions: Roast peanuts and cashews separately in a pan without oil until light brown. Dry roast sesame seeds in a pan for 1-2 minutes. In a large bowl, mix puffed rice with dry roasted items. In a small pan, heat oil with asafoetida, add chilli powder, turmeric powder, mix well. Pour this tempering over puffed rice mixture. Add dry mango powder and salt. Mix thoroughly and let cool completely. Store in an airtight container.

name: Paneer Dum Biryani
instructions: Cut paneer into cubes and deep fry until light golden, set aside. Slice onions thinly. Fry half the onions in oil until golden brown for garnish, set aside. Cook remaining onions with ginger-garlic paste until soft. Add tomatoes and cook until mushy. Add red chilli powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, biryani masala. Add fried paneer and mix. Boil water with whole spices (bay leaves, cinnamon, javitri). Add rice and cook 70% done, drain. Layer rice and paneer mixture alternately in a heavy-bottomed pot. Sprinkle mint, coriander, fried onions. Soak saffron in warm milk for 10 minutes and pour. Dot with ghee. Cover with foil and then place lid tightly (dum cooking). Cook on high heat for 1-2 minutes until steam forms, then low heat for 45 minutes.

name: sooji and spinach appe
instructions: Grind fresh coconut, roasted chana dal, green chillies, ginger, and garlic with salt and water to make fine chutney. Mix semolina with yogurt and chopped spinach, add finely chopped green chilli, grated carrot, capsicum, onion, baking soda and salt. Add water gradually to make thick batter. Heat appe pan and lightly oil the molds. Pour batter into molds filling halfway and cook over medium-high heat with lid covered until edges turn golden. Remove using fork and deep fry in oil until fully golden and crispy. Prepare tadka by heating oil and tempering mustard seeds and curry leaves. Serve hot appe with fresh coconut chutney and tadka.

name: bread pakora
instructions: Mix boiled and mashed potatoes with green chilli, coriander leaves, chilli powder, garam masala, dry mango powder, half of gram flour, ginger garlic paste, and salt. Prepare a coating batter by mixing remaining gram flour with water and a pinch of salt. Cut bread slices into halves or desired shape. Spread potato mixture evenly on bread slices. Heat oil in a deep pan over medium-high heat. Dip coated bread in batter and deep fry until golden and crispy on all sides. Drain on paper towels and serve hot with mint chutney.

name: masala peanuts
instructions: Peel and blanch raw peanuts if desired or use as-is. Pound garlic cloves to make a coarse paste. Mix gram flour, rice flour, red chilli powder, salt, asafoetida, turmeric, and coriander powder in a bowl. Add garlic paste and mix well. Sprinkle water gradually to make a thick coating that clings to peanuts. Add peanuts to the mixture and toss until all peanuts are well coated. Heat oil in a deep pan over medium heat. Deep fry coated peanuts in batches until golden brown and crispy, stirring frequently. Drain on paper towels. Once cooled slightly, sprinkle chaat masala, chilli powder, and salt over the fried peanuts. Toss well and store in an airtight container.

name: manchow soup
instructions: Boil noodles in salted water until al dente, drain and set aside. Coat noodles lightly with oil and cornflour to prevent sticking. Heat butter in a large pan and add finely chopped garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Saute for 30 seconds until fragrant. Add finely chopped onion, carrot, french beans, capsicum, and cabbage. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes on high heat. Add 2 cups of water and bring to a boil. Mix cornflour with a little water and add to the soup to thicken slightly. Add soya sauce, tomato ketchup, salt, and black pepper. Add boiled noodles to the soup and mix well. Simmer for 2-3 minutes and serve hot with extra black pepper and spring onions if desired.

name: til gud chikki
instructions: Dry roast sesame seeds in a heavy-bottomed pan on medium heat for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until fragrant. Do not let them burn. Set aside. In the same pan, add jaggery and melt it on low heat, stirring occasionally. Once jaggery is completely melted, add ghee and mix well. Add roasted sesame seeds and quickly mix until all seeds are coated. Immediately transfer the mixture to a buttered plate or parchment paper and flatten slightly using the back of a greased spoon or roller. Let it cool for 2-3 minutes until it starts to set but is still pliable. Cut into desired shapes using an oiled knife or break by hand into pieces. Once completely cooled and hardened, store in an airtight container.

name: methi palak pakora
instructions: Wash and chop methi leaves, spinach leaves, and coriander leaves finely. Place all greens in a large bowl. Add gram flour, black pepper, ajwain, coriander seeds, sugar, asafoetida, and salt. Mix well. Add lemon juice and water gradually to make a thick batter that just holds together. Add baking soda just before frying. Heat oil in a deep pan over medium-high heat. Drop spoonfuls of batter carefully into hot oil and fry on both sides until golden brown and crispy. The pakoras should puff up slightly. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Serve hot with mint chutney or ketchup.

name: turmeric pickle
instructions: Clean raw turmeric with a damp cloth and cut into bite-sized pieces. Deseed and cut green chillies into desired sizes. Cut lemons into halves or quarters. In a clean, sterilized glass jar, layer turmeric pieces, green chillies, and lemon pieces alternately. Sprinkle salt between layers ensuring even distribution. Fill the jar with water until all vegetables are submerged. Close the jar and shake well. Keep in sunlight for 1-2 hours daily for 5-7 days, shaking the jar occasionally. The pickle will develop a tangy flavor and the turmeric will soften. After fermentation, store in a cool, dry place. This zero-oil pickle can be consumed directly or mixed with rice and ghee.

name: Aloo Tikki Chaat
instructions: Grate raw potatoes and squeeze out excess moisture. Mix with rajgira aata and sendha namak. Prepare green chutney with green chillies, ginger, coriander leaves, sendha namak and curd. Shape potato mixture into tikki patties. Fry until golden and crispy. Serve hot topped with green chutney, curd, roasted cumin powder, chilli powder, pomegranate, coriander leaves and farali chivda.

name: Sooji Halwa
instructions: Roast besan in ghee until fragrant. Add sooji and toast for 2-3 minutes. Gradually add water while stirring to avoid lumps. Cook until sooji absorbs water and becomes tender. Stir in sugar, saffron, cardamom powder, and dry fruits. Mix well and serve warm.

name: Eggless Panna Cotta
ingredients: coconut cream or fresh cream 100 gm
instructions: Heat milk and cream together. Dissolve agar agar in warm milk and cream mixture. Add sugar and mix well. Pour into moulds and refrigerate for 2-3 hours until set. Prepare strawberry compote by heating strawberries with sugar and lemon juice. Unmould panna cotta and serve topped with strawberry compote.

name: Cabbage Pakora
instructions: Crush coriander seeds and mix with black pepper, carom seeds, ginger, green chillies and salt. Combine with cabbage and coriander leaves. Mix gram flour and rice flour separately into the mixture. Drop spoonfuls into hot oil and fry until golden and crispy on all sides. Drain on paper towels and serve with chutney.

name: Onion Kachori
instructions: Make dough with flour, maida, oil, salt and water. Knead well and let rest. Prepare onion filling by tempering cumin seed and hing, adding garlic, then finely chopped onions. Cook until softened and add turmeric, red chilli powder, amchur and roasted besan. Cool filling. Roll dough thin, fill with onion mixture, seal edges and form kachori shape. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Serve with chutney.

name: Veg Momos
instructions: Make dough with flour, water and salt. Knead until smooth and let rest. Prepare filling by mixing chopped cabbage, carrot, onion, bell pepper, ginger and green chilli. Roll dough into thin circles. Place filling on each circle, gather edges and form momo shape. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Serve hot with chutney.

name: Cheese Methi Paratha
instructions: Chop methi leaves finely and mix with wheat flour. Add green chilli, coriander leaves, ginger, turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder and salt. Mix well and knead into dough with water. Divide into portions. Roll out dough, place cheese slice and mixed herbs on one side, fold and seal. Roll again gently. Cook on hot griddle with ghee or oil until golden brown spots appear on both sides. Serve hot.

name: Baingan Masala
instructions: Roast sesame seeds and peanuts separately and set aside. Heat mustard oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add minced garlic and ginger paste, sauté for a minute. Add chopped onions and cook until golden. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft. Add brinjal pieces and cook covered for 8-10 minutes. Add roasted peanuts and sesame seeds. Add spice powders (turmeric, red chilli, coriander, cumin, garam masala) and mix well. Cook until brinjal is tender. Garnish with coriander leaves. Serve hot with paratha.

name: Mix Vegetable Sabzi
instructions: Dice all vegetables into small pieces. Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add minced garlic and ginger, sauté for 30 seconds. Add chopped onions and cook until golden brown. Add bay leaves. Add the harder vegetables first (potatoes, cauliflower) and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes. Add remaining vegetables and tomatoes. Add spices (turmeric, red chilli powder, sabzi masala) and mix well. Cook covered on medium-low heat for 8-10 minutes until vegetables are tender but retain their shape. Add salt to taste. Garnish with coriander leaves. Serve hot.

name: Amla Ka Murabba
instructions: Wash amlas thoroughly and prick them with a fork multiple times. In a large pan, add amlas and cover with water. Boil for 5-7 minutes until partially softened. Drain and let cool. In the same pan, add rock sugar and enough water to dissolve it. Bring to boil and add the boiled amlas. Add crushed black cardamom and rock salt. Cook on medium heat for 30-40 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the amlas become translucent and the syrup thickens. Add red chilli powder and mix. Once cooled, store in airtight containers. The murabba will last for several months.

name: White Sauce Pasta
instructions: Heat butter and oil together in a pan. Add minced garlic and sauté for 30 seconds. Add finely chopped onions, capsicum, and carrot. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes. Add sweetcorn and cook for another minute. Sprinkle all-purpose flour over the vegetables and mix well. Cook for 1 minute while stirring continuously to avoid lumps. Gradually add milk while stirring constantly to form a smooth, creamy sauce (about 3-4 minutes). Add chilli flakes, mixed herbs, black pepper, and salt. Add the boiled pasta and toss well to coat evenly with the white sauce. Cook for 1-2 minutes until everything is heated through. Serve hot.

name: Vegetable Soup
instructions: Melt butter in a pot. Add minced garlic and sauté briefly. Add diced onions and cook until translucent. Add all chopped vegetables and water. Bring to boil and simmer until vegetables are tender. Season with salt and black pepper. Finish with fresh lemon juice. Garnish with spring onions and serve hot.

name: Veg Chilli Milli
instructions: Blend onion, tomato, ginger, garlic, dry red chilli, and cinnamon into a paste. Heat butter and oil, sauté the masala paste until fragrant. In a separate pan, sauté cumin seeds, then add ginger, garlic, and green chilli. Add all chopped vegetables and cook. Combine with the masala preparation. Add turmeric, coriander, and chilli powder. Cook until vegetables are tender. Finish with fresh cream, adjust salt, and serve hot.

name: Matar Mushroom
instructions: Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds, bay leaves, black cardamom, and cinnamon stick for tempering. Add chopped onions and cook until golden. Add ginger, garlic, and green chilli paste. Add mushrooms and green peas, cook until softened. Stir in turmeric, chilli, and coriander powders. Add tomato paste and cook well. Fold in curd and kasuri methi. Sprinkle garam masala and fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot.

name: Sabudana Cutlet
instructions: Soak sabudana in water for 30 minutes until swollen. Mix soaked sabudana with mashed boiled potatoes, peanut powder, ginger-chilli paste, lemon juice, and coriander leaves. Season with sendha salt. Form into cutlet or vada shapes. Deep fry in oil until golden and crispy on all sides. Drain on paper towel and serve hot.

name: Sindhi Aloo Tuk
instructions: Cut potatoes into small bite-sized pieces. Deep fry in oil until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towel. In a bowl, mix all dry spices including turmeric, chilli, coriander, cumin, garam masala, and hing. Toss fried potatoes with the spice mix. Add dry mango powder, fresh coriander leaves, and lemon juice. Mix well and serve as a crispy snack.

name: ragda patties chaat
instructions: Boil soaked white peas with turmeric, asafoetida, and salt until completely soft and cooked through. Prepare green chutney by grinding mint, coriander leaves, 12 garlic cloves, green chilli, lemon juice, and water into a smooth paste. Prepare tamarind chutney by heating tamarind pulp with jaggery, salt, roasted cumin powder, and red chilli powder. Prepare garlic chutney by grinding 25 gm garlic with red chilli powder, lemon juice, roasted cumin powder, salt, and water into a paste. Boil potatoes until soft, peel, and cut into pieces. Mix potato pieces with turmeric, red chilli powder, cumin, coriander, garam masala, dry mango powder, and salt. Shape into small patties and shallow fry in oil until golden on both sides. Assemble chaat: place aloo patties on a plate, top with hot ragda, add spoons of green, tamarind, and garlic chutneys, garnish with chopped onion, tomato, coriander leaves, sev, and salt. Serve immediately.

name: crispy corn chaat
instructions: Drain sweet corn kernels thoroughly and pat dry with paper towel. In a mixing bowl, combine corn kernels with ginger garlic paste, minced green chilli, chopped onion, and coriander leaves. Add corn flour, rice flour, salt, chaat masala, chilli powder, red chilli powder, black pepper, and lemon juice. Mix everything together until well combined. Heat oil in a wok or deep frying pan to medium-high temperature. Drop spoonfuls of the corn mixture into hot oil, flattening slightly. Fry until golden and crispy on all sides, turning occasionally to ensure even browning. Drain fried corn pieces on paper towels. Serve hot, optionally with extra chaat masala sprinkled on top.

name: Vegetable dalia khichdi
instructions: Wash dalia and moong dal. Heat oil and ghee, temper with cumin seeds. Add green chilli, garlic, and asafoetida. Stir in onion and cook for 2 minutes. Add curry leaves and vegetables, cook for 4-5 minutes. Add dalia, dal, and spices. Mix well, add water and salt. Close lid and pressure cook for 5 whistles. Turn off and let cool. Open, stir, and serve with curd and ghee.

name: Chutney stuffed aloo pakoda
instructions: Boil potatoes and mash with spices for filling. Make green chutney by grinding coriander, mint, ginger, garlic, and sev bhujiya with water and salt. Slice potatoes into rounds, create cavity, fill with green chutney. Prepare besan batter with turmeric, chilli powder, and garam masala. Dip prepared potato slices in batter and deep fry until golden brown. Serve hot.

name: Sattu Paratha
instructions: Make dough with whole wheat flour, oil, salt and water. For filling: combine roasted chana sattu, salt, kalonji, ajwain, green chilli, ginger, onion, coriander, lemon juice, mango pickle and mustard oil with water to make filling. Roll out dough circle, spread filling, fold and roll into paratha. Fry on ghee/oil until golden brown on both sides.

name: Indian style cheesy pasta
instructions: Boil pasta until al dente, drain and set aside. Heat butter and oil, add garlic and green chilli. Add diced onion, capsicum, carrot, and french beans. Cook for 2-3 minutes. Add tomatoes and simmer for 2 minutes. Add cooked pasta, chilli flakes, mixed herbs, and salt. Stir in tomato ketchup and mayonnaise. Top with grated cheese and serve hot.

name: Street style kachumber sandwich
instructions: Dice tomato, cucumber, and onion finely. Mix with chopped green chilli, coriander leaves, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper to make kachumber filling. Apply butter on bread slices. Spread kachumber mixture on one slice. Cover with second slice. Grill on a tawa or griddle with butter until golden and crispy. Serve hot.

name: Palak Soup
instructions: Boil spinach, onion, and garlic together until soft. Blend the mixture into smooth puree. Heat butter in a pan, add the spinach puree and milk. Season with black pepper and salt. Simmer for a few minutes until well combined and heated through.

name: Besan Pitod Ki Sabzi
instructions: Prepare besan katli by mixing gram flour with water, spices, oil, and asafoetida to form a dough. Roll and cut into small pieces. For the sabzi, heat oil and add cumin seeds. Add ginger-garlic paste, onion, and tomato. Cook until soft. Add spice powders, curd, and cook for 4 minutes. Add the besan katli pieces and cook until tender. Garnish with green chilli and coriander leaves.

name: Ram Laddoo
instructions: Soak and boil the mixed lentils (moong, chana, urad dal) until cooked. Mix with ginger, garlic, green chilli, and grated mooli. Form into balls and fry. For green chutney, blend mooli leaves, coriander, green chilli, garlic, and add lemon juice and salt. Mix in sev bhujiya. Serve laddoos with the chutney.

name: Masala Fish Fry
instructions: Crush garlic and mix with red chilli powder and salt to form a paste. Apply onto fish pieces and marinate briefly. Mix gram flour, rice flour, turmeric, red chilli powder, cumin, coriander, and garam masala with water to form a thick coating batter. Coat fish pieces in the batter and deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy.

name: Radish Carrot Pickle
instructions: Cut mooli, carrot, and green chilli into small pieces. Finely mince garlic and ginger. Heat mustard oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds, fennel seeds, and coriander seeds. When seeds crackle, add minced ginger-garlic. Add cut vegetables, asafoetida, turmeric, and chilli powder. Stir well and cook for 5 minutes. Add salt and mix. Cool and store in a jar.

name: Shakkarpara
instructions: Mix all purpose flour with ghee, cardamom powder, powdered sugar, and milk to form a soft dough. Knead well. Roll out the dough to medium thickness and cut into diamond or square shapes. Heat oil in a pan and fry the pieces until light golden and crispy. Remove on a paper towel to drain excess oil.

name: Pumpkin Flower Pakoda
instructions: Clean pumpkin flowers and remove the stamen. Mix rice flour and besan with turmeric, red chilli powder, and salt. Finely chop green chilli and garlic, add to the flour mixture. Add water to make a thick batter. Dip each pumpkin flower in the batter and deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels.

name: Mooli ke Patto ki Sabzi
instructions: Heat mustard oil in a pan. Add fenugreek seeds and let them crackle. Add chopped garlic and green chillies and fry for a few seconds. Add diced mooli leaves and baingan pieces. Sprinkle turmeric powder and salt. Cook on medium heat until vegetables are tender and slightly dry, about 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally.

name: Gobhi ka Paratha
instructions: Make a dough with wheat flour and salt. Grate cauliflower and mix with ginger, green chillies, spices, roasted besan, and garam masala to make the filling. Roll dough into thin circles, fill with cauliflower mixture, seal edges, and roll again. Cook on a hot griddle with ghee on both sides until golden and cooked through, about 3-4 minutes per paratha.

name: Banarasi Dum Aloo
instructions: Make paneer-onion-coriander filling and stuff into parboiled potatoes. Deep fry stuffed potatoes until crispy. Grind soaked cashew, onion, ginger, garlic, and coriander leaves into a paste. Temper cumin seeds, bay leaves, and whole spices in oil. Add ground paste and cook 8-10 minutes. Add tomato-based gravy, bring to boil, add fried potatoes, simmer 3 minutes covered. Finish with kasuri methi and butter.

name: Vrat ka Nasta
ingredients: barnyard millet sama ka chawal 1/2 cup
sendha namak rock salt to taste
instructions: Soak sabudana for 30 minutes. Mix sama ka chawal, soaked sabudana, chilli garlic paste, chilli flakes, and rock salt. Heat oil and deep fry mixture into golden crispy cutlets. For chutney, grind roasted peanuts, coriander leaves, green chillies with rock salt and lemon juice. Serve cutlets hot with peanut chutney.

name: Pound Cake
instructions: Cream butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in eggs one at a time. Fold in maida gently. Pour into greased pan and bake at 180C for 30-35 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean. Let cool. Mix strawberries with 1 tbsp sugar and lemon juice. Serve cake sliced with strawberry topping.

name: Afghani Egg Masala
instructions: Coat boiled eggs with salt and black pepper, then fry in oil for 2 minutes. Make a paste with ginger, garlic, green chillies, onion, coriander leaves, and curd. Temper cumin seeds, bay leaves, and whole spices in oil. Add ground paste and cook 8-10 minutes. Add water, bring to boil, add fried eggs, simmer 3 minutes. Finish with kasuri methi and butter.

name: Pinwheel Mathri
instructions: Mix wheat flour with salt. Add spices and ghee. Make a stiff dough with minimal water. Roll out thin and fold to create pinwheel pattern. Cut into desired shapes. Heat oil to medium heat and deep fry mathri until golden and crispy, 2-3 minutes per batch. Cool on paper towels. Store in airtight container for months.

name: Poha Chivda
instructions: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a large pan. Add curry leaves and let them crackle. Add thin poha and roast on medium heat for 2-3 minutes. Add roasted chana dal, peanuts, almonds, and cashews.
