Continue roasting for another 3-4 minutes. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, and mix well. Add powdered sugar and dry mango powder, mix thoroughly. Add dry coconut and roast for 1-2 more minutes. Cool completely and store in airtight container.

name: Kala Chana Sabzi
instructions: Soak chanas overnight or for 8 hours. Heat ghee in a pressure cooker, add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Add ginger pieces and green chillis, fry for a minute. Add bay leaves and black cardamom. Add soaked chanas with 2 cups water. Close lid and pressure cook for 6-7 whistles on high heat. Once pressure releases, open cooker. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder, and cumin powder. Simmer for 3-4 minutes. Add peanut oil and salt to taste. Serve hot.

name: Methi Besan Cheela
instructions: For chutney: blend coriander leaves, green chillis, garlic, ginger, roasted gram with yogurt and lemon juice until smooth. For cheela: wash and finely chop methi leaves. Mix with grated carrot, onion, and green chillis. Add besan, ginger-garlic paste, all spices, and salt. Add water to make thick batter. Heat oil in pan, pour batter and spread into round shape. Cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes until golden, then flip and cook other side. Serve hot with green chutney.

name: Til Gud Laddoo
instructions: Dry roast sesame seeds in a pan on medium-low heat for 3-4 minutes until fragrant, stirring continuously. Remove and set aside. Dry roast raw peanuts for 2-3 minutes, then crush them coarsely. In a pan, add jaggery and melt it on low heat for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally. Once melted, add roasted sesame seeds and crushed peanuts. Mix well until combined. Grease palms lightly with ghee. Take a spoonful of mixture and roll into ball while still warm. Place on plate to cool and set. Store in airtight container.

name: aloo pyaz mirchi pakode
instructions: 1. Combine gram flour with toasted coriander seeds, black pepper, carom seeds, and hing. 2. Add grated potatoes, onions, ginger-garlic paste, green chilli, spring onion, and coriander leaves. 3. Mix in lemon juice and baking soda. 4. Season with salt and mix well to form a batter. 5. Heat oil in a pan for deep frying to 180°C. 6. Drop spoonfuls of batter into hot oil and fry until golden brown and crispy. 7. Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

name: street style egg bhurji
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a large pan or tawa over high heat. 2. Add ginger-garlic, cook for 30 seconds. 3. Add onions and green chillies, sauté until onions are translucent. 4. Add diced tomatoes and cook until soft, about 2-3 minutes. 5. Add turmeric powder, chilli powder, coriander powder, and garam masala. 6. Beat the eggs and pour into the pan. 7. Scramble continuously until eggs are just cooked through. 8. Garnish with coriander leaves. 9. Butter the pav bread and serve alongside the bhurji.

name: kadhai aloo
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a kadhai or wok over medium-high heat. 2. Add cumin seeds and let them crackle. 3. Add bay leaves, black and green cardamom, star anise, cloves, and mace. 4. Add onions and cook until golden brown. 5. Add ginger-garlic and cook for 1 minute. 6. Add tomatoes and cook until soft. 7. Add turmeric, red chilli powder, dry mango powder, and salt. 8. Add raw baby potatoes and stir well to coat. 9. Cover and cook on medium heat for 30-40 minutes, stirring occasionally, until potatoes are tender. 10. Add green chilli and adjust seasoning. 11. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: no bake chocolate biscuit cake
instructions: 1. Crush biscuits into small, uneven pieces. 2. Melt milk chocolate chunks and butter using double boiler method. 3. Mix cocoa powder with the melted chocolate and butter. 4. Fold crushed biscuits into chocolate mixture and mix well. 5. Add chopped almonds, cashews, and pistachios. 6. Line a container or pan with butter paper. 7. Press the mixture into the container, spreading evenly. 8. Refrigerate for 2-3 hours until set. 9. For topping: whip cream with sugar until stiff peaks form. 10. Melt extra chocolate for shavings. 11. Demould the cake and top with whipped cream, chocolate shavings, mint leaves, and cherries. 12. Slice and serve chilled.

name: crispy laccha aloo tikki chaat
instructions: 1. Squeeze excess moisture from grated potatoes. 2. Mix potatoes with roasted cumin, green chilli, ginger, salt, pepper, fresh coriander, and singhara flour. 3. Heat oil/ghee in a pan. 4. Form thin, long strands of the potato mixture and shallow fry until golden and crispy. 5. Prepare sweet curd by mixing thick yogurt with powdered sugar and a pinch of salt. 6. Prepare sweet-sour tamarind chutney by mixing water, anardana powder, ginger, roasted cumin powder, salt, pepper, and grated jaggery. 7. Arrange crispy potato lacchas on a plate. 8. Top with generous portions of sweet curd. 9. Drizzle with sweet-sour chutney. 10. Serve immediately while potatoes are crispy.

name: veg hot dog
instructions: 1. Boil soya chunk kheema in water with salt, green chilli, and ginger until soft. 2. Drain and set aside. 3. For filling: heat oil and butter, add cumin seeds and lichen. 4. Add chopped garlic and cook for 30 seconds. 5. Add onions and cook until soft. 6. Add green capsicum and cooked soya keema. 7. Add turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder, and garam masala. 8. Add tomato puree and cook until thick. 9. Add kasoori methi and fresh coriander. 10. For cheese sauce: melt butter, add flour to make roux. 11. Add crushed garlic and chilli powder. 12. Gradually add milk while stirring to avoid lumps. 13. Add cheese slices and cook until sauce thickens. 14. Toast hot dog buns with butter. 15. Assemble: place filling in bun, add diced paneer, chopped tomato, and fresh coriander. 16. Pour cheese sauce on top and serve hot.

name: nan khatai
instructions: 1. Cream together ghee and powdered sugar until light and fluffy. 2. Add cardamom powder and mix well. 3. Sift together all-purpose flour, gram flour, semolina, baking powder, and baking soda. 4. Add sifted dry ingredients to the ghee-sugar mixture. 5. Mix until a soft dough forms. 6. Divide into small portions and roll into balls. 7. Flatten slightly into a disc shape. 8. Garnish with a chopped pista in the center. 9. Arrange on a baking tray or tawa. 10. Bake at 180°C for 15 minutes until light golden (or roast on a tawa on stovetop until light brown). 11. Cool on a wire rack. 12. Store in an airtight container.

name: Green Peas Paratha
instructions: Boil green peas in salted water with sugar. Drain and fry peas in oil with heeng, jeera, saunf until fragrant. Add chilli powder, coriander, turmeric, amachur, black salt, green chilli, and mint to make filling. Make dough with atta, salt and water. Roll dough flat, spread filling, roll into paratha, and pan-fry with ghee until golden brown.

name: Eggless Pancakes
instructions: Make apple sauce by cooking diced apples with sugar, water, cinnamon, and lemon juice until soft and jammy. Make pancake batter by mashing banana and mixing with milk, flour, sugar, salt, baking powder, vanilla extract, and melted butter. Heat buttered pan on medium heat and pour batter in portions. Cook until bubbles form on surface, flip and cook until golden. Serve pancakes with apple sauce.

name: Soya Chunks Pulao
instructions: Boil soya chunks in water with salt, ginger, and green chilli for 15 minutes, then drain. Make garam masala by roasting and grinding cloves, cardamom, cinnamon, and saunf. Heat ghee and add black cardamom, cumin, pather phool, then onions until golden. Add garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Add raw potato chunks and fry for 2 minutes. Add turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder, then tomato and boiled soya. Cook until potato is half done. Add basmati rice, boiled green peas, garam masala, kasoori methi. Pour water and cook covered until rice is done. Garnish with mint.

name: Bhutte Ki Kees
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan and add heeng until fragrant. Add mustard seeds and let them crackle. Add cumin seeds. Add chopped ginger and green chilli, then curry leaves. Add corn niblets and fry for 2 minutes. Add turmeric and chilli powder. Pour milk or water and cook covered until corn becomes thick and creamy, about 5-7 minutes. Finish with lemon juice. Garnish with grated coconut and fresh coriander before serving.

name: Pizza Pani Puri
instructions: Make cheese sauce by melting butter, adding maida, then milk gradually while stirring to avoid lumps. Add grated parmesan cheese and simmer until thickened. Make tomato sauce by heating olive oil, adding garlic and onion, cooking until soft, then adding chilli flakes, celery, basil, and tomato. Simmer until thick. Prepare Italian salad by combining finely chopped tomato, capsicums, zucchini, sweet corn, oregano, salt, chilli flakes, olive oil, lemon juice, and parmesan cheese. Fill gol gappa shells with Italian salad, top with cheese sauce and tomato sauce. Serve as pizza pani puri.

name: Moong Dal Mughlai
instructions: Soak moong dal for 30 minutes, then cook in pressure cooker or pot until completely soft and mushy. In a separate pan, heat oil or ghee and fry onion slices until deep golden brown. Add turmeric, cook for 30 seconds. Add green chilli and chopped ginger, fry for 1 minute. Add the cooked moong dal, salt to taste, and butter. Mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes on medium heat. For tadka, heat ghee in a small pan, add dry red chillies and cloves, let them crackle and release aroma. Pour the hot tadka over the dal just before serving. Squeeze lemon wedge on top and serve hot.

name: Vegetarian Caesar Salad
instructions: Make croutons by heating 2 tbsp olive oil in a pan, adding diced bread, minced garlic, and frying until golden and crispy on all sides. Remove and drain on paper towel. Prepare Caesar dressing by blending or whisking vegetarian mayonnaise, grated cheese, mustard paste, lemon juice, garlic clove, black pepper, salt, and 0.5 cup olive oil until emulsified and smooth. Wash and chop romaine lettuce. Toss romaine lettuce with the Caesar dressing until well coated. Divide between two plates. Top with homemade croutons and cheese shavings. Serve immediately.

name: mango pudding
instructions: Dice mangoes and mix with sugar. Dissolve custard powder in milk, combine with mango mixture and lemon juice. Pour into dish, refrigerate until set. Demould and garnish with mango pieces and mint leaves before serving.

name: chicken tikka masala
instructions: Marinate chicken with salt, lemon, ginger and garlic paste. Prepare second marinade with mustard oil, spices, hung curd. Cook tikka on pan or grill. Make masala by tempering oil and butter, frying onions, adding ginger, garlic, green chilli, cooking tomatoes, adding chilli powder and water. Combine cooked tikka with masala, add cream and fenugreek leaves. Garnish with coriander and serve.

name: kala khatta
instructions: Boil jamuns in water for 10-15 minutes. Strain through a sieve to remove pulp and seeds. Add sugar, salt, black pepper powder, and black salt to the strained liquid. Mix in crushed roasted cumin and lemon juice. Stir well. Chill in refrigerator. Serve in glasses with ice cubes, garnish with mint leaves.

name: vegetable lasagna
instructions: Make tomato sauce: heat olive oil, cook celery, onion, and carrot, add zucchini and broccoli, season with salt and pepper, add chopped tomatoes and tomato puree. Make white sauce: melt butter, add flour to make roux, gradually add milk, season with salt, pepper, nutmeg. Layer lasagne: alternate tomato sauce, lasagne sheets, white sauce, and mozzarella cheese. Bake at 180C for 30-40 minutes until golden. Alternative: use thinly sliced potatoes instead of lasagne sheets.

name: ginger and garlic paste
instructions: For garlic paste: measure 1 cup peeled garlic cloves, add 0.5 tbsp salt and 0.5 cup oil. Grind until smooth. For ginger paste: measure 1 cup chopped fresh ginger, add 0.5 tbsp salt and 0.5 cup oil. Grind until smooth. Store each in separate glass jars with tight-fitting lids. Refrigerate. Drizzle extra oil on top after each use to preserve longer. Can be stored for up to one month. Use 1 part ginger paste to 2 parts garlic paste in recipes.

name: oats dosa
instructions: For dosa batter: Mix oats, urad dal, methi seeds, water, green chilli, onion, curry leaves, ginger, cumin, and salt to make a smooth batter. For tomato chutney: Toast dry red chilli, cumin, chana dal, urad dal in ghee, add coconut and spices. Grind into paste. Make tomato puree chutney by cooking tomatoes with jaggery and spices. Heat oil on griddle and spread dosa batter thinly. Cook until golden, serve with chutneys.

name: mango smoothie
instructions: Dice fresh mangoes. Add mango, yogurt, honey, cardamom powder, water, and chia seeds to blender. Blend until smooth. Add ice cubes and blend briefly. Pour into glasses. Garnish with rosemary sprigs and serve immediately.

name: cheese potato bread rolls
instructions: Mix boiled mashed potatoes with onion, coriander seeds, chilli flakes, green chilli, ginger, coriander leaves, and salt. Dunk bread slices in water and squeeze gently. Place potato filling on bread, insert cheese cube, fold sides and shape into cylinder. Deep fry in hot oil until golden brown. For chutney: mix amchur powder, sugar, chilli powder, cumin, salt and water to taste. Serve hot bread rolls with amchur chutney.

name: soya chilli manchurian
instructions: Boil soya nuggets in water with soya sauce, salt, garlic, onion, green chilli, and ginger for 10 minutes. Drain and cool. Mix cooked soya with spring onions, coriander, salt, green chilli, lemon juice, pepper, cornstarch, and flour to form balls. Deep fry until golden. For sauce: heat oil, add dry chilli, garlic, ginger, onion, capsicums. Add chilli sauce, soya sauce, ketchup, vinegar, and stock. Toss fried soya balls in sauce. Garnish with spring onions and coriander.

name: chocolate ice cream
ingredients: dairy cream or whipping cream 1 cup 250 gms
condensed milk 150.5 gms or half cup
instructions: Whip dairy cream or whipping cream until peaks form. Add condensed milk and mix well. Sift cocoa powder and fold into cream mixture. Pour into freezer-safe container. Freeze for 2-3 hours, stirring every 30 minutes to prevent ice crystals. Serve topped with roasted almonds, chocolate chips, and cinnamon powder. Garnish with mint leaves.

name: pizza bread bomb
instructions: Mix all vegetables with oregano, chilli flakes, salt, pepper, ketchup, chilli sauce, and mozzarella cheese. Place filling on bread slices, top with cheese slice. Fold bread and seal edges with flour-water paste. Deep fry until golden. For garlic bread: mix softened butter with chopped garlic, chilli flakes, oregano, and pepper powder. Spread on bread chunks and toast until crispy. Serve pizza bombs hot with garlic bread.

name: south indian tomato rasam
ingredients: water or toor dal water 8 cups
instructions: Dry roast kashmiri chillies, methi seeds, chana dal, cumin, black pepper, and coriander seeds in ghee. Add curry leaves and roast until fragrant. Cool and grind into rasam powder. Soak tamarind and extract juice from water. In pot, add tamarind water, turmeric, tomato puree, jaggery, and salt. Bring to boil. Add rasam powder and simmer 10 minutes. For tempering: heat ghee, add mustard seeds, asafoetida, and curry leaves. Pour tempering into rasam. Garnish with coriander leaves. Serve hot.

name: vegetable spring rolls
ingredients: corn flour 50 gms plus extra for dusting
instructions: Mix flour, cornflour, salt, and water to make thin smooth batter. Heat non-stick pan, rub with oil-soaked cloth. Pour ladle of batter, swirl quickly, pour back. Let cook briefly, remove and place on cornflour-dusted plate. For filling: heat oil, add ginger and garlic, add onions, carrots, cabbage, capsicum. Stir-fry 2-3 minutes. Add salt, sugar, pepper, soya sauce, vinegar. Sprinkle cornflour and toss. Cook 2-3 minutes until water evaporates. Add sesame oil and cool. Place filling on wrapper, fold sides and roll tightly. Seal edge with flour-water paste. Deep fry until crisp and golden.

name: Crispy Vegetable Cheese Cutlet
instructions: Boil and chop beans, carrots, and cauliflower. Boil potatoes and mash. Heat oil, add asafoetida and cumin seeds, then add chopped green chillies and ginger. Add vegetables and cook until soft. Mix in all spices and salt. Combine with mashed potatoes and let cool. Add cheese cubes and some breadcrumbs. Shape into cutlet patties. Prepare slurry with maida flour and water. Dip each cutlet in slurry, then coat with remaining breadcrumbs. Deep fry in oil until golden and crispy. Drain and serve hot.

name: Sandwich without Bread (Italian & South Indian)
instructions: Mix semolina, salt, yogurt, water, and baking soda to form thick batter. For Italian version: mix chilli flakes, oregano, chopped onion, capsicum, corn, and tomato ketchup into a portion of batter. For South Indian version: heat oil with dry red chillies and asafoetida, add channa dal and mustard seeds, then curry leaves; add ginger, green chilli, and coriander to remaining batter. Let batter rest 5 minutes. Pour batter portions onto a greased skillet and cook like crepes until golden. Add desired fillings if making savory sandwiches. Roll and serve hot.

name: Cappuccino Coffee
instructions: Mix instant coffee powder with sugar in a cup. Add 2 tablespoons of hot water and whisk vigorously for 2-3 minutes until the mixture becomes light, frothy, and pale brown (creating the coffee foam). In a separate vessel, warm the milk gently and froth it well by whisking or using a frother. Pour the frothed coffee mixture into serving cups. Slowly pour the warm frothed milk into each cup. Top with remaining milk foam. Sprinkle a pinch of cinnamon powder on top if desired. Serve immediately while hot and frothy.

name: Veg Biryani Rice
instructions: Soak basmati rice in water for 15-20 minutes, then drain. For biryani stock: heat oil in a heavy pot, add bay leaves, cinnamon, cloves, peppercorns, cardamom, javitri, and shahi jeera. Let spices crackle for 1 minute. Add sliced onions and fry until golden brown. Add slit green chillies, roughly chopped garlic and ginger. Add orange pieces and mint leaves. Pour in water and bring to a boil. Add salt and adjust to taste. Add drained rice to the boiling stock and let it cook halfway (3-4 minutes). Meanwhile, dissolve saffron in milk. Drain the rice when half-cooked and layer it back into the stock. Pour saffron milk, rosewater, kewra water, and meetha ittar over rice. Dot with ghee. Cover and cook on medium heat until rice is fully cooked and aromatic. Fluff gently with a fork and serve.

name: Badam Ka Halwa
instructions: Blanch almonds in hot water for 2-3 minutes, then drain and peel off skins. Grind peeled almonds into a fine paste using a mixer. Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat. Add the almond paste and stir continuously. Toast the almond paste in ghee for 8-10 minutes until it becomes light golden and aromatic, stirring regularly to prevent burning. In a separate vessel, dissolve sugar in water or milk over heat to make a thin syrup. Once almond paste is toasted, slowly pour the sugar syrup into it while stirring continuously. Continue cooking and stirring until the mixture becomes thick and begins to hold together. Add cardamom powder, rosewater, and kewra water. Stir in the dissolved saffron and mix well. Cook for another 2-3 minutes until all flavors are infused. Transfer the halwa to a greased plate or dish. Garnish with crushed roasted almonds. Allow to cool slightly before serving.

name: Beetroot Cutlet
ingredients: bread crumbs 3 tbsp dried plus 1 cup for coating
instructions: Prepare Bengali garam masala by grinding cinnamon, cardamom pods, and cloves together. Boil beetroot until soft, peel off skin, and finely grate or chop. Boil potato until soft and mash well. Combine grated beetroot with mashed potato. Add salt, chopped green chilli, ginger, roasted crushed peanuts, and roasted crushed cumin. Add chopped coriander leaves, mustard oil, kasundi, and Bengali garam masala. Mix 3 tablespoons dried breadcrumbs into the mixture. Divide and shape into flat round or oval cutlets. Pour 1 cup breadcrumbs into a plate and coat each cutlet thoroughly. Heat oil for frying in a shallow pan over medium-high heat. Fry cutlets 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy. For mustard dip: mix kasundi with mayonnaise. Drain cutlets on paper towels and serve hot with the mustard dip.

name: Methi Mathri
ingredients: kasoori methi dried fenugreek leaves 3 tbsp
instructions: Sift together maida flour, salt, crushed coriander seeds, ajwain, baking powder, and baking soda in a large bowl. Add kasoori methi and mix well. Cut ghee into small pieces and add to dry mixture. Using fingertips, rub ghee into flour until mixture resembles breadcrumbs. Add water gradually and knead into a stiff, firm dough. Knead for 3-5 minutes until smooth. Roll dough into small balls about 1 inch diameter. Flatten each ball slightly into a round disc using palm of hand or a rolling pin. Heat oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat. In batches, fry flattened mathis a few at a time for 1-2 minutes per side until they puff slightly and turn golden. Remove with slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Let cool completely until they become crispy. Store in an airtight container.

name: Bajra Methi Paratha with Tamatar Chutney
ingredients: methi fenugreek leaves 2 cups chopped fresh
instructions: For paratha dough: Mix bajra atta with whole wheat flour, salt, and mashed potato. Add chopped onion, green chilli, ginger, coriander, sesame seeds, ajwain, chilli powder, black pepper, turmeric, mango pickle masala, and black salt. Add chopped fresh methi leaves and mix well. Add ghee and water gradually to form a soft, pliable dough. Knead for 3-4 minutes until smooth. Let dough rest 10 minutes covered. For chutney: Blend tomatoes, green chilli, onion quarter, garlic clove, and coriander with lemon juice, salt, and a dash of mustard oil. To cook parathas: Heat a tawa or griddle over medium heat. Take a portion of dough, shape into a ball, dust with flour, and roll into thin circle. Place on hot tawa, cook 30-40 seconds until it starts to brown. Flip and spread a small amount of ghee. Cook until light spots appear and paratha is cooked through. Flip once more, add ghee, and cook until both sides have brown spots. Serve hot parathas with the fresh tomato chutney.

name: Sponge Rasgulla
instructions: Curdle milk with vinegar to form paneer (chenna). Drain and mash paneer into smooth paste. Mix maida with water to make slurry for fluffy texture. Form paneer mixture into balls. Prepare sugar syrup with 4 cups sugar and 2 cups water. Boil water with maida slurry, add rasgulla balls, simmer until they float and become spongy. Soak in cooled sugar syrup with rose water. Garnish with rose petals.

name: Dhaba Style Dal Fry
instructions: Boil urad dal, rajma, and chana dal with water, ginger slices, garlic cloves, green chilli and turmeric until soft. In separate pan, heat ghee/oil, add dry red chillies and cumin. Add chopped garlic, ginger, onion and green chillies. Sauté until onions turn translucent. Add spices (turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder). Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft. Add boiled dal mixture, stir well. Add butter and simmer. Finish with second tadka: heat ghee with kashmiri chilli powder and pour over dal.

name: Tandoori Momos
instructions: Make dough with maida, salt, oil and warm water. Knead until smooth. For filling: sauté garlic, ginger, green chilli, onion. Add carrots, cabbage, mushrooms, paneer. Season with pepper, soya sauce, spring onion and cornstarch. Roll out dough, cut circles, fill with vegetable mixture, fold and seal. Deep fry until golden. For tandoori coating: mix mustard oil, kashmiri chilli powder, lemon juice, garlic paste, ginger paste, hung curd, kasoori methi, salt. Brush on cooked momos. Dust with chaat masala.

name: Paneer 65
instructions: Marinate paneer cubes in salt, kashmiri chilli powder, turmeric, garam masala, coriander powder, cumin powder, curry leaves, coriander, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice and whisked curd for 15 minutes. Coat marinated paneer in mixture of cornstarch and rice flour. Deep fry until golden brown. For tossing: heat oil, add cumin, garlic, ginger and dry red chillies. Add fried paneer, toss quickly. Season with salt, coriander and chaat masala. Serve with lemon wedges.

name: Gatte Ki Curry
instructions: For gattes: mix gram flour with coriander seeds, cumin, chilli flakes, coriander powder, turmeric, salt, baking soda and curd. Knead into stiff dough. Roll into cylindrical shapes and steam for 10 minutes. Cut into pieces. For curry: heat ghee, add asafoetida and dry red chilli. Add cumin and coriander seeds. Add ginger, onion and green chillies, sauté until soft. Add spices (chilli powder, coriander powder, turmeric). Whisk yogurt and add to pan. Add water and gatte pieces. Simmer until gattes are well cooked. Finish with kasoori methi and coriander.

name: Instant Rava Idli
instructions: Make batter with rava, curd, salt and water. Add baking soda and mix well. Heat oil and add mustard seeds, cumin, green chilli, ginger, chana dal, urad dal and curry leaves. Add cashew nuts, turmeric, black pepper and grated carrots to the tempering. Pour tempering into batter and mix. Grease idli moulds with oil and fill with batter. Steam for 12 minutes until cooked.

name: Hummus Dip
instructions: Toast sesame seeds in oil to make tahini paste. Soak and boil chickpeas with baking soda in water until tender. Blend tahini, garlic, salt and lemon juice. Add boiled chickpeas and a dash of iced water and blend until smooth. Mix in olive oil and cumin powder. Garnish with olive oil, boiled chickpeas and serve with pita bread.

name: Onion Rings
instructions: Peel and slice onions into rings. Dust with flour. Make batter with flour, salt, chilli flakes, oregano, pepper, coriander, garlic and water. Dip onion rings in batter then coat with bread crumbs. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. For dip: melt butter, sauté onion and green chilli with bay leaf and salt. Cool and mix with mayonnaise, coriander and chilli powder. Serve hot onion rings with the dip and lemon.

name: Chocolate Paan Shake
instructions: Tear paan leaves into smaller pieces. Add leaves, gulkand, 4 glazed cherries, saunf, vanilla ice cream, milk and crushed ice to blender. Blend until smooth. Pour chocolate sauce on the sides of a tall glass. Pour the chilled shake into the glass. Serve immediately. Garnish with a glazed cherry on a toothpick.

name: Rava Cake
instructions: Cream butter with sugar until light and fluffy. Beat egg whites until stiff peaks form. Mix egg yolks into creamed butter and sugar. Fold in grated fresh coconut, suji, vanilla extract, cardamom and nutmeg powder. Gently fold in stiffly beaten egg whites. Pour into greased and lined baking pan. Arrange cashew nuts on top. Bake at 180°C for 45 minutes until a toothpick inserted comes out clean. Cool completely and cut into slices.

name: Masala Chai
instructions: Dry roast cardamom, cloves, peppercorn, cinnamon and nutmeg over medium heat. Add saunf, dry rose petals and ginger powder. Grind or crush coarsely to make chai masala powder. Boil water in a pot and add tea leaves. Simmer for 1-2 minutes. Add milk and sugar. Brew for 1-2 minutes more. Add 3/4 tsp chai masala powder. Stir well and pour into teacups through a strainer. Serve hot.

name: paneer chettinad
instructions: Prepare chettinad masala by roasting bay leaves, black pepper, cumin, coriander seeds, fennel, dry red chillies, mace, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, and kalpasi. Grind with fresh coconut into a paste. In a separate pot, heat oil and temper with fennel seeds. Add chopped onions and sauté until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for 1-2 minutes. Add the prepared chettinad masala and turmeric. Mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add curd, chilli powder, and coriander powder. Stir and add tomato pieces. Simmer for 10-15 minutes. Add paneer cubes and cook for another 5-10 minutes until paneer is heated through. Garnish with fresh coriander and curry leaves. Serve hot.

name: grilled chicken sandwich
instructions: Boil chicken breast with peppercorn, garlic cloves, bay leaf, ginger, and salt for 15-20 minutes until cooked. Cool and shred the chicken. Mix shredded chicken with mayonnaise, chopped onion, celery, coriander, capsicum pieces, grated cheese, mustard sauce, ketchup, chilli sauce, and salt to taste. Spread the mixture on bread slices and top with another slice. Butter the outer sides of the sandwich. Grill on a hot tawa or griddle until golden brown and crispy on both sides, about 2-3 minutes per side. Serve hot with additional sauces on the side.

name: watermelon ice cream
instructions: Blend fresh watermelon and strain to get clear juice (about 3 cups). Whip dairy cream until soft peaks form. Gently fold in condensed milk, vanilla extract, and rosewater into the whipped cream. Add pink food colour for desired shade. Fold in the watermelon juice carefully to maintain the airy texture. Pour the mixture into ice cream moulds or a container. Cover with plastic wrap and freeze for 6-8 hours or overnight until firm. Once set, unmould if using moulds and scoop into bowls. Top with roasted pistachios and serve immediately.

name: vrat ka dhokla
instructions: Soak barnyard millet and sabudana separately for 30 minutes. Blend soaked millet and sabudana with ginger paste, green chilli paste, and sendha salt. Transfer to a bowl and add sugar (optional), curd, and water to make a batter. Just before steaming, add baking soda or fruit salt and mix gently to incorporate air. Grease a steaming pan or mould with oil. Pour the batter into the greased container. Steam for 15-20 minutes until a toothpick inserted comes out clean. Cool for 2-3 minutes and de-mould. For tempering: heat oil in a small pan, add cumin seeds, slit green chillies, and curry leaves. Pour this tempering over the cooked dhokla. For the chutney: blend coriander leaves, mint leaves, roasted cumin, anardama powder, black pepper powder, sendha salt, and chilled water into a smooth paste. Serve hot dhokla with vrat ki chutney.

name: Nachos with Cheese Sauce and Salsa
instructions: 1. Mix maize flour, all-purpose flour, salt and warm water to make dough. 2. Knead and rest dough. 3. Roll and cut into nacho shapes. 4. Deep fry nachos until golden and crispy. 5. For cheese sauce: melt butter, add flour, cook, add milk and cheese to make smooth sauce. 6. For salsa: combine chopped tomato, onion, green chilli, salt, pepper, lemon juice and coriander. 7. Plate nachos and top with cheese sauce, salsa, rajma, corn, olives and jalapenos.

name: Ghewar
ingredients: oil/desi ghee for frying up to 1.5 liters
instructions: 1. Prepare batter with desi ghee, water and maida to correct consistency. 2. Heat oil/ghee to proper temperature. 3. Fry batter from height using wooden stick to create web-like pattern. 4. Prepare sugar syrup with sugar, water and cardamom. 5. Soak fried ghewar in sugar syrup with ice. 6. Prepare rabri by reducing milk with sugar and cardamom. 7. Prepare custard cream by cooking custard powder in milk and folding whipped cream. 8. Assemble ghewar and top with rabri/custard cream and chopped nuts, mango, berries.

name: Raj Kachori
instructions: 1. Make dough with suji and warm water, knead well. 2. Prepare stuffing mixture with besan, suji and baking soda. 3. Roll dough thin and place stuffing ring in center. 4. Deep fry hollow spheres until crispy. 5. Prepare fillings: boil potatoes and cut into dice, boil chickpeas, prepare chutneys. 6. Fill each kachori with boiled potatoes, dal moth, chickpeas and spices. 7. Top with sev, curd, chutneys, ginger, beetroot and pomegranate before serving.

name: Milk Cake
instructions: 1. Pour full fat milk into heavy-bottomed pan. 2. Heat milk until it reaches boiling point. 3. Add vinegar to curdle the milk and stop heating. 4. Strain the milk solids through cheesecloth to separate whey. 5. Collect the paneer/milk solids. 6. In a separate pan, heat sugar in minimal water to create sugar syrup. 7. Add paneer to sugar syrup and cook while crushing and mixing continuously. 8. Add ghee and continue cooking until mixture starts to come together into granules. 9. Spread on parchment paper and cut into pieces once set.

name: Khatte Meethe Aloo
instructions: 1. Heat oil in pan and add asafoetida. 2. Add all dry spices: dry red chilli, fenugreek seeds, nigella seeds, mustard seeds, fennel seeds, curry leaves, ginger and cumin. Let them crackle. 3. Add chopped onion and saute until translucent. 4. Add chopped raw mango and cook for 2 minutes. 5. Add boiled potato pieces. 6. Season with salt, green chilli, turmeric and chilli powder. 7. Add fresh mango puree and 1 cup water. 8. Simmer for 10 minutes until flavors meld. 9. Adjust seasoning with black salt. 10. In separate pan, heat ghee with kashmiri chilli powder for tempering. Pour over prepared curry.

name: Misal Pav
instructions: Boil sprouts to prepare usal. Make wet masala by heating oil, add curry leaves, hing, sliced onions, tomatoes, turmeric, chilli powder and garam masala. Combine wet masala with boiled sprouts and cooking liquid, simmer until well mixed. Assemble misal pav by placing usal mixture in bowl, top with onion, coriander, serve hot with pav and lemon.

name: Pineapple Coconut Burfi
instructions: 1. Prepare sugar syrup by boiling sugar and water, add cardamom powder. 2. Soak and soften pineapple pieces in the syrup. 3. Chop coconut roughly and mix with pineapple. 4. Mix custard powder with the pineapple-coconut mixture. 5. Heat ghee in heavy-bottomed pan. 6. Add pineapple-coconut-custard mixture to ghee and cook on medium heat. 7. Stir continuously until mixture becomes thick and starts to leave sides of pan. 8. Spread mixture on parchment paper to about 0.5 inch thickness. 9. Cool completely until it becomes firm. 10. Cut into squares and top with chopped nuts. 11. Store in airtight container.

name: Pani Puri Flavored Waters
instructions: For pudina pani: Blend mint, coriander, green chilli, ginger. Strain. Mix with roasted cumin powder, chaat masala, amchur, black salt, lemon juice, and chilled water. For imli pani: Mix tamarind chutney with water, kashmiri chilli powder, and sugar. For guava pani: Mix guava juice with kashmiri chilli powder, roasted cumin, lemon juice, salt, black salt, and mint. For cola pani: Mix cola with lemon juice, black salt, salt, roasted cumin, and chaat masala. For orange pani: Mix orange juice with coriander powder, chaat masala, salt, and chopped mint. Serve chilled with gol gappe and fillings.

name: Chicken Burger
instructions: Mix chicken mince with chopped ginger, garlic, salt, chilli flakes, green chilli, processed cheese, garam masala, mint, and coriander. Form into 4 patties. Fry in oil on both sides until cooked through and crispy. For garlic chilli sauce: Mix mayonnaise, cheese spread, chopped garlic, chilli flakes, kasoori methi powder, and milk. Toast burger buns with butter. Spread sauce on bun base. Place chicken patty, add mustard sauce, tomato slice, onion slice, cheese slice, salad leaf, and gherkins. Cover with top bun. Serve with french fries.

name: Eggless Red Velvet Cake
instructions: Cream together butter and powdered sugar. Add sliced banana and mash while creaming. Add yogurt and mix well. Add red food color and mix evenly. Fold in all-purpose flour, baking powder, baking soda, and cocoa powder using a spatula. Line a glass container with butter paper. Pour batter and level the surface. Bake in microwave at moderate heat (reheat setting) for 4 minutes. Check for doneness. Allow to cool for 2 minutes, then remove and place on cooling rack. For icing: Whip cream cheese and butter until soft and light. Add powdered sugar and whisk until incorporated. Once cake cools completely, trim edges evenly. Spread icing on cooled cake. Decorate as desired.

name: Chicken Lollipops
ingredients: corn flour slurry - 1 tbsp corn flour + 3 tbsp water
instructions: Soak dry kashmiri chillies in water and grind into paste. Mix chicken wings with salt, pepper, ginger, garlic, soya sauce, chilli paste, cornstarch, flour, baking soda, and egg. Marinate for 30 minutes. Deep fry marinated wings until golden and cooked through. Drain on paper. For sauce: Heat oil, add dry red chillies, chopped ginger, garlic, and onion. Sauté until fragrant. Add chilli paste, tomato ketchup, soya sauce, sugar, and water. Simmer. Add cornstarch slurry to thicken. Add fried chicken wings, toss well. Add spring onion and vinegar. Toss again. Serve immediately with lemon wedges and salad.

name: vada pav burger
instructions: 1. Heat oil, add mustard seeds, ginger, garlic, green chilli, curry leaves. 2. Add crushed boiled potatoes with turmeric and salt. 3. Make besan batter with turmeric, salt, chilli powder and water. 4. Heat oil for deep frying. 5. Coat potato mixture in batter and fry until golden. 6. Make garlic chutney by blending garlic, peanuts, sesame seeds, kashmiri chilli, coriander, salt and coconut. 7. Toast pao buns. 8. Assemble burger: layer lettuce, fried vada, cheese, onion slices, mayo, chutneys and pomegranate seeds.

name: cold coffee 3 ways
instructions: Classic: Blend chilled milk, coffee powder, vanilla ice cream and sugar, top with cinnamon. Mocha Frappuccino: Blend ice cubes, milk, chocolate sauce, coffee powder and powdered sugar with vanilla extract. Caramel Frappuccino: Make caramel sauce by melting sugar with lemon juice, adding cream and butter; blend with ice, milk, coffee powder and vanilla extract, top with whipped cream.

name: kolkata chicken biryani
instructions: 1. Cut and shallow fry potatoes until golden. 2. Toast and grind spices for biryani masala. 3. Marinate chicken with ginger paste, garlic paste, chilli powder, saffron, kewra, rosewater, bay leaf, masala, yogurt and ghee. 4. Fry onions until golden and crispy. 5. Cook basmati rice in boiling salted water with whole spices and fried onion oil. 6. Make saffron milk by soaking saffron in milk with kewra, rosewater and ittar. 7. Layer marinated chicken, potatoes, fried onions and rice in heavy-bottomed pot. 8. Pour saffron milk over layers. 9. Seal pot with dough and cook on high heat for 2-3 minutes until steam forms, then low heat for 45-60 minutes.

name: egg pakora
instructions: 1. Make creamy dip: mix boiled eggs, mayo, salt, onion, coriander, green chilli, ginger, pepper, chilli powder, chaat masala, garlic and lemon juice. 2. Prepare besan batter: mix besan with ginger, green chilli, ajwain, salt, chilli powder, turmeric, cumin, coriander, kasoori methi and water to make smooth batter. 3. Dip each whole boiled egg in batter coating completely. 4. Heat oil for deep frying. 5. Fry coated eggs until golden brown on all sides. 6. Drain on paper towels. 7. Serve with creamy dip and garnish with chopped peppers.

name: fried onions
instructions: 1. Peel and core onions completely. 2. Slice onions into uniform thin slices. 3. Remove excess moisture by spreading on paper towels. 4. Heat oil to appropriate frying temperature. 5. Add sliced onions to hot oil in batches. 6. Fry onions continuously, stirring occasionally with slotted spoon to ensure even browning. 7. Continue frying until onions turn golden brown and crispy (approximately 30-45 minutes total). 8. Remove onions with slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. 9. Cool completely before storing in airtight container. 10. Store in cool dry place; shelf life 2-3 weeks.

name: watermelon juice
instructions: 1. Dice fresh watermelon into cubes removing seeds. 2. Slice raw mango into pieces. 3. Slice cucumber. 4. Add watermelon, mango, cucumber pieces and ginger to blender. 5. Blend until smooth. 6. Add sugar, salt, pepper powder and mint leaves. 7. Blend again for 10-15 seconds. 8. Strain through fine sieve if desired. 9. Pour into glasses and serve chilled over ice cubes.

name: Atta Biscuits
instructions: Cream together ghee and powdered sugar. Mix in atta, besan, baking powder, cardamom powder, and salt. Add a dash of milk to form dough. Knead well. Shape into small discs and decorate with nuts and chocolate chips. Layer salt in a kadai with a metal ring, place biscuits on the ring, cover and bake on low heat for 15-20 minutes until golden and crispy. Alternatively use an oven at 350F for similar time.

name: Bharwan Dum Aloo
instructions: Boil potatoes with turmeric until tender. For curry: Heat butter, add dry spices (cinnamon, cloves, mace, star anise, black cardamom, cardamom), sauté briefly. Add roughly chopped onion and garlic, cook till soft. Add ginger, green chillies, tomatoes, and cashew nuts. Simmer until tomatoes are soft. Cool slightly and grind to smooth paste. Strain through fine sieve. Heat remaining butter, add cumin, kashmiri chilli powder, turmeric, salt. Pour the strained curry, add water and simmer. For stuffing: Mix grated paneer, cheese, capsicum, coriander, green chilli, ginger, garam masala, salt, and pomegranate seeds. Scoop centers from boiled potatoes and stuff with paneer mixture. Fry stuffed potatoes in butter until golden. Add to curry, simmer for 10 minutes. Finish with cream, kasoori methi powder, and sugar to taste.

name: Veg Sweet Corn Soup
instructions: Boil sweet corn kernels in water until soft. Make puree of boiled corn. Heat oil in a pan, add dry red chillies, chopped garlic and ginger, sauté for 1 minute. Add chopped onion and cook until translucent. Add chopped carrot, cabbage, and beans, stir fry for 2-3 minutes. Pour water and bring to boil, add corn puree. Add soya sauce, white pepper, vinegar, and salt. Simmer for 5-7 minutes. Mix corn starch with water to make slurry, add to soup to thicken. Stir well and cook for 2 minutes. Garnish with chopped spring onions and serve hot.

name: Chilli Garlic Noodles
instructions: For chilli paste: Heat oil in a pan, add chopped garlic, ginger, and spring onions, cook briefly. Add chilli flakes and mix well. Add soya sauce and sugar, mix and cool. For noodles: Boil noodles in salted water, drain and set aside. Heat oil in a wok or large pan, add dry red chillies, cook for 30 seconds. Add garlic, ginger, and sliced onion, stir fry for 1 minute. Add julienned carrot, shredded cabbage and all capsicums, stir fry for 2-3 minutes. Add boiled noodles to the wok. Add 3 tbsp of prepared chilli paste, light soya sauce, vinegar, and salt to taste. Toss everything together for 2-3 minutes until well combined and hot. Garnish with chopped spring onions and serve immediately.

name: Coconut Chutney
instructions: Add grated coconut, roasted channa dal, ginger, green chilli, tamarind, salt, and water to a blender or grinder. Blend until smooth, adding more water if needed to achieve a creamy consistency. Heat oil in a small pan, add dry red chillies and urad dal, fry until golden. Add mustard seeds and let them crackle. Add curry leaves and fry for 10 seconds. Pour the tempering over the coconut chutney and mix well. Serve with idli, dosa, medu vada, or dal vada.

name: Roasted Potato Salad
instructions: Peel and cut potatoes into chunks. In a bowl, mix baking soda and salt with water. Add potato chunks and let soak for 5 minutes to remove starch. Drain and pat dry. Heat oil in a large pan or wok, add chopped garlic and fry until fragrant. Add dried potato chunks and stir fry continuously over medium-high heat for 10-12 minutes until golden and crispy on all sides. Remove from heat and cool slightly. For dressing: Mix mayonnaise (or hung curd) with grated soft cheese, kasoori methi powder, and chilli flakes in a bowl. Heat mustard oil separately, add chopped onions and sauté until light brown. Add chopped tomatoes and cook for 2 minutes. Cool the onion-tomato mixture and mix into the cheese mayo. Add chopped spring onions and coriander. Combine roasted potatoes with the dressing, toss gently. Top with papri and serve immediately.

name: Gaund and Dry Fruit Laddoos
instructions: Roast gaund in ghee until crispy. Separately roast almonds, cashews, peanuts, pumpkin seeds, khus-khus and sesame. Grind all roasted ingredients together. Roast besan and aatta in ghee until fragrant. Combine roasted flour with ghee, add ground nuts and dry fruits, cardamom powder, black cardamom seeds, ginger powder and sugar. Mix well and shape into laddoos while still warm.

name: Grilled Cheese Sandwich
instructions: Spread butter on bread slices. Layer grated cheddar and mozzarella cheese on bread, sprinkle black pepper. Place on hot tawa or pan and toast until golden brown and cheese melts. For dip: mix mayonnaise, mustard paste, black pepper and chopped coriander together.

name: Sweet Corn Chaat Salad
instructions: Boil corn nibblets with milk, water and salt until tender. Prepare amchur chutney by mixing amachur powder, water, kashmiri chilli powder, black salt, sugar and roasted cumin. Chop all vegetables and salad leaves. Combine boiled corn with fresh vegetables and salad leaves. Dress with amchur chutney and lemon juice. Garnish with chaat masala and serve.

name: Cheese Burst Tawa Pizza
instructions: Make pizza dough with flour, semolina, salt, baking powder, baking soda, sugar, italian seasoning, curd, oil and water; knead smooth and let rest. Prepare tomato sauce by heating olive oil, saute garlic and chillies, add onion and tomato, season with salt, pepper and basil. Cook vegetables separately with oil. Heat tawa on high heat, roll dough into circle, place on tawa and cook until bottom sets. Spread sauce on dough, add cooked vegetables, cheese spread and mozzarella. Cover and cook until cheese melts and crust is crispy.

name: Poori and Halwai Style Aloo Bhaji
instructions: Make poori dough with whole wheat flour, semolina, salt, oil and water; knead smooth and let rest. Boil potatoes. Make aloo bhaji by heating ghee, add cumin and cracked coriander seeds, add asafoetida, chopped ginger and green chillies, add turmeric and chilli powder, add tomato and boiled potatoes, pour water, simmer until gravy thickens, finish with garam masala and coriander. Deep fry poori dough balls in hot oil until puffed and golden. Serve hot with aloo bhaji.

name: Bread Rasmalai
instructions: Reduce full fat milk with condensed milk until thickened and creamy. Remove bread crusts and cut into cubes. Stuff bread cubes with khoya mixed with cardamom powder and chopped pista. Gently shape into round balls. Pour thickened milk into serving dish, carefully add bread-khoya balls. Add saffron, rose water and kewra water. Garnish generously with sliced cashew, almond and pista. Chill thoroughly and serve cold.

name: Indori Poha
instructions: Make jeeravan masala: Dry roast whole spices (cumin, coriander, cinnamon, fennel, peppercorns, cloves, bay leaf) on low flame until aromatic. Cool to room temperature, add grated nutmeg. Transfer to grinding jar, add powdered spices (ginger powder, dry mango powder, black salt, red chilli powder, kashmiri red chilli powder, salt, turmeric, asafoetida) and sugar. Grind to fine powder, store in airtight container. Make poha: Add poha to sieve and rinse very gently with water. Let rest 10 minutes in sieve without soaking. Separate poha using spoon. Add salt, sugar, lemon juice, turmeric powder and mix gently. Make tadka: Heat oil in pan, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, fennel seeds, green chilli, asafoetida, turmeric powder, stir briefly. Pour tadka over poha and mix gently. Place sieve over stock pot with boiling water, cover and steam 10 minutes. Top with lemon juice, jeeravan masala, ratlami sev, chopped onions, and pomegranate.

name: Assamese Roasted Chicken
instructions: Cut chicken into small pieces. First marination: mix with salt, turmeric, chilli powder, cumin powder, black pepper, and lemon juice. Let marinate for 30 minutes. Make black sesame paste by grinding black sesame with onion, garlic, ginger, and green chilli into a fine paste. Second marination: coat marinated chicken with this sesame paste and mustard oil. Marinate for another 30 minutes. Place in OTG tray or roasting pan and roast at high temperature for 25-30 minutes, basting with ghee or butter halfway through. Chicken should be cooked through and edges charred. Serve hot with lime wedges.

name: Eggless Chocolate Lava Cake
instructions: For ganache filling: melt 20gm dark chocolate with 20gm butter and mix in 1tsp cocoa powder. Set aside. For batter: sift together 110gm maida, 1tsp baking powder, 30gm cocoa powder, and 90gm powdered sugar. In another bowl, whisk 110ml milk with 1tsp vanilla essence and 40ml oil. Combine wet and dry ingredients smoothly. Butter a ramekin (8.5 cm diameter, 3 cm height) and dust with cocoa powder. Pour half the batter into ramekin. Place ganache in center. Top with remaining batter. Place ramekin in a round baking dish with 1kg salt (salt bath for even heating). Cook in pressure cooker without whistle for 12 minutes. Cool slightly and invert onto plate. Garnish with icing sugar, sliced strawberries, and mint sprigs.

name: Kolkata Egg Roll
instructions: For dough: mix 2 cups maida, generous pinch salt, 1/2 tsp sugar, and 2 tbsp ghee. Add lukewarm water gradually while kneading to form soft dough. Knead for 5-7 minutes and let rest 15 minutes. Divide into 4 equal balls. Roll each into thin paratha (thinner than regular). Heat tawa lightly smeared with ghee and cook parathas until light golden spots appear on both sides. For filling: scramble 4 eggs with salt in a pan. Prepare veggie mix: combine sliced cucumber, julienned carrots, sliced onion, and chopped green chillies. Mix with 4 tbsp lemon juice, generous pinch chaat masala, and generous pinch black salt. Assembly: place cooked paratha on flat surface. Add one scramble egg serving in a line down the center. Top with veggie mix. Add 2 tbsp tomato ketchup and 1 tbsp green chilli chutney over filling. Roll tightly from one side to form a cylinder. Serve immediately with extra ketchup and chutney on the side.

name: Rumali Roti
ingredients: refined flour as required for dusting
instructions: Mix refined flour, wheat flour, salt, and sugar in a bowl. Add milk in batches and combine well. Transfer to table top and knead with stretch and fold method for 10-12 minutes, sprinkling flour if sticky. Form into a dough ball, place in greased bowl, drizzle oil over surface, cover with damp cloth, and rest for at least 2 hours. Prepare salt water by mixing salt and water. Heat iron tawa on high heat until very hot. Sprinkle salt water over hot tawa; water evaporates leaving salt coating. Take a portion of dough, form roundel, coat with flour, and roll into extremely thin roti while dusting with flour. Place on hot tawa and flip after 10-15 seconds. Cook from both sides until tiny brown spots appear. Remove and serve hot.

name: Chocolate Walnut Brownie
instructions: In a microwave-safe container or mug, combine 22gm salted butter and 30gm dark chocolate. Microwave for 30 seconds until melted and smooth. Add 10gm powdered sugar and a few drops of vanilla extract. Mix well. Add 35gm all-purpose flour (maida) and 30ml milk. Mix into smooth batter using a fork or spoon. Fold in crushed walnuts. Transfer batter to butter paper-lined microwave-safe container or ceramic mug. Top with chocolate chunks. Microwave on high power for 75 seconds (1 minute 15 seconds). Brownie will be fudgy inside with edges set. Remove from microwave carefully (container will be hot). Serve warm immediately. Can dust with icing sugar and serve with vanilla ice cream if desired.

name: Mawa Kachori
instructions: 1. Prepare kachori dough with maida, ghee, oil and water. 2. For filling, combine dates, toasted nuts, khoya and all spice powders with powdered sugar. 3. Make sugar syrup with sugar, water and saffron. 4. Stuff the dough with filling mixture. 5. Shape into kachoris. 6. Deep fry until golden brown in oil. 7. Soak in warm sugar syrup. 8. Garnish with chopped nuts and serve.

name: Eggless Banana Bread
instructions: 1. Peel and mash ripe bananas. 2. Mix sugar, curd and oil with bananas and vanilla extract. 3. In separate bowl, combine flour, baking powder, baking soda and cinnamon powder. 4. Fold dry ingredients into wet mixture. 5. Add crushed walnuts. 6. Pour into baking mould lined with butter paper. 7. Bake in oven at 180°C for 40-45 minutes until golden, or steam in kadhai for about 25-30 minutes. 8. Cool and slice to serve.

name: Lauki Ki Barfi
instructions: 1. Peel and grate lauki (bottle gourd). 2. In a large pan, heat milk and add grated lauki. 3. Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally, until milk reduces and lauki becomes soft. 4. Add sugar and continue cooking until mixture thickens and becomes creamy. 5. Add ghee and cardamom powder. 6. Add green food colour if desired. 7. Continue stirring until it reaches a fudge-like consistency. 8. Pour into a greased pan and spread evenly. 9. Sprinkle chopped nuts on top. 10. Cool and cut into pieces. Serve at room temperature or chilled.

name: Ginger Turmeric Lemonade
instructions: 1. Take water in a pitcher or glass. 2. Add fresh turmeric slices and fresh ginger pieces. 3. Squeeze fresh lemon juice into the water. 4. Add honey for sweetness. 5. Stir well to combine. 6. Add ice cubes. 7. Serve chilled immediately.

name: Aloo Changezi
ingredients: tender coriander stems with roots 2 tbsp
instructions: 1. Cut potatoes into thick slices and marinate with red chilli powder, turmeric, salt, ghee and ginger-garlic paste. 2. In mixer grinder, blend fried onions, cashew nuts, salt, turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, water and coriander stems into smooth paste. 3. Heat oil in pan and fry marinated potatoes until half cooked, drain on paper. 4. In cast iron tawa, heat remaining oil and add bay leaf, cinnamon, cardamom, peppercorns, cloves and ginger-garlic paste. 5. Add tomato puree and curd, saute until cooked. 6. Add prepared paste, red chilli powder, coriander powder and salt. 7. Cook 7-8 minutes on medium flame. 8. Add sugar and water to adjust consistency and cook until thick. 9. Add fried potatoes and mix well. 10. Finish with crushed fenugreek leaves and garnish with coriander. 11. Serve hot with tandoori roti or naan.

name: anda bhurji masala
ingredients: 1 heaped tsp degi red chili powder
instructions: Make ginger garlic paste by grinding garlic, green chillies and ginger. In a kadai add ghee and oil, heat and add cumin seeds and ginger garlic paste, saute 1 minute. Add sliced onions and cook until translucent. Add coriander powder, degi red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder and saute well. Add salt, tomato puree, capsicum, butter and dry fenugreek leaves, cook 7-8 minutes. Add water and whole eggs, scramble well. Add more water to adjust consistency, add butter and finish with coriander leaves. Serve hot with ladi pav, garnished with butter, lemon and coriander.

name: appe dal
instructions: Soak chana dal and urad dal overnight. Grind soaked dals with curd, ginger, green chillies, curry leaves into smooth paste. Mix with salt, chopped green chili, coriander stems, curry leaves and baking soda. In appe chatti, add oil and spoon of batter, cook 5-6 minutes both sides until cooked. For dal: pressure cook toor dal, yellow moong dal, water, salt, turmeric, red chili powder, asafoetida for 4-5 whistles. For tadka: heat oil, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, red chillies, curry leaves and splutter. Add boiled dal, tamarind water, jaggery, simmer. Serve dal with appe, garnish with green chili, onion, curry leaves and lemon juice.

name: dimer kosha
ingredients: 1 heaped tsp degi red chili powder
instructions: Fry diced potatoes in mustard oil until golden brown and cooked. Transfer to paper. Fry hard boiled eggs in same oil until golden brown. For curry: add mustard oil to kadai, heat until smoky, add bay leaf, dry red chillies, cumin seeds and splutter. Add onions and saute until golden. Mix coriander powder, cumin powder, degi red chili powder, turmeric powder, ginger garlic paste with water and add to pan. Cook 2-3 minutes. Add chopped tomatoes, fried potatoes, water and green chillies, cover and cook until tomatoes are mushy. Adjust consistency with water, add salt and fried eggs, give quick boil. Serve hot with paratha or rice, garnished with coriander.

name: eggless christmas pudding
instructions: Mix refined flour, almond flour, dry ginger powder, cinnamon powder, clove powder, baking soda, nutmeg powder, cocoa powder. Add milkmaid, prepared wet mixture (tutti frutti, raisins, apricots, orange juice, butter boiled together and cooled), oil, caramel colour, orange juice and mix well. Pour into greased mold and steam 15-20 minutes until cooked. Cool and demold. For sauce: caramelize butter with sugar, add milkmaid and water. For caramelized orange: cook sugar with orange wedges until caramelized. For syrup: boil water, orange marmalade and salt. Prick pudding and pour syrup over it. Serve warm in a plate with sauce, pudding, candied cherries, caramelized orange, icing sugar and mint sprig.

name: Kantola Sabji (No Onion Garlic)
instructions: Grind green chillies, ginger, and salt into a coarse paste. Heat oil in a kadai, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, and curry leaves until they splutter. Add the ginger-chili paste and asafoetida, sauté for a minute. Add sliced spine gourd and cook for 2-3 minutes on medium flame. Sprinkle degi red chili powder, turmeric powder, and coriander powder, sauté until half-cooked. Cover with lid and cook for 5-6 minutes. Separately, prepare nuts and coconut mixture by frying cashews, raisins, and peanuts in oil until golden, then add grated coconut and cook for 5-6 minutes, finish with red chili powder, mango powder, and salt. Add this mixture to the sabji along with salt and fresh coriander leaves. Finish with lemon juice. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with coriander. Serve hot with dal and rice or chapati.

name: Gujarati Patra
instructions: Shred colocasia leaves and mix with salt, degi red chili powder, asafoetida, turmeric powder, gram flour, carom seeds, coriander leaves, tamarind and jaggery mixture to form a filling. Prepare batter by blending jaggery water, tamarind pulp, oil, garlic, green chillies, ginger, and salt. Spread this batter on colocasia leaves, roll tightly, and cut into pieces. Prepare a curd mixture by whisking curd with gram flour, water, red chili powder, turmeric powder, and coriander powder. Heat oil in a kadai, add dry kashmiri red chillies, mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds, green chillies, and ginger. Pour in the curd mixture and water, bring to a simmer. Shallow fry the patra pieces and add them to the gravy. Cook until tender. Prepare a tadka of oil, mustard seeds, asafoetida, sesame seeds, curry leaves, and pour over the dish. Garnish with grated coconut and coriander. Serve hot.

name: Paneer Kaju Makhana Masala
instructions: Heat ghee and oil in a handi. Add chopped garlic and sliced onions, sauté until translucent. Add beaten curd, green chili, ginger garlic paste, coriander powder, degi red chili powder, turmeric powder, and salt. Cook for 5-6 minutes on medium flame. Add foxnuts, soaked and halved cashew nuts, and water. Cover and cook for a while until ready. Transfer to mixer grinder and blend into a smooth paste. In the same handi, add ghee and the prepared gravy paste, simmer with water for 2-3 minutes. Add cubed paneer, salt, and tender coriander stems. Finish with cashew nuts and sugar. Separately, fry foxnuts in ghee with salt and red chili powder until crispy. Transfer curry to serving dish and garnish with fried foxnuts, fried cashew nuts, crushed fenugreek leaves, fresh cream, chili oil, and coriander sprig. Serve hot with roti or paratha.

name: stuffed tinda masala
instructions: Scrape tinda lightly to remove outer skin. Mix turmeric, coriander powder, fennel powder, kashmiri chilli, dry fenugreek, mango powder, and garam masala. Slit each tinda and carefully stuff with masala. Heat mustard oil, add cumin and asafoetida. Add chopped tomato, ginger, green chilli. Place stuffed tindas, add remaining salt and spices. Cover and cook on low-medium heat for 15-20 minutes until tindas are tender. Stir gently once or twice. Serve hot garnished with coriander leaves.

name: Adraki Aloo Gobhi
instructions: Clean cauliflower by soaking in salted water, then drain. Grind ginger, green chili, coriander stems with roots, and beaten curd into a smooth paste. Heat mustard oil in a kadai until smoky hot, add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Add prepared paste, turmeric powder, coriander powder, degi red chili powder, and asafoetida. Cook for a while. Add diced potatoes and water, cook for 2-3 minutes. Add cauliflower florets and salt, mix well. Cover with lid and cook for 12-15 minutes on medium flame until vegetables are tender. Finish with lemon juice, julienned ginger, sliced green chillies, fresh coriander leaves, and ghee. Toss gently. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with ginger, green chillies, and coriander sprig. Serve hot with roti.

name: Shahi Kaju Katli
instructions: In a non-stick pan or wok, heat ghee and add cashew nuts paste. Cook until doughy consistency is reached. Add Nestlé MILKMAID condensed milk and cook for 5 minutes, stirring well. Transfer the mixture to a greased tray and spread evenly. Refrigerate for 2-3 hours until completely set. Remove from refrigerator and transfer to a non-stick kadai. Add rose syrup and desiccated coconut, mix well. Cook for 4-5 minutes on low flame, stirring constantly. Transfer this mixture to a greased tray and spread evenly. Garnish with silver vark and golden vark. Refrigerate for 2-3 hours to set completely. Demold the set mixture and cut into desired diamond or square shapes. Transfer to a serving platter and garnish generously with chopped pistachios and fresh rose petals. Serve chilled.

name: Dhaba Style Chicken Curry
instructions: Fry sliced onions in oil and mustard oil for 7-8 minutes until golden, transfer to paper. Blend tomatoes, ginger, green chillies, salt, curd, red chili powder, coriander powder, turmeric, cumin powder, coriander stems and fried onions into smooth paste. Marinate chicken with mustard oil, salt, coriander powder, turmeric, red chili powder and ginger-garlic paste for 10 minutes. Fry marinated chicken in the same oil for 5-6 minutes until half cooked. Add prepared paste to the oil, cook briefly, add fried chicken, cinnamon stick and water, cover and cook 10-15 minutes on medium heat. Finish with ginger and fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot with roti or naan.

name: Aloo Tikki Chaat with Aam ka Murabba
instructions: For aloo tikki: Mix grated raw and boiled potatoes with oil, salt and cornstarch, cover and rest. Divide into balls and shallow fry in ghee-oil mixture on tawa until crispy golden. For aam ka murabba: Heat oil, add cumin seeds and dry chillies, add chopped raw mangoes, water, jaggery, salt and red chili powder, cook 10-12 minutes until mushy, add asafoetida water and cool. Make masala by dry roasting peppercorns, coriander seeds, fennel seeds, cumin seeds and salt, grind coarsely. For serving: Place crushed aloo tikki in dish, top with sweetened curd, sprinkle masala, add aam ka murabba and pomegranate pearls. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Goan Ros Omelette
instructions: For paste: Heat oil, add bay leaf and cumin seeds, let splutter. Add coriander seeds, peppercorns, black cardamom, cloves and cinnamon, saute until aromatic. Add sliced onions and salt, cook until translucent. Add garlic, ginger, coriander leaves, red chili powder and turmeric, saute well. Cool and grind into smooth paste. For ros: Heat oil in same pan, add prepared paste, cook until it leaves the sides. Add water, salt and sugar, simmer 7-8 minutes on low heat. For omelette: Crack eggs, add green chillies, coriander stems and salt, beat well. Heat oil in pan, pour egg mixture, cook 2-3 minutes on both sides. For salad: Mix chopped onion, tomatoes, green chili, coriander stems, coriander leaves, oil, salt and lemon juice. Serve hot omelette with ros, salad and ladi pav.

name: Gavar Phali Sabji
instructions: For dry sabji: Heat mustard oil in iron kadai, add cumin and fennel seeds, let splutter. Add sliced onion and asafoetida, saute briefly. Add sliced potatoes, cook 4-5 minutes on low heat until half cooked. Add chopped guar beans, salt, turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder and ginger, saute well. Cover and cook 7-8 minutes on low heat. Finish with green chili, coriander leaves and mango powder. For gravy sabji: Heat oil, add cumin and fennel seeds, let splutter. Add sliced onion and ginger, saute 2-3 minutes. Add turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder, curd and salt, cook briefly. Add potatoes and jaggery, cook until potatoes are half done. Add guar beans and water, cover and cook 10-12 minutes on medium heat. Finish with tomato, coriander leaves, sugar and mango powder. Serve hot with roti.

name: Lemon Rice with Crispy Rice Vada
instructions: For lemon rice: Heat oil in wok/kadai, add mustard seeds, let splutter. Add urad dal, chana dal, dry red chillies, peanuts and cashew nuts, saute until golden. Add green chillies, curry leaves and turmeric powder, saute 1 minute. Add lemon juice, sugar, cooked rice and salt, mix well. Finish with lemon juice, curry leaves, salt and water, cover and cook briefly. Serve garnished with fried cashews, red chillies and curry leaves. For rice vada: Mix curd and semolina, let rest 4-5 minutes. Add green chillies, ginger, prepared lemon rice and curry leaves, mix well. Divide into balls and shape as desired. Deep fry in hot oil until golden brown. In same oil, fry green chillies 1 minute and cashew nuts until golden. Serve vada hot with lemon rice.

name: Methi Dal Tadka
instructions: In a pressure cooker, add ghee, oil, cumin seeds, dry kashmiri red chillies, washed dals and salt. Add degi red chilli powder, turmeric powder, water, cover and cook for 3-4 whistles. In a handi, add oil and ghee, add cumin seeds, asafoetida, ginger, dry red chilli and saute for a minute. Add tomatoes, coriander stems, green chillies, soaked fenugreek leaves and salt, saute until tomatoes are mushy. Add fresh tomato puree and cook for 5-6 minutes on high heat. Add coriander powder, degi red chilli powder, fresh fenugreek leaves and ghee, saute well. Add cooked dal, salt and let simmer for 7-8 minutes on medium heat. Finish with pinch of sugar, lemon juice and fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot with roti and steamed rice.

name: Pahadi Khatte Aloo
instructions: Boil yellow mustard seeds in water with salt for 4-5 minutes, then strain. Make a paste with coriander stems, ginger, green chillies, garlic, and boiled mustard seeds. Marinate halved potatoes in mustard oil, degi red chilli powder and salt. Shallow fry marinated potatoes in hot mustard oil until half cooked. In a handi with remaining oil, saute the paste for 2-3 minutes. Add onions, salt and sugar, cook until golden. Add turmeric, coriander and degi red chilli powder, cook for a minute. Add tomatoes and water, cook until mushy. Add fried potatoes, additional onion pieces and water to adjust consistency. Cover and cook for 7-8 minutes on medium heat. Finish with dry mango powder, oil, lemon juice and coriander leaves. In a separate pan, fry dry kashmiri chillies in hot oil until crispy. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with fried chillies and coriander.

name: Aloo Tamatar Pyaaz Ki Sabji
instructions: In a pressure cooker, add mustard oil and heat until smoky. Add cumin seeds, fennel seeds, mustard seeds, black peppercorns and let them splutter well. Add asafoetida and onions, cook for a while. Add tomatoes and cook briefly, then add degi red chilli powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, diced potatoes, salt and water. Mix well, cover with lid and pressure cook for 1-2 whistles until cooked well. For tadka: In a pan, add ghee and heat, add dry red chillies and fry until crispy, add diced tomato and salt, toss for a minute. Finish the sabji with prepared tadka and fresh coriander leaves, mix well. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with coriander sprigs. Serve hot with roti.

name: Rasam Vada
instructions: Boil toor dal in water with salt and turmeric for 30-40 minutes until cooked. For rasam powder: Heat oil, add chana dal, kashmiri chillies, curry leaves, urad dal and toss for a minute. Add coriander seeds, cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds, black peppercorns and toss for 2-3 minutes. Add degi red chilli powder, turmeric and salt, mix well, transfer to grinder and grind into fine powder. For vada mixture: Mix semolina, salt, curd, let rest for 10 minutes. Add water, prepared tadka and baking soda, rest for 5-6 minutes. Deep fry lemon-sized balls in hot oil until golden, transfer to absorbent paper. For tadka: Heat oil, add mustard seeds, curry leaves and let splutter. Add urad dal, coriander stems, ginger, green chillies and saute for 2-3 minutes. For rasam: Heat oil, add mustard seeds, button chillies, curry leaves, crushed garlic and saute for a minute. Add tomatoes, salt and cook until soft. Add rasam powder, coriander stems and saute well. Add toor dal water, salt and jaggery, simmer for 10-15 minutes on low heat. Add finishing mixture of tomatoes, coriander, curry leaves, salt, crushed peppercorns and lemon juice. Transfer to serving bowl, add fried vada, garnish with lemon wedge, coriander sprig and crushed black peppercorns. Serve hot.

name: Surmai Fish Curry
instructions: Marinate fish pieces with salt and turmeric. Grind garlic, ginger, coriander stems, red chili, oil, green chillies and curry leaves into paste. Heat oil in kadai, add fenugreek, mustard and cumin seeds to splutter. Add shallots and sauté 2-3 minutes. Add grated onions and cook well. Add paste and cook until golden. Add water, dried mangosteen and bring to boil. Add coconut milk, fried fish and cook 4-5 minutes. Finish with curry leaves and quick boil. Serve with steamed rice.

name: Kerala Fish Curry
instructions: Soak red rice 2 hours, cook in water 15-20 minutes until done, strain and reserve rice water. Make 1st coconut milk batch: grind 200g coconut with 1.5 cups water, strain through muslin. Make 2nd batch: grind remaining coconut with 0.5 cup coconut water, strain. Grind 550g coconut, shallots, green chili, ginger, garlic, spices and 1/3 cup coconut water into smooth paste. Heat coconut oil, add mustard and fenugreek seeds. Add shallots and curry leaves, cook 1 minute. Add paste and cook 8-10 minutes. Add 2nd coconut milk batch, brindle berries, rice water, simmer 5-6 minutes. Add water, raw mango, marinated fish, salt and 1st coconut milk, cook on low. Prepare tadka with shallots and green chili in coconut oil. Finish curry with tadka. Serve hot with red rice.

name: Dum Ka Anda Curry
instructions: Mix curd, degi red chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder, salt, ginger-garlic paste, green chillies, tender coriander stems, fried onions and ghee in bowl. Add boiled eggs, kewra water and rose water, mix well. Transfer to handi and cook on low to medium flame for 12-15 minutes. Add hot water to adjust consistency and bring to quick boil. Finish with fresh coriander leaves and mix well. Transfer to serving dish and garnish. Serve hot with roti or paratha.

name: lauki kofta
instructions: Grate bottle gourd, add salt and let sit to release moisture. Squeeze out water using muslin cloth. Mix with ginger garlic paste, fenugreek leaves, semolina, gram flour, green chillies, peppercorns and curd. Add water, salt, coriander, oil and baking soda. Grease steaming mold with carom seeds, pour mixture and steam for 12-15 minutes. Cool and cut into desired shapes. For gravy: heat oil, add fenugreek and cumin seeds, green chilli, cook. Add onions and ginger garlic paste until golden. Add turmeric, red chili, coriander powders. Add tomato puree and cook 5-6 minutes. Add water and simmer. Shallow fry prepared koftas until golden. Combine with gravy, garnish with coriander. Serve hot with roti and rice.

name: keema pulav
instructions: Marinate keema with salt, red chili, turmeric, coriander powder, ghee, curd, ginger garlic paste and lemon juice. Heat oil and ghee, add cinnamon, bay leaf, cloves, peppercorns and cardamom. Add onions and saute until golden. Add marinated keema and cook 20-25 minutes until 60-70% done. Add coriander stems, green chillies, mint leaves and fried onions, cook 1 minute. Add water, salt, ginger, pre-soaked basmati rice and tomatoes. Add kewra and rose water. Bring to boil, cover and cook on low heat 10-15 minutes. Cool and transfer to serving dish. Garnish with coriander and mint. Prepare chutney with garlic, red chili powder, salt and curd. Serve hot with prepared lehsun mirch chutney.

name: chicken malai tikka
instructions: Marinate chicken with salt, ginger garlic paste and lemon juice. For second marination, blend fresh cream, soaked cashews, cheese, cheese spread, garlic and coriander stems into paste. Mix with hung curd. Add ginger garlic paste, cardamom powder and egg yolk. Combine both marinades and finish with nutmeg, green chillies, coriander leaves, crushed black pepper and salt. Dry roast black peppercorns, cardamom and fenugreek leaves, grind into powder. Heat ghee, add crushed garlic until golden. Mix with fresh cream and lemon juice. Thread marinated chicken on skewers. Grill in pan with ghee from all sides, brushing with flavoured mixture. Place hot coal in steel bowl, add ghee for smoking effect. Garnish with pickled onions, coriander, masala powder and lemon wedge. Serve hot with mint chutney.

name: kashmiri khubani chicken
instructions: Boil water with salt, turmeric and garlic, add chicken legs and cook 2-3 minutes until tender. Wash gently and keep aside. Fry potato in hot mustard oil to absorb smokiness, remove. Fry kashmiri onions until golden. Soak apricots and cockscomb in hot water separately. Heat mustard oil, add chili powder mixture and water, cook 1 minute. Add cockscomb water, fennel powder, dry ginger and turmeric. Add blanched chicken, salt and chicken stock. Cover and cook 10-12 minutes on medium flame. Finish with soaked apricots, wazwan garam masala and fried onions. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with almonds, fried onions and coriander. For walnut chutney: grind walnuts, onion, green chillies and coriander into paste. Mix with hung curd and salt. For salad: mix sliced onion, lemon juice, red chili powder, coriander leaves, salt and roasted cumin. Serve hot with steamed rice, walnut chutney and salad.

name: gujarati kadhi with lilva khichdi
instructions: Rinse surti kolam rice and soak 10-15 minutes. In pressure cooker, heat ghee, add cumin seeds and fresh pigeon peas, saute 1 minute. Add turmeric powder, salt, soaked rice and water. Pressure cook 2-3 whistles until done. Transfer to serving dish. For kadhi: mix gram flour, curd, water, ginger-green chilli paste, salt and jaggery. Heat ghee in handi, add cloves, cinnamon and asafoetida. Add curd mixture and bring to boil. Add water to adjust consistency, salt and jaggery. Simmer 10-12 minutes on low flame until thick. Add prepared tadka and mix well. For roasted chillies: roast fresh green and red chillies 2-3 minutes, deskin them. For tadka: heat ghee, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, curry leaves and button chillies, saute 1 minute. Garnish kadhi with coriander sprig and chilli oil. Serve hot with lilva khichdi and roasted chillies.

name: chicken lazeez
instructions: Marinate chicken with salt, ginger garlic paste, red chili powder, coriander powder, dry fenugreek leaves, oil and mustard oil for 10-12 minutes. Heat oil in pan, add marinated chicken and cook 2-3 minutes on both sides until half cooked. Set aside. For vegetables: heat oil, add diced onion, tomato and capsicum with salt, toss on high flame 2-3 minutes. Set aside. For curry: heat oil in kadai, add black peppercorns, cloves, green cardamom and mace until they splutter. Add chopped onion and kashmiri red chili, saute until translucent. Add ginger garlic paste and cook 1 minute. Add red chili powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder and curd. Cook a while. Add salt and fresh tomato puree, cook 10-12 minutes on medium flame. Add ghee and half-cooked chicken, mix well. Add water, coriander stems and simmer 3-4 minutes until thick. Finish with salt, sugar and sauteed vegetables, cook 2-3 minutes. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with coriander sprig. Serve hot with roti or naan.

name: mushroom kali mirch
instructions: Boil mushrooms in water with salt and vinegar. Strain and set aside. Mix curd with ginger garlic paste. Heat ghee in handi, add bay leaf and cinnamon stick until they splutter. Add curd mixture and saute 3-4 minutes until cooked. Add cashew nuts, water, salt, sugar and butter. Boil 4-5 minutes until thick. Blend with hand blender until smooth. Add green chillies and ginger, cook 1 minute. Remove whole spices and ginger. Add boiled mushrooms and cook 4-5 minutes until well cooked. Finish with crushed black peppercorns and mix well. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with coriander, crushed peppercorns and chilli oil. For Kashmiri Pulao: heat ghee in pan, add ginger, green chillies, onions, apricots, almonds and cashews. Toss 1 minute until onions are translucent. Add cooked rice and toss well. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with coriander sprig. Serve hot together.

name: Bande ki Sabji
ingredients: 1 heaped tbsp degi red chili powder
instructions: Fry peeled and cut yam batons in hot mustard oil and refined oil until golden, transfer to absorbent paper. In the same oil, splutter dry red chillies, fennel, cumin, nigella seeds and ginger. Add onions, curry leaves and salt, sauté until translucent. Add coriander, cumin, turmeric and degi red chili powders. Add yogurt and cook 4-5 minutes. Add tomato puree and cook well. Add fried yam, coriander stems and water to adjust consistency. Simmer 10-12 minutes on medium flame until cooked. Garnish with coriander sprig and serve hot with roti or rice.

name: Cheesy Mexican Pizza Rice
instructions: Heat oil and sauté sliced onions until translucent. Add oregano, chilli flakes, tomato puree and ketchup, cook until thick. Add green chillies, salt, sugar and soaked basmati rice, mix well. Add kidney beans, black eyed beans, sweet corn, water and salt. Cover and cook on low 10-12 minutes. Finish with coriander stems. Separately prepare salsa by mixing chopped tomatoes, onion, green chillies, coriander, tomato puree, ketchup and garlic. Make spice mix with coriander, cumin, oregano, chili powder and garlic powder. Layer rice with salsa in baking dish, top with cheese blend and bake at 180°C for 4-5 minutes until cheese melts. Sprinkle spice mix, top with salsa, nachos and garnish with coriander. Serve hot.

name: RB Style Rajma
ingredients: 1 tbsp tender coriander stems with roots
1 heaped tbsp degi red chili powder
instructions: In pressure cooker, heat ghee and oil, splutter cloves, peppercorns, black cardamom, green cardamom and bay leaf. Add green chillies, onions and salt, sauté 2-3 minutes until golden. Add coriander stems and tomato puree, cook briefly. Add coriander, chili and turmeric powders, cumin powder, mix well. Add kidney beans and ghee, stir. Add water, cover and pressure cook 4-5 whistles on medium-high flame until cooked. Depressurize. Finish with fenugreek leaves, green chillies, ginger, coriander leaves and water to adjust consistency. Boil briefly. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with coriander sprig. Serve hot with steam rice or roti.

name: Shahi Paneer Patiala
