instructions: For gravy: Heat ghee and oil in handi, add bay leaf and garlic, sauté onions until translucent. Add ginger garlic paste and dry spice powders, sauté. Add tomatoes and yogurt, cook until mushy. Add cashew nuts, salt and water, cook 10-12 minutes. Cool and grind to fine paste. For stuffing: Mix paneer crumble with coriander stems, green chillies, fenugreek, chili powder, mango powder and cumin. Stuff between paneer slabs, sprinkle salt and chili powder, cut in halves. Prepare papad rolls by dipping paneer pasanda in refined flour slurry and coating with crushed roasted papad, fry until golden. Finish gravy with fenugreek, garam masala, salt and sugar. Place fried paneer in serving dish with gravy, garnish with fresh cream and coriander. Serve hot with roti or naan.

name: Lahori Mutton Curry
ingredients: 1 heaped tbsp degi red chili powder
instructions: For cooking mutton: In pressure cooker, heat ghee and oil, add black cardamom, bay leaf, cinnamon, dry chillies and peppercorns. Add mutton with bones, cook 1 minute. Add tomatoes, salt, yogurt, degi chili powder, coriander and cumin powders, coriander stems. Cover and pressure cook 7-8 whistles until cooked. For masala: Combine peppercorns, mace, cloves, black cardamom, cinnamon, bay leaf, coriander seeds, cumin seeds and green cardamom with salt. Grind to fine powder. For Lahori mutton: In handi, heat oil, add bay leaf and cumin seeds to splutter. Add sliced onions, ginger and crushed garlic, sauté until golden. Add cooked mutton and prepared masala, sauté 10-12 minutes. Finish with julienned ginger, green chillies, fenugreek leaves and coriander leaves, mix well. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with ginger, green chillies and coriander sprig. Serve hot with paratha or tandoori roti.

name: Aloo Pyaaz Kadhi
instructions: Prepare ginger-garlic paste by grinding garlic cloves, ginger, green chili, and salt in a mortar-pestle. Prepare curd mixture by beating curd with water, gram flour, degi red chili powder, and turmeric. Heat ghee and oil; temper with fenugreek seeds, cumin seeds, and coriander seeds. Add ginger-garlic paste and kashmiri chillies; sauté 1 minute. Add sliced onions and cook until translucent. Sprinkle water and add curd mixture with salt; simmer 7-8 minutes on medium flame. Prick baby potatoes with fork; fry in hot oil until golden brown. Season fried potatoes with cumin seeds, degi red chili powder, and salt. Transfer kadhi to serving bowl, top with masala fried potatoes, garnish with coriander, and serve hot with steamed rice.

name: Dhaba Style Shahi Paneer Masala
ingredients: 1 heaped tbsp degi red chili powder
1 heaped tbsp black peppercorns for masala
instructions: Prepare dry masala by dry roasting black cardamom, green cardamom, coriander seeds, cumin seeds, black peppercorns, cloves, and fennel seeds for 3-4 minutes on low flame; add kasuri methi and grind to fine powder. For gravy, heat ghee, temper with cloves and cardamom, add ginger, green chili, and onions; cook until translucent. Add degi red chili powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder; sauté 1 minute. Add tomatoes, salt, and curd; cook until tomatoes are mushy. Add cashew nuts and water; cover and cook 10-12 minutes. Cool and grind to smooth paste. For paneer masala, heat mustard oil, temper with cumin seeds and green chili, add onion, capsicum, tomato, and coriander stems; cook 7-8 minutes on high flame. Add degi red chili powder and prepared dry masala, then paneer; sauté on medium flame. Combine gravy with paneer masala in the pan, add remaining paneer, salt, and sugar; cook 5-6 minutes. Finish with fresh coriander leaves and ghee. Garnish with coriander, kasuri methi, and fresh cream.

name: Mawa Modak
instructions: Heat milk in heavy bottom pan over high flame until it boils. Lower flame and collect cream layer from edges using spatula, repeating until milk reduces to 1/3. When milk reduces to half, remove some hot milk, add saffron strands and let them bloom for 4-5 minutes, then add back to milk. Once milk reduces to 1/3 and darkens, add sugar and cardamom powder, cook until grainy texture forms. Cool to room temperature. Shape using mold or hands with ghee, creating cone shape and pinching to form modak. Can also be shaped using mold by applying ghee and pressing mixture inside. Store in fridge for 2 weeks or at room temperature for 3-4 days.

name: Lahori Chicken Curry with Green Chickpeas
ingredients: degi red chili powder 1 heaped tbsp
instructions: Grind green chillies, garlic, and ginger into coarse paste. Heat oil and ghee in handi, splutter cinnamon, bay leaf, cumin seeds. Add ginger-garlic paste and onions, saute 5-6 mins until translucent. Add turmeric, coriander, and chili powders, cook 1 min. Add salt, tomatoes, cook until mushy. Add green chickpeas, mix well. Add chicken, beaten curd, and salt, cook 1 min on medium flame. Add water and roasted masala powder, simmer 5-6 mins until cooked. Finish with ghee. For masala: dry roast green cardamom, peppercorns, cloves, coriander seeds for 1 min, grind to fine powder. Serve hot with roti or paratha.

name: Dhungar Dal Tadka
ingredients: degi red chili powder 1 heaped tsp
instructions: Heat ghee in pressure cooker, add kashmiri chillies, toor dal, moong dal, saute 1 min. Add salt, turmeric, water, ghee. Pressure cook 3-4 whistles, mash dal. For chutney: grind ginger, garlic, chili powder, asafoetida into paste. Heat ghee and oil, splutter cumin, fennel, clove. Add onions and tender coriander stems, saute until translucent. Add ginger-garlic chutney, saute 3-4 mins. Add cooked dal, water, salt, sugar, boil. Place steel bowl in kadai with burnt coal and ghee, cover to smoke for 1-2 mins. Mix well. Finish with tadka. For tadka: heat mustard oil and oil until smoky, add kashmiri chillies, fry until crispy, sprinkle chili powder. Serve hot.

name: Pudina Chicken Handi
ingredients: potatoes 2 large peeled cut into quarters
instructions: Saute chicken in handi with ghee, salt, ginger-garlic paste until half cooked. Transfer to tray. Fry potatoes in kadai with oil until done, add onions, cook 3-4 mins until translucent. Transfer to bowl. For curd mixture: combine curd, turmeric, coriander powder, chili powder, cumin powder, cardamom, black cardamom, peppercorns, fennel seeds, fresh mint, coriander stems, curd, ginger-garlic paste. In earthen pot: add oil, splutter bay leaf and cinnamon. Add sauteed chicken and curd mixture, fried potatoes and onions, salt, mix well. Add green chillies, oil, fresh mint leaves. Seal edges with dough, cover with lid, cook 20-25 mins. Rest 5-6 mins. Serve hot with roti or naan. Garnish with mint.

name: Panjiri
instructions: For Frying Nuts: Heat ghee, fry edible gum until golden, transfer to tray. In same ghee, fry almonds and cashew nuts until golden, transfer to tray. For Panjiri: Heat remaining ghee, add whole wheat flour and dry roast 25-30 minutes until golden. Add crushed black peppercorns, carom seeds, fried nuts & edible gum, dry ginger powder and mix well. Cool at room temperature. Add powdered sugar and sugar, mix well. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with almond slivers. Serve with hot milk. For Sugarcane Shikanji: Mix sugarcane jaggery, ginger, hot water until jaggery melts. Add water, black pepper powder, fresh mint leaves, salt, lemon juice and serve cold.

name: Dal Dhoka
instructions: For Paste: Grind soaked urad dal, soaked chana dal, ginger, green chillies, degi red chili powder, turmeric powder, asafoetida, coriander leaves, salt into smooth paste. For Cooking Dal: Heat ghee in pressure cooker, add asafoetida, green chili, ginger and sauté 1 minute. Add soaked chana dal, salt, turmeric powder, water and cook for 2-3 whistles. Mash dal with wooden spoon and keep aside. For Steaming Dal Vadi: Grease steaming plates with oil, spread prepared paste evenly. Steam for 15-20 minutes. Cool and cut into desired shape. For Frying Dal Dhoka: Heat oil and mustard oil until smoky hot, fry dal vadi until golden brown. For Tadka: Heat oil, add ginger, green chillies, degi red chili powder, dry mango powder and sauté 1 minute. Add water, sugar, salt and boil. For Dal Dhoka: Bring cooked dal to boil, add tadka, salt, coriander leaves, fried vadi and boil 5-6 minutes. Finish with lemon juice. Serve hot with steamed rice.

name: Jodhpuri Aloo
ingredients: coriander stems and roots 1 tbsp chopped
instructions: 1. Mix curd with water and set aside. 2. Heat ghee in handi, add baby potatoes, prepared curd mixture, salt, turmeric powder and mix well. 3. Add water, cover and cook for 7-8 minutes until potatoes are cooked. 4. In a separate kadai, heat sesame oil until smoky hot. 5. Add cumin seeds, fennel seeds, carom seeds, chilli flakes, dry red chillies and let splutter well. 6. Add ginger, green chillies, coriander stems, asafoetida and sauté for a while. 7. Add cooked potatoes and water, cook for 3-4 minutes. 8. Add water, cover and cook until well done. 9. Finish with dry mango powder and fresh coriander leaves, mix well. 10. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with toasted sesame seeds and coriander sprig. 11. Serve hot with paratha or roti.

name: Arbi Tuk
instructions: 1. Flatten par-boiled arbi with bowl or napkin and keep in tray. 2. Heat oil in non-stick kadai, add flattened arbi and fry until half cooked. 3. Transfer to tray for a minute and refry on high flames until golden brown. 4. Transfer to absorbent paper and keep aside. 5. In a bowl, mix degi red chilli powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, dry mango powder, salt. 6. In a tray, add fried arbi, sprinkle prepared tuk masala and gently toss well. 7. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with coriander sprig. 8. Serve hot with green chutney and chai.

name: Pesto Pasta
instructions: 1. Make pesto: Add 2-3 tbsp olive oil in chopper along with garlic, walnut, pinch of salt, grind together to emulsify oil nicely. 2. Add basil leaves, parmesan cheese, 3 tbsp olive oil, grind again until semi-coarse paste forms, scrape sides. 3. Pesto is ready. 4. Boil pasta: Bring water to roiling boil in stock pot. 5. Add salt and pasta, boil for 8 minutes. 6. Strain pasta using large spider, reserve pasta water. 7. In bowl add boiled pasta, parmesan cheese, pesto as per taste and mix well. 8. Add 1-2 tbsp hot pasta water to bring pasta to right consistency. 9. Taste and adjust seasoning if required. 10. Serve immediately.

name: Pizza Omelette
instructions: 1. Make pizza sauce: Grind 7-8 chopped tomatoes into puree. Heat 2-3 tbsp oil in pan on high flame. 2. Add onion and garlic, stir and cook on high flame until onions turn translucent. 3. Add kashmiri chilli powder, stir, then add tomato puree with remaining sauce ingredients. 4. Stir all ingredients, cover and cook sauce on medium-low flame for 8-10 minutes. 5. Remove lid, add basil leaves, mix well. Pizza sauce is ready. 6. Make garlic butter: Add melted butter in bowl with chopped garlic, mix well. 7. Make pizza omelette: Cut bread slices into small squares. 8. Break 4 eggs in bowl, add salt, black pepper, coriander, green chillies, whisk until surface becomes frothy. 9. Add oil to hot pan, pour whisked eggs, pull edges with spatula and tilt pan so raw egg pours down and omelette cooks evenly. 10. Place cut bread pieces on omelette covering entire surface, apply garlic butter over bread, press with spatula. 11. Carefully flip entire omelette, remove pan from flame and turn flame off (bread will burn if flame stays on). 12. Build pizza on omelette: spread pizza sauce, add mozzarella cheese, toppings (capsicum, bell pepper, sweet corn, oregano, chilli flakes). 13. Keep pan over low flame, cover and cook 8-10 minutes until cheese melts and bread toasts. 14. Remove pizza omelette from pan, cut into slices and serve.

name: Veg Masala Grill Sandwich
instructions: For sandwich masala: Set pan on medium heat, add whole spices, dry roast until fragrant, cool. Transfer to grinding jar, add powdered spices and salt, grind to fine powder. For chutney: Add coriander in mixer grinder with green chillies, garlic, blanched spinach, curry leaves, sandwich masala, salt, bread slice and ice cube, grind into fine paste using water. For aloo masala: Set pan over high flame, add oil, mustard seeds, cumin seeds, curry leaves, chopped green chillies and ginger garlic paste, stir well and cook briefly. Lower flame, add powdered spices, stir immediately and add asafoetida, green peas and roughly mashed boiled potatoes, stir well. Mash using potato masher until well combined, add lemon juice and fresh coriander. To assemble: First layer - apply soft butter on bread slice, spread sandwich chutney, add potato masala, onion and sandwich masala, top with another buttered bread slice with chutney. Second layer - apply butter and chutney on bread, add tomato slices, sandwich masala, capsicum and processed cheese, top with buttered bread slice and chutney. Grill on hot pan until golden brown on both sides.

name: Veg Hot & Sour Soup
ingredients: hot water or vegetable stock 800-900 ml
instructions: Cut all vegetables in julienne. Finely chop spring onion greens. Set wok over high flame, add oil and let heat. Add ginger and garlic, stir and saute over low flame for 30 seconds. Add mushrooms and cook over high flame for 1 minute. Add all julienned vegetables, stir and toss over high flame for 1 minute. Add stock or hot water with green chilli paste, sugar, soy sauce, vinegar, red chilli sauce, white pepper powder and salt, stir well and bring to boil. Mix corn flour with water to make slurry, add slurry to soup while mixing continuously to desired semi-thick consistency. Finally add chopped coriander and spring onion greens, stir well. Taste and adjust seasoning as per preference.

name: Schezwan Manchurian Frankie
instructions: For manchurian balls: Mix chopped cabbage, carrot, french beans, garlic, ginger, green chilli paste, coriander, spring onion, red chilli sauce, soy sauce, sugar, butter, white pepper and salt. Add corn flour and refined flour to bind. Form into balls. For schezwan sauce: Heat oil, add chopped garlic and coriander stems, cook briefly. Add ginger and kashmiri red chilli paste, cook well. Add ketchup, red chilli sauce, soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, salt, white pepper and green chilli paste. Add onion, capsicum and spring onion bulbs, cook well. Add vegetable stock, mix corn flour slurry and add to thicken. Garnish with coriander and spring onion greens. For frankie roti: Mix refined flour, curd, salt, water and oil to form dough. Roll into rotis and half-cook on hot pan with oil. For frankie masala: Mix all powdered spices. To assemble: Heat pan, add butter and lay half-cooked frankie roti, cook until golden brown and crispy on both sides, pressing with spatula. Add mayonnaise, schezwan manchurian balls with sauce, processed cheese, frankie masala and julienned cabbage. Roll tightly.

name: Kada Parshad
instructions: Set deep pan over high flame and once hot, add ghee. When ghee gets hot, add flour while continuously stirring to prevent lumps. Cook flour over low flame until it turns dark biscuity in colour, keep stirring continuously. Once flour and ghee turn dark biscuity colour, add sugar and water. Increase flame to medium-low and cook halwa until all ghee separates. Switch off flame once ghee separates completely. Kada parshad is ready to serve.

name: Aloo Tiffin Roll
instructions: Chop carrot, peas, cauliflower, french beans and capsicum coarsely. Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds, green chilli, ginger garlic paste and asafoetida. Add chopped vegetables and salt, cook for 2-3 minutes. Add boiled mashed potatoes with powdered spices, fresh coriander and lemon juice. Mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes until filling is ready. Shape filling into a long patty, cook in butter until golden brown. In the same pan, cook the roti briefly on both sides. Apply ketchup and red chilli sauce on roti, place the patty, grate cheese, sprinkle frankie masala, roll and wrap in butter paper.

name: Prawns Koliwada
instructions: Marinate prawns with salt, turmeric powder and lemon juice for 15 minutes. Grind fresh coriander, garlic, ginger, green chilli and water into a fine paste for vaatan. Add vaatan and all powdered spices to marinated prawns along with oil and mix until coated well. Add egg white, gram flour and corn flour and mix thoroughly. Heat oil to 170C, fry prawns in small batches until light golden. Remove onto sieve and rest for 5 minutes. Heat oil to higher temperature and flash fry prawns until crisp and golden brown. Drain on sieve.

name: Anda Tawa Masala
ingredients: kashmiri red chilli powder a pinch
instructions: Grind garlic, ginger, green chilli, fresh coriander and water into a fine paste. Cut boiled eggs into halves. Heat a tawa with oil, place eggs cut side down and add green chillies, turmeric powder, kashmiri red chilli powder and salt. Fry eggs until light golden from both sides. Set aside. In the same pan, heat oil, add cumin seeds and onions, cook until golden. Add green paste and cook for 2-3 minutes. Lower flame, add powdered spices, salt and garlic cloves with hot water. Cook until oil separates. Add tamarind pulp, stir and adjust seasoning. Add fried eggs to gravy and coat well. Garnish with fresh coriander. Toast pavs over flame and serve hot with the gravy.

name: Masala Pattice Pav
ingredients: kashmiri red chilli 15 deseeded and soaked
instructions: Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds, ginger and green chillies. Add boiled potatoes with salt, turmeric powder and garam masala. Mash potatoes using a potato masher and cook for 3-4 minutes. Add fresh coriander and transfer to a bowl to cool completely. Shape patties and shallow fry in butter until golden brown from both sides. To make red garlic chutney, grind soaked kashmiri red chillies with garlic, green chillies and water into a fine paste. Heat oil in a pan, add chilli paste, salt, cumin powder and pav bhaji masala. Cook over medium flame for 3-4 minutes, add lemon juice. To make masala pav, heat butter in a pan, add red garlic chutney and fresh coriander. Place sliced pav in the pan and coat well from both sides. Assemble by placing patty in pav, add tomato and onion slices, chaat masala, nylon sev and fresh coriander.

name: Butter Chicken Burger
ingredients: refined flour as required for coating
instructions: Marinate chicken with salt, kashmiri red chilli powder, lemon juice and ginger garlic paste for 30 minutes. In another bowl, mix mustard oil, spice powders, ginger garlic paste and curd. Add this mixture to chicken and marinate further. Prepare batter with refined flour and ice cold water. Coat chicken pieces in batter and deep fry until golden and cooked through. For makhani sauce, heat oil and butter, add cumin seeds and onions, cook until golden. Add tomatoes, garlic, ginger, green chilli, coriander stem and cashew. Add spices and cook until tomatoes are mushy and oil separates. Grind mixture to smooth paste. For final cooking, heat oil and butter, add chopped ginger, garlic and kashmiri red chilli powder. Add the cooked sauce, honey, fresh cream and garam masala. For laccha pyaaz (pickled onions), mix sliced onions with spice powders, lemon juice and mustard oil. Toast burger buns with butter. Assemble burger with lettuce, crispy chicken fillet, makhani sauce and laccha pyaaz.

name: Nankhatai
instructions: Grind sugar into powder. Sieve sugar into bowl, add ghee, cardamom powder, baking soda and curd, mix with whisk. Sieve in refined flour and mix well. Form mixture into dough without kneading. Shape into small roundels, press gently, place on butter paper lined tray with 2-3 inch gaps. Top with slivered almonds and pistachios. Bake in preheated 170C oven for 12-14 minutes. Cool completely before serving. For no-oven method: preheat kadhai with salt over high heat for 20 minutes. Place shaped nankhatai on butter-paper lined plate, set on steamer stand in kadhai, cover with lid, bake over low flame for 20-25 minutes.

name: Aloo Corn Frankie
instructions: Make roti dough with refined flour, salt, curd, oil and water, rest briefly. Prepare frankie masala by mixing red chilli powder, turmeric, coriander powder, black salt, pomegranate powder, dry mango powder, cumin powder, garam masala, black pepper and salt. Heat oil and butter, add cumin seeds, green chilli, asafoetida and ginger garlic paste, cook 1 minute. Add corn and cook. Boil potatoes and mix with corn, spices, sauces and coriander. Make schezwan mayo by mixing mayonnaise with schezwan sauce. Roll out roti dough thin, place filling in center, spread mayo and masala, add onion, chilli vinegar and cheese, roll up burrito-style and pan-fry until golden.

name: Surti Undhiyu
instructions: Soak papdi with tur dal in hot water with baking soda. Make methi muthiya dough with fenugreek, gram flour, wheat flour, oil and spices, form into roundels and deep fry until golden. Fry diced vegetables separately until light golden. Make green masala by grinding green garlic, coriander, peanuts, sesame seeds and green chilli, then mix with remaining green masala ingredients. Stuff brinjals with green masala and coat fried vegetables. Heat oil with carrom seeds, add soaked papdi and salt, cook 70% done. Add stuffed brinjals, cook semi-soft. Add remaining green masala, cook 3-4 minutes. Layer brinjals, green masala and methi muthiya, add hot water and steam 15 minutes. Garnish with coriander.

name: Khandvi
ingredients: ginger chilli paste from 2 inch ginger and 2 green chillies
instructions: Whisk together sour curd, gram flour, ginger chilli paste, salt, sugar and turmeric powder until smooth. Add water and mix well to form liquidy batter, then strain. Pour batter into nonstick pan and cook over low heat, stirring continuously, until thick and shiny (about 20 minutes). Spread cooled batter thinly and evenly onto backside of trays or plates using scraper/spatula. Let cool for 10 minutes. Cut into 2-3 inch pieces and roll from one end. Heat oil for tempering, add mustard seeds, let crackle, then add green chilli, asafoetida, white sesame seeds and curry leaves. Pour tempering over rolled khandvi. Garnish with fresh coriander and desiccated coconut.

name: Dal Tadka with Jeera Rice
instructions: Wash and soak three dals for 30 minutes-1 hour. Pressure cook dals with green chilli, salt, turmeric powder, ginger and water for 3-4 whistles over medium-high heat. Let cool naturally. For tadka: heat ghee, add cumin seeds, red chilli, chopped ginger, garlic and green chilli, cook 1 minute. Add chopped onion and cook until light golden. Add tomato and salt, cook until mushy. Add powdered spices, stir and add water splash. Tilt pan so flame reaches pan briefly for smoky flavor. Add boiled dal and bring to boil. Add kasuri methi, dry mango powder, butter and coriander. For second tempering: heat ghee in small pan, fry sliced onion until golden, add red chilli, asafoetida and kashmiri red chilli powder, pour over dal. For jeera rice: wash and soak rice 30-45 minutes. Boil water with salt and lemon juice, add soaked rice, cook 90% done (4-5 minutes). Drain in sieve and cool. Heat ghee, add cumin seeds, let crackle, add rice and coriander, toss over high flame briefly.

name: Batata Poha
instructions: Rinse poha gently in sieve and let rest for 8-10 minutes. Heat oil in pan, add cumin seeds, mustard seeds, green chilli, curry leaves, and peanuts, cook briefly on low flame. Add diced potatoes and cook on high flame until half cooked. Add chopped onion and sugar, stir well and cook until onion turns light golden brown. Add salt and turmeric powder, mix well. Add soaked poha, fresh coriander, and lemon juice, stir gently. Add a splash of water, cover with lid, switch off flame and let rest for 3-4 minutes. Sprinkle freshly chopped coriander and serve with grated coconut.

name: Mirchi Vada
instructions: Make batter: Mix gram flour, salt, carom seeds, and baking soda. Add water gradually while whisking vigorously to incorporate air. Rest batter. Make filling: Grind whole spices (coriander, cumin, fennel) coarsely. Heat oil, add ground spices and asafoetida, cook briefly on low flame. Add ginger-garlic-green chilli paste, powdered spices, kasuri methi, and hot water. Cook 1-2 minutes. Add crushed boiled potatoes, dry mango powder, and salt. Mash and mix well. Cool and add chopped onion and coriander. Deseed whole green chillies carefully. Fill chillies with potato mixture pressing firmly. Heat oil to 165C. Dip chillies in batter, let excess drip, drop in hot oil. Fry on medium flame until golden brown.

name: Laccha Paratha
ingredients: wheat flour for dusting as required
instructions: Make dough: Mix flour and salt. Add water gradually while kneading to make semi-soft dough. Knead until smooth and soft on platform. Add oil and knead until incorporated. Form into dough ball, place in oiled bowl, drizzle oil, cover with damp cloth, rest 1 hour. Divide dough into equal balls, keep covered. Shaping Method 1: Coat dough with flour, roll thin chapati, spread melted ghee, dust flour, roll from end to end to form cylinder, then roll again and tuck end. Method 2: Roll thin circular chapati, spread ghee, dust flour, make single slit from center to edge, lift slit end to form cone, flatten. Method 3: Roll chapati, spread ghee, dust flour, fold with pleats, roll and tuck inward. Cooking: Heat tawa on high heat. Place paratha, cook until brown spots appear, flip and cook other side. Add ghee and cook until crisp and golden brown on both sides.

name: Crispy Corn Bhajiya
instructions: For corn bhajiya: Cut corn kernels by placing cut side down and moving knife from top to bottom. Add corn kernels to bowl with salt and red chilli powder. Add gram flour in batches and mix well. Add splash of water and mix until mixture comes together. Heat oil to 170C. Scoop small portions and drop carefully in hot oil. Fry on high flame 1 minute without stirring, then reduce flame to medium-low. Fry until golden brown while stirring occasionally. Transfer to sieve. For sukhi chutney: Make semi-thick besan batter with gram flour, salt, and water. Heat oil, dip fingers in batter and drizzle in thin streams over hot oil. Fry crisp and light golden, transfer to sieve. Fry garlic cloves briefly in spider. Grind chura and garlic with salt, garam masala, spicy red chilli powder, and kashmiri red chilli powder coarsely. Serve bhajiya hot with sukhi chutney and masala chai.

name: Cheesy Paneer Bread Pockets
instructions: Make filling: Grate half the paneer and crush remaining paneer roughly by hand. Combine both portions in large bowl. Grate and add cheese. Add green chilli, spring onion greens, crushed peppercorns, salt, powdered sugar, grated garlic, thick curd, mayonnaise, and lemon juice. Mix really well. Flatten 2 bread slices completely using rolling pin. Apply splash of water to breads to prevent excessive oil absorption. Place spoonful of filling in center of one bread slice. Place second bread slice on top. Use glass, bowl, or round cutter to cut bread and filling into circles. Seal edges using fork. Heat oil in deep pan to 170C. Carefully drop pockets in hot oil. Fry until light golden brown and crisp. Transfer to sieve to drain excess oil. Serve hot with ketchup and mayonnaise.

name: Ghevar
instructions: Melt ghee and grind with ice cubes. Add refined flour and gram flour, mix until crumbly. Add chilled water in batches to make smooth flowy batter. Transfer to squeezy bottle and refrigerate. Heat oil in flat bottom vessel with ring (5-inch round, 2 inch height). Pour chilled batter in batches 6-7 times to form layer. Make hole in centre. Pour batter 10-12 times more in centre. Fry until golden. For Rabadi: boil milk, collect cream layers until milk reduces to 1/3. Add sugar and cook 7-8 minutes. Add cardamom. Cool and refrigerate. For sugar syrup: dissolve sugar in water with lemon juice, boil until one string consistency. Serve ghevar dipped in syrup, topped with rabadi and nuts.

name: Paneer Tikka Shawarma
instructions: Marinate paneer cubes in spiced yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, spices for 35-40 minutes. Prepare pita dough with warm water, milk, curd, olive oil, sugar, flours, baking powder, baking soda, salt. Knead and let rest. Make tahini by grinding white sesame seeds with salt and olive oil. Make hummus by cooking chickpeas and blending with tahini, garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, salt. Prepare garlic sauce by mixing mayonnaise, curd, lemon juice, sugar, black pepper, grated garlic, coriander. Grill marinated paneer tikka. Prepare pita bread. Assemble: spread hummus on pita, add hot sauce, layer paneer tikka, french fries, pickle, drizzle tahini and garlic sauce, garnish with greens.

name: Lauki Ka Halwa
instructions: Heat milk in deep pan. Peel bottle gourd, trim ends, halve, quarter, remove seeds. Grate gourd flesh quickly. Heat ghee in kadhai over high flame. Add grated gourd and stir well, cook until translucent (4-5 minutes). Pour heated milk into kadhai and stir well. Cook over medium-high flame, stirring frequently at intervals until milk reduces to 1/3 (approximately 45 minutes). Add sugar and cardamom powder, stir until halwa thickens again. Heat ghee in separate pan, add chironji, almonds and cashews, fry until light golden brown. Transfer fried nuts to halwa and mix well. Serve hot.

name: Butter Garlic Chicken
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 1 tsp
instructions: Cut chicken breasts into thin flat slices. Mix with salt, black pepper powder, vinegar, light soy sauce, ginger-garlic-green chilli paste. Add corn flour, refined flour and egg white, mix well. Heat oil in pan over high flame to moderate temperature. Fry chicken until golden brown on both sides, transfer to sieve. In wok, melt butter over high flame. Add roughly chopped garlic, green chilli and coriander stems, cook for 2-3 minutes over medium flame. Add hot water, salt (pinch), light soy sauce, vinegar, black pepper powder and sugar. Make corn flour slurry with water and add to wok, stir continuously to thicken sauce. Add fried chicken, lemon juice, lemon zest and spring onions. Toss well to coat. Serve hot, garnish with spring onion greens.

name: Bhutte ka Kees
instructions: Grate fresh corn. Heat ghee and temper with mustard seeds and cumin. Add green chilli and ginger, then grated corn. Cook with gram flour, turmeric, and coriander powder. Add milk and simmer until soft. Season with kashmiri chilli, sugar, salt, lemon juice. Garnish with coriander, jeeravan masala, and coconut.

name: veg red thai curry
ingredients: coriander roots and stems handful chopped
kashmiri red chilli 10-12 nos deseeded soaked
spicy red chilli 4-5 nos deseeded soaked
instructions: Make the curry paste by grinding coriander seeds, cumin seeds, and white peppercorns into powder. Grind chopped lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, garlic, shallots, coriander roots/stems, and lemon peel into a paste, then add soaked red chillies and grind smooth. Add powdered spices and mix well. For the curry, heat 100ml coconut milk in a wok, add curry paste and cook until oil separates. Add remaining coconut milk and water, bring to boil. Add baby corn, cook 3-4 minutes, then add remaining vegetables and boil 2-3 minutes. Adjust seasoning, add brown sugar, then add basil and kaffir lime leaves and cook 1-2 minutes. Finally add fresh red chillies and serve.

name: veg matka
instructions: Heat oil in a handi over high flame. Add cumin seeds and sliced onions, cook until golden brown. Add ginger, garlic, green chillies and coriander stalks, cook 1-2 minutes. Lower flame, add powdered spices and hot water immediately. Cook until oil separates. Add chopped tomatoes and salt, cover and cook 10-12 minutes until mushy. Cool and grind into fine puree for base gravy. For final gravy, heat oil in earthen pot, add onion, garlic, ginger, green chilli and cook until onions turn translucent. Add kashmiri red chilli powder and base gravy, cook 10 minutes. In separate pan, heat oil and toss babycorn, french beans and carrot for 2-3 minutes. Add green peas and remaining vegetables, toss 1-2 minutes. Add onion petals and capsicum separately. Add all vegetables to gravy with fresh cream, butter, garam masala, kasuri methi and ginger. Optional: add charcoal smoke. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve.

name: punjabi samosa
instructions: For dough: Mix refined flour, semolina, salt, and crushed carom seeds. Add ghee and mix until breadcrumb texture. Gradually add cold water and knead stiff tight dough for 7-8 minutes. Cover with damp cloth and rest 30 minutes. For filling: Grind ginger and green chillies into paste. Grind cumin, coriander and fennel seeds to coarse powder. Cut boiled potatoes into quarters and slice. Heat ghee in pan on low heat, cook ground spices for 30-40 seconds. Add ginger-chilli paste and cook 1-2 minutes. Add potatoes, green peas, salt, pepper, chaat masala, kasuri methi and fresh coriander. Cook 6-7 minutes on medium-high flame until colored, mash lightly keeping chunks intact. Cool to room temperature. Shape samosas: Knead rested dough, divide into balls, flatten into ovals, cut width-wise in half. Apply water on straight edge, bring ends together to form cone. Fill with potato mixture, seal openings to shape proper samosa. For deep frying: Heat oil to 140-150°C, fry samosas on low-medium flame until crisp and golden brown. For air frying: Preheat air fryer to 180°C, brush samosas with ghee, air fry 20-25 minutes until crisp, turning halfway. Serve with chutneys and chai.

name: eggless burger buns
instructions: In a bowl, add lukewarm water and sugar, stir to dissolve. Add instant yeast and dissolve. Cover and set aside 5-10 minutes until frothy. In large bowl, mash boiled potato until smooth. Add refined flour and salt to potato and mix well. Add yeast-water mixture and combine using hands to form dough. Knead on flat surface for 3-4 minutes. Add soft unsalted butter in 2 batches and knead thoroughly for 10-12 minutes until dough is super soft and smooth. Check gluten window by stretching a small portion - should form thin transparent sheet. Form into large dough ball, transfer to greased bowl, drizzle oil on surface. Cover with cling wrap and let ferment in warm place until double in size (1-1.5 hours depending on temperature). After doubling, punch dough down and divide into 80g portions (about 7 portions). Cover portions with damp cloth. Pull edges of each portion toward center, flip and roll into tight smooth ball. Place on butter paper lined baking tray and gently flatten each ball with hand. Cover with damp cloth and rest 30 minutes. After 30 minutes, brush surface with milk and place in 190°C preheated oven. Bake 12-14 minutes until light golden brown. Remove from oven and brush immediately with melted butter. Transfer to cooling rack and cool completely. Serve fresh.

name: cheese burst aloo tikki burger
instructions: For cheese disc: Set double boiler with bowl over water without touching bottom. Add grated cheese, milk and butter to bowl, whisk well and melt until smooth and semi-thick. Pour onto butter paper lined square plate, cool to room temperature, then refrigerate overnight until set. Cut into 3 inch squares with 1 cm thickness. Freeze until needed. For creamy tangy mayo: Mix mayonnaise, red chilli sauce and ketchup in bowl, set aside. For crispy aloo tikki: Heat oil and butter in pan, add green chillies and ginger, sauté 1-2 minutes. Add green peas and sauté 1-2 minutes. Add boiled potatoes, soaked flattened rice, salt, all powdered spices, lemon juice and fresh coriander. Mix and cook 1-2 minutes. Transfer to bowl and cool, then refrigerate 10-12 minutes. For slurry: Mix refined flour, cornstarch, oil and salt, add water to make semi-thin consistency. Season panko bread crumbs with salt. Take spoonful of mixture, shape into ball, press to make cavity, quickly insert frozen cheese square, seal and shape into tikki slightly smaller than burger buns. Dip in slurry, coat with panko. Deep fry on medium-high heat until crisp and golden brown. For assembly: Toast burger buns lightly with butter on inside. Spread creamy mayo on top bun, place fried tikki on bottom bun with tomato and onion slices, close with top bun. Serve with french fries.

name: chicken chopper rice
ingredients: spring onion greens small handful chopped
instructions: For egg fried rice: Heat oil in wok over high flame. Add spring onion bulbs, french beans, carrot, cabbage and sugar. Cook over high flame for 1 minute. Whisk 1 egg with salt in separate bowl and pour into wok, scramble briefly with vegetables. Add cooked sela basmati rice, salt, white pepper powder, aromat powder, light soy sauce and vinegar. Toss for 1-2 minutes until well combined. Finally add chopped spring onion greens and set aside. For chicken gravy: Cut chicken breasts into small dices. Whisk eggs with salt in separate bowl. Season diced chicken with salt, black pepper powder, vinegar, whisked eggs and corn flour, mixing to coat evenly. Heat oil in wok over high flame, add ginger, garlic and green chilli, cook 30 seconds to 1 minute. Add seasoned chicken and cook 3-4 minutes over high flame. Add whisked eggs, scramble and mix with chicken. Add ketchup, red chilli sauce, schezwan sauce, green chilli paste, spring onion bulbs, salt, white pepper powder and dark soy sauce. Stir well and add hot water or chicken stock to create gravy. Mix corn flour and water separately and add gradually to gravy while stirring continuously. Finally add chopped spring onion greens and mix well. Serve chicken gravy over egg fried rice.

name: Veg Makhanwala
instructions: Heat oil, add whole spices and onions, cook 1 minute. Add tomatoes, coriander stalk, garlic, ginger, green chilli, kashmiri chilli and cook 1-2 minutes. Add hot water to cover tomatoes, cover and cook 15-20 minutes on low heat until tomatoes are mushy. In parallel, toss cauliflower and carrot with oil and salt for 2-3 minutes, add french beans, peas, capsicum and cook briefly. Cool the makhani gravy, grind to smooth paste. Heat oil and butter, add ginger-garlic-green chilli, cook 1 minute. Lower heat, add red chilli powder and strained makhani paste. Cook 4-5 minutes, add cooked vegetables and cook 3-4 minutes. Add honey, fresh cream, kasuri methi powder, garam masala, coriander and butter. Stir well and serve.

name: Paneer Hot Pan
instructions: Cut paneer into diamond shapes, season with salt and black pepper, coat with corn flour and splash of water. Heat oil to medium-high (175°C), carefully add paneer pieces and fry until crisp and golden brown, separate while frying to prevent sticking. Drain on sieve. For sauce: heat oil in wok on high, add ginger and garlic, cook 1 minute. Add bell peppers and toss well, add schezwan sauce, red chilli sauce, ketchup, soy sauce and stir 1-2 minutes. Add salt, white pepper, green chillies. Make corn flour slurry with water, add gradually to thicken sauce. Add fried paneer, spring onion greens and bulbs, toss well. For sizzler serving: heat sizzler plate, quickly cook onions and cabbage in wok with oil, place on hot sizzler, add paneer mixture, butter cubes and serve immediately.

name: Chicken Lollipops with Drums of Heaven
ingredients: kashmiri red chillies 20 nos deseeded soaked
ginger garlic green chilli paste 4-5 garlic cloves 1/2 inch ginger 1 green chilli
instructions: Prepare Kashmiri red chilli paste by soaking chillies in hot water for 1-2 hours, then grinding with a splash of water. Heat oil in pan, add chilli paste and cook over medium-high flame for 4-5 minutes until crumbly and oil releases. Add vinegar and cool. Shape chicken lollipops by scraping meat from drumette and wingette bones. Marinate shaped lollipops with salt, pepper, ginger-garlic paste, red chilli paste, soy sauce, vinegar, corn flour, refined flour, egg white, and baking soda for 30 minutes. Heat oil to 160C and fry marinated lollipops for 6-7 minutes until 80-90% cooked. Transfer to sieve and rest for 15 minutes. Deep fry again at 180C for 1-2 minutes until fully cooked. For sauce: heat wok, add oil with ginger, garlic, spring onion bulbs, green chilli and saute 1-2 minutes. Add red chilli paste, ketchup, red chilli sauce, dark soy sauce, vinegar, pepper, salt and cook 1-2 minutes. Add hot water and corn flour slurry to thicken. Flash fry cooked lollipops at 180C for 1-2 minutes and toss in sauce with spring onion greens.

name: Dal Vada
instructions: For red coconut chutney: Heat pan, add oil, roast kashmiri red chillies for 3-4 minutes until crisp, cool, and add to mixer jar with fresh coconut, green chilli, garlic, onion, mint, tamarind, roasted chana dal, water, salt, and sugar. Grind into semi-coarse chutney. For vada: Wash and soak chana dal 7-8 hours or overnight. Discard water and add to mixer jar, reserve 20% of dal. Add ginger, garlic, green chilli and grind to coarse paste using pulse mode. Grind whole spices (cumin, coriander, fennel, peppercorns) into coarse mix. Mix ground dal paste with spice mix, reserved chana dal, chopped onion, salt, curry leaves, coriander, rice flour, hot oil, and asafoetida. Shape mixture into round vadas with slightly thinner edges. Deep fry in 165C preheated oil. Don't stir for first 1-2 minutes to hold shape, then stir and fry until crisp and golden brown. Transfer to sieve to drain excess oil.

name: Gujarati Khichdi Kadhi
ingredients: ginger chilli paste of 2 inch ginger and 2 green chillies
fresh coriander a small handful chopped
instructions: For kadhi: Whisk sour curd, gram flour, ginger chilli paste, and salt in large bowl. Add water and whisk to liquidy batter. Pour into stock pot and bring to boil over high flame while stirring regularly. Lower flame and simmer. Heat ghee in tadka pan, add whole spices (cumin, mustard, cinnamon, cloves, red chilli, asafoetida, curry leaves, methi seeds). Pour tadka into kadhi and cover for 30 seconds. Remove lid, stir well, add jaggery, and cook 2-3 minutes. Taste and adjust salt and jaggery. Cover and cook 4-5 minutes over low heat. Add fresh coriander and stir. For khichdi: Wash rice and dal several times, then soak for 20-25 minutes. Discard water. Heat ghee in pressure cooker, add cumin seeds and asafoetida. Add soaked rice and dal, stir and cook 1-2 minutes. Add water, salt, turmeric powder, and bring to boil. Cover and pressure cook for 4 whistles over medium-high flame. Switch off and let de-pressurize naturally. Stir well and adjust consistency. Serve hot khichdi with hot kadhi.

name: Eggless Fluffy Pancakes
ingredients: maple syrup or honey for serving
instructions: 1. Mix warm milk with vinegar and let it rest for 2-3 minutes to make buttermilk. 2. In a bowl, mix refined flour, powdered sugar, baking soda, baking powder, and salt. 3. Add prepared buttermilk, melted butter, and vanilla essence to the dry ingredients. 4. Whisk well until batter is fluffy but avoid over-whisking. 5. Transfer batter to a piping bag. 6. Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat. 7. Pipe batter onto hot pan using 2 cm diameter hole, creating round pancakes. 8. Cook for 1 minute on one side until golden. 9. Carefully flip and cook for 1 minute on the other side. 10. Transfer to serving plate. 11. Drizzle with maple syrup, honey, or chocolate spread and dust with powder sugar. Serve hot.

name: Soft White Dhokla
instructions: 1. Wash rice and dal together until water runs clear. 2. Add fenugreek seeds to rice-dal mixture and soak for 4-5 hours. 3. Wash poha and soak for 5-10 minutes. 4. Add soaked poha to rice-dal mixture. 5. Grind all soaked ingredients with sour curd and water to semi-fine paste using mixer grinder. 6. Transfer batter to cooker/stock pot. 7. Add asafoetida and sugar, whisk for 1-2 minutes. 8. Cover and keep in warm place to ferment (12-15 hours in cold weather, 8-10 hours in summer). 9. Once fermented and puffed, add ginger-chilli paste, oil, baking soda, and salt to batter very gently. 10. Brush dhokla steaming plates with oil. 11. Pour batter and spread evenly. 12. Sprinkle crushed black peppercorns. 13. Steam for 10-12 minutes until surface is not sticky and knife comes out clean. 14. Cool slightly and cut into squares or diamonds. 15. For chutney: grind coriander, green chillies, ginger, peanuts, curd, and spices to fine paste. Serve hot dhokla with chutney.

name: Butter Chicken Momos
instructions: 1. Make makhani sauce: Heat oil and butter, add cumin seeds. 2. Add sliced onions and cook until golden. 3. Add tomatoes, garlic, ginger, green chilli, coriander stem, cashew, kashmiri red chilli, and green cardamom. 4. Cook well and add turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, salt, and hot water. 5. Simmer until tomatoes are completely soft. 6. Grind the cooked mixture into smooth paste. 7. Strain through sieve. 8. In final cooking stage, heat butter and add chopped ginger and garlic. 9. Add the tomato-cashew paste and cook. 10. Add red chilli powder, honey, and fresh cream. 11. Season with kasuri methi, garam masala, and coriander. Adjust salt. 12. For dough: Make soft dough with refined flour, salt, and water. 13. For filling: Mix minced chicken with onion, spring onion greens, coriander, green chilli, ginger, garlic, lemon juice, black salt, dry mango powder, chaat masala, red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala, turmeric, and black pepper powder. 14. Mix in ghee and water. 15. Make momo wrappers and fill with chicken mixture. 16. Shape into traditional momo pleats. 17. Heat oil and butter in wok, add water. 18. Cook momos in the liquid until cooked through. 19. Serve hot with makhani sauce.

name: Surti Locho
instructions: 1. Make green chutney: Grind fresh coriander, mint, green chilli, ginger, gathiya, cumin seeds, sugar, lemon juice, salt, and ice cube with minimal water to thick consistency. 2. Make locho special masala: Mix kashmiri red chilli powder, asafoetida, black pepper powder, black salt, and cumin powder in bowl. 3. For locho: Wash chana dal and urad dal together until water runs clear, soak for 5-6 hours or overnight. 4. Discard water and grind soaked dals. 5. Wash poha and soak for 5-10 minutes. 6. Add soaked poha to mixer grinder with sour curd and water, grind to semi-coarse batter using pulse mode. 7. Transfer batter to large bowl and whisk for 3-4 minutes. 8. Cover and ferment in warm place for 5-6 hours. 9. Mix fermented batter gently and add ginger-green chilli paste, asafoetida, salt, turmeric powder, and oil. 10. Gradually add water and mix until combined. 11. Ensure steamer is ready and grease trays with oil. 12. Add eno to batter and mix well, batter will rise quickly. 13. Pour batter into tray, filling 3/4 full. 14. Steam for 12-15 minutes until done. 15. Scrape locho with spatula and serve hot. 16. Top with melted butter, locho masala, and sev. Serve with green chutney and chopped onions.

name: Crispy Chicken
instructions: Cut chicken breast thin against grain and marinate with salt, pepper, ginger-garlic paste, vinegar and soy sauce. Make batter with corn flour, refined flour, baking powder and egg white with water. Fry marinated chicken at 175°C until light golden (2-3 minutes). Prepare red chilli paste by soaking and grinding kashmiri chillies, then cook in oil until coarse. Make sauce with garlic, ginger, chillies, capsicum, spring onion, chilli paste, sauces and vinegar. Flash fry chicken again at 185°C until golden and crisp (1-1.5 minutes). Toss fried chicken in sauce with spring onion greens.

name: Cold Coffee - Caramel Frappuccino
instructions: Add ice to blender. Pour sugar syrup base (made from 1 cup sugar, 1 cup water, vanilla essence and xanthan gum) and caramel sauce (made by caramelizing sugar with butter and cream). Add instant coffee powder and chilled milk. Blend until frothy and smooth. Pour into glass and serve immediately.

name: Junglee Grill Sandwich
instructions: Roast whole spices until fragrant, cool and grind with powdered spices and salt to make sandwich masala. Blend coriander, spinach, green chillies, garlic, curry leaves, chana dal, sandwich masala with water and bread to make green chutney. Mix cabbage and carrot with mayo, schezwan sauce and sandwich masala for coleslaw. Apply butter and chutney on bread, layer with boiled potatoes and sandwich masala. Second layer: tomato slices and coleslaw with sweet corn and cheese. Third layer: complete with butter and chutney. Heat grill pan and butter it, grill sandwich until crisp and golden on both sides, applying pressure with plate.

name: Chatpata Paneer Toast
instructions: Heat oil in pan, add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add chopped onions, tomatoes, capsicum and cabbage. Stir and cook 1-2 minutes. Add schezwan sauce, red chilli sauce, ketchup, pav bhaji masala, garam masala, kashmiri chilli powder and salt. Mash everything with potato masher until combined. Add fresh coriander and mix well. Apply soft butter on bread slice, spread vegetable masala evenly, add diced paneer, sprinkle pav bhaji masala and grate processed cheese. Heat pan with butter, place toast on pan and cover with lid. Cook on low heat until cheese melts and bread is toasted golden from bottom. Cut as preferred and serve hot.

name: Amritsari Chole Bhature
instructions: For chana masala: roast whole spices until fragrant, cool and grind with powdered spices and salt. Soak chickpeas overnight. Boil with dry aavla, tea leaves and whole spices (carom, cardamom, cloves, pepper, cinnamon, bay leaf) until tender. For base masala: heat oil, crackle carom seeds, add onions and cook. Add ginger-garlic paste, green chillies and cook. Add turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder and chana masala. Add tomato puree and cook until oil separates. Add cooked chickpeas with water, salt and bring to simmer. Finish with ghee, kasuri methi, green chillies, ginger and fresh coriander. For bhature: mix refined flour, semolina, sugar, baking powder, baking soda and salt. Add curd and water to make soft dough. Cover and let rise 3-4 hours. Divide into portions, roll into oval shape. Deep fry in hot oil until puffed and golden.

name: Chicken Tikka Biryani
ingredients: kashmiri red chilli powder 1/2 tsp first marinade 1 tbsp second
instructions: First marinate chicken with salt, kashmiri chilli powder, ginger garlic chilli paste and lemon juice. Make birista by cutting onions lengthwise into even slices, separating layers. Heat oil to 135C and fry onions on high flame for 15-20 minutes until light golden, stirring continuously. Remove with spider strainer and cool on tissue paper. Use 80% for marinade, 20% for layering. Make second marinade with mustard oil, kashmiri chilli powder, hung curd, ginger garlic paste, turmeric, coriander, garam masala, cumin, black salt, chaat masala and kasuri methi. Combine with 80% birista. In separate pot, make chicken base masala with curd, ginger garlic paste, powdered spices, birista oil, remaining birista, coriander, mint and tomato. Cook rice with whole spices and aromatics until 70% cooked. Layer biryani: base masala, then rice, then remaining base, then rice. Top with ghee, saffron milk, remaining birista and fresh herbs. Cook in sealed pot (dum) over high flame until steam forms, then low heat for 45 minutes.

name: Tomato Rasam
instructions: Toast byadgi chillies over low flame until crisp and set aside. Add ghee to pan, roast chana and tur dal until aromatic. Add black peppercorns and cook briefly. Add cumin and coriander seeds and roast. Add curry leaves, fenugreek seeds and asafoetida and cook for few seconds. Cool completely. Break red chilli stalks and grind into fine powder, then add cooled spices and grind to semi-fine powder. Store in airtight container. Pressure cook soaked tur dal with water and turmeric for 3-4 whistles. Let depressurize naturally and whisk until smooth and watery. Heat oil in pot, add red chillies, mustard seeds, curry leaves and garlic, cook briefly. Add asafoetida, tomato and coriander stem, cook until tomatoes are mushy. Add turmeric and tamarind pulp with hot water, cook 3-4 minutes until raw tamarind smell is gone. Add cooked tur dal, green chilli and jaggery, add hot water to adjust consistency. Simmer 7-8 minutes. Mix rasam powder with water into smooth paste and add to rasam. Stir well, adjust salt, add crushed black peppercorns and coriander, cook 2-3 minutes. Serve hot.

name: Saoji Mutton
instructions: Prepare vatan paste: Heat oil, toast whole spices (cinnamon, cardamom, peppercorns, star anise, cloves, cumin, caraway, coriander, nutmeg, mace) on medium-low for 3-4 minutes. Add byadgi chillies, rice, chana dal and bay leaf, cook 1-2 minutes. Add stone flower, poppy seeds, sesame seeds and cook 1-2 minutes more. Cool and transfer to mixer. Fry sliced onions separately until light golden, cool and add to mixer with chopped coconut. Grind everything with water into thick paste. Separately grind ginger, garlic and fresh coriander with water into liquidy paste. Heat oil in handi, add ginger-garlic paste and cook 2-3 minutes on high. Lower flame, add turmeric and spicy red chilli powder, stir and immediately add vatan paste. Cook until oil separates and paste darkens and turns crumbly (about 15 minutes). Add mutton, coat well with masala and cook 5-6 minutes on high. Place parat on handi as lid, add water and cook on low until mutton releases its water (10-12 minutes). Add heated water from parat to reach semi-thick consistency. Place parat back and cook covered on low flame until mutton is tender, stirring twice or thrice. Adjust salt and consistency as desired. Add fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: Mutton Rogan Josh
ingredients: spicy red chilli powder 1 tsp optional
instructions: Heat mustard oil in kadhai until smoking point, lower flame and cool slightly. Add all whole spices along with mutton, stir and cook on high for 7-8 minutes to sear meat. In separate bowl, whisk curd with all powdered spices (fennel powder, asafoetida, ginger powder, saffron, kashmiri chilli powder, spicy chilli powder, cumin powder, garam masala), ensuring no lumps. Add whisked curd mixture to mutton with salt, stir and cook on medium-high until curd cooks and oil separates. Add hot water (just barely covering mutton). Stir, cover kadhai and cook on low heat until mutton is fully cooked, opening lid at intervals to stir and check doneness. Taste and adjust salt. In small pan, heat ghee, lower flame and add ratan jot, cook with ghee for 2-3 minutes. Strain ghee over mutton and mix well. Serve hot with rotis and rice.

name: Aam Ki Launji
instructions: Peel skin from raw mangoes and cut into thick strips. Set wok on medium heat and heat oil. Add cumin seeds along with all remaining whole spices (carom, fennel, onion seeds, asafoetida, mustard, fenugreek seeds), stir for few seconds. Add raw mango strips, mix well and cook for 1-2 minutes. Add water, salt, kashmiri chilli powder and garam masala, stir well. Cover and cook on medium-low for 2-3 minutes until raw mangoes soften slightly. Add jaggery, stir well and increase flame to melt jaggery. Continue cooking for 5-6 minutes until mixture thickens. Taste and adjust salt. Add black salt and chaat masala, stir well and switch off flame. Cool down completely and serve as condiment with paratha or other meals. Store in well-sterilized glass jar and refrigerate for months.

name: Chicken Do Pyaza
instructions: Marinate chicken with salt, turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, lemon juice and ginger-garlic paste. Heat ghee and oil in handi over high heat. Add all whole spices along with asafoetida and sliced onions, stir and cook until onions turn light golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and turmeric powder, stir and continue cooking until onions turn completely golden. Add chopped tomatoes, salt and kashmiri chilli powder, stir and cook on high until oil separates (add hot water if gravy gets too dry). Mix curd in separate bowl with all powdered spices (coriander, spicy chilli, cumin, fennel powders) ensuring no lumps. Add curd-spice mixture to gravy, stir and cook until oil separates again. Add marinated chicken, stir and sear for 4-5 minutes. Cover handi and cook on low for 10-12 minutes. Meanwhile cut onion into petals, dice tomato and crush coriander seeds. Remove lid after 10-12 minutes, add hot water to adjust consistency (gravy should be neither too thick nor too thin). Heat ghee in separate pan, add kashmiri red chillies, crushed coriander seeds, slit green chillies, onion petals and tomato quarters, toss over high heat for 30 seconds and immediately add to chicken, stir. Cook on high until chicken is fully cooked. Add roasted kasuri methi powder and garam masala, stir and taste, adjust seasoning. Finally add fresh coriander and stir. Serve hot with tandoori rotis.

name: Masala Khichdi
instructions: In large bowl, add rice and both dals, wash with water 2-3 times until water is clear. Add plenty of water and soak for 10-15 minutes. Heat ghee and oil in pressure cooker, add cumin seeds, bay leaf, red chilli, asafoetida and chopped onion, stir and cook on medium until onion turns translucent. Add chopped ginger, garlic and green chillies, stir and cook on high for 1 minute. Add chopped tomato with pinch of salt and cook until tomato gets mushy. Add turmeric, kashmiri red chilli and coriander powders, stir and immediately add diced potato, carrot, cauliflower florets, french beans and peas, stir and cook on high for 1 minute. Drain water from soaked rice and dals, add to cooker and stir. Add water and salt to taste, stir again. Put on lid and cook khichdi for 4 whistles on medium heat. Switch off and let cooker depressurize naturally. Open lid and mix khichdi gently. Add hot water and cook briefly if needed to adjust consistency as per preference. Add garam masala, roasted kasuri methi powder and fresh coriander, stir well. Serve hot with curd, pickle, papad and onions.

name: Crispy Chicken Burger
instructions: Make peri-peri spice mix by adding kashmiri red chilli powder, spicy red chilli powder, garlic powder, onion powder, dry mango powder, black pepper powder, oregano, sugar and salt to mixer grinder, grind well to fine powder. Make burger sauce by mixing mayonnaise, ketchup, mustard sauce, honey and red chilli sauce in bowl. Poke multiple incisions on chicken thighs, transfer to bowl and marinate with salt, black pepper powder, red chilli powder, lemon juice and ginger-garlic-chilli paste for 30 minutes. Add refined flour to large flat bowl or plate and season with salt and black pepper powder. Place chicken piece in flour, massage to coat completely, lift and shake off excess. Dip into ice cold water for 5-10 seconds, drop back into flour and massage again to coat, creating perfect crumb with crispy crevices. Place crumbed patty on plate and repeat with remaining chicken. Heat oil to 170C and carefully drop chicken patties, fry until light golden and crispy on all sides. Remove and place in sieve to drain excess oil. Sprinkle peri-peri powder on both sides while hot. Add soft butter to hot pan, place burger bun halves cut-side down and press gently, toast until light golden and crispy. Assemble burger: toasted bun, burger sauce, lettuce, crispy chicken patty, sliced tomato, flaky salt and black pepper powder, toasted bun with burger sauce. Serve with fries.

name: Railway Tomato Soup
ingredients: coriander roots and stems handful roughly chopped
instructions: Heat oil in pan over high heat. Add bay leaf, black cardamom seeds, garlic, ginger, green chilli, roughly chopped onion and beetroot, stir and cook on medium until onions turn translucent. Add roughly chopped tomatoes, coriander roots and stems, salt and kashmiri red chilli powder, stir and cook on high for 2-3 minutes. Add remaining powdered spices (coriander, cumin, black pepper), stir and add hot water, cover pan and cook on medium heat for 20-25 minutes. Switch off and let mixture cool. Remove bay leaf from cooled mixture and add to mixer grinder jar along with some water, grind into fine puree. Strain puree through sieve into stock pot and place over flame. Add hot water as required, stir and then add ketchup, lemon juice, chaat masala, sugar and butter, stir well and bring soup to boil. Taste and adjust seasoning as per requirement. Serve hot, garnish with soft butter and black pepper powder and serve with breadsticks.

name: Paneer Butter Masala
instructions: Soak cashews in boiling water 10-15 minutes. Grind tomato, garlic, ginger, coriander stems, green chilli, chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder and soaked cashews into fine paste. Heat oil and butter in pan, add cubed paneer with salt and kashmiri chilli powder, toss 1-2 minutes and set aside. In wok, heat oil and butter, add whole spices, garlic and green chilli, stir well. Add onions and cook until light golden. Add turmeric and kashmiri chilli powder with splash of water, cook 2-3 minutes. Add prepared paste with salt and sugar, cook until oil separates. Add hot water for gravy consistency, cook 2-3 minutes. Add paneer, butter and remaining ingredients, cook 1-2 minutes only. Finish with fresh coriander and serve hot with naan or roti.

name: Amrakhand
instructions: Place muslin cloth over sieve set on pot, transfer curd into cloth. Lift cloth and squeeze gently to drain excess water, twist cloth and place in sieve with weight on top. Keep in fridge overnight for 12-15 hours minimum. Cut mangoes and collect flesh in bowl. Grind mango flesh in mixer until fine paste. Heat pan over medium flame, add mango pulp with sugar, stir frequently and cook 15-20 minutes until thickened to jammy consistency. Switch off flame and cool completely. Take hung curd, transfer to sieve and strain into bowl. Whisk hung curd 3-4 minutes, then add cooled mango concentrate in 3 batches and mix well. Serve chilled. Can garnish with mango cubes, pistachio and dried rose petals.

name: Chocolate Granola Bars
instructions: Heat pan over medium-high heat, add rolled oats and toast while stirring until slightly golden, transfer to mixing bowl. Toast chopped almonds, cashews and walnuts until golden, add to bowl. Toast all seeds similarly and add to bowl. Cool mixture completely. Grind pitted dates into fine paste in mixer. Add dried berries and honey to cooled oats mixture, mix well. Add date paste and combine using spatula or hands. Transfer to butter-paper lined tray and level with spatula. Press evenly with potato masher (important for bar integrity). Cover with plastic sheet and roll pin over surface to further level. Place tray in fridge to set. Melt dark chocolate with coconut oil in heatproof bowl over boiling water stockpot. Remove tray from fridge and pour melted chocolate over granola, spread evenly. Return to fridge and let set 4-5 hours or overnight. Demold, remove butter paper and cut into bars. Store in airtight container.

name: Ussal Vada
instructions: For wet tempering: Heat oil, add ginger, garlic, cumin seeds, caraway seeds, coriander seeds, cinnamon, black pepper, cardamom, cloves, poppy seeds, sesame seeds and coconut, cook until fragrant. For tarri: Heat 4-5 tbsp oil, add mustard seeds and cumin seeds, add chopped onion with turmeric and kashmiri chilli powder, cook. Add kanda lehsun masala and white peas with 2 litres hot water, cook until peas soften. Add salt and lemon juice, finish with fresh coriander. For vada batter: Mix gram flour with baking soda, carom seeds and salt, add water to form semi-thin batter. Make chilli-garlic paste with green chillies and garlic. Prepare potato mixture by tempering boiled mashed potatoes with mustard seeds, curry leaves, asafoetida, turmeric, garam masala and fresh coriander. Shape vadas by dipping in batter and frying in moderately hot oil (165°C) until golden. Serve hot vadas with tarri and chutney.

name: Veg Railway Cutlet
instructions: Heat oil in pan over high heat, add cumin seeds, ginger, green chilli and asafoetida, cook briefly. Add carrot, beetroot, french beans and green peas, stir and cook 2-3 minutes. Add all powdered spices and salt, mix well and cook 1-2 minutes. Transfer to bowl and cool completely. Mash boiled potatoes on a large plate. Once veggies cool, add corn flour, breadcrumbs, mashed potatoes, salt, dry mango powder and fresh coriander, mix well with hands (do not knead). Check binding by pressing small portion - mixture should hold shape. To shape cutlets: form portion into roundel, form into droplet shape using palms, then press in single stroke to create flat cutlet shape. Make slurry by whisking refined flour, corn flour, salt and water into semi-thin consistency. Place breadcrumbs mixed with salt in tray. Dip shaped cutlet in slurry to coat well, use fork to remove, let excess drip off. Place in breadcrumbs and coat evenly on all sides. Heat oil to 165°C, carefully drop cutlets and fry over medium-high heat until golden brown and crispy. Transfer to sieve to drain excess oil. Serve hot with bread slices, butter and ketchup.

name: Chatpate Bhindi Aloo
instructions: Wash and dry lady fingers for 5-10 minutes. Cut into thick dices and place in a bowl. Cut potato into similar dices and soak in water. Cut onions into thick slices. Heat mustard oil in a kadhai until it reaches smoking point, then lower flame. Add cumin seeds, garlic, green chillies, and asafoetida; cook for 1-2 minutes. Add onion slices and cook on high flame until translucent. Add potatoes and cook for 2-3 minutes on high flame, then cover and cook on low flame for 4-5 minutes until almost cooked. Add all powdered spices and kasuri methi, cook for 1-1.5 minutes. Add lady fingers, stir well, cover and cook on medium-low flame until done. Adjust salt, add garam masala and fresh coriander.

name: Tari Waale Aloo
instructions: Make tomato puree by grinding tomatoes, ginger, and green chilli together. Heat mustard oil in a pressure cooker until smoking point, then lower flame. Add whole spices, curry leaves, chopped ginger, and asafoetida. Add powdered spices and stir quickly. Add tomato puree and salt, cook for 2-3 minutes. Add jaggery, potatoes, and water; stir well and cover. Cook on high flame for 2-3 whistles, then on low flame for 10 minutes. Let cooker depressurize naturally. Cook on high flame to thicken gravy and mash potatoes gently with a ladle; cook for 4-5 minutes. Add julienned ginger, kasuri methi, and fresh coriander.

name: Crispy Honey Chilli Potatoes
instructions: Cut potatoes into thick french fries. Boil water with vinegar and salt; cook potatoes for 3-4 minutes. Remove potatoes and let cool completely on a sieve. Make batter with refined flour, corn flour, salt, black pepper, baking soda, and chilled water. Dip blanched potatoes in batter and fry in moderately hot oil (165C) until light golden brown. Rest for 5 minutes, heat oil to 180C, and double fry until golden brown and crispy. Transfer to sieve. Heat wok with oil, add ginger, garlic, and green chilli; cook for a few seconds. Add spring onion bulbs and remaining sauce ingredients; cook for 1 minute. Add a splash of water and bring to simmer. Make corn flour slurry and add while stirring; let thicken. Add fried potatoes with coriander and spring onion greens; toss gently without overcooking.

name: Tandoori Chicken Strips
ingredients: roasted kasuri methi powder a pinch
instructions: Cut chicken breasts into long strips against the grain. First marinade: mix salt, kashmiri red chilli powder, lemon juice, and ginger garlic paste; marinate chicken. Second marinade: whisk mustard oil with kashmiri red chilli powder, add remaining powdered spices, ginger garlic paste, and curd; coat chicken strips well. Optional: smoke with live charcoal and ghee for 3-4 minutes. To crumb: add refined flour to a bowl with salt; have ice cold water ready. Coat marinated strips in flour, shake off excess, and dip in cold water for 5-10 seconds. Return to flour and coat again for crispy texture. Fry in moderately hot oil (170C) until golden brown and crisp. Transfer to sieve to drain. Garlic dip: whisk thick curd with mayonnaise, vinegar, powdered sugar, black pepper, salt, coriander, and grated garlic.

name: Ragda Puri
instructions: Wash and soak white vatana for 5-6 hours. Pressure cook soaked peas with potatoes, salt, turmeric and green chilli for 4-5 whistles. Mash potatoes once cooked. Cook over high flame for 3-4 minutes until ragda thickens. For green chutney: grind fresh coriander, mint, green chilli, ginger, black salt in mixer. For red chutney: grind soaked kashmiri red chillies, garlic, green chilli. Temper ragda with oil, cumin seeds, crushed green chilli, ginger, and powdered spices. Assemble puri with ragda, chutneys, onion, tomato, sev, chana dal, lemon juice and chaat masala.

name: Malvani Chicken Curry
instructions: Grind fresh coriander, green chilli, ginger, garlic with minimal water to make green vaatan. Marinate chicken with salt, turmeric powder, malvani masala, green vaatan, and lemon juice. For wet vaatan: Heat oil in kadhai, add sliced onions and cook briefly, add coriander seeds, black peppercorns, cloves, cinnamon. Once onions turn translucent, add garlic, ginger and grated coconut. Cook until coconut turns slightly golden. Cool completely and grind with minimal water into fine paste. Heat oil in handi, add chopped onions and cook until light golden brown. Add kashmiri red chilli powder, malvani masala with hot water splash. Stir and cook spices for 2-3 minutes. Add wet vaatan and cook while stirring frequently until oil separates. Add marinated chicken with salt and stir well. Add hot water and partially cover. Cook for 45 minutes (desi chicken) or 20-25 minutes (broiler chicken) until done and oil floats on top. Adjust seasoning and garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Sindhi Kadhi
instructions: Heat oil in stock pot over high flame. Once hot, add cumin seeds, mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds and asafoetida. Stir well and add gram flour. Cook over low flame while stirring continuously until gram flour turns biscuity in colour. Add curry leaves and once they crackle, add hot water stirring to prevent lumps. Add remaining water with salt, turmeric powder, kashmiri red chilli powder, ginger, green chilli and tomato puree. Stir well and bring to boil. Once boiling, add drumstick, cluster beans and potatoes. Cover and cook over medium-low flame for 10-12 minutes. Meanwhile, heat 1 tbsp oil in separate pan and fry lady finger over medium-high flame for 3-4 minutes. Transfer to bowl. Fry brinjal similarly and transfer to bowl. After cooking kadhi for 10-12 minutes, remove lid and add fried lady finger and brinjal. Stir well and continue cooking for 10-15 more minutes. Check potato doneness. Taste and adjust salt and tamarind pulp as required.

name: Chicken Kali Mirch Tikka
ingredients: roasted kasuri methi powder a pinch
instructions: Cut chicken into thin slices and place in bowl. Add salt, lemon juice and ginger garlic paste, mix well and apply marinade over chicken. Marinate for 30 minutes. For second marinade: add soaked cashews to mixer grinder with fresh cream, green chilli, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala, black salt, roasted kasuri methi powder and cardamom powder. Grind into fine paste. Add hung curd to plate followed by cashew paste, coarsely crushed black peppercorns and corn flour. Mix everything well until smooth marinade forms. Add first marinated chicken to second marinade and apply well. Optional smoking: place bowl with live charcoal, pour ghee on it and cover for few minutes. Skewer chicken on bamboo skewers. For pan cooking: set pan over high heat, add ghee and once hot place skewers. Cook over medium-high heat for 3-4 minutes then flip and cook similarly. For char effect, apply melted butter and hold over open flame. For oven cooking: preheat oven to 200C. Place chicken on baking pan using metal skewers. Bake for 15 minutes. When 2-3 minutes remain, apply melted butter and return to oven. Serve hot with lemon juice and chaat masala.

name: spring roll dosa
instructions: 1. Wash rice and urad dal, add fenugreek seeds and soak 3-4 hours. 2. Drain and grind into fine paste with little water batches. 3. In separate bowl, mix gram flour, semolina with water to smooth paste. 4. Add ground rice-dal mixture to flour paste, mix well, gradually add water to thick lump-free batter. 5. Cover and ferment 4-5 hours in warm place. 6. Check consistency, adjust water and salt. 7. For filling, heat oil in wok, add onion, cabbage, capsicum and all spices, cook 2-3 minutes on high heat. 8. Heat dosa tawa, sprinkle water to control temp. 9. Pour batter on tawa, spread circular using spoon. 10. Add butter, spread filling evenly, add cheese if using, add spring onion and coriander. 11. Cook 1-2 minutes on medium heat. 12. Transfer to board, cut into strips, roll. 13. Serve with coconut chutney and schezwan sauce.

name: mutton dum biryani
instructions: 1. Cut onions lengthwise into even slices, separate layers. 2. Heat oil to 135C, fry onions on high heat till slightly golden brown (15-20 min), stirring constantly. 3. Remove with spider, spread on tissue-lined tray, dab excess oil, cool 5-6 minutes to make birista. 4. Toast biryani masala spices in dry pan, grind coarsely. 5. Marinate mutton with salt, lemon juice, ginger garlic paste, green chilli paste, turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, coriander powder, 2 tbsp biryani masala, mint, coriander, 80% birista, 5 tbsp birista oil, and curd. 6. Cook marinated mutton in pressure cooker with ghee. 7. Soak basmati rice 30 minutes, boil in salted water with star anise, bay leaf, green chilli, cloves, black peppercorns, green cardamom, caraway seeds, lemon slice till 70% cooked. 8. Drain rice. 9. In heavy-bottomed pot, layer: fried potatoes if using, cooked mutton with marinade, cooked rice alternately. 10. Dot with remaining birista, mint, coriander, biryani masala, saffron milk, ghee. 11. Cover tightly and dum cook on high heat 3-4 minutes until steam forms, then low heat 12-15 minutes. 12. Remove from heat, let rest 5 minutes. 13. Open carefully and serve.

name: eggless chocolate strawberry cake
instructions: 1. Whisk butter and castor sugar till pale and fluffy. 2. Add condensed milk and vanilla essence, whisk well. 3. Add milk and mix well. 4. Mix curd and vinegar in separate bowl, add to batter. 5. Sieve refined flour, cocoa powder, baking soda into wet ingredients. 6. Mix dry with wet till no flour remains, avoid overmixing. 7. Pour into 7-inch parchment-lined tin, bake 170C for 30 minutes. 8. For ganache: place chopped chocolate and fresh cream in heatproof bowl over boiling water. 9. Whisk till melted, remove from heat, cover with cling with holes, refrigerate 30 minutes. 10. Mix chopped strawberries with powdered sugar. 11. Dissolve sugar in boiling water, cool completely for sugar water. 12. Once cooled, cut sponge into 3 equal layers with serrated knife. 13. Spread ganache on plate, place bottom layer, brush sugar water. 14. Spread ganache layer, add half strawberries. 15. Repeat with second layer. 16. Place third layer, brush remaining sugar water. 17. Spread nutella evenly, can make swirls. 18. Decorate with halved strawberries. 19. Refrigerate at least 1 hour before serving.

name: matar kulcha
instructions: 1. Mix lukewarm water, sugar, instant yeast, let activate. 2. Add refined flour and salt. 3. Add yeast mixture, curd, oil to flour. 4. Knead into smooth dough, cover and let rise till doubled (1.5-2 hours). 5. Divide dough into portions, roll into round shapes. 6. Heat skillet or tawa till very hot. 7. Place dough on tawa, cook till puffed and golden on both sides. 8. Apply butter, sprinkle fresh coriander and kasuri methi. 9. Boil white vatana peas in salted water till soft. 10. For green chutney: blend fresh coriander, mint, green chilli, ginger, salt, ice, water till smooth. 11. For tamarind water: soak tamarind in hot water, extract liquid. 12. Prepare spice mix by combining all spice powder ingredients. 13. In bowl, add cooked matar, green chutney, tamarind water, spice mix, ginger julienne, onion, tomato, coriander, lemon juice. 14. Mix well and adjust flavors. 15. Serve hot kulcha with matar mixture on the side.

name: veg pulao
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 2 tbsp
instructions: Wash basmati rice and soak for 30-45 minutes. In a large vessel, heat oil and ghee, add whole spices and sliced onions, cook until onions are light golden. Add ginger garlic green chilli paste, tomato and salt, cook for 2-3 minutes. Add powdered spices and cook for 1-2 minutes. Mix biryani masala with curd and add to the mixture, cook for a minute. Add potato, carrot, french beans, green peas, fresh coriander and mint, cook until potatoes are half done. Drain soaked rice and add with fresh water, stir gently and bring to simmer. Cover and cook on medium-low flame for 10 minutes. Check if water has dried, if not cook 2-3 more minutes. Let rest for 5-10 minutes, then serve by mixing from the sides.

name: thalipeeth
instructions: Grind green chillies, garlic, cumin seeds and salt into a coarse paste. In a large bowl, mix all flours with salt, coriander powder, turmeric powder, kashmiri red chilli powder, chilli-garlic paste, onion, fresh coriander and white sesame seeds. Add water gradually to form a semi-soft dough and knead for 3-4 minutes. Add oil and knead for 2-3 more minutes. Cover with damp cloth and rest for 5-10 minutes. Heat a tawa until hot. Dip muslin cloth in water and squeeze out. Place cloth on a platform, take a portion of dough, form a disc, and place on cloth. Wet your hand and press the dough to flatten into a semi-thin chapati shape. Make 3-4 holes using your finger. Add ghee on the tawa, spread it with a kitchen towel. Flip the cloth to place thalipeeth on the tawa and peel off the cloth. Add ghee in the holes and on sides, separate from tawa using a spatula, cover and cook on medium-low flame for 2-3 minutes. Flip, add ghee again and cook for 2-3 minutes more. Serve hot with curd, pickle and butter.

name: paneer pahadi tikka
ingredients: dry mango powder a large pinch
instructions: Blend fresh coriander, mint, ginger, garlic, green chilli and ice cube in a mixer grinder to make a fine green paste. In a mixing bowl, combine the green paste with hung curd, salt, roasted besan, carom seeds, kasuri methi, garam masala, chaat masala, dry mango powder, black salt, mustard oil and lemon juice. Mix well and smoke the marinade by pouring ghee over burning charcoal for 3-4 minutes. Cut paneer into cubes or cuboids, capsicum into squares, tomato into chunks using flesh (save pulp for other use), and quarter the onion to separate petals. Coat the paneer and vegetables with the marinade. Skewer on metal skewers, placing vegetables first and last to secure paneer in the middle. Cook on open flame to get charred texture, brush with melted butter and cook briefly again on flame. Sprinkle chaat masala and serve with mint-coriander chutney or dip of choice.

name: motichoor laddu
instructions: Sieve gram flour into a bowl and add water in batches while whisking continuously to make a smooth, lump-free, thin batter. Pour batter into a piping bag and tie a knot at the open end. Heat oil to 170C (moderately hot). Cut the tip of the piping bag to make a fine hole, hold it over the hot oil and gently squeeze to produce a stream of small droplets (motis) that fall directly into oil. Fry on medium flame until light golden brown, being careful not to over-fry. Transfer fried motis to a sieve using a ladle and shake gently to remove excess oil. Place on tissue paper to cool completely. Dissolve food colour in water, then combine with sugar in a pan over high heat. Stir until sugar dissolves, turn off heat and add all cooled motis, mix well.
