Turn heat back on to low and cook until all sugar syrup is absorbed by the motis, adding hot water if mixture gets dry. Cover and rest for 10 minutes. Transfer to a bowl, add melted ghee and melon seeds, mix gently and cool to room temperature. Form into laddus by taking spoonfuls and rolling in your hands. Garnish with varak (silver foil) and chopped pistachio.

name: chicken pot rice
ingredients: fresh red or green chillies 2-3 nos sliced
fresh red or green chillies 1-2 nos sliced
chicken stock or hot water 500-600 ml
instructions: Wash and soak rice for at least 30 minutes. Boil water with star anise, fresh chillies and salt in a stock pot. Add soaked rice and boil for 4-5 minutes until almost cooked (rice should have a slight bite). Drain and spread on a sieve, fluff with a fork and set aside. For chicken, cut breast into thin diagonal strips. Mix with ginger garlic paste, black pepper powder, whole egg and cornflour. Deep fry the chicken slices on medium-high heat for about 2-2.5 minutes until color changes, being careful not to overcook. Set aside. For pot rice gravy, set a wok on high heat and add oil, onions, garlic, ginger, fresh chillies and star anise. Cook for 1 minute. Add chicken stock and bring to boil. Add all sauces, green chilli paste, honey, vinegar, aromat powder, salt and white pepper powder. Mix cornstarch with a little water to make a slurry, add gradually while stirring to thicken the gravy. Add all vegetables and cook for 2-3 minutes on high flame. Add cooked chicken and spring onion greens. To assemble, spread cooked rice in an earthen pot or wok and pour the gravy over it. Cook on low heat for 5 minutes, then serve hot.

name: Green Soya Kheema
ingredients: salt a pinch and to taste
instructions: Make green paste by grinding fresh coriander, mint, spinach, green chilli, coriander seeds, black peppercorns, cumin seeds, green cardamom, black cardamom and an ice cube into fine paste using mixer grinder. Boil water in stock pot, add green chilli, ginger, salt and sugar for 2-3 minutes. Add soya granules and boil 3-4 minutes, then strain using sieve and press out excess moisture using spoon or ladle. Heat a tawa over high flame, add oil, add chopped onions and stir well, cook until golden brown. Add ginger garlic paste and continue cooking until onions turn completely golden brown. Add boiled soya and green peas, cook 1-2 minutes over high flame, then add prepared green paste and stir well. In separate bowl, whisk curd with all powdered spices (coriander powder, red chilli powder, cumin powder) and salt, add mixture to tawa and mix well. Cook masala 4-5 minutes, add chopped dill leaves and stir, add hot water and continue cooking over medium-low flame until oil separates. Taste and adjust seasoning, add garam masala, green chillies and fresh coriander and stir well. Heat small pan, add butter, once melted add kasuri methi and immediately pour tadka over keema. Stir well and serve hot with pav.

name: Dal Panchmel
ingredients: ghee 1 tsp and 2 tbsp for tempering
instructions: Add all dals to a bowl and wash thoroughly using water. Soak in fresh water for 2-3 hours. Discard water and transfer dals to pressure cooker with salt, turmeric powder, ghee and water (1 inch above dal). Cover and cook over high flame for 1 whistle, then over medium flame for 1-2 more whistles. Switch off flame, let cooker depressurize naturally, open lid and stir well. For tempering, add ghee to hot handi, once hot add cumin seeds with red chillies, garlic, ginger, green chillies and asafoetida. Stir well and cook ginger and garlic 1-2 minutes over high flame. Add onion and cook over high flame until golden brown. Lower flame and add powdered spices (turmeric powder, kashmir red chilli powder, coriander powder), stir and cook for 1 minute. Add chopped tomato with salt, stir and add splash of hot water. Cook over high flame until tomato turns mushy. Add cooked dal, stir well and bring to boil, cook 2-3 minutes. Taste and adjust salt, add kasuri methi, garam masala and fresh coriander. Stir well and serve.

name: Sev Khamani
ingredients: oil 4-5 tbsp for cooking and 2-3 tbsp for tadka
fresh coriander a small handful chopped
instructions: Wash chana dal and soak for 7-8 hours or overnight. Transfer to mixer grinder and grind once, gradually add water and grind to make semi-coarse mixture. Transfer to bowl with salt, turmeric powder, hing and eno, mix well and set aside. Heat non-stick pan, add oil, once hot add ground dal mixture and stir well. Lower flame, cover and cook 5-6 minutes. Open lid and stir, scraping any stuck layer on pan and mixing with dal mixture. Cover and cook 3-4 minutes on low flame. Add splash of hot water and stir well, cover and continue cooking on low flame (total cooking 20-25 minutes). Can add more hot water if mixture tends to dry. After 25 minutes, mixture should turn crumbly. Check doneness by shaping small portion into ball - should not stick to hand. Add sugar and stir, cook until dissolved (add little hot water to melt quickly), cover and keep on low flame, then switch off. For tadka, heat oil in pan on medium heat, add mustard seeds, garlic, ginger, green chilli, asafoetida and remaining ingredients. Stir well, add 1-2 tbsp hot water and mix. Pour tadka over cooked khamani, add lemon juice and freshly chopped coriander. Serve hot garnished with nylon sev, fresh coriander and pomegranate seeds with squeeze of lemon juice.

name: Veggies in Tandoori Sauce with Spinach Rice
instructions: Set wok over high flame, add oil and heat well. Once hot add ginger garlic chilli paste and cook briefly over high flame. Add zucchini and mushroom, toss for 1 minute over high flame. Add remaining veggies (baby corn, bell peppers, broccoli) with chilli flakes, toss all veggies over high flame for 1 minute. Set aside tossed veggies. Heat pan, add butter, once melted add flour and mix well. Cook over low flame until texture becomes sandy. Add milk in three batches while whisking continuously to avoid lumps, whisk sauce until smooth. Once smooth add all powdered spices (kashmir red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, dry mango powder, black salt, kasuri methi, turmeric powder, garam masala) and stir well. Continue cooking sauce over medium flame 2-3 minutes then switch off. For smoky flavour, place bowl with small piece of live charcoal over sauce, pour melted ghee on top, cover pan with lid and leave 2-3 minutes. Remove lid and remove charcoal, switch on flame and add tossed veggies. Stir well and cook 1-2 minutes. Taste sauce and adjust salt, add chopped coriander. For spinach rice, blanch spinach in boiling water 15-20 seconds, transfer to ice cold water immediately. Transfer blanched spinach to mixer grinder jar with mint, coriander, green chilli and grind into fine puree. Add oil and butter to hot wok, once oil gets hot add garlic, ginger and onion. Stir and cook over high flame until onions turn light golden brown. Add spinach puree with salt, sugar and red chilli flakes, stir well and cook 3-4 minutes over medium-high flame. Add cooked basmati rice and gently stir to coat rice with spinach puree, cook 2-3 minutes. Add lemon juice and stir well. Serve tandoori veggies with spinach rice.

name: Masala Puri
ingredients: water 1 inch above peas and 1-1.5 litre for masala
oil 2 tbsp for peas and 2 tbsp for paste and 2 tbsp for masala
tamarind a small lemon sized ball
instructions: Add dried peas to bowl and soak 6-7 hours at least or overnight. Discard water from soaked peas and transfer to pressure cooker. Add water and salt, pressure cook peas 3-4 whistles over medium-high flame. Once cooked let cooker depressurize naturally then open lid. To make paste for masala, set pan over high flame, add oil with all whole spices (star anise, cinnamon, black peppercorns, cloves), onion, ginger and garlic. Stir and cook over medium-high flame until onions turn translucent. Add tomatoes, salt and splash of water, cover and cook over medium flame until tomatoes turn mushy. Switch off flame, cool mixture completely and add to mixer grinder jar with tamarind, fresh coriander and mint leaves. Grind into fine paste. To cook masala, set deep pan over high flame and add oil once hot. Add prepared paste, stir and cook 1-2 minutes, then add all powdered spices (turmeric powder, kashmir red chilli powder, coriander powder, chaat masala). Mix well and cook spices with masala over medium-low flame 2-3 minutes. Add boiled and mashed potato, stir well and add water (1-1.5 litre approximately), mix well. Continue cooking masala 7-8 minutes until it thickens slightly. To assemble masala puri, crush few puris in plate, add boiled green peas followed by masala gravy, lemon juice, chaat masala, sev, chopped onions and grated carrot. Can also add green chutney and sweet tamarind chutney if desired.

name: Egg Butter Masala
instructions: Grind tomato, garlic, ginger, coriander stems and green chilli into a fine puree. In a pan with butter and oil, sauté the boiled eggs with salt, turmeric powder, kashmiri red chilli powder and fresh coriander until a thin brown layer forms, then transfer to bowl. Set a deep pan over high heat with butter and oil. Add cumin seeds, cinnamon, green cardamom, green chilli, garlic and ginger, stir well and cook for 1 minute. Add chopped onions and cook until golden brown over medium-high flame. Lower the flame, add turmeric and kashmiri red chilli powder with hot water splash, stir and cook spices for 1-2 minutes. Add the tomato puree, salt and sugar, cook until gravy leaves the surface of the pan (15-20 minutes). Add hot water to adjust consistency. Add the sautéed eggs, fresh cream, butter, fresh coriander, kasuri methi powder and garam masala, cook for 1-2 minutes, taste and adjust salt.

name: Kutchi Dabeli
instructions: Make dabeli masala: Toast whole spices over low flame until fragrant, cool and grind coarsely. Make masala peanuts: Heat peanut oil, add turmeric and kashmiri red chilli powder, add peanuts and roast, then add dabeli masala, powdered sugar, coriander powder, cumin powder and salt. Make garlic chutney: Grind garlic with water into paste, heat peanut oil, add salt and garlic paste, cook until oil separates, add kashmiri and spicy red chilli powder, cool. Make khatti meethi chutney: Soak tamarind, strain, heat peanut oil, add turmeric and kashmiri red chilli powder, add tamarind water, add dabeli masala, sugar, salt, cumin and coriander powder. Make dabeli filling: Heat peanut oil, add turmeric, kashmiri and spicy red chilli powder, add dabeli masala, add boiled and mashed potatoes with salt, add garlic chutney and fresh coriander, cool and add chopped onions. Assembly: Slice pav in middle, fill with large spoonful of filling, add khatti meethi chutney, garlic chutney, masala peanuts, chopped onions, close the pav. Heat pan with butter, place assembled dabeli in pan and coat well with butter and heat.

name: Pyaaz Ki Kachori
ingredients: fresh coriander a large handful chopped
instructions: Make garlic chutney: Grind soaked kashmiri red chillies with garlic and ginger into fine paste. Heat oil, add cumin seeds to crackle, add paste with salt, cook until oil separates and texture turns crumbly while stirring. Add lemon juice. Make kachori dough: Add refined flour, salt, carom seeds and ghee in bowl, mix well, rub flour between palms for shortening, add water gradually and knead using stretch and fold method until smooth and soft. Form into dough ball, transfer to greased bowl, brush with oil, cover with moist cloth, let rest for 2 hours. Toast whole spices over low flame until fragrant, cool and grind coarsely. Heat oil, add ground spices and boiled potatoes, mash potatoes, add salt and all powdered spices, add garlic chutney and fresh coriander, mix well. Cool the filling completely and add chopped onions. Shape filling portions into roundels and dough portions. Take a dough ball larger than filling roundel, flatten using finger, place filling in centre, press down, bring edges together to seal. Flatten into large thick disc using palms. Place on parchment-lined tray, cover with damp cloth. Heat oil in kadhai until 160°C, carefully drop kachoris and fry until crisp and golden brown. Transfer to sieve to drain excess oil.

name: Gur Papdi
instructions: Set a pan over high flame and lower it once hot. Add melon seeds and white sesame seeds, fry roast over low flame until almost light golden brown, transfer to bowl. Add coconut to same pan and dry roast until light golden brown, transfer to separate bowl and cool. Remove a few teaspoons of melon and sesame seeds as garnish. Transfer remaining melon and sesame seeds to mixer grinder and grind into coarse powder, transfer to bowl. Add edible gum to mixer grinder and grind into coarse powder. Add cashews and almonds to chopper and grind into coarse powder. Heat ghee into a hot deep pan, once ghee melts add wheat flour and karkara aata, keep stirring continuously to prevent lumps. Cook flour over low flame until it turns into a dark biscuity colour (12-15 minutes). Once flour becomes dark biscuity, add edible gum powder carefully as the ghee and flour mixture will fluff up, stir and cook for 1-2 minutes over low flame. Switch off the flame and add melon and sesame seed powder, cashew almond powder, coconut, cardamom powder, long pepper powder, ginger powder and nutmeg powder, mix well. Add chopped jaggery and mix until all jaggery dissolves with flame off. Grease a large plate with ghee and add the mixture. Level out and flatten using spatula and potato masher. Garnish with reserved melon and sesame seeds. Let set for 10 minutes, then cut into squares. Demould once completely set.

name: Chicken Triple Schezwan Fried Rice
ingredients: kashmiri red chilli 20-25 nos deseeded soaked
instructions: Make homemade red chilli paste: Grind soaked kashmiri red chilli with water into paste, heat oil, add salt and paste, cook until paste turns into a fine consistency. Marinate chicken: Add salt, black pepper, ginger garlic paste, light soy sauce, red chilli paste and egg white to chicken, mix and marinate. Fry chicken: Coat marinated chicken with corn flour and fry in oil until cooked through. Make schezwan gravy: Heat oil, add chopped garlic, ginger, spring onion bulbs, green chilli, cook, add light soy sauce, ketchup, red chilli sauce, vinegar, green chilli paste, coriander stems, white pepper powder and red chilli paste with hot water. Make slurry from corn flour and water, add to gravy to thicken, add julienned cabbage, carrot, capsicum, spring onion and spring onion greens, cook well. Make schezwan rice: Heat oil, add chopped garlic, ginger, spring onion, add chopped cabbage, carrot, french beans, add beaten egg, scramble, add cooked rice, boiled noodles, light soy sauce, vinegar, ketchup, green chilli paste, red chilli paste, sugar, white pepper powder, salt and spring onion greens. Combine: Mix fried chicken with schezwan gravy, toss the schezwan rice separately.

name: Punjabi Style Mutton Keema
instructions: Marinate minced mutton with salt, turmeric, chilli powder, ginger-garlic-chilli paste, curd, coriander and mint; set aside. Saute cumin seeds and onions until golden, add ginger-garlic-chilli paste, then powdered spices with a splash of hot water until oil separates. Add tomatoes, coriander stems and salt, cook until oil separates again for the base masala. In a pressure cooker, heat oil and ghee, add whole spices and green chilli, then the marinated mutton keema, sear 5-6 minutes. Add the base masala, mix, and pressure cook (1 whistle high + 2-3 whistles low). Let it depressurize naturally, then finish with garam masala, black pepper powder, kasuri methi powder, green chilli, coriander and ghee. Serve hot with pav.

name: fafda
instructions: Mix papad khar, asafoetida and 4 tbsp water. Sieve gram flour and add salt, carom seeds and 2 tbsp oil. Massage into flour to create shortening. Add papad khar solution and knead with 2 tbsp water to make semi-soft dough. Knead with oil until dough becomes stretchy and smooth. Cover with damp cloth. Heat oil to 160C in flat-bottomed vessel. Place wet kitchen towel on chopping board to prevent slipping. Take small portion of dough, roll into thick cylinder, place lengthwise on board and flatten using palm base. Peel shaped fafda and fry directly over medium-low heat for a few minutes until cooked on both sides. Remove from oil and place on sieve. Make spice mix with black salt, asafoetida and black pepper powder. Sprinkle over fafda while serving.

name: veg maratha kofta curry
instructions: Prepare vaatan: Heat 2 tbsp oil, add all whole spices, coconut, sliced onions (3), garlic and ginger. Cook until browned. Add green chilli and cashews. Add chopped tomato and cook until soft. Grind to smooth paste. Prepare kofta: Mix boiled mashed potato, paneer, carrot, french beans and cabbage. Add 1 tbsp chopped ginger, green chilli, kasuri methi, chaat masala, red chilli powder, coriander powder, corn flour and gram flour with ghee and salt. Shape into balls. Prepare gravy: Heat 2 tbsp oil, add 1 medium chopped onion until golden. Add 2 tbsp chopped garlic, 1 tbsp chopped ginger and sliced green chilli. Add red chilli powder, coriander powder and hot water splash. Add curd slowly and cook until oil separates. Add prepared vaatan paste and adjust water consistency. Add koftas carefully and simmer together. Finish with garam masala and fresh coriander.

name: dal mughlai
instructions: Soak dal for 6-8 hours. Tie all whole spices in muslin cloth. Pressure cook dal with spice bag, salt and water for 3 whistles on medium-high. Depressurize naturally. Check dal is cooked but grains intact. Heat oil and ghee in kadhai, add cumin and 3 sliced onions. Cook until light golden brown. Add garlic, ginger and green chilli, cook until dark golden. Lower flame, add powdered spices and hot water splash. Stir immediately. Increase flame and cook spices 3-4 minutes. Lower flame, add whisked curd slowly while stirring continuously. Increase flame and cook until oil separates. Add cooked dal and hot water to adjust consistency. In separate pan, heat 1/2-3/4 cup oil until moderately hot. Add remaining 3 medium sliced onions, fry frequently adjusting heat until light golden brown. Remove with spider and squeeze excess oil on tissue paper. Cool slightly. Crush and add 80% fried onions to dal. Add fresh cream, butter, kasuri methi and garam masala. Mix well. Taste and adjust salt. Add fresh coriander. Serve with remaining fried onion garnish and bread.

name: atta gur papdi
instructions: Add jaggery and water in vessel. Cook while stirring occasionally until jaggery dissolves completely. Turn off flame and cool slightly. Sieve cooled jaggery water. Put wheat flour, ghee, sesame seeds and salt in bowl and mix well. Add little jaggery water and knead dough like poori dough. Add more water if needed. After kneading, apply ghee on hands and mash dough lightly. Cover for 15 minutes. Apply ghee on hands again and massage dough lightly. Cut dough into 4 parts, keep rest covered. Apply ghee on board and rolling pin. Mash dough and shape like peda. Roll out lightly. Cut round papdi using glass or bowl. Roll each papdi lightly and prick with fork. Spread cloth and place papdi on it. Roll all same way and keep on cloth. Put under fan and air dry 15 minutes each side. In pan, put half ghee and half oil, heat to medium temperature on low-medium flame. Place some papdi in hot ghee-oil and fry 2 minutes. Turn and fry until golden brown on both sides. Remove and drain on tissue paper. Fry rest the same way. Store in airtight container.

name: sun-dried potato chips
instructions: Select potatoes, preferably white burbank variety. Peel potatoes thoroughly. Slice potatoes very thinly and uniformly using mandoline or knife. Optionally blanch potato slices in hot water to remove surface starch, or soak in water for few minutes. Drain well. Spread potato slices in single layer on cloth or thin mat. Place in direct bright sunlight. Turn slices every 2-3 hours to ensure even drying on both sides. Continue drying for several hours until completely crispy and brittle, typically full sunny day or longer depending on humidity. Slices should bend and snap when fully dry. Sprinkle salt to taste if desired while still slightly warm. Allow to cool completely. Store in airtight container in cool dry place. Can last for months to years when stored properly. Can be eaten as-is or fried briefly in hot oil to enhance crispness and flavor.

name: Rice Kachri
instructions: Soak rice, grind to a coarse paste, mix with salt and baking soda. Make small mounds and deep fry until golden and crispy, about 2-3 minutes per batch until puffed up completely.

name: Chana Dal Rice Dhokla
instructions: Soak chana dal and rice separately for 3-4 hours. Grind to a coarse paste with ginger, green chilli, turmeric and salt. Add curd and mix well. Add baking soda just before cooking. Pour into greased steamer tray. Steam for 15-20 minutes until done. Cool slightly and cut into pieces. For tadka: heat oil, add mustard seeds, sesame seeds, green chilli and curry leaves. Pour over dhokla and serve warm.

name: Atta Modak
instructions: Heat ghee, add wheat flour and semolina, roast until fragrant, about 5-6 minutes. Remove from heat, add all dry ingredients, nuts, and malai. Mix well. Add powdered sugar, cardamom powder, and milk. Mix until it comes together. Cool slightly, make small balls and shape into modak form using hands.

name: Bajra Burfi
instructions: Roast sesame seeds in a pan until fragrant and slightly darker, set aside. Heat ghee, add pearl millet flour, roast for 3-4 minutes stirring constantly. In another pan, dissolve jaggery with a little water, strain and heat until it reaches hard ball stage. Mix the roasted flour with sesame seeds and cardamom powder. Pour jaggery into the mixture, mix well. Pour onto a greased tray, press down, let cool slightly. Cut into pieces and garnish with almond flakes.

name: Mixed Vegetable Stuffed Paratha
instructions: Make dough with wheat flour, salt, and oil, let rest 30 minutes. For stuffing: heat oil, add cumin seeds, then green chilli. Add fenugreek leaves and salt, cook 2 minutes. Add grated potato, paneer, coriander leaves, red chilli and turmeric. Mix and cool. Take dough portion, flatten, fill with stuffing, seal edges, and roll into paratha. Cook on a hot tava (griddle) with ghee on both sides until golden brown and crispy.

name: Masala Namakpara
instructions: Make dough with refined flour, salt, spices (cumin, red chilli, black pepper, fennel flower, carom seeds, asafoetida), and ghee. Rest for 30 minutes. Make saata by mixing ghee and flour. Roll out dough thin, spread saata, fold into layers, roll again. Cut into diamond or rectangular shapes. Deep fry in oil until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels.

name: Honey Amla Candy
instructions: Wash and dry amlas thoroughly. Remove pits and cut into small pieces. Mix with honey in a large bowl, stirring well to coat all pieces. Spread on a clean cloth or drying tray and allow to dry in sunlight for several days, stirring occasionally, until the pieces become firm and candy-like with a dry exterior.

name: Dry Mango Pickle
ingredients: raw mango (kairi) 1 kg, cut into pieces
instructions: Cut raw mangoes into thin slices or pieces. Mix mangoes with salt and spread on a cloth to dry in sun for 2-3 days. Heat mustard oil and temper with mustard seeds, fennel, fenugreek seeds, carom seeds, and asafoetida. Dry roast turmeric and chilli powder separately. Mix all spices with dried mango pieces and oil, stirring thoroughly. Store in clean, dry glass jars in sunlight for 2-3 weeks, stirring occasionally, until fully cured and firm.

name: Madhuban Vada (Dahi Bhalla)
instructions: Soak moong and urad dal separately for 4 hours. Grind both dals with minimal water into a fluffy batter. Fry almonds and cashews in ghee until golden, add to batter with cumin seeds and salt. Deep fry spoonfuls of batter in oil until golden brown. Soak fried vadas in warm water for 15 minutes until soft. Toast all spices for tempering and powder coarsely. Whisk yogurt with powdered sugar and milk until smooth. Arrange soaked vadas in a serving dish, pour yogurt mixture over them. Garnish with powdered spice mix, sev, raisins, and pomegranate seeds.

name: Soft Semolina Burfi (Rava Burfi)
instructions: Lightly roast semolina in a heavy-bottomed pan with 2 tbsp ghee until fragrant, 3-4 minutes. Add cream and stir continuously to prevent lumps. Once combined, add milk and continue stirring on medium heat for 8-10 minutes until the mixture becomes thick and pulls away from the pan. Add powdered cardamom and sugar, mixing well. Pour onto a greased plate or tray, spread evenly to about 1/2 inch thickness. Garnish with almond flakes. Cool completely, then cut into squares.

name: Makki ki Roti with Masala Paratha
instructions: For Makki Roti: Mix cornmeal with salt, carom seeds, and oil. Add warm water gradually, kneading into a soft dough. Divide into portions and press between plastic into thin rounds. Cook on a hot griddle until light brown spots appear, flipping once. For Masala Paratha: Toast cumin seeds, red chilli, and turmeric. Mix chopped greens with roasted spices, sesame seeds, ginger-green chilli paste, and salt. Roll dough thin, spread green mixture, fold and roll again. Cook on griddle with oil until golden brown spots appear, flipping to cook both sides.

name: Stuffed Bread Pakora
instructions: Prepare idli batter by seasoning with salt, red chilli, and chaat masala. For potato filling: Mash boiled potatoes, add salt, chaat masala, coriander powder, ginger, green chilli, and coriander leaves. For paneer filling: Crumble paneer, season with chaat masala, salt, coriander powder, ginger, green chilli, and coriander leaves. Trim bread crusts, flatten each slice with a rolling pin. Spread filling on one slice, cover with another slice, and cut diagonally to make triangles. Dip each triangle in seasoned idli batter, coating completely. Deep fry in hot oil until golden brown and crispy on both sides. Serve hot with chutneys.

name: Farsan Kachori (Dry Masala Kachori)
ingredients: besan bhujia (gram flour noodles) 1/2 cup, crushed
instructions: Toast all whole spices (coriander, fennel, cloves, pepper, black cardamom, cumin) and sesame seeds; grind to coarse powder. Make dough with refined flour, salt, carom seeds, oil, and water; knead until smooth and let rest 15 minutes. For filling: Combine crushed farsan papdi, besan bhujia, peanuts, cashews, ground spice powder, turmeric, chilli powder, dry mango powder, and tamarind chutney. Mix well and adjust seasoning. Roll small portions of dough thin, place spoonful of filling in center, seal edges carefully. Deep fry in hot oil at medium-high temperature until golden brown, turning to cook both sides evenly. Drain on paper towels.

name: Soft Besan Burfi (Gram Flour Burfi)
ingredients: muskmelon seeds (kharbooje ke beej) 1/2 cup (60 grams)
instructions: Dry roast gram flour in a heavy pan over medium heat, stirring continuously for 8-10 minutes until light golden and fragrant; set aside. Dry roast semolina separately for 3-4 minutes. Coarsely grind cardamom pods. Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan, add roasted gram flour and semolina, stirring constantly to prevent lumps, for 3-4 minutes. Add milk and mix well. In a separate pan, dissolve sugar in 1/2 cup water, boil to make a thick syrup (thread consistency). Add sugar syrup gradually to the flour mixture, stirring continuously. Add roasted muskmelon seeds and ground cardamom, mix thoroughly. Continue stirring on medium heat for 3-4 minutes until the mixture becomes thick and dry. Transfer to a greased plate, spread evenly to about 1/2 inch thickness. Cool completely, then cut into squares or diamond shapes.

name: Makhana Modak
instructions: Roast makhana in ghee. Boil milk in a heavy pot. Add roasted makhana to boiling milk and cook until soft. Add sugar and cardamom powder. Continue cooking, stirring occasionally until milk thickens. Add chopped almonds, cashews, coconut, and pistachio flakes. Stir well and cook for 2-3 more minutes. Shape into modak using a traditional mould or hands. Serve warm or cold.

name: Carrot Halwa
instructions: Peel, wash and grate carrots, removing yellow middle part. Put grated carrots and fresh malai in pressure cooker. Close and cook on high flame until one whistle, then low flame for 2 minutes. Cool and open. Heat again on medium-high flame, stirring continuously until milk dries and blends with carrots. Add sugar and continue cooking until sugar dissolves and halwa reaches desired consistency. Garnish with cashews and almonds. Serve warm.

name: Bread Dahi Poha
instructions: Crumble leftover bread into small pieces. In a bowl, mix yogurt with salt and chaat masala. Add crumbled bread to yogurt. Heat oil in a small pan, add mustard seeds and curry leaves until they crackle, pour over bread mixture. Mix in chopped vegetables and green chilis. Toss well and serve immediately.

name: Mirchi Masala Bonda
instructions: Make filling: Heat 1 tbsp oil, add cumin seeds and asafetida. Add peas and chopped green chilis, cook briefly. Add ginger paste, coriander, chili, and turmeric powders. Add mashed boiled potatoes, amchoor, salt and coriander leaves. Mix well. Prepare batter: Soak bread in water, squeeze and mix to paste. Make vertical slit in each large green chili, remove seeds carefully, stuff with potato filling. Dip stuffed chilis in bread batter. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels. Serve hot with chutney.

name: Mayonnaise Cutlets
instructions: Make cutlet mixture: Mash 500g boiled potatoes, mix with salt, coriander, chili powder, ginger-chili paste, amchoor, garam masala, roasted cumin, and coriander leaves. Make mayo filling: Mash 100g boiled potato, add salt, vinegar, and fresh malai to make a creamy paste. Divide cutlet mixture into portions, stuff each with mayo filling, shape into patties. Coat with maida and black pepper. Roll in bread crumbs. Deep fry in hot oil until golden. Drain on paper towels. Serve hot.

name: Eggless Cheesy French Toast
instructions: Mix custard powder and sugar powder in a bowl. Add milk gradually, stirring to avoid lumps. Add vanilla essence and mix well. This is the coating mixture. Heat butter on a non-stick pan or griddle over medium heat. Dip each bread slice briefly in the custard mixture on both sides. Place on hot pan. Place a cheese slice on top and cook until golden and cheese starts melting. Flip carefully and cook the other side until golden. Serve hot with butter or jam.

name: Zero Oil Shahi Paneer
instructions: Heat a heavy-bottomed pot. Add cumin seeds, bay leaves, cinnamon, cloves, peppercorns, and large cardamom. Toast until fragrant. Chop tomatoes, ginger, and green chili. Add tomatoes to spices and cook until soft. Grind cashews with water to make paste. Strain tomato-spice mixture and add cashew paste back. Add turmeric, coriander, and Kashmiri chili powders. Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally. Add paneer cubes and salt. Simmer gently until paneer is tender. Add kasuri methi and stir. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot with naan or rice.

name: Stuffed Shimla Mirch with Gravy
instructions: Halve capsicums and remove seeds. Mix boiled potatoes with peas, ginger, green chilli, spices and stuff into capsicum halves. In a pan heat oil, add cumin seeds and asafoetida. Add tomato, ginger, green chilli paste, cook until soft. Add coriander and turmeric powder. Place stuffed capsicums in gravy, cook covered for 15-20 minutes until capsicums are tender. Garnish with coriander leaves and fenugreek.

name: Bengali Langcha
instructions: Boil milk and curdle with lemon juice. Strain and press paneer/chena. Mix chena with mawa, refined flour and baking powder. Knead into soft dough. Roll into cylindrical shapes. Prepare sugar syrup with water and add crushed cardamom. Deep fry langcha in ghee until golden, then dip in warm syrup. Keep dipped in syrup for absorption.

name: Namakpare and Nimki
instructions: Mix refined flour, salt, oil and carom seeds. Knead into stiff dough with minimal water. Divide dough into three portions and add different spice mixtures to each. Roll each portion thin and cut into desired shapes. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towel. Store in airtight container.

name: Vrat ke Aloo Stuffed Balls and Tikki
instructions: Rinse and cook barnyard millet until done but firm. Mix cooked millet with boiled potatoes, rock salt, ginger, green chilli, black pepper and cumin powder. Form into balls or patties. Heat ghee in pan with cumin seeds and additional green chilli for tempering. Pan-fry the millet-potato balls/tikkis until golden on both sides. Serve with lemon juice and coriander leaves.

name: Mini Gulab Jamun
instructions: Mix mawa, wheat flour and baking soda. Knead with milk into soft dough. Cover and rest for 15 minutes. Prepare sugar syrup by boiling sugar with water until dissolved, then add crushed cardamom and keep covered. Apply ghee on hands and knead dough. Form small round balls ensuring no cracks. Heat ghee in pan to medium temperature. Fry one gulab jamun at a time on low-medium flame until it floats and turns dark brown on all sides. Immediately transfer to lukewarm syrup. Cover and soak for 2 hours before serving.

name: Dilpasand
instructions: Mix refined flour, salt and baking powder. Add 1 tbsp ghee and knead into dough. Rest 15 minutes. Prepare layer mixture by roasting flour in 1.5 tbsp ghee. Mix coconut, tutti frutti, nuts, raisins, cardamom and sugar powder for filling. Roll dough thin and apply layer mixture, then apply filling mixture. Roll tightly and coil into spiral shape. Place on greased pan. Brush with ghee and bake at 180°C for 25-30 minutes until golden, or pan-fry over low heat in a covered pan.

name: mushroom veggie balls with kofta curry
instructions: Mix soaked soya chunks with finely chopped mushroom, ginger-chilli paste, coriander powder, chaat masala, red chilli powder, garam masala, cumin powder, grated potato, salt, and corn flour. Shape into balls. Fry in oil until golden. For curry: heat butter and oil, add chopped ginger-chilli, turmeric, coriander powder. Add chopped tomatoes, kashmiri chilli, cumin powder, chaat masala, tomato sauce and salt. Simmer 3-4 minutes. Add fried balls and chopped coriander stem. Serve hot.

name: creamy macaroni pasta
instructions: Boil water with salt and 1 tsp oil. Add macaroni and cook until al dente. Drain and set aside. Dice all vegetables. Heat 2 tbsp oil, add chopped ginger and green chilli. Add diced vegetables and cook 2-3 minutes. Add tomato sauce, salt, black pepper, and chilli flakes. Simmer 2 minutes. Add cooked macaroni and malai (cream). Mix well and cook for 1-2 minutes. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: crispy rice dosa
instructions: Soak rice in water for 3-4 hours. Drain water and grind rice with fresh water until smooth batter forms. Transfer to bowl and add water as needed to reach pourable consistency. Add salt, cumin seeds, ginger-chilli paste, black pepper, and coriander leaves. Mix well and let batter rest 10 minutes. Heat tawa on medium-high heat, apply oil evenly. Pour batter and spread into thin round shape. Cook until light brown on bottom, about 2-3 minutes per side. Serve hot.

name: black masoor pulao
instructions: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot. Add bayleaf, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, black pepper, and cumin seeds. Cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds. Add chopped ginger and green chilli, cook 1 minute. Add diced potato and black masoor dal. Stir and cook 2 minutes. Add rice and salt. Add 2 cups water, bring to boil. Reduce heat, cover and cook on low until rice and dal are tender, about 15-18 minutes. Finish with lemon juice and mix gently. Serve hot.

name: singhara barfi
instructions: Powder cardamom coarsely. Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan on low heat. Add water chestnut flour and roast for 3-4 minutes, stirring constantly. Add desiccated coconut and almond powder, roast for 2 more minutes. Gradually add milk while stirring to avoid lumps. Keep stirring to prevent sticking. Add sugar and continue stirring until mixture thickens and ghee starts separating, about 8-10 minutes. Add powdered cardamom and mix. Pour onto greased tray, flatten evenly and decorate with almond flakes. Cool completely before cutting into squares.

name: sheermal
instructions: Mix yeast with a little warm milk and let sit 5 minutes until foamy. In a bowl, combine flour, salt, and powdered sugar. Add yeast mixture and remaining warm milk. Mix and knead into soft dough, about 8 minutes. Add ghee gradually while kneading until fully incorporated. Cover and let rise in warm place for 2 hours until doubled. Divide into balls and let rest 15 minutes. Roll each into flat bread. Cook on preheated tawa or griddle on medium-low heat for 2-3 minutes per side until light golden and cooked through. Brush with ghee. Serve warm.

name: Cornmeal Dosa
instructions: 1. Mix cornmeal, wheat flour, and water to thin running consistency batter. 2. Add cumin, salt, red chilli, coriander powder, ginger-green chilli paste, turmeric, and coriander leaves. Let rest 15-20 minutes. 3. Blend roasted peanuts, chickpeas, salt, green chillies, ginger, coriander leaves, lemon juice, and water to make chutney. 4. Season chutney with tadka: heat oil, add mustard seeds, red chillies, and curry leaves. 5. Prepare filling: heat oil, add mustard seeds, cumin, curry leaves. Add ginger-chilli paste, cook peas with asafoetida and water. Mash peas, add tomato and turmeric, cook. Mash boiled potatoes and mix with spices. 6. Heat tawa, pour and spread batter thinly, add oil around edges, fry until golden brown, flip and roast. 7. Add filling to one side, fold dosa, serve with chutney.

name: Spicy Pani Pakoda
instructions: 1. Soak black gram and green gram separately for 2-3 hours. 2. Drain water completely. 3. In mixer, grind soaked lentils with green chillies, ginger, and salt until coarse paste forms. 4. Take out paste and heat oil in kadhai to medium-high temperature. 5. Drop lentil paste using spoon to shape vada in hot oil. Fry until golden. 6. For special pani: Grind coriander leaves, mint leaves, ginger, green chillies with water. 7. Mix in black salt, cumin powder, crushed black pepper, red chilli powder, asafoetida, and salt. 8. Add lemon juice and water to make flavored pani. 9. Serve pani pakoda in buttermilk or yogurt water with spiced pani.

name: Sheer Pira
instructions: 1. Sift milk powder and sugar together to remove lumps. 2. In a bowl, mix sifted milk powder and sugar well. 3. Add crushed cardamom powder to the mixture. 4. Chop almonds, cashews, pistachios, and raisins finely. 5. Mix all dry fruits into the milk powder mixture. 6. Knead the mixture into a stiff dough using minimal water or condensed milk. 7. Shape into flat rectangles or balls. 8. Press gently and cut into desired pieces. 9. Serve as is. Fudge sets naturally without cooking.

name: Corn Pakora
instructions: 1. Remove corn kernels from cob using knife, chop finely. 2. Chop spinach finely, removing excess water. 3. Chop potato finely. 4. Grate ginger and chop green chilli. 5. In bowl, mix gram flour with all spices (red chilli, asafoetida, carom seeds, salt). 6. Add corn kernels, spinach, potato, ginger, green chilli, coriander leaves, and kasuri methi. 7. Mix well with water to form thick batter. 8. Heat oil in pan to medium-high temperature. 9. Drop spoonfuls of batter and fry until golden brown and crispy. 10. For chutney: grind coriander leaves, ginger, green chilli, salt, cumin powder, asafoetida, and curd into paste. 11. Serve pakora with chutney.

name: Buff Vada
instructions: 1. Grate boiled potatoes and add rock salt, black pepper, and arrowroot. Mix and knead soft dough, add more arrowroot if needed. Cover. 2. For stuffing: mix peanut powder, coconut, sesame seeds, chopped cashews, chopped raisins, ginger, green chilli, black pepper, rock salt, sugar powder, coriander leaves, pomegranate seeds, and lemon juice. 3. Make 6 stuffing balls. 4. Mix remaining stuffing into dough, knead well with more arrowroot if needed. Make 6 balls. 5. For tikki vada: make 6 flat tikki-shaped balls. 6. Heat oil to medium-high, fry stuffed vada until golden brown. 7. Fry mixed vada same way. 8. Shallow fry tikki vada with less oil at low-medium flame until golden. 9. For chutney: grind coriander, ginger, green chilli, rock salt, cumin, curd, and water into paste. 10. Serve all three vada types with farali chutney.

name: Millet Dosa
instructions: Jowar Dosa: 1. Mix sorghum flour, wheat flour, curd, salt, ginger-green chilli paste, and baking soda. 2. Add water to make thin batter. Let rest 10 minutes. Bajra Dosa: 1. Mix pearl millet flour, gram flour, curd, salt, carom seeds, and baking soda. 2. Add water to make thin batter. Let rest 10 minutes. Tomato Peanut Chutney: 1. Heat oil, add fenugreek seeds, cumin seeds, asafoetida. 2. Add turmeric, chopped tomatoes, ginger, dry red chilli, green chilli. 3. Cook until soft. 4. Grind with roasted peanuts, coriander powder, and salt into paste. Aloo Masala: 1. Heat oil, add cumin seeds, asafoetida, coriander powder. 2. Add ginger-green chilli paste, turmeric, chopped tomato. 3. Cook and add mashed boiled potatoes, red chilli, and coriander leaves. Dosa: 1. Heat tawa, pour batter while spreading thinly. 2. Add oil around edges. 3. Fry until golden brown, flip and roast lightly. 4. Add aloo masala filling or serve plain. Serve with chutney.

name: urad dal kadhi
instructions: Soak urad dal for 1 hour. Grind to a thick batter. Deep fry spoonfuls in hot mustard oil until golden, making pakora. Make gravy by mixing curd with turmeric, red chilli, salt, and asafoetida in water. Heat oil, add cumin seeds, ginger, green chilli. Pour curd mixture into boiling water. Add the fried dal pakora carefully. Prepare tadka: heat oil with cumin, asafoetida, dry chilli, curry leaves, kashmiri chilli. Pour tadka over kadhi. Cook on low heat for 5 minutes. Garnish with coriander and serve hot.

name: gond ke laddu
instructions: Dry roast gond in a pan on medium heat until crispy, about 3-4 minutes. Crush coarsely and set aside. Heat ghee in heavy pan, add wheat flour gradually, stirring constantly on low heat for 8-10 minutes until golden and fragrant. Add roasted gond, almond flakes, dry coconut, and mix. In another pan, melt jaggery with milk, stirring until smooth. Filter through muslin cloth. Pour jaggery mixture into flour mixture off heat. Add cardamom powder and dry ginger powder. Mix quickly and thoroughly. Cool slightly. Roll into balls of desired size. Store in airtight container.

name: creamy tomato soup
instructions: Wash and chop 4 tomatoes and half beetroot. Add to pressure cooker with chopped ginger, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp black pepper, and 1/2 cup water. Pressure cook on high for 1 whistle, then low heat for 2 minutes. Let cool and strain. Blend strained tomatoes with 1/4 cup water and roasted cumin powder. Strain paste through sieve. Pour strained juice into bowl, add 1.25 cup water and 1/2 tsp black salt. Bring to boil. Mix 1 tsp cornflour with water to make solution. Pour cornflour solution into boiling soup while stirring. Add 1.5 tsp sugar and 1 tsp butter. Cook on low heat 4 minutes, stirring occasionally. Serve hot garnished with cream.

name: poha capsicum cutlet
instructions: Rinse poha under water quickly. Boil potatoes, peel, and mash. Finely chop capsicum and carrot. Mix mashed potatoes, poha, ginger-chilli paste, capsicum, carrot, and green coriander. Add coriander powder, red chilli, amchur powder, cumin powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix thoroughly. Shape mixture into oval or round cutlets. Heat oil in pan on medium heat. Shallow fry cutlets on both sides until golden and crispy, about 3-4 minutes per side. Remove on absorbent paper. Serve hot with tamarind or mint chutney.

name: soya vegetable pancake
instructions: Soak soya vadi and rice separately for 30 minutes. In mixer jar, add soaked soya vadi, rice, curd, green chilli, and ginger. Grind until smooth batter is formed. Pour batter into bowl. Add finely chopped capsicum, grated carrot, chopped tomato, cumin seeds, coriander leaves, and salt. Mix well. Just before cooking, add a pinch of baking soda and mix gently. Heat oil in non-stick pan on medium heat. Pour 1/4 cup batter onto pan and spread gently to form pancake. Cook until bottom is golden and set, about 2-3 minutes. Flip carefully and cook other side. Serve hot with chutney.

name: cheese angoori
instructions: Crumble paneer and mix with shredded mozzarella, arrowroot powder, chopped green chilli, salt, kasuri methi, black pepper, and green coriander. Shape into balls and deep fry in hot oil until golden. Remove and set aside. For gravy: Heat oil and ghee, add cinnamon, tejpat, cumin, black cardamom, and clove. Add chopped tomato and cook for 5 minutes. Grind soaked cashew with ginger and green chilli to smooth paste. Add paste to tomato mixture. Add turmeric powder, coriander powder, and kashmiri chilli. Cook for 5-7 minutes on low heat. Add kasuri methi, fresh cream, and remaining shredded cheese. Mix gently. Add the fried cheese balls to gravy carefully. Simmer for 2-3 minutes without boiling. Serve hot garnished with green coriander.

name: crispy potato nuggets
ingredients: oil for frying 2 teaspoons plus more
instructions: Boil and cool potatoes. Peel and mash them. Mix mashed potatoes with rice flour, salt, red chilli powder, cumin seeds and chopped coriander leaves to form a dough. Shape into small nuggets or bites. Heat oil in a pan and deep fry the potato nuggets until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

name: arbi leaves patra
ingredients: oil 2 tablespoons plus more for frying
instructions: Clean arbi leaves thoroughly. Mix gram flour with turmeric, red chilli, coriander powder, carom seeds, asafoetida and salt. Add finely chopped ginger and green chilli. Make a thick paste by adding water. Spread this mixture on arbi leaves, roll them up tightly, and coil them. Steam or fry the coiled leaves until crispy and golden. Cut into pieces and serve hot.

name: saag muthia
ingredients: oil 1 tablespoon plus 2 tablespoons for tadka
instructions: Chop spinach, bathua and fenugreek leaves finely. Mix with wheat flour, gram flour, ginger, green chilli, cumin seeds, coriander powder, red chilli, turmeric and carom seeds. Add curd, sesame seeds, baking soda and salt. Mix well to form a dough. Heat oil in a pan, add mustard seeds and cumin seeds for tadka. Add sesame seeds. Pinch portions of dough and add to the hot oil. Fry until golden. Serve hot with chutney.

name: pakoda kadhi
ingredients: oil 1 tablespoon plus 1.5 teaspoons for tadka
instructions: Mix gram flour with turmeric and salt. Make a thick batter by adding water slowly. Heat oil in a pressure cooker. Add fenugreek seeds, cumin seeds and whole green chillies. Pour the gram flour batter into the hot oil to make pakoras. Once pakoras are formed, add yogurt mixed with water to create the kadhi. Season with red chilli and salt. Close the cooker and cook on high pressure for 3-4 minutes. Open and add tadka of oil, cumin, dry red chilli and kashmiri chilli. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: moong dal cheela
ingredients: salt 1 teaspoon plus 0.75 teaspoon plus 0.25 teaspoon
instructions: Wash and soak moong dal and rice for 1 hour. Grind to a coarse paste with water. Add ginger-chilli paste and salt to batter. Whip for 1-2 minutes. For veggie-potato stuffing: heat oil, add ginger and chilli, saute carrot and capsicum, add salt and chilli powder, add tomato and tomato sauce, mix in grated potato, chilli and coriander. For paneer-cheese filling: mix green chutney, paneer and coriander. Heat tawa, spread batter thin, roast on high heat, flip and roast other side. Add green chutney and stuffing on one half, top with paneer and sev. Fold and roast both sides. Serve hot.

name: crispy coconut cutlets
ingredients: rock salt 1 teaspoon plus 0.5 teaspoon plus 0.25 teaspoon
ghee 1 teaspoon plus oil for frying
instructions: Soak sama rice for 30 minutes and cook until soft. Mix cooked rice with ginger, green chilli, black pepper, cumin seeds, rock salt and ghee to form dough. For stuffing: mash boiled potatoes, mix with grated coconut, almond flakes, raisins, green chilli and black pepper. Shape dough into balls, fill with stuffing and form cutlet shape. Roll in chestnut flour and crushed peanuts. Deep fry in oil until golden and crispy. For chutney: blend green coriander, ginger, green chilli, rock salt and lemon juice. For fruit curd: mix yogurt with mashed banana, grated apple, pomegranate seeds and sugar. Serve cutlets with chutney and fruit curd.

name: Schezwan Noodles Dosa
instructions: Heat oil and stir-fry ginger, green chilli, carrot, capsicum and cabbage until crisp. Add soya sauce, tomato sauce, vinegar and salt. Mix with schezwan sauce and boiled noodles. Spread dosa batter on griddle, cook until golden, fill with noodle mixture, and serve.

name: Besani Crispy Karela
instructions: Slice bitter gourd, salt and let sit. For crispy version: coat with gram flour mixture and fry. For gravy version: temper mustard seeds and cumin in oil, add ginger-chilli paste and tomato, cook bitter gourd with spices until tender.

name: Ram Laddu
instructions: Soak chana dal, moong dal, and urad dal separately. Grind soaked dal with asafoetida and salt to a coarse mixture. Shape into balls and fry until golden. Blend radish leaves, coriander, chilli, ginger, lemon, black salt, amchur and cumin for special chutney. Serve laddus with chutney and grated radish topped with chaat masala.

name: Potato Chips Rolls
instructions: Boil and peel potatoes. Grate or slice potatoes into thin sheets. Mix with corn flour and salt. Shape into rolls. Deep fry until golden and crispy. Serve hot as a tea-time snack.

name: Adhirasam
instructions: Soak rice for 3-4 hours. Grind soaked rice coarsely. Powder jaggery and cardamom. Mix rice powder with jaggery, cardamom, ghee, and sesame seeds to form dough. Shape into balls. Deep fry in ghee until golden and crispy. Cool and store.

name: Mango Malpua
instructions: Mix wheat flour, semolina, and mango pulp. Add milk to make thin batter. Heat ghee and pour batter to make thin discs (malpua), fry until light golden. In separate pan, make sugar syrup with cardamom and kesar. Soak fried malpuas in warm syrup. Garnish with almond and pistachio flakes.

name: red-green chilli pickle
instructions: Chop green and red chillies and ginger. Dry roast fennel seeds, fenugreek seeds, carom seeds, and black mustard seeds. Grind roasted seeds into powder. Mix chillies with salt, ginger, seed powder, turmeric, kashmiri chilli, kalonji, and asafoetida. Heat mustard oil and pour over the mixture. Add lemon juice and vinegar. Mix well and store in a sterilized jar.

name: veg cheese sandwich
instructions: Mix gram flour, refined flour, salt, red chilli powder, and coriander leaves. Add water gradually to make a coating batter. Apply pizza sauce on some bread slices and green chutney on others. Add chopped capsicum and cheese. Dip or coat bread with batter. Cook on tawa on medium heat until golden brown on both sides. Serve hot.

name: mohanthal burfi
instructions: Roast gram flour in ghee until golden and fragrant. Add malai and milk gradually, stirring to avoid lumps. Keep stirring on medium heat. Add sugar gradually and continue stirring until mixture thickens to dough consistency. Add crushed cardamom. Pour into greased pan and garnish with almond and pistachio flakes. Let cool and set. Cut into pieces and serve.

name: crispy sweet corn
instructions: Boil water with salt and add corn kernels. Boil 5-6 minutes and drain. Mix corn flour and refined flour with salt. Coat corn kernels by sprinkling water and mixing repeatedly until well coated. Remove excess flour by sifting. Heat oil and fry half the corn covered for 2-3 minutes, then uncovered until golden brown. Drain in sieve. Repeat with remaining corn. Melt butter, add ginger and green chilli, fry lightly. Add tomato sauce, kashmiri chilli powder, and salt on low flame. Add fried corn and mix. Add chaat masala, coriander, and lemon juice. Serve hot.

name: makhana dry fruit burfi
instructions: Dry roast makhana at medium flame until crispy, cool, and grind into fine powder. Grind cashews in pulse mode to make powder. Mix cashew powder with makhana powder. Dry roast desiccated coconut for half a minute and add to mixture. Add cardamom powder and mix well. Grease a tray with ghee. Pour milk in pan and cook until half is left. When half milk remains, add sugar and cook until dissolved. Reduce flame to low and add ghee. Add powder mixture and stir well until dough forms. Transfer to greased tray and press evenly. Garnish with almond and pistachio flakes. Let cool for 1 hour. Cut into pieces and serve.

name: sabudana namkeen
instructions: Roast sabudana until crispy. Roast peanuts until golden. Roast fox nuts until crispy. Toast coconut pieces. Mix all roasted ingredients together. Add raisins and rock salt, mix well. Add sugar powder and mix thoroughly. Store in an airtight container.

name: wheat flour chakli
instructions: Mix wheat flour with salt, turmeric, cumin seeds, carom seeds, red chilli powder, asafoetida, and sesame seeds. Add water gradually to make a stiff dough. Knead well. Heat oil in a pan. Use a chakli maker or sieve to form chakli shapes directly into hot oil. Fry on both sides until golden and crispy. Remove with slotted spoon and drain on paper towel. Store in an airtight container.

name: Corn Kofta Curry
instructions: Boil corn cobs and extract kernels. Mix corn with gram flour, salt, ginger, green chilli and coriander leaves to form kofta paste. Heat oil and deep fry small koftas until golden. In a separate pan, heat oil and temper with cumin seeds and asafoetida. Add ginger-green chilli paste, then tomatoes and cook until soft. Add turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder and salt. Add the fried koftas to the gravy and simmer for 2-3 minutes. Finish with ghee and dried fenugreek leaves.

name: Susla-Sushila
instructions: Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds and let them crackle. Add cumin seeds and curry leaves. Add roasted peanuts and stir. Add finely chopped green chilli, ginger if desired. Add diced tomato and potato and cook until potato softens. Add turmeric powder, salt, sugar, and asafoetida. When potato is cooked, gently fold in puffed rice to avoid breaking it. Mix well and turn off heat. Finish with lemon juice and fresh coriander leaves.

name: Dryfruit Burfi
instructions: Heat ghee in a large pan on medium-high heat. Deep fry edible gum pieces until they puff up, then remove. In the same ghee, roast wheat flour on low-medium heat for 3-4 minutes until fragrant, stirring continuously. Coarsely powder the roasted nuts and seeds. Crush roasted gum into smaller pieces. Add all nuts, seeds, gum, and coconut to the roasted flour and mix well. Grind cardamom coarsely and add along with nutmeg and dry ginger powder. In another pan, melt jaggery with 2 tbsp water on low heat. When jaggery becomes liquid, add the flour-nut mixture and stir constantly until well combined. Spread on a greased tray and press gently. Cut into squares while warm and garnish with almond flakes.

name: Matar Ke Chole
instructions: Soak dry peas overnight. Drain and pressure cook with baking soda and salt for 20-25 minutes until cooked but not mushy. Drain the peas. Heat oil in a pan and add the cooked peas. Add finely chopped ginger, green chilli and cook for 1-2 minutes. Add chopped tomatoes and stir. Add cumin powder, coriander powder, chaat masala, black salt, red chilli powder and black pepper. Mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add dry mango powder and lemon juice. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and coriander chutney. Serve hot.

name: Chana Dal Laddu
instructions: Soak chana dal for 2-2.5 hours. Drain and grind coarsely with 2 tbsp water. Heat ghee on medium-high heat. Form small pakoras from the dal batter and deep fry for 2 minutes per side until golden. Remove and cool completely. Break into small pieces. Roast almonds, cashews and pistachios in ghee until fragrant, then add the fried dal pieces. Roast for 1-2 minutes on low heat. Add raisins and ground cardamom. Divide mixture into 2 parts. For churma laddus: add boora to first half, mix with ghee, and shape into laddus. For motichoor laddus: heat sugar with water to form one-thread consistency, add to second half, cover for 5 minutes, then shape laddus with ghee on hands.

name: Dal Bafla
instructions: For baflas: Mix wheat flour, semolina, salt, carom seeds and baking soda. Add ghee and curd to make a stiff dough. Divide into egg-sized balls and steam for 15 minutes. For dal: Pressure cook chana dal and arhar dal together with crushed spices (black pepper, cloves, cinnamon, black cardamom) for 3-4 whistles. Heat ghee and temper with cumin seeds and asafoetida. Add ginger, green chilli paste and cook. Add chopped tomato, turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli powder and kashmiri red chilli. Cook until tomato softens. Add the cooked dal and adjust consistency. Season with salt. Serve hot baflas with dal curry and garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Stuffed Tomato Curry
instructions: Heat oil and add cumin seeds, asafoetida. Add turmeric powder and ginger-green chilli paste, then finely chopped capsicum and tomato. Add salt, coriander powder, kashmiri red chilli, and garam masala. Mix well and cool. Stuff this mixture into whole tomatoes. In a separate pan, heat oil and prepare a tomato-based curry. Arrange stuffed tomatoes in the curry and cook until tender.

name: Moong Dal Burfi
ingredients: 15-20 kesar strands soaked in 1 tbsp milk
instructions: Clean moong dal with wet cloth. Dry roast dal at low-medium flame while stirring continuously until brown and fragrant. Cool and grind into fine powder, then sieve. Heat fresh malai in pan and add soaked kesar strands. Add moong dal flour and roast while mixing. Add sugar powder and coarsely ground cardamom seeds. Mix well and roast at low flame, stirring continuously until mixture turns thick. Pour into ghee-greased plate and level evenly. Garnish with pistachio and almond flakes, pressing lightly. Cool and refrigerate for 1 hour. Heat plate slightly from below to set, then cut and separate pieces.

name: Peanut Mathri
instructions: Wash peanuts well. Grind peanuts, green chilli and ginger into slightly coarse paste. In a bowl add refined flour, crushed cumin seeds, crushed carom seeds, crushed dry fenugreek leaves, red chilli, salt and oil. Mix well. Add peanut paste and mix well. Add water little by little and knead hard dough. Cover and keep for 20 minutes. Mash dough and divide into 4 parts. Round and make logs. Break small balls and roll thick like mathri. Cut with glass for rounds, cut strips for rectangular namak pare, or use bottle cap for cashew-shaped pieces. Heat oil to less hot temperature with low-medium flame. Fry mathri for 2-3 minutes, flip and fry till golden brown. Cool completely and store in airtight container.

name: Bajra Masala Paratha
instructions: Boil and mash potatoes. Mix millet flour and wheat flour. Add mashed potatoes, chopped spinach, fenugreek leaves, grated ginger, green chillies, red chilli, turmeric powder, cumin seeds, carom seeds, asafoetida, sesame seeds, salt, and coriander leaves. Mix well and add oil. Knead dough to smooth consistency. Divide into portions and roll parathas to 1/4 inch thickness. Cook on hot griddle on both sides until golden brown spots appear and paratha is cooked through.

name: Sindhi Masala Koki
instructions: Mix wheat flour, salt, carom seeds, cumin seeds, fenugreek leaves, red chilli, green chilli, turmeric powder, coriander leaves, and finely chopped cabbage. Add ghee and knead dough with water to soft consistency. Roll round flat breads about 1/4 inch thick. Heat griddle and cook koki on both sides until golden brown spots appear and it is cooked through. Serve hot.

name: Ulta Vada Pav
instructions: Boil and cube potatoes for filling. Mix gram flour with salt, baking soda, carom seeds and spices. Roast mustard seeds and add ginger, green chilli and curry leaves to gram flour batter. Fold in boiled potato pieces. Heat oil for deep frying. Dip potato cubes in batter and fry until golden brown on all sides. Serve hot vada with pav and green chutney.

name: Churma Laddu
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan on low flame. Dry roast gram flour in ghee for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly. Add semolina and roast for another 2 minutes. Slowly add milk while stirring to prevent lumps. Cook for 5-7 minutes until mixture thickens. Add chopped cashews, almond flakes and desiccated coconut. Mix in crushed jaggery and cardamom powder. Let cool completely. Shape into round balls using your hands, pressing firmly.

name: Matar Makhana Curry
instructions: Heat oil in a pan and roast cashews until golden, set aside. In the same oil, add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add ginger and green chilli paste, stir for 30 seconds. Add tomato pieces and cook until soft. Add turmeric, coriander powder and kashmiri red chilli powder. Add makhana, green peas and salt. Cook on medium heat for 5-7 minutes. Add cashew paste, garam masala and cumin powder. Simmer for another 3-4 minutes. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.

name: Lauki Handvo
instructions: Mix rice flour and gram flour with curd until smooth. Add grated gourd, salt, turmeric, red chilli and green chilli. Add ginger, coriander leaves, cumin seeds, sesame seeds and curry leaves. Let the batter ferment for 30 minutes. Just before cooking, add eno fruit salt to make the batter airy. Heat oil in a pan and pour batter. Shallow fry on both sides until golden brown. For chutney: grind peanuts, chana, coconut, ginger and green chilli with salt and lemon juice. Temper with oil, mustard seeds, dry red chilli and curry leaves. Serve hot handvo with chutney.

name: Rasiya Muthiya
instructions: Mix cooked rice with gram flour, ginger-green chilli paste and salt. Add asafoetida, turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli powder, dry mango powder and coriander leaves. Mix well. For gravy: heat oil and add cumin seeds, ginger-green chilli paste and dry red chilli. Add buttermilk and cook for 3-4 minutes. Add turmeric, red chilli and coriander powder. Bring to boil. Shape rice-gram flour mixture into small dumplings (muthias) and gently drop into the gravy. Simmer for 5-7 minutes. For tadka: heat oil, add cumin seeds, curry leaves and kashmiri red chilli. Pour over the curry.

name: Instant Masala Dosa
instructions: Mix leftover toor dal, semolina, curd and salt in a bowl. Add baking soda and mix gently until well combined, let rest for 5 minutes. Heat oil on a flat pan or griddle. Pour batter in a thin stream to make crepe-like dosas. Cook on both sides until crispy and golden. For peanut chutney: dry roast and grind peanuts with green chilli, ginger, lemon juice and salt. Heat oil in a pan, add mustard seeds and let them crackle. Add dry red chilli and curry leaves, then mix into the ground peanut mixture. Serve hot dosas with peanut chutney.

name: Wheat Flour Laddu
instructions: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan on low flame. Add semolina and roast for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly. Add wheat flour and roast for another 2-3 minutes until fragrant. Slowly add milk while stirring to avoid lumps. Cook for 5-7 minutes until the mixture thickens and becomes golden. Add watermelon seeds and mix well. Remove from heat and let cool for 2-3 minutes. Mix in sugar powder and cardamom powder thoroughly. Once cooled enough to handle, shape into round balls by pressing firmly between your palms.

name: moong dal spinach fritters
instructions: Soak moong dal for 2-3 hours. Drain well. Mix dal with finely chopped spinach, fenugreek leaves, green chilli, ginger, red chilli powder, asafoetida, salt and coriander leaves. Heat oil in deep pan. Drop spoonfuls of mixture into hot oil and fry until golden and crispy on all sides. Remove and drain on paper towel. Serve hot.

name: poha paratha
instructions: Grind flattened rice into powder. Boil and mash potatoes. Mix potassium, ginger, cumin, carom seeds, turmeric, chilli powder, coriander leaves and salt. Add oil and water gradually to knead soft dough. Rest 10 minutes. Divide into portions, flatten and cook on tawa with oil until light brown spots appear. For raita: whip curd with milk, add boondi and black salt. Temper cumin seeds, green chilli and curry leaves in oil, add to raita. Serve paratha hot with raita.

name: gluten-free chilla pancake
instructions: Soak rice for 2 hours. Soak sago for 30 minutes. Grind soaked rice, sago, green chilli and ginger to smooth batter. Add curd and salt. Just before cooking, mix eno fruit salt into batter. Heat tawa with oil. Pour batter as circular pancakes. Cook on medium heat until lightly golden on both sides. Serve hot.

name: maize flour sev
instructions: Peel and mash boiled potatoes finely. Mix in maize flour, salt, red chilli, turmeric, asafoetida, baking soda, oil and chaat masala. Knead into firm dough. Using sev maker, press long strands of dough into hot oil. Fry until golden brown and crispy. Remove and drain on paper towel. Serve hot or store in airtight container.

name: pumpkin sabzi
instructions: Heat mustard oil. Add fenugreek and fennel seeds, let them crackle. Add asafoetida. Add chopped pumpkin and cook for 2 minutes. Add tomato, ginger and green chilli. Add turmeric, coriander powder, kashmiri chilli, dry fenugreek leaves and salt. Cover and cook on medium flame for 15-20 minutes until pumpkin is tender. Add dry mango powder, garam masala and sugar. Mix well. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot with puri or paratha.

name: falafel with hummus
instructions: Soak kabuli chana overnight and drain. Grind with sesame seeds, salt, lemon juice, olive oil, curd, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, pepper and cumin seeds to form thick paste. Heat ghee, temper with cumin seeds and red chilli powder. Divide chickpea mixture into portions and shape into balls or patties. Deep fry in medium oil until golden brown and cooked through. For hummus: blend cooked chickpeas with sesame paste, lemon juice, olive oil and spices. Serve warm falafel with cold hummus dip.

name: chana dal mathri
instructions: Soak chana dal for 2 hours. Heat oil in cooker, add drained dal and roast for 1 minute. Close cooker and cook at high flame till one whistle. Reduce heat and cook 4-5 minutes more. Cool and mash dal to coarse paste. Mix mashed dal, refined flour, salt, turmeric, cumin seeds, carom seeds, fenugreek leaves and oil. Knead into hard dough. Rest for 15 minutes. Divide into 4 portions. Roll each portion thin and cut into shapes with cookie cutter. Prick with fork. Heat oil and fry mathri on low-medium heat until golden brown. Drain and cool on paper towel. Store in airtight container.

name: raw turmeric halwa
instructions: Clean and chop raw turmeric into small pieces. Roast gram flour in ghee over low heat for 5-7 minutes until lightly golden and aromatic. Add chopped turmeric and continue roasting for 10-15 minutes until turmeric is cooked and oil separates. Add roasted almonds and mix. Dissolve jaggery with 1/2 cup water and strain. Add jaggery syrup to halwa mixture and stir continuously on low flame for 5 minutes until well combined. Add ground cardamom. Transfer to greased plate and spread evenly. Cool until set. Cut into squares and garnish with almond flakes. Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Veg Thali
instructions: Prepare a complete vegetarian meal platter with rice, lentil curry, flatbread, seasonal vegetables, yogurt, pickle, and garnish with ghee. Arrange all items on a large plate to serve as a traditional meal.

name: Jodhpuri Rabadi Laddu
instructions: Roast gram flour in ghee until golden and fragrant. Add milk gradually and cook until thick. Stir in sugar, crushed cardamom, muskmelon seeds, and mawa. Mix well and spread on greased surface. Cool and cut into pieces. Store at room temperature for months.

name: Gujarati Palak Thepla
instructions: Mix wheat flour, gram flour, spinach puree, yogurt, and spices together. Knead into soft dough with water as needed. Rest 20 minutes. Roll into thin circles and cook on griddle with ghee until golden and crispy. Serve hot with pickles or yogurt.

name: Gur Pare
instructions: Knead refined flour with ghee and milk into stiff dough. Rest 15 minutes. Roll out and cut into rhombus or square shapes. Deep fry in ghee until light golden. Prepare jaggery syrup by melting jaggery with ginger powder. Toss fried pieces in syrup or serve on the side. Stays crispy for up to 3 months.

name: Til Peanut Coconut Laddu
instructions: Lightly roast sesame seeds and peanuts if not already done. Mix with dry coconut. Heat jaggery in pan until runny. Add crushed cardamom to jaggery. Quickly mix in sesame-peanut-coconut mixture before jaggery cools. Form into laddus (balls) while warm. Cool before storing. Energy and protein-rich snack.

name: Fresh Aloo Bukhara Chutney
instructions: Chop plums and remove pits. Cook plums with sugar over medium heat until soft and jam-like consistency. Add crushed cardamom, cumin powder, salt, black salt, red chilli powder, and garam masala. Cook for few more minutes. Add muskmelon seeds and raisins. Cool and store in airtight jar. Serve with samosas, dahi bhalla, or chaat items.

name: Methi Mushroom Matar Malai
instructions: Heat oil and add bay leaf, cinnamon, cloves, and black pepper. Add cumin seeds and let crackle. Add ginger, green chilli, and turmeric powder. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft. Add mushrooms and peas, cook 5 minutes. Add coriander powder, red chilli powder, and fenugreek leaves. Simmer until mushrooms are tender. Season with salt. Serve hot with rice or bread.

name: til mawa barfi
instructions: Dry roast sesame seeds until fragrant. Mix roasted sesame seeds with mawa. Add powdered sugar and ground cardamom. Heat ghee and mix into the dry mixture. Stir continuously on low heat. Once combined and warm, transfer to a greased surface. Flatten and decorate with almond and pistachio flakes. Cool completely and cut into pieces.

name: halkova burfi
instructions: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan on medium flame. Add maida and roast continuously for 10-12 minutes until light golden and fragrant. Add powdered sugar and mix well. Continue stirring until the mixture turns into a crumbly texture. Add kewra essence and mix. Transfer to a greased tray, press firmly, and let cool. Cut into desired shapes.

name: sweet amla candy
instructions: Cut amlas and remove seeds. Boil amlas with salt and water until soft. Drain and cool. Mix boiled amla with ginger, sugar, and lemon juice. Cook on medium heat for 15-20 minutes, stirring frequently. Cool completely. Dust with cornflour mixed with powdered sugar. Store in an airtight container.

name: soya keema matar curry
instructions: Soak soya chunks in water for 15 minutes and squeeze dry. Heat oil and temper with bay leaves, cinnamon, cloves, black pepper, large cardamom, and cumin seeds. Add chopped ginger and green chilli. Add tomatoes and cook until soft. Add turmeric, coriander powder, and red chilli powder. Mix in soya chunks and beetroot. Add green peas and salt. Cook on medium heat until soya is tender, about 10-15 minutes. Sprinkle fenugreek leaves and garnish with coriander. Finish with a teaspoon of ghee.

name: makka til tikki
instructions: Melt jaggery in a pan. Mix cornmeal and sesame seeds together. Add melted jaggery to the dry mixture and mix well. Add 2 tsp ghee and combine into a dough. Shape into round tikkis on a greased surface. Heat oil in a deep pan and fry tikkis until golden and crispy on both sides. Drain on paper towels.

name: karonda sweet pickle
instructions: Wash karonda and prick with a fork. Heat mustard oil in a pan and add black cardamom. Add cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds, mustard seeds, fennel seeds, and asafoetida. Stir for a few seconds. Add turmeric powder, kashmiri red chilli powder, cumin powder, fennel powder, and garam masala. Mix in jaggery and cook until dissolved. Add karonda, salt, black salt, and raisins. Mix well and cook for 3-4 minutes on medium heat. Cool and transfer to sterilized jars. Store in a cool, dark place for 2-3 days before serving.

name: White Laddu
instructions: Melt khoya in a pan on low heat, add powdered sugar and crushed cardamom, mix well. Add chopped dry fruits. Cool slightly and roll into balls. Store in airtight container.

name: Rava Kachori
instructions: Mix semolina, salt, carom seeds and oil to form crumbly dough, add water and knead into stiff dough. For filling: heat oil, add cumin, ginger, chili, then peas and potatoes with spices. Let cool. Stuff dough portions with filling, seal and fry until golden.

name: Cheese Chilli Toast
instructions: Mix butter with chopped chili, capsicum and coriander. Spread on bread slices. Top with grated cheese and chili flakes. Toast on tawa/griddle until cheese melts and bread is golden crispy.

name: Mooli Pickle
instructions: Cut radish into desired shape. Mix with green chili and ginger. Dry roast mustard seeds, fenugreek, fennel and nigella. Add all spices, salt and vinegar to radish. Add mustard oil and mix well. Store in sterilized jar. Keep in sunlight for a few days.

name: Pudachi Wadi
instructions: Mix maida, besan, salt and spices with oil. Prepare filling by sauteing ginger, chili, poppy seeds, sesame, coconut, peanut with spices and dry fruits. Make dough balls, flatten, fill and seal. Fry until golden brown on both sides.

name: Aloo Kachori
instructions: Make soft dough with maida, salt, carom seeds and oil. Rest for 20 minutes. Prepare filling by mashing potatoes and sauteing with ginger, chili and spices. Shape dough into balls, flatten, add filling and seal. Fry in medium hot oil until golden, turning occasionally for even cooking.

name: Aloo Muthia
instructions: Boil and mash potatoes. Mix with rice flour, gram flour, yogurt, ginger, chili, cumin, turmeric, salt, coriander and baking soda. Shape into cylinders and steam for 15-20 minutes. Temper with oil, mustard seeds, sesame and curry leaves. Serve hot.

name: Chana Dal Idli
instructions: Soak chana dal and rice separately. Grind with curd to fine batter. Add salt, ginger, green chilli, and vegetables. Temper mustard, urad dal, and curry leaves in oil, add to batter. Add Eno salt and mix gently. Pour into greased idli moulds and steam for 10-12 minutes. Prepare chutney by grinding peanut, coconut, green chilli, lemon juice and salt. Serve warm idli with chutney.

name: Suran Ka Bharta
instructions: Peel and finely chop elephant yam. Boil until tender. Heat mustard oil, add cumin and asafoetida. Add ginger-green chilli paste. Add tomatoes and cook until soft. Add boiled yam and spices: turmeric, coriander, kashmiri red chilli. Add dry fenugreek leaves. Cook until well combined. Finish with garam masala, lemon juice and coriander leaves.

name: Kishmish Rabdi
instructions: Boil milk with crushed cardamom. Mix ararot powder in 2 tbsp cold milk, then add to hot milk while stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Add sugar and cook until thickened. Add raisins, chopped cashew, almond flakes. Finish with kesar strands soaked in 2 tbsp milk. Cook for 2-3 minutes more. Garnish with pistachio. Serve warm or chilled.

name: Suran Kofta Curry
instructions: Boil and mash elephant yam. Mix with salt, turmeric, lemon juice, chopped green chilli, ginger, coriander powder, red chilli, dry mango powder, and baking soda. Add gram flour and bind. Shape into koftas and deep fry until golden. For gravy: heat mustard oil, add whole spices (peppercorn, cloves, cinnamon, cardamom, bay leaf). Add cumin. Add tomato paste and cook. Add fresh curd. Add boiled water to desired consistency. Add turmeric, coriander powder, kashmiri red chilli, fenugreek, garam masala. Add fried koftas and simmer until flavors blend.

name: Atta Malpua Rabdi
instructions: For malpua: Mix wheat flour, milk, and mawa. Make a thick batter. Heat ghee and pour small amounts into hot oil with a small ladle. Fry until golden on both sides. For rabdi: Boil 1 liter milk with cardamom. Mix cornflour in 2 tbsp milk and add to boiling milk. Cook until thickened. Add sugar and almonds. For chashni: Boil sugar with 1 cup water and 2 crushed cardamom until one-string consistency. Add kesar strands. Dip fried malpuas in chashni and soak in rabdi. Serve warm.

name: Schezwan Veg Chowmein
instructions: Boil noodles with salt and 5ml oil until cooked. Drain and set aside. Chop vegetables: cabbage, capsicum, and carrot thinly. Heat 30ml oil in a wok or large pan. Add ginger and green chilli. Quickly add all vegetables and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes on high heat. Add boiled noodles. Pour soya sauce, vinegar, schezwan sauce, and tomato sauce. Toss well. Add crushed black pepper. Stir-fry for 2 more minutes. Serve hot garnished with coriander leaves.

name: Amla Chunda
instructions: Grate or chop amla finely. Mix with jaggery and let sit. Add all spices (black salt, black pepper, cardamom, cinnamon, turmeric, kashmiri chilli, garam masala). Squeeze lemon juice and mix well. Let it sit for a few hours. Store in glass jars for up to a year.

name: Guava Candy
instructions: Peel and deseed guavas. Cook with water until soft. Puree the cooked guava. Add grated beetroot for color. Mix in sugar and cook until thickened, stirring frequently for 45-60 minutes. Add butter and lemon juice. Spread on parchment paper to cool and dry. Cut into pieces when set.

name: Mixed Sprouts Salad
instructions: Soak black chickpeas, moong, and moath separately overnight. Sprout in a damp cloth for 2-3 days until small sprouts appear. Store in refrigerator for up to a week. For salad: chop cucumber and tomato. Mix sprouted beans with chopped vegetables. Drizzle olive oil, add black salt, cumin powder and coriander chutney. Top with chopped almonds. Toss and serve immediately.

name: Moong Dal Mangodi Curry
instructions: Soak moong dal 4 hours. Grind to a thick paste with asafoetida. Boil and shape into small balls (mangodi). Peel and dice potatoes. Heat oil, add cumin seeds. Add ginger-chilli paste, cook until fragrant. Add tomato and cook until soft. Add turmeric, coriander powder, red chilli powder. Add boiled mangodi and potatoes. Simmer 15-20 minutes. Season with salt and garam masala. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Methi Chaman Paneer
instructions: Blanch spinach and methi leaves in boiling water with salt for 4 minutes. Cool and grind into a fine paste. Heat oil, fry paneer pieces until light brown. In remaining oil, add cumin seeds and whole spices (cloves, pepper, cardamom, bay leaves). Add ginger and green chilli. Add chopped tomato and cook until soft. Add spinach-methi puree and roast for 5 minutes covered. Prepare cashew paste by grinding soaked cashews with water. Add cashew paste to the gravy with sugar. Add fried paneer pieces and cook covered for 5 minutes. Finish with lemon juice and dry fenugreek leaves.

name: Sooji Aloo Poori
instructions: Mix semolina, wheat flour, boiled and mashed potatoes, salt, chopped green chillies, red chilli powder, cumin seeds, carom seeds, and coriander leaves. Add water gradually and knead into a soft dough using oil. Let rest for 15-20 minutes. Divide into balls and roll into discs. Heat oil until medium hot.
