Deep fry each poori until it puffs up, turning to cook both sides evenly until golden brown. Drain on paper towels and serve hot with pickle and raita.

name: Bihari Thekua
ingredients: ghee 1/4 cup (60 ml) plus extra for frying
instructions: Melt jaggery with a little water and strain to remove impurities. In a bowl, mix wheat flour, semolina, fennel seeds, almond flakes, chopped raisins, grated dry coconut, and crushed green cardamom. Add melted jaggery and ghee to the dry mixture and combine well. Knead gently until the mixture comes together (should not be wet). Shape into small balls or press into a mold and cut into shapes. Heat ghee or oil for deep frying. Fry the thekua pieces on medium heat until golden brown on both sides, stirring gently. Remove and drain on paper towels. Cool completely before storing.

name: Aloo Vada Chaat
instructions: Boil and peel potatoes, then dice them into small pieces. Coat potato pieces with cornflour, salt, red chilli powder, and chaat masala. Heat ghee in a pan and fry the coated potatoes until golden and crispy on all sides. Remove and drain on paper towels. In a bowl, whisk curd with black salt, powdered sugar, and a little water until smooth. Layer the fried aloo pieces in a serving bowl, pour the seasoned curd on top. Garnish with red chilli powder, chaat masala, black salt, cumin powder, and sev. Top with sweet chutney and green chutney. Serve immediately while the potatoes are crispy.

name: Bedmi Poori
ingredients: oil 2 tbsp plus oil for frying
split black gram dal 1/4 cup (50 gms)
instructions: For stuffing: Roast split black gram dal until fragrant. Crush fennel, coriander, and cumin seeds. Mix roasted dal with crushed seeds, turmeric, red chilli, garam masala, mango powder, salt, and asafoetida. Roast briefly in a tsp oil. For dough: Mix wheat flour, semolina, salt, carom seeds, and fenugreek. Add oil and knead with water into a firm dough. Rest for 20 minutes. Divide dough into balls, flatten each ball, add a spoonful of filling in the center, seal edges, and flatten gently. Heat oil for deep frying. Fry each poori on medium heat until it puffs and turns golden brown on both sides. Drain on paper towels and serve hot with aloo sabzi.

name: Vrat Porous Dosa
ingredients: oil 1 tbsp plus oil for dosa
instructions: Soak samak rice for 30 minutes. Boil and mash 2 potatoes. Grind soaked rice with mashed potato and rock salt into a smooth batter using minimal water. For peanut chutney: Lightly roast peanuts, then grind with lemon juice, rock salt, black pepper, green chillies, curry leaves, and a little oil. For potato masala: Heat ghee, add cumin seeds, ginger, green chilli, and curry leaves. Add boiled and cubed potatoes, season with salt and black pepper, cook until warm. Heat a non-stick dosa pan/griddle with oil. Pour batter and spread thin to form a dosa. Cook until edges crisp, then flip and cook other side. Serve hot dosa with peanut chutney and potato masala on the side.

name: Chana Dal Pancake
instructions: Soak gram dal and rice in water for 3-4 hours. Drain and grind together into a fine batter with curd, green chillies, and ginger using minimal water. Add salt, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, and cumin seeds. Mix in finely chopped carrot, capsicum, tomato, and coriander leaves. Just before cooking, add eno fruit salt to the batter and mix gently. Heat a non-stick pan or griddle with a little oil. Pour batter and spread into a thin circle (like a pancake). Cook on medium heat until the bottom is golden, then flip and cook the other side. Serve hot with green chutney or pickle.

name: Chaulai Saag
instructions: Heat ghee and temper with cumin seeds and asafoetida. Add ginger and green chilli, sauté briefly. Add tomatoes and cook until soft. Mix in boiled potatoes. Add amaranth leaves and salt. Sprinkle wheat flour mixed with water to bind, stir well. Cook until leaves are tender and flavors blend, about 10-15 minutes.

name: Til Gud Laddu
instructions: Dry roast sesame seeds until golden. Toast coconut separately. Crush cardamom and remove skin. Heat milk with jaggery until jaggery dissolves. Add roasted sesame seeds, coconut, and cardamom powder. Cool slightly. Add ghee and mix well. Shape into balls while still warm.

name: Methi Laccha Paratha
instructions: Prepare dough with hot water, wheat flour, salt, and oil. Let rest. For filling: sauté mustard seeds and asafoetida in oil, add ginger and green chilli, add fenugreek leaves with salt and spices. Roll dough thin, spread filling, roll and layer by coiling dough and rolling again into circle. Cook on hot griddle with ghee until golden spots appear on both sides.

name: Veg Kebab Paratha Roll
instructions: Soak chana dal for 2 hours, grind with spices (cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, black pepper, cardamom, cumin) into coarse paste. Heat oil, add ginger and green chilli, add dal mixture and cook until dry. Add coriander powder, fennel, red chilli, amchur, corn flour and coriander leaves. Prepare paratha dough with wheat flour, refined flour, salt, oil and water. Roll dough into thin circles, fill with kebab mixture, roll into cylindrical shape. Cook on hot griddle with oil until golden.

name: Aloo Papdi
instructions: Boil potatoes and mash. Mix rice flour, refined flour, salt, cumin seeds, and carom seeds. Add mashed potatoes and oil. Knead into dough. Roll thin and cut into diamond shapes. Deep fry until crispy golden. For chaat: layer papdi with spiced mashed potatoes, yogurt, green chutney, sweet chutney, black salt, and sev.

name: Homemade Butter from Yogurt
instructions: Take fresh yogurt/curd. Pour into mixer and churn on high speed for several minutes. The fat separates from the whey. Collect the butter that forms. Rinse under cold water to remove remaining whey. Squeeze out excess moisture. Ready to use homemade butter.

name: Gunda Spicy Pickle
ingredients: yellow mustard seeds 4 tbsp coarsely ground
instructions: Soften lasoda with salt. Make spice mix by coarsely grinding mustard seeds. Heat mustard oil and add cumin, fenugreek, fennel, carom seeds, then asafoetida. Add turmeric and chilli powder. Add the softened lasoda and raw mango pieces. Add salt and ground mustard seeds. Mix thoroughly. Store in airtight jar for preservation over months.

name: Sabudana Burfi
instructions: Boil milk and cook sago in it until translucent and soft. Add sugar and ghee while stirring constantly. Cook until the mixture thickens and separates from the pan. Pour onto a greased tray. Garnish with almond flakes and crushed cashews. Cool completely and cut into pieces.

name: Gajar Burfi
instructions: Reduce full cream milk with grated carrots over high heat, stirring often until carrots are cooked and liquid reduces significantly. Fry flattened rice lightly in ghee. Add fried rice to carrot-milk mixture. Add sugar and cook on low heat, stirring continuously until mixture thickens and leaves the pan sides. Add cardamom powder, desiccated coconut, and almond powder. Mix well. Pour onto greased tray, garnish with crushed cashews and almonds. Cool and cut into pieces.

name: Bael Milkshake
instructions: Take woodapple pulp in a glass. Add milk and sugar. Mix well. Add cardamom powder. Blend if smooth consistency is desired. Serve immediately.

name: Dum Aloo in Palak Gravy
instructions: Blanch spinach and blend into smooth paste. Boil and mash cashews with little water into paste. Heat oil, add cumin seeds, bay leaf. Temper with cardamom, black pepper, cloves. Add ginger-green chilli paste. Add gram flour and cook. Add spinach paste and salt. Simmer. Add curd, cashew paste, kasuri methi. Add boiled potatoes and salt. Cook covered. Add fresh cream at the end. Garnish with coriander.

name: Besan Halwa
instructions: Heat ghee in heavy-bottomed pan. Add besan and roast on medium-low heat for 3-4 minutes until fragrant and light brown. Add semolina and continue roasting. Grate jaggery finely. Add fried nuts to the besan mixture. Add milk gradually to avoid lumps. Add grated jaggery and stir continuously on low heat until mixture thickens and becomes granular. Cook until it leaves the pan sides and turns golden. Serve warm.

name: suji gulab jamun
instructions: Make a dough with semolina, milk, ghee, cardamom powder and baking powder. Let it rest. Prepare sugar syrup with sugar, cardamom and lemon juice. Shape dough into small balls. Deep fry until golden brown. Soak fried balls in warm sugar syrup.

name: dahi papdi chaat
instructions: Make papdi dough with flour, salt, carom seeds, oil. Roll thin and cut into squares. Deep fry until crisp. Prepare potato masala with boiled potatoes, green chilli, ginger, cumin, salt and peas. Mix curd with sugar and black salt. Assemble by layering papdis with potato masala, curd, chutneys and toppings.

name: paneer makhana curry
instructions: Grind tomatoes, green chilli, ginger and cashews into a paste. Cut paneer into pieces. Roast makhana in ghee until golden. Heat ghee-oil and temper with bay leaf, cinnamon, pepper, cloves, black cardamom and cumin. Add ground paste and water. Add coriander, turmeric and red chilli powder. Fry until oil separates. Add kasuri methi and fry further. Add cream and fry till oil separates again. Add water and salt. Bring to boil. Add paneer, makhana and roasted cashews. Cook covered for 3-4 minutes.

name: kairi pudina sharbat
instructions: For fresh sharbat: Cut unripe mango into pieces. Blend mango, mint leaves, black salt, cumin powder, black pepper and fennel seeds. Strain the juice. Add sugar and mix well. Serve chilled with water. For concentrate: Process large batch of mangoes and mint. Prepare concentrate and store for long shelf life.

name: sweet makhana
instructions: For jaggery version: Roast makhana in ghee on low-medium flame. Heat ghee separately, add jaggery and let it melt. Add roasted makhana and coat well. Add ginger powder, black pepper, black salt and sesame seeds. Mix and spread on paper to cool. For sugar version: Roast makhana in ghee. Heat ghee, add sugar, let it caramelize. Coat makhana in sugar, add cardamom and pepper.

name: sattu kachori
instructions: Make dough with flour, salt, carom seeds, kalonji, ghee and oil. Let rest. For filling: temper cumin and fennel seeds, add green chilli-ginger paste, turmeric, asafoetida. Mix in sattu powder, coriander, red chilli, garam masala and salt. Stuff dough portions with filling. Roll gently and deep fry until golden and puffed. Serve hot with chutneys.

name: khopra patties
instructions: Boil and mash potatoes. Mix with rice flour, corn flour and salt to form binding dough. For filling: combine dry coconut, almonds, cashews, raisins, ginger-chilli paste, poppy seeds, spices and chutney. Take potato dough, stuff with filling and shape into patties. Deep fry until golden brown until outside becomes crispy.

name: til gur gajak
instructions: Roast sesame seeds until fragrant. Heat jaggery in ghee until it melts and turns golden. Roast gram flour separately until brown. Add roasted sesame seeds to melted jaggery. Add roasted gram flour and almond flakes. Mix quickly on high heat. Spread on greased paper and cut into pieces once cooled.

name: Besan Pinni
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan. Add gond and fry until golden. Add fox nuts, melon seeds, and fry. Add ground cardamom and nutmeg powder. In another pan, dry roast besan on low heat until fragrant. Add roasted besan to ghee mixture. Add almond flakes, cashew, raisins. Melt jaggery separately and add to the mixture. Stir continuously for 2-3 minutes. Remove from heat and shape into balls or store in a container.

name: Fried Masala Dhokla
instructions: Mix besan with sugar, salt, turmeric, 1 tbsp oil, lemon juice to make a batter. Add baking soda and mix well. Pour batter into greased dhokla mould and steam for 10-12 minutes. For tadka: heat 1 tsp oil, add mustard seeds, add sugar and stir. Pour over cooked dhokla. For masala: heat 1 tbsp oil, add mustard seeds, curry leaves, sauté ginger and chilli. Add tomatoes, salt, red chilli powder. Mix in tomato sauce and grated coconut. Simmer for 2-3 minutes. Deep fry cooled dhokla pieces until golden. Pour masala on top.

name: Air Fryer Karela Chips
instructions: Wash and cut bitter gourd in half, scoop out seeds. Slice into 2-inch batons. Sprinkle 1 tsp salt and set aside 10 minutes. Squeeze excess water and pat dry. Add remaining salt, turmeric, red chilli, garam masala, black pepper, coriander, and aamchur powder. Mix in rice flour and oil. Preheat air fryer to 175°C for 2-3 minutes. Spread karela in single layer and air fry for 25-30 minutes, shaking after 7 minutes. Cool and serve.

name: Andhra-Style Mashed Brinjal
instructions: Wash and dry brinjals whole. Roast brinjals over an open flame or on a griddle until skin is charred and flesh is soft, turning occasionally for 8-10 minutes. Cool slightly, peel and mash the soft flesh. Heat oil in a pan, add mustard seeds and let them crackle. Add cumin seeds and curry leaves. Add mashed brinjal, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, and salt. Mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Taushe Bhakri
instructions: Peel and grate cucumber into a bowl. Add coconut, ginger, green chillies, cumin seeds, salt, and sugar. Mix well and crush lightly. Add semolina and mix to form soft dough. Cover with damp muslin cloth and rest for 10-15 minutes. Sprinkle water on a tawa and wet your palms. Take a portion of dough and spread evenly on the tawa. Place on medium heat, drizzle ghee around, and cook for 4-5 minutes. Flip and cook for another 4-5 minutes. Serve hot with coconut chutney.

name: creamy farfalle broccoli pasta
instructions: 1. Heat water in a deep pan with a sieve and blanch broccoli florets. 2. Heat extra virgin olive oil in a non-stick wok, add onion and garlic, and sauté. 3. Remove broccoli and place in cold water. 4. Cook pasta in the sieve until al dente. 5. Blend half the broccoli florets with boiled potato and 1 cup milk. 6. Drain farfalle and add to the sautéed onion-garlic mixture with salt and pepper powder, toss well. 7. Add the broccoli-potato mixture and mix well, grate parmesan cheese and combine. 8. Transfer to a serving bowl, garnish with remaining broccoli florets, sprinkle red chilli powder on top and serve hot.

name: arbi ke patton ki sabji
ingredients: 3.5 tbsp split bengal gram chana dal soaked
instructions: 1. Place colocassia leaves, chana dal, salt, 2 tbsp tamarind pulp and 2-3 cups water in a deep non-stick pan. Cover and cook for 3-4 minutes. 2. Add raw peanuts, cover and cook until soft. 3. Heat oil in another pan, add asafoetida, fenugreek seeds, mustard seeds, curry leaves, garlic, green chillies and turmeric powder, sauté until fragrant. 4. Add gram flour and mix well, sauté for 2-3 minutes. 5. Add the colocassia-dal mixture with water, mix and add another 0.5 cup water along with remaining tamarind pulp. Mix well. 6. Add jaggery, mix again and cook on medium heat for 5-10 minutes. 7. Add coconut, mix well, adjust salt and cook for another 5 minutes. 8. Serve hot.

name: tandoori aloo
instructions: 1. Dock the potatoes with a toothpick. 2. Boil water in a pan, add salt and potatoes, cook till half done. Drain and pat dry. 3. Mix yogurt with turmeric, kasuri methi, amchur, besan, garam masala, red chilli powder, salt, Kashmiri red chilli paste, ginger-garlic paste and mustard oil. 4. Add potatoes to marinade and mix well. Marinate for 30-40 minutes. 5. Preheat air fryer at 190°C for 2 minutes. Line basket with foil and arrange potatoes in single layer. 6. Air fry for 10 minutes. Spray oil and air fry for 5 more minutes. 7. Serve hot with green chutney.

name: pumpkin mousse
instructions: 1. Combine pumpkin puree with sugar. 2. Whip cream until soft peaks form. 3. Fold whipped cream into pumpkin mixture gently. 4. Add vanilla extract and cinnamon powder. 5. Divide into serving glasses and chill. 6. Serve cold.

name: green moong paratha
instructions: 1. Mash boiled green gram in a bowl. 2. Add turmeric, red chilli powder, salt, green chillies, ginger, garam masala, amchur and coriander; mix well. 3. Divide dough into balls, make a dent, stuff filling and seal edges. 4. Dust flour and roll into thick disk. 5. Heat tawa, place paratha and cook 2-3 minutes on medium heat. 6. Flip, drizzle ghee and cook 2-3 minutes more. 7. Flip again, drizzle ghee and cook 1 minute. 8. Serve hot with pickle and yogurt.

name: kesar coconut laddu
instructions: 1. Heat a shallow nonstick pan and add saffron, cook on low heat for 30 seconds. 2. Transfer to mortar and crush with pestle. 3. Heat ghee in nonstick kadai, add coconut and crushed saffron, sauté for 4-5 minutes. 4. Add sugar and cook till sugar dissolves. 5. Add milk and green cardamom powder, cook till mixture thickens. 6. Transfer to plate and cool completely. 7. Divide into portions and roll into balls. 8. Roll balls in desiccated coconut and serve.

name: chane ka khatta
ingredients: black chana 1 cup soaked and cooked
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a pan. 2. Add cumin seeds and let them crackle. 3. Add onions and sauté till golden. 4. Add ginger, garlic and green chillies, cook for 1-2 minutes. 5. Add tomatoes and cook till soft. 6. Add turmeric, red chilli and coriander powders, mix well. 7. Add cooked black chana and stir. 8. Add water to desired consistency. 9. Simmer for 10-15 minutes. 10. Add lemon juice and salt. 11. Serve hot.

name: Chicken Makhani Quesadilla
instructions: 1. Squeeze lemon juice over chicken strips, sprinkle salt and red chilli powder, mix well and let sit 5 minutes. 2. Heat oil from bhuna masala in a non-stick pan on high heat, add marinated chicken and sauté until fully cooked. Transfer to a bowl. 3. Heat the tortillas on a non-stick tawa for 30 seconds each and set aside. 4. In the same pan, add chopped onion, tomato and capsicum, stir in bhuna masala and cook for 2 minutes. 5. Place first tortilla on tawa, spread cooked chicken pieces over it, sprinkle half the processed cheese and mozzarella over the chicken. 6. Place second tortilla over the cheese, press lightly with spatula. Spread the vegetable-masala mixture over this second tortilla. 7. Sprinkle remaining processed cheese and mozzarella on top, place third tortilla and press lightly. 8. Drizzle oil around and over the top tortilla, cook on medium heat until the underside turns crisp and golden. 9. Carefully flip the entire layered quesadilla and cook the other side until crisp. 10. Remove onto a plate, cut into 4-6 triangular pieces, arrange on serving plate, garnish with green and red capsicum strips and serve immediately.

name: Lotus Stem and Potato Kofta
instructions: 1. Boil lotus stem pieces until just tender (about 5 minutes), drain well. 2. Boil potatoes separately, peel and mash while still warm. 3. Finely chop onion and green chillies, mince ginger-garlic. 4. Mix boiled lotus stem with mashed potato in a bowl. 5. Add chopped onion, green chillies, ginger-garlic paste and fresh coriander to the lotus-potato mixture. 6. Sprinkle cumin powder, black pepper, red chilli powder and salt, mix gently. 7. Add gram flour and breadcrumbs to bind the mixture. 8. Shape mixture into oval or cylindrical koftas, about 2 inches long. 9. Heat oil in a shallow pan or tawa on medium-high heat. 10. Shallow fry koftas in batches until golden brown on all sides, turning gently. 11. Drain on paper towels and serve hot with green chutney or yogurt.

name: Afghani Mutton Keema
instructions: 1. Heat oil in a kadai. Add green cardamoms, cloves, black peppercorns, bay leaf, and cinnamon stick and sauté till fragrant. 2. Add sliced onions, green chillies, and ginger and mix well. 3. Add cashew nuts and salt and sauté for 2-3 minutes. 4. Add 2 cups water and mix well. Once the mixture comes to a boil, cover and cook for 15-20 minutes. 5. Discard bay leaf and cinnamon and allow the mixture to cool. 6. Transfer to a grinder jar, add 0.5 cup water and grind to fine paste. 7. Heat ghee in a nonstick kadai. Add cumin seeds and once they start to change color, add dried red chillies and sauté for a few seconds. 8. Add chopped garlic and sauté till golden brown. 9. Add chopped onions and sauté till translucent. 10. Add mutton mince and salt and sauté for 2-3 minutes. 11. Add yogurt and mix well. Cover and cook on medium heat for 15-20 minutes. 12. Add ground paste and mix well. Add 1 cup water and mix well. Cover and cook for 10-15 minutes more. 13. Add garam masala powder, kasuri methi powder, fresh cream and butter and mix well. 14. Transfer to a serving bowl and garnish with fried red chillies and coriander sprigs. 15. Drizzle fresh cream and serve hot.

name: Spicy Chilli Oil Eggs
instructions: Boil 4 eggs in water for 12 minutes. Cool eggs in chilled water, then peel and halve them. Finely chop garlic cloves, ginger, bird's eye chillies and spring onions. Arrange halved eggs on a serving plate. Heat oil in a pan. Pour hot oil over the chopped ingredients. Drizzle the chilli oil mixture over the boiled eggs and serve immediately.

name: hot and sour vegetable soup
instructions: Boil water with vegetable stock. Add mixed vegetables (corn, mushrooms, bell peppers, carrots). Simmer until vegetables are cooked. Add soy sauce, vinegar, and chili sauce for hot and sour flavor. Mix cornflour with water to create slurry, add to thicken soup. Season with salt and pepper. Garnish with spring onions and serve hot.

name: prawn tikki with hara pyaaz
instructions: Grind mint, coriander, garlic, ginger, and green chillies to a fine paste. Heat oil in a pan, sauté chopped spring onion bulbs until translucent. Add ground paste and dry spices (turmeric, coriander, cumin, garam masala). Sauté for 2 minutes. Add chopped prawns and cook for 2-3 minutes on high heat. Add spring onion greens and mix. Cool completely. Mash boiled potatoes in a bowl. Combine cooled prawn mixture with mashed potatoes, squeeze lemon juice, add chaat masala and breadcrumbs. Mix well. Divide into equal portions, roll into balls, flatten into tikki shapes, and dust with cornflour. Heat oil in a shallow pan and shallow fry tikkis on high heat until golden brown on both sides. Drain on absorbent paper. Serve hot with red chilli-garlic chutney and garnish with microgreens.

name: Delhi Street Style Seekh Kebab
ingredients: 0.5 tsp roasted and crushed cumin seeds
instructions: Grate onion, squeeze excess water and set aside. Take chicken mince in food processor, add ginger-garlic paste, green chillies, roasted and crushed cumin seeds, crushed black peppercorns, garam masala powder, green cardamom powder, coriander, mint, fried onions, salt and grated onion, process to fine mixture. Add butter, egg white and fresh breadcrumbs and continue to grind. Transfer ground mixture to piping bag. Grease nonstick grill pan with butter and pipe long cylinders of chicken mixture directly into pan. Cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes, flip, drizzle melted butter and cook for 3-4 minutes. Transfer to serving plate, sprinkle chaat masala and serve hot with salad and green chutney.

name: Kathal ki Sabzi
instructions: Heat oil and ghee. Add cumin seeds, cardamom, cinnamon, cloves. Add onions and fry until golden. Add chopped jackfruit and cook. Mix in curd, tomato, and all spices. Pressure cook for 2-3 whistles until jackfruit is tender. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Pepper Rasam
instructions: Soak rice and grind with black pepper and cumin seeds. Add garlic and grind to paste. Boil water and add tamarind juice. Mix in the ground paste and tomato pieces. Add turmeric powder. Temper with oil, mustard seeds, red chilli, and curry leaves. Simmer and add salt to taste. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Foxtail Millet Khichdi
instructions: Rinse millet and dal together. Heat ghee, add cardamom, bay leaf, cumin seeds, and asafoetida. Add onions and ginger-garlic paste. Add diced vegetables and sauté. Add millet and dal, mix well. Add water and all spices. Pressure cook for 2-3 whistles until completely cooked. Serve hot.

name: Chana Dal Tadka with Jeera Matar Rice
instructions: For dal: Heat ghee, add cardamom and cinnamon. Fry onions until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and tomato. Add soaked dal and cook in pressure cooker for 2-3 whistles until soft. Temper with remaining ghee and spices. For rice: In separate cooker, heat ghee with cumin seeds, cardamom, and bay leaves. Add rice and water. Cook in pressure cooker for 2 whistles. Mix peas. Serve dal and rice together.

name: Achari Pyaaz Paratha
instructions: Chop onions finely and mix with green chilli, coriander leaves, and pickle masala. Knead wheat flour with salt and minimal water. Roll out dough round, spread onion filling in center, fold and seal edges. Flatten into disc. Cook on griddle with oil until golden brown spots appear on both sides. Serve hot with yogurt or pickle.

name: Alfredo Pasta
instructions: Cook pasta until al dente and drain. In a pan, melt butter and add minced garlic. Cook for 1 minute. Sprinkle flour and stir well. Gradually add milk while stirring to avoid lumps. Add salt, pepper powder, herbs, and garlic powder. Simmer until sauce thickens. Add fresh cream and cheese. Toss cooked pasta in the white sauce until well coated. Serve hot.

name: Mini Samosa
instructions: Prepare dough by mixing maida, carrom seeds, salt and oil. For filling: boil and mash potatoes, mix with ginger-garlic paste, green chilli, and spices (cumin seeds, red chilli powder, garam masala, chaat masala). Stuff the dough, shape into triangles and deep fry until golden brown.

name: Green Moong Waffle
instructions: Soak green moong overnight. Grind soaked moong with garlic, ginger and water into smooth paste. Mix in grated carrot, capsicum, onion, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander, cumin powder, salt and baking soda. Heat waffle maker and grease with ghee/oil. Pour batter and spread evenly. Add grated beetroot on top and cook for 2 minutes. Optional: sprinkle grated cheese. Serve with chutney and ketchup.

name: Maharashtrian Chicken Masala
instructions: Heat oil and add cumin seeds. Add ginger-garlic paste and roast. Add turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala and red chilli powder. Add chopped chicken pieces and mix well. Add kanda lahsun masala and dry coconut. Simmer on medium heat until chicken is fully cooked and oil separates from the gravy. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Spicy Chana Chaat
instructions: Soak bengal gram for 2-3 hours. Boil soaked bengal gram with salt and water for 2 whistles until soft. Heat oil and add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder and garam masala. Add boiled potatoes, diced cucumber, tomatoes and green chilli. Mix in the cooked bengal gram. Season with chaat masala, lemon juice and salt. Top with coriander leaves and green chutney. Serve immediately.

name: Egg Bread Roll
instructions: Boil 5 eggs and separate yolks. Mix 2 raw eggs with boiled egg yolks, finely chopped onion, capsicum, green chilli, coriander leaves, mixed herbs, chilli flakes and salt. Spread the mixture on bread slices. Remove crusts and roll tightly. Coat each roll with bread crumbs. Heat oil in a pan and deep fry the rolls until golden brown. Drain on paper towels. Serve hot with ketchup or sauce.

name: Green moong idli coins
instructions: Soak green moong and grind with garlic, ginger, and water to make a smooth batter. Add grated carrot, capsicum, onion, turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander, cumin powder, baking soda, and salt. Mix well. Pour into mini idli molds and steam until cooked (8-10 mins). For tempering: heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves, pour over idlis. Serve hot with chaat masala and coriander leaves garnish.

name: Eggless vegetable omelette
instructions: Mix gram flour, all-purpose flour, baking powder, and salt. Add water to make a smooth batter consistency. Chop spinach, carrot, mushroom, broccoli, and french beans finely. Add vegetables and grated cheese to the batter and mix. Heat oil in non-stick pan on medium heat. Pour batter and cook until bottom is golden. Flip and cook the other side until cooked through. Serve hot.

name: Bharleli vangi
instructions: Grind roasted peanuts with green chilli, kanda lahsun masala, salt, and cumin seeds to make filling. Make slits in brinjals and stuff with peanut masala. Heat oil and add mustard seeds and cumin seeds. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook. Add onions and fry for 4 mins. Add tomatoes with turmeric, red chilli, coriander, cumin, and garam masala powder. Mix well. Add stuffed brinjals gently. Cook for 5 mins. Add remaining filling and salt. Cook 3 mins more. Add water and simmer for 15 mins, stirring occasionally. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Louki ka nasta
instructions: Peel and grate bottle gourd. Mix with gram flour, onion, coriander leaves, ginger-garlic-chilli paste, turmeric, red chilli powder, and salt. Mix well to combine. Shape into patties or fry as a mixture. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan and cook the mixture by flattening it or fry spoonfuls until golden and cooked through. For optional tadka: heat oil, add mustard and sesame seeds, then pour over the cooked louki nasta. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Chicken masala pav
instructions: Cut boneless chicken into small pieces. Heat oil and add green chilli. Add onion and fry until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and chicken pieces. Cook on high heat. Add capsicum, turmeric powder, red chilli, and chicken masala. Mix well and cook covered for 15 mins until chicken is cooked through. Add mixed herbs and chilli flakes. Cook for 2 more mins. Slice pav bread and butter it. Place cheese slice on bread. Top with chicken masala mixture and garnish with coriander leaves. Serve hot.

name: Masala Fish Curry
instructions: Dry roast brown mustard seeds, yellow mustard seeds, coriander seeds, cumin seeds and black pepper for a few seconds. Grind them coarsely. Clean and cut the fish into pieces. Prepare a paste with garlic cloves and green chilli. Mix ginger garlic paste, turmeric powder, red chilli powder and salt into the fish pieces and let marinate. Heat oil in a pan and lightly fry the marinated fish. Remove and set aside. In the same pan, add the ground mustard-spice paste and water. Let it simmer until aromatic. Add the fried fish back to the pan and cook until the fish is fully done and curry thickens. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Jowar Uttapam
ingredients: cooking oil 2 tbsp for tadka plus for cooking
instructions: Mix jowar flour with curd, salt and water to make a batter. Let it rest for 15 minutes. For chutney, grind fresh coconut, green chilli, ginger, roasted chana dal with salt and water until smooth. Heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan and add urad dal, mustard seeds and dry red chilli for tadka. Add this to the batter along with chopped vegetables and coriander leaves. Heat an oil-greased skillet or tawa. Pour the batter and spread to make a pancake about 1/4 inch thick. Once the bottom turns golden, add more chopped vegetables and chutney on top. Flip carefully or fold. Cook until golden on both sides. Serve hot with additional chutney on the side.

name: Chicken Tikka Sandwich
instructions: Mix chicken pieces with curd, ginger garlic paste, dry fenugreek leaves, turmeric powder, red chilli powder, tandoori masala, mustard oil and salt. Marinate for at least 30 minutes. Heat cooking oil in a pan and cook the marinated chicken over medium-high heat until completely cooked and browned. Cool the cooked chicken. Cut the sub bread lengthwise and lightly toast if desired. Spread mustard sauce on one side and burger sauce on the other side. Layer lettuce leaves as the base. Arrange the cooked chicken tikka pieces on the lettuce. Add sliced cucumber, tomato and onion. Drizzle tomato ketchup and mayonnaise. Sprinkle black pepper powder and salt to taste. Close the sandwich and cut diagonally. Serve immediately.

name: Kathal Masala
instructions: Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds, cardamom, bay leaves, and cinnamon stick. Add sliced onions and sauté until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 2 minutes. Add tomatoes and cook until soft. Add turmeric, kashmiri red chilli powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, and garam masala. Add prepared kathal (jackfruit) pieces and mix well. Cook on medium heat for 25-30 minutes until kathal is tender and flavors are absorbed. Sprinkle roasted kasuri methi on top. Serve hot.

name: Egg Chaat
instructions: Heat oil and butter in a pan. Cut boiled eggs into pieces and fry for 30 seconds. Add red chilli powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, and black pepper. Fry for 2 minutes. Add oil and stir-fry for another 2 minutes. Add onion, tomato, green chilli, and capsicum. Cook for 1 minute until vegetables are slightly softened but retain crunch. Add green chutney and mix well. Stir in chopped coriander leaves. Top with cheese slices. Serve hot.

name: Egg Cheese Sandwich
instructions: Prepare green chutney: Blend coriander leaves, mint leaves, green chilli, garlic cloves, lemon juice, salt, and water to make a smooth paste. Prepare filling: Boil and chop eggs. Mix with finely chopped onion, capsicum, green chilli, coriander leaves, mixed herbs, chilli flakes, and salt. Butter 2 bread slices and spread green chutney on them. Spread egg mixture on the chutney. Layer with another buttered bread slice. Heat a pan or tawa and cook the sandwich until golden brown on both sides. Slice diagonally and serve hot.

name: Chicken Parcel
instructions: Prepare dough: Mix maida, carom seeds, and salt. Add water gradually and knead into soft dough. Let it rest for 15 minutes. Prepare filling: Heat oil in a pan, add green chilli and onions, cook until onions are soft. Add ginger garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Add diced boneless chicken and cook for 5 minutes. Add turmeric powder, red chilli, and chicken masala. Cook for 2 more minutes. Add capsicum, mixed herbs, chilli flakes, coriander leaves, and cheese slices. Mix well and let cool. Assembly: Roll out dough into small rectangles. Place filling in center, fold edges and seal. Deep fry in oil until golden brown and crispy. Drain on paper towels. Serve hot with chutney.

name: Healthy Oats Cutlet
instructions: Soak rolled oats. Mix with curd, paneer, and finely chopped vegetables (carrot, french beans, broccoli, onion, spinach). Add minced garlic, ginger, and green chilli. Mix in turmeric powder, red chilli powder, cumin powder, garam masala, and salt. Form into cutlet shapes. Shallow fry in oil until golden brown on both sides.

name: Bhandare Wale Aloo Ki Sabzi
instructions: Boil potatoes until tender and cut into desired sizes. Heat oil in a pan and add cumin seeds and fennel seeds until they crackle. Add finely chopped ginger and green chilli. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft. Add turmeric powder, red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala, and asafoetida. Add boiled potatoes and dry mango powder. Mix well and cook for 5-10 minutes. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.

name: Veg Thai Green Curry
instructions: Grind coriander seeds, cumin seeds, black pepper, lemongrass, onion, bird eye chilli, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, garlic, and coriander stems into a fine paste. Heat oil in a pan and fry the paste until fragrant. Add coconut milk and water. Bring to a boil. Add diced vegetables (capsicum, zucchini, mushroom, broccoli, carrot). Simmer for 8-10 minutes until tender. Season with salt and garnish with fresh thai basil.

name: Sabudana Drink
ingredients: rooh afza or rose syrup 3-4 tbsp
instructions: Chill milk in refrigerator. Pour milk in glasses. Add condensed milk to taste. Add rooh afza or rose syrup and stir well. Dissolve jelly pieces in the drink. Add chopped dry nuts as garnish. Mix well and serve chilled.

name: soyabean masala
instructions: Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and bay leaf. When they crackle, add onions and cook until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Add tomato paste and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add soya chunks and mix well. Add all spices (turmeric, red chilli, coriander, cumin, garam masala) and cook for 2 minutes. Add curd and cook for 3-4 minutes on high heat until the masala thickens. Finish with fresh coriander leaves.

name: paneer tikka pizza
instructions: For dough: Mix flour, sugar, salt, yeast in a bowl. Add lukewarm water and oil, knead into soft dough. Let rise for 1-2 hours. For paneer tikka: Cut paneer into cubes, marinate in curd, turmeric, chilli powder, coriander, cumin, garam masala, ginger-garlic paste, fenugreek leaves, mustard oil and salt for 15-20 minutes. Roll dough into pizza base, spread pizza sauce, layer marinated paneer tikka pieces, add cheese, onion, peppers, jalapeno and olives. Bake at 200°C until golden.

name: stuffed okra masala
instructions: Clean bhindi and slit lengthwise without cutting completely. Mix coriander powder, cumin powder, red chilli powder, dry mango powder with a little salt to create stuffing. Carefully stuff each bhindi piece with the spice mixture. Heat oil in pan, add cumin seeds. When they crackle, add onions and cook until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and tomato paste, cook for 2-3 minutes. Add stuffed bhindi carefully and cook on high heat for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add curd at the end and cook for 2-3 minutes. Finish with garam masala and fresh coriander.

name: masala pav
instructions: Heat oil, add cumin seeds. When they crackle, add finely chopped onions and cook until golden. Add ginger-green chilli paste and cook for 1 minute. Add tomato pieces and cook until they break down. Add all spices (turmeric, red chilli, coriander, garam masala) and cook for 3-4 minutes. The masala should be thick and chunky. Cut pav in half, spread butter on both sides, and dip in the hot masala. Serve immediately.

name: no-bake cheesecake
instructions: Crush digestive biscuits into fine crumbs using hands or grinder. Mix crumbs with melted butter. Press firmly into silicone mold using flat base of cup. Refrigerate for 30 minutes. Mix hung curd, condensed milk, cream, and vanilla extract in a bowl. In separate bowl, mix warm milk with cornstarch until smooth, add to curd mixture. Mix well to avoid lumps. Pour filling onto biscuit base. Place silicone mold on microwave-safe plate. Microwave at 800-900 watts for 10 minutes. Cool completely, then refrigerate for 2+ hours. For sauce, heat strawberries with sugar and water for 8 minutes, cool. Remove cheesecake from mold, slice, pipe whipped cream on top, drizzle strawberry sauce, garnish with mint.

name: ghee roast paneer
instructions: Cut paneer into cubes. Mix hung curd, salt, turmeric, bhuna besan, chaat masala, lemon juice and make a marinade. Marinate paneer cubes and onion/capsicum chunks for 15-20 minutes. Prepare ghee roast masala: Toast kashmiri chilli, peppercorn, coriander seeds, cumin, fennel seeds in a dry pan until aromatic, cool and grind. Make paste with garlic, ginger and water. Heat ghee in a pan, add masala paste, cook for 2-3 minutes. Add curry leaves. Carefully add marinated paneer cubes and vegetables. Cook on medium-high heat for 5-7 minutes until paneer is coated with masala. Thread onto wooden skewers if desired. Serve hot.

name: Thai Garlic Sesame Peanut Salad
instructions: Make the dressing by combining chopped garlic, ginger, lemon juice, soya sauce, chilli sauce, peanut butter, sesame oil, powdered jaggery and salt. Shred cabbage and papaya, julienne carrots, slice mango and capsicum, chop spring onions. Toss all vegetables and fruits together in a bowl. Pour the dressing over the salad and mix well. Top with fresh mint leaves, coriander and toasted sesame seeds. Serve immediately.

name: Cheesy Egg Sandwich
instructions: Crack eggs into a bowl, add salt and pepper, whisk for a minute. Heat a non-stick pan or tawa, add oil and pour in whisked eggs. Turn off heat and swirl the pan to spread eggs evenly. Place 2 bread slices on top, smear butter over them and flip the egg with bread. Cook over heat until bread turns crisp. Turn off heat, smear cheese spread on egg side and place cheese slice. Add chopped onion, tomato, capsicum, coriander and Italian herbs. Place another cheese slice on top. Turn on low heat, lift one bread side and place it over the other, cook until crisp and serve.

name: Crispy Potato Wedges
instructions: Cut large potatoes into wedge shapes with skin intact. Boil or partially cook the wedges until soft. Drain and cool. Make the batter by combining all purpose flour, rice flour, cornstarch, besan, salt, chilli flakes, oregano, coriander, pepper powder, garlic chopped, butter and water to get a thick coating consistency. Heat oil for deep frying. Coat each potato wedge with the batter and deep fry until golden brown and crispy on all sides. Drain on paper towels. Serve hot with chaat masala sprinkled on top and sweet chilli sauce or sour cream on the side.

name: Crispy Masala Macaroni
instructions: Boil macaroni in salted water until cooked through. Drain completely and spread on a towel to dry. Heat oil for deep frying and fry the cooked macaroni in batches until golden and crispy. Mix all masala ingredients in a bowl. Toss the hot fried macaroni with the masala mixture immediately. Spread on a tray to cool and crisp up further. Store in an airtight container. Serve as a crunchy snack.

name: Til Revdi
instructions: Roast sesame seeds in a dry pan until fragrant and slightly darker. Heat jaggery with a dash of water over medium heat, stirring until it melts and reaches soft-ball stage. Add roasted sesame seeds, crushed black cardamom seeds, and cardamom powder. Mix well. Add ghee and stir. When the mixture begins to cool slightly, add baking soda which will make it fluffy. Pour onto a greased surface quickly and cut into pieces with oiled hands or knife before it hardens completely. Cool completely on a wire rack.

name: Cauliflower Soup
instructions: Heat butter in a pot, add kalonji seeds and bay leaf. Add chopped garlic and onion, sauté until golden brown. Add cauliflower florets and cook for 2-3 minutes. Sprinkle all-purpose flour and cook for another minute. Pour in water and milk, bring to boil. Simmer for 10-12 minutes until cauliflower is very soft. Blend the soup until smooth or leave partially chunky as desired. Add grated cheese and season with salt and pepper powder. Serve hot.

name: Tomato Basil Soup
instructions: Heat butter and olive oil, sauté chopped garlic and onion until soft. Add kashmiri chilli powder and diced tomatoes. Cook until tomatoes become soft and pulpy. Add water and fresh basil leaves, bring to boil. Simmer for 15 minutes. Blend the soup until smooth consistency. Season with salt, pepper powder and a pinch of sugar if needed. For garlic bread: lightly toast bread slices, spread butter mixed with chopped garlic, top with diced tomato, salt, pepper and fresh basil. Drizzle with olive oil and toast briefly. Serve soup hot with garlic bread.

name: Spicy Paneer Burger
instructions: Cut paneer into thin rectangular slices and marinate with ginger-garlic paste, garam masala, salt and pepper for 15 minutes. Heat butter in a pan and shallow fry or grill the paneer slices until light golden on both sides. Prepare smokey chilli mayonnaise by blending mayonnaise with green chilli, ginger, garlic, chilli powder and a pinch of salt. Lightly toast burger buns. Layer the bottom bun with chilli mayonnaise, lettuce, tomato slices, onion slices and grilled paneer. Top with the second bun half. Serve immediately with fries or salad.

name: roasted jacket potato
instructions: Arrange potatoes in salt bed and roast until crispy outside and fluffy inside. Prepare cheese sauce by melting butter and mixing with grated cheese and milk. Cut open roasted potatoes and top with cheese spread, butter cubes, oregano, chilli flakes, corn nibbles and spring onion. Serve hot.

name: punjabi dodi
instructions: Soak khus khus, cashews, almonds and melon seeds in water for 30 minutes. Drain water and blend soaked ingredients into smooth paste. Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan. Add the paste and cook on low heat stirring continuously for 5-7 minutes. Add milk and bring to boil. Simmer for 10 minutes. Add cardamom powder and sugar to taste. Serve hot or warm.

name: dahi wale aloo
instructions: Heat oil and temper with black cardamom, heeng and green chilli. Add cumin and bay leaf. Fry onions until golden. Add ginger garlic paste and cook 2 minutes. Add potatoes and fry 5 minutes. Add turmeric, chilli and coriander powders. Pour 1 cup water and cook until potatoes are tender. Mix gram flour in yogurt and add to curry. Stir in kasoori methi and remaining water. Simmer 5 minutes. Garnish with coriander and ghee drizzle. Serve hot.

name: crispy corn salt and pepper
instructions: Coat boiled corn niblets with corn starch and water paste. Deep fry until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towel. Heat 3 tbsp oil in wok, add garlic and ginger. Stir-fry onion, capsicum and green chilli. Add fried corn and toss well. Add soya sauce, chilli sauce, salt and black pepper. Mix thoroughly. Finish with spring onion and lemon juice. Serve hot and crispy.

name: ghee rice pulao
instructions: Soak rice for 15 minutes and drain. Heat ghee in heavy-bottomed pot, add cumin seeds, bay leaf, cardamom, cinnamon and cloves. Let seeds crackle. Add nuts and fry until golden. Add drained rice and fry for 2-3 minutes stirring gently. Pour hot water and add salt. Cover and cook on high heat until steam forms. Lower heat and cook 12-15 minutes until rice is cooked and liquid is absorbed. Turn off heat and let rest 5 minutes covered. Fluff with fork and serve hot.

name: Khichdi
instructions: Pressure cook rice and three types of dal with water and turmeric. Separately heat ghee and temper with mustard seeds, cumin, dry chillies, and heeng. Add garlic, ginger, green chillies, and onions, sauté. Add chopped tomatoes and cook till soft. Mix in cooked khichdi with this tempering. Season with salt. Finish with kasoori methi and lemon juice. For second tempering, heat ghee with chilli powder and add fried onions. Garnish with coriander and serve hot.

name: Vegetable Pulao
instructions: Heat oil in a pot. Add black cardamom and peppercorns, let them crackle. Add cumin seeds. Add slit green chillies and sliced onions, sauté until golden. Add diced potatoes, carrots, beans, and peas. Stir well and add washed basmati rice. Toast the rice for 2-3 minutes. Add 4 cups water and salt to taste. Cover and cook on high heat until steam forms, then reduce heat and cook until rice is done and water is absorbed. Fluff with a fork and serve.

name: Eggless Plum Cake
instructions: Soak crushed nuts and dried fruits (cashews, almonds, pistachios, raisins, candied fruits, blueberries, cranberries) in orange juice for at least 2 hours. In a bowl, whisk together flour, cocoa powder, clove powder, cinnamon, nutmeg, and ginger powder. In another bowl, cream sugar and melted butter with vanilla extract. Add milk to the butter mixture. Combine the dry ingredients with the wet mixture. Fold in the soaked fruits and nuts with their liquid, and chocolate chips. Pour into a greased baking dish and bake at 350F (175C) for 45-50 minutes until a toothpick inserted comes out clean. Cool before serving.

name: Chana Dal Fry
ingredients: chilli powder 1/2 tsp for second tempering
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan. Add heeng and let it crackle, add dry red chillies and cumin seeds. Add chopped garlic and ginger, sauté for a minute. Add slit green chillies and chopped onions, sauté until golden. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft. Add boiled chana dal with water, turmeric, and chilli powder. Mix well and simmer for 5-7 minutes. Season with salt. Add coriander and lemon juice. For second tempering, heat ghee and mix in chilli powder, pour over the dal. Serve hot.

name: Eggless Chocolate Mousse
instructions: Chop dark chocolate finely and place in a heatproof bowl. Add butter and heat over a double boiler or hot water bath until melted and smooth. Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Whip the melted chocolate mixture with sugar and cocoa powder until light and fluffy. Add milk gradually while whipping to achieve a mousse consistency. Add vanilla extract. Divide into serving bowls or glasses. Chill for at least 30 minutes and serve cold.

name: Chicken Soup
instructions: Heat butter in a pot. Add bay leaf and peppercorns, let them infuse for a minute. Add chopped garlic and onions, sauté until soft. Sprinkle maida (flour) and stir to make a roux, cook for 1-2 minutes. Add chicken pieces (with bone) and thyme. Pour in 1.25 liters of water and bring to a boil. Season with salt. Lower heat and simmer for 30-35 minutes until chicken is cooked and tender. Remove chicken pieces and shred the meat, discard bones. Add shredded chicken back to the soup. Add chopped spring onions and optional cream. Simmer for 2-3 minutes. Serve hot.

name: Gur Ke Chawal
instructions: Wash basmati rice 3-4 times in clean water to remove excess starch. Soak rice in water for 30 minutes. In a heavy-bottomed pot, heat ghee over medium heat. Add bay leaf, cinnamon stick, cardamom pods, and cloves. Let the spices infuse for 30-40 seconds. Add the drained soaked rice and stir gently for 2-3 minutes. Pour in 2 1/2 cups water and add jaggery pieces. Stir well to combine. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and cook until rice is cooked and water is absorbed, about 15-20 minutes. Stir in melon seeds, chopped almonds, and cashewnuts. Mix in orange peel chunks. Fluff with a fork and serve hot as a dessert.

name: Extra Fluffy Omelet
instructions: Crack eggs and separate yolks and whites. Whisk the whites with an electric whisk until fluffy, then whisk yolks separately. Combine and add salt, whisk until mixture doesn't fall off when bowl is inverted. Heat pan with oil, pour beaten eggs and set heat to low. Do not stir or shake. After 30 seconds, gently lift omelet from one side and drop small butter pieces around it. Repeat around the edges. Carefully place a flat ladle under the omelet and flip it gently. Cook for 1 minute and serve immediately.

name: Til Ke Ladoo
instructions: Toast sesame seeds lightly. Heat ghee in a pan and add sesame seeds. Stir well. Melt jaggery separately to soft-ball stage syrup (around 240°F). Combine toasted sesame seeds with jaggery syrup and cardamom powder. Mix well and let cool slightly. Grease your hands with ghee and shape the mixture into round balls or ladoos. Store in an airtight container.

name: Veg Aloo Tikki Burger
instructions: For patty: Heat oil and add cumin, then ginger and green chilli. Sauté and add peas and beans, cook 3-4 minutes. Add salt, turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder and toss. Add mashed potatoes and mix, sprinkle chaat masala and garam masala. Cool and add coriander, bread crumbs, cheese and paneer. Shape into patties. Make batter with flour, salt and water. Coat patties in batter then bread crumbs. Shallow or deep fry until golden on both sides. Toast burger buns with butter. Assemble: mustard sauce on base, salad leaf, mayo, gherkin, patty, cheese slice, onion, tomato, mint mayo on top. Serve with fries.

name: Gur Ka Halwa
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan and add semolina, chickpea flour and whole wheat flour. Roast on medium heat for 3-4 minutes stirring constantly until golden. In another pan, make jaggery syrup with water and jaggery, heat until it reaches soft-ball stage. Add more ghee to the roasted flour mixture and continue roasting. Pour hot jaggery syrup into the flour mixture while stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Keep stirring until the mixture comes together. Add nuts and cardamom powder. Transfer to a serving plate and allow to cool.

name: Veg Paratha Quesadilla
instructions: Heat oil and add cumin, coriander seeds. Add ginger and green chilli, sauté. Add turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder and amachur. Add crushed potatoes and mix well with paneer cubes. Season with salt and black salt, add chopped coriander. For salad, mix hung curd with salt, black salt, roasted cumin, garlic and chopped vegetables. On a tortilla, spread a portion of potato-paneer filling and sprinkle cheese. Place another tortilla on top. Cook on a griddle until golden and cheese melts. Serve with fresh vegetable salad.

name: White Sauce Spaghetti
instructions: Cook spaghetti in salted boiling water until al dente, drain and set aside. In a pan, heat butter and olive oil, add chopped garlic and onions, cook until soft. Sprinkle flour and stir to make a roux, cook for 1 minute. Gradually add cream and milk while stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Simmer for 3-4 minutes until sauce thickens. Add grated cheese, salt, pepper and nutmeg, stir until cheese melts. Toss cooked spaghetti in the white sauce and serve hot.

name: Malabar Paratha
instructions: Mix flour, salt, and sugar. Add milk and oil gradually while kneading into soft dough. Let rest for 30 minutes covered. Divide into 6 portions and roll thin. Apply oil generously and roll into spiral. Coil the rolled dough and flatten again. Heat griddle and cook each paratha on high heat, pressing gently until golden brown and flaky on both sides. Serve hot.

name: Amritsari Pindi Chole
instructions: Boil chickpeas with tea leaves, baking soda, and amla slices until completely tender. Dry toast all spices separately by size (larger spices together, smaller together) until fragrant, being careful not to burn. Grind toasted spices into fine masala powder. Heat oil in heavy-bottomed pot, add heeng, then garlic and ginger, cook until brown. Add onion and green chilli, cook until golden. Add turmeric and kashmiri chilli powder, stir well. Add ground chole masala and a dash of water, cook for 1 minute. Add boiled and drained chickpeas, pureed tomatoes, salt, black salt, and tamarind water. Simmer for 10-15 minutes until flavors meld. Temper with ghee and kashmiri chilli powder. Garnish with boiled potato, paneer cubes, pickled chilli, and onion rings. Serve hot.

name: Kanda Poha
instructions: Rinse poha under water and drain completely. Heat oil in a wok or large pan. Add mustard seeds and let them crackle and pop. Add curry leaves and chopped green chilli. Add diced onion and sauté until translucent and golden. Add turmeric powder and salt, mix well. Add drained poha and toss to coat with oil. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes on medium-high heat, breaking up any lumps, until heated through and each grain is coated with oil. Finish with fresh lemon juice, roasted peanuts, and fresh coriander. Serve hot.

name: Paneer Kalimirch
instructions: Blend onion chunks with green chillies, bay leaf, and black cardamom into a smooth thick paste. Heat oil in heavy-bottomed pan, add garlic and ginger, cook until golden brown. Add onion-spice paste and cook on low-medium heat for 15-20 minutes, stirring frequently, until oil separates on top. Soak cashew nuts briefly and grind with curd into fine paste. Add cashew-curd mixture to the cooked onion paste and mix well. Add water gradually and simmer for 5 minutes. Season with salt. Heat butter separately, add ginger julienne and paneer cubes, cook gently for 2-3 minutes without browning. Carefully add cooked paneer to the gravy. Dust generously with black pepper powder and mix. For rogan, heat ghee with kashmiri chilli powder and drizzle over gravy. Serve hot.

name: Dahi Bhalla
instructions: Make urad dal batter by grinding soaked urad dal with water, cumin, ginger, green chilli and salt. Deep fry spoonfuls of batter until golden. Prepare sweet curd by mixing curd, sugar and salt. Make tamarind chutney by cooking tamarind with water, chilli powder, cumin powder and salt. Soak fried vadas in the tamarind chutney, then place on a plate and top with sweet curd. Garnish with mint chutney, pomegranate, chaat masala and serve.

name: Chocolate Pudding
instructions: Crush plain cookies and mix with melted butter to make crust base in serving glasses. Heat milk and cream with cinnamon stick. Mix cocoa powder, corn starch and sugar together. Add cocoa mixture to hot milk slowly, stirring continuously. Add chopped dark chocolate and let it melt. Add orange rind and vanilla extract. Cool the mixture and pour into cookie crust glasses. Chill until set. Garnish with almonds, mint leaves and grated chocolate before serving.

name: Meethi Boondi
instructions: Make besan batter by mixing besan with water and yellow colour until smooth consistency. Heat oil in kadai. Pour batter through a boondi ladle into hot oil, frying until crispy. Drain fried besan dana in strainer. Prepare one-string consistency sugar syrup with water and sugar. Add cardamom powder, yellow colour and black cardamom seeds to syrup. Pour hot besan dana into sugar syrup and give quick boil. Turn off heat and cover for 1 minute to allow soaking. Spread on tray to cool slightly. Sprinkle melon seeds on top and serve warm.

name: Chatpate Aloo Fry
instructions: Heat oil in a large pan. Add heeng and dry red chillies, let them splutter. Add cumin and mustard seeds. When seeds splutter, add onion and cook until translucent. Add garlic and ginger, fry for 1 minute. Add diced raw potatoes and cook on high heat for 3-4 minutes. Add black salt, salt, turmeric, chilli powder and coriander powder. Mix well and cook covered for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally until potatoes are cooked through. Add amachur, chaat masala and kasoori methi, mix well. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: coleslaw salad
instructions: Shred cabbage, carrots, and capsicum. Grate onions and chop spring onions. In a bowl, mix mayonnaise with milk and mustard powder to make dressing. Combine all vegetables. Add dressing and season with salt, pepper, and sugar. Squeeze lemon juice and mix well. Serve chilled.

name: badam milk
ingredients: saffron pinch dissolved in 2 tbsp water
instructions: Soak almonds in warm water for 30 minutes. Peel the skins and grind almonds with fresh water to a smooth paste. Strain through a fine sieve. Heat the almond milk, add saffron dissolved in water, sugar, turmeric, nutmeg, cardamom powder, rose water, and kewra water. Simmer gently. Add soaked chia seeds. Serve warm or chilled.

name: zarda pulao
instructions: Soak rice for 30 minutes. Heat ghee and temper with cinnamon, cardamom, black cardamom, and cloves. Add dry coconut and dry fruits, roast briefly. Add soaked rice and coat well. Add water and turmeric, bring to boil. Cover and simmer on low heat. When rice is cooked, heat milk with saffron separately. Pour saffron milk over rice, add rose water and kewra water. Cover and let it rest for 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork and serve.

name: roasted tomato chutney
instructions: Roast tomatoes, garlic, onion, and green chillies over flame or in oven until charred. Let cool and peel off skin. Grind or mash the roasted vegetables coarsely. Add roasted cumin seeds, regular salt, black salt, and chopped coriander. Mix well and add mustard oil. Squeeze fresh lemon juice. For smoking: heat ghee in a small spoon, add cinnamon stick until it splutters, pour the hot ghee and cinnamon over the chutney. Mix gently and serve.

name: paneer do pyaza
ingredients: onion finely chopped or puree 1.5 cup
instructions: Heat oil, add bay leaf, black cardamom, cardamom, cinnamon, and cumin. Add onion puree and cook until golden. Add ginger paste and garlic paste, cook for 1 minute. Add turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, and coriander powder, mix well. Add yogurt and stir continuously. Add tomato puree and cook until oil separates. Add water and simmer. Separately, heat butter and oil, sauté diced onions until crispy. Add turmeric and sliced green chilli, then add paneer cubes and toss gently. Add the gravy to paneer and onions, mix cashew nut paste. Simmer for 5 minutes. Garnish with coriander and garam masala. Serve hot.

name: lachha paratha
instructions: Mix all-purpose flour, semolina, sugar, and salt. Add water gradually to form a soft dough. Rest for 30 minutes. Make small balls and rest again for 15 minutes. Roll out thin, make pleats, fold into pinwheel shape. Cook on tawa (griddle) until golden, flipping occasionally. Serve hot with butter.

name: tawa kulcha
instructions: Mix maida, curd, baking powder, baking soda, salt, and melted butter. Knead with water to soft dough. Rest for 1 hour. Make small balls, rest 30 minutes. Roll out and place on nonstick pan. Top with nigella seeds, coriander, methi leaves. Add water to sides, cover, cook until golden. Flip and cook other side. Remove and apply butter.

name: cheese balls
instructions: Mix mashed potato with all vegetables, cheese, herbs, and seasonings. Check seasoning. Take ping pong ball-sized portion, place cheese cube inside, cover with mixture, and roll into sphere. Make flour-salt-water slurry. Dip balls in slurry, coat with bread crumbs evenly. Deep fry in moderate-hot oil until golden brown. Remove and serve hot.

name: mutton kali mirch
instructions: Make ginger-garlic-green chili-coriander stem paste with peppercorns and curd. Dry roast cloves, cardamom, peppercorns, stone flower, fenugreek, and mace; grind to powder. Marinate mutton with paste, curd, chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder, cumin powder for 5-10 minutes. Pressure cook marinated mutton with cinnamon and bay leaf for 6-7 whistles. In handi, heat ghee and oil, add dry red chili and bay leaf, saute. Add sliced onions, cook until golden. Add coriander powder and tomato puree, cook 5-6 minutes. Add cooked mutton, salt, and prepared masala. Simmer until gravy thickens. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: lahori chole
instructions: Pressure cook soaked chickpeas with ghee, ginger, green chili, garlic, salt, water, black cardamom, and black peppercorns for 3-4 whistles. Divide cooked chickpeas into two bowls. Dry roast peppercorns, black cardamom, fenugreek; grind to powder. Grind green chillies, ginger, coriander stem, garlic into paste. Grind onions into paste. Heat ghee and oil, add dry red chili, coriander seeds, cumin seeds. Add ginger-garlic paste, saute 1 minute. Add onion paste, cook until golden. Add coriander powder, turmeric, tomato puree, curd; cook until well combined. Add salt, chickpeas (whole and mashed), sugar; simmer until thick. Finish with coriander leaves and prepared masala. Garnish with tomato, green chili, coriander, ginger, lemon.

name: murgh musallam
ingredients: ginger garlic paste 1 heaped tbsp plus 1 tbsp
degi red chili powder 1 tsp plus 0.5 tbsp
almonds chopped 3-4 plus sliver for garnish
ghee 3-4 tbsp plus 3 tbsp plus 1 heaped tbsp
instructions: Rub whole chicken with salt. Mix lemon juice, curd, ginger-garlic paste, chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric; coat chicken and marinate. Prepare stuffing: mix mince with fried onions, green chillies, ginger, almonds, cashew nuts, chili powder, salt, coriander stem. Stuff chicken and truss with thread. Sear in hot ghee from all sides until half-cooked. Add water, mace, cardamom, cloves; simmer covered for 10-12 minutes. For gravy: heat ghee and oil, saute onions until golden, add ginger-garlic paste and salt, add curd, cook. Add chili powder, coriander powder, turmeric; add chicken broth and cook 4-5 minutes. Strain gravy. In final handi, add ghee, bay leaf, cinnamon, prepared gravy. Add water and cashew paste; cook 7-8 minutes. Add kewra, rose water, mace-cardamom powder. Add cooked chicken, seal edges with dough, cover lid, place burnt coal on lid, cook 7-8 minutes on low. Garnish with silver vark, almonds, eggs, tomato, mint, coriander, saffron.

name: Paneer Handi
instructions: Grind ginger, green chillies and garlic into paste. Heat oil and ghee in handi, add bay leaves, peppercorns, cardamom and cumin seeds until fragrant. Add ginger-garlic paste and saute 1 minute. Add onions and cook until translucent. Add coriander powder, crushed peppercorns, turmeric and chili powder. Saute, then add yogurt and cook. Add tomatoes and cook 5-6 minutes until mushy. Add water and boil. Finish with handi masala, coriander leaves, salt and paneer. Simmer 5-6 minutes on low heat. Serve with roti or paratha.

name: Cheese Pav Bhaji
instructions: Boil cauliflower, potatoes, capsicum, peas and carrots in pressure cooker with butter and salt for 4-5 whistles. Heat oil and butter in handi, add ginger and onions, saute until translucent. Add green chillies, capsicum and ginger-garlic paste, saute 1 minute. Add degi red chili powder and tomatoes, saute until mushy. Add pav bhaji masala, boiled vegetables and butter. Simmer until cooked well. Finish with masala and coriander leaves. For cheese pav bhaji: slit pav in middle, add prepared bhaji and place on pan. Add butter, top with cheese and cook 3-4 minutes until melted. Serve with coriander, onions and lemon wedges.

name: Hyderabadi Chicken Masala
instructions: Make paste: heat oil, add garlic, ginger, cumin seeds, coriander seeds and white sesame seeds, saute 1 minute. Add onions and salt, saute until golden brown. Add black cardamom, stone flower, green cardamom, cloves, turmeric powder, chili powder and yogurt. Cook, then transfer to mixer grinder and grind smoothly. Marinate chicken with salt, chili powder, yogurt and ginger-garlic paste for 10-15 minutes. In same handi, add marinated chicken, fresh mint leaves and green chillies. Cover and cook 7-8 minutes. Add water and salt, give quick boil. Add prepared paste and coriander stems, cook 10-12 minutes on medium flames. Garnish with coriander sprig, lemon slices and onion rings. Serve hot with roti or naan.

name: Corn Manchurian
instructions: For balls: grind sweet corn kernels, coriander stems, green chillies and ginger into smooth paste. Transfer to bowl, add cornstarch, refined flour, rice flour, black pepper powder, vinegar and baking soda, mix well. In kadai, heat oil, drop spoonfuls of mixture and fry until golden on medium flames. For sauce mix: combine dark soy sauce, vinegar, green chili sauce, schezwan sauce, tomato ketchup, black pepper powder, water and sugar in bowl. For gravy: heat oil in shallow pan, add ginger, garlic, green chilli and saute 1 minute. Add onions and saute until translucent. Add boiled corn and salt, saute 2-3 minutes. Add prepared sauce mix and mix well. Add water and cornstarch slurry, boil until thickened. Finish with coriander leaves and spring onions, add fried corn manchurian balls and toss. Serve hot.

name: Cooker Pasta
instructions: Heat oil in pressure cooker, add ginger and garlic, saute 1 minute. Add onion, butter and salt, saute until translucent. Add fresh tomato puree and cook 5-6 minutes. Add degi red chili powder and coriander powder, saute 1 minute. Add sugar, corn kernels, tomato, butter, water, coriander stems, oregano, chilli flakes and penne pasta, mix well. Cover and cook 1 whistle, depressurize cooker. Finish with tomato ketchup and mix. For garlic butter: crush garlic and mix with butter. For garlic bread: spread garlic butter on bread, sprinkle oregano and chillies, place cheese slice and cover with another bread slice. Toast in pan with garlic butter and olive oil 2-3 minutes per side until golden. Transfer pasta to serving dish, garnish with grated cheese, oregano and basil. Serve hot with garlic bread.

name: Crispy Onion Rings
instructions: Make fresh bread crumbs from trimmed bread slices, grind and toast with semolina. Prepare batter with gram flour, refined flour, salt, turmeric, chili powder, curd, ginger-garlic paste and water. Make dry mixture with cornstarch, gram flour, salt, chili powder and black pepper. Cut onions into roundels and separate layers. Coat in dry mixture, dip in batter, coat with bread crumbs. Deep fry until crispy and golden brown. Serve hot with tadka ketchup dip and Italian chilli garlic mayonnaise dip.

name: Ghugni Chaat
instructions: Boil white peas with water, sliced onion, potato, salt and turmeric for 20-25 minutes until soft. Grind ginger, garlic cloves, green chilli and salt to coarse paste. Heat mustard oil and ghee, fry fresh coconut bits until golden, set aside. In same oil, fry onions and diced potatoes until golden brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and tomato, saute 1 minute. Add turmeric, chili powder, coriander and cumin powder, saute well. Add boiled peas, boiled potato and water, cover and cook 7-8 minutes on medium heat. Adjust consistency. Dry roast coriander seeds, cumin seeds, fennel and green cardamom, grind to fine powder. Transfer to serving bowl, garnish with raw mango, green chilli, nylon sev, fried coconut, roasted masala powder, chopped onion, lemon wedge and coriander sprig. Serve hot.

name: Mixed Dal Tadka
instructions: In pressure cooker, add washed moong and urad dals, water, turmeric, salt and ghee. Cover and cook for 2-3 whistles on medium heat. Depressurize and mash the dal. For tomato pappu tadka: heat oil and ghee, add mustard seeds, curry leaves, fenugreek and dried chillies, saute until aromatic. Add tomato, asafoetida, salt, tomato puree and chili powder, cook 7-8 minutes until mushy. Add ginger and cooked dal with water, boil briefly. For palak dal tadka: heat oil, add cumin seeds, garlic and asafoetida, saute until golden. Add onion and salt, cook until translucent. Add spinach and water, cook 4-5 minutes. Add cooked dal, water and green chilli, simmer. For gujarati dal tadka: heat ghee, add asafoetida, fenugreek, mustard seeds and clove, saute until aromatic. Add curd, cook 2-3 minutes. Add cooked dal, curd, salt and green chilli, boil 4-5 minutes. Garnish with lemon, garam masala and chaat masala. Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Crispy Moonglet
ingredients: 2 cups yellow moong dal washed and soaked
instructions: Grind ginger, green chillies, garlic cloves and soaked moong dal to a smooth paste with salt and sugar. For amchur mixture, mix water and dry mango powder. For amchur chutney, heat oil with cumin and fennel seeds until they splutter, add asafoetida, amchur mixture, salt and sugar, and boil until thickened. For paste, grind garlic, ginger, green chillies, coriander and salt to a smooth consistency. For dahi chutney, mix curd with the prepared paste. For moonglet batter, combine carrot, green chilli, carom seeds, coriander leaves and moong dal batter, let rest 20-25 minutes. Heat ghee in a pan, add a ladleful of batter and cook 3-4 minutes until golden. Top with green chillies, cheese, amchur chutney and paneer, cover with another ladleful of batter. Flip, add butter and cook 4-5 minutes until crispy and golden brown. Serve hot with both chutneys.

name: Chicken Tawa Pulao
ingredients: 600 gms chicken thigh boneless cut into strips
3 heaped tbsp degi red chili powder
instructions: Marinate chicken with salt, oil, lemon juice, ginger, degi red chili powder, coriander powder and turmeric powder. For tawa masala: Heat ghee and oil, add cumin seeds and onions, saute until translucent. Add ghee, prepared masala, green chillies, tomatoes, butter, salt and cook 7-8 minutes on high flames. Add water to adjust consistency. Make dry masala by combining degi red chili powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder and cumin powder. Saute green peas with ghee and water for 5-6 minutes. In a tawa, heat butter and cook marinated chicken 8-10 minutes on high flames. Cut into bite-size pieces and cook 2-3 minutes more. On same tawa, add butter, saute green capsicum and red bell pepper for a minute. Add cooked rice, sauteed peas, prepared tawa masala, sauteed chicken, salt and a little water. Saute well. Finish with fenugreek leaves, butter, spring onion, coriander and garam masala. Saute for a minute. Garnish with tomato, cucumber, onions, cabbage, boiled eggs, lemon wedge, green chillies, ginger and butter. Serve hot.

name: Tawa Chicken Masala
ingredients: 1 inch ginger peeled and roughly chopped
1 kg chicken thighs boneless cut into strips
instructions: Grind ginger, garlic, green chillies and salt coarsely in mortar pestle. Marinate chicken thighs with ginger-garlic paste, degi red chili powder and oil for 10-15 minutes. Toast cumin seeds, coriander seeds, fennel seeds, fenugreek seeds, black peppercorns, cloves, black cardamom and mace until aromatic. Transfer to tray, add dry fenugreek leaves and cinnamon stick, then grind smoothly. Heat oil on cast iron tawa, add marinated chicken and saute until half cooked on medium-high flame. Set aside. In same tawa, add remaining oil, add bay leaves and onions, cook 4-5 minutes until translucent. Add turmeric powder, coriander powder, degi red chili powder and cook 1 minute. Add tomatoes and water, cook 2-3 minutes until soft. Add butter, tender coriander stems and sauteed chicken, cook 7-8 minutes. Finish with prepared masala, coriander leaves and green chillies. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with fried cashew nuts, lemon wedge, coriander sprig and ginger. Serve hot with ladi pav.

name: Home Style Bhindi
ingredients: 2 medium potatoes peeled and roughly sliced
0.5 inch ginger peeled and roughly chopped
instructions: Heat mustard oil in iron kadai until smoky hot. Add onion and potatoes, fry until golden. Grind ginger and green chili into coarse paste. Add paste to kadai and saute 1 minute. Add ladyfinger, salt, coriander powder, mango powder, turmeric powder and degi red chili powder. Mix well and cook 10-12 minutes on medium flame until cooked through. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with coriander sprig. For fried bhindi: Heat mustard oil in non-stick pan, add dry red chili and ladyfinger, saute until golden brown. Add salt and degi red chili powder, toss for a minute. For raita: In serving bowl, add beaten curd and top with fried bhindi. Garnish with coriander sprig. Serve bhindi sabji hot with roti and raita.

name: dal paratha
instructions: Knead leftover dal with whole wheat flour. Add ginger, garlic, coriander leaves and pickle oil, knead until soft. Cover with cloth and rest. Take lemon-sized ball, flatten, add pickle oil and close. Roll into desired shape. Heat tawa with oil, cook paratha on both sides until golden brown. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with butter. Serve hot with onion, green chillies, curd and mango pickle.

name: dum aloo kali mirch
instructions: Heat oil in handi, add cinnamon stick, bay leaf, onions and ghee, sauté. Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for a minute. Add potatoes, salt, coriander stems and black peppercorns. Cook until potatoes are half done. Add beaten curd, cover with lid and cook for 10-12 minutes. Finish with sugar and masala mixture, cook for 2-3 minutes on low flame. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with coriander and crushed peppercorns. Serve hot with paratha or roti.

name: anda do pyaza
instructions: Boil eggs in water with salt and vinegar for 10-12 minutes, transfer to chilled water and shell. Heat oil and ghee, add cumin and fennel seeds, sauté onions until translucent. Add tomatoes, garlic, ginger and sauté. Add spice powders and curd, cook well. Grind into smooth paste. In same pan, add ghee and paste, add potatoes, cover and cook for 10-12 minutes until done. Add salt, sugar, water and ghee, bring to boil. Finish with sautéed eggs and vegetables, spring onion. Garnish with green chili and coriander. Serve hot with roti.

name: peri peri panini
instructions: Prepare red garlic chutney by grinding soaked deseeded chillies with garlic, cumin powder and black salt. Prepare green chutney by grinding coriander, curry leaves, green chillies, garlic, ginger, chana dal with minimal water. Mix peri peri spice powder from red chilli powder, black salt, sugar, ginger powder, garlic powder, oregano, cinnamon and salt. Combine paneer with capsicum, onion, cabbage, corn, chillies, peri peri spice, red chutney and mayo. Slice panini bread in half, spread butter on both halves, apply green chutney on one half and red chutney on other. Spread filling on one half, place second slice on top. Heat grill pan with butter, cook panini on both sides until grill marks appear. Serve hot.

name: veg green thai curry
instructions: Dry roast coriander seeds, cumin seeds and white peppercorns on low flame until aromatic. Pound in mortar pestle to powder. Add remaining paste ingredients (galangal, turmeric, lemon grass, garlic, shallots, chillies, lime, coriander) and pound into fine paste. Heat 200 ml coconut milk and cook for 2-3 minutes until it thickens. Add curry paste, stir and cook for 4-5 minutes until color changes and oil separates. Add remaining coconut milk and bring to boil. Add baby corn, cook for 2-3 minutes. Add remaining vegetables, brown sugar and salt. Cook for few minutes. Add kafir lime leaves, thai basil and red chillies. Cook for 2-3 minutes and serve hot with rice.

name: burnt garlic fried rice with basil paneer
instructions: For paneer: Make batter with cornflour, refined flour, salt, pepper and water. Coat paneer cubes in batter and deep fry until golden. For gravy: Heat oil, add ginger, garlic and green chillies, cook for a minute. Add onions and capsicum, add veg stock. Add soy sauce, vinegar, pepper, sugar and salt. Add cornflour slurry to thicken. Add fried paneer, spring onion greens and basil leaves, cook for a minute. For fried rice: Heat wok well, add garlic flavored oil and chopped garlic, cook until color changes. Add spring onion bulbs and greens, cook until translucent. Add cooked rice, season with salt, pepper, sugar, aromat powder, soy sauce and vinegar. Stir and cook for 1-2 minutes. Serve hot.

name: gajar matar aloo
instructions: Grind tomatoes with ginger, garlic and green chillies to make fine puree. Heat ghee in wok, add cumin seeds, let them crackle. Add asafoetida and onions, cook until golden brown. Lower flame, add spice powders and water to avoid burning. Cook for 3-4 minutes until ghee separates. Add tomato puree and salt, cook for 10-12 minutes until tomatoes are cooked and ghee separates. Add raw potatoes with water, cover and cook for 5-6 minutes until 3/4 done. Add carrots and green peas, cook for 2-3 minutes. Add hot water, cover and cook for 5-6 minutes. Add garam masala, kasuri methi and coriander leaves. Finish with more coriander. Serve hot with chapati or paratha.

name: eggless red velvet cupcakes
instructions: Preheat oven to 165C and line cupcake trays. Cream butter, castor sugar and vanilla essence until light and fluffy. Add condensed milk and whisk until incorporated. Add milk and mix well. Add flour and fold using spatula until just combined. In separate bowl, add yogurt, baking soda, cocoa powder, vinegar and food coloring. Mix well and pour into cupcake batter. Mix until combined. Using ice cream scooper, fill lined cupcake trays. Tap on surface lightly. Bake at 165C for 15 minutes until toothpick comes out clean. For frosting: Cream butter until smooth, add cream cheese and cream together. Add lemon zest and mix. Add icing sugar in batches while whisking until smooth and creamy. Refrigerate for 2-3 hours before serving.

name: Dal Khichdi
instructions: Wash and soak all dals for 30 minutes. Heat ghee in pressure cooker, add cumin seeds, ginger, green chilli. Add soaked dals, turmeric, salt, and water to 1 inch above dal. Pressure cook 3-4 whistles on medium heat, let cool naturally. Separately, wash and soak rice 15-20 minutes. Boil salted water and add soaked rice, boil 6-7 minutes until cooked, then drain. For first tadka: Heat ghee, add cumin seeds, crackle, add onions, garlic, ginger, green chillies until translucent. Add whole red chillies, tomatoes, salt, cook until mushy. Add powdered spices and hot water. Increase flame to high and tilt pan to catch fire for smoky flavour. Add cooked dal and rice, stir and cook 2-3 minutes. Adjust consistency with hot water if needed. Add dry mango powder, kasuri methi, lemon juice, and fresh coriander. Cook 1 minute.
