For second tadka: Heat ghee, add whole red chillies, switch off flame, add asafoetida, red chilli powder, and birista. Pour over hot khichdi. Serve with papad and salads.

name: Veg Hakka Noodles
instructions: Prep vegetables: For carrots, peel and create flat base, cut thin diagonal slices, arrange in line, cut into matchsticks. For cabbage, divide into halves, remove stem and core, flatten and cut into thin chiffonade shreds. For capsicum, trim top and bottom, make slit from top to bottom, lay flat and roll knife outwards to remove core, spread out and cut into juliennes. For onions, peel, cut in halves, place flat side down, cut even slices lengthwise, separate layers. For spring onions, cut 1 inch strips for cooking and fine strips for garnish. Boil salted water with 1 tsp oil. Add noodles and boil 30 seconds until separated. Cover for 2-2.5 minutes. Remove with tongs, rinse in cold water, drizzle oil. Heat wok on high flame with 1 tbsp oil. Add garlic, onions, carrots, capsicum, cabbage and stir-cook 30 seconds on high flame. Add sugar, green chilli paste, spring onion greens and boiled noodles. Add remaining ingredients (salt, white pepper, soy sauce, vinegar, aromat powder). Stir and mix well, cook 1-1.5 minutes. Garnish with finely chopped spring onion greens and serve hot.

name: Aloo Tokri Chaat
instructions: For basket: Boil potatoes and slice thin. Dip in batter of corn flour and turmeric. Deep fry in ice water bath to create crispy basket shape. For aloo tikki: Boil and mash potatoes, add chopped green chillies, cumin seeds, salt, and corn flour. Form into patties and shallow fry in ghee until golden. For masale wale chole: Heat ghee, add cooked kabuli chana with salt, coriander powder, yellow chilli powder, dry mango powder, garam masala, ginger julienne, and fresh coriander. For matar ki chaat: Heat ghee, add cooked white vatana with salt, crushed coriander seeds, red chilli flakes, and lemon juice. For dahi bhalle: Soak and grind yellow moong dal and urad dal with very little water to make thick paste. Add salt, raisins, cumin seeds, chopped green chillies, grated ginger, and asafoetida. Drop in hot oil by spoon and fry until golden. Soak in dahi made with curd, black salt, and powdered sugar. Assembly: Place crispy potato basket, top with aloo tikki, add masale chole, matar chaat, and dahi bhalle. Drizzle with green chutney, sweet chutney, and additional dahi. Sprinkle with crushed papdi, crushed coriander seeds, red chilli flakes, sev murmura, pomegranate seeds, and fresh coriander. Serve immediately.

name: Aam Ka Chunda
instructions: Wash totapuri mangoes thoroughly and dry completely with cloth or tissues. Peel mangoes and grate through large holes of grater. Discard seed when reached at centre. Transfer grated mangoes to large clean and dry heatproof glass bowl or stainless steel vessel. Add salt and turmeric powder, mix well using hands until everything combines. Add powdered sugar in batches, sieving before each addition, and mix well to remove lumps. Cover bowl tightly with muslin cloth using string. Keep indoors overnight. Next morning, place outside on terrace under direct sunlight until evening. Bring indoors after sunset, open cloth and stir well with clean spoon (sugar settles at bottom). Cover again with muslin cloth and keep indoors overnight. Repeat sun-drying and indoor overnight process for 6-10 days until sugar syrup thickens and reaches almost one-string consistency. Once thickened, add kashmiri red chilli powder, cumin powder, and garam masala. Mix well with clean spoon. Cover with muslin cloth and keep under sunlight for final 2 hours. Transfer to airtight glass jar for storage. Will last 1-2 years.

name: Veg Manchurian Dry
instructions: For manchurian balls: In large bowl add chopped cabbage, carrot, french beans, spring onion greens, fresh coriander, chopped ginger, garlic, and green chilli paste. Add all seasonings and sauces (light soy sauce, red chilli sauce, salt, white pepper, sugar). Mix lightly to avoid vegetables releasing moisture. Add corn flour and refined flour in batches, mixing by hand. Test consistency by forming a ball - if it holds together, consistency is right. Heat oil for frying on high heat. Dip fingers in water, take small amount of mixture, roughly shape into ball and drop in hot oil. Don't stir initially, allow balls to form layer. Lower flame to medium-low and cook manchurian balls until light golden brown (about 3-4 minutes total), stirring intervals. Remove and keep in strainer to rest 5-7 minutes. Can keep half-cooked balls aside and refry later. When ready to toss, increase heat to high until oil is very hot. Flash fry half-cooked manchurian balls for few seconds until super crispy and golden brown. Set aside. For sauce: Heat pan on high flame with 2 tbsp oil. Add chopped onions, minced garlic, ginger, coriander stems, chopped green chillies, and chopped capsicum. Stir and cook 1-2 minutes. Add stock/hot water, green chilli paste, dark soy sauce, vinegar, white pepper powder, salt, and sugar. Bring to boil. Make slurry of corn starch and water, add to boiling sauce while stirring, cook until thickened. Add crispy fried manchurian balls, fresh coriander, spring onion bulbs and greens. Stir well to coat. Garnish with spring onion greens and serve hot.

name: Chicken Manchow Soup
instructions: Heat wok on high flame until heated well. Add oil and let heat. Add chopped ginger, minced garlic, and coriander stems. Stir and cook 1-2 minutes on high flame. Add roughly minced chicken and stir constantly, separating pieces with spatula so it doesn't form a patty. Cook 2-3 minutes on high flame. Add chopped tomatoes, cabbage, carrot, and capsicum. Stir and cook veggies on high flame only a few seconds. Add chicken stock and bring to boil. Once boiling, add light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, white pepper powder, green chilli paste, and salt to taste. Stir well. Add dark soy sauce until soup becomes blackish in colour and adjust salt carefully as sauces already contain salt. To thicken soup, make slurry of corn flour and water in separate bowl. Pour slurry into soup while continuously stirring. Cook until soup thickens. In separate bowl, beat egg well. Add beaten egg into soup in thin stream while stirring very gently. Once egg sets, taste soup for seasoning and adjust as needed. Finally add fresh coriander and spring onion greens and stir well. For fried noodles: Heat oil in pan or kadhai until moderately hot. Carefully drop boiled noodles in oil (oil will rise fast, use deep vessel). Don't stir initially, let fry slowly. Once noodles form disc, flip using tongs and fry until light golden brown on both sides. Transfer to sieve and rest 4-5 minutes. Gently break noodles to form fried noodle clusters. Serve hot chicken manchow soup garnished with fried noodles and spring onion greens.

name: Chicken Changezi
instructions: Marinate chicken with salt, kashmiri chilli powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, ginger garlic paste, green chilli paste, and lemon juice. Cook in hot oil on high flame for 2-3 minutes per side, then cover and cook on medium flame for 10-12 minutes until 75% cooked. Make base gravy by blanching and grinding tomatoes, then tempering oil with onions until golden, adding ginger garlic paste, powdered spices, and tomato puree, cooking for 20-25 minutes. For final gravy, heat tawa with oil, add base gravy with curd and fresh cream, cook on high for 20-25 minutes until dark, then add cooked chicken with green chillies, chaat masala, kasuri methi, cook on low for 5-10 minutes until oil separates. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot with tandoori rotis.

name: Kanda Bhajji
instructions: Cut onions in half, peel and slice lengthwise into thin layers, separate the layers by hand. Coat onion layers with salt and kashmiri red chilli powder. Add gram flour in small batches and mix gently, add a splash of water and knead until everything comes together. Heat oil to 170C (moderately hot). Dip hand in cold water, scoop small portions of mixture and drop into hot oil without shaping. Fry on high flame for 30 seconds without stirring, then on medium-low flame while stirring until golden brown and crispy. Fry on high for final 30 seconds to prevent oil absorption. Transfer to sieve to drain excess oil.

name: Dabeli Tacos
instructions: Make dough by mixing corn meal, refined flour, salt, turmeric powder and water, knead well and rest for 15-20 minutes. For filling, heat oil, add cumin seeds, asafoetida, green chilli paste and dabeli masala, add mashed potatoes and tamarind pulp, cook until smooth consistency. After dough rests, make balls and roll into thin chapatis, cut smaller rotis with 4-inch cutter, poke holes with fork. Fry in medium-hot oil on medium-low flame, fold over rolling pin into taco shape. Make sauce by whisking mayonnaise, red chutney, and sweet chutney. Fill taco shells with dabeli filling, coat in masala peanuts then coconut then coriander, drizzle sauce and top with pomegranate seeds.

name: Tawa Paneer
instructions: For base gravy, heat oil in pan, add whole spices and sliced onions, stir until onions turn golden brown, add ginger, garlic, coriander stems and cook until deep golden. Lower flame, add powdered spices and hot water immediately to prevent burning, cook for 3-4 minutes. Add capsicum, tomatoes, green chillies, salt and cashew nuts with hot water, cover and cook on medium-low for 4-5 minutes until tomatoes soften. Cool completely, remove some whole spices if desired, blend finely with water. For final gravy, heat tawa with ghee, add cumin seeds, chopped onions, garlic, ginger and green chillies, cook on medium-high until onions turn light golden. Add turmeric and kashmiri red chilli powder, then add base gravy and cook for 10 minutes on medium flame, adding hot water if too dry. In separate pan, heat 1 tsp ghee and toss diced onion and capsicum for 30 seconds, add to gravy. Add diced paneer, garam masala, kasuri methi, fresh coriander and grated paneer, stir well and taste for seasoning. Serve hot with rumali roti.

name: Jini Dosa
instructions: Wash rice and urad dal together 3-4 times, soak with methi seeds for 1 hour minimum. Drain and grind into fine paste with small quantities of water. Mix besan, sooji, salt with water to make smooth paste. Combine rice-dal paste with besan-sooji paste, whisk well, gradually add water to make thick lump-free batter, cover and ferment in warm place for 3-4 hours minimum. Check consistency after fermentation and adjust if needed, verify salt. For masala, heat tawa and add butter, oil and cumin seeds to crackle. Add onions and cook until translucent, add tomatoes and salt, add capsicum and cabbage, add schezwan chutney, ketchup and red chilli sauce, add garam masala and kashmiri red chilli powder, mix well and mash lightly, cook for 1-2 minutes. To make dosa, heat dosa tawa on high, test heat by sprinkling water, place spoonful of batter and spread in circular motion to form thin layer. Lower flame, spread jini masala evenly, grate paneer and processed cheese on top, add fresh coriander and spring onion greens. Once cooked, cut into strips and roll, serve with grated cheese and chutneys.

name: Pav Bhaji Toast
instructions: Mix all filling ingredients (potatoes, onions, tomatoes, capsicum, chillies, garlic paste, spices, salt, lemon juice, coriander) in a bowl. Apply filling evenly on bread slice. Set tawa on medium heat, add filling-applied bread and toast until crisp and golden brown on both sides. Cut into squares or triangles and serve hot with ketchup or green chutney.

name: Korean Fried Chicken
instructions: Marinate bite-size chicken pieces with ginger, garlic, salt and black pepper for even coating. Coat thoroughly with corn flour. Deep fry in hot oil at medium heat for 3-4 minutes until light golden brown. Remove and rest. Re-fry at 190°C on high heat until crispy and golden brown. Prepare sauce with gochujang, soy sauce, honey, mirin, sesame oil, garlic and ginger. Bring to simmer and toss crispy fried chicken in sauce without further cooking. Garnish with sesame seeds and spring onion greens. Serve immediately.

name: Cream of Spinach Soup
instructions: Blanch spinach in boiling water for 15-20 seconds, immediately transfer to ice water and squeeze dry. Cook spinach with garlic, salt and pepper in pan. Add to blender with mint, coriander, ginger, green chillies and water, grind to fine puree. Make roux by heating olive oil with wheat flour for 2-3 minutes. Add milk gradually in batches while stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Add bay leaf, nutmeg and pepper, cook for 3-4 minutes. Add spinach puree and adjust consistency with hot water. Bring to boil and cook 3-4 minutes. Season with honey and lemon juice. Finish by adding fresh cream. Serve hot with garlic bread, garnish with cream and red chilli flakes.

name: Crispy Rava Dosa
instructions: Mix rice flour, rava, refined flour, cumin seeds, crushed black pepper, ginger, green chillies, curry leaves and salt in bowl. Add 2 cups water initially and mix well to avoid lumps. Add remaining water and mix thoroughly. Rest batter for at least 30 minutes. Heat non-stick dosa tawa on high heat and check temperature by sprinkling water. Spread chopped onions all over tawa, stir batter once and pour all over forming mesh-like texture. Lower flame to medium and cook slowly. Pour ghee or oil as preferred. As moisture evaporates, dosa becomes crisp and golden brown. Fold as desired and serve hot with coconut chutney and sambhar.

name: Dahi Bhalle
instructions: Soak green gram and black gram separately for 5-6 hours. Drain, grind urad dal to fine paste with minimal water. Grind moong dal similarly. Mix both dals with salt. Whisk mixture for 8-10 minutes until fluffy and pale (test by scooping on spoon and flipping upside down, should not drop). Add raisins, cumin seeds, green chillies and ginger, mix gently. Prepare soaking water with lukewarm water, salt and asafoetida. Heat oil to 170°C. Dip hands in ice water, scoop batter, drop carefully in oil and splash with hot oil to puff. Fry over medium-high heat until light golden brown. Transfer immediately to soaking water, soak 4-5 minutes until bhalle sink. Squeeze out excess water. For yogurt: mix with powdered sugar and black salt, pass through sieve for smoothness. Assemble: layer soaked bhalle, tamarind chutney, mint chutney, sweet yogurt, masala. Repeat layers. Garnish with chilli powder and pomegranate. Serve chilled.

name: Veg Lasagna
instructions: Make red sauce: heat olive oil, add onion and garlic, cook 2-3 minutes until translucent. Add kashmiri red chilli powder, both tomato purees, salt, chilli flakes, oregano, sugar and pepper. Cover and cook 10-12 minutes on low flame. Add torn basil leaves. Make white sauce: melt butter, add flour and cook 2-3 minutes until sandy texture. Add milk in 3 batches while whisking continuously to avoid lumps. Cook until sauce thickens and becomes smooth. Add salt and nutmeg. Saute vegetables: heat olive oil, add garlic, cook 1-2 minutes. Add carrots and zucchini, cook 1-2 minutes. Add remaining vegetables with sugar, oregano, chilli flakes and salt, cook 1-2 minutes. Make lasagna dough: mix flour, salt and water to semi-tough consistency. Rest 10-15 minutes, knead 7-8 minutes, rest 30 minutes. Divide into 4 parts, roll thin, trim into rectangles. Create makeshift oven: fill handi with salt, place small ring mold inside, preheat 10-15 minutes. Layer in baking tray: red sauce, sheet, red sauce, veggies, white sauce, mozzarella, parmesan. Repeat 4-5 times. Bake 30-45 minutes in makeshift oven. Serve hot.

name: peri peri oodles
instructions: Heat oil in pan, add french beans and remaining vegetables. Toss for 1 minute. Add water and bring to boil. Add saffola oodles, tastemaker, and peri peri spice mix. Stir well to combine. Cook for 4-5 minutes while stirring at regular intervals until noodles are cooked. Serve hot.

name: eggless sizzling brownie
ingredients: dark chocolate compound 1/2 cup plus 90 grams
castor sugar 1/3 cup plus 1 tbsp plus 1.5 cup
instructions: For chocolate sauce: Heat milk and sugar, add cocoa powder and salt. Melt dark chocolate and butter in it. Add more butter in batches and vanilla essence. For brownie batter: Double boiler melt chocolate and butter, add sugar and vanilla essence, whisk well. Add curd and whisk until sugar dissolves. Sift and fold in flour, cocoa powder and salt. Pour into 5x5 inch tray lined with parchment paper. Preheat pressure cooker on high flame for 10-15 minutes with a stand. Place tray in cooker and cook for 20-25 minutes or until toothpick inserted comes out clean. Cool, cut into squares. Serve on hot sizzler pan with chocolate sauce, ice cream and nuts.

name: tawa pulao
ingredients: kashmiri red chilli 30-35 nos deseeded soaked
instructions: Wash and soak basmati rice for 15-20 minutes. Par boil rice in salted boiling water for 3-4 minutes, strain and fluff with fork. Make red chilli garlic paste by grinding soaked kashmiri chillies with garlic and green chillies. Heat tawa, add butter, oil and cumin seeds. Add sliced onions and cook until light golden brown. Add all vegetables and stir well. Add powdered spices, salt and red chilli garlic paste with hot water. Cook for 2-3 minutes on high flame. Add par-boiled rice with garam masala, kasuri methi, coriander and lemon juice. Stir everything well and sprinkle fresh coriander.

name: Paneer Patiala
instructions: Prepare onion tomato masala by cooking chopped onions, ginger garlic paste, green chillies and tomatoes with spices. Separately, prepare onion base gravy puree by blending soaked onions, green chillies, cloves, bay leaf, black cardamom, garlic, ginger, cashew nuts and melon seeds. Make paneer filling by mixing paneer cubes with ginger, green chillies, spices and kasuri methi. Shallow fry papad and stuff with paneer mixture. For final gravy, cook aromatic spices, add tomato puree, OTM and onion base puree, finish with butter, cream and fresh coriander. Serve papad paneer roll with the hot gravy.

name: Paneer Kathi Roll
instructions: Prepare dough by mixing refined flour, sugar and salt, adding ghee and warm water, then kneading for 10-12 minutes until soft and non-sticky. Rest dough covered for 1 hour. For paneer filling, whisk mustard oil with kashmiri red chilli powder, add garam masala, ginger garlic paste, lemon juice and curd. Add paneer cubes and marinate. Sauté marinated paneer on high heat for 2-3 minutes, then add onions, capsicum, tomato and red chilli powder. Roll dough portions into thin chapati, brush ghee and flour, pleat and roll into spiral. Flatten peda with rolling pin and cook on hot pan without oil, press with napkin. Add oil and cook until crispy on both sides. Assemble by placing sautéed paneer filling in centre of paratha, adding onions, green chillies, chutneys, lemon juice and fresh coriander. Roll and serve hot.

name: Veg Thukpa Soup
instructions: Boil noodles in salted water until soft, drain and drizzle with oil, set aside. For thukpa, heat wok on medium heat, add oil, then ginger, garlic and green chillies. Cook for few minutes, add sliced onions and cook until translucent, add tomatoes and cook for a minute. Add all powdered spices and cook for a minute, then add hot stock and bring to boil. Add all vegetables, soy sauce, salt and black pepper, cook for 2-3 minutes keeping vegetables crunchy. Finish with lemon juice, fresh coriander and spring onion greens. For momo chutney, soak dry red chillies in hot water for 1 hour, roast tomatoes on open flame until charred, peel skin. Pulse all chutney ingredients in food processor until semi-coarse texture. Serve thukpa hot with cooked noodles and momo chutney on the side.

name: Veg Schezwan Manchurian
instructions: For manchurian balls, combine chopped vegetables with soy sauce, red chilli sauce, butter, salt, pepper and sugar. Mix in corn flour and refined flour until consistency holds together when formed into balls. Heat oil and deep fry shaped balls on high heat initially without stirring, then lower flame to cook through. Fry until light golden brown for 3-4 minutes, drain and cool. For schezwan sauce, heat wok with oil, add garlic, coriander stems, ginger and kashmiri red chilli paste, cook until paste darkens and becomes crumbly. Add chopped vegetables, sauces, sugar and salt. Cook for 1-2 minutes, add stock and green chilli paste, cook for 1-2 minutes. Make cornstarch slurry with water and add to sauce to thicken. Add fried manchurian balls and toss to coat evenly. Garnish with fresh coriander and spring onion greens. Serve hot.

name: Jhal Muri
instructions: For bhaja masala, heat pan on high heat, lower flame and add cumin seeds, toast for 1-2 minutes stirring constantly. Add remaining spices and toast on low flame until colour changes. Cool spices completely, then grind into fine powder. For assembly, combine puffed rice, sev, chana chur, dry coconut, sprouted black chana and boiled vatana in a large bowl. Add chopped green chillies, boiled potato, tomatoes and onions. Sprinkle bhaja masala and black salt. Drizzle mango pickle oil and mustard oil, add green chilli paste and squeeze lemon juice. Mix well and serve immediately while puffed rice is crispy.

name: Kadak Rumali Roti
ingredients: kashmiri red chilli powder for topping
instructions: Mix refined flour, wheat flour, sugar and salt, add oil and milk in batches, knead well for 10-12 minutes until soft and smooth, stretching dough while kneading to develop gluten. Divide into equal dough balls, cover with damp cloth and rest for 1 hour. Heat inverted kadhai on flame until very hot but not too hot. For each roti, sprinkle dry flour and flatten, roll into very thin sheet using rolling pin, thin enough to see hands through it. Place rolled roti on hot kadhai inverted surface, cook on low flame using napkin to press and turn continuously, evaporate excess moisture to make it crisp. Cook only on one side to create dome shape, takes 7-8 minutes. Once crisp and golden brown inside, remove carefully and let cool. Top with melted butter, chaat masala, kashmiri red chilli powder, lemon juice, mint and coriander leaves. Optional second topping: peri peri spice, chopped onions, tomatoes, green chillies, grated cheese and nylon sev.

name: egg curry dhaba style
ingredients: whole spices cumin dalchini cardamom tej patta
instructions: Heat ghee and fry boiled eggs with chilli powder and turmeric, set aside. In wok, add ghee with whole spices and cook onions until golden brown. Add ginger-garlic-chilli paste and cook 3-4 minutes. Add powdered spices with hot water to avoid burning. Add tomatoes and salt, cook 8-10 minutes. Add hot water and cooked fried eggs, cook on high 5-6 minutes. Add ginger, green chillies, kasuri methi, garam masala and coriander. Adjust consistency with hot water. Serve hot with roti or Indian bread.

name: crispy veggie burger
instructions: Soak and mash poha. Heat oil and butter, add onions and garlic until translucent. Add french beans, carrots, peas and green chillies, cook 2-3 minutes. Add spices and grated potatoes, cook until combined. Add soaked poha and coriander. Refrigerate 12-15 minutes. Make batter with maida, cornflour, salt and water. Coat bread crumbs with salt and pepper. Shape patties and coat in batter then breadcrumbs. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Toast burger buns with butter. Assemble with lettuce, mayo and patty. Serve immediately.

name: moong dal bhajiya
instructions: Soak moong dal 4-5 hours, drain excess water. Grind using pulse mode to semi-coarse paste without water. Transfer to bowl, add salt, green chillies, curry leaves and crushed black pepper. Do not overmix batter. Heat oil for frying. Dip fingers in water, take small portions and drop in hot oil without shaping. Fry on medium heat until crisp and golden brown. For chutney: grind all ingredients to fine paste. Serve hot bhajiya with spicy coconut chutney.

name: spicy mexican burger
instructions: Make Mexican seasoning by grinding cumin, coriander, chilli, oregano, aamchur, garlic and onion powders. Make spicy mayo by mixing mayo, chilli sauce, mustard, honey and Mexican seasoning. Make salsa by combining tomatoes, onion, green chillies, coriander, ketchup, lemon and salt. For patty: heat oil, add onions, garlic, green chillies, cook 1-2 minutes. Add carrots, french beans, cook 2-3 minutes. Add mushrooms and capsicum, cook 1-2 minutes. Cool completely. Mix in black beans, rice, coriander, chilli sauce and Mexican seasoning. Mash with potato masher, add breadcrumbs. Shape into patties. Shallow fry on both sides until golden brown. Toast buns with butter. Assemble with lettuce, mayo, salsa and nachos. Serve immediately.

name: pasta arrabbiata
instructions: Make pasta dough with refined flour, salt and water. Rest 10-15 minutes covered. Knead well 7-8 minutes until smooth. Rest 30 minutes. Divide into 4 parts and roll into thin sheets. Dust flour, stack and trim edges. Dust and fold, cut 1 cm thick strips. Separate layers carefully. For sauce: heat olive oil, add garlic and cook. Add basil, red chilli flakes, oregano and kashmiri chilli powder, cook on low heat. Add fresh and canned tomato puree with salt and sugar. Cover and cook 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add torn basil near end. Boil water with salt and oil, cook fresh pasta 4-5 minutes. Add to sauce and mix well. Add pasta water if too dry. Serve hot with parmesan cheese and garlic bread.

name: mawa cake
instructions: Cream butter and sugar until pale yellow and fluffy. Add condensed milk and grated mawa, whisk well 2-3 minutes. Add milk and vanilla essence, mix well. Mixture may appear curdled, this is normal. Sieve dry ingredients (refined flour, baking powder, baking soda) into wet mixture and fold well using spatula. Add cardamom powder and mix. Do not overmix. Line cupcake trays with liners. Using ice-cream scooper, fill 2/3 of liners. Tap tray gently. Bake at 180°C for 13-15 minutes until skewer comes out clean and golden brown crust forms. Cool 10-15 minutes before serving.

name: noodle frankie
instructions: Make dough with refined flour, curd, salt and water. Knead well 10-15 minutes. Tuck edges inward, coat with oil, cover and rest 30 minutes. Divide into roundels, roll like chapati. Set pan on high heat with oil. Briefly cook roti on both sides on extremely hot pan. For noodles: heat oil, add red onion, garlic, ginger, green chilli paste, cook 1-2 minutes. Add cabbage, capsicum, carrots, spring onion bulbs with pinch of sugar, cook 1-2 minutes. Add boiled noodles with schezwan sauce, ketchup, red chilli sauce, soy sauce, vinegar and white pepper, cook 2-3 minutes. Add finely chopped spring onion green. For frankie masala: pulse all spices in mixer grinder. To assemble: heat pan with butter, cook half-done frankie roti until golden and crispy. Spread mayo, schezwan sauce, frankie masala on roti. Add chopped onions, noodles, grated cheese. Fold and roll. Serve immediately.

name: Plum Cake
instructions: Soak dried fruits in orange juice for at least 24 hours (refrigerate). Prepare molasses: caramelize sugar and jaggery over medium heat until dark brown, add water gradually, cool until thick. Sift together flour, salt, cinnamon, clove, nutmeg, ginger, baking powder, baking soda. Heat butter with orange juice, brown sugar, molasses to boil, cool slightly. Add soaked fruits, condensed milk, vanilla essence. Combine wet and dry ingredients using cut-and-fold method. Add almonds, orange and lemon zest. Thin batter with reserved orange juice to achieve medium consistency. Line 8x4 inch loaf tin with butter paper, pour batter, tap to remove air pockets. Top with dried cranberries, tutti fruity, walnuts, almonds. Bake at 150°C for 1 hour. Cool, remove paper, slice and store in fridge up to 1 week.

name: Chilli Paneer
instructions: Coat paneer cubes with salt, pepper, and corn starch. Heat oil, shallow fry paneer on medium heat until golden brown on all sides. Set pan on high heat, add oil, garlic, ginger, green chillies, spring onion bulbs, sauté for 3-4 minutes. Add capsicum, spring onion greens, sugar, sauté for 1 minute. Add soy sauce and red chilli sauce, sauté for 1 minute. Mix cornflour with water, add to pan along with salt and pepper, cook until sauce thickens. Fold in fried paneer gently, coat well with sauce. Finish with fresh chopped spring onion greens. Serve immediately as party appetizer with schezwan sauce.

name: Chicken Seekh Kabab
instructions: Grind chicken thighs and breast with onion, salt to fine paste. Mix in green chillies, ginger, garlic, coriander, mint. Add all powdered spices, lemon juice, black pepper, ghee. Form seekh: mold mixture onto metal skewers, ensure no cracks. For red chutney: blend soaked kashmiri chillies, green chillies, tamarind pulp, jaggery, cumin, black salt, garlic. For green chutney: blend coriander, mint, ginger, garlic, green chillies, black salt, anardan powder with ice cubes. For onion salad: toss sliced onions with coriander, mint, red chilli powder, chaat masala, lemon juice, mustard oil. Grill seekhs or pan-fry in ghee until cooked through. Serve in rumali roti with chutneys, salad, lemon juice, and chaat masala.

name: Punjabi Pakora Kadhi
instructions: Coarsely crush coriander seeds in mortar pestle. Make curd mixture: whisk together curd, besan, turmeric, crushed coriander seeds, red chilli powder, ginger garlic paste, salt, and water until lump-free. For kadhi base: heat ghee in kadhai, add cumin, methi seeds, black pepper, kashmiri red chillies, sliced onion, hing. Stir and cook on high for 1-2 minutes. Add diced potatoes, cook until onions turn translucent (2-3 minutes). Add curd mixture, bring to boil on high flame. Reduce to low flame, cover and cook 30-35 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add chopped coriander. For pakoras: mix sliced onions, green chillies, crushed carrom seeds, coriander, potato, kashmiri red chilli powder, turmeric, crushed coriander seeds, salt, besan, baking soda, dry mango powder. Add little water to bind (not pasty). Heat oil on medium, roughly shape and fry until light golden. Drain on sieve for 5-6 minutes. Increase heat to high, flash fry pakoras for 15-20 seconds until crisp and golden. Drain excess oil. Add pakoras to hot kadhi 10 minutes before serving.

name: Baby Corn Chilli
instructions: Cut baby corn into bite-sized diagonal pieces. Boil water with pinch of salt, add baby corn, cook 7-8 minutes until almost done. Drain and cool. Make batter: whisk cornflour, refined flour, baking powder, salt, black pepper powder with water to make thick lump-free batter. Coat boiled baby corn pieces in batter. Heat oil on medium-high, fry coated corn until crisp and light golden brown. Drain on sieve. For sauce: heat 2 tbsp oil in wok on high flame, add onions, ginger, garlic, coriander stems, green chillies. Cook for 1 minute. Add vegetable stock/hot water, bring to boil. Add light soy, dark soy, green chilli paste, sugar, salt, white pepper, mix well. Make slurry: mix cornflour and water (no lumps). Add slurry to sauce while whisking, sauce will thicken. Lower flame, add fried baby corn, capsicum, spring onion bulbs, fresh coriander. Toss gently to coat without making crispy corn soggy. Top with spring onion greens and serve immediately.

name: Chicken Handi
ingredients: kashmiri red chilli powder pinch + 1 tbsp + 1 tsp
instructions: Marinate chicken: mix salt, lemon juice, turmeric powder, kashmiri red chilli powder pinch, whisked curd. Marinate chicken in mixture while preparing gravy. Base gravy: heat oil, add cumin seeds, 6-7 sliced onions, stir and cook 7-8 minutes on medium-high until golden brown. Add ginger-garlic-chilli paste, cook 2-3 minutes. Lower flame, add all powdered spices, stir immediately and add hot water to prevent burning. Add tomatoes, salt, coriander stalks. Cover and cook 10-15 minutes until tomatoes fully cooked, stir regularly, add water as needed. Cool completely and grind to fine paste. Final gravy: heat oil and ghee in handi, coat base well. Heat nicely, add whole spices (cumin seeds, black cardamom, green cardamom, cinnamon, black pepper corns, bay leaf), chopped onion, ginger-garlic paste, green chillies. Cook 3-4 minutes until onion golden brown. Lower flame, add kashmiri red chilli powder 1 tbsp, stir. Add marinated chicken, mix well, cook high for 3-4 minutes until outer layer cooked. Add base gravy, mix, bring to boil. Cover handi with lid, seal using dough, pierce skewer into seal for steam to escape. Cook on very low flame for 25 minutes. Turn off flame, let rest 5-7 minutes. Break seal carefully, open lid, turn flame to medium-high. Add remaining ingredients (ginger julienne, green chilli slit, garam masala 1 tsp, kasuri methi, fresh coriander). Stir well, taste and adjust salt. Serve hot.

name: Kolhapuri Mutton Tambda Pandhra Rassa
instructions: 1. Marinate 2kg mutton with ginger-garlic-chilli paste, turmeric, and salt for 30 minutes. 2. Toast whole red chillies (kashmiri and byadgi) for 20-25 minutes to crisp, then grind to powder. 3. Prepare vaatan (spice blend) by dry roasting coriander, cumin, caraway, fennel, mustard, sesame, poppy seeds in batches until aromatic. 4. Cook 8 medium onions in oil until golden (10-15 minutes). 5. Add ginger, garlic, green chillies, cook 2-3 minutes. 6. Mix curd with powdered spices and salt, add to pan and cook until ghee separates (7-8 minutes). 7. Add tomato puree, cook 7-8 minutes, add water, cook until ghee separates again. 8. For tamda rassa: In separate pan, heat oil with kashmiri chilli powder, kanda lehsun masala, add mutton stock and lemon juice, simmer 10 minutes. 9. For sukha mutton: Fry marinated mutton pieces in oil with onions until cooked and crispy. 10. For pandhra rassa: Toast cinnamon, cloves, black cardamom, star anise in oil, add cashew paste made from boiled cashews, add coconut milk and salt, simmer 5-7 minutes. 11. Plate sukha mutton with tamda and pandhra rassor on top, garnish with coriander.

name: Peri Peri French Fries
instructions: 1. Mix all peri peri spice ingredients (kashmiri chilli, spicy chilli, garlic powder, onion powder, dry mango powder, black pepper, oregano, salt, sugar) in mixer grinder and grind to fine powder. Alternatively shake in jar if using powdered sugar. 2. Peel potatoes and submerge in cold water to prevent oxidation. 3. Cut potatoes vertically, trim all four sides to remove edges, cut into 1 cm thick slices. 4. Cut thick slices into 1 cm wide french fry strips, place in ice cold water and rinse thoroughly. 5. Boil water in large pot with vinegar and salt, add fries and blanch 10 minutes, stirring regularly with spider. 6. Remove fries and place on wire rack or paper towel, let rest 10-15 minutes. 7. Heat oil to 200°C and fry fries for 45-60 seconds, remove and rest on rack another 10-15 minutes. 8. Heat oil to 200°C again and fry fries second time until light golden brown and crispy, maintaining temperature at 170-175°C. 9. Transfer to bowl and immediately sprinkle salt and black pepper to taste, toss well for regular fries. For peri peri fries, add peri peri spice mix instead and toss.

name: Aloo Bhuna Frankie
ingredients: oil 1 tbsp plus 2 tbsp plus 3 tbsp for cooking
kashmiri red chilli powder 2 tbsp plus 3 tbsp
instructions: 1. For frankie roti: Mix maida, salt, curd, and water to form dough. Knead on platform 10-15 minutes until soft and stretchy. Apply oil, cover with damp cloth, rest 30 minutes. 2. Divide dough into balls, coat with flour, flatten and roll into slightly thick chapati. Cook on hot tawa with oil for 20-30 seconds per side, cool and stack covered. 3. For bhuna masala: Heat oil and ghee, add jeera, cook onions until golden (10-15 minutes). Add ginger, garlic, green chillies, cook 2-3 minutes. 4. Mix curd with powdered spices and salt separately, add to pan on low flame, stir quickly. Cook 7-8 minutes until ghee separates. 5. Add tomato puree and salt, cook 7-8 minutes, add hot water, cook 7-8 minutes until ghee separates again. Add garam masala, kasuri methi, fresh coriander. 6. For tadka per 2-3 rolls: Heat butter, add sliced onions, capsicum, green chillies, boiled potato dice. Cook 1 minute, add bhuna gravy 3-4 tbsp and fresh coriander, cook 2-3 minutes. 7. Make tamarind chutney by grinding coriander leaves, mint, black salt, amchur, green chillies, ginger, tamarind pulp, salt with ice cubes. 8. Make frankie masala by mixing kashmiri chilli powder, turmeric, coriander, black salt, anardana, amchur, cumin, garam masala, black pepper, salt. 9. Make chilli vinegar by mixing vinegar and chopped green chillies. 10. Assemble: Butter half-cooked roti, add aloo bhuna tadka, chopped onions, chilli vinegar, tamarind chutney, sprinkle frankie masala, roll and serve hot.

name: Chicken Shawarma Sev Puri
ingredients: black pepper powder 1 pinch plus to taste
kashmiri red chillies 15-20 whole soaked deseeded
instructions: 1. For garlic dip: Whisk curd with mayonnaise, grated garlic, vinegar, powdered sugar, salt, black pepper, fresh coriander until smooth. 2. For hot sauce: Grind soaked kashmiri chillies (deseeded), green chillies, garlic, vinegar, sugar, jeera powder, black salt, black pepper, salt, olive oil, water to completely smooth paste. 3. For shawarma chicken: Cut chicken breasts into thin slices. 4. Mix marinade ingredients (curd, garlic paste, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, cinnamon powder, cardamom powder, black pepper, vinegar, salt) until smooth. 5. Coat chicken slices in marinade and let rest. 6. Heat oil in pan on high heat until very hot. 7. Add chicken slices and cook until one side is done, flip and cook other sides (slices cook quickly). 8. Cool cooked chicken and cut into small pieces, mix well with oil collected in bowl. 9. For assembly: Place puri, spread garlic dip on top. 10. Layer with shredded lettuce, chicken pieces, small dices of pickled beetroot and carrot, crispy potato salli. 11. Serve immediately.

name: Dahi Waale Aloo
ingredients: salt large pinch plus to taste
mustard oil sarson ka tel 3 tbsp
kashmiri red chilli powder 2 tbsp total
instructions: 1. Cut medium potatoes into 1 inch dice. 2. Boil salted water and add diced potatoes, cook 7-8 minutes until 90% cooked (slight fork resistance). Drain and cool completely. 3. Mix marination ingredients (mustard oil, kashmiri chilli powder, coriander powder, turmeric, cumin powder, kasuri methi, fennel powder, black salt) and coat cooled potatoes well. 4. Heat oil in pan on medium-high heat until hot, add marinated potatoes and shallow fry until golden brown and crispy on all sides, turning carefully. Set aside. 5. For gravy: Boil cashews and melon seeds in water until cashews soften, drain and grind to very fine paste with little water. 6. Whisk 2 cups curd with powdered spices (spicy red chilli powder, turmeric, coriander powder) until smooth, no lumps. 7. Heat mustard oil in wok on medium heat until smoking, switch off and cool slightly. 8. Add cumin seeds, cloves, green cardamom pods, green chillies, julienned ginger, cook 1 minute. 9. Lower flame and add kashmiri red chilli powder, stir quickly, add splashes of water to prevent burning. 10. Add prepared cashew-melon seed paste and curd mixture, stir continuously, cook 2-3 minutes. 11. Lower to medium heat and cook 7-8 minutes while stirring continuously until gravy thickens. Adjust consistency with hot water if needed. 12. Add shallow fried potatoes, garam masala, kasuri methi, stir gently and cook few more minutes. 13. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot with chapati or Indian bread.

name: Crispy Threaded Chicken
ingredients: refined flour maida 2 cups plus 2 tbsp
oil 1 tsp plus 2 tbsp for frying plus as required for cooking
boneless chicken breast 250 gm sliced lengthwise
kashmiri red chilli paste from 3-4 soaked chillies
green chilli paste from 2 green chillies
instructions: 1. For fresh noodles: Mix flour and salt, gradually add water to form dough. Transfer to platform, add oil, knead 8-10 minutes until soft and smooth. Cover with damp cloth, rest 1 hour. 2. Divide dough into 7-8 balls, cover and rest another 30 minutes. 3. Flatten balls with hands, roll into small thick chapati (3-4 total). Brush oil and dry flour between layers, stack 3-4 chapatis together. 4. Roll stacked chapatis into thin single sheet using rolling pin. 5. Heat tawa and cook layered sheet briefly (10-15 seconds per side with napkin turning), separate sheets while still hot. Trim to square shape. 6. Stack cooled sheets, roll tightly, cut into thin strips with sharp knife, separate and set aside. 7. For chicken: Wash and cut boneless chicken breast into lengthwise strips. 8. Mix marinade (ketchup, kashmiri chilli paste, green chilli paste, red chilli powder, garam masala, soy sauce, ginger garlic paste, egg, refined flour, cornflour, vinegar, salt, black pepper). 9. Coat chicken strips thoroughly in marinade. 10. Roughly cut noodles into smaller pieces, spread on flat surface, coat marinated chicken strips with noodles, press and flatten with hands to coat thoroughly. 11. Heat oil in wok on medium heat. 12. Deep fry coated chicken strips until tender and golden brown and crispy. 13. Serve hot with sweet chilli sauce or dip of choice.

name: Veg Lollipop
ingredients: oil 1 tbsp plus 2 tbsp for coating
instructions: 1. In pan add oil with ginger, garlic, green chillies, cook 1 minute on high heat. 2. Add carrots and remaining vegetables (beans, peas, corn, capsicum), toss and cook on high heat 2-3 minutes keeping vegetables crunchy. 3. Remove vegetables to bowl and cool completely. 4. In large bowl add boiled grated potatoes, cooled sauteed vegetables, all powdered spices (kashmiri chilli, coriander, cumin, turmeric, black salt, amchur, garam masala, kasuri methi), fresh coriander, fresh mint, salt, black pepper. 5. Mix all ingredients well by hand, if mixture is too moist add breadcrumbs to bind. 6. For lollipop shaping method 1: Take spoonful of mixture in hand, press ice cream stick into bottom half, press mixture around stick to form lollipop shape, ready for coating. 7. For lollipop shaping method 2: Take spoonful of mixture, form into round ball by applying pressure and rotating in hand, insert breadstick later. 8. Make slurry coating by whisking refined flour, salt, water in separate bowl. 9. Coat lollipops by dipping in slurry first then panko breadcrumbs (hold ice cream stick for method 1, use hands for method 2). 10. Heat oil in deep pan or kadhai until medium hot. 11. Carefully drop coated lollipops into hot oil. 12. Fry while lightly stirring at intervals until crisp and golden brown. 13. Remove using spider and place on paper towel lined plate. 14. Break breadsticks in half and insert into round-shaped lollipops. 15. Serve with schezwan sauce and green chutney.

name: Kaffir Lime Chicken Tikka
instructions: Wash and slice chicken breast thin. Apply first marination with salt, lemon juice and ginger garlic paste for 15 minutes. Make paste from kaffir lime leaves, green chillies, mint, coriander, ginger garlic paste and curd. Mix cured marinade paste with marinated chicken and refrigerate for at least 1 hour. Skewer chicken and cook in tandoor for 6-8 minutes or pan-fry until tender. Brush with butter. For chutney: heat oil, add mustard seeds, ginger, green chillies and curry leaves. Add pineapple juice and bring to simmer. Add chopped pineapple and spices. Cook until reduced to less than half, add ketchup, vinegar and sugar. Serve hot together.

name: Kolhapuri Misal Pav
instructions: Boil sprouted moth in 1.5-2 litres water with turmeric and salt. For wet vatan: heat oil and toast whole spices, add onions, cook until soft, add remaining ingredients, cover and cook 15 minutes, cool and grind to paste. For tarri: heat oil, add onions, masala and chilli powder. For matkichi ussad: temper oil with mustard seeds, add green chillies, add cooked moth with spices and salt. For batayachi bhaji: temper with seeds, add potatoes, spices and coriander. Assembly: layer batayachi bhaji, matkichi ussad, farsan, tarri, kat, coriander, onion, lemon and pav. Serve with chaas and dahi.

name: Potli Samosa
instructions: Make dough: combine flour, salt, ajwain and ghee, rub between hands until resembles breadcrumbs. Add water gradually and knead for 5-6 minutes. Cover and rest 30 minutes. For aloo mixture: coarsely grind whole spices, heat ghee and add spice mix with peas, add diced potatoes and remaining ingredients, cook on high flame spreading on pan to get brown edges, flip and repeat 2-3 times. Roll dough thin, trim edges to rectangle and cut 4-inch squares. Add filling on squares, lift edges together between thumb and index finger and seal. Fry in medium-hot oil on low flame until golden brown (15-20 minutes). Remove and drain in sieve.

name: Dahi Batata Puri
instructions: For green chutney: grind coriander, mint, ginger, green chillies, chana dal, kala namak, jeera powder, amchur, salt and ice cubes. For tamarind chutney: soak imli and khajoor, grind to paste, strain through sieve, add jaggery and spices, cook 30 minutes on medium flame stirring occasionally until thin syrup. For dahi bhalla masala: dry roast cumin seeds, add peppercorns and chillies, cool and grind with kala namak and chaat masala. For spice mix: same as dahi bhalla masala. Assembly: strain dahi, mix with sugar and black salt, add ice. Mix boiled potato with salt and coriander. Break top of pani puri with thumb, fill with potato, chutneys, dahi, boondi, moong, sev, repeat layers, add spice mix, pomegranate, chana dal and coriander. Serve immediately.

name: Paneer Shashlik Sizzler
instructions: For paneer shashlik: dice paneer and cut capsicum and onion to match size. Skewer together with bamboo skewer. Mix oil, ginger garlic paste, soy sauce, salt and black pepper. Apply marinade to skewers. Grill on medium heat until golden on all sides. For charred onions: heat oil and butter on high flame, add thickly sliced onions with sugar and salt. Char from both sides until dark brown. For sauteed veggies: add garlic and stir on high flame, add french beans with splash of water, add carrots and remaining spices, cook on high flame until tender. For garlic pepper sauce: melt brown butter, add flour and cook 2-3 minutes, gradually add stock stirring to avoid lumps, add garlic paste, coriander stems, soy sauce, green chilli sauce, salt, crushed black pepper and sugar, cook 7-8 minutes removing scum. Assembly: heat sizzler pan on highest flame, layer cabbage, rice, veggies, onions, fries, top with paneer shashlik, pour hot sauce over, garnish with cheese. Serve immediately.

name: schezwan chutney
instructions: Deseed and soak kashmiri red chillies in hot water for 1 hour (or boil 20-25 mins). Grind soaked chillies into fine paste with minimal water. Heat oil in wok on high flame. Add star anise, garlic, ginger, celery, stir and cook until garlic turns golden brown (4-5 mins). Add red chilli paste and green chilli paste with salt, cook for 15 minutes while stirring frequently until color darkens and texture becomes crumbly. Add ketchup and remaining ingredients, stir well, cook 3-4 minutes. Cool completely before transferring to airtight container. Can be refrigerated for 1-1.5 months.

name: veg bhuna masala
ingredients: kashmiri red chilli powder 2 tbsp + 1 tsp
instructions: Heat ghee and oil in handi on high flame. Add cumin seeds and let crackle. Add chopped onions, stir and cook on medium flame until completely golden brown (12-15 mins). Add garlic, ginger, green chillies, stir and cook on medium flame for 2-3 minutes until onions turn completely golden brown. Lower flame. In separate bowl, mix curd with all powdered spices using whisk. Add curd mixture to handi with coriander stems, stir and cook on low flame for 1 minute. Increase flame and cook masala until ghee separates (4-5 mins). Add tomato puree and salt, stir well, cook on high flame for 5 minutes. Add hot water, stir, cover and cook on low flame for 20 minutes, stirring at regular intervals. Add hot water if masala dries. Once cooked and browned, add garam masala, kasuri methi, fresh coriander. In separate pan, heat 1.5 tsp ghee, add 1.5 tsp cumin seeds, add french beans and carrots, toss 1 minute, add baby corn, broccoli, peas, sweet corn, toss 1 minute, add to gravy. In same pan, heat 0.5 tsp ghee with 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, add onion petals and capsicum, toss 30 seconds, add to gravy, stir well, cook 1-2 minutes. Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve hot with tandoori rotis.

name: jain pav bhaji
instructions: For pav bhaji masala: Heat pan on high flame until hot, lower heat, add whole spices and roast until fragrant, transfer to plate and cool to room temperature, grind with powdered spices to make fine powder. For hing water: Mix hing with water. For red chilli paste: Soak deseeded kashmiri red chillies in water, grind to fine runny paste. Boil raw bananas for 10-15 minutes, peel and mash. Heat butter and oil in large tawa or pan, add cumin seeds, add chopped capsicum, cook 1 minute. Add chopped tomatoes, boiled peas, cauliflower, green chilli paste, coriander powder, turmeric, red chilli powder, pav bhaji masala, salt, fresh coriander, hing water, and 60% of red chilli paste. Mix all vegetables well, mash with potato masher while leaving some texture. Add hot water to desired consistency, mix well, cook on medium-high flame. When base is ready, simmer on low flame. For tadka: Heat butter, add cumin seeds, pav bhaji masala, garam masala, kasuri methi, remaining 40% red chilli paste, cook 1-2 minutes, add to bhaji, mix well. Check seasoning, adjust salt, add fresh coriander leaves, butter, and lemon juice, cook 2-3 minutes. For pav: Slit pav buns, heat tawa or pan on medium heat, add butter with red chilli paste and fresh coriander, place pav on tawa, coat with butter mixture, cook briefly until soft (or toast for crispier texture). Serve hot bhaji with buttered pav and accompaniments of cucumber, tomato, and lemon.

name: prawns ghee roast
instructions: Roast coriander seeds, cumin seeds, black peppercorns, fenugreek seeds, mustard seeds, and poppy seeds on low flame until fragrant. Deseed red chillies using scissors. Soak deseeded chillies and cashews in hot water. Transfer soaked chillies, cashews, and roasted spices to mixer grinder along with jaggery, garlic, tamarind paste, and salt. Grind into fine paste using minimal water. De-vein prawns and wash thoroughly. Marinate prawns with salt, turmeric powder, and lemon juice, set aside. Heat 3 tbsp ghee in pan on high flame, add prepared paste, cook on medium flame while stirring continuously until it darkens and becomes crumbly. Add some hot water to adjust consistency, switch off flame. In separate pan, heat remaining 3 tbsp ghee on high flame. Add marinated prawns and cook nicely from both sides, stirring on high flame (do not overcook or they become chewy). Add cooked prawns to masala, stir well, add curry leaves, lemon juice, and coriander powder, stir and cook on high flame for 2 minutes. Taste for salt and adjust accordingly. Serve hot.

name: paneer makhani burger
instructions: For makhani sauce: Heat 2 tbsp oil in wok on medium-high flame, add 1 tsp cumin seeds, add sliced onions and cook until light golden brown. Add all makhani puree ingredients (garlic, ginger, green chillies, green cardamom, bay leaf, coriander stem, kashmiri red chillies, tomatoes, salt, cashew nuts, turmeric, kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder), stir and cook for 10-15 minutes until tomatoes become mushy (cover to speed process). Add 300-400 ml hot water, stir well, lower flame, cover and cook for 10 minutes. Cool mixture to room temperature, transfer to mixer grinder and grind to fine paste using very little water. Strain puree using sieve. For tadka: Heat 3 tbsp butter in pan on medium heat, add oil and heat slightly, add 2 tbsp chopped garlic, 1 inch julienne ginger, 1-2 chopped green chillies, stir and cook on high flame for 1-2 minutes. Lower flame, add 1 tbsp kashmiri red chilli powder quickly, add strained makhani puree, stir and cook for 5-6 minutes. Adjust consistency with hot water (should be spreadable). Add garam masala, kasuri methi, honey, fresh cream, fresh coriander, stir and mix well. Check and adjust salt. For paneer patty: Mix refined flour, corn flour, ginger garlic paste, kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, dry mango powder, black salt, garam masala, kasuri methi, 1 tsp oil in a bowl. Add water in batches to make smooth lump-free batter. In another bowl, mix panko bread crumbs with salt. Cut paneer into less than 1 cm thick slices, dip in batter, coat thoroughly with bread crumbs. Heat oil in wok for frying, deep fry coated patty on medium-high flame until crisp and golden brown. Drain excess oil on sieve. Assembly: Layer toasted burger bun with crispy paneer patty, makhani sauce, mint mayo, romaine lettuce, and onion rings. Serve hot.

name: aloo bhuna
instructions: Set handi on high heat, add oil, once heated add cumin seeds and let crackle. Add chopped onions, stir well and cook on medium flame until completely golden brown. Once onions turn light golden brown, add chopped garlic, ginger, green chillies, stir well and continue cooking until onions turn completely golden brown (7-8 minutes total). Lower flame. In separate bowl, mix curd with all powdered spices (turmeric, kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder) using whisk to ensure no lumps. Add curd mixture to handi with coriander stems, stir and cook on low flame for 1 minute. Increase flame and cook until oil separates. Add tomato puree and salt, stir well, cook on high flame for 5 minutes. Add 500 ml hot water, stir well, cover handi with lid and cook on low flame for 20 minutes, stirring at regular intervals. If gravy turns dry, add hot water as required. Open lid and taste for seasoning, adjust salt accordingly. Add garam masala, kasuri methi, fresh coriander, stir well. Bhuna gravy is ready. In separate pan, heat 2 tbsp butter, add sliced onions, capsicum, green chillies, toss on high flame for 1 minute. Add boiled and diced potatoes with chaat masala, stir well and cook only for 1 minute. Now add prepared bhuna gravy with hot water as required, stir well and cook for 2-3 minutes. Finish with fresh coriander. Serve hot with rumali roti, green and red chutney.

name: chicken manchurian
ingredients: chicken stock or hot water 150 ml
instructions: Clean chicken breast well and cut into 1-inch cubes. Transfer chicken cubes to mixing bowl, add salt, black pepper powder, 2 tsp vinegar, 2 tsp soy sauce, egg white, and 3 tbsp cornflour, mix well to coat chicken pieces thoroughly. Set oil in wok on medium-high heat, deep fry coated chicken in hot oil on medium heat. Do not disturb chicken initially for 15-20 seconds while a light coating forms, then stir carefully to fry evenly and prevent sticking/burning. Fry until chicken is cooked, tender, and light golden in color. Keep aside. Cut red bell pepper: trim head and bottom, cut through sideways, trim inside seeds with minimal wastage, cut into thick strips then triangles. Cut capsicum the same way. Cut onions: trim head and bottom, cut in two halves, remove center bulb, cut diagonally to triangle shape, separate each layer to get petal shapes. Set wok on high flame, let heat nicely, add 1 tbsp oil, swirl to coat wok, add 2 tbsp chopped garlic, 1 tbsp chopped ginger, 2-3 chopped green chillies, stir and cook on high flame for 1 minute. Add cut vegetables (bell pepper, capsicum, onion), stir and cook on high flame for 1 minute, add oyster sauce, 1 tsp soy sauce, 1 tsp vinegar, brown sugar, and 150 ml chicken stock, stir and cook for 1 minute on high flame. Mix 2 tbsp cornflour with 100 ml water to make smooth slurry, add to wok, stir and cook until sauce thickens. Add fried chicken and spring onion greens, stir and mix well to coat with sauce. Garnish with freshly chopped spring onion greens. Serve hot with schezwan sauce or with Indian bread and rice.

name: Anda Ghotala
instructions: Set a pan on high heat, add 1 tsp oil and 2 tbsp butter. Add 1/2 chopped medium onion, 1/4 cup chopped green garlic, small handful fresh coriander, and 1 tsp green chilli paste. Stir and cook on high flame for 1-2 minutes until onions are cooked. Lower the flame and add turmeric powder, coriander powder, jeera powder, garam masala, red chilli powder, and black pepper powder to taste. Stir well and add hot water, cook on high flame for 1 minute. Use a potato masher to mash the masala properly. Grate 2 boiled eggs into this mixture. Add salt to taste, keep stirring and adjust consistency by adding hot water as needed while cooking on high flame. Separately set a small pan and heat oil in it. Break 1 raw egg directly into the hot oil and season with salt, red chilli powder, black pepper powder and coriander. Do not overcook; the yolk should remain runny. Once the half-fried egg is ready, add it to the ghotala, break it and mix nicely using a spatula without overcooking. For masala pav: slit 2 pav from the centre, add 1 tbsp soft butter to a heated pan and sprinkle coriander and kashmiri red chilli powder, lay the pav on the pan and coat nicely. Serve anda ghotala hot with masala pav.

name: Mumbai Green Mutton Kheema
instructions: First prepare green paste: in a mixer grinder jar, add 1 cup fresh coriander, 10-12 mint leaves, optional 3-4 spinach leaves, 3-4 green chillies, 1 tbsp coriander seeds, 1 tsp cumin seeds, 2 green cardamom, 4-5 black peppercorns, 1 black cardamom, 2-3 ice cubes, and water as required. Grind into a fine paste. Set a tawa on high heat and add 1/3 cup oil. Add 6-7 chopped medium onions and cook on medium-high flame until golden brown. Once golden, add 4-5 tbsp ginger garlic paste, stir and cook for 3-4 minutes until raw flavour of garlic is gone. Add 1 kg mutton keema, salt to taste and turmeric powder. Keep stirring to prevent the meat from clumping up, cook on high flame for 10-15 minutes. Now add the prepared green paste to the keema. In a separate bowl, combine 1/3 cup curd with 2 tsp spicy red chilli powder, 3 tbsp coriander powder, and 1 tbsp cumin powder, whisk everything well and add to the keema. Stir and cook for 15-20 minutes on low flame, keeping stirring at regular intervals. Add 1/2 cup green peas, 1/4 cup dill leaves, and 3-4 slit green chillies, stir and cook on low flame until keema is fully cooked. Add hot water in small quantities if keema gets dry. Take a pan and add 1-2 tbsp butter, once melted add 1 tbsp kasuri methi, stir and cook briefly. Add this tadka to keema and mix well. Add garam masala, stir well, check seasoning and adjust salt accordingly. Finally add big handful fresh coriander and stir everything well. Serve hot with pav.

name: Moong Churi Paratha
instructions: Soak 1/2 cup green grams (moong dal) in water for 30 minutes, then drain well. In a large bowl, mix 2 cups wheat flour with 1 tsp salt. Add the drained moong dal, 1/2 tsp coarsely ground red chilli, 2 finely chopped green chillies, 1 tsp grated ginger, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1/2 tsp cumin seeds, 1 pinch asafoetida, 1/2 tsp turmeric powder, 1/4 tsp garam masala, 2 tsp oil, and 2 tbsp chopped coriander leaves. Add water gradually (about 1/4 cup) and knead into a soft and smooth dough. Cover and rest for 15-20 minutes. Divide dough into 4-5 equal portions and roll each into a thin circle about 7-8 inches diameter on a lightly floured surface. Heat a griddle (tawa) or pan on medium-high heat. Place paratha on the griddle and cook for 1-2 minutes until light brown spots appear. Flip the paratha, apply a small amount of oil or ghee on top, and cook for another 1 minute until golden. Flip again, apply oil on this side and cook until crispy and golden brown. Remove to a plate and keep warm. For roasted tomato chutney: heat a pan on medium flame and place 2 whole tomatoes directly on the heat. Roast them, turning occasionally, until the skin splits completely and they become soft (about 5 minutes). Remove and cool. Once cooled, add to a mixer along with 3-4 tbsp fresh coriander leaves, 3-4 green chillies, 1/2 inch ginger baton, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp black salt, and 1/2 tsp roasted cumin. Blend until you get a coarse chutney consistency (not too fine). Serve hot moong churi paratha with roasted tomato chutney on the side.

name: leftover rice stuffed paratha
instructions: Prepare dough with wheat flour, salt and oil. Knead with water gradually to make soft dough. Rest for 20 minutes. Heat oil in pan, roast cumin seeds, add ginger and green chilli. Add turmeric and coriander powder, roast briefly. Add cooked rice, red chilli powder, dry mango powder, garam masala and salt. Fry the rice mixture, add water and coriander leaves. Cook for 1-2 minutes and cool. Roll dough portions, stuff with rice filling, seal corners and flatten. Roll into paratha sheet, apply ghee and fry on tawa until golden brown spots appear on both sides.

name: crispy potato 65
instructions: Boil and cut potatoes into pieces. Mix refined flour, corn flour, salt, red chilli powder and curry leaves. Coat potato pieces in the mixture. Deep fry until crispy. For sauce: heat oil, add green and red chillies, curry leaves. Mix curd with red chilli powder and corn flour, add to oil. Cook until sauce thickens. Combine fried potatoes with the sauce. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: kutchhi dabeli
instructions: Roast all dabeli masala spices individually and grind together to powder. For spicy chutney: soak and grind dried chillis with peanut, cumin and asafoetida, add lemon juice and salt. Mix peanut powder with dabeli masala and red chilli powder. Boil and mash potatoes, season with dabeli masala, oil, coriander leaves, coconut and pomegranate seeds. Hollow pav, butter the inside, add mashed potato filling, add sev, top with spicy chutney and sweet chutney. Toast on tawa and serve.

name: instant dosa
instructions: Grind peanuts, coconut, green chilli and ginger with salt and lemon juice to make chutney. Grind rice and black gram with fenugreek seeds, mix with curd and oil. Add salt and baking soda just before use to create fluffiness. Heat oil, add mustard and cumin seeds, curry leaves. Add tomato, ginger and green chilli, turmeric and coriander powder. Add boiled mashed potatoes and salt to make aloo masala. Heat tawa, pour dosa batter and spread thin, add aloo masala in center, fold and cook until crispy. Prepare tadka with oil, mustard seeds, red chilli and curry leaves separately. Serve dosa with peanut chutney, aloo masala and tadka.

name: chawal ki pinni
instructions: Roast rice flour until aromatic. Heat full cream milk and simmer until dense (rabadi consistency). When rice is aromatic, add desiccated coconut and roast for 2 minutes. Turn off heat, add ghee and mix well. When milk reaches rabadi consistency, add it to roasted flour mixture and mix thoroughly. Add crushed cardamom, almond flakes, pistachio flakes and chopped raisins. Divide mixture into two equal parts. Add boora to one half and mix to make sugar pinni. Prepare jaggery syrup at one-string consistency and mix with second half to make jaggery pinni. Shape into balls or serve as is.

name: paneer nargisi kofta curry
instructions: Crumble paneer and season with turmeric, red chilli powder and salt. Add chopped raisins and cashews. Mix corn flour, refined flour, salt and black pepper. Soak soya vadi, squeeze and add to paneer mixture. Form into kofta balls and deep fry until golden. For gravy: heat oil, add whole spices (bay leaf, cinnamon, black pepper, black cardamom, cloves, cumin). Add turmeric and coriander powder, kasuri methi. Add chopped tomatoes, green chilli, ginger and kashmiri red chilli. Cook until tomatoes soften. Add ground cashews and melon seeds. Add butter and cooked koftas. Add salt and garam masala. Simmer for 5-10 minutes. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: milk powder peda
instructions: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan. Add milk and bring to simmer. Add milk powder gradually, stirring continuously to avoid lumps. Cook until mixture becomes thick and starts leaving sides of pan. Add sugar powder and mix well. Add crushed cardamom and stir. Turn off heat and let cool for 2-3 minutes. Shape into peda using hands or molds while still warm. Garnish with pistachio flakes.

name: aloo puff patties
instructions: Make puff pastry dough by rubbing butter into flour, salt, and lemon juice. Roll and fold multiple times to create layers. Boil potatoes and mix with peas, spices, ginger, and herbs to make filling. Stuff pastry sheets with spiced potato mixture. Fry until golden and puffed. Serve hot.

name: rodga
instructions: Make stiff dough with wheat flour, semolina, salt, carom seeds, and ghee. Prepare dal by tempering cumin, cloves, pepper, and other spices in ghee. Add tuvar and mung dal, cook until soft. Make paste of tomato, ginger, green chilli, and add to dal with coriander powder. Form dough into balls or logs and cook in dal gravy until dough pieces float. Finish with red chilli tadka.

name: moong dal ras vada
instructions: Soak moong dal for 2-3 hours and grind to fine paste. Mix ground dal with grated paneer. Prepare sugar syrup with water and cook until it reaches single-thread consistency. Form small vadas from dal-paneer mixture and deep fry until golden brown. Immediately dip fried vadas into hot sugar syrup. Garnish with cardamom powder and saffron strands.

name: palak sakauda chaat
instructions: Chop spinach finely and mix with gram flour, green chilli, salt, and spices. Form spinach pakoras and deep fry until golden. For gravy: roast whole spices (black pepper, cloves, cardamom, cinnamon, nutmeg) and grind. Temper oil with cumin and asafoetida, add tomato-ginger paste. Add ground spice mix with coriander and chilli powders. Add water and simmer. Add fried pakoras to gravy and cook. Finish with amchur powder.

name: malai cake
instructions: For cake: mix refined flour, baking powder, and baking soda. In another bowl, beat malai with sugar powder until fluffy. Add oil and milk. Fold flour mixture into wet ingredients gently. Pour into greased pan and cook over low heat until set. For rabari malai: boil milk, add corn flour slurry to thicken. Add sugar and cook until slightly thick. Add saffron and cardamom. Cool and use as topping. Top cake with rabari malai and garnish with nuts.

name: doda barfi
instructions: Soak wheat grains overnight and sprout in wet cloth for 2-3 days. Grind sprouted wheat coarsely. Add yogurt and full cream milk to ground wheat in kadai. Cook over low-medium heat, stirring occasionally until milk thickens and curdles. Prepare glucose syrup by cooking sugar with water until single-thread stage. Mix brown sugar into wheat mixture. Add grated coconut, chopped cashews, ground cardamom, nutmeg powder. Mix cocoa powder in milk and add to mixture for color. Stir in glucose syrup. Finally add ghee and cook until mixture curdles and pulls together. Pour into greased barfi tray, press evenly. Garnish with nuts and let set for 2 hours. Cut into pieces.

name: Tandoori Naan
instructions: Prepare dough by mixing instant active yeast, sugar, refined flour, salt, milk and oil. Cover and let rise for 30 minutes. Make potato stuffing by mixing boiled mashed potatoes with green chilli, ginger, turmeric powder, coriander powder, red chilli, dry mango powder, garam masala and coriander leaves. Divide dough into portions, roll out, fill with potato stuffing, roll again and cook on tawa or pan over medium heat until golden brown spots appear on both sides.

name: Pakwan Chaat
instructions: Mix refined flour, gram flour, carom seeds, salt and oil with water to make stiff dough. Let rest 20 minutes. Divide dough into lumps, roll thin sheets, prick with fork, fry some as mini pakwans and some as large rounds. For filling, mash boiled potatoes and mix with chopped tomato, green chilli, ginger, salts, red chilli, dry mango powder, cumin powder and coriander leaves. Layer potato stuffing on fried pakwans, top with curd, chutneys, black salt, cumin powder, coriander leaves and sev. Serve immediately.

name: Layered Mawa Gujiya
instructions: Prepare dough by mixing refined flour with ghee and water. Roll out dough sheets. For filling, mix mawa, almond flakes, chopped cashews, charoli seeds, grated dry coconut, raisins, semolina and ghee. Mix sugar powder and ground cardamom. Create layers by spreading mawa mixture between dough layers. Roll and shape into gujiya form. Prepare sugar syrup (sata) by mixing ghee and cornflour. Cook gujiya and coat with sata.

name: Sama Rice Fara
instructions: Cook sama rice with boiling water, rock salt, black pepper and oil. Add finely chopped green chilli, ginger, and carrot. Mix in fresh coriander leaves and sesame seeds. Prepare chutney by blending coriander leaves, green chillies, ginger, tomato, rock salt and black pepper. Serve steamed fara with coriander chutney on the side.

name: Cheese ke Sholey
instructions: Grate cheese and paneer together. Finely chop capsicum, carrot, green chilli and ginger. Mix grated cheese, paneer, chopped vegetables, salt, chilli flakes and coriander leaves. Make coating paste by mixing refined flour with water. Remove bread crust and flatten slices. Spread cheese-paneer mixture on bread, roll tightly, coat with flour paste, and deep fry in hot oil until golden brown. Serve hot with ketchup or chutney.

name: Instant Rabdi
instructions: Heat full cream milk in a heavy-bottomed pan. Tear white bread into small pieces and add to the hot milk, stirring occasionally. Add condensed milk and simmer for 5-10 minutes. Crush cardamom pods and soak kesar strands in a tablespoon of warm milk. Pour rabdi into serving bowls, garnish with soaked kesar, almond flakes, chopped cashews and crushed cardamom. Serve warm or chilled.

name: Rice Flour Masala Paratha
instructions: Bring water to boil with salt and cumin seeds. Turn off flame and add rice flour, stirring well. Cover and let cool for 5 minutes. Transfer to bowl and mash. Once cooled, add desiccated coconut, grated ginger, chopped green chilli and coriander leaves. Mix and knead into smooth dough. Apply oil to hands and knead again. Let rest 15 minutes. Roll out thick sheets dusting with dry rice flour. Cook on tawa with ghee over medium heat until brown spots appear on both sides. Serve hot.

name: Schezwan Sauce
instructions: Boil water with Kashmiri red chilies and dried red chilies for 20 minutes. Transfer soaked chilies to blender with 2 tablespoons water and ginger chunks, blend to fine paste. Heat oil in pan and roast red chilli paste for 1-2 minutes. Add crushed black pepper, oregano and coriander stems, roast for 2 minutes. Add salt, sugar and 1/2 cup water, cover and cook over low heat for 15 minutes. Add soya sauce, tomato sauce and vinegar, mix well. Cool completely. Store in airtight jar.

name: coconut makhana paag
ingredients: white or black pepper 1 tsp crushed
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan. Roast makhana and dry coconut. Add crushed spices (pepper, cardamom, nutmeg). Mix in sugar and combine well until everything is coated. Continue roasting until golden and fragrant.

name: karonda chilli pickle
instructions: Clean karonda and green chilli. Heat mustard oil and add all tempering seeds. Once seeds crackle, add turmeric, red chilli powder, and asafoetida. Add karonda and green chilli with salt. Roast until oil separates. Add vinegar and mix well. Store in glass jar.

name: crispy aloo tikki
instructions: Mash boiled potatoes with rice flour, gram flour, salt and pepper. Prepare filling by cooking peas, paneer, ginger, green chilli with spices. Divide potato mixture into portions, stuff with paneer filling, shape into patties. Fry in oil until golden and crispy on both sides.

name: aloo matar malai
instructions: Fry chopped potatoes in oil until golden. Add cumin seeds, turmeric, coriander powder, and dry fenugreek. Add tomato-ginger-chilli paste and roast until oil separates. Add red chilli powder and roast. Add peas and cook covered for 2 minutes. Add fresh cream and roast. Add potatoes, salt, and water. Cook covered for 5 minutes. Add maggi masala and coriander leaves. Mix well and serve.

name: veg manchurian momos
ingredients: oil 1 tsp and 1 tbsp and 1 tbsp
salt 1/4 tsp and more than 1/2 tsp and less than 1/2 tsp
cabbage 2 cup grated and 2 tbsp grated
green capsicum 1/4 cup and 2 tbsp chopped
instructions: Make dough with refined flour, oil, and salt. Knead well. Prepare filling by cooking ginger, green chilli, cabbage, capsicum, carrot with salt and pepper. Divide dough into portions, roll thin, fill with vegetable mixture, and fold into momo shape. Steam momos for 8-10 minutes. For sauce: heat oil, add ginger, chilli, vegetables, soya sauce, red chilli sauce, tomato sauce, vinegar, and corn flour slurry. Cook until thick. Toss steamed momos in sauce and serve.

name: crispy potato chips
instructions: Slice potatoes thinly using a mandoline or knife. Soak slices in water briefly to remove starch. Pat dry with cloth. Heat oil for deep frying. Fry potato slices in batches until golden and crispy. Remove with slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Sprinkle salt and black pepper while still hot. Serve as snack.

name: sago fruit dessert
ingredients: full cream milk 1/2 litre and 1/2 cup
instructions: Soak sago in water for 30 minutes, then drain. Boil milk in a pan and add soaked sago. Cook on medium heat, stirring frequently until sago becomes transparent (about 15-20 minutes). Cool the mixture. Cut mango into pieces. Add all fruits (mango, grapes, pomegranate, raisins) to cooled sago mixture. Add condensed milk and ground cardamom. Mix well. Fold in almond and cashew pieces. Chill until serving. Serve cold or at room temperature.

name: Crispy Malai Layered Vermicelli
instructions: Toast the vermicelli in ghee. Prepare custard using custard powder and milk. Layer the roasted vermicelli with malai, sugar, and custard in a mold. Add condensed milk, cardamom, nuts, and saffron soaked in milk. Chill before serving.

name: Soya Chaap Kathi Rolls
instructions: Make dough with flour, salt, and oil. Knead and rest. Cook soya chaap pieces with masala (cumin, turmeric, coriander, chilli, garam masala). Add tomato, ginger, green chilli, capsicum, and cream to the soya chaap. Roll out dough into thin circles, fill with cooked soya chaap mixture, fold into rolls. Brush with sauce, mayonnaise, and green chutney. Serve hot.

name: Instant Sandwich Dhokla
instructions: Soak rice, urad dal, moong dal, fenugreek seeds, and flattened rice. Grind into a smooth batter. Ferment the batter or use Eno salt for instant fermenting. Prepare green chutney with coriander, green chilli, ginger, raw mango, and cumin seeds. Prepare peanut chutney with roasted peanuts, green chilli, and lemon. Layer dhokla batter into steamer. Prepare tadka with mustard, sesame, curry leaves, and chilli. Pour tadka over cooked dhokla. Serve as sandwich with chutneys.

name: Soya Chaap Dum Biryani
instructions: Cook basmati rice with whole spices (bay leaf, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, pepper) until 70% done. Prepare soya chaap gravy: roast cashew, add tomato, ginger, green chilli, cook with yogurt and ground spices (turmeric, coriander, chilli powder). Fry cauliflower and carrot pieces. Layer rice and soya chaap gravy alternately in a heavy-bottomed pot. Soak saffron in milk. Pour saffron milk over the layers. Cover and steam (dum) on high heat for 45 minutes. Garnish with coriander leaves and ginger julienne.

name: Mawa Mishri Laddu
instructions: Crumble mawa finely into a bowl. Grate paneer into the mawa. Powder the mishri (rock candy) until fine. Mix the powdered mishri with the mawa and paneer. Add coarsely ground cardamom to the mixture. Roll the mixture into balls (laddus) and garnish with pistachio flakes. Store in an airtight container.

name: Andhra Ginger Pickle
instructions: Heat 4 tbsp oil in a kadai. Add mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds, coriander seeds, chana dal, urad dal, and cumin seeds. Roast on low heat until dals turn brown. Add chopped ginger and fry until crispy. Add asafoetida and kashmiri red chilli and roast together. Cool completely and grind the roasted mixture. In the grinder, add jaggery, tamarind pulp, water, salt, and ground spices. Grind coarsely. Heat 1 tbsp oil separately. Add mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds, dried red chilies, and curry leaves. Pour this tadka into the ground pickle mixture and mix well. The pickle is ready to serve.

name: paneer momos
instructions: 1. Make dough with maida, salt, and water. Let rest 30 minutes. 2. Make red chutney: heat oil, add cumin seeds, hing, turmeric, add tomatoes and red chillies with spices, cook until soft, blend. 3. Make white sauce: blend boiled potato, cream, salt, sugar, milk. 4. Make filling: heat oil, add ginger and green chilli, add capsicum and cabbage, fry briefly, add grated paneer, salt, pepper, cool. 5. Roll dough into 3-inch sheets, add 1 tsp filling to each sheet. 6. Pleat edges and seal momos. 7. Steam on greased sieve for 10 minutes. 8. Serve with chutneys.

name: mint spinach sev
instructions: 1. Grind mint leaves, spinach leaves, and green chillies together. 2. Strain and extract juice from the paste. 3. Mix chickpea flour, oil, salt, and juice in a bowl. 4. Add water gradually to make soft dough of right consistency. Mix well for 3-4 minutes and let rest 10 minutes. 5. Heat oil for frying. 6. Fit sieve with fine pores in sev maker machine. Grease hands with oil. 7. Put dough in machine, extrude into hot oil when sufficiently hot. 8. Fry sev, flip once foam settles, fry until golden (1-1.5 minutes total). 9. Remove and drain on plate. 10. Cool and store in airtight container for 1-2 months.

name: masala fries
instructions: 1. Cut potatoes into finger-like fries. 2. Boil or par-cook the fries until just cooked. 3. Mix salt, red chilli powder, coriander powder, carom seeds, and ginger-chilli paste. 4. Coat the cooked fries with this spice mix. 5. Dust with ararot or corn flour for crispiness. 6. Deep fry until golden and crispy. 7. For chutney: grind coriander leaves, green chillies, ginger with salt and black salt. Add roasted cumin and curd. 8. Serve hot fries with chutney.

name: vegetable mayonnaise sandwich
instructions: 1. Slice or julienne all vegetables (capsicum, cucumber, carrot, tomato). 2. Season vegetables with salt, black pepper, chilli flakes, green chilli, and coriander leaves. 3. Spread mayonnaise on bread slices. 4. Layer seasoned vegetables on bread. 5. For cool sandwich: place another bread slice on top, press gently. 6. For grilled sandwich: place on sandwich maker/grill and toast until golden. 7. For tawa sandwich: butter the outside and cook on tawa until golden and crispy. 8. Serve immediately.

name: achari bharwa baingan
instructions: 1. Halve eggplants lengthwise and cut slits without cutting through completely. 2. Roast mustard seeds, fennel seeds, fenugreek seeds in a dry pan briefly. 3. Add turmeric powder, coriander powder, red chilli, dry mango powder, cumin powder, garam masala, salt, and hing. 4. Mix in finely chopped green chilli and mustard oil to form spice filling paste. 5. Stuff eggplant halves generously with spice mixture. 6. Heat oil in a pan and shallow fry or bake eggplant until tender and golden. 7. Can also steam with the spice filling. 8. Serve hot as a main vegetable dish.

name: instant sambar
instructions: 1. Soak arhar dal in warm water for 20-25 minutes. 2. Chop vegetables into bite-size pieces: bottle gourd, pumpkin, carrot, tomato. Cut drumstick into 1-1.5 inch pieces. 3.
