Soak tamarind in warm water for 30 minutes. 4. Grind soaked dal coarsely. 5. Add ground dal and 1.5 cups water, chopped vegetables, turmeric powder, and salt to pressure cooker. Cook for 1 whistle, then 2 minutes on low heat. 6. Mash and strain tamarind pulp. 7. Add tamarind pulp, sambar masala, ginger, coriander leaves, and 1 more cup water to cooked sambar. Cook 3-4 minutes. 8. Prepare tadka: heat oil, add mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds, hing, curry leaves, red chillies, kashmiri chilli powder. 9. Pour tadka into sambar and mix well. 10. Serve hot.

name: suji veg tikka
instructions: 1. Mix gram flour, semolina, curd, hing, cumin seeds, turmeric powder, red chilli, green chilli, ginger in a bowl. 2. Add finely chopped peas, cauliflower, capsicum, carrot, salt, and coriander leaves. 3. Mix everything well to form a thick batter. 4. Grease a shallow pan and pour the mixture. 5. Cut into squares or diamond shapes and fry until golden on both sides. 6. For chutney: grind coriander leaves, ginger, hing with salt and black salt. Add dry mango powder. 7. Serve hot snacks with coriander chutney.

name: maida ke laddu
instructions: Mix maida with ghee and milk, rest 30 minutes. Sift to remove lumps. Roast maida in ghee on low-medium flame until fragrant. Add dry fruits (almonds, pistachios, chironji) and roast. Add desiccated coconut and roast for 1 minute. Add fresh cream and roast for 2 minutes. Cool mixture. Mix in powdered sugar, raisins, and crushed cardamom. Shape into laddoos by hand.

name: shahi shikanji
instructions: Soak almonds, cashews, and raisins. Blend soaked nuts with curd to make a smooth paste. Boil milk and add saffron strands. Cool the milk to room temperature. Mix curd-nut paste into the milk. Add sugar and ground cardamom. Mix well and serve chilled.

name: eggless chocolate banana cake
instructions: Mash 2 bananas and mix with sugar. Add oil and vanilla essence, mix well. Add lemon juice and milk. In separate bowl, sift together maida, cocoa powder, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Fold dry ingredients into wet mixture gently. Fold in chopped walnuts and chocolate chips. Grease and line a cooker-safe container. Pour batter and place in cooker on medium heat for 30-35 minutes or until toothpick comes out clean. Cool in cooker, then turn out.

name: mushroom malai curry
instructions: Wash mushrooms thoroughly, wipe dry, remove stalks, slice. Heat oil in pan on low flame. Add cumin, turmeric, coriander powder, and dried fenugreek, fry lightly. Make paste of tomatoes, green chilli, ginger, and cashews, add to pan. Add kashmiri red chilli powder, fry on low-medium flame while stirring. After roasting, add fresh cream and fry spices until they leave the oil. Add sliced mushrooms, salt, and coriander leaves, mix well, cook 1-2 minutes. Add water and cover, cook 3-4 minutes. Add maggi masala sachet, mix well, cover and cook 2 more minutes. Serve with roti or rice.

name: namkeen puran poli
instructions: Soak gram dal for 30 minutes, drain and boil until 3/4 cooked. Make dough with wheat flour, refined flour, salt, and ghee, add water gradually, knead smooth. Add soaked cooked gram dal to dough. In a pan, heat ghee, add carom seeds, cooked gram dal, green chillies, ginger, turmeric, coriander powder, cumin powder, red chilli powder, garam masala, dry mango powder, and coriander leaves. Cook mixture until dry. Cool filling. Divide dough into balls, roll thin between plastic sheets, place filling on one half, fold into triangle or circle, seal edges. Roast on tava with ghee until golden spots appear.

name: urad dal masala papad
ingredients: black gram flour (urad dal atta) 2 cups (300g)
instructions: Mix black gram flour with all spices (cumin seeds, black pepper, salt) and papad khar in a bowl. Add water gradually and knead into a stiff dough. Divide dough into equal portions. Roll each portion very thin between plastic sheets to form papad. Prick with fork to prevent puffing. Dry the papads in sun for 3-4 days until completely dry and brittle. Store in airtight container. To serve, deep fry in hot oil until puffed and crispy, or roast over flame, or microwave.

name: Cauliflower Kofta Curry
instructions: Prepare kofta: Blanch and grate 250g cauliflower, add salt, turmeric, red chilli, coriander powder, chopped green chilli, grated ginger, and 1/2 cup gram flour. Mix well and form into balls. Heat oil and deep fry koftas until golden, set aside. Prepare gravy: Heat 3 tbsp oil, temper with bay leaves, cinnamon stick, cloves, black pepper, black cardamom, cumin seeds. Add crushed cashews and fry briefly. Add chopped tomatoes, ginger batons, green chilli, cook until soft. Add turmeric, coriander, kashmiri red chilli, mix well. Add cream, cooked koftas, garam masala, and salt. Simmer for 5-7 minutes until well combined. Serve hot.

name: Makhana Panjiri
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan, roast fox nuts until crispy and light golden, remove and set aside. In the same ghee, roast melon seeds, almond flakes, cashews, and grated dry coconut separately until fragrant, each for 1-2 minutes. Crush green cardamom pods and remove seeds. Combine all roasted ingredients with cardamom seeds and boora. Mix thoroughly until well combined. Store in an airtight container. Serve at room temperature.

name: Chola Poori
instructions: Prepare poori dough: Mix refined flour with salt, oil, and sugar. Add water gradually to form a soft dough. Cover and rest for 20 minutes. Prepare matar chola: Heat oil in pan, add cumin seeds, pomegranate seeds, let crackle. Add ginger batons, green chilli, tomatoes, cook until soft. Add turmeric, coriander, red chilli powder, fresh peas, salt. Saute on medium heat for 7-8 minutes until peas are cooked. Add garam masala and roasted cumin powder. Finish with coriander leaves and crumbled paneer. For poori: Divide dough into small portions, roll into thin circles. Heat oil for frying to medium-high heat. Fry pooris one at a time until they puff up and turn golden on both sides. Serve hot pooris with matar chola.

name: Besan Peda
instructions: Heat ghee in a non-stick pan over medium heat. Add chickpea flour and roast for 3-4 minutes, stirring continuously until it turns golden and aromatic. Reduce heat and gradually add condensed milk, mixing continuously to avoid lumps. Add sugar powder and keep stirring for 2-3 minutes until the mixture thickens and starts leaving the sides of the pan. Transfer to a plate and let cool for 2-3 minutes. When cool enough to handle, shape into round pedas using your palms or a peda mold. Garnish with almond flakes. Store in an airtight container.

name: Raw Mango Sweet Chutney
instructions: Peel and chop raw mangoes into small pieces. In a heavy-bottomed pan, add chopped mangoes, jaggery, sugar, salt, and water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add ginger powder, cumin powder, garam masala, kashmiri red chilli powder, pitted dry dates, and raisins. Simmer for another 10-15 minutes until the mixture thickens and reaches a jam-like consistency, stirring frequently to prevent sticking. Cool completely before transferring to sterilized glass jars. Seal and store at room temperature for several months. Can be served with various chaats, pakoras, and snacks.

name: Gobi Manchurian Fried Rice
instructions: Cook basmati rice in salted water until 70% done, spread on a plate to cool completely for at least 2 hours. Prepare cauliflower fritters: Cut cauliflower into small florets, make a batter with refined flour, cornflour, salt, turmeric, kashmiri red chilli, and grated ginger. Dip florets in batter and deep fry until golden, set aside. Heat oil in a wok or large pan, add ginger, chopped green chilli and roast briefly. Add julienned carrot, capsicum, and chopped french beans, stir-fry for 2 minutes. Add cooled rice, breaking up any lumps, and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes. Add soya sauce, vinegar, red chilli sauce, salt, black pepper, and tomato sauce. Toss well. Top with fried gobi (cauliflower) pieces and serve immediately.

name: Green Peas Kachori
instructions: Prepare dough: Mix refined flour, salt, crushed carom seeds, and ghee thoroughly. Add water gradually to form a soft dough. Cover and rest for 20 minutes. Prepare filling: Grind green peas coarsely. Heat oil in a pan, add crushed cumin seeds, fennel seeds, coriander seeds, and let them crackle briefly. Add chopped green chilli, grated ginger, crushed black pepper, and roast for 30 seconds. Add ground peas, crushed red chilli, salt, dry mango powder, and garam masala. Saute on low-medium flame for 7-8 minutes until filling is cooked and dried. Cool on a plate. Assembly: Divide cooled dough into small portions, flatten into a shallow bowl shape, place a small ball of filling in the center, gather dough edges and seal. Flatten gently between palms. Fry 2-3 kachoris at a time in oil over low-medium heat until golden brown on both sides. Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

name: Mixed Vegetable Fried Rice
instructions: Cook basmati rice in salted water until done but still separate, spread on a plate to cool completely. Heat oil in a wok or large pan over high heat. Add grated ginger and finely chopped green chilli, stir-fry for 20-30 seconds. Add chopped carrot, beans, cabbage, and julienned green and yellow capsicums. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes on high heat. Add cooled rice, breaking up any lumps, and toss continuously for 2-3 minutes. Add green chilli sauce, soya sauce, vinegar, and salt to taste. Sprinkle maggi masala and toss everything together. Finish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot immediately.

name: Spicy Raw Banana Tawa Fry
instructions: Peel and slice raw bananas. Mix rice flour with salt, all spices, and lemon juice. Coat banana slices evenly with the spiced flour mixture. Heat oil on tawa and fry the coated banana slices until golden and crispy, turning occasionally. Serve hot.

name: Vegetable Tawa Pulao
instructions: Heat oil and butter, add ginger and green chilli. Add diced carrots, capsicum, beans, and tomatoes, cook until soft. Add cooked rice and all vegetables. Season with salt, red chilli powder, and tomato sauce. Mix well on high heat until everything is combined and hot. Serve immediately.

name: Raw Mango Murabba
instructions: Cut raw mangoes into small pieces. Mix with sugar and let sit for 2-3 hours. Cook over medium heat with cinnamon sticks, stirring occasionally. When the mixture thickens to a jam-like consistency (after 45-60 minutes), add crushed cardamom and saffron strands. Cook for another 2-3 minutes. Cool completely and store in sterilized jars.

name: Guvar Dhokli
instructions: Make dough with wheat flour, chickpea flour, salt, spices and oil. Let it rest for 15 minutes. Roll thin and cut into thin strips. Prepare the curry: Heat oil, crackle mustard seeds and cumin, add asafoetida. Add ginger and green chilli, then tomatoes, cook until soft. Add turmeric, coriander powder, and spices. Drop dhokli strips into the simmering curry. Cook until they float and are cooked through. Finish with garam masala, lemon juice, and fresh coriander.

name: Karonda Jam
instructions: Clean and wash karonda fruits. Combine with sugar in a heavy-bottomed pot and cook over medium heat. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. When the mixture thickens and reaches jam consistency (after 45-50 minutes), add cardamom powder and cinnamon. Cook for another 2-3 minutes. Cool to room temperature and transfer to sterilized jars. Store in a cool, dry place.

name: Urad Dal Chandiya
instructions: Soak urad dal in water for 2-3 hours. Drain and grind to a thick, fluffy paste using minimal water (no water is preferred). Mix in salt, black salt, black pepper, and asafoetida. Heat oil for deep frying. Drop spoonfuls of the dal mixture carefully into hot oil using another spoon. Fry until golden and puffed, turning occasionally. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Serve hot.

name: Mango Pickle with Asafoetida
instructions: Cut raw mangoes into small cubes. Mix with salt in a glass or ceramic container and let it sit for 1-2 hours. Add turmeric powder, black salt, and asafoetida, mix well. Pour mustard oil over the mixture and stir thoroughly to combine. Cover and store in a glass jar away from direct sunlight. The pickle will be ready to eat after 3-4 days as flavors develop.

name: Stuffed Capsicum Bonda
ingredients: oil 2 tbsp plus oil for frying
instructions: Prepare batter: Mix chickpea flour with salt, half of red chilli powder, turmeric, half of coriander powder, carom seeds, and baking soda. Add water to make a medium-thick batter. Prepare filling: Heat oil, add ginger and green chilli, then mashed potatoes. Add cumin seeds, remaining coriander powder, red chilli powder, dry mango powder, garam masala, salt, and fresh coriander. Mix well. Cut capsicums in half lengthwise, remove seeds. Fill each half generously with potato mixture. Dip filled capsicums in batter. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Serve hot with chutney.

name: Crunchy Chana Chilli
instructions: Soak kabuli chana overnight. Mix corn flour and salt, coat the chana. Deep fry until golden and crispy. In a separate pan, heat oil and add ginger, green chilli, capsicum. Add soya sauce, vinegar, tomato sauce, red chilli powder, black pepper. Toss fried chana in the sauce and mix well. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot.

name: Matar Chole (Zero Oil)
instructions: Soak dry peas overnight. Pressure cook peas with baking soda and salt for 2-3 whistles until soft. In a pan, add tomato, green chilli, ginger and cook until soft. Add coriander powder, turmeric, cumin powder, asafoetida and mix. Add cooked peas and simmer for 5 minutes. Add kashmiri red chilli and garam masala. Cook until gravy thickens. Garnish with coriander leaves.

name: Milk Rusk (Eggless)
ingredients: full cream milk - 1/2 cup plus 1/2 tablespoon
instructions: Mix maida, sugar, salt and yeast. Add lukewarm milk gradually and knead soft dough. Add oil and knead until smooth. Cover and let rise for 1.5 hours. Knead again for 5-6 minutes. Shape into log, place on baking tray, let rise for 1 hour. Bake at 170°C for 10 minutes, then 5 minutes. Cool for 30 minutes. Slice with zig-zag knife. Bake slices at 170°C for 15 minutes, flip, bake at 160°C for 10 minutes until crispy.

name: Dahi Wali Arbi (Arbi in Curd Gravy)
instructions: Boil taro root until 80% cooked and peel. Whisk curd with besan, salt and spices. Heat oil for tempering, add carom seeds and red chilli powder. Pour the curd mixture over cooked arbi pieces. Add green chilli. Cook on low heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring gently to avoid curd curdling. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve warm.

name: Paneer Narmada (Stuffed Paneer)
instructions: Divide paneer into 4 parts, cut into triangles and make pocket pairs. Blend cashew, melon seeds, sesame seeds, coriander to paste. Mix with curd, salt and red chilli powder. Stuff paneer pockets. Make slurry with maida, corn flour and salt. Heat oil, dip paneer in slurry and deep fry until light brown. In another pan, heat oil, fry cashews, add chilies, ginger, capsicums, tomato. Add sauces and spices. Add fried paneer, add corn flour mixture, cook on low heat until gravy thickens. Serve hot.

name: Paneer Ghotalo (Surat Street Food)
instructions: Heat oil, add cumin seeds. Add tomato, green chilli, ginger and cook until soft. Add turmeric, coriander powder, kasuri methi and mix. Add paneer cubes and cheese cubes. Pour in fresh cream and stir gently. Add salt, red chilli powder, garam masala. Cook on medium heat for 3-4 minutes. Add green capsicum and coriander leaves. Add butter at the end. Stir well and serve hot.

name: Malai Laddu (Mawa Laddu)
instructions: Melt 700 grams malai on low-medium heat, stirring occasionally. Once malai melts, simmer on medium heat until ghee separates. Continue simmering until mawa turns pinkish (about 14-15 minutes total). Strain ghee into a bowl. Dry roast wheat flour on low-medium flame for 7-8 minutes until color changes to brown. Mix roasted flour with leftover malai mawa in a bowl. Add powdered sugar and crushed cardamom powder. Mix well. Shape small portions into round laddus with hands. Cool and store in an airtight container.

name: green peas pickle
instructions: Boil peas in sugar-water for 1.5 minutes. Cool in ice water for 2-3 minutes and drain completely. Dry peas on cloth for 15-20 minutes. Dry roast coriander seeds, fennel seeds, cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds, carom seeds, and black pepper for 1-1.5 minutes. Add mustard seeds and roast together for 15-20 seconds. Cool and coarsely grind all spices. Heat 1/2 cup mustard oil until it smokes, then cool. Add asafoetida and black cumin seeds to cooled oil. Mix peas with ground spices, turmeric powder, red chili powder, salt, and black salt in a bowl. Add 2 tbsp vinegar and mix well. Let sit for 2 days, stirring once daily.

name: soya vadi mixed vegetable idli
instructions: Mix 1 cup semolina, 1 cup curd, and 1/2 cup soaked soya vadi in a bowl. Add 1/4 cup peas, 1/4 cup carrot, 1-2 tbsp grated coconut, green coriander, 2 chopped green chilies, and 1/2 inch grated ginger. Heat 1-2 tbsp oil in a pan, add 1/2 tsp mustard seeds and let them crackle. Add 10-12 curry leaves. Pour tempering into the batter. Add salt and mix well. Just before cooking, add 1 tsp Eno fruit salt and stir. Pour batter into idli mould and steam for 8-10 minutes. Serve hot.

name: bhakose
instructions: Knead 1 cup wheat flour with 1/4 cup semolina, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1 tsp ghee, adding water gradually to make soft dough. Cover for 20 minutes. Soak 1/2 cup chickpea dal and 2 tbsp black gram for 2 hours. Grind soaked dals with 2-3 green chilies and 1/2 inch ginger lightly. Mix ground dal with 1/4 cup peas, 1/4 cup chopped capsicum, 1/4 cup chopped french beans, 3/4 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp crushed red chili, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp coriander powder, 1/2 tsp amchur, 1/4 tsp garam masala, asafoetida, and 2-3 tbsp coriander leaves. Boil water with ghee in a pan. Break small dough balls, roll thick like paratha, add 1-1.5 tbsp filling in middle, fold like gujiya and seal edges. Boil in water for 4-5 minutes until they float, then cook partially covered at high heat for 15 minutes. Remove, drain, and cool. Cut into 2-3 pieces. Heat 2 tsp ghee, add 1/2 tsp mustard seeds, 1/2 tsp cumin, 15-20 curry leaves. Add cut bhakose, 2 pinch salt, and 1/2 tsp crushed red chili. Fry on medium flame while stirring occasionally until well coated. Serve with chutneys.

name: rajasthani magad choorma
instructions: Dry roast 1 cup wheat flour in 3-4 tbsp ghee on low heat, stirring constantly until golden and fragrant, about 5-7 minutes. Add 2-3 tbsp milk gradually to maintain consistency. Add 2-3 tbsp almond flakes and 2-3 tbsp cashews. Mix in 1/2 cup grated dry coconut and 2 tbsp raisins. Coarsely grind 5 green cardamom and add to the mixture. Lastly, add 3/4 cup boora (powdered sugar) and mix well. Transfer to a greased tray and press gently. Cool completely before cutting into pieces.

name: besan stuffed parwal
instructions: Slit pointed gourds lengthwise and carefully remove seeds using a spoon. Mix 3 tbsp gram flour with 1.5 tsp fennel powder, 1.5 tsp coriander powder, 1/2 tsp turmeric powder, 1.5 tsp kashmiri red chili, 1/2 tsp cumin powder, 2 chopped green chilies, 1/2 tsp grated ginger, 3/4 tsp dry mango powder, 1/4 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp salt, and 2 tbsp coriander leaves. Stuff this mixture inside the slit parwal pieces. Heat 3 tbsp oil in a pan and fry the stuffed parwal on medium heat until golden brown on both sides and parwal becomes tender, about 8-10 minutes. Serve hot.

name: green peas makhani curry
instructions: Heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan. Add 1/4 tsp cumin seeds and 1/2 pinch asafoetida and let crackle briefly. Add 1/4 tsp turmeric powder and 1 tsp coriander powder and roast. Add 2 tomatoes, 1 green chili, and 1/4 inch ginger paste to the pan. Add 1/2 tsp red chili powder and roast the masala until oil separates. When masala is half cooked, add 1 cup green peas and 3/4 tsp salt. Mix well and cover. Cook over low heat for 5 minutes. Add 1/4 tsp garam masala and 1 tbsp butter to the sabzi. Once butter melts, add 1 tbsp gram flour and roast until golden brown. Add 2-3 tbsp water and chopped coriander leaves. Cook for 2 minutes until the curry reaches desired consistency. Serve hot.

name: besan ki barfi
instructions: Heat 3/4 cup ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan on low to medium heat. Add 1.5 cups chickpea flour gradually while stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Continue roasting the flour in ghee for 8-10 minutes until fragrant and slightly darkened in color, stirring occasionally. Add 3/4 cup milk powder and mix well, continuing to roast for another 3-4 minutes. The mixture should be smooth and have a creamy appearance. Coarsely grind 4 green cardamom pods and add to the mixture. Add 1 cup sugar and mix thoroughly until all sugar crystals dissolve into the warm mixture. Transfer the mixture immediately to a greased rectangular tray or plate and press gently with a spatula to even the surface. Sprinkle 1 tbsp almond flakes and 1 tbsp pistachio flakes on top. Allow to cool completely at room temperature for 1-2 hours. Once firm, cut into square pieces using a sharp knife. Store in an airtight container.

name: jeera aloo
instructions: Boil 5 potatoes, peel and cut into pieces. Heat 2-3 tbsp oil on low flame, add cumin, asafoetida, turmeric, green chili and ginger, fry lightly. Add potatoes and salt, fry on medium-high heat. Add dry fenugreek and fry 3-4 minutes. Add coriander powder, red chili, black pepper, dry mango powder and garam masala, roast well. Garnish with green coriander and serve hot with parathas or roti.

name: paneer kali mirch makhani
instructions: Prepare paneer marinade: mix paneer with curd, cream, cornflour, fenugreek, coriander stem, black pepper, cardamom and salt. Make gravy: soak cashews and muskmelon seeds, grind with green chili and ginger into paste. Heat oil and butter, add spices and gravy paste, fry until oil separates. Add cream, salt, sugar and remaining curd. Simmer and add marinated paneer pieces. Cook until paneer is tender. Serve hot.

name: kolhapuri kat vada
ingredients: oil 5 tbsp plus 1-1.5 tbsp plus frying oil
instructions: Make kat: Roast cumin, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, pepper, black cardamom, coriander and poppy seeds. Add grated coconut and ginger, roast. Add chopped tomatoes and salt, cook covered 4-5 minutes. Blend when cool. Make vada filling: Heat oil, add mustard seeds, asafoetida, ginger, green chili, curry leaves. Add boiled mashed potato, salt, amchoor, garam masala. Add coriander and roast 5 minutes. Make vada batter: Mix gram flour, rice flour, turmeric, red chili, salt, asafoetida, baking soda with water to pouring consistency. Shape filling into potato balls, coat with batter. Fry in hot oil until golden. Prepare kat: heat oil with spices, add blended masala, fry until oil separates. Add water, salt and coriander, simmer 7 minutes. Pour hot kat into plate, add fried vadas, garnish with coriander and sev.

name: dahi bengan
instructions: Roast cloves, black pepper, coriander powder, turmeric and cumin seeds over low heat. Add ginger and roast. Add chopped tomato and salt, cook covered 4-5 minutes. Stir and cook another 2 minutes. Heat oil separately, add mustard seeds to crackle. Add asafoetida, ginger, green chili and curry leaves. Layer in dish: spread half the curd, add eggplant pieces, top with masala mixture and remaining curd. Season with cumin seeds and red chili oil on top. Cook covered on low heat or steam until eggplant is tender.

name: gajar ki kanji
ingredients: black mustard seeds 2 tbsp coarsely ground
instructions: Cut black carrots into pieces or chips. Place in a clean glass jar. Add coarsely ground mustard seeds, salt, black salt, black pepper and asafoetida. Cover with water completely. Cover the jar loosely with cloth or lid. Leave to ferment for 3-5 days at room temperature. The kanji will become dark and develop tangy fermented flavor. Drink as a traditional digestive beverage.

name: steamed modak
instructions: Prepare filling: Mix jaggery, grated coconut, almond flakes, cashew powder and ground cardamom. Keep aside. Make dough: Mix rice flour with salt. Add ghee and water gradually to make soft dough. Knead well. Let dough rest few minutes. Prepare modak: Oil hands lightly, take portion of dough, make shallow cup shape, fill with spoon of jaggery-coconut mixture. Close edges and shape into traditional modak form. Steam modaks in steamer for 10-15 minutes until cooked through. Serve warm.

name: veg mughlai paratha
ingredients: oil 2 tsp plus 1 tsp plus 3 tbsp for frying
instructions: Prepare dough: Mix flour, salt and baking powder. Add oil and water gradually, knead into soft dough. Rest dough. Prepare filling: Heat oil, add ginger and green chili. Add grated carrot, chopped capsicum and cauliflower. Add turmeric, red chili, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala, amchoor and salt. Cook until vegetables are tender. Add crumbled paneer and coriander leaves, mix well. Cool filling. Roll dough into thin circle, place filling in center, gather edges and seal. Flatten gently. Heat oil in pan, fry paratha until golden brown on both sides. Serve hot with chutney or achar.

name: eggless chocolate cupcake
instructions: Mix flour, cornflour, cocoa powder, sugar powder, baking soda and baking powder in bowl. In another bowl, mix oil, milk, vinegar and vanilla essence. Combine wet and dry ingredients, mix until smooth batter forms. Pour into cupcake molds or small cups. For no-bake version: refrigerate for 2-3 hours until set. For baked version: bake at 180C for 15-18 minutes. Garnish with almond flakes. Serve chilled.

name: masala fulwadi
instructions: Mix gram flour, semolina, turmeric, cumin powder, coriander seeds, fennel seeds, black pepper, red chilli, sesame seeds, dry mango powder, and salt. Add curd, sugar powder, 6 tbsp oil, and baking soda. Mix well to form a soft dough. Knead for 5 minutes. Heat oil for frying. Using a sev maker or squeeze bottle, extrude long strips of dough directly into hot oil. Fry until golden and crisp. Drain on paper towels. Store in an airtight container.

name: matar ki muthiya
instructions: Grind green peas coarsely. Mix with wheat flour, gram flour, fenugreek leaves, salt, turmeric, coriander powder, green chilli, red chilli, ginger, cumin seeds, and carom seeds. Add baking soda mixed with curd and knead to soft dough with water. Boil water and grease a sieve with oil. Divide dough into 2 equal parts and roll into logs. Steam on sieve for 2 minutes on high heat, then 20 minutes on medium heat. Cut into 1/2 inch pieces. Heat oil in pan, add sesame seeds, cumin seeds, salt, and red chilli powder. Add muthiya pieces and crisp both sides. Make chutney from coriander leaves, green chilli, ginger, black salt, salt, cumin, and amchur. Serve muthiya with chutney.

name: khasta mathri
instructions: Mix wheat flour, refined flour, semolina, carom seeds, cumin seeds, black pepper, kalonji, kasuri methi, and salt. Add 1/4 cup oil and ghee, and rub into flour until breadcrumb texture. Add water gradually and knead to stiff dough. Let rest for 30 minutes. Divide dough into portions. Roll each thin, brush with ghee and 2 tbsp refined flour, roll up, coil, and flatten. Roll again to thin disc. Heat oil for frying. Fry mathri until light golden and crisp on both sides. Drain on paper towels. Cool and store in airtight container.

name: thai basil chicken
instructions: Mix dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, castor sugar, and chicken stock in a bowl. Heat oil in a kadai. Add chopped garlic and saute till golden brown. Add chopped red chillies and mix. Add chicken pieces and saute on high heat for 2-3 minutes. Add spring onion bulbs and saute for 2 minutes. Pour in sauce mixture, add salt, and cook for 6-8 minutes. Add Thai basil leaves and white sesame seeds, mix well. Transfer to serving bowl and garnish with fresh red chillies. Serve hot with sticky rice.

name: saffron cookies
instructions: Soak saffron in warm milk and let infuse. Beat butter lightly in a bowl. Add icing sugar and beat till light and fluffy. Add refined flour, green cardamom powder, and salt and beat well. Transfer mixture into a piping bag fitted with a star nozzle. Line a baking tray with greased parchment paper and pipe small cookie portions. Sprinkle pistachios on top. Freeze for 10-15 minutes. Preheat oven to 180°C. Bake in preheated oven for 10-12 minutes till light golden. Allow to cool completely. Store in an airtight container.

name: pasta in saffron sauce
ingredients: yellow capsicum 0.5 small seeded cut into triangles
red capsicum 0.5 small seeded cut into triangles
green capsicum 0.5 small seeded cut into triangles
instructions: Heat olive oil and butter in a pan. Add refined flour and saffron, saute till fragrant. Add chopped garlic and saute till golden brown. Add red chilli flakes and mix well. Pour in milk gradually, stirring constantly, and cook till sauce thickens. Add blanched broccoli florets, yellow capsicum triangles, red capsicum triangles, green capsicum triangles, and cooked mushroom slices. Cook for 2-3 minutes. Add boiled penne pasta and salt, mix gently and cook for 2-3 minutes more. Transfer to serving bowl, garnish with fresh basil sprig, and serve hot with garlic bread.

name: Mushroom and Turmeric Soup
instructions: Heat butter in a pan and sauté garlic until golden brown. Add onion and cook until translucent. Add turmeric, shitake mushrooms, button mushrooms, salt, black pepper and herbs. Cook for 4-5 minutes. Add water and bring to boil, then simmer covered for 6-8 minutes. Cool and grind to a fine mixture. In a fresh pan, heat the ground mixture with water, discard scum, add fresh cream. In another pan, sauté shimeji mushrooms in butter on high heat for 2-3 minutes. Pour soup into serving bowls, top with sautéed shimeji mushrooms, sprinkle pepper, and serve hot with lavash.

name: Moong Osaman
instructions: Heat oil in a pan and add cumin seeds. When they crackle, add finely chopped garlic and ginger, sauté for a minute. Add chopped onion and cook until golden. Add turmeric powder and salt. Add soaked whole moong and roast for 2-3 minutes. Add water and bring to boil. Simmer until moong is cooked through but retains its shape, about 20-25 minutes. Add green chillies towards the end. Serve hot.

name: Peanut Sauce
instructions: Crush garlic cloves and shallots in a mortar with red chilli powder. Grind roasted peanuts to fine powder in a grinder. Heat a pan and add coconut milk, turmeric, coriander, crushed garlic-shallot mixture and Thai red curry paste. Cook for 2-3 minutes. Add peanut powder and cook for another 2-3 minutes. Add tamarind paste, lemon juice, salt and brown sugar. Cook until sugar melts. Cool completely. Garnish with bird eye chillies and serve as a dip or dressing.

name: Green Moong Mathri
instructions: Grind soaked green gram coarsely in a grinder. In a bowl, mix whole wheat flour, semolina, asafoetida, onion seeds, carom seeds, baking soda, fennel seeds, cumin seeds, black pepper, turmeric, salt, fenugreek leaves and melted ghee. Add ground green gram and mix well. Add water and knead to a soft dough. Add oil and knead well. On an oiled surface, roll dough into semi-thick discs, dock all over with a fork and cut into small roundels using a cookie cutter. Heat sufficient oil and deep fry until golden brown and crisp. Drain on absorbent paper and cool completely before storing.

name: Rice Parantha
instructions: In a bowl, mix whole wheat flour, salt, 2 tsp ghee and yogurt. Add water and knead to soft dough with 2 tsp oil. Cover with moist cloth for 15-20 minutes. In another bowl, mix cooked rice, red chilli powder, cumin powder, chopped green chillies, coriander, salt and chopped onion. On a floured surface, roll dough into thin roundels and press between palms. Flatten into thick disc, drizzle 1 tsp ghee on it and stuff with rice mixture. Seal edges and roll out into parantha. Heat a tawa, place parantha on it and cook. Drizzle 2 tsp ghee on both sides and shallow fry until golden brown. Cut into quarters and serve hot with pickle.

name: Peas Cashew Samosa
ingredients: date and tamarind chutney for serving
instructions: For dough: Mix refined flour, carom seeds, salt, ghee and water. Knead to stiff dough and cover for 10-15 minutes. For stuffing: Dry roast coriander, fennel and cumin seeds until fragrant and crush coarsely. Heat oil, add ginger, green chillies, roasted spices and asafoetida. Sauté for 30 seconds. Add cashew nuts, dried mango powder, red chilli powder and salt. Mix well. Add green peas and boiled mashed potatoes. Cook for 2-3 minutes. Cool. Divide dough into 5 portions and roll into thick ovals. Cut into half, apply water on edges and shape into cones. Stuff with potato mixture and seal. Preheat air fryer to 180C for 2-3 minutes. Spray oil on basket, arrange samosas and spray oil on them. Air fry for 40-45 minutes until golden. Serve with green chutney and date-tamarind chutney.

name: Mushroom Satay
ingredients: spring onion greens chopped for garnish
instructions: 1. Grind shallots, garlic, galangal, lemongrass, and red chilli powder coarsely with 1 tbsp water. 2. Add coconut milk, Thai red curry paste and brown sugar and grind to fine paste. 3. Mix mushrooms in bowl with ground paste, turmeric and coriander powder and salt. 4. Thread mushroom pieces on wooden skewers. 5. Heat oil in nonstick grill pan and arrange skewers. 6. Cook on medium heat 3-4 minutes per side. 7. Transfer to serving plate, sprinkle spring onion greens and serve hot with peanut sauce.

name: Saffron Madeleines
instructions: 1. Preheat oven to 180°C. 2. Soak saffron in warm milk and set aside. 3. Beat eggs with castor sugar gradually until light and fluffy. 4. Sift refined flour, baking powder, salt and green cardamom powder together. 5. Fold dry ingredients into egg mixture. 6. Add saffron milk and beat well. 7. Transfer to piping bag and pipe into madeleine mould. 8. Bake in preheated oven for 10-12 minutes. 9. Allow to cool and serve.

name: Green Moong and Avocado Hummus
instructions: 1. Soak and cook green moong dal until soft. 2. Take cooked moong and avocado in a blender. 3. Add garlic cloves, lemon juice, olive oil and salt. 4. Blend until creamy. 5. Add cumin powder and adjust seasonings. 6. Serve as a dip with vegetables or crackers.

name: Meetho Lolo
instructions: 1. Heat 1 cup water in nonstick pan, add sugar and stir until dissolved. 2. Add 1/4 tsp green cardamom powder and cook until syrup forms. 3. Mix flour with remaining 1/4 tsp cardamom powder. 4. Add sugar syrup to flour and knead into stiff dough. 5. Divide dough into lemon-sized portions and roll into balls. 6. Flatten balls and roll into thick rotis. 7. Roast on nonstick tawa on low heat, turn over and drizzle oil. 8. Cook until both sides are golden. 9. Serve hot or at room temperature.

name: Sui Mai
ingredients: refined flour 1.5 cups plus for dusting
soaked dried shitake mushrooms chopped 2-3 tbsps
instructions: 1. Mix turmeric powder with 3/4 cup water. 2. Knead refined flour with salt, egg yolk and turmeric water into stiff dough, rest 30 minutes. 3. Mix chicken mince with ground prawns, spring onion greens, mushrooms, spring onion bulbs, soy sauce, sesame oil, sugar, stock powder and salt. 4. Divide dough into small portions, roll into thin disks. 5. Place disk between thumb and finger and stuff filling. 6. Press lightly and pinch sides. 7. Sprinkle chopped carrot on top and arrange in dim sum basket. 8. Steam for 8-10 minutes. 9. Serve hot with chilli oil dip.

name: Quick Chicken Tikka
ingredients: 400 gms boneless chicken breasts, cut into 1/2 inch pieces
2 medium green capsicums, cut into 1/2 inch pieces
instructions: 1. Marinate chicken pieces with red chilli paste, salt, ginger-garlic paste, yogurt, garam masala powder and lemon juice. 2. Add 2 tbsps oil and marinate for a few hours in refrigerator. 3. Heat non-stick pan and pour orange juice into it. 4. String chicken and capsicum pieces alternately onto skewers. 5. Heat non-stick tawa, drizzle oil and place skewers on it. 6. Pack rice into mould and demould on serving plate, drizzle 1 tbsp oil over it. 7. Add salt, pepper powder and sugar to orange juice and cook. 8. Turn skewers so chicken cooks evenly all around. 9. When sauce thickens, remove from heat, add butter and mix well. 10. Place skewers over rice, drizzle sauce and serve immediately.

name: Chicken Popcorn
ingredients: 300 grams boneless chicken breast, cut into 1/2 inch pieces
instructions: 1. Take chicken pieces in bowl. Add salt, red chilli powder, black pepper powder, ginger-garlic paste and vinegar, mix well. 2. Sift refined flour, salt and baking powder in a bowl. 3. Roll chicken pieces in refined flour mixture and dip in ice cold water for 30 seconds. 4. Roll in refined flour mixture again. 5. Preheat air fryer at 190°C for 2-3 minutes. 6. Spray oil on air fryer basket and arrange chicken pieces in single layer. 7. Air fry for 6 minutes. Flip chicken pieces, spray more oil and air fry for 6 more minutes. 8. Serve hot.
